holds the derivative of f at a. More generally, if the input carries a
derivative seed s (its Der field), the output's Der field holds f'(a)*s by
the chain rule.

funcs.name: Abs
funcs.doc:
Abs returns |x|; its derivative is the sign of x and is undefined at zero,
where this implementation returns a zero derivative.

funcs.name: Acos
funcs.doc: Acos returns arccos(x); its derivative is -1/√(1-x²).

funcs.name: Acosh
funcs.doc:
Acosh returns the inverse hyperbolic cosine arcosh(x); its derivative is
1/√(x²-1) and is defined for x > 1.

funcs.name: Asin
funcs.doc: Asin returns arcsin(x); its derivative is 1/√(1-x²).

funcs.name: Asinh
funcs.doc:
Asinh returns the inverse hyperbolic sine arsinh(x); its derivative is
1/√(x²+1).

funcs.name: Atan
funcs.doc: Atan returns arctan(x); its derivative is 1/(1+x²).

funcs.name: Atan2
funcs.doc:
Atan2 returns the two-argument arctangent atan2(y, x). Its differential is
(x·dy - y·dx)/(x²+y²), giving the correct gradient of the polar angle.

funcs.name: Atanh
funcs.doc:
Atanh returns the inverse hyperbolic tangent artanh(x); its derivative is
1/(1-x²) and is defined for |x| < 1.

funcs.name: Cbrt
funcs.doc: Cbrt returns the cube root ∛x; its derivative is 1/(3·x^(2/3)).

funcs.name: Constant
funcs.doc:
Constant returns the dual number val + 0*ε, representing a quantity whose
derivative with respect to the independent variable is zero.

funcs.name: Cos
funcs.doc: Cos returns cos(x); its derivative is -sin(x).

funcs.name: Cosh
funcs.doc: Cosh returns cosh(x); its derivative is sinh(x).

funcs.name: Cot
funcs.doc:
Cot returns the cotangent cot(x) = cos(x)/sin(x); its derivative is
-csc²(x) = -1/sin²(x).

funcs.name: Coth
funcs.doc:
Coth returns the hyperbolic cotangent coth(x) = cosh(x)/sinh(x); its
derivative is -csch²(x) = -1/sinh²(x).

funcs.name: Csc
funcs.doc:
Csc returns the cosecant csc(x) = 1/sin(x); its derivative is
-csc(x)·cot(x).

funcs.name: Csch
funcs.doc:
Csch returns the hyperbolic cosecant csch(x) = 1/sinh(x); its derivative is
-csch(x)·coth(x).

funcs.name: Erf
funcs.doc:
Erf returns the error function erf(x); its derivative is
(2/√π)·e^(-x²).

funcs.name: Erfc
funcs.doc:
Erfc returns the complementary error function erfc(x) = 1-erf(x); its
derivative is -(2/√π)·e^(-x²).

funcs.name: Exp
funcs.doc: Exp returns e^x; its derivative is e^x.

funcs.name: Exp2
funcs.doc: Exp2 returns 2^x; its derivative is ln(2)·2^x.

funcs.name: Expm1
funcs.doc: Expm1 returns e^x - 1 accurately for small x; its derivative is e^x.

funcs.name: Hypot
funcs.doc:
Hypot returns √(x²+y²) computed without spurious overflow; its differential
is (x·dx + y·dy)/hypot(x,y).

funcs.name: Log
funcs.doc: Log returns the natural logarithm ln(x); its derivative is 1/x.

funcs.name: Log10
funcs.doc:
Log10 returns the base-10 logarithm log₁₀(x); its derivative is
1/(x·ln10).

funcs.name: Log1p
funcs.doc: Log1p returns ln(1+x) accurately for small x; its derivative is 1/(1+x).

funcs.name: Log2
funcs.doc: Log2 returns the base-2 logarithm log₂(x); its derivative is 1/(x·ln2).

funcs.name: Max
funcs.doc:
Max returns the larger of x and y as a dual number, propagating the
derivative of the selected argument. Ties select x.

funcs.name: Min
funcs.doc:
Min returns the smaller of x and y as a dual number, propagating the
derivative of the selected argument. Ties select x.

funcs.name: NewDual
funcs.doc: NewDual returns the dual number val + der*ε.

funcs.name: Pow
funcs.doc:
Pow returns x^y where both the base and exponent are dual numbers. Using
x^y = exp(y·ln x), its differential is
x^y·(y'·ln x + y·x'/x). The base must be positive.

funcs.name: PowReal
funcs.signature: func PowReal(x Dual, p float64) Dual
funcs.doc:
PowReal returns x^p for a constant real exponent p; its derivative is
p·x^(p-1).

funcs.name: Powf
funcs.signature: func Powf(a float64, x Dual) Dual
funcs.doc:
Powf returns a^x for a constant real base a and dual exponent x. Its
derivative is ln(a)·a^x.

funcs.name: Relu
funcs.doc:
Relu returns the rectified linear unit max(0, x); its derivative is 1 for
positive x and 0 otherwise, with the sub-gradient at zero taken as 0.

funcs.name: Sec
funcs.doc:
Sec returns the secant sec(x) = 1/cos(x); its derivative is
sec(x)·tan(x).

funcs.name: Sech
funcs.doc:
Sech returns the hyperbolic secant sech(x) = 1/cosh(x); its derivative is
-sech(x)·tanh(x).

funcs.name: Sigmoid
funcs.doc:
Sigmoid returns the logistic function σ(x) = 1/(1+e^(-x)); its derivative is
σ(x)·(1-σ(x)).

funcs.name: Sin
funcs.doc: Sin returns sin(x); its derivative is cos(x).

funcs.name: Sinh
funcs.doc: Sinh returns sinh(x); its derivative is cosh(x).

funcs.name: Softplus
funcs.doc:
Softplus returns the smooth rectifier ln(1+e^x); its derivative is the
logistic function σ(x).

funcs.name: Sqrt
funcs.doc: Sqrt returns the square root √x; its derivative is 1/(2√x).

funcs.name: Tan
funcs.doc: Tan returns tan(x); its derivative is sec²(x) = 1/cos²(x).

funcs.name: Tanh
funcs.doc: Tanh returns tanh(x); its derivative is sech²(x) = 1-tanh²(x).

funcs.name: Variable
funcs.doc:
Variable returns the dual number val + 1*ε, the seed for the independent
variable of a forward-mode differentiation. The unit derivative propagates
the chain rule so that the output's Der field holds a true derivative.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the sum x + y.

methods.name: AddReal
methods.doc:
AddReal returns x + k where k is a real constant, leaving the derivative
unchanged.

methods.name: Div
methods.doc:
Div returns the quotient x / y using the quotient rule
(a/b)' = (a'*b - a*b')/b². It returns a number with NaN or infinite parts
if y.Val is zero, matching IEEE-754 division.

methods.name: Inv
methods.doc: Inv returns the reciprocal 1/x, whose derivative is -x'/x².

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc:
Mul returns the product x * y using the product rule
(a*b)' = a'*b + a*b'.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns the negation -x.

methods.name: Real
methods.doc:
Real returns the primal (real) part of the dual number, discarding the
derivative. It is shorthand for the Val field.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc:
Scale returns x multiplied by the real scalar k, differentiating k as a
constant so that (k*x)' = k*x'.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String formats the dual number in the canonical "a+bε" notation.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the difference x - y.

types.name: HyperDual
types.doc:
HyperDual is a second-order forward-mode number of the form

	Val + E1·ε₁ + E2·ε₂ + E12·ε₁ε₂,

where ε₁ and ε₂ are independent nilpotent infinitesimals with ε₁² = ε₂² = 0
but ε₁ε₂ ≠ 0. Evaluating a twice-differentiable function f on the number
a + ε₁ + ε₂ yields

	f(a) + f'(a)·ε₁ + f'(a)·ε₂ + f''(a)·ε₁ε₂,

so the E12 slot delivers the second derivative exactly, with no subtractive
cancellation. Seeding the two first-order slots with distinct directions
yields mixed second partial derivatives, which is what [Hessian] exploits.

funcs.name: HyperConstant
funcs.doc:
HyperConstant returns the hyper-dual number val with all infinitesimal parts
zero, representing a quantity with vanishing first and second derivatives.

funcs.name: HyperVariable
funcs.doc:
HyperVariable returns the hyper-dual seed val + ε₁ + ε₂ for a single
independent variable. After evaluating a scalar function, its E12 slot holds
the second derivative and either first-order slot holds the first derivative.

funcs.name: NewHyperDual
funcs.signature: func NewHyperDual(val, e1, e2, e12 float64) HyperDual
funcs.doc: NewHyperDual returns the hyper-dual number val + e1·ε₁ + e2·ε₂ + e12·ε₁ε₂.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the sum x + y.

methods.name: AddReal
methods.doc: AddReal returns x + k where k is a real constant.

methods.name: Cos
methods.doc: Cos returns cos(x); it uses cos' = -sin and cos” = -cos.

methods.name: Cosh
methods.doc: Cosh returns cosh(x); it uses cosh' = sinh and cosh” = cosh.

methods.name: Div
methods.doc: Div returns the quotient x / y as x · y⁻¹.

methods.name: Exp
methods.doc: Exp returns e^x; every derivative of the exponential is itself.

methods.name: Inv
methods.doc:
Inv returns the reciprocal 1/x, obtained by applying the reciprocal function
and its derivatives r(a)=1/a, r'(a)=-1/a², r”(a)=2/a³.

methods.name: Log
methods.doc: Log returns ln(x); it uses (ln)' = 1/x and (ln)” = -1/x².

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc:
Mul returns the product x · y using the full second-order product rule,
including the cross term E1·E2 + E2·E1 that feeds the mixed slot.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns the negation -x.

methods.name: PowReal
methods.doc:
PowReal returns x^p for a constant real exponent p, with first derivative
p·x^(p-1) and second derivative p·(p-1)·x^(p-2).

methods.name: Real
methods.doc: Real returns the primal part of the hyper-dual number.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns x multiplied by the real scalar k.

methods.name: Sigmoid
methods.doc:
Sigmoid returns the logistic function σ(x) = 1/(1+e^(-x)); it uses
σ' = σ(1-σ) and σ” = σ(1-σ)(1-2σ).

methods.name: Sin
methods.doc: Sin returns sin(x); it uses sin' = cos and sin” = -sin.

methods.name: Sinh
methods.doc: Sinh returns sinh(x); it uses sinh' = cosh and sinh” = sinh.

methods.name: Sqrt
methods.doc: Sqrt returns √x; it uses derivatives 1/(2√x) and -1/(4·x^(3/2)).

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the difference x - y.

methods.name: Tan
methods.doc: Tan returns tan(x); it uses tan' = sec² and tan” = 2·sec²·tan.

methods.name: Tanh
methods.doc: Tanh returns tanh(x); it uses tanh' = 1-tanh² and tanh” = -2·tanh·(1-tanh²).

types.name: Tape
types.doc:
Tape records the elementary operations of a computation in evaluation order so
that adjoints can be propagated backward from an output to every registered
input in a single sweep. Reverse mode computes the whole gradient of a scalar
function at a cost proportional to one function evaluation, independent of the
number of inputs, which is what makes it the method of choice for
high-dimensional optimization. A Tape holds no global state; construct one
per computation with [NewTape].

funcs.name: NewTape
funcs.doc: NewTape returns a new, empty operation tape ready to record a computation.

methods.name: Backward
methods.doc:
Backward propagates adjoints from the output node y backward through the tape
and returns the gradient of y with respect to every registered independent
variable, in the order the variables were created. Because nodes are appended
after their parents, a single reverse sweep over the tape suffices.

methods.name: Constant
methods.doc:
Constant records a constant with value v on the tape. Its adjoint is computed
but it is not returned by [Tape.Backward] because it is not an independent
variable.

methods.name: NumVars
methods.doc: NumVars returns the number of independent variables registered on the tape.

methods.name: Variable
methods.doc:
Variable records an independent variable with value v on the tape and
registers it so that its adjoint appears in the gradient returned by
[Tape.Backward], in registration order.

types.name: Var
types.doc:
Var is a handle to a single node on a [Tape]. Operations on Var record their
structure onto the tape as a side effect and return a new Var for the result.
A Var is only meaningful together with the Tape that produced it.

methods.name: Abs
methods.doc:
Abs records and returns |v| with derivative sign(v); the derivative at zero
is taken as zero.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add records and returns the sum v + w.

methods.name: AddReal
methods.doc: AddReal records and returns v + k for a real constant k.

methods.name: Cos
methods.doc: Cos records and returns cos(v) with derivative -sin(v).

methods.name: Div
methods.doc: Div records and returns the quotient v / w with partials 1/w and -v/w².

methods.name: Exp
methods.doc: Exp records and returns e^v.

methods.name: Log
methods.doc: Log records and returns ln(v) with derivative 1/v.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc:
Mul records and returns the product v · w, whose local partials are the
opposite operand's value.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg records and returns the negation -v.

methods.name: PowReal
methods.doc:
PowReal records and returns v^p for a constant real exponent p, with
derivative p·v^(p-1).

methods.name: Recip
methods.doc: Recip records and returns the reciprocal 1/v with derivative -1/v².

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale records and returns k·v for a real constant k.

methods.name: Sigmoid
methods.doc:
Sigmoid records and returns the logistic function σ(v) with derivative
σ(v)·(1-σ(v)).

methods.name: Sin
methods.doc: Sin records and returns sin(v) with derivative cos(v).

methods.name: Sqrt
methods.doc: Sqrt records and returns √v with derivative 1/(2√v).

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub records and returns the difference v - w.

methods.name: Tan
methods.doc: Tan records and returns tan(v) with derivative sec²(v).

methods.name: Tanh
methods.doc: Tanh records and returns tanh(v) with derivative 1-tanh²(v).

methods.name: Value
methods.doc: Value returns the primal value stored at this node.

funcs.name: Derivative
funcs.signature: func Derivative(f func(Dual) Dual, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Derivative returns f'(x), the first derivative of the scalar function f at
the point x, obtained by seeding a unit derivative and reading the output's
derivative slot.

funcs.name: Derivatives2
funcs.signature: func Derivatives2(f func(HyperDual) HyperDual, x float64) (value, first, second float64)
funcs.doc:
Derivatives2 returns f(x), f'(x) and f”(x) together from one hyper-dual
evaluation of the scalar function f at x.

funcs.name: DirectionalDerivative
funcs.signature: func DirectionalDerivative(f func([]Dual) Dual, x, dir []float64) float64
funcs.doc:
DirectionalDerivative returns the derivative of the scalar field f: Rⁿ → R at
x along the direction dir, that is ∇f(x)·dir. It seeds each input's
derivative with the corresponding component of dir, so the whole quantity is
obtained from one evaluation. The direction need not be a unit vector; to get
the rate of change per unit length, normalize dir first. x and dir must have
equal length.

funcs.name: Gradient
funcs.doc:
Gradient returns the gradient ∇f of the scalar field f: Rⁿ → R evaluated at
x. It performs n forward-mode passes, one per coordinate, seeding a unit
derivative in a single input at a time. The returned slice has the same
length as x and is freshly allocated.

funcs.name: GradientHessian
funcs.signature: func GradientHessian(f func([]HyperDual) HyperDual, x []float64) (grad []float64, hess [][]float64)
funcs.doc:
GradientHessian returns both the gradient and the Hessian of the scalar field
f: Rⁿ → R at x from the same set of hyper-dual passes. The gradient's i-th
component is read from the ε₁ slot when coordinate i carries the ε₁ seed.

funcs.name: GradientReverse
funcs.signature: func GradientReverse(f func(t *Tape, x []Var) Var, x []float64) []float64
funcs.doc:
GradientReverse returns the gradient of the scalar function f: Rⁿ → R at x
using a single reverse-mode sweep. The function f receives a fresh [Tape] and
the input variables already registered on it, and must return the scalar
output built from those variables. This is the reverse-mode counterpart of
[Gradient] and is preferable when n is large.

funcs.name: Hessian
funcs.doc:
Hessian returns the Hessian matrix of second partial derivatives of the
scalar field f: Rⁿ → R at x. The result H is n×n with H[i][j] = ∂²f/∂xᵢ∂xⱼ.
It seeds coordinate i in the ε₁ slot and coordinate j in the ε₂ slot for each
pair, reading the mixed slot of the output. Symmetry is exploited so only the
upper triangle is evaluated, taking n(n+1)/2 passes; the matrix returned is
fully populated and symmetric.

funcs.name: HessianVectorProduct
funcs.signature: func HessianVectorProduct(f func([]HyperDual) HyperDual, x, v []float64) []float64
funcs.doc:
HessianVectorProduct returns H(x)·v, the product of the Hessian of the scalar
field f: Rⁿ → R at x with the vector v, without forming the full Hessian. It
seeds the direction v into the ε₁ slots and a unit ε₂ into one coordinate at
a time, so each of the n passes yields one component of the result. x and v
must have equal length.

funcs.name: Jacobian
funcs.doc:
Jacobian returns the Jacobian matrix of the vector field f: Rⁿ → Rᵐ at x. The
result J is an m×n matrix with J[i][j] = ∂fᵢ/∂xⱼ, where m is the length of the
slice f returns. It performs n forward-mode passes. The number of outputs is
inferred from the first evaluation and assumed constant.

funcs.name: JacobianVectorProduct
funcs.doc:
JacobianVectorProduct returns J(x)·v, the product of the Jacobian of
f: Rⁿ → Rᵐ at x with the vector v, without forming J. It seeds the inputs
with v and reads the derivative slots of all outputs in a single pass, which
is the efficient forward-mode primitive for tangent propagation. x and v must
have equal length; the result has length m.

funcs.name: PartialDerivative
funcs.signature: func PartialDerivative(f func([]Dual) Dual, x []float64, i int) float64
funcs.doc:
PartialDerivative returns ∂f/∂xᵢ, the partial derivative of the scalar field
f: Rⁿ → R with respect to its i-th argument, evaluated at x. It seeds a unit
derivative in coordinate i only and needs a single evaluation.

funcs.name: SecondDerivative
funcs.signature: func SecondDerivative(f func(HyperDual) HyperDual, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
SecondDerivative returns f”(x), the second derivative of the scalar function
f at x, from a single hyper-dual evaluation with both first-order slots
seeded to one.

funcs.name: ValueAndDerivative
funcs.signature: func ValueAndDerivative(f func(Dual) Dual, x float64) (value, derivative float64)
funcs.doc:
ValueAndDerivative returns both f(x) and f'(x) from a single forward-mode
evaluation of f at x.

funcs.name: ValueAndGradient
funcs.signature: func ValueAndGradient(f func([]Dual) Dual, x []float64) (value float64, grad []float64)
funcs.doc:
ValueAndGradient returns f(x) together with its gradient ∇f(x). The value is
taken from the first pass, so f is assumed deterministic.

packages.name: automata
packages.synopsis: Package automata implements finite automata, regular expressions and related machines of formal-language theory in pure Go using only the standard library.
packages.doc:
Package automata implements finite automata, regular expressions and related
machines of formal-language theory in pure Go using only the standard
library.

The package is organised around a small number of concrete machine types and
a large collection of algorithms that transform and analyse them:

  - [DFA] is a deterministic finite automaton with a possibly partial
    transition function. It supports acceptance testing, completion,
    complementation, trimming of unreachable and dead states, shortest-word
    and word-enumeration queries, exact word counting via [DFA.CountAcceptedWords],
    and emptiness/finiteness/universality decisions.

  - [NFA] is a nondeterministic finite automaton with epsilon transitions. It
    supports epsilon-closure computation ([NFA.EpsilonClosure]), simulation,
    epsilon elimination, reversal and conversion to a [DFA] by the powerset
    construction ([SubsetConstruction]).

  - Regular expressions are parsed by [ParseRegex] into a [RegexNode] syntax
    tree and compiled to automata by Thompson's construction ([Thompson]).
    [Compile] returns a DFA-backed [Regexp] for fast repeated matching.

  - DFA minimisation is available through both Hopcroft's ([Hopcroft]) and
    Moore's ([Moore]) partition-refinement algorithms, which serve as mutual
    cross-checks and expose the Myhill–Nerode equivalence classes.

  - Boolean combinations of regular languages are built with the product
    construction: [Union], [Intersection], [Difference] and
    [SymmetricDifference]. Language equivalence ([Equivalent]), containment
    ([Subset]) and disjointness ([Disjoint]) are decided by reducing to
    emptiness, and [Witness] returns a shortest distinguishing string.

  - Language operations on NFAs include [Concatenate], [UnionNFA], [StarNFA],
    [PlusNFA], [OptionalNFA], [PowerNFA] and [ReverseNFA].

  - The pumping lemma is made constructive by [PumpingLength] and
    [PumpingDecomposition], which return a valid pumping length and an
    explicit x·y·z factorisation of any sufficiently long accepted word.

  - Beyond regular languages, [PDA] simulates nondeterministic pushdown
    automata (accepting by final state or by empty stack) and [TM] simulates
    deterministic single-tape Turing machines, both with bounded-resource
    guarantees against non-termination.

States are identified by the integers 0..NumStates-1; input, tape and stack
symbols are runes, so the machines operate naturally on Go strings. All
constructions are deterministic and free of global mutable state, and no
algorithm depends on any package outside the Go standard library.

types.name: AcceptMode
types.signature: type AcceptMode int
types.doc: AcceptMode selects a pushdown automaton's acceptance condition.

types.name: Alternate
types.signature: type Alternate struct{ L, R RegexNode }
types.doc: Alternate denotes the alternation (union) of two sub-expressions L or R.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders an alternation with parentheses.

types.name: AnyChar
types.signature: type AnyChar struct{}
types.doc: AnyChar denotes the wildcard "." matching any single alphabet symbol.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the wildcard as ".".

types.name: Concat
types.signature: type Concat struct{ L, R RegexNode }
types.doc: Concat denotes the concatenation of two sub-expressions L then R.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders a concatenation.

types.name: DFA
types.doc:
DFA is a deterministic finite automaton over an alphabet of runes. States are
identified by the integers 0..NumStates-1. The transition function may be
partial: a symbol with no outgoing edge from a state is treated as a
transition to an implicit non-accepting dead state, so that a run simply
rejects. Use Complete to materialise the dead state explicitly when a total
transition function is required.

funcs.name: Difference
funcs.doc: Difference returns a DFA accepting L(a) \ L(b).

funcs.name: Hopcroft
funcs.doc:
Hopcroft returns the minimal DFA equivalent to d using Hopcroft's partition
refinement algorithm, which runs in O(n·|Σ|·log n) time. Unreachable states
are removed before minimisation.

funcs.name: Intersection
funcs.doc: Intersection returns a DFA accepting L(a) ∩ L(b).

funcs.name: Moore
funcs.doc:
Moore returns the minimal DFA equivalent to d using Moore's classic O(n²·|Σ|)
partition-refinement algorithm. It provides an independent cross-check of the
faster Hopcroft implementation.

funcs.name: NFAToDFA
funcs.doc: NFAToDFA is a package-level convenience wrapper around SubsetConstruction.

funcs.name: NewDFA
funcs.signature: func NewDFA(numStates int, alphabet []rune, start int) *DFA
funcs.doc:
NewDFA constructs a DFA with the given number of states, alphabet and start
state, and no transitions or accepting states.

funcs.name: Product
funcs.signature: func Product(a, b *DFA, accept func(aAcc, bAcc bool) bool) *DFA
funcs.doc:
Product builds the synchronous product of two DFAs over the combined
alphabet. A product state is accepting exactly when accept(aAcc, bAcc) is
true for the accepting status of its two components, allowing union,
intersection, difference and symmetric difference to be expressed uniformly.
Both inputs are completed over the shared alphabet before the product is
formed, and the result is trimmed of unreachable states.

funcs.name: RegexToDFA
funcs.doc:
RegexToDFA parses pattern, builds an NFA and determinises it to a minimal
DFA.

funcs.name: ReverseDFA
funcs.doc:
ReverseDFA returns a DFA accepting the reversal of L(d), obtained by reversing
the automaton and determinising.

funcs.name: SubsetConstruction
funcs.doc:
SubsetConstruction converts an NFA (possibly with epsilon transitions) into
an equivalent DFA using the classic powerset construction. Each DFA state
corresponds to an epsilon-closed set of NFA states reachable from the start.
Only subsets actually reachable during the construction are materialised.

funcs.name: SymmetricDifference
funcs.doc:
SymmetricDifference returns a DFA accepting the symmetric difference
(L(a) \ L(b)) ∪ (L(b) \ L(a)), i.e. strings accepted by exactly one of a, b.

funcs.name: Union
funcs.doc: Union returns a DFA accepting L(a) ∪ L(b).

methods.name: AcceptedWords
methods.doc:
AcceptedWords returns all accepted strings of length at most maxLen, in
shortlex order (shorter first, ties broken by symbol order).

methods.name: AcceptingStates
methods.doc: AcceptingStates returns the accepting states in ascending order.

methods.name: Accepts
methods.doc: Accepts reports whether the DFA accepts the input string.

methods.name: AcceptsAll
methods.doc:
AcceptsAll reports whether the DFA accepts every string over its alphabet,
i.e. its complement is empty.

methods.name: AddAccept
methods.doc: AddAccept marks each of the given states as accepting.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns a deep copy of the DFA.

methods.name: Complement
methods.doc:
Complement returns a DFA accepting exactly the strings over the alphabet that
this DFA rejects. The automaton is completed first so that stuck runs become
accepting runs in the complement.

methods.name: Complete
methods.doc:
Complete returns an equivalent DFA whose transition function is total. If the
original is already complete a clone is returned; otherwise a single dead
(non-accepting, self-looping) state is added to absorb missing transitions.

methods.name: Concatenate
methods.doc: Concatenate returns a DFA accepting L(d)·L(other).

methods.name: CountAcceptedWords
methods.doc:
CountAcceptedWords returns the exact number of accepted strings of the given
length as an arbitrary-precision integer, using dynamic programming over path
counts.

methods.name: Difference
methods.doc: Difference returns a DFA accepting the set difference L(d) \ L(other).

methods.name: EquivalenceClasses
methods.doc:
EquivalenceClasses returns the blocks of the Myhill–Nerode equivalence over
the reachable, completed DFA: each block is a set of mutually
indistinguishable states (indices refer to the completed automaton).

methods.name: Intersection
methods.doc: Intersection returns a DFA accepting the intersection of the two languages.

methods.name: IsAccepting
methods.doc: IsAccepting reports whether the given state is accepting.

methods.name: IsComplete
methods.doc:
IsComplete reports whether the transition function is total, i.e. every state
has an outgoing edge for every alphabet symbol.

methods.name: IsEmptyLanguage
methods.doc: IsEmptyLanguage reports whether the DFA accepts no strings at all.

methods.name: IsEquivalentTo
methods.doc: IsEquivalentTo reports whether this DFA is language-equivalent to other.

methods.name: IsFiniteLanguage
methods.doc:
IsFiniteLanguage reports whether the language accepted by the DFA is finite.
The language is infinite exactly when a cycle exists among states that are
both reachable and productive.

methods.name: IsSubsetOf
methods.doc: IsSubsetOf reports whether this DFA's language is contained in other's.

methods.name: IsUniversal
methods.doc: IsUniversal is an alias for AcceptsAll.

methods.name: Minimize
methods.doc:
Minimize returns the unique (up to renumbering) minimal DFA equivalent to the
receiver. It first trims unreachable states, completes the transition
function, then merges indistinguishable states using Hopcroft's algorithm.

methods.name: MinimizeMoore
methods.doc: MinimizeMoore is a method form of Moore's minimisation algorithm.

methods.name: NumTransitions
methods.doc: NumTransitions returns the number of defined edges in the DFA.

methods.name: Plus
methods.doc: Plus returns a DFA accepting L(d)+.

methods.name: Productive
methods.doc:
Productive returns the set of states from which some accepting state is
reachable. Such states are also called live or co-reachable states.

methods.name: Reachable
methods.doc:
Reachable returns the set of states reachable from the start state by any
sequence of defined transitions.

methods.name: RemoveDead
methods.doc:
RemoveDead returns an equivalent DFA with all non-productive (dead) states
removed. The start state is always retained.

methods.name: RemoveUnreachable
methods.doc:
RemoveUnreachable returns an equivalent DFA with all states unreachable from
the start removed and the remaining states renumbered compactly.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.doc: Reverse returns a DFA accepting the reversal of this DFA's language.

methods.name: Run
methods.doc:
Run feeds input to the DFA starting from the start state and returns the
final state reached together with a boolean that is false if the run got
stuck on a missing transition.

methods.name: SetAccept
methods.doc:
SetAccept marks state as accepting when accepting is true, or non-accepting
when it is false.

methods.name: SetTransition
methods.signature: func (d *DFA) SetTransition(from int, sym rune, to int)
methods.doc:
SetTransition sets the edge from state from on symbol sym to state to. The
symbol is added to the alphabet if not already present.

methods.name: ShortestAccepted
methods.doc:
ShortestAccepted returns a shortest string accepted by the DFA and true, or
the empty string and false if the language is empty. When several shortest
words exist the lexicographically smallest by symbol order is returned.

methods.name: Star
methods.doc: Star returns a DFA accepting L(d)*.

methods.name: States
methods.doc: States returns all state indices in ascending order.

methods.name: Step
methods.signature: func (d *DFA) Step(from int, sym rune) (int, bool)
methods.doc: Step is an alias for Transition, advancing one symbol from a single state.

methods.name: Symbols
methods.doc: Symbols returns the alphabet in ascending order.

methods.name: SymmetricDifference
methods.doc:
SymmetricDifference returns a DFA accepting the symmetric difference of the
two languages.

methods.name: ToNFA
methods.doc: ToNFA returns an NFA that accepts the same language as the DFA.

methods.name: Transition
methods.signature: func (d *DFA) Transition(from int, sym rune) (int, bool)
methods.doc:
Transition returns the target state of the edge from state from on symbol
sym, and whether such an edge exists.

methods.name: Trim
methods.doc:
Trim returns an equivalent DFA containing only states that are both reachable
from the start and productive (able to reach an accepting state).

methods.name: Union
methods.doc: Union returns a DFA accepting the union of this DFA's language with other's.

methods.name: Validate
methods.doc:
Validate checks the structural well-formedness of the DFA: valid start state,
transition endpoints and accepting states all within range.

types.name: Direction
types.signature: type Direction int
types.doc:
Direction is the head-movement direction of a Turing machine or the tape
motion of a two-way automaton.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders a Direction as "L", "S" or "R".

types.name: EmptySet
types.signature: type EmptySet struct{}
types.doc: EmptySet denotes the regular expression matching no strings at all (∅).

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the empty set as the symbol ∅.

types.name: EmptyString
types.signature: type EmptyString struct{}
types.doc:
EmptyString denotes the regular expression matching only the empty string
(ε).

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the empty string as the symbol ε.

types.name: NFA
types.doc:
NFA is a nondeterministic finite automaton with epsilon transitions over an
alphabet of runes. States are the integers 0..NumStates-1. Symbol edges and
epsilon (empty-string) edges are stored separately.

funcs.name: Concatenate
funcs.doc:
Concatenate returns an NFA accepting the concatenation L(a)·L(b): every
string formed by an a-word followed by a b-word.

funcs.name: EmptyLanguageNFA
funcs.doc: EmptyLanguageNFA returns an NFA over alphabet accepting no strings.

funcs.name: EpsilonNFA
funcs.doc: EpsilonNFA returns an NFA over alphabet accepting only the empty string.

funcs.name: LiteralNFA
funcs.doc: LiteralNFA returns an NFA accepting exactly the string s.

funcs.name: NewNFA
funcs.signature: func NewNFA(numStates int, alphabet []rune, start int) *NFA
funcs.doc:
NewNFA constructs an NFA with the given number of states, alphabet and start
state, and no transitions or accepting states.

funcs.name: OptionalNFA
funcs.doc: OptionalNFA returns an NFA accepting L(a) ∪ {ε}.

funcs.name: PlusNFA
funcs.doc:
PlusNFA returns an NFA accepting L(a)+ = L(a)·L(a)*, i.e. one or more
repetitions.

funcs.name: PowerNFA
funcs.doc:
PowerNFA returns an NFA accepting exactly n consecutive copies of L(a). For
n = 0 it accepts only the empty string.

funcs.name: RegexToNFA
funcs.doc:
RegexToNFA parses pattern and returns an equivalent NFA via Thompson's
construction. The NFA's alphabet is the set of literal symbols in the
pattern; wildcards expand over that same set.

funcs.name: RegexToNFAWithAlphabet
funcs.doc:
RegexToNFAWithAlphabet is like RegexToNFA but additionally includes the given
alphabet symbols, which is required when the pattern uses '.' and the
intended alphabet is larger than the literals present.

funcs.name: ReverseNFA
funcs.doc: ReverseNFA returns an NFA accepting the reversal of L(a).

funcs.name: StarNFA
funcs.doc:
StarNFA returns an NFA accepting the Kleene star L(a)*, including the empty
string.

funcs.name: SymbolNFA
funcs.doc: SymbolNFA returns an NFA accepting exactly the single-character string sym.

funcs.name: Thompson
funcs.doc:
Thompson builds an NFA from a regex syntax tree using Thompson's
construction. Wildcards ('.') are expanded over alphabet together with any
literal symbols in the tree; if alphabet is nil only the literals are used.
The resulting NFA has exactly one accepting state and uses epsilon edges to
glue fragments together.

funcs.name: UnionNFA
funcs.doc:
UnionNFA returns an NFA accepting L(a) ∪ L(b) via a fresh start state with
epsilon edges into both operands.

methods.name: AcceptingStates
methods.doc: AcceptingStates returns the accepting states in ascending order.

methods.name: Accepts
methods.doc:
Accepts reports whether the NFA accepts input, i.e. whether some accepting
state is active after consuming it.

methods.name: AddAccept
methods.doc: AddAccept marks each of the given states as accepting.

methods.name: AddEpsilon
methods.doc: AddEpsilon adds an epsilon (empty-string) edge from state from to state to.

methods.name: AddTransition
methods.signature: func (n *NFA) AddTransition(from int, sym rune, to int)
methods.doc:
AddTransition adds an edge from state from on symbol sym to state to. The
symbol is added to the alphabet if not already present.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns a deep copy of the NFA.

methods.name: Concat
methods.doc: Concat is the method form of Concatenate.

methods.name: Determinize
methods.doc:
Determinize is an alias for ToDFA that additionally removes unreachable
states (the powerset construction already avoids them) and is provided for
readability at call sites.

methods.name: EpsilonClosure
methods.doc:
EpsilonClosure returns the set of states reachable from any state in the
input set using zero or more epsilon transitions.

methods.name: EpsilonClosureState
methods.doc: EpsilonClosureState returns the epsilon-closure of a single state.

methods.name: EpsilonSuccessors
methods.doc: EpsilonSuccessors returns the direct epsilon-successors of state q.

methods.name: HasEpsilon
methods.doc: HasEpsilon reports whether the NFA contains any epsilon transition.

methods.name: Move
methods.signature: func (n *NFA) Move(states StateSet, sym rune) StateSet
methods.doc:
Move returns the set of states reachable from the input set by a single
symbol edge on sym, without taking epsilon closures.

methods.name: NumEpsilon
methods.doc: NumEpsilon returns the number of epsilon edges.

methods.name: NumTransitions
methods.doc: NumTransitions returns the number of symbol edges (epsilon edges excluded).

methods.name: Optional
methods.doc: Optional is the method form of OptionalNFA.

methods.name: Plus
methods.doc: Plus is the method form of PlusNFA.

methods.name: Power
methods.doc: Power is the method form of PowerNFA.

methods.name: Reachable
methods.doc:
Reachable returns the set of states reachable from the start state using
symbol and epsilon transitions.

methods.name: RemoveEpsilon
methods.doc:
RemoveEpsilon returns an equivalent NFA without epsilon transitions. Each
state's epsilon-closure is folded into direct symbol edges, and a state
becomes accepting when its closure contains an accepting state.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.doc:
Reverse returns an NFA accepting the reversal of this NFA's language: every
edge is reversed, a fresh start state epsilon-connects to all former
accepting states, and the former start state becomes the sole accepting
state.

methods.name: Run
methods.doc:
Run simulates the NFA on input and returns the set of active states after the
whole string has been consumed.

methods.name: SetAccept
methods.doc: SetAccept marks state as accepting when accepting is true, else clears it.

methods.name: Star
methods.doc: Star is the method form of StarNFA.

methods.name: StartSet
methods.doc:
StartSet returns the epsilon-closure of the start state, i.e. the set of
states active before any input is read.

methods.name: States
methods.doc: States returns all state indices in ascending order.

methods.name: Step
methods.signature: func (n *NFA) Step(states StateSet, sym rune) StateSet
methods.doc:
Step advances the NFA one symbol from the current set of states, applying the
epsilon closure after the symbol move. The input set is assumed already
epsilon-closed.

methods.name: Symbols
methods.doc: Symbols returns the alphabet in ascending order.

methods.name: Targets
methods.doc:
Targets returns the set of states reachable from state from on symbol sym in
one step (not including epsilon moves).

methods.name: ToDFA
methods.doc: ToDFA converts the NFA to an equivalent DFA via subset construction.

methods.name: Union
methods.doc: Union is the method form of UnionNFA.

methods.name: Validate
methods.doc: Validate checks the structural well-formedness of the NFA.

types.name: Optional
types.signature: type Optional struct{ X RegexNode }
types.doc: Optional denotes zero-or-one occurrence of a sub-expression.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders an optional, parenthesising compound operands.

types.name: PDA
types.doc:
PDA is a nondeterministic pushdown automaton with epsilon moves over rune
input and rune stack alphabets.

funcs.name: NewPDA
funcs.signature: func NewPDA(start string, initialStack rune, mode AcceptMode) *PDA
funcs.doc:
NewPDA constructs a pushdown automaton with the given start state, initial
stack symbol and acceptance mode.

methods.name: Accepts
methods.doc:
Accepts reports whether the PDA accepts input under its acceptance mode. The
search explores the configuration graph breadth-first with a visited set and
a step budget (PDA.MaxSteps or a default derived from the input length) to
ensure termination.

methods.name: AddAccept
methods.doc: AddAccept marks each of the given states as accepting.

methods.name: AddEpsilonTransition
methods.signature: func (p *PDA) AddEpsilonTransition(state string, top rune, next string, push ...rune)
methods.doc:
AddEpsilonTransition adds an epsilon move (consuming no input) from state,
popping top, entering next and pushing the given symbols.

methods.name: AddTransition
methods.signature: func (p *PDA) AddTransition(state string, in rune, top rune, next string, push ...rune)
methods.doc:
AddTransition adds a move on input symbol in (popping top): from state, read
in and pop top, go to next and push the given symbols.

types.name: PDATransition
types.doc:
PDATransition is one nondeterministic move of a pushdown automaton: enter
NextState and replace the popped stack top with the symbols in Push, where
Push[0] becomes the new top. An empty Push pops without pushing.

types.name: Plus
types.signature: type Plus struct{ X RegexNode }
types.doc: Plus denotes one-or-more repetitions of a sub-expression.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders a plus, parenthesising compound operands.

types.name: RegexNode
types.doc:
RegexNode is a node in a parsed regular-expression syntax tree. The concrete
implementations are EmptySet, EmptyString, Symbol, Concat, Alternate, Star,
Plus, Optional and AnyChar.

funcs.name: Alt
funcs.doc:
Alt builds the left-associative alternation of the given nodes. With no
arguments it returns ∅; with one it returns that node unchanged.

funcs.name: DFAToRegex
funcs.doc:
DFAToRegex converts a DFA into an equivalent regular expression using the
state-elimination method. Fresh start and final states are added, then the
original states are eliminated one by one, accumulating regex labels on the
remaining edges. The returned node denotes exactly the DFA's language; it is
EmptySet when the language is empty.

funcs.name: Dot
funcs.doc: Dot builds an AnyChar (.) wildcard node.

funcs.name: Empty
funcs.doc: Empty builds an EmptySet (∅) node.

funcs.name: Eps
funcs.doc: Eps builds an EmptyString (ε) node.

funcs.name: Kleene
funcs.doc: Kleene builds a Star node over x.

funcs.name: Lit
funcs.doc: Lit builds a Symbol node matching the single rune r.

funcs.name: LiteralNode
funcs.doc:
LiteralNode builds a syntax tree matching exactly the string s (a
concatenation of its runes; ε when s is empty).

funcs.name: MustParseRegex
funcs.doc:
MustParseRegex is like ParseRegex but panics if the pattern is invalid. It is
intended for package-level expressions and tests.

funcs.name: NFAToRegex
funcs.doc:
NFAToRegex converts an NFA to an equivalent regular expression by first
determinising it and then applying state elimination.

funcs.name: OneOrMore
funcs.doc: OneOrMore builds a Plus node over x.

funcs.name: ParseRegex
funcs.doc:
ParseRegex parses a regular expression written in the supported syntax and
returns its syntax tree. Supported constructs are concatenation, alternation
with '|', Kleene star '*', plus '+', optional '?', grouping with parentheses,
the wildcard '.', and backslash escaping of metacharacters. An empty pattern
parses to ε.

funcs.name: Seq
funcs.doc:
Seq builds the left-associative concatenation of the given nodes. With no
arguments it returns ε; with one it returns that node unchanged.

funcs.name: ZeroOrOne
funcs.doc: ZeroOrOne builds an Optional node over x.

types.name: Regexp
types.doc:
Regexp is a compiled regular expression backed by a DFA, offering fast
repeated matching.

funcs.name: Compile
funcs.doc:
Compile parses pattern and returns a compiled Regexp whose alphabet is the
set of literal symbols in the pattern.

funcs.name: CompileWithAlphabet
funcs.doc:
CompileWithAlphabet parses pattern using the supplied alphabet for wildcard
expansion and returns a compiled Regexp.

funcs.name: MustCompile
funcs.doc: MustCompile is like Compile but panics on a malformed pattern.

methods.name: DFA
methods.doc:
DFA returns the underlying deterministic automaton (a clone, so callers may
mutate it freely).

methods.name: Matches
methods.doc:
Matches reports whether the whole input string is matched by the regular
expression.

methods.name: Pattern
methods.doc: Pattern returns the source pattern the Regexp was compiled from.

types.name: Star
types.signature: type Star struct{ X RegexNode }
types.doc: Star denotes the Kleene star of a sub-expression (zero or more repetitions).

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders a Kleene star, parenthesising compound operands.

types.name: StateSet
types.signature: type StateSet map[int]bool
types.doc:
StateSet is a set of automaton states represented as a map from state index
to presence. It is used throughout the package for subset construction,
epsilon-closure computation and nondeterministic simulation.

funcs.name: NewStateSet
funcs.doc: NewStateSet returns a StateSet containing exactly the given states.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add inserts state q into the set and returns the set for chaining.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent copy of the set.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc: Contains reports whether state q is a member of the set.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether two StateSets contain exactly the same states.

methods.name: Intersect
methods.doc: Intersect returns a new StateSet containing states present in both operands.

methods.name: IsEmpty
methods.doc: IsEmpty reports whether the set contains no states.

methods.name: Key
methods.doc:
Key returns a canonical string key for the set, suitable for use as a map
index. Two StateSets have the same Key if and only if they contain the same
states.

methods.name: Len
methods.doc: Len returns the number of states in the set.

methods.name: Sorted
methods.doc: Sorted returns the members of the set as an ascending slice of ints.

methods.name: Union
methods.doc: Union returns a new StateSet containing every state in either operand.

types.name: Symbol
types.signature: type Symbol struct{ R rune }
types.doc: Symbol denotes a regular expression matching the single character R.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders a single symbol, escaping regex metacharacters.

types.name: TM
types.doc:
TM is a deterministic single-tape Turing machine. The tape is unbounded in
both directions and filled with the Blank symbol outside the written region.

funcs.name: NewTM
funcs.signature: func NewTM(start, accept, reject string, blank rune) *TM
funcs.doc:
NewTM constructs a Turing machine with the given start, accept and reject
states and blank symbol.

methods.name: Accepts
methods.signature: func (m *TM) Accepts(input string, maxSteps int) (bool, error)
methods.doc:
Accepts reports whether the machine accepts input within maxSteps transitions
and returns an error if the step budget was exhausted before halting.

methods.name: Compute
methods.signature: func (m *TM) Compute(input string, maxSteps int) (string, error)
methods.doc:
Compute runs the machine on input and returns the trimmed tape contents when
it halts in the accept state, modelling a transducer. It errors if the machine
rejects or fails to halt within maxSteps.

methods.name: InitialConfig
methods.doc:
InitialConfig returns the starting configuration for the given input, with the
head at the leftmost input cell.

methods.name: Rule
methods.signature: func (m *TM) Rule(state string, read rune) (TMRule, bool)
methods.doc: Rule returns the transition for (state, read) and whether one is defined.

methods.name: Run
methods.signature: func (m *TM) Run(input string, maxSteps int) TMResult
methods.doc:
Run simulates the machine on input for up to maxSteps transitions and reports
the outcome. A non-positive maxSteps means run until the machine halts, which
may not terminate for some machines and inputs.

methods.name: SetRule
methods.signature: func (m *TM) SetRule(state string, read rune, next string, write rune, move Direction)
methods.doc: SetRule installs the transition δ(state, read) = (next, write, move).

methods.name: Step
methods.doc:
Step applies one transition to c and returns the successor configuration and
true, or c unchanged and false if the machine has halted (no applicable rule
or an accept/reject state).

types.name: TMConfig
types.doc:
TMConfig is an instantaneous description of a Turing-machine computation: the
current state, the tape contents and the head position.

methods.name: Read
methods.doc:
Read returns the symbol under the head, extending the tape view with blanks
as needed (without mutating the configuration).

methods.name: TapeString
methods.doc:
TapeString returns the tape contents as a string with leading and trailing
blank symbols trimmed.

types.name: TMResult
types.doc: TMResult reports the outcome of running a Turing machine.

types.name: TMRule
types.doc:
TMRule is the action of a deterministic Turing-machine transition: the state
to enter, the symbol to write, and the direction to move the head.

funcs.name: CollectAlphabet
funcs.doc:
CollectAlphabet returns the sorted set of literal symbols appearing in the
syntax tree (wildcards contribute nothing).

funcs.name: DFAToRegexString
funcs.doc:
DFAToRegexString returns the state-elimination regular expression for d
rendered as a string.

funcs.name: Disjoint
funcs.doc: Disjoint reports whether two DFAs accept no common string.

funcs.name: Equivalent
funcs.doc:
Equivalent reports whether two DFAs accept exactly the same language. It works
by checking that their symmetric difference accepts nothing.

funcs.name: Match
funcs.doc:
Match is a one-shot helper that reports whether pattern matches the entire
input string.

funcs.name: NFAEquivalent
funcs.doc:
NFAEquivalent reports whether two NFAs accept the same language, by
determinising both and comparing the resulting DFAs.

funcs.name: PumpingDecomposition
funcs.signature: func PumpingDecomposition(d *DFA) func(w string) (x, y, z string, ok bool)
funcs.doc:
PumpingDecomposition splits an accepted string w of length at least
PumpingLength into parts x, y, z with w = xyz, |xy| ≤ p, |y| ≥ 1, such that
x·yⁱ·z is accepted for every i ≥ 0. It returns the three parts and true on
success, or empty strings and false if w is too short or is not accepted.

funcs.name: PumpingLength
funcs.doc:
PumpingLength returns a valid pumping length for the DFA's language: the
number of states of the trimmed, minimal automaton. Every accepted string of
at least this length admits a pumping decomposition.

funcs.name: Subset
funcs.doc:
Subset reports whether L(a) is a subset of L(b), i.e. every string accepted by
a is also accepted by b. This holds exactly when L(a) \ L(b) is empty.

funcs.name: Witness
funcs.doc:
Witness returns a shortest string on which two DFAs disagree (accepted by
exactly one of them) together with true, or the empty string and false if the
DFAs are equivalent.

examples.name: DFA (CountAcceptedWords)
examples.code: fmt.Println(evenA().CountAcceptedWords(3))

examples.name: NFA (ToDFA)
examples.code:
n := NewNFA(3, []rune{'a', 'b'}, 0)
n.AddTransition(0, 'a', 0)
n.AddTransition(0, 'b', 0)
n.AddTransition(0, 'a', 1)
n.AddTransition(1, 'b', 2)
n.AddAccept(2)
d := n.ToDFA().Minimize()
fmt.Println(d.Accepts("aab"), d.Accepts("abb"))

examples.name: Compile
examples.code:
re := MustCompile("a(b|c)*")
fmt.Println(re.Matches("abcbc"))
fmt.Println(re.Matches("a"))
fmt.Println(re.Matches("b"))

packages.name: bayesian
packages.synopsis: Package bayesian is a dependency-free toolkit for Bayesian inference written entirely with the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package bayesian is a dependency-free toolkit for Bayesian inference written
entirely with the Go standard library.

It brings together the workhorses of applied Bayesian statistics behind a
small, value-oriented API. Nothing samples or requires random numbers; every
routine is deterministic and analytic (or, where a closed form does not
exist, evaluated by a well-conditioned numerical method).

# Distributions

The package provides the continuous conjugate families [Beta], [Gamma],
[InverseGamma], [Normal] and the location-scale [StudentT], the multivariate
[Dirichlet], and the discrete [Poisson], [Binomial], [BetaBinomial] and
[NegativeBinomial] laws. Each distribution offers the usual density/mass,
cumulative, quantile, mean, variance and (where meaningful) entropy methods.

# Conjugate updates

Closed-form posterior updates are provided for the standard conjugate models:
Beta-Bernoulli/Binomial ([BetaBernoulliPosterior], [BetaBinomialPosterior]),
Gamma-Poisson ([GammaPoissonPosterior]), Normal-Normal with known variance
([NormalKnownVariancePosterior]), the Normal-Inverse-Gamma model for unknown
mean and variance ([NormalInverseGammaPosterior]), Inverse-Gamma updates for
a variance with known mean ([InverseGammaVariancePosterior]), and
Dirichlet-Multinomial ([DirichletMultinomialPosterior]).

# Predictions and summaries

Posterior predictive distributions ([BetaBinomialPredictive],
[GammaPoissonPredictive], [NormalNormalPredictive] and the Student-t
predictive of the Normal-Inverse-Gamma model), equal-tailed and
highest-density credible intervals ([EqualTailedInterval],
[HighestDensityInterval]), and tail/interval probabilities round out the
summary tooling.

# Model comparison

Log marginal likelihoods are available for each conjugate model
(for example [LogMarginalLikelihoodBetaBinomial]), together with
[BayesFactor], [PosteriorOdds], [PosteriorModelProbabilities] and a
qualitative [BayesFactorInterpretation].

# Classifiers and graphical models

The package includes Gaussian, multinomial and Bernoulli naive-Bayes
classifiers ([GaussianNB], [MultinomialNB], [BernoulliNB]) and a discrete
Bayesian network ([BayesianNetwork]) supporting joint, marginal and
conditional inference by variable elimination over its [Factor] potentials.

# Special functions

Underlying everything is a small set of exported special functions —
[LogGamma], [LogBeta], [Digamma], [Trigamma], [RegularizedIncompleteBeta],
[RegularizedGammaP] and their inverses — that may be useful on their own.

vars.signature: var ErrFactor = errors.New("bayesian: invalid factor")
vars.doc:
ErrFactor is returned for malformed factor operations, such as a table whose
length does not match the product of the variable cardinalities.

vars.signature: var ErrNetwork = errors.New("bayesian: invalid network operation")
vars.doc:
ErrNetwork is returned for malformed Bayesian-network definitions and
queries.

vars.signature: var ErrNotFitted = errors.New("bayesian: classifier has not been fitted")
vars.doc: ErrNotFitted is returned by classifier prediction methods called before Fit.

vars.signature: var ErrParam = errors.New("bayesian: invalid distribution parameter")
vars.doc:
ErrParam is returned by constructors and updates when a distribution
parameter is outside its valid domain.

vars.signature: var ErrShape = errors.New("bayesian: inconsistent input shape")
vars.doc:
ErrShape is returned when input matrices and label vectors have inconsistent
or empty shapes.

types.name: BayesianNetwork
types.doc:
BayesianNetwork is a discrete directed graphical model. Each variable has a
finite cardinality and a conditional probability table (CPT) given its
parents. The joint distribution factorizes as the product of the CPTs.

funcs.name: NewBayesianNetwork
funcs.doc: NewBayesianNetwork returns an empty Bayesian network.

methods.name: AddVariable
methods.signature: func (bn *BayesianNetwork) AddVariable(name string, card int) error
methods.doc:
AddVariable declares a categorical variable with the given cardinality. It
returns ErrNetwork if the variable already exists or the cardinality is
non-positive.

methods.name: CPT
methods.doc:
CPT returns the conditional probability factor for name and reports whether
it has been set.

methods.name: Cardinality
methods.doc: Cardinality returns the cardinality of the named variable, or 0 if unknown.

methods.name: Conditional
methods.doc:
Conditional returns the posterior distribution over the query variables given
the evidence, as a normalized Factor.

methods.name: ConditionalProbability
methods.signature: func (bn *BayesianNetwork) ConditionalProbability(variable string, value int, evidence map[string]int) (float64, error)
methods.doc:
ConditionalProbability returns P(variable = value | evidence), a scalar
posterior for a single variable and category.

methods.name: EvidenceProbability
methods.doc:
EvidenceProbability returns P(evidence), the probability of the observed
assignment obtained by summing the joint distribution consistent with it.

methods.name: JointProbability
methods.doc:
JointProbability returns P(assignment) for a full assignment of every
variable, the product of each CPT evaluated at the assignment. It returns 0
if any variable is unassigned or out of range.

methods.name: Marginal
methods.doc:
Marginal returns the marginal distribution over the query variables as a
normalized Factor.

methods.name: MarginalProbability
methods.signature: func (bn *BayesianNetwork) MarginalProbability(variable string, value int) (float64, error)
methods.doc:
MarginalProbability returns P(variable = value), a scalar marginal for a
single variable and category.

methods.name: Parents
methods.doc: Parents returns the parent variables of name in the order they were declared.

methods.name: SetCPT
methods.signature: func (bn *BayesianNetwork) SetCPT(name string, parents []string, table []float64) error
methods.doc:
SetCPT installs the conditional probability table P(name | parents). The
table is laid out in row-major order with the parents most significant (in
the order given) and the child variable least significant, so its length must
equal card(name)·Πcard(parents). Each parent configuration must define a
proper distribution over the child. It returns ErrNetwork on any shape or
declaration error.

methods.name: Variables
methods.doc: Variables returns the network's variable names in declaration order.

types.name: BernoulliNB

funcs.name: NewBernoulliNB
funcs.doc:
NewBernoulliNB returns an unfitted Bernoulli naive-Bayes classifier. Features
with value greater than threshold are treated as present (1); alpha is the
additive smoothing parameter (replaced by 1 if non-positive).

methods.name: Classes
methods.doc: Classes returns the sorted class labels learned by Fit.

methods.name: Fit
methods.doc: Fit estimates class priors and smoothed feature-presence probabilities.

methods.name: JointLogLikelihood
methods.doc:
JointLogLikelihood returns the unnormalized log joint probabilities for the
binary feature vector x under each class.

methods.name: Predict
methods.doc: Predict returns the most probable class label for x.

methods.name: PredictProba
methods.doc: PredictProba returns the posterior class probabilities for x.

types.name: Beta
types.doc:
Beta is a Beta distribution with shape parameters Alpha, Beta > 0. It is the
conjugate prior for the success probability of Bernoulli and Binomial
sampling and for the rate of a geometric/negative-binomial model.

funcs.name: BetaBernoulliPosterior
funcs.signature: func BetaBernoulliPosterior(prior Beta, successes, failures int) Beta
funcs.doc:
BetaBernoulliPosterior returns the posterior Beta(α+successes, β+failures)
after observing the given counts of Bernoulli successes and failures.

funcs.name: BetaBernoulliSequentialPosterior
funcs.doc:
BetaBernoulliSequentialPosterior applies a sequence of Bernoulli outcomes
(true = success) to the prior and returns the resulting posterior.

funcs.name: BetaBinomialPosterior
funcs.signature: func BetaBinomialPosterior(prior Beta, k, n int) Beta
funcs.doc:
BetaBinomialPosterior returns the posterior after observing k successes in n
Binomial trials. It is identical to a Beta-Bernoulli update with k successes
and n−k failures.

funcs.name: HaldaneBetaPrior
funcs.doc:
HaldaneBetaPrior returns Beta(ε,ε) with a tiny ε, an approximation of the
improper Haldane prior Beta(0,0) whose posterior mean equals the sample
proportion.

funcs.name: JeffreysBetaPrior
funcs.doc:
JeffreysBetaPrior returns Beta(½,½), the Jeffreys prior for a Bernoulli
success probability.

funcs.name: NewBeta
funcs.doc:
NewBeta constructs a Beta distribution, returning ErrParam if either shape is
non-positive.

funcs.name: UniformBetaPrior
funcs.doc: UniformBetaPrior returns Beta(1,1), the uniform prior on a probability.

methods.name: CDF
methods.doc: CDF returns the cumulative distribution function P(X ≤ x).

methods.name: Entropy
methods.doc: Entropy returns the differential entropy of the distribution in nats.

methods.name: LogPDF
methods.doc:
LogPDF returns the natural logarithm of the density at x in (0,1). It returns
math.Inf(-1) outside the open unit interval.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns the expected value α/(α+β).

methods.name: MeanLog
methods.doc: MeanLog returns E[ln X] = ψ(α) − ψ(α+β).

methods.name: Mode
methods.doc:
Mode returns the mode (α−1)/(α+β−2). It is defined only when α,β > 1; for
other shapes it returns NaN because the mode lies at a boundary or is not
unique.

methods.name: PDF
methods.doc: PDF returns the probability density at x in [0,1].

methods.name: Quantile
methods.doc: Quantile returns the p-quantile (inverse CDF) for p in [0,1].

methods.name: Skewness
methods.doc: Skewness returns the distribution's skewness.

methods.name: StdDev
methods.doc: StdDev returns the standard deviation, the square root of the variance.

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc: Variance returns αβ/((α+β)²(α+β+1)).

types.name: BetaBinomial
types.doc:
BetaBinomial is the Beta-Binomial distribution: the number of successes in N
trials where the success probability is drawn from Beta(Alpha, Beta). It is
the posterior predictive of a Beta-Bernoulli model.

funcs.name: BetaBinomialPredictive
funcs.signature: func BetaBinomialPredictive(post Beta, m int) BetaBinomial
funcs.doc:
BetaBinomialPredictive returns the posterior predictive distribution for the
number of successes in m future Binomial trials, given a Beta posterior.

funcs.name: NewBetaBinomial
funcs.signature: func NewBetaBinomial(n int, alpha, beta float64) (BetaBinomial, error)
funcs.doc:
NewBetaBinomial constructs a Beta-Binomial distribution, returning ErrParam
for invalid parameters.

methods.name: CDF
methods.doc: CDF returns P(X ≤ k) computed by summation over the support.

methods.name: LogPMF
methods.doc: LogPMF returns the natural logarithm of the mass at k.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns N·α/(α+β).

methods.name: PMF
methods.doc: PMF returns the probability mass P(X = k) for k in [0,N].

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc: Variance returns the variance of the Beta-Binomial distribution.

types.name: Binomial
types.doc:
Binomial is a Binomial distribution with N ≥ 0 trials and success
probability P in [0,1].

funcs.name: NewBinomial
funcs.signature: func NewBinomial(n int, p float64) (Binomial, error)
funcs.doc:
NewBinomial constructs a Binomial distribution, returning ErrParam for an
out-of-range probability or negative N.

methods.name: CDF
methods.doc: CDF returns P(X ≤ k).

methods.name: LogPMF
methods.doc: LogPMF returns the natural logarithm of the mass at k.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns N·P.

methods.name: PMF
methods.doc: PMF returns the probability mass P(X = k).

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc: Variance returns N·P·(1−P).

types.name: Dirichlet
types.doc:
Dirichlet is a Dirichlet distribution over the probability simplex with
concentration vector Alpha, all entries strictly positive. It is the
conjugate prior for the category probabilities of a Categorical/Multinomial
likelihood.

funcs.name: DirichletCategoricalPosterior
funcs.doc:
DirichletCategoricalPosterior updates a Dirichlet prior with a sequence of
observed category indices in [0,K) and returns the posterior.

funcs.name: DirichletMultinomialPosterior
funcs.doc:
DirichletMultinomialPosterior returns the posterior Dirichlet after observing
category counts. The counts slice must have the same length as the prior
concentration vector; a length mismatch returns the prior unchanged.

funcs.name: NewDirichlet
funcs.doc:
NewDirichlet constructs a Dirichlet distribution from a concentration vector,
returning ErrParam if it is empty or has a non-positive entry. The slice is
copied.

methods.name: Covariance
methods.doc:
Covariance returns the K×K covariance matrix of the distribution as a slice
of rows.

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc: Dim returns the number of categories.

methods.name: Entropy
methods.doc: Entropy returns the differential entropy of the distribution in nats.

methods.name: LogPDF
methods.doc:
LogPDF returns the natural logarithm of the density at the point x on the
simplex. It returns math.Inf(-1) when x has the wrong length, a non-positive
coordinate, or does not sum to one within a small tolerance.

methods.name: Marginal
methods.doc:
Marginal returns the marginal distribution of component i, which is
Beta(αᵢ, α₀−αᵢ).

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns the mean vector αᵢ/α₀.

methods.name: MeanLog
methods.doc: MeanLog returns the vector E[ln Xᵢ] = ψ(αᵢ) − ψ(α₀).

methods.name: Mode
methods.doc:
Mode returns the mode vector (αᵢ−1)/(α₀−K); it is defined only when every
αᵢ > 1, otherwise the returned slice is nil.

methods.name: PDF
methods.doc: PDF returns the density at the point x on the simplex.

methods.name: Sum
methods.doc: Sum returns the total concentration α₀ = Σαᵢ.

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc: Variance returns the per-component marginal variances.

types.name: Factor
types.doc:
Factor is a discrete potential over a set of named categorical variables. The
Table holds one non-negative value per joint assignment, laid out in
row-major order with the first variable most significant and the last least
significant.

funcs.name: NewFactor
funcs.doc:
NewFactor constructs a Factor from parallel variable-name and cardinality
slices and a value table. It returns ErrFactor when the shapes are
inconsistent.

methods.name: Get
methods.doc:
Get returns the factor value for a full or partial assignment given as a map
from variable name to category index. Every variable of the factor must be
present in assign; a missing variable yields 0.

methods.name: Marginalize
methods.doc:
Marginalize returns the factor obtained by summing out the named variable. If
the variable is not in scope the factor is returned unchanged.

methods.name: Multiply
methods.doc:
Multiply returns the factor product of a and b, whose scope is the union of
their variables. Shared variables are matched by name.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns a copy of the factor scaled so its entries sum to one. If
the total mass is zero the original factor is returned unchanged.

methods.name: Reduce
methods.doc:
Reduce returns the factor obtained by fixing the variables in evidence to the
given category indices and dropping them from scope. Variables not in scope
are ignored.

methods.name: Scope
methods.doc: Scope returns a sorted copy of the factor's variable names.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc: Size returns the number of entries in the factor's table.

methods.name: Sum
methods.doc: Sum returns the total mass of the factor.

types.name: Gamma
types.doc:
Gamma is a Gamma distribution with Shape > 0 and Rate > 0 (rate = 1/scale).
It is the conjugate prior for the rate of a Poisson or exponential model and
for the precision of a normal likelihood.

funcs.name: GammaExponentialPosterior
funcs.signature: func GammaExponentialPosterior(prior Gamma, sumX float64, n int) Gamma
funcs.doc:
GammaExponentialPosterior returns the posterior for the rate of an
exponential likelihood with a Gamma prior, after observing n samples whose
sum is sumX: Gamma(shape+n, rate+sumX).

funcs.name: GammaPoissonPosterior
funcs.signature: func GammaPoissonPosterior(prior Gamma, sumCounts, n int) Gamma
funcs.doc:
GammaPoissonPosterior returns the posterior Gamma(shape+Σcounts, rate+n)
after observing n Poisson counts whose total is sumCounts.

funcs.name: GammaPoissonPosteriorData
funcs.doc:
GammaPoissonPosteriorData returns the Gamma-Poisson posterior for a slice of
observed non-negative integer counts.

funcs.name: NewGamma
funcs.doc:
NewGamma constructs a Gamma distribution in the shape/rate parameterization,
returning ErrParam if either parameter is non-positive.

methods.name: CDF
methods.doc: CDF returns the cumulative distribution function P(X ≤ x).

methods.name: Entropy
methods.doc: Entropy returns the differential entropy in nats.

methods.name: LogPDF
methods.doc: LogPDF returns the natural logarithm of the density at x > 0.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns Shape/Rate.

methods.name: MeanLog
methods.doc: MeanLog returns E[ln X] = ψ(Shape) − ln(Rate).

methods.name: Mode
methods.doc: Mode returns (Shape−1)/Rate for Shape ≥ 1, and 0 otherwise.

methods.name: PDF
methods.doc: PDF returns the density at x ≥ 0.

methods.name: Quantile
methods.doc: Quantile returns the p-quantile for p in [0,1].

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the scale parameter 1/Rate.

methods.name: Skewness
methods.doc: Skewness returns 2/√Shape.

methods.name: StdDev
methods.doc: StdDev returns the standard deviation.

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc: Variance returns Shape/Rate².

types.name: GaussianNB
types.doc:
GaussianNB is a Gaussian naive-Bayes classifier: each continuous feature is
modeled as a class-conditional normal distribution with features assumed
conditionally independent given the class.

funcs.name: NewGaussianNB
funcs.doc: NewGaussianNB returns an unfitted Gaussian naive-Bayes classifier.

methods.name: Classes
methods.doc: Classes returns the sorted class labels learned by Fit.

methods.name: Fit
methods.doc:
Fit estimates class priors and per-feature class-conditional means and
variances from the training matrix X (rows are samples) and labels y. A small
variance floor proportional to the largest feature variance is added for
numerical stability.

methods.name: JointLogLikelihood
methods.doc:
JointLogLikelihood returns, for the sample x, the unnormalized log joint
probabilities log P(class) + log P(x | class) for each class.

methods.name: Predict
methods.doc: Predict returns the most probable class label for the sample x.

methods.name: PredictBatch
methods.doc: PredictBatch returns predictions for every row of X.

methods.name: PredictProba
methods.doc: PredictProba returns the posterior class probabilities for the sample x.

methods.name: Priors
methods.doc: Priors returns the estimated class prior probabilities aligned with Classes.

types.name: Interval
types.doc: Interval is a closed real interval [Lower, Upper].

funcs.name: BetaCredibleInterval
funcs.signature: func BetaCredibleInterval(d Beta, level float64) Interval
funcs.doc:
BetaCredibleInterval returns the equal-tailed credible interval of the given
mass for a Beta distribution.

funcs.name: BetaHDI
funcs.signature: func BetaHDI(d Beta, level float64) Interval
funcs.doc:
BetaHDI returns the highest-density credible interval of the given mass for a
Beta distribution.

funcs.name: EqualTailedInterval
funcs.signature: func EqualTailedInterval(d quantiler, level float64) Interval
funcs.doc:
EqualTailedInterval returns the equal-tailed credible interval of mass level
(for example 0.95) for any distribution with a Quantile method: it cuts
(1−level)/2 from each tail.

funcs.name: GammaCredibleInterval
funcs.signature: func GammaCredibleInterval(d Gamma, level float64) Interval
funcs.doc:
GammaCredibleInterval returns the equal-tailed credible interval of the given
mass for a Gamma distribution.

funcs.name: GammaHDI
funcs.signature: func GammaHDI(d Gamma, level float64) Interval
funcs.doc:
GammaHDI returns the highest-density credible interval of the given mass for
a Gamma distribution.

funcs.name: HighestDensityInterval
funcs.signature: func HighestDensityInterval(d densityQuantiler, level float64) Interval
funcs.doc:
HighestDensityInterval returns the highest-density credible interval of the
given mass for a unimodal continuous distribution exposing PDF and Quantile
methods. It searches over the lower-tail probability that minimizes the
interval width. For symmetric distributions the result matches the
equal-tailed interval.

funcs.name: InverseGammaCredibleInterval
funcs.signature: func InverseGammaCredibleInterval(d InverseGamma, level float64) Interval
funcs.doc:
InverseGammaCredibleInterval returns the equal-tailed credible interval of
the given mass for an Inverse-Gamma distribution.

funcs.name: NormalCredibleInterval
funcs.signature: func NormalCredibleInterval(d Normal, level float64) Interval
funcs.doc:
NormalCredibleInterval returns the equal-tailed credible interval of the
given mass for a Normal distribution.

funcs.name: StudentTCredibleInterval
funcs.signature: func StudentTCredibleInterval(d StudentT, level float64) Interval
funcs.doc:
StudentTCredibleInterval returns the equal-tailed credible interval of the
given mass for a Student-t distribution.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc: Contains reports whether x lies within the closed interval.

methods.name: Width
methods.doc: Width returns Upper − Lower.

types.name: InverseGamma
types.doc:
InverseGamma is an Inverse-Gamma distribution with Shape > 0 and Scale > 0.
If Y ~ Gamma(Shape, rate=Scale) then 1/Y ~ InverseGamma(Shape, Scale). It is
the conjugate prior for the variance of a normal likelihood.

funcs.name: InverseGammaVariancePosterior
funcs.signature: func InverseGammaVariancePosterior(prior InverseGamma, mu float64, data []float64) InverseGamma
funcs.doc:
InverseGammaVariancePosterior returns the posterior over the variance of a
normal likelihood with known mean mu, given an Inverse-Gamma prior and the
data. The update adds n/2 to the shape and ½Σ(xᵢ−mu)² to the scale.

funcs.name: NewInverseGamma
funcs.doc:
NewInverseGamma constructs an Inverse-Gamma distribution, returning ErrParam
for non-positive parameters.

methods.name: CDF
methods.doc:
CDF returns the cumulative distribution function P(X ≤ x). For an
Inverse-Gamma this equals Q(Shape, Scale/x), the upper regularized gamma.

methods.name: LogPDF
methods.doc: LogPDF returns the natural logarithm of the density at x > 0.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns Scale/(Shape−1) for Shape > 1, else +Inf.

methods.name: Mode
methods.doc: Mode returns Scale/(Shape+1).

methods.name: PDF
methods.doc: PDF returns the density at x > 0.

methods.name: Quantile
methods.doc: Quantile returns the p-quantile for p in [0,1].

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc: Variance returns the variance for Shape > 2, else +Inf.

types.name: MultinomialNB
types.doc:
MultinomialNB is a multinomial naive-Bayes classifier suitable for count
features such as word frequencies, using additive (Laplace/Lidstone)
smoothing controlled by Alpha.

funcs.name: NewMultinomialNB
funcs.doc:
NewMultinomialNB returns an unfitted multinomial naive-Bayes classifier with
the given additive smoothing parameter alpha (alpha = 1 is Laplace
smoothing). A non-positive alpha is replaced by 1.

methods.name: Classes
methods.doc: Classes returns the sorted class labels learned by Fit.

methods.name: Fit
methods.doc:
Fit estimates class priors and smoothed per-feature log probabilities from
the count matrix X and labels y.

methods.name: JointLogLikelihood
methods.doc:
JointLogLikelihood returns the unnormalized log joint probabilities for the
count vector x under each class.

methods.name: Predict
methods.doc: Predict returns the most probable class label for the count vector x.

methods.name: PredictProba
methods.doc: PredictProba returns the posterior class probabilities for the count vector x.

methods.name: Priors
methods.doc: Priors returns the estimated class prior probabilities aligned with Classes.

types.name: NegativeBinomial
types.doc:
NegativeBinomial is parameterized by a real number R > 0 of "failures" and a
success probability P in (0,1); the mass is over the number of counts k ≥ 0.
It is the posterior predictive of a Gamma-Poisson model, where R equals the
Gamma shape and P = Rate/(Rate+1).

funcs.name: GammaPoissonPredictive
funcs.doc:
GammaPoissonPredictive returns the posterior predictive distribution for the
count of a single future Poisson observation, a Negative-Binomial with R =
shape and P = rate/(rate+1).

funcs.name: GammaPoissonPredictiveExposure
funcs.signature: func GammaPoissonPredictiveExposure(post Gamma, t float64) NegativeBinomial
funcs.doc:
GammaPoissonPredictiveExposure returns the posterior predictive for the count
observed over a future exposure t, a Negative-Binomial with R = shape and
P = rate/(rate+t).

funcs.name: NewNegativeBinomial
funcs.doc:
NewNegativeBinomial constructs a Negative-Binomial distribution, returning
ErrParam for invalid parameters.

methods.name: CDF
methods.doc: CDF returns P(X ≤ k) via the regularized incomplete beta function.

methods.name: LogPMF
methods.doc:
LogPMF returns the natural logarithm of the mass at k ≥ 0 using the
generalized (real-valued R) binomial coefficient.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns R·(1−P)/P.

methods.name: PMF
methods.doc: PMF returns the probability mass P(X = k) for k ≥ 0.

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc: Variance returns R·(1−P)/P².

types.name: Normal
types.doc:
Normal is a univariate normal (Gaussian) distribution with mean Mu and
standard deviation Sigma > 0.

funcs.name: NewNormal
funcs.doc: NewNormal constructs a Normal distribution, returning ErrParam if Sigma ≤ 0.

funcs.name: NormalKnownVariancePosterior
funcs.signature: func NormalKnownVariancePosterior(prior Normal, sigma2 float64, n int, xbar float64) Normal
funcs.doc:
NormalKnownVariancePosterior returns the posterior over the mean of a normal
likelihood with known sampling variance sigma², given a normal prior and the
summary statistics n (sample size) and xbar (sample mean).

funcs.name: NormalKnownVariancePosteriorData
funcs.signature: func NormalKnownVariancePosteriorData(prior Normal, sigma2 float64, data []float64) Normal
funcs.doc:
NormalKnownVariancePosteriorData is like NormalKnownVariancePosterior but
takes the raw data slice and known sampling variance sigma².

funcs.name: NormalKnownVariancePredictive
funcs.signature: func NormalKnownVariancePredictive(post Normal, sigma2 float64) Normal
funcs.doc:
NormalKnownVariancePredictive returns the posterior predictive distribution
for a single new observation with known sampling variance sigma², given the
posterior over the mean. Its variance is the posterior variance plus sigma².

funcs.name: NormalNormalPredictive
funcs.signature: func NormalNormalPredictive(post Normal, sigma2 float64) Normal
funcs.doc:
NormalNormalPredictive returns the posterior predictive distribution for a
single new observation with known sampling variance sigma², given the
posterior over the mean. It is an alias for NormalKnownVariancePredictive.

methods.name: CDF
methods.doc: CDF returns the cumulative distribution function P(X ≤ x).

methods.name: Entropy
methods.doc: Entropy returns the differential entropy in nats.

methods.name: LogPDF
methods.doc: LogPDF returns the natural logarithm of the density at x.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns Mu.

methods.name: PDF
methods.doc: PDF returns the density at x.

methods.name: Quantile
methods.doc: Quantile returns the p-quantile for p in [0,1].

methods.name: StdDev
methods.doc: StdDev returns Sigma.

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc: Variance returns Sigma².

types.name: NormalInverseGamma
types.doc:
NormalInverseGamma is the conjugate prior for a normal likelihood with both
mean and variance unknown. Its hierarchy is σ² ~ InverseGamma(Alpha, Beta)
and μ | σ² ~ Normal(Mu, σ²/Kappa).

funcs.name: NewNormalInverseGamma
funcs.signature: func NewNormalInverseGamma(mu, kappa, alpha, beta float64) (NormalInverseGamma, error)
funcs.doc:
NewNormalInverseGamma constructs a Normal-Inverse-Gamma prior, returning
ErrParam if Kappa, Alpha or Beta is non-positive.

funcs.name: NormalInverseGammaPosterior
funcs.doc:
NormalInverseGammaPosterior updates a Normal-Inverse-Gamma prior with the
observed data and returns the posterior parameters.

methods.name: ExpectedVariance
methods.doc:
ExpectedVariance returns E[σ²] under the marginal posterior, Beta/(Alpha−1)
for Alpha > 1.

methods.name: MarginalMean
methods.doc:
MarginalMean returns the marginal posterior over the mean, a location-scale
Student-t with 2·Alpha degrees of freedom.

methods.name: MarginalVariance
methods.doc:
MarginalVariance returns the marginal posterior over the variance, an
Inverse-Gamma(Alpha, Beta) distribution.

methods.name: Predictive
