methods.doc:
Predictive returns the posterior predictive distribution for a single new
observation, a location-scale Student-t with 2·Alpha degrees of freedom.

types.name: Poisson
types.doc: Poisson is a Poisson distribution with rate Lambda ≥ 0.

funcs.name: NewPoisson
funcs.doc:
NewPoisson constructs a Poisson distribution, returning ErrParam for a
negative rate.

methods.name: CDF
methods.doc: CDF returns P(X ≤ k).

methods.name: LogPMF
methods.doc: LogPMF returns the natural logarithm of the mass at k ≥ 0.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns Lambda.

methods.name: PMF
methods.doc: PMF returns the probability mass P(X = k) for integer k ≥ 0.

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc: Variance returns Lambda.

types.name: StudentT
types.doc:
StudentT is a location-scale Student's t distribution with Nu > 0 degrees of
freedom, location Loc and scale Scale > 0. It arises as the posterior
predictive and the marginal for the mean in a normal model with unknown
variance.

funcs.name: NewStudentT
funcs.signature: func NewStudentT(nu, loc, scale float64) (StudentT, error)
funcs.doc:
NewStudentT constructs a location-scale Student-t distribution, returning
ErrParam for non-positive Nu or Scale.

methods.name: CDF
methods.doc: CDF returns the cumulative distribution function P(X ≤ x).

methods.name: LogPDF
methods.doc: LogPDF returns the natural logarithm of the density at x.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns Loc for Nu > 1, else NaN.

methods.name: PDF
methods.doc: PDF returns the density at x.

methods.name: Quantile
methods.doc: Quantile returns the p-quantile for p in [0,1].

methods.name: StdDev
methods.doc: StdDev returns the standard deviation.

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc: Variance returns the variance for Nu > 2, +Inf for 1 < Nu ≤ 2, else NaN.

funcs.name: Accuracy
funcs.signature: func Accuracy(clf interface { Predict([]float64) (int, error) }, X [][]float64, y []int) (float64, error)
funcs.doc:
Accuracy returns the fraction of samples in X whose predicted label from the
given classifier matches the true label in y. The classifier is any type with
a Predict([]float64) (int, error) method.

funcs.name: BayesFactor
funcs.doc:
BayesFactor returns the Bayes factor B₁₂ = exp(logEvidence1 − logEvidence2)
comparing model 1 against model 2 from their log marginal likelihoods.

funcs.name: BayesFactorInterpretation
funcs.doc:
BayesFactorInterpretation returns a qualitative label for the strength of
evidence carried by a Bayes factor on the Jeffreys/Kass–Raftery scale
("negligible", "positive", "strong", or "decisive"). The magnitude is taken
so that values below 1 are interpreted in favor of the alternative.

funcs.name: BetaBernoulliPredictiveProb
funcs.doc:
BetaBernoulliPredictiveProb returns the probability that the next Bernoulli
trial is a success under the posterior, which equals the posterior mean.

funcs.name: BetaFunc
funcs.doc: BetaFunc returns the Beta function B(a,b) = Γ(a)Γ(b)/Γ(a+b) for a,b > 0.

funcs.name: Digamma
funcs.doc:
Digamma returns the digamma function ψ(x) = d/dx ln Γ(x). It uses the
recurrence ψ(x) = ψ(x+1) − 1/x to shift the argument into the asymptotic
regime and then an asymptotic expansion. It is valid for x > 0.

funcs.name: DirichletMultinomialPredictive
funcs.doc:
DirichletMultinomialPredictive returns the predictive category probabilities
for the next single draw under the posterior, i.e. the posterior mean αᵢ/α₀.

funcs.name: ErfInv
funcs.doc:
ErfInv returns the inverse error function, the value y such that erf(y) = x
for x in (−1,1). It uses a rational approximation refined by a Newton step.

funcs.name: GammaFunc
funcs.doc:
GammaFunc returns the Gamma function Γ(x). It is a convenience wrapper around
math.Gamma provided so callers of this package need not import math directly.

funcs.name: InverseRegularizedGammaP
funcs.doc:
InverseRegularizedGammaP returns the value x such that RegularizedGammaP(a,x)
= p, for a > 0 and p in [0,1]. It uses a bracketing bisection combined with
Newton refinement and is accurate to roughly 1e-12.

funcs.name: InverseRegularizedIncompleteBeta
funcs.signature: func InverseRegularizedIncompleteBeta(p, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
InverseRegularizedIncompleteBeta returns the value x in [0,1] such that
RegularizedIncompleteBeta(x,a,b) = p, i.e. the quantile of the Beta(a,b)
distribution. It uses bisection with Newton refinement.

funcs.name: LogBayesFactor
funcs.doc: LogBayesFactor returns the log Bayes factor logEvidence1 − logEvidence2.

funcs.name: LogBeta
funcs.doc:
LogBeta returns the natural logarithm of the Beta function,
ln B(a,b) = ln Γ(a) + ln Γ(b) − ln Γ(a+b), for a,b > 0.

funcs.name: LogChoose
funcs.doc:
LogChoose returns the natural logarithm of the binomial coefficient
ln C(n,k). It returns math.Inf(-1) when k is outside [0,n].

funcs.name: LogFactorial
funcs.doc:
LogFactorial returns ln(n!) computed in the log-gamma domain as ln Γ(n+1).
It returns NaN for negative n.

funcs.name: LogGamma
funcs.doc:
LogGamma returns the natural logarithm of the absolute value of the Gamma
function, ln|Γ(x)|. It is a thin, sign-discarding wrapper around the standard
library's math.Lgamma and underlies most of the log-domain computations in
this package.

funcs.name: LogMarginalLikelihoodBetaBinomial
funcs.signature: func LogMarginalLikelihoodBetaBinomial(prior Beta, k, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
LogMarginalLikelihoodBetaBinomial returns the log marginal likelihood (log
evidence) of observing k successes in n Binomial trials under a Beta(a,b)
prior on the success probability.

funcs.name: LogMarginalLikelihoodDirichletMultinomial
funcs.doc:
LogMarginalLikelihoodDirichletMultinomial returns the log marginal likelihood
of the observed category counts under a Dirichlet prior.

funcs.name: LogMarginalLikelihoodGammaPoisson
funcs.doc:
LogMarginalLikelihoodGammaPoisson returns the log marginal likelihood of the
observed Poisson counts under a Gamma(shape, rate) prior on the rate.

funcs.name: LogMarginalLikelihoodNormalInverseGamma
funcs.doc:
LogMarginalLikelihoodNormalInverseGamma returns the log marginal likelihood
of the data under a normal likelihood with unknown mean and variance and a
Normal-Inverse-Gamma prior.

funcs.name: LogMarginalLikelihoodNormalKnownVariance
funcs.signature: func LogMarginalLikelihoodNormalKnownVariance(prior Normal, sigma2 float64, data []float64) float64
funcs.doc:
LogMarginalLikelihoodNormalKnownVariance returns the log marginal likelihood
of the data under a normal likelihood with known sampling variance sigma²
and a Normal prior on the mean. It is computed exactly via the sequential
posterior-predictive factorization.

funcs.name: LogMultinomialCoefficient
funcs.doc:
LogMultinomialCoefficient returns the natural logarithm of the multinomial
coefficient (Σkᵢ)! / Πkᵢ! for the non-negative counts ks.

funcs.name: LogSumExp
funcs.doc:
LogSumExp returns ln(Σ exp(xᵢ)) computed in a numerically stable way by
factoring out the maximum element. It returns math.Inf(-1) for an empty slice.

funcs.name: MarginalLikelihoodBetaBinomial
funcs.signature: func MarginalLikelihoodBetaBinomial(prior Beta, k, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
MarginalLikelihoodBetaBinomial returns the marginal likelihood (evidence) of
k successes in n trials under a Beta prior.

funcs.name: PosteriorMean
funcs.signature: func PosteriorMean(d interface{ Mean() float64 }) float64
funcs.doc:
PosteriorMean returns the posterior mean of a distribution exposing a Mean
method. It is provided for symmetry with PosteriorVariance.

funcs.name: PosteriorModelProbabilities
funcs.doc:
PosteriorModelProbabilities returns the posterior probabilities of a set of
models given their log marginal likelihoods and (unnormalized) prior
probabilities. The two slices must have equal length; a mismatch returns nil.

funcs.name: PosteriorOdds
funcs.doc:
PosteriorOdds returns the posterior odds of model 1 versus model 2, the
product of the Bayes factor and the prior odds.

funcs.name: PosteriorVariance
funcs.signature: func PosteriorVariance(d interface{ Variance() float64 }) float64
funcs.doc:
PosteriorVariance returns the posterior variance of a distribution exposing a
Variance method.

funcs.name: ProbabilityBetaExceedsBeta
funcs.doc:
ProbabilityBetaExceedsBeta returns the probability P(X > Y) that a draw from
Beta a exceeds an independent draw from Beta b, computed by numerical
integration over the density of X times the CDF-complement of Y. This is the
core quantity in Bayesian A/B testing.

funcs.name: ProbabilityBetaGreater
funcs.signature: func ProbabilityBetaGreater(d Beta, threshold float64) float64
funcs.doc:
ProbabilityBetaGreater returns the posterior probability that a Beta random
variable exceeds threshold, P(θ > t).

funcs.name: ProbabilityBetween
funcs.signature: func ProbabilityBetween(d interface{ CDF(float64) float64 }, lo, hi float64) float64
funcs.doc: ProbabilityBetween returns P(lo < X ≤ hi) for a distribution exposing a CDF.

funcs.name: ProbabilityGreater
funcs.signature: func ProbabilityGreater(d interface{ CDF(float64) float64 }, threshold float64) float64
funcs.doc:
ProbabilityGreater returns P(X > threshold) for a distribution exposing a
CDF method.

funcs.name: RegularizedGammaP
funcs.doc:
RegularizedGammaP returns the regularized lower incomplete gamma function
P(a,x) = γ(a,x)/Γ(a) for a > 0 and x >= 0. It equals the CDF of a Gamma(a,1)
random variable at x.

funcs.name: RegularizedGammaQ
funcs.doc:
RegularizedGammaQ returns the regularized upper incomplete gamma function
Q(a,x) = 1 − P(a,x) = Γ(a,x)/Γ(a) for a > 0 and x >= 0.

funcs.name: RegularizedIncompleteBeta
funcs.signature: func RegularizedIncompleteBeta(x, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
RegularizedIncompleteBeta returns the regularized incomplete beta function
Iₓ(a,b) for x in [0,1] and a,b > 0. It equals the CDF of a Beta(a,b) random
variable evaluated at x.

funcs.name: StdNormalCDF
funcs.doc:
StdNormalCDF returns the standard normal cumulative distribution function
Φ(z) = ½(1 + erf(z/√2)).

funcs.name: StdNormalPDF
funcs.doc: StdNormalPDF returns the standard normal probability density φ(z).

funcs.name: StdNormalQuantile
funcs.doc:
StdNormalQuantile returns the p-quantile of the standard normal distribution,
the value z such that Φ(z) = p for p in (0,1).

funcs.name: Trigamma
funcs.doc:
Trigamma returns the trigamma function ψ₁(x) = d²/dx² ln Γ(x), the derivative
of the digamma function, for x > 0.

examples.name: BayesianNetwork
examples.code:
bn := NewBayesianNetwork()
bn.AddVariable("Rain", 2)
bn.AddVariable("Wet", 2)
bn.SetCPT("Rain", nil, []float64{0.8, 0.2})
bn.SetCPT("Wet", []string{"Rain"}, []float64{
	0.9, 0.1,
	0.2, 0.8,
})
p, _ := bn.ConditionalProbability("Rain", 1, map[string]int{"Wet": 1})
fmt.Printf("P(Rain=1 | Wet=1) = %.3f\n", p)

examples.name: BetaBernoulliPosterior
examples.code:
prior := UniformBetaPrior()
post := BetaBernoulliPosterior(prior, 8, 2)
fmt.Printf("posterior mean = %.3f\n", post.Mean())
ci := BetaCredibleInterval(post, 0.95)
fmt.Printf("95%% CI = [%.3f, %.3f]\n", ci.Lower, ci.Upper)

examples.name: GammaPoissonPosterior

packages.name: bigfloat
packages.synopsis: Package bigfloat implements arbitrary-precision real analysis on top of math/big.Float.
packages.doc:
Package bigfloat implements arbitrary-precision real analysis on top of
math/big.Float.

Everything in the package is computed to a caller-requested precision,
expressed in bits of mantissa. Each routine accepts a prec argument and
returns a freshly allocated *big.Float carrying exactly that precision,
rounded to nearest. Internally the routines work at a slightly higher
"guard" precision and, where the argument magnitude demands it, add extra
bits so that argument reduction does not lose significance; the final
result is then rounded down to the requested precision.

The package is organised in families:

  - Mathematical constants to N bits: [Pi], [E], [Ln2], [Ln10],
    [EulerGamma], [Catalan], [Apery], [GoldenRatio] and many more.
  - Elementary transcendental functions built from range-reduced power
    series and the arithmetic-geometric mean: [Exp], [Log], [Sqrt], [Cbrt],
    [Pow], [Sin], [Cos], [Tan], [Atan], [Asin], [Acos], [Sinh], [Cosh],
    [Tanh] and their relatives.
  - The gamma family: [Factorial], [Gamma], [Lgamma], [Digamma], [Beta] and
    the Bernoulli numbers via [Bernoulli].
  - Newton-based inverse functions such as [LambertW], [PlasticNumber] and
    the AGM-based elliptic integrals [EllipticK] and [EllipticE].
  - Utility layers for construction, conversion, comparison and rounding of
    *big.Float values.

Only the Go standard library is used. Functions whose mathematical domain is
restricted (for example [Log] of a non-positive number, or [Asin] of an
argument outside [-1, 1]) return an error rather than panicking; total
functions return a *big.Float directly.

The package is a self-contained sibling of github.com/malcolmston/algebra and
does not depend on the parent module.

vars.doc: Domain errors returned by the partial functions in this package.

funcs.name: AGM
funcs.signature: func AGM(a, b *big.Float, prec uint) (*big.Float, error)
funcs.doc:
AGM returns the arithmetic-geometric mean of a and b to prec bits. It
requires a >= 0 and b >= 0 and returns ErrNegative otherwise.

funcs.name: Abs
funcs.doc: Abs returns |x| rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: Acos
funcs.doc:
Acos returns the arccosine of x to prec bits, in [0, pi]. It returns
ErrDomain unless -1 <= x <= 1.

funcs.name: Acosh
funcs.doc:
Acosh returns the inverse hyperbolic cosine log(x + sqrt(x^2-1)) to prec bits.
It returns ErrDomain if x < 1.

funcs.name: Acot
funcs.doc:
Acot returns the arccotangent atan(1/x) to prec bits (principal value in
(0, pi)); Acot(0) == pi/2.

funcs.name: Acoth
funcs.doc:
Acoth returns the inverse hyperbolic cotangent (1/2)*log((x+1)/(x-1)) to prec
bits. It returns ErrDomain unless |x| > 1.

funcs.name: Acsc
funcs.doc:
Acsc returns the arccosecant asin(1/x) to prec bits. It returns ErrDomain if
|x| < 1.

funcs.name: Acsch
funcs.doc:
Acsch returns the inverse hyperbolic cosecant asinh(1/x) to prec bits. It
returns ErrDomain if x == 0.

funcs.name: Add
funcs.doc: Add returns x + y rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: AlmostEqual
funcs.doc: AlmostEqual reports whether |x-y| <= tol.

funcs.name: Apery
funcs.doc:
Apery returns Apery's constant zeta(3) to prec bits using

	zeta(3) = (5/2) * sum_{n>=1} (-1)^{n-1} / (n^3 * C(2n,n)).

funcs.name: Asec
funcs.doc:
Asec returns the arcsecant acos(1/x) to prec bits. It returns ErrDomain if
|x| < 1.

funcs.name: Asech
funcs.doc:
Asech returns the inverse hyperbolic secant acosh(1/x) to prec bits. It
returns ErrDomain unless 0 < x <= 1.

funcs.name: Asin
funcs.doc:
Asin returns the arcsine of x to prec bits, in [-pi/2, pi/2]. It returns
ErrDomain unless -1 <= x <= 1.

funcs.name: Asinh
funcs.doc:
Asinh returns the inverse hyperbolic sine log(x + sqrt(x^2+1)) to prec bits,
for any real x.

funcs.name: Atan
funcs.doc: Atan returns the arctangent of x to prec bits, in (-pi/2, pi/2).

funcs.name: Atan2
funcs.doc: Atan2 returns the angle of the point (x, y) in (-pi, pi], to prec bits.

funcs.name: Atanh
funcs.doc:
Atanh returns the inverse hyperbolic tangent (1/2)*log((1+x)/(1-x)) to prec
bits. It returns ErrDomain unless -1 < x < 1.

funcs.name: Bernoulli
funcs.doc:
Bernoulli returns the n-th Bernoulli number B_n as an exact *big.Rat, using
the convention B_1 = -1/2.

funcs.name: BernoulliFloat
funcs.doc: BernoulliFloat returns the n-th Bernoulli number rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: Beta
funcs.signature: func Beta(a, b *big.Float, prec uint) (*big.Float, error)
funcs.doc:
Beta returns the beta function B(a,b) = Gamma(a)Gamma(b)/Gamma(a+b) to prec
bits, for a > 0 and b > 0. It returns ErrDomain otherwise.

funcs.name: Binomial
funcs.signature: func Binomial(n, k uint, prec uint) *big.Float
funcs.doc: Binomial returns the binomial coefficient C(n,k) rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: BinomialBig
funcs.doc: BinomialBig returns the binomial coefficient C(n,k) as an exact *big.Int.

funcs.name: BinomialReal
funcs.signature: func BinomialReal(x, y *big.Float, prec uint) (*big.Float, error)
funcs.doc:
BinomialReal returns the generalised binomial coefficient
Gamma(x+1)/(Gamma(y+1)*Gamma(x-y+1)) to prec bits, for real x and y where all
three gamma values are finite. It returns an error at a pole.

funcs.name: Catalan
funcs.doc:
Catalan returns Catalan's constant G to prec bits using the rapidly
convergent identity

	G = (pi/8)*ln(2+sqrt(3)) + (3/8) * sum_{k>=0} 1/((2k+1)^2 * C(2k,k)).

funcs.name: Cbrt
funcs.doc:
Cbrt returns the real cube root of x to prec bits, for any real x (negative
arguments yield negative roots).

funcs.name: Ceil
funcs.doc: Ceil returns the least integer >= x, to prec bits.

funcs.name: Clamp
funcs.signature: func Clamp(x, lo, hi *big.Float, prec uint) *big.Float
funcs.doc: Clamp returns x confined to the interval [lo, hi], rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: Cmp
funcs.doc: Cmp compares x and y and returns -1, 0, or +1.

funcs.name: Copysign
funcs.doc: Copysign returns a value with the magnitude of x and the sign of y.

funcs.name: Cos
funcs.doc: Cos returns the cosine of x (radians) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Cosh
funcs.doc: Cosh returns the hyperbolic cosine of x to prec bits.

funcs.name: Cospi
funcs.doc: Cospi returns cos(pi*x) to prec bits, reducing x modulo 2 exactly.

funcs.name: Cot
funcs.doc: Cot returns the cotangent of x (radians) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Coth
funcs.doc:
Coth returns the hyperbolic cotangent of x to prec bits. It returns ErrPole
if x == 0.

funcs.name: Coversin
funcs.doc: Coversin returns the coversed sine 1 - sin(x) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Csc
funcs.doc: Csc returns the cosecant 1/sin(x) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Csch
funcs.doc:
Csch returns the hyperbolic cosecant 1/sinh(x) to prec bits. It returns
ErrPole if x == 0.

funcs.name: Cube
funcs.doc: Cube returns x*x*x rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: Deg2Rad
funcs.doc: Deg2Rad converts x degrees to radians to prec bits.

funcs.name: Degree
funcs.doc: Degree returns the size of one degree in radians, pi/180, to prec bits.

funcs.name: Digamma
funcs.doc:
Digamma returns the digamma function psi(x) = Gamma'(x)/Gamma(x) to prec bits,
for any real x that is not a non-positive integer, for which it returns
ErrPole.

funcs.name: Dim
funcs.doc: Dim returns max(x-y, 0) rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: Div
funcs.doc: Div returns x / y rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: DoubleFactorial
funcs.doc: DoubleFactorial returns the double factorial n!! rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: DoubleFactorialBig
funcs.doc: DoubleFactorialBig returns the double factorial n!! as an exact *big.Int.

funcs.name: E
funcs.doc: E returns Euler's number e = exp(1) to prec bits.

funcs.name: EllipticE
funcs.doc:
EllipticE returns the complete elliptic integral of the second kind E(k) to
prec bits, via the descending AGM sequence

	E(k) = K(k) * (1 - sum_{n>=0} 2^{n-1} c_n^2),

with a_0 = 1, b_0 = sqrt(1-k^2), c_0 = k. It returns ErrDomain unless
-1 < k < 1.

funcs.name: EllipticK
funcs.doc:
EllipticK returns the complete elliptic integral of the first kind K(k) to
prec bits, using K(k) = pi / (2 * AGM(1, sqrt(1-k^2))). It returns ErrDomain
unless -1 < k < 1.

funcs.name: Equal
funcs.doc: Equal reports whether x and y are exactly equal.

funcs.name: EulerGamma
funcs.doc:
EulerGamma returns the Euler-Mascheroni constant gamma to prec bits, computed
with the Brent-McMillan series

	gamma = S(n)/I(n) - ln(n),

where I(n) = sum (n^k/k!)^2 and S(n) = sum (n^k/k!)^2 * H_k.

funcs.name: Exp
funcs.doc: Exp returns e**x to prec bits.

funcs.name: Exp10
funcs.doc: Exp10 returns 10**x to prec bits.

funcs.name: Exp2
funcs.doc: Exp2 returns 2**x to prec bits.

funcs.name: Expm1
funcs.doc: Expm1 returns e**x - 1 to prec bits, accurate even when x is small.

funcs.name: Exsec
funcs.doc: Exsec returns the exsecant sec(x) - 1 to prec bits.

funcs.name: FMA
funcs.signature: func FMA(x, y, z *big.Float, prec uint) *big.Float
funcs.doc:
FMA returns x*y + z rounded to prec bits (computed at higher internal
precision to reduce intermediate rounding).

funcs.name: Factorial
funcs.doc: Factorial returns n! rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: FactorialBig
funcs.doc: FactorialBig returns n! as an exact *big.Int.

funcs.name: FallingFactorial
funcs.signature: func FallingFactorial(x *big.Float, n uint, prec uint) *big.Float
funcs.doc: FallingFactorial returns x*(x-1)*...*(x-n+1) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Float64
funcs.doc:
Float64 returns the value of x as a float64, together with the accuracy of
the conversion.

funcs.name: Floor
funcs.doc: Floor returns the greatest integer <= x, to prec bits.

funcs.name: Frac
funcs.doc: Frac returns the fractional part x - Trunc(x), to prec bits.

funcs.name: Frexp
funcs.signature: func Frexp(x *big.Float, prec uint) (frac *big.Float, exp int)
funcs.doc:
Frexp breaks x into a normalised fraction f in [0.5,1) and exponent e such
that x == f * 2**e. The fraction is returned to prec bits.

funcs.name: FromBig
funcs.doc: FromBig returns the big.Int n as a *big.Float with the given precision.

funcs.name: FromFloat64
funcs.doc: FromFloat64 returns the float64 x as a *big.Float with the given precision.

funcs.name: FromInt
funcs.doc: FromInt returns the integer n as a *big.Float with the given precision.

funcs.name: FromRat
funcs.doc: FromRat returns the rational q as a *big.Float with the given precision.

funcs.name: Gamma
funcs.doc:
Gamma returns the gamma function Gamma(x) to prec bits, for any real x that
is not a non-positive integer (a pole), for which it returns ErrPole.

funcs.name: GoldenRatio
funcs.doc: GoldenRatio returns the golden ratio (1+sqrt(5))/2 to prec bits.

funcs.name: Half
funcs.doc: Half returns 1/2 with the given precision.

funcs.name: HalfPi
funcs.doc: HalfPi returns pi/2 to prec bits.

funcs.name: Haversin
funcs.doc: Haversin returns the haversine (1 - cos(x))/2 to prec bits.

funcs.name: Hypot
funcs.doc: Hypot returns sqrt(x*x + y*y), computed without undue overflow, to prec bits.

funcs.name: InvE
funcs.doc: InvE returns 1/e to prec bits.

funcs.name: InvGoldenRatio
funcs.doc: InvGoldenRatio returns 1/phi = (sqrt(5)-1)/2 to prec bits.

funcs.name: InvPi
funcs.doc: InvPi returns 1/pi to prec bits.

funcs.name: InvSqrt2
funcs.doc: InvSqrt2 returns 1/sqrt(2) to prec bits.

funcs.name: InvSqrtPi
funcs.doc: InvSqrtPi returns 1/sqrt(pi) to prec bits.

funcs.name: IsInf
funcs.doc: IsInf reports whether x is an infinity.

funcs.name: IsInteger
funcs.doc: IsInteger reports whether x has no fractional part.

funcs.name: IsZero
funcs.doc: IsZero reports whether x is zero.

funcs.name: LambertW
funcs.doc:
LambertW returns the principal branch W0(x) of the Lambert W function to prec
bits, i.e. the solution w of w*e^w = x with w >= -1. It is computed by
Halley's (Newton-type) iteration. It returns ErrDomain if x < -1/e.

funcs.name: Ldexp
funcs.signature: func Ldexp(x *big.Float, n int, prec uint) *big.Float
funcs.doc: Ldexp returns x * 2**n, to prec bits.

funcs.name: Lgamma
funcs.signature: func Lgamma(x *big.Float, prec uint) (val *big.Float, sign int, err error)
funcs.doc:
Lgamma returns the natural logarithm of the absolute value of Gamma(x) and
the sign of Gamma(x) (+1 or -1), to prec bits. It returns ErrPole at the
non-positive integers.

funcs.name: Ln10
funcs.doc: Ln10 returns the natural logarithm of 10 to prec bits.

funcs.name: Ln2
funcs.doc: Ln2 returns the natural logarithm of 2 to prec bits.

funcs.name: Ln2Pi
funcs.doc: Ln2Pi returns the natural logarithm of 2*pi to prec bits.

funcs.name: LnPi
funcs.doc: LnPi returns the natural logarithm of pi to prec bits.

funcs.name: Log
funcs.doc:
Log returns the natural logarithm of x to prec bits. It returns ErrDomain if
x <= 0.

funcs.name: Log10
funcs.doc:
Log10 returns the base-10 logarithm of x to prec bits. It returns ErrDomain
if x <= 0.

funcs.name: Log10E
funcs.doc: Log10E returns log base 10 of e, i.e. 1/ln(10), to prec bits.

funcs.name: Log1p
funcs.doc:
Log1p returns log(1+x) to prec bits, accurate for small x. It returns
ErrDomain if x <= -1.

funcs.name: Log2
funcs.doc:
Log2 returns the base-2 logarithm of x to prec bits. It returns ErrDomain if
x <= 0.

funcs.name: Log2E
funcs.doc: Log2E returns log base 2 of e, i.e. 1/ln(2), to prec bits.

funcs.name: LogAddExp
funcs.doc: LogAddExp returns log(e**x + e**y) to prec bits, computed stably.

funcs.name: LogBase
funcs.signature: func LogBase(x, base *big.Float, prec uint) (*big.Float, error)
funcs.doc:
LogBase returns the logarithm of x in the given base to prec bits. It returns
ErrDomain if x <= 0 or base <= 0 or base == 1.

funcs.name: LogBeta
funcs.signature: func LogBeta(a, b *big.Float, prec uint) (*big.Float, error)
funcs.doc:
LogBeta returns log(B(a,b)) to prec bits, for a > 0 and b > 0. It returns
ErrDomain otherwise.

funcs.name: Logit
funcs.doc:
Logit returns log(x/(1-x)) to prec bits, the inverse of Sigmoid. It returns
ErrDomain unless 0 < x < 1.

funcs.name: Max
funcs.doc: Max returns the larger of x and y (rounded to prec bits).

funcs.name: Min
funcs.doc: Min returns the smaller of x and y (rounded to prec bits).

funcs.name: Mod
funcs.doc: Mod returns the floating-point remainder x - y*Trunc(x/y), to prec bits.

funcs.name: Mul
funcs.doc: Mul returns x * y rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: MustParse
funcs.doc:
MustParse is like Parse but panics on a parse error. It is intended for
package-level initialisers and tests with known-good literals.

funcs.name: Neg
funcs.doc: Neg returns -x rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: New
funcs.doc: New returns a new zero value with the given precision in bits.

funcs.name: One
funcs.doc: One returns 1 with the given precision.

funcs.name: Parse
funcs.signature: func Parse(s string, prec uint) (*big.Float, error)
funcs.doc:
Parse parses s (in any base-10 float syntax accepted by big.Float.SetString)
into a *big.Float with the given precision. It returns ErrParse on failure.

funcs.name: Pi
funcs.doc:
Pi returns the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter, pi, to prec
bits.

funcs.name: PlasticNumber
funcs.doc:
PlasticNumber returns the plastic number, the real root of x^3 = x + 1, to
prec bits. It is found by Newton's method on f(x) = x^3 - x - 1.

funcs.name: Pochhammer
funcs.signature: func Pochhammer(x *big.Float, n uint, prec uint) *big.Float
funcs.doc: Pochhammer is an alias for RisingFactorial.

funcs.name: Pow
funcs.signature: func Pow(x, y *big.Float, prec uint) (*big.Float, error)
funcs.doc:
Pow returns x**y to prec bits, defined as exp(y*log(x)) for x > 0. Special
cases: x**0 == 1 for any x; 0**y == 0 for y > 0. Negative x is supported only
when y is an integer, via PowInt. It returns ErrDomain otherwise.

funcs.name: PowInt
funcs.signature: func PowInt(x *big.Float, n int64, prec uint) *big.Float
funcs.doc:
PowInt returns x**n for integer n to prec bits, via binary exponentiation.
It is exact up to the final rounding and valid for any real x (with x != 0
when n < 0).

funcs.name: QuarterPi
funcs.doc: QuarterPi returns pi/4 to prec bits.

funcs.name: Rad2Deg
funcs.doc: Rad2Deg converts x radians to degrees to prec bits.

funcs.name: Recip
funcs.doc: Recip returns 1 / x rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: Remainder
funcs.doc: Remainder returns the IEEE 754 remainder x - y*Round(x/y), to prec bits.

funcs.name: RisingFactorial
funcs.signature: func RisingFactorial(x *big.Float, n uint, prec uint) *big.Float
funcs.doc:
RisingFactorial returns the Pochhammer symbol x*(x+1)*...*(x+n-1) to prec
bits.

funcs.name: Root
funcs.signature: func Root(x *big.Float, n int, prec uint) (*big.Float, error)
funcs.doc:
Root returns the real n-th root of x to prec bits. It returns ErrDomain if
n == 0, or if x < 0 with n even.

funcs.name: Round
funcs.doc: Round returns x rounded to the nearest integer, ties away from zero.

funcs.name: Rsqrt
funcs.doc:
Rsqrt returns the reciprocal square root 1/sqrt(x) to prec bits. It returns
ErrDomain if x <= 0.

funcs.name: Sec
funcs.doc: Sec returns the secant 1/cos(x) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Sech
funcs.doc: Sech returns the hyperbolic secant 1/cosh(x) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Sigmoid
funcs.doc: Sigmoid returns the logistic function 1/(1+e**-x) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Sign
funcs.doc: Sign returns -1, 0, or +1 according to the sign of x.

funcs.name: Signbit
funcs.doc: Signbit reports whether x is negative or negative zero.

funcs.name: SilverRatio
funcs.doc: SilverRatio returns the silver ratio 1+sqrt(2) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Sin
funcs.doc: Sin returns the sine of x (radians) to prec bits.

funcs.name: SinCos
funcs.doc: SinCos returns both the sine and cosine of x (radians) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Sinc
funcs.doc:
Sinc returns the unnormalised cardinal sine sin(x)/x to prec bits, with
Sinc(0) == 1.

funcs.name: Sinh
funcs.doc: Sinh returns the hyperbolic sine of x to prec bits.

funcs.name: Sinpi
funcs.doc:
Sinpi returns sin(pi*x) to prec bits, reducing x modulo 2 exactly so it stays
accurate for large x.

funcs.name: Softplus
funcs.doc:
Softplus returns log(1+e**x) to prec bits, computed in a numerically stable
way for large |x|.

funcs.name: Sqrt
funcs.doc:
Sqrt returns the square root of x to prec bits. It returns ErrNegative if
x < 0.

funcs.name: Sqrt2
funcs.doc: Sqrt2 returns the square root of 2 to prec bits.

funcs.name: Sqrt3
funcs.doc: Sqrt3 returns the square root of 3 to prec bits.

funcs.name: Sqrt5
funcs.doc: Sqrt5 returns the square root of 5 to prec bits.

funcs.name: SqrtPi
funcs.doc: SqrtPi returns sqrt(pi) to prec bits.

funcs.name: SqrtTwoPi
funcs.doc: SqrtTwoPi returns sqrt(2*pi) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Square
funcs.doc: Square returns x*x rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: String
funcs.doc: String formats x using big.Float's default text representation.

funcs.name: Sub
funcs.doc: Sub returns x - y rounded to prec bits.

funcs.name: Tan
funcs.doc: Tan returns the tangent of x (radians) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Tanh
funcs.doc: Tanh returns the hyperbolic tangent of x to prec bits.

funcs.name: Tanpi
funcs.doc: Tanpi returns tan(pi*x) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Text
funcs.signature: func Text(x *big.Float, format byte, digits int) string
funcs.doc:
Text formats x with the given format byte and number of digits, matching
big.Float.Text.

funcs.name: Trunc
funcs.doc: Trunc returns the integer part of x (rounding toward zero), to prec bits.

funcs.name: Two
funcs.doc: Two returns 2 with the given precision.

funcs.name: TwoInvPi
funcs.doc: TwoInvPi returns 2/pi to prec bits.

funcs.name: TwoPi
funcs.doc: TwoPi returns 2*pi to prec bits.

funcs.name: Versin
funcs.doc: Versin returns the versed sine 1 - cos(x) to prec bits.

funcs.name: Zero
funcs.doc: Zero returns 0 with the given precision.

funcs.name: Zeta3
funcs.doc: Zeta3 is an alias for Apery; it returns zeta(3) to prec bits.

examples.name: Exp
examples.doc:
ExampleExp computes e to 50 decimal digits by evaluating exp(1) at 200 bits
of precision.

examples.name: LambertW
examples.doc: ExampleLambertW solves w*exp(w) = 1 for w, the omega constant.

examples.name: Pi
examples.doc: ExamplePi prints pi to 30 decimal places.

packages.name: calendrical
packages.synopsis: Package calendrical implements calendar and date arithmetic using only the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package calendrical implements calendar and date arithmetic using only the
Go standard library. It is a pure-arithmetic library: the core conversions
do not depend on the time package and are deterministic — identical inputs
always yield identical outputs.

The package is organised around the Rata Die (RD) fixed-day count, an
integer number of days elapsed since the proleptic Gregorian date
1 January 1 (RD 1, a Monday). Every calendar is converted to and from this
common representation, following the algorithms described by Nachum
Dershowitz and Edward M. Reingold in Calendrical Calculations. Julian Day
Numbers (JDN), Modified Julian Dates (MJD) and Unix day counts are exposed
as thin conversions on top of the fixed-day count.

Supported calendars:

  - Proleptic Gregorian calendar.
  - Proleptic Julian calendar (no year zero).
  - ISO-8601 week date (year, week, weekday).
  - Hebrew (Jewish) calendar, including molad and holiday helpers.
  - Islamic calendar in its tabular/arithmetical form.
  - Persian (Jalali) calendar in its arithmetical (2820-year) form.
  - Coptic and Ethiopic calendars.

In addition the package provides day-of-week arithmetic (k-day search),
leap-year predicates, date differences and offsets, Gregorian (Western) and
Julian (Orthodox) Easter via the computus, and low-precision astronomical
approximations for the equinoxes, solstices and the phases of the Moon based
on Jean Meeus's Astronomical Algorithms.

Unless a function name says otherwise, "fixed" arguments and results are RD
day counts (int), month and day numbers are 1-based, and Gregorian years use
astronomical numbering (there is a year 0). Astronomical routines return
Julian Ephemeris Days (JDE, float64) in Terrestrial Time.

consts.signature:
const (
	Thoout = iota + 1
	Paope
	Athor
	Koiak
	Tobe
	Meshir
	Paremotep
	Parmoute
	Pashons
	Paone
	Epep
	Mesore
	Epagomenae
)
consts.doc:
Coptic month numbers. The Coptic year has twelve 30-day months followed by a
short thirteenth month (the epagomenae) of five or six days.

Epoch constants expressed as Rata Die (RD) fixed-day counts. RD 1 is the
proleptic Gregorian date 1 January 1 (a Monday).

consts.signature:
const (
	January = iota + 1
	February
	March
	April
	May
	June
	July
	August
	September
	October
	November
	December
)
consts.doc: Gregorian month numbers.

consts.signature:
const (
	Nisan = iota + 1
	Iyyar
	Sivan
	Tammuz
	Av
	Elul
	Tishri
	Marheshvan
	Kislev
	Tevet
	Shevat
	Adar
	AdarII
)
consts.doc:
Hebrew month numbers. The Hebrew year begins in Tishri (month 7); months 1–6
(Nisan through Elul) fall in the second half of the civil year. In a leap
year Adar is split into Adar I (month 12) and Adar II (month 13).

consts.signature:
const (
	Muharram = iota + 1
	Safar
	RabiI
	RabiII
	JumadaI
	JumadaII
	Rajab
	Shaban
	Ramadan
	Shawwal
	DhuAlQadah
	DhuAlHijjah
)
consts.doc: Islamic month numbers (tabular calendar).

consts.signature:
const (
	Farvardin = iota + 1
	Ordibehesht
	Khordad
	Tir
	Mordad
	Shahrivar
	Mehr
	Aban
	Azar

	Bahman
	Esfand
)
consts.doc: Persian (Jalali) month numbers.

consts.signature: const JDEpoch = -1721424.5
consts.doc:
JDEpoch is the RD moment corresponding to Julian Date 0.0. Because Julian
days begin at noon, this value has a fractional part of one half.

consts.signature: const MeanLunarMonth = 29.530588861
consts.doc:
MeanLunarMonth is the mean length of the synodic month (New Moon to New Moon)
in days.

consts.signature: const MeanTropicalYear = 365.242189
consts.doc: MeanTropicalYear is the mean length of the tropical year in days.

vars.doc: Errors returned by validating constructors and parsers.

types.name: CopticDate
types.doc: CopticDate is a date in the Coptic (Alexandrian) calendar.

funcs.name: CopticFromFixed
funcs.doc: CopticFromFixed converts an RD fixed day to a Coptic date.

funcs.name: NewCoptic
funcs.signature: func NewCoptic(year, month, day int) (CopticDate, error)
funcs.doc:
NewCoptic returns a validated CopticDate, or an error if the fields do not
describe a real date.

methods.name: Fixed
methods.doc: Fixed returns the RD fixed day of the Coptic date.

methods.name: JDN
methods.doc: JDN returns the Julian Day Number of the Coptic date.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String formats the Coptic date as "Day MonthName Year".

methods.name: Valid
methods.doc: Valid reports whether the receiver is a valid Coptic date.

methods.name: Weekday
methods.doc: Weekday returns the day of the week of the Coptic date.

types.name: EthiopicDate
types.doc:
EthiopicDate is a date in the Ethiopic calendar, which shares the Coptic
structure but uses a different epoch.

funcs.name: EthiopicFromFixed
funcs.doc: EthiopicFromFixed converts an RD fixed day to an Ethiopic date.

funcs.name: NewEthiopic
funcs.signature: func NewEthiopic(year, month, day int) (EthiopicDate, error)
funcs.doc:
NewEthiopic returns a validated EthiopicDate, or an error if the fields do not
describe a real date.

methods.name: Fixed
methods.doc: Fixed returns the RD fixed day of the Ethiopic date.

methods.name: JDN
methods.doc: JDN returns the Julian Day Number of the Ethiopic date.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String formats the Ethiopic date as "Day MonthName Year".

methods.name: Valid

methods.name: Weekday
methods.doc: Weekday returns the day of the week of the Ethiopic date.

types.name: GregorianDate
types.doc:
GregorianDate is a proleptic Gregorian date. Years use astronomical
numbering, so the year before 1 is year 0 (1 BCE) and the year before that is
-1 (2 BCE).

funcs.name: GregorianEasterDate
funcs.doc:
GregorianEasterDate returns Western Easter Sunday of the given year as a
Gregorian date.

funcs.name: GregorianFromFixed
funcs.doc: GregorianFromFixed converts an RD fixed day to a Gregorian date.

funcs.name: JulianToGregorian
funcs.signature: func JulianToGregorian(year, month, day int) GregorianDate
funcs.doc:
JulianToGregorian converts a Julian-calendar date to the equivalent
Gregorian date for the same instant.

funcs.name: NewGregorian
funcs.signature: func NewGregorian(year, month, day int) (GregorianDate, error)
funcs.doc:
NewGregorian returns a validated GregorianDate, or an error if the fields do
not describe a real date.

funcs.name: OrthodoxEasterDate
funcs.doc:
OrthodoxEasterDate returns Orthodox Easter Sunday of the given year as the
equivalent Gregorian (civil) date.

methods.name: After
methods.doc: After reports whether the receiver follows the other Gregorian date.

methods.name: Before
methods.doc: Before reports whether the receiver precedes the other Gregorian date.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether the receiver denotes the same day as the other date.

methods.name: Fixed
methods.doc: Fixed returns the RD fixed day of the Gregorian date.

methods.name: JDN
methods.doc: JDN returns the Julian Day Number of the Gregorian date.

methods.name: PlusDays
methods.doc: PlusDays returns a new GregorianDate n days after the receiver.

methods.name: PlusMonths
methods.doc:
PlusMonths returns the GregorianDate n calendar months after the receiver.
If the resulting month is shorter than the receiver's day, the day is clamped
to the last valid day of that month (so 31 January plus one month is
28 February, or 29 February in a leap year).

methods.name: PlusYears
methods.doc:
PlusYears returns the GregorianDate n years after the receiver, clamping
29 February to 28 February in common years.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String formats the date in ISO extended form (YYYY-MM-DD), using a sign for
non-positive years.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the signed number of days from the other date to the receiver.

methods.name: Valid
methods.doc: Valid reports whether the receiver is a valid Gregorian date.

methods.name: Weekday
methods.doc: Weekday returns the day of the week of the Gregorian date.

types.name: HebrewDate
types.doc: HebrewDate is a date in the Hebrew (Jewish) calendar.

funcs.name: HebrewFromFixed
funcs.doc: HebrewFromFixed converts an RD fixed day to a Hebrew date.

funcs.name: NewHebrew
funcs.signature: func NewHebrew(year, month, day int) (HebrewDate, error)
funcs.doc:
NewHebrew returns a validated HebrewDate, or an error if the fields do not
describe a real Hebrew date.

methods.name: Fixed

methods.name: JDN
methods.doc: JDN returns the Julian Day Number of the Hebrew date.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String formats the Hebrew date as "Day MonthName Year".

methods.name: Valid

methods.name: Weekday
methods.doc: Weekday returns the day of the week of the Hebrew date.

types.name: ISODate
types.doc:
ISODate is an ISO-8601 week date: a week-numbering year, an ISO week number
(1–53) and a day of the week numbered 1 (Monday) through 7 (Sunday).

funcs.name: ISOFromFixed
funcs.doc: ISOFromFixed converts an RD fixed day to an ISO week date.

funcs.name: NewISO
funcs.signature: func NewISO(year, week, day int) (ISODate, error)
funcs.doc:
NewISO returns a validated ISODate, or an error if the fields are not a real
ISO week date.

methods.name: Fixed
methods.doc: Fixed returns the RD fixed day of the ISO week date.

methods.name: JDN
methods.doc: JDN returns the Julian Day Number of the ISO week date.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String formats the ISO week date as YYYY-Www-D.

methods.name: Valid
methods.doc: Valid reports whether the receiver is a valid ISO week date.

methods.name: Weekday
methods.doc: Weekday returns the day of the week of the ISO week date.

types.name: IslamicDate
types.doc:
IslamicDate is a date in the tabular (arithmetical) Islamic calendar. This is
the civil approximation to the observational lunar calendar; individual dates
may differ from a local sighting by a day.

funcs.name: IslamicFromFixed
funcs.doc: IslamicFromFixed converts an RD fixed day to a tabular Islamic date.

funcs.name: NewIslamic
funcs.signature: func NewIslamic(year, month, day int) (IslamicDate, error)
funcs.doc:
NewIslamic returns a validated IslamicDate, or an error if the fields do not
describe a real date.

methods.name: Fixed
methods.doc: Fixed returns the RD fixed day of the Islamic date.

methods.name: JDN
methods.doc: JDN returns the Julian Day Number of the Islamic date.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String formats the Islamic date as "Day MonthName Year AH".

methods.name: Valid
methods.doc: Valid reports whether the receiver is a valid Islamic date.

methods.name: Weekday
methods.doc: Weekday returns the day of the week of the Islamic date.

types.name: JulianDate
types.doc:
JulianDate is a proleptic Julian-calendar date. The Julian calendar has no
year zero: the year before 1 CE is 1 BCE, represented here as -1. Use
JulianYearToAstronomical to convert to a continuous numbering when needed.

funcs.name: GregorianToJulian
funcs.signature: func GregorianToJulian(year, month, day int) JulianDate
funcs.doc:
GregorianToJulian converts a Gregorian date to the equivalent Julian-calendar
date for the same instant.

funcs.name: JulianFromFixed
funcs.doc: JulianFromFixed converts an RD fixed day to a Julian-calendar date.

funcs.name: NewJulian
funcs.signature: func NewJulian(year, month, day int) (JulianDate, error)
funcs.doc:
NewJulian returns a validated JulianDate, or an error if the fields do not
describe a real date.

methods.name: Fixed
methods.doc: Fixed returns the RD fixed day of the Julian-calendar date.

methods.name: JDN
methods.doc: JDN returns the Julian Day Number of the Julian-calendar date.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String formats the Julian date as YYYY-MM-DD.

methods.name: Valid
methods.doc: Valid reports whether the receiver is a valid Julian-calendar date.

methods.name: Weekday
methods.doc: Weekday returns the day of the week of the Julian-calendar date.

types.name: MoonPhase
types.signature: type MoonPhase int
types.doc: MoonPhase identifies one of the four principal lunar phases.

consts.doc: The four principal phases of the Moon.

types.name: PersianDate
types.doc:
PersianDate is a date in the arithmetical Persian (Jalali) calendar, which
approximates the astronomical Persian calendar using a 2820-year intercalation
cycle.

funcs.name: NewPersian
funcs.signature: func NewPersian(year, month, day int) (PersianDate, error)
funcs.doc:
NewPersian returns a validated PersianDate, or an error if the fields do not
describe a real date.

funcs.name: PersianFromFixed
funcs.doc: PersianFromFixed converts an RD fixed day to a Persian (Jalali) date.

methods.name: Fixed
methods.doc: Fixed returns the RD fixed day of the Persian date.

methods.name: JDN
methods.doc: JDN returns the Julian Day Number of the Persian date.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String formats the Persian date as YYYY-MM-DD.

methods.name: Valid
methods.doc: Valid reports whether the receiver is a valid Persian date.

methods.name: Weekday
methods.doc: Weekday returns the day of the week of the Persian date.

types.name: Season
types.signature: type Season int
types.doc: Season identifies one of the four astronomical season boundaries.

consts.doc:
The four astronomical seasons, named for the event that begins them in the
northern hemisphere.

types.name: Weekday
types.signature: type Weekday int
types.doc:
Weekday identifies a day of the week. The zero value is Sunday, matching the
convention used throughout this package where the week begins on Sunday.

consts.signature:
const (
	Sunday Weekday = iota
	Monday
	Tuesday
	Wednesday
	Thursday
	Friday
	Saturday
)
consts.doc: Days of the week.

funcs.name: GregorianWeekday
funcs.signature: func GregorianWeekday(year, month, day int) Weekday
funcs.doc: GregorianWeekday returns the day of the week of a Gregorian date.

funcs.name: WeekdayFromFixed
funcs.doc:
WeekdayFromFixed returns the day of the week of an RD fixed day, with Sunday
being zero.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the English name of the weekday.

funcs.name: AddBusinessDays
funcs.doc:
AddBusinessDays returns the RD fixed day reached by advancing n business days
(Monday–Friday) from the given fixed day. n may be negative to move backward.
If the starting day is a weekend, counting begins from the next business day
in the direction of travel.

funcs.name: AddDays
funcs.doc:
AddDays returns the RD fixed day n days after the given fixed day (n may be
negative).

funcs.name: AdjustedMod
funcs.doc:
AdjustedMod returns the value in [1, y] congruent to x modulo y, for y > 0.
It panics if y is zero.

funcs.name: AdventSunday
funcs.doc:
AdventSunday returns the RD fixed day of the first Sunday of Advent (the
fourth Sunday before Christmas) in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: Ascension
funcs.doc:
Ascension returns the RD fixed day of Ascension Thursday (39 days after
Western Easter) in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: AshWednesday
funcs.doc:
AshWednesday returns the RD fixed day of Ash Wednesday (46 days before Western
Easter) in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: AstronomicalToJulianYear
funcs.doc:
AstronomicalToJulianYear converts a continuous astronomical year number to a
Julian-calendar year that omits zero.

funcs.name: AutumnEquinoxJD
funcs.doc:
AutumnEquinoxJD returns the Julian Ephemeris Day of the September equinox of
the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: BusinessDaysBetween
funcs.doc:
BusinessDaysBetween returns the number of Monday-to-Friday days in the
half-open interval [start, end). The result is negative if end precedes
start.

funcs.name: Christmas
funcs.doc:
Christmas returns the RD fixed day of Christmas Day (25 December) in the given
Gregorian year.

funcs.name: CopticDaysInMonth
funcs.doc:
CopticDaysInMonth returns the number of days in the given month of the given
Coptic year (30 for months 1–12; 5 or 6 for the epagomenae).

funcs.name: CopticDaysInYear
funcs.doc:
CopticDaysInYear returns the number of days in the given Coptic year (365 or
366).

funcs.name: CopticLeapYear
funcs.doc:
CopticLeapYear reports whether the given Coptic year is a leap year (every
fourth year, with the leap day added to the thirteenth month).

funcs.name: CopticMonthName
funcs.doc: CopticMonthName returns the transliterated name of a Coptic month number.

funcs.name: CopticValid
funcs.signature: func CopticValid(year, month, day int) bool
funcs.doc: CopticValid reports whether year, month and day form a valid Coptic date.

funcs.name: DayDifference
funcs.doc: DayDifference returns the signed number of days from fixed1 to fixed2.

funcs.name: DayOfWeekFromFixed
funcs.doc:
DayOfWeekFromFixed returns the day-of-week index (0 = Sunday) of an RD fixed
day. It is the integer form of WeekdayFromFixed.

funcs.name: DayOfYearGregorian
funcs.doc:
DayOfYearGregorian returns the ordinal day number within the year (1 = 1
January) of an RD fixed day.

funcs.name: DaysInHebrewYear
funcs.doc:
DaysInHebrewYear returns the length in days of the given Hebrew year (353,
354, 355, 383, 384 or 385).

funcs.name: EpiphanyGregorian
funcs.doc:
EpiphanyGregorian returns the RD fixed day of Epiphany (6 January) in the
given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: EthiopicDaysInMonth
funcs.doc:
EthiopicDaysInMonth returns the number of days in the given month of the given
Ethiopic year.

funcs.name: EthiopicDaysInYear
funcs.doc: EthiopicDaysInYear returns the number of days in the given Ethiopic year.

funcs.name: EthiopicLeapYear
funcs.doc: EthiopicLeapYear reports whether the given Ethiopic year is a leap year.

funcs.name: EthiopicMonthName
funcs.doc: EthiopicMonthName returns the transliterated name of an Ethiopic month number.

funcs.name: EthiopicValid
funcs.signature: func EthiopicValid(year, month, day int) bool
funcs.doc: EthiopicValid reports whether year, month and day form a valid Ethiopic date.

funcs.name: FirstKDay
funcs.signature: func FirstKDay(k Weekday, fixed int) int
funcs.doc:
FirstKDay returns the RD of the first weekday k on or after the fixed anchor
day. It is a convenience wrapper for NthKDay(1, k, fixed).

funcs.name: FirstWeekdayOfMonth
funcs.signature: func FirstWeekdayOfMonth(k Weekday, year, month int) int
funcs.doc:
FirstWeekdayOfMonth returns the RD fixed day of the first given weekday in the
specified Gregorian month.

funcs.name: FixedFromCoptic
funcs.signature: func FixedFromCoptic(year, month, day int) int
funcs.doc: FixedFromCoptic converts a Coptic year, month and day to an RD fixed day.

funcs.name: FixedFromEthiopic
funcs.signature: func FixedFromEthiopic(year, month, day int) int
funcs.doc: FixedFromEthiopic converts an Ethiopic year, month and day to an RD fixed day.

funcs.name: FixedFromGregorian
funcs.signature: func FixedFromGregorian(year, month, day int) int
funcs.doc:
FixedFromGregorian converts a Gregorian year, month and day to an RD fixed
day.

funcs.name: FixedFromHebrew
funcs.signature: func FixedFromHebrew(year, month, day int) int
funcs.doc: FixedFromHebrew converts a Hebrew year, month and day to an RD fixed day.

funcs.name: FixedFromISO
funcs.signature: func FixedFromISO(year, week, day int) int
funcs.doc:
FixedFromISO converts an ISO week-date (year, week, weekday 1..7 with Monday
= 1) to an RD fixed day.

funcs.name: FixedFromIslamic
funcs.signature: func FixedFromIslamic(year, month, day int) int
funcs.doc: FixedFromIslamic converts an Islamic year, month and day to an RD fixed day.

funcs.name: FixedFromJDN
funcs.doc: FixedFromJDN converts a Julian Day Number to an RD fixed-day count.

funcs.name: FixedFromJulian
funcs.signature: func FixedFromJulian(year, month, day int) int
funcs.doc:
FixedFromJulian converts a Julian-calendar year, month and day to an RD fixed
day.

funcs.name: FixedFromMJD
funcs.doc: FixedFromMJD converts a Modified Julian Date to an RD fixed-day count.

funcs.name: FixedFromMoment
funcs.doc: FixedFromMoment returns the RD fixed day containing an RD moment (its floor).

funcs.name: FixedFromPersian
funcs.signature: func FixedFromPersian(year, month, day int) int
funcs.doc:
FixedFromPersian converts a Persian (Jalali) year, month and day to an RD
fixed day.

funcs.name: FixedFromUnixDay
funcs.doc:
FixedFromUnixDay converts a day count since the Unix epoch to an RD fixed-day
count.

funcs.name: FixedFromUnixSeconds
funcs.doc:
FixedFromUnixSeconds converts a count of Unix seconds to the RD fixed-day
count of the day containing that instant.

funcs.name: FloorDiv
funcs.doc:
FloorDiv returns the floor of a divided by b (rounded toward negative
infinity). It panics if b is zero.

funcs.name: FloorMod
funcs.doc:
FloorMod returns the mathematical modulus of a by b, carrying the sign of b.
It panics if b is zero.

funcs.name: GoodFriday
funcs.doc:
GoodFriday returns the RD fixed day of Good Friday (the Friday before Western
Easter) in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: GregorianDateDifference
funcs.signature: func GregorianDateDifference(y1, m1, d1, y2, m2, d2 int) int
funcs.doc:
GregorianDateDifference returns the number of days from the first Gregorian
date to the second (positive when the second date is later).

funcs.name: GregorianDayNumber
funcs.signature: func GregorianDayNumber(year, month, day int) int
funcs.doc:
GregorianDayNumber returns the ordinal day-of-year (1 = 1 January) of the
given Gregorian date.

funcs.name: GregorianDaysInMonth
funcs.doc:
GregorianDaysInMonth returns the number of days in the given Gregorian month
of the given year.

funcs.name: GregorianDaysInYear
funcs.doc:
GregorianDaysInYear returns the number of days in the given Gregorian year
(365 or 366).

funcs.name: GregorianDaysRemaining
funcs.signature: func GregorianDaysRemaining(year, month, day int) int
funcs.doc:
GregorianDaysRemaining returns the number of days remaining in the year after
the given Gregorian date.

funcs.name: GregorianEaster
funcs.doc:
GregorianEaster returns the RD fixed day of Western (Gregorian) Easter Sunday
in the given Gregorian year, computed via the Gregorian computus.

funcs.name: GregorianLeapYear
funcs.doc: GregorianLeapYear reports whether the given Gregorian year is a leap year.

funcs.name: GregorianMonthName
funcs.doc: GregorianMonthName returns the English name of a Gregorian month (1 = January).

funcs.name: GregorianNewYear
funcs.doc: GregorianNewYear returns the RD fixed day of 1 January of the given year.

funcs.name: GregorianValid
funcs.signature: func GregorianValid(year, month, day int) bool
funcs.doc:
GregorianValid reports whether year, month and day form a valid Gregorian
date.

funcs.name: GregorianYMD
funcs.signature: func GregorianYMD(fixed int) (year, month, day int)
funcs.doc:
GregorianYMD returns the year, month and day components of an RD fixed day as
a convenience over GregorianFromFixed.

funcs.name: GregorianYearEnd
funcs.doc: GregorianYearEnd returns the RD fixed day of 31 December of the given year.

funcs.name: GregorianYearFromFixed
funcs.doc: GregorianYearFromFixed returns the Gregorian year containing an RD fixed day.

funcs.name: HanukkahStart
funcs.doc:
HanukkahStart returns the RD fixed day of the first day of Hanukkah
(25 Kislev) occurring in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: HebrewCalendarElapsedDays
funcs.doc:
HebrewCalendarElapsedDays returns the number of days elapsed from the Hebrew
epoch to the mean conjunction (molad) of Tishri of the given year, applying
the traditional postponement (dehiyyah) of the new year.

funcs.name: HebrewDaysInMonth
funcs.doc: HebrewDaysInMonth is an alias for LastDayOfHebrewMonth.

funcs.name: HebrewLeapYear
funcs.doc:
HebrewLeapYear reports whether the given Hebrew year is embolismic (has a
thirteenth month), under the 19-year Metonic cycle.

funcs.name: HebrewMolad
funcs.doc:
HebrewMolad returns the RD moment (a real number of days since RD 0) of the
mean lunar conjunction — the molad — that begins the given Hebrew month.

funcs.name: HebrewMonthName
funcs.doc:
HebrewMonthName returns the English transliteration of a Hebrew month number.
In a leap year month 12 is "Adar I".

funcs.name: HebrewMonthsElapsed
funcs.doc:
HebrewMonthsElapsed returns the number of lunar months from the Hebrew epoch
to the start (molad) of the given month of the given Hebrew year.

funcs.name: HebrewNewYear
funcs.doc:
HebrewNewYear returns the RD fixed day of Rosh ha-Shanah (1 Tishri) of the
given Hebrew year.

funcs.name: HebrewNewYearDelay
funcs.doc:
HebrewNewYearDelay returns the year-length correction (0, 1 or 2 days) applied
to Rosh ha-Shanah so that the year has a permissible length.

funcs.name: HebrewValid
funcs.signature: func HebrewValid(year, month, day int) bool
funcs.doc: HebrewValid reports whether year, month and day form a valid Hebrew date.

funcs.name: HebrewYearFromGregorian
funcs.doc:
HebrewYearFromGregorian returns the Hebrew year whose autumn new year
(Rosh ha-Shanah) falls in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: ISOLongYear
funcs.doc:
ISOLongYear reports whether the given ISO week-numbering year is a "long"
year of 53 weeks (rather than 52).

funcs.name: ISOValid
funcs.signature: func ISOValid(year, week, day int) bool
funcs.doc: ISOValid reports whether year, week and day form a valid ISO week date.

funcs.name: ISOWeekOfYear
funcs.doc: ISOWeekOfYear returns the ISO week number of an RD fixed day.

funcs.name: ISOWeeksInYear
funcs.doc:
ISOWeeksInYear returns the number of ISO weeks (52 or 53) in the given ISO
week-numbering year.

funcs.name: ISOYearOf
funcs.doc:
ISOYearOf returns the ISO week-numbering year of an RD fixed day, which may
differ from the Gregorian year near a year boundary.

funcs.name: IsWeekday
funcs.doc: IsWeekday reports whether an RD fixed day falls on a Monday through Friday.

funcs.name: IsWeekend
funcs.doc: IsWeekend reports whether an RD fixed day falls on a Saturday or Sunday.

funcs.name: IslamicDaysInMonth
funcs.doc:
IslamicDaysInMonth returns the number of days in the given month of the given
Islamic year: odd months have 30 days, even months 29, and the twelfth month
has 30 in a leap year.

funcs.name: IslamicDaysInYear
funcs.doc:
IslamicDaysInYear returns the number of days in the given Islamic year (354 or
355).

funcs.name: IslamicLeapYear
funcs.doc:
IslamicLeapYear reports whether the given Islamic year is a leap year in the
tabular calendar (11 leap years in each 30-year cycle).

funcs.name: IslamicMonthName
funcs.doc:
IslamicMonthName returns the English transliteration of an Islamic month
number.

funcs.name: IslamicNewYear
funcs.doc:
IslamicNewYear returns the RD fixed day of 1 Muharram of the given Islamic
year.

funcs.name: IslamicValid
funcs.signature: func IslamicValid(year, month, day int) bool
funcs.doc:
IslamicValid reports whether year, month and day form a valid tabular Islamic
date.

funcs.name: JDFromMJD
funcs.doc: JDFromMJD converts a Modified Julian Date to a Julian Date.

funcs.name: JDFromMoment
funcs.doc: JDFromMoment converts an RD moment to a Julian Date.

funcs.name: JDNFromFixed
funcs.doc:
JDNFromFixed converts an RD fixed-day count to a Julian Day Number, the
integer count of days at noon since the start of the Julian Period.

funcs.name: JulianDaysInMonth
funcs.doc:
JulianDaysInMonth returns the number of days in the given month of the given
Julian-calendar year.

funcs.name: JulianDaysInYear
funcs.doc:
JulianDaysInYear returns the number of days in the given Julian-calendar year
(365 or 366).

funcs.name: JulianEaster
funcs.doc:
JulianEaster returns the RD fixed day of Orthodox (Julian) Easter Sunday in
the given year, computed via the Julian computus. The result is an RD fixed
day; convert with GregorianFromFixed to obtain the civil (Gregorian) date.

funcs.name: JulianLeapYear
funcs.doc:
JulianLeapYear reports whether the given Julian-calendar year is a leap year.
Every fourth year is a leap year; because there is no year zero, negative
years are leap when year mod 4 == 3.

funcs.name: JulianValid
funcs.signature: func JulianValid(year, month, day int) bool
funcs.doc:
JulianValid reports whether year, month and day form a valid Julian-calendar
date. Year zero is rejected.

funcs.name: JulianYearToAstronomical
funcs.doc:
JulianYearToAstronomical converts a Julian-calendar year that omits zero
(…, -1 = 1 BCE, 1 = 1 CE, …) to a continuous astronomical year number.

funcs.name: KDayAfter
funcs.signature: func KDayAfter(k Weekday, fixed int) int
funcs.doc:
KDayAfter returns the RD of the first day that is weekday k strictly after
the given fixed day.

funcs.name: KDayBefore
funcs.signature: func KDayBefore(k Weekday, fixed int) int
funcs.doc:
KDayBefore returns the RD of the last day that is weekday k strictly before
the given fixed day.

funcs.name: KDayNearest
funcs.signature: func KDayNearest(k Weekday, fixed int) int
funcs.doc:
KDayNearest returns the RD of the day that is weekday k nearest to the given
fixed day (ties resolve to the later day).

funcs.name: KDayOnOrAfter
funcs.signature: func KDayOnOrAfter(k Weekday, fixed int) int
funcs.doc:
KDayOnOrAfter returns the RD of the first day that is weekday k on or after
the given fixed day.

funcs.name: KDayOnOrBefore
funcs.signature: func KDayOnOrBefore(k Weekday, fixed int) int
funcs.doc:
KDayOnOrBefore returns the RD of the last day that is weekday k on or before
the given fixed day.

funcs.name: LastDayOfHebrewMonth
funcs.doc:
LastDayOfHebrewMonth returns the number of days (29 or 30) in the given month
of the given Hebrew year.

funcs.name: LastKDay
funcs.signature: func LastKDay(k Weekday, fixed int) int
funcs.doc:
LastKDay returns the RD of the last weekday k on or before the fixed anchor
day. It is a convenience wrapper for NthKDay(-1, k, fixed).

funcs.name: LastMonthOfHebrewYear
funcs.doc:
LastMonthOfHebrewYear returns the number of the final month of the Hebrew
year: 12 in a common year, 13 in a leap year.

funcs.name: LastWeekdayOfMonth
funcs.signature: func LastWeekdayOfMonth(k Weekday, year, month int) int
funcs.doc:
LastWeekdayOfMonth returns the RD fixed day of the last given weekday in the
specified Gregorian month.

funcs.name: LongMarheshvan
funcs.doc:
LongMarheshvan reports whether Marheshvan has 30 days in the given Hebrew
year (a "complete" year).

funcs.name: LunationNumber
funcs.doc:
LunationNumber returns the integer lunation index k whose mean New Moon is
nearest to the given Julian Ephemeris Day. It is the inverse of MeanNewMoonJD
rounded to the closest New Moon.

funcs.name: MJDFromFixed
funcs.doc: MJDFromFixed converts an RD fixed-day count to a Modified Julian Date.

funcs.name: MJDFromJD
funcs.doc: MJDFromJD converts a Julian Date to a Modified Julian Date.

funcs.name: MeanNewMoonJD
funcs.doc:
MeanNewMoonJD returns the Julian Ephemeris Day of the mean New Moon for the
given lunation index k, where k = 0 corresponds to the New Moon of 6 January
2000. Non-integer k of the form k+0.25, k+0.5, k+0.75 select the mean first
quarter, full moon and last quarter respectively (Meeus, chapter 49).

funcs.name: MeanSeasonJD
funcs.signature: func MeanSeasonJD(year int, s Season) float64
funcs.doc:
MeanSeasonJD returns the Julian Ephemeris Day of the mean (uncorrected)
equinox or solstice for the given Gregorian year, using Meeus's polynomial
(Astronomical Algorithms, chapter 27) valid for years -1000 to +3000.

funcs.name: MomentFromJD
funcs.doc:
MomentFromJD converts a (possibly fractional) Julian Date to an RD moment,
a real number of days since RD 0 at midnight.

funcs.name: MoonPhaseFixed
funcs.signature: func MoonPhaseFixed(k int, phase MoonPhase) int
funcs.doc:
MoonPhaseFixed returns the RD fixed day (in Terrestrial Time) of the requested
principal phase for the lunation nearest index k.

funcs.name: MoonPhaseJD
funcs.signature: func MoonPhaseJD(k int, phase MoonPhase) float64
funcs.doc:
MoonPhaseJD returns the Julian Ephemeris Day of the requested principal phase
for the lunation nearest index k. It applies the principal periodic
corrections from Meeus chapter 49 and is accurate to a few minutes for dates
near the present era.

funcs.name: NextMoonPhaseJD
funcs.signature: func NextMoonPhaseJD(jd float64, phase MoonPhase) float64
funcs.doc:
NextMoonPhaseJD returns the Julian Ephemeris Day of the first occurrence of
the requested principal phase on or after the given Julian Ephemeris Day.

funcs.name: Nowruz
funcs.doc:
Nowruz returns the RD fixed day of Nowruz (the Persian new year, 1 Farvardin)
occurring in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: NthKDay
funcs.signature: func NthKDay(n int, k Weekday, fixed int) int
funcs.doc:
NthKDay returns the RD of the n-th weekday k relative to the given fixed
anchor day. When n is positive it counts forward (n = 1 is the first k on or
after the anchor); when n is negative it counts backward (n = -1 is the last
k on or before the anchor). n must not be zero.

funcs.name: NthWeekdayOfMonth
funcs.signature: func NthWeekdayOfMonth(n int, k Weekday, year, month int) (int, bool)
funcs.doc:
NthWeekdayOfMonth returns the RD fixed day of the n-th given weekday in the
specified Gregorian month. When n is positive it counts from the start of the
month (n = 1 is the first such weekday); when n is negative it counts from the
end (n = -1 is the last such weekday). It returns 0 and false if there is no
such day (for example a fifth Monday that does not exist).

funcs.name: PalmSunday
funcs.doc:
PalmSunday returns the RD fixed day of Palm Sunday (the Sunday before Western
Easter) in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: Passover
funcs.doc:
Passover returns the RD fixed day of the first day of Passover (15 Nisan)
occurring in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: Pentecost
funcs.doc:
Pentecost returns the RD fixed day of Pentecost (49 days after Western Easter)
in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: PersianDaysInMonth
funcs.doc:
PersianDaysInMonth returns the number of days in the given month of the given
Persian year: 31 for the first six months, 30 for months 7–11, and 29 (or 30
in a leap year) for Esfand.

funcs.name: PersianDaysInYear
funcs.doc:
PersianDaysInYear returns the number of days in the given Persian year (365 or
366).

funcs.name: PersianLeapYear
funcs.doc:
PersianLeapYear reports whether the given Persian year is a leap year in the
arithmetical (2820-year) calendar.

funcs.name: PersianMonthName
funcs.doc: PersianMonthName returns the transliterated name of a Persian month number.

funcs.name: PersianValid
funcs.signature: func PersianValid(year, month, day int) bool
funcs.doc: PersianValid reports whether year, month and day form a valid Persian date.

funcs.name: PersianYearFromFixed
funcs.doc: PersianYearFromFixed returns the Persian year containing an RD fixed day.

funcs.name: Purim
funcs.doc:
Purim returns the RD fixed day of Purim occurring in the given Gregorian
year. Purim falls on 14 Adar in a common year and 14 Adar II in a leap year.

funcs.name: RamadanStart
funcs.doc:
RamadanStart returns the RD fixed day of 1 Ramadan of the given Islamic year
in the tabular calendar.

funcs.name: RoshHaShanah
funcs.doc:
RoshHaShanah returns the RD fixed day of Rosh ha-Shanah (1 Tishri) occurring
in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: SeasonFixed
funcs.signature: func SeasonFixed(year int, s Season) int
funcs.doc:
SeasonFixed returns the RD fixed day (in Terrestrial Time, at Greenwich) of
the apparent equinox or solstice for the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: SeasonJD
funcs.signature: func SeasonJD(year int, s Season) float64
funcs.doc:
SeasonJD returns the Julian Ephemeris Day of the apparent equinox or solstice
for the given Gregorian year, applying Meeus's periodic corrections to the
mean value. The result is accurate to roughly a minute for years near the
present and to within about an hour over the -1000 to +3000 range.

funcs.name: Shavuot
funcs.doc:
Shavuot returns the RD fixed day of Shavuot (6 Sivan) occurring in the given
Gregorian year.

funcs.name: ShortKislev
funcs.doc:
ShortKislev reports whether Kislev has 29 days in the given Hebrew year (a
"deficient" year).

funcs.name: SpringEquinoxJD
funcs.doc:
SpringEquinoxJD returns the Julian Ephemeris Day of the March equinox of the
given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: SubtractDays
funcs.doc: SubtractDays returns the RD fixed day n days before the given fixed day.

funcs.name: Sukkot
funcs.doc:
Sukkot returns the RD fixed day of the first day of Sukkot (15 Tishri)
occurring in the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: SummerSolsticeJD
funcs.doc:
SummerSolsticeJD returns the Julian Ephemeris Day of the June solstice of the
given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: TimeFromMoment
funcs.doc:
TimeFromMoment returns the fractional time-of-day component of an RD moment,
in the half-open interval [0, 1).

funcs.name: TuBShevat
funcs.doc:
TuBShevat returns the RD fixed day of Tu BiShvat (15 Shevat) occurring in the
given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: USElectionDay
funcs.doc:
USElectionDay returns the RD fixed day of United States federal Election Day
(the Tuesday after the first Monday in November) for the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: USIndependenceDay
funcs.doc:
USIndependenceDay returns the RD fixed day of United States Independence Day
(4 July) for the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: USLaborDay
funcs.doc:
USLaborDay returns the RD fixed day of United States Labor Day (the first
Monday in September) for the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: USMemorialDay
funcs.doc:
USMemorialDay returns the RD fixed day of United States Memorial Day (the last
Monday in May) for the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: USThanksgiving
funcs.doc:
USThanksgiving returns the RD fixed day of United States Thanksgiving (the
fourth Thursday in November) for the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: USThanksgivingCanada
funcs.doc:
USThanksgivingCanada returns the RD fixed day of Canadian Thanksgiving (the
second Monday in October) for the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: UnixDayFromFixed
funcs.doc:
UnixDayFromFixed converts an RD fixed-day count to the number of whole days
since the Unix epoch (1 January 1970).

funcs.name: UnixSecondsFromFixed
funcs.doc:
UnixSecondsFromFixed converts an RD fixed-day count (interpreted at midnight
UTC) to the corresponding number of Unix seconds.

funcs.name: WeekDifference
funcs.doc:
WeekDifference returns the signed number of whole weeks from fixed1 to fixed2
(truncated toward zero).

funcs.name: WeekOfYearGregorian
funcs.doc:
WeekOfYearGregorian returns the week number of an RD fixed day counting weeks
from 1 January, where the first week is week 1 and weeks are Sunday-based.

funcs.name: WinterSolsticeJD
funcs.doc:
WinterSolsticeJD returns the Julian Ephemeris Day of the December solstice of
the given Gregorian year.

funcs.name: YearMonthDayDifference
funcs.signature: func YearMonthDayDifference(fixed1, fixed2 int) (years, months, days int)
funcs.doc:
YearMonthDayDifference returns the difference between two RD fixed days broken
into whole Gregorian years, months and days, with fixed1 <= fixed2 assumed
for a non-negative result (the sign is preserved when reversed). It counts
complete calendar periods, matching the usual notion of "n years, m months
and d days between two dates".

funcs.name: YomKippur
funcs.doc:
YomKippur returns the RD fixed day of Yom Kippur (10 Tishri) occurring in the
given Gregorian year.

examples.name: GregorianEasterDate
examples.code:
fmt.Println("Western Easter 2024:", GregorianEasterDate(2024))
fmt.Println("Orthodox Easter 2024:", OrthodoxEasterDate(2024))

examples.name: GregorianFromFixed
examples.code:
fixed := FixedFromJDN(2451545)
d := GregorianFromFixed(fixed)
fmt.Printf("%s is a %s\n", d, d.Weekday())
fmt.Printf("JDN round-trips: %d\n", d.JDN())

examples.name: HebrewFromFixed
examples.code:
f := FixedFromGregorian(2024, 10, 3)
h := HebrewFromFixed(f)
fmt.Printf("%d %s %d\n", h.Day, HebrewMonthName(h.Year, h.Month), h.Year)

packages.name: cellular
packages.synopsis: Package cellular implements cellular automata in pure Go using only the standard library.
packages.doc:
Package cellular implements cellular automata in pure Go using only the
standard library.

The package covers the classical families of discrete dynamical systems
studied by Wolfram, Conway and others:

  - Elementary one-dimensional automata ([ElementaryRule]) with the standard
    Wolfram numbering 0–255, single-step and spacetime evolution, the four
    symmetry conjugates (mirror, complement and mirror-complement), the 88
    inequivalent equivalence classes, additivity and totalistic tests, the
    Langton lambda parameter and a Wolfram-class heuristic ([ElementaryRule.Class]).

  - k-state, r-radius totalistic ([TotalisticRule]) and outer-totalistic
    ([OuterTotalisticRule]) rules, together with fully general lookup-table
    rules ([GeneralRule]). All of these satisfy the [Rule1D] interface and are
    driven by the shared [Step1D] and [Evolve1D] evolvers under a selectable
    [Boundary] condition (periodic, fixed-zero, fixed-one or reflecting).

  - Two-dimensional life-like automata built from a [LifeRule] (a B/S
    rulestring such as "B3/S23") on a toroidal or bounded [Grid], with the
    Moore and von Neumann neighbourhoods and a library of named rules and
    starting patterns.

  - Reversible automata: second-order (Fredkin) elementary rules
    ([SecondOrderCA]) that run forwards and backwards exactly, and
    block/Margolus rules ([MargolusRule]) whose reversibility is guaranteed by
    using a permutation of the block alphabet.

  - Spacetime-diagram utilities: ASCII rendering, densities, Hamming distance
    and damage spreading, and Shannon/block entropies used both for analysis
    and for the class heuristics.

States are represented as []int slices (values 0..k-1) for one-dimensional
automata and by the [Grid] type in two dimensions. No third-party packages,
randomness sources or cgo are used; where pseudo-random initial conditions are
needed they are produced by a small deterministic splitmix64 generator seeded
by an explicit uint64 so that every result in the package is reproducible.

vars.signature: var ErrBadState = errors.New("cellular: cell value out of range")
vars.doc: ErrBadState is returned when a cell value falls outside the range 0..k-1.

types.name: Boundary
types.signature: type Boundary int
types.doc:
Boundary selects how cells beyond the ends of a one-dimensional lattice (or
the borders of a two-dimensional grid) are read when applying a rule.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a human-readable name for the boundary condition.

types.name: ElementaryRule
types.signature: type ElementaryRule uint8
types.doc:
ElementaryRule is an elementary (k=2, r=1) one-dimensional cellular-automaton
rule identified by its Wolfram number 0..255. Bit p of the number (for a
neighbourhood value p = 4*left + 2*centre + right) gives the next value of the
centre cell.

funcs.name: AdditiveElementaryRules
funcs.doc:
AdditiveElementaryRules returns the eight additive (GF(2)-linear) elementary
rules in increasing order.

funcs.name: AllElementaryRules
funcs.doc: AllElementaryRules returns all 256 elementary rules in numeric order.

funcs.name: InequivalentElementaryRules
funcs.doc:
InequivalentElementaryRules returns the 88 canonical elementary rules, one per
equivalence class under reflection and complementation, in numeric order.

funcs.name: MustElementaryRule
funcs.doc:
MustElementaryRule is like NewElementaryRule but panics on an out-of-range
number. It is intended for package-level rule constants and tests.

funcs.name: NewElementaryRule
funcs.doc:
NewElementaryRule returns the ElementaryRule with Wolfram number n. It returns
an error if n is not in the range 0..255.

funcs.name: Rule102
funcs.doc:
Rule102 returns elementary rule 102, an additive rule mirror-equivalent to
rule 60.

funcs.name: Rule110
funcs.doc: Rule110 returns elementary rule 110, proved capable of universal computation.

funcs.name: Rule126
funcs.doc: Rule126 returns elementary rule 126, a chaotic rule.

funcs.name: Rule150
funcs.doc:
Rule150 returns elementary rule 150, the additive sum-mod-2 rule of all three
neighbours.

funcs.name: Rule18
funcs.doc:
Rule18 returns elementary rule 18, a chaotic rule generating a Sierpinski-like
fractal.

funcs.name: Rule184
funcs.doc: Rule184 returns elementary rule 184, the traffic/ballistic-annihilation rule.

funcs.name: Rule22
funcs.doc: Rule22 returns elementary rule 22, a chaotic outer-totalistic rule.

funcs.name: Rule250
funcs.doc: Rule250 returns elementary rule 250, which fills space from a single seed.

funcs.name: Rule30
funcs.doc:
Rule30 returns elementary rule 30, a chaotic, additive-looking rule used as a
random-number source.

funcs.name: Rule4
funcs.doc: Rule4 returns elementary rule 4, a class-2 rule whose isolated cells persist.

funcs.name: Rule45
funcs.doc:
Rule45 returns elementary rule 45, a chaotic rule often used for random
generation.

funcs.name: Rule54
funcs.doc:
Rule54 returns elementary rule 54, a class-4 candidate with particle-like
structures.

funcs.name: Rule60
funcs.doc: Rule60 returns elementary rule 60, an additive (Pascal-triangle mod 2) rule.

funcs.name: Rule90
funcs.doc:
Rule90 returns elementary rule 90, the additive XOR rule that draws the
Sierpinski triangle from a single seed.

funcs.name: RuleFromBits
funcs.doc:
RuleFromBits builds an elementary rule from an 8-entry output table indexed by
neighbourhood value 0..7. It returns an error if bits has the wrong length or
contains values other than 0 and 1.

funcs.name: SierpinskiRule
funcs.doc:
SierpinskiRule is an alias for Rule90, whose single-seed evolution is the
Sierpinski triangle.

funcs.name: TotalisticElementaryRules
funcs.doc:
TotalisticElementaryRules returns the elementary rules that are totalistic in
the strict sense (output depends only on the neighbourhood sum).

funcs.name: TrafficRule
funcs.doc: TrafficRule is an alias for Rule184, the elementary traffic model.

methods.name: ActiveCount
methods.doc:
ActiveCount returns the number of neighbourhoods that map to 1 (the population
count of the rule number).

methods.name: Apply
methods.doc:
Apply implements the Rule1D interface for a length-3 neighbourhood slice
(left, centre, right).

methods.name: ApplyLCR
methods.signature: func (r ElementaryRule) ApplyLCR(left, centre, right int) int
methods.doc:
ApplyLCR returns the next centre value for the ordered neighbourhood
(left, centre, right), each 0 or 1.

methods.name: Bit
methods.doc:
Bit returns the output bit (0 or 1) for the neighbourhood value p in 0..7,
where p = 4*left + 2*centre + right.

methods.name: Class
methods.doc:
Class returns a heuristic Wolfram class (1, 2 or 3) for an elementary rule,
obtained by evolving a fixed reproducible pseudo-random configuration on a
prime-width ring and observing the asymptotic behaviour:

  - Class 1: the configuration collapses to a spatially uniform state.
  - Class 2: it settles into a short-period cycle (period at most 32).
  - Class 3: it remains aperiodic within the observation window (chaotic).

The genuinely complex rules that Wolfram places in class 4 (such as rule 110)
are not separated from class 3 by this simple heuristic and are reported as
class 3; use it as an indicative, not definitive, classifier.

methods.name: ComplementRule
methods.doc:
ComplementRule returns the rule obtained by exchanging the roles of 0 and 1 in
both inputs and outputs. Its evolution is the bitwise complement of r's
evolution from the complemented initial condition.

methods.name: EquivalenceClassSize
methods.doc:
EquivalenceClassSize returns the number of distinct rules in r's equivalence
class (1, 2 or 4).

methods.name: Equivalents
methods.doc:
Equivalents returns the sorted set of rules in the same equivalence class as r
under left-right reflection and 0/1 complementation (1, 2 or 4 members).

methods.name: IsAdditive
methods.doc:
IsAdditive reports whether the rule is additive (linear over GF(2)): its
output equals a fixed XOR combination of the three neighbourhood cells. The
additive elementary rules are 0, 60, 90, 102, 150, 170, 204 and 240.

methods.name: IsCanonical
methods.doc:
IsCanonical reports whether r is the minimal representative of its equivalence
class.

methods.name: IsLegal
methods.doc:
IsLegal reports whether the rule is "legal" in Wolfram's original sense:
quiescent (blank stays blank) and left-right symmetric.

methods.name: IsOuterTotalistic
methods.doc:
IsOuterTotalistic reports whether the rule depends only on the centre cell and
the sum of its two neighbours (left+right), rather than on their individual
values.

methods.name: IsQuiescent
methods.doc:
IsQuiescent reports whether the all-zero neighbourhood maps to 0, so that a
blank background stays blank.

methods.name: IsSymmetric
methods.doc:
IsSymmetric reports whether the rule is invariant under left-right reflection,
i.e. it equals its own mirror rule.

methods.name: IsTotalistic
methods.doc:
