for which the locator polynomial lambda evaluates to zero at alpha^{-i}.

methods.name: Div
methods.doc: Div returns a/b in GF(2^m). It panics if b is zero.

methods.name: Elements
methods.doc: Elements returns a fresh slice of all 2^m field elements in ascending order.

methods.name: Exp
methods.doc:
Exp returns alpha^e, the e-th power of the field's primitive element. e may
be any integer and is reduced modulo 2^m-1.

methods.name: Inv
methods.doc:
Inv returns the multiplicative inverse of a in GF(2^m). It panics if a is
zero.

methods.name: Log
methods.doc:
Log returns the discrete logarithm of a non-zero element a to base alpha,
i.e. the e in [0, 2^m-1) with alpha^e = a. It panics if a is zero.

methods.name: M
methods.doc: M returns the extension degree m of GF(2^m).

methods.name: MinimalPoly
methods.doc:
MinimalPoly returns the minimal polynomial over GF(2) of the element
alpha^e, packed as a GF(2) polynomial int (bit i is the coefficient of x^i).
It is the product over the cyclotomic coset of e of (x - alpha^s).

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns a*b in GF(2^m) via log/antilog tables.

methods.name: Order
methods.doc: Order returns the multiplicative order 2^m-1 of the field's cyclic group.

methods.name: Poly
methods.doc:
Poly returns the primitive polynomial (with the x^m bit set) defining the
field.

methods.name: PolyAdd
methods.doc: PolyAdd returns the sum of two polynomials over GF(2^m).

methods.name: PolyDegree
methods.doc:
PolyDegree returns the degree of a polynomial over GF(2^m). The zero
polynomial has degree -1.

methods.name: PolyDeriv
methods.doc:
PolyDeriv returns the formal derivative of p over GF(2^m). Because the field
has characteristic two, coefficients of even-power terms vanish.

methods.name: PolyDivMod
methods.doc:
PolyDivMod divides a by the non-zero polynomial b over GF(2^m), returning the
quotient and remainder with deg(remainder) < deg(b). It panics if b is zero.

methods.name: PolyEqual
methods.doc:
PolyEqual reports whether a and b are the same polynomial, ignoring trailing
zero coefficients.

methods.name: PolyEval
methods.doc:
PolyEval evaluates the polynomial p at the field element x using Horner's
rule.

methods.name: PolyMod
methods.doc: PolyMod returns a modulo the non-zero polynomial b over GF(2^m).

methods.name: PolyMonic
methods.doc:
PolyMonic returns p scaled so its leading coefficient is one. The zero
polynomial is returned unchanged.

methods.name: PolyMul
methods.doc: PolyMul returns the product of two polynomials over GF(2^m).

methods.name: PolyScale
methods.doc: PolyScale multiplies every coefficient of p by the scalar s in GF(2^m).

methods.name: PolySub
methods.doc:
PolySub returns the difference of two polynomials; over GF(2^m) this equals
PolyAdd.

methods.name: Pow
methods.doc:
Pow returns a raised to the integer power e in GF(2^m); e may be negative.
Pow(0, 0) is defined to be 1 and Pow(0, e) for e>0 is 0.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc: Size returns the number of elements 2^m in the field.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns a-b in GF(2^m); over characteristic two this equals Add.

methods.name: Trace
methods.doc:
Trace returns the field trace Tr(a) = a + a^2 + a^4 + ... + a^(2^(m-1)) of an
element a, an element of GF(2) (0 or 1).

types.name: GolayCode
types.doc:
GolayCode is the perfect binary Golay code together with a complete
syndrome-to-error table for exact correction of up to three bit errors.

funcs.name: NewGolayCode
funcs.doc:
NewGolayCode returns the perfect binary Golay(23,12) code with its decoding
table precomputed.

methods.name: Cyclic
methods.doc: Cyclic returns the underlying cyclic code.

methods.name: Decode
methods.signature: func (g *GolayCode) Decode(word []int) (corrected []int, numErrors int, err error)
methods.doc:
Decode corrects up to three bit errors in a received 23-bit word and returns
the corrected code word and the number of errors corrected.

methods.name: DecodeMessage
methods.signature: func (g *GolayCode) DecodeMessage(word []int) (msg []int, numErrors int, err error)
methods.doc: DecodeMessage decodes a received 23-bit word and returns the 12 message bits.

methods.name: Encode
methods.doc: Encode systematically encodes a 12-bit message into a 23-bit Golay code word.

methods.name: K
methods.doc: K returns the message dimension 12.

methods.name: MinimumDistance
methods.doc: MinimumDistance returns the minimum distance 7 of the Golay(23,12) code.

methods.name: N
methods.doc: N returns the code length 23.

methods.name: Syndrome
methods.doc: Syndrome returns the 11-bit syndrome of a received 23-bit word.

types.name: HammingCode
types.doc:
HammingCode is a general binary Hamming code of length n = 2^r-1 with
dimension k = n-r and minimum distance 3, correcting any single bit error.
The parity-check matrix has as its columns all non-zero r-bit vectors in
ascending numeric order, so the syndrome of a single error equals the binary
index (1-based) of the erroneous position.

funcs.name: NewHammingCode
funcs.doc:
NewHammingCode returns the Hamming code with r parity bits (r >= 2), of
length 2^r-1 and dimension 2^r-1-r. It returns ErrFieldParam for r < 2.

methods.name: Decode
methods.signature: func (c *HammingCode) Decode(word []int) (corrected []int, errPos int, err error)
methods.doc:
Decode corrects up to one bit error in a received length-n word and returns
the corrected code word together with the error position (-1 if none).

methods.name: K
methods.doc: K returns the message dimension 2^r-1-r.

methods.name: LinearCode
methods.doc:
LinearCode converts the Hamming code into a general LinearCode built from its
parity-check matrix.

methods.name: N
methods.doc: N returns the code length 2^r-1.

methods.name: ParityCheck
methods.doc: ParityCheck returns a copy of the r-by-n parity-check matrix.

methods.name: R
methods.doc: R returns the number of parity bits r.

methods.name: Syndrome
methods.doc: Syndrome returns the r-bit syndrome H*wᵀ of a received length-n word.

methods.name: SyndromePosition
methods.doc:
SyndromePosition returns the 0-based position of a single bit error implied
by a received word, or -1 if the syndrome is zero. The syndrome, read as a
big-endian binary number, is the 1-based column index of the error.

types.name: HuffmanNode
types.doc:
HuffmanNode is a node of a Huffman code tree. Leaves carry a symbol and have
nil children; internal nodes have two children and a Symbol of -1.

funcs.name: BuildHuffmanTree
funcs.doc:
BuildHuffmanTree builds a Huffman tree for the given symbol weights (symbol
id -> positive weight). Ties are broken deterministically by weight then
symbol id so the tree is reproducible. It returns ErrHuffman if there are
fewer than one symbol or a non-positive weight.

methods.name: IsLeaf
methods.doc: IsLeaf reports whether the node is a leaf.

types.name: LDPCCode
types.doc:
LDPCCode wraps a binary parity-check matrix H (m rows by n columns) over
GF(2). A length-n word c is a code word exactly when H*cᵀ = 0.

funcs.name: NewLDPCCode
funcs.doc:
NewLDPCCode builds an LDPC code from a rectangular binary parity-check matrix.
It returns ErrShape on an empty or ragged matrix and ErrBit on non-bit entries.

methods.name: BitFlipDecode
methods.signature: func (c *LDPCCode) BitFlipDecode(received []int, maxIter int) (decoded []int, iters int, ok bool, err error)
methods.doc:
BitFlipDecode runs Gallager's hard-decision bit-flipping algorithm for up to
maxIter iterations, flipping in each round every bit that fails a majority of
its parity checks. It returns the decoded word, the number of iterations
used, and whether all checks were satisfied. The received word must have
length n.

methods.name: CheckDegrees
methods.doc: CheckDegrees returns the row weights (check-node degrees) of H.

methods.name: Density
methods.doc: Density returns the fraction of ones in the parity-check matrix.

methods.name: IsCodeword
methods.doc: IsCodeword reports whether a length-n word satisfies all parity checks.

methods.name: IsRegular
methods.doc:
IsRegular reports whether every variable node has the same degree and every
check node has the same degree.

methods.name: M
methods.doc: M returns the number of parity checks (check nodes).

methods.name: N
methods.doc: N returns the code length (number of variable nodes).

methods.name: ParityCheck
methods.doc: ParityCheck returns a copy of the parity-check matrix.

methods.name: Syndrome
methods.doc: Syndrome returns the length-m syndrome H*wᵀ of a received length-n word.

methods.name: VariableDegrees
methods.doc: VariableDegrees returns the column weights (variable-node degrees) of H.

types.name: LinearCode
types.doc:
LinearCode is a binary linear [n,k] block code over GF(2) described by a
k-by-n generator matrix G and an (n-k)-by-n parity-check matrix H with
G*Hᵀ = 0. Messages of length k are encoded as m*G; a received word r is a
code word exactly when H*rᵀ = 0.

funcs.name: NewLinearCode
funcs.doc:
NewLinearCode builds a linear code from a k-by-n generator matrix G. G must
be non-empty, rectangular and full row rank; the parity-check matrix is
derived as a basis of the null space of G. It returns ErrShape or ErrRank on
invalid input.

funcs.name: NewLinearCodeGH
funcs.doc:
NewLinearCodeGH builds a linear code from explicit generator and parity-check
matrices. It verifies that shapes are consistent and G*Hᵀ = 0.

methods.name: Codewords
methods.doc:
Codewords returns all 2^k code words in message-counting order. It is
intended for small codes; it allocates 2^k slices.

methods.name: DecodeSyndrome
methods.doc:
DecodeSyndrome performs syndrome (coset-leader) decoding of a received word
using a table built by SyndromeTable, returning the most likely code word.
It returns ErrLength on a bad word length.

methods.name: Encode
methods.doc:
Encode encodes a length-k message into a length-n code word as m*G. It
returns ErrLength if the message length is not k.

methods.name: Generator
methods.doc: Generator returns a copy of the generator matrix.

methods.name: IsCodeword
methods.doc:
IsCodeword reports whether a length-n word is a valid code word (zero
syndrome).

methods.name: K
methods.doc: K returns the message dimension k.

methods.name: MinimumDistance
methods.doc:
MinimumDistance returns the minimum Hamming distance of the code, computed as
the minimum weight over all non-zero code words. It is exponential in k and
meant for small codes.

methods.name: N
methods.doc: N returns the code length n.

methods.name: ParityCheck
methods.doc: ParityCheck returns a copy of the parity-check matrix.

methods.name: Rate
methods.doc: Rate returns the code rate k/n.

methods.name: Redundancy
methods.doc: Redundancy returns the number of parity checks n-k.

methods.name: Syndrome
methods.doc:
Syndrome returns the syndrome H*rᵀ of a received length-n word. It returns
ErrLength if the word length is not n.

methods.name: SyndromeTable
methods.doc:
SyndromeTable builds a standard-array coset-leader table mapping each
syndrome (packed as a big-endian integer) to a minimum-weight error pattern.
It is exponential in n and intended for small codes.

types.name: ReedSolomon
types.doc:
ReedSolomon is a Reed-Solomon code over GF(2^m). It has length n = 2^m-1,
dimension k = n - nsym and can correct up to nsym/2 symbol errors. The nsym
generator roots are consecutive powers alpha^fcr … alpha^(fcr+nsym-1) of the
field's primitive element.

Messages and code words are big-endian []int symbol slices: index 0 holds
the first (highest-order) symbol. Systematic code words carry the k message
symbols first followed by nsym parity symbols.

funcs.name: NewReedSolomon
funcs.doc:
NewReedSolomon returns a Reed-Solomon code over the given field with nsym
parity symbols and first consecutive root exponent fcr=0. It returns
ErrFieldParam if nsym is not in [1, 2^m-1].

funcs.name: NewReedSolomonFCR
funcs.signature: func NewReedSolomonFCR(field *Field, nsym, fcr int) (*ReedSolomon, error)
funcs.doc:
NewReedSolomonFCR returns a Reed-Solomon code with nsym parity symbols and a
configurable first-consecutive-root exponent fcr.

methods.name: Correction
methods.doc:
Correction returns the maximum number of symbol errors the code can correct,
nsym/2.

methods.name: Decode
methods.signature: func (rs *ReedSolomon) Decode(received []int) (corrected []int, numErrors int, err error)
methods.doc:
Decode corrects up to nsym/2 symbol errors in a received length-n word,
returning the corrected code word and the number of corrected symbols. It
returns ErrTooManyErrors when the error pattern is uncorrectable.

methods.name: DecodeMessage
methods.signature: func (rs *ReedSolomon) DecodeMessage(received []int) (msg []int, numErrors int, err error)
methods.doc:
DecodeMessage decodes a received word and returns the k recovered message
symbols.

methods.name: Encode
methods.doc:
Encode systematically encodes a length-k message into a length-n code word.
It returns ErrLength if the message length is not k.

methods.name: FCR
methods.doc: FCR returns the first-consecutive-root exponent.

methods.name: Field
methods.doc: Field returns the underlying field.

methods.name: GeneratorPoly
methods.doc:
GeneratorPoly returns the generator polynomial as a big-endian symbol slice
(index 0 is the highest-degree coefficient).

methods.name: IsCodeword
methods.doc: IsCodeword reports whether a received length-n word has all-zero syndromes.

methods.name: K
methods.doc: K returns the message dimension n-nsym.

methods.name: N
methods.doc: N returns the code length 2^m-1.

methods.name: NSym
methods.doc: NSym returns the number of parity symbols.

methods.name: Syndromes
methods.doc:
Syndromes returns the nsym syndrome values S_j = R(alpha^(fcr+j)) of a
received length-n word, in order j = 0 … nsym-1.

funcs.name: ArithmeticDecode
funcs.signature: func ArithmeticDecode(bits []int, freqs []int, count int) ([]int, error)
funcs.doc:
ArithmeticDecode decodes exactly count symbols from a bit slice produced by
ArithmeticEncode under the same frequency model.

funcs.name: ArithmeticEncode
funcs.doc:
ArithmeticEncode encodes a sequence of symbols (each an index into freqs)
under the static frequency model freqs, returning the compressed bit slice.
Frequencies must be positive and sum to less than 2^30. It returns ErrHuffman
for an invalid model or out-of-range symbol.

funcs.name: BinaryToGray
funcs.doc:
BinaryToGray converts a non-negative integer to its reflected binary Gray
code, in which consecutive values differ in exactly one bit.

funcs.name: BitsToPoly
funcs.doc:
BitsToPoly converts a little-endian bit slice (index i = coefficient of x^i)
into a GF(2) polynomial packed as an int.

funcs.name: BitsToUint
funcs.doc:
BitsToUint packs a bit slice (most-significant bit first) into a uint64. It
panics if the slice is longer than 64 entries.

funcs.name: BlockDeinterleave
funcs.signature: func BlockDeinterleave(data []int, rows, cols int) ([]int, error)
funcs.doc:
BlockDeinterleave inverts BlockInterleave, restoring the original row-major
order. The input length must equal rows*cols.

funcs.name: BlockInterleave
funcs.signature: func BlockInterleave(data []int, rows, cols int) ([]int, error)
funcs.doc:
BlockInterleave writes the input row by row into a rows-by-cols matrix and
reads it out column by column, returning the interleaved sequence. The input
length must equal rows*cols.

funcs.name: BuildHuffmanCodes
funcs.doc:
BuildHuffmanCodes is a convenience wrapper returning the code map directly
from symbol weights.

funcs.name: CumulativeFreqs
funcs.doc:
CumulativeFreqs returns the cumulative-frequency table of a frequency slice:
a slice of length len(freqs)+1 where entry i is the sum of freqs[:i]. The
final entry is the total frequency.

funcs.name: DotGF2
funcs.doc:
DotGF2 returns the GF(2) inner product (parity of the pairwise ands) of two
equal-length bit vectors. It panics on a length mismatch.

funcs.name: ExtendedGolayDecode
funcs.signature: func ExtendedGolayDecode(g *GolayCode, word []int) (msg []int, numErrors int, err error)
funcs.doc:
ExtendedGolayDecode decodes a 24-bit extended Golay code word. It corrects up
to three errors using the inner Golay code and reports the number of errors
corrected in the 23-bit prefix.

funcs.name: ExtendedGolayEncode
funcs.doc:
ExtendedGolayEncode encodes a 12-bit message into a 24-bit extended Golay
code word by appending an overall even-parity bit to the Golay(23,12) code
word.

funcs.name: ExtendedHamming74Decode
funcs.signature: func ExtendedHamming74Decode(word []int) (data []int, doubleError bool, err error)
funcs.doc:
ExtendedHamming74Decode decodes an eight-bit extended Hamming code word. It
corrects any single error and flags (via doubleError) an uncorrectable
double error detected by the overall parity bit.

funcs.name: ExtendedHamming74Encode
funcs.doc:
ExtendedHamming74Encode encodes four data bits into an eight-bit extended
Hamming(8,4) SECDED code word by appending an overall even-parity bit to the
Hamming(7,4) code word.

funcs.name: GF2PolyAdd
funcs.doc:
GF2PolyAdd returns the sum a+b of two GF(2) polynomials, which equals their
bitwise exclusive-or.

funcs.name: GF2PolyDegree
funcs.doc:
GF2PolyDegree returns the degree of the GF(2) polynomial p, i.e. the index
of its highest set bit. The zero polynomial has degree -1.

funcs.name: GF2PolyDiv
funcs.doc:
GF2PolyDiv returns the quotient of the GF(2) polynomial a divided by the
non-zero polynomial b.

funcs.name: GF2PolyDivMod
funcs.signature: func GF2PolyDivMod(a, b int) (quotient, remainder int)
funcs.doc:
GF2PolyDivMod divides the GF(2) polynomial a by the non-zero polynomial b
and returns the quotient and remainder, so that a = quotient*b + remainder
with GF2PolyDegree(remainder) < GF2PolyDegree(b). It panics if b is zero.

funcs.name: GF2PolyEval
funcs.doc:
GF2PolyEval evaluates the GF(2) polynomial p at the bit value x (0 or 1),
returning 0 or 1. For x=0 it is the constant term; for x=1 it is the parity
of the number of terms.

funcs.name: GF2PolyGCD
funcs.doc:
GF2PolyGCD returns a greatest common divisor of the GF(2) polynomials a and
b using the Euclidean algorithm. GF2PolyGCD(0,0) is 0.

funcs.name: GF2PolyMod
funcs.doc:
GF2PolyMod returns the remainder of the GF(2) polynomial a modulo the
non-zero polynomial b.

funcs.name: GF2PolyMul
funcs.doc: GF2PolyMul returns the carry-less product a*b of two GF(2) polynomials.

funcs.name: GF2PolyMulMod
funcs.signature: func GF2PolyMulMod(a, b, m int) int
funcs.doc: GF2PolyMulMod returns (a*b) mod m for GF(2) polynomials, with m non-zero.

funcs.name: GF2PolyPowMod
funcs.signature: func GF2PolyPowMod(base, exp, m int) int
funcs.doc:
GF2PolyPowMod returns (base^exp) mod m for GF(2) polynomials using
square-and-multiply. exp must be non-negative and m non-zero.

funcs.name: GF2PolyString
funcs.doc:
GF2PolyString renders a GF(2) polynomial in descending powers of x, e.g.
"x^3 + x + 1". The zero polynomial renders as "0".

funcs.name: GF2PolySub
funcs.doc:
GF2PolySub returns the difference a-b of two GF(2) polynomials. Over GF(2)
subtraction and addition coincide, so this equals GF2PolyAdd(a, b).

funcs.name: GallagerLDPC
funcs.signature: func GallagerLDPC(n, wc, wr int, seed int64) ([][]int, error)
funcs.doc:
GallagerLDPC constructs a regular (wc, wr) Gallager parity-check matrix of
length n. It requires n to be a multiple of wr and returns an m-by-n matrix
with m = wc*n/wr, every column of weight wc and every row of weight wr. The
seed makes the random column permutations of the sub-bands reproducible.

funcs.name: GrayBitsToBinary
funcs.doc:
GrayBitsToBinary converts a fixed-width Gray-code bit slice (most-significant
bit first) back to its integer value.

funcs.name: GrayCodeBits
funcs.doc:
GrayCodeBits returns the Gray code of value with a fixed width of nbits,
most-significant bit first.

funcs.name: GrayCodeSequence
funcs.doc:
GrayCodeSequence returns the length-2^n reflected Gray code sequence as a
slice of integers, where consecutive entries differ in exactly one bit.

funcs.name: GrayCodeTable
funcs.doc:
GrayCodeTable returns the length-2^n reflected Gray code sequence as bit
slices of width n (most-significant bit first).

funcs.name: GrayToBinary
funcs.doc:
GrayToBinary converts a reflected binary Gray code back to the ordinary
binary integer it encodes.

funcs.name: GrayToNary
funcs.doc:
GrayToNary inverts NaryToGray, converting modular n-ary Gray digits
(most-significant first) back to ordinary base-radix digits.

funcs.name: HadamardDecode
funcs.doc:
HadamardDecode maximum-likelihood decodes a length-2^m Hadamard code word by
mapping bits to +/-1 and taking the fast Walsh-Hadamard transform: the index
of the largest-magnitude coefficient is the message. It returns the recovered
m message bits. The word length must be a power of two.

funcs.name: HadamardEncode
funcs.doc:
HadamardEncode encodes an m-bit message into a length-2^m binary Hadamard
code word: the bit at position x equals the GF(2) inner product of the
message with the binary expansion of x. The code is linear [2^m, m, 2^{m-1}].

funcs.name: HadamardMatrix
funcs.doc:
HadamardMatrix returns the 2^k-by-2^k Sylvester-Hadamard matrix with entries
+1 and -1, built recursively from H_1 = [[1]] and the Kronecker doubling
H_{2n} = [[H,H],[H,-H]].

funcs.name: Hamming74Decode
funcs.signature: func Hamming74Decode(word []int) (data, corrected []int, errPos int, err error)
funcs.doc:
Hamming74Decode corrects up to one bit error in a received seven-bit word and
returns the four recovered data bits along with the corrected code word and
the (0-based) error position, which is -1 when the syndrome is zero.

funcs.name: Hamming74Encode
funcs.doc:
Hamming74Encode encodes four data bits into a seven-bit systematic
Hamming(7,4) code word. It returns ErrLength if len(data) != 4.

funcs.name: Hamming74Generator
funcs.doc:
Hamming74Generator returns the 4-by-7 generator matrix of the systematic
Hamming(7,4) code with layout [data | parity]. Row i encodes the i-th data
bit together with the parity bits it participates in.

funcs.name: Hamming74ParityCheck
funcs.doc:
Hamming74ParityCheck returns the 3-by-7 parity-check matrix matching
Hamming74Generator.

funcs.name: Hamming74Syndrome
funcs.doc:
Hamming74Syndrome returns the three-bit syndrome of a received Hamming(7,4)
word. A zero syndrome indicates no detected single-bit error.

funcs.name: HammingDistance
funcs.doc:
HammingDistance returns the number of positions in which a and b differ. It
panics if the slices have different lengths.

funcs.name: HammingDistanceUint
funcs.doc: HammingDistanceUint returns the number of differing bits between a and b.

funcs.name: HammingWeight
funcs.doc: HammingWeight returns the number of non-zero entries of v.

funcs.name: HammingWeightUint
funcs.doc: HammingWeightUint returns the population count of an unsigned integer's bits.

funcs.name: HuffmanAverageLength
funcs.signature: func HuffmanAverageLength(codes map[int][]int, weights map[int]int) float64
funcs.doc:
HuffmanAverageLength returns the expected code-word length in bits per symbol
for the given code map and symbol weights.

funcs.name: HuffmanCodes
funcs.doc:
HuffmanCodes walks a Huffman tree and returns the code words as bit slices
keyed by symbol id (left edge = 0, right edge = 1).

funcs.name: HuffmanDecode
funcs.doc:
HuffmanDecode decodes a bit slice back into symbols using a Huffman tree. It
returns ErrHuffman if the bits do not correspond to a sequence of complete
code words.

funcs.name: HuffmanEncode
funcs.doc:
HuffmanEncode encodes a symbol sequence into a bit slice using the given code
map. It returns ErrHuffman if a symbol has no code.

funcs.name: IdentityGF2
funcs.doc: IdentityGF2 returns the n-by-n identity matrix over GF(2).

funcs.name: Interleave
funcs.signature: func Interleave(data []int, branches, delay int) ([]int, error)
funcs.doc:
Interleave runs a fresh convolutional interleaver over an entire sequence and
returns the emitted sequence of equal length.

funcs.name: InterleavePermutation
funcs.doc:
InterleavePermutation returns the permutation applied by a rows-by-cols block
interleaver, i.e. out[i] = input[perm[i]].

funcs.name: InverseWalshHadamardTransform
funcs.doc:
InverseWalshHadamardTransform returns the inverse Walsh-Hadamard transform,
i.e. the forward transform divided by len(a).

funcs.name: InvertPermutation
funcs.doc: InvertPermutation returns the inverse of a permutation.

funcs.name: IsPowerOfTwo
funcs.doc: IsPowerOfTwo reports whether n is a positive power of two.

funcs.name: MajorityVote
funcs.doc: MajorityVote returns the majority bit of a bit slice; ties resolve to 0.

funcs.name: MatMulGF2
funcs.doc:
MatMulGF2 returns the product a*b of two GF(2) matrices. It panics on a
dimension mismatch.

funcs.name: MatVecGF2
funcs.doc:
MatVecGF2 multiplies the GF(2) matrix m (rows of equal length) by the column
vector v, returning m*v over GF(2). It panics on a dimension mismatch.

funcs.name: NaryToGray
funcs.doc:
NaryToGray converts a value expressed as base-radix digits (most-significant
digit first) to its modular n-ary Gray code digits: consecutive values differ
in exactly one digit, and that digit changes by one modulo radix. The input
digits are not modified.

funcs.name: ParityBit
funcs.doc:
ParityBit returns the even-parity bit of a bit slice (the exclusive-or of all
entries), so that appending it makes the total parity even.

funcs.name: PermuteInts
funcs.doc:
PermuteInts applies a permutation to a slice, returning out[i]=data[perm[i]].
It returns ErrLength if the lengths disagree.

funcs.name: PolyToBits
funcs.doc:
PolyToBits converts a GF(2) polynomial packed as an int into a length-n bit
slice in little-endian order, where index i holds the coefficient of x^i.

funcs.name: RankGF2
funcs.doc:
RankGF2 returns the rank of a GF(2) matrix computed by Gaussian elimination.
The input matrix is not modified.

funcs.name: RepetitionDecode
funcs.doc:
RepetitionDecode decodes a repetition-coded word by majority vote over each
block of n symbols, recovering the original message. The word length must be
a multiple of n.

funcs.name: RepetitionEncode
funcs.doc:
RepetitionEncode repeats each message bit n times, producing a code word of
length n*len(msg). It returns ErrBit on a non-bit input.

funcs.name: ShannonCodeLength
funcs.doc:
ShannonCodeLength returns the ideal (fractional) number of bits to encode a
symbol of probability p under an optimal arithmetic coder, -log2(p). It
returns 0 for p<=0.

funcs.name: SingleParityCheck
funcs.doc:
SingleParityCheck reports whether a word has even parity (no detected single
error).

funcs.name: SingleParityDecode
funcs.signature: func SingleParityDecode(word []int) (msg []int, errorDetected bool, err error)
funcs.doc:
SingleParityDecode strips the trailing parity bit and reports whether the
received word passed the even-parity check (errorDetected is true when an
odd number of bit errors is present).

funcs.name: SingleParityEncode
funcs.doc:
SingleParityEncode appends an even-parity bit to the message, producing a
length-(k+1) code word of even weight.

funcs.name: TransposeGF2
funcs.doc: TransposeGF2 returns the transpose of a rectangular GF(2) matrix.

funcs.name: UintToBits
funcs.doc:
UintToBits unpacks the low n bits of x into a bit slice, most-significant bit
first.

funcs.name: VecMatGF2
funcs.doc:
VecMatGF2 multiplies the row vector v by the GF(2) matrix m, returning v*m
over GF(2). It panics on a dimension mismatch.

funcs.name: WalshCode
funcs.doc:
WalshCode returns the index-th Walsh code of length 2^k as a bit slice, taken
from the rows of the Sylvester-Hadamard matrix (a +1 entry maps to bit 0 and
-1 to bit 1). Distinct Walsh codes of the same length are mutually orthogonal.

funcs.name: WalshHadamardTransform
funcs.doc:
WalshHadamardTransform returns the natural-ordered fast Walsh-Hadamard
transform of a, whose length must be a power of two. The transform is its own
inverse up to the scale factor len(a) (see InverseWalshHadamardTransform).

funcs.name: WalshHadamardTransformInt
funcs.doc:
WalshHadamardTransformInt is the integer-valued forward Walsh-Hadamard
transform for a slice whose length is a power of two.

funcs.name: XORVectors
funcs.doc:
XORVectors returns the entrywise exclusive-or of two equal-length bit
vectors. It panics on a length mismatch.

examples.name: Hamming74Decode
examples.code:
data := []int{1, 0, 1, 1}
cw, _ := Hamming74Encode(data)
cw[2] ^= 1
recovered, _, pos, _ := Hamming74Decode(cw)
fmt.Println(pos, recovered)

examples.name: ReedSolomon
examples.code:
f := NewGF256()
rs, _ := NewReedSolomon(f, 4)
msg := make([]int, rs.K())
for i := range msg {
	msg[i] = i % 7
}
code, _ := rs.Encode(msg)

code[3] ^= 0x55
code[10] ^= 0xAA
decoded, nerr, _ := rs.DecodeMessage(code)
fmt.Println(nerr, eqInts(decoded, msg))

packages.name: combin
packages.synopsis: Package combin implements combinatorics: counting, arrangements and combinatorial numbers.
packages.doc:
Package combin implements combinatorics: counting, arrangements and
combinatorial numbers.

The package collects the classical counting functions used throughout
discrete mathematics. Where results grow without bound the routines return
arbitrary-precision *big.Int values so that answers stay exact; parallel
float64 and natural-logarithm variants are provided for the cases where an
approximate but fast answer (or one that survives magnitudes far beyond the
range of a machine integer) is preferable.

The following families are covered:

  - factorials: Factorial, DoubleFactorial, Multifactorial, Subfactorial
    (derangements), Superfactorial, HyperFactorial and Primorial;
  - arrangements: Permutations (nPr) and Permutations with repetition;
  - selections: Combinations (nCr), Binomial, combinations with repetition,
    the central binomial coefficient and the real-argument GammaBinomial;
  - Multinomial coefficients;
  - Rising and Falling factorials (Pochhammer symbols);
  - the combinatorial number triangles and sequences: Catalan, Motzkin,
    Schroeder, Bell, Stirling numbers of the first and second kind, Lah
    numbers, Narayana, Eulerian, Delannoy and telephone numbers;
  - Pascal's triangle rows and integer-partition counts.

Every routine is deterministic and depends only on the Go standard library.

funcs.name: AllPermutations
funcs.doc:
AllPermutations returns all len(items)! permutations of items (treating equal
values as distinct by position), each as a fresh slice.

funcs.name: Bell
funcs.doc:
Bell returns the nth Bell number, the number of partitions of a set of n
elements. The sequence begins 1, 1, 2, 5, 15, 52, 203, … It panics when n
is negative.

funcs.name: BellTriangleFull
funcs.doc:
BellTriangleFull returns rows 0 through n of the Bell (Aitken/Peirce)
triangle. The first entry of row i equals the i-th Bell number.

funcs.name: BellTriangleRow
funcs.doc:
BellTriangleRow returns row n of the Bell (Aitken) triangle. Its first entry
is the nth Bell number and its last entry is the (n+1)th Bell number.
It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: BernoulliNumber
funcs.doc:
BernoulliNumber returns the n-th Bernoulli number B(n) as an exact rational,
using the convention B(1) = -1/2.

funcs.name: BernoulliNumberFloat
funcs.doc: BernoulliNumberFloat returns the n-th Bernoulli number as a float64.

funcs.name: BernoulliSequence
funcs.doc:
BernoulliSequence returns the Bernoulli numbers B(0), B(1), ..., B(n) as exact
rationals, using the convention B(1) = -1/2.

funcs.name: Binomial
funcs.doc:
Binomial returns the binomial coefficient C(n, k) = n!/(k!·(n-k)!) using a
multiplicative scheme that keeps every intermediate value integral. It
returns 0 when k is outside [0, n] and panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: BinomialFloat
funcs.doc:
BinomialFloat returns the binomial coefficient C(n, k) as a float64. Small
results are rounded to the nearest integer; very large results are returned
as their floating-point approximation. It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: BinomialRow
funcs.doc:
BinomialRow is a synonym for PascalRow: it returns the binomial coefficients
C(n, 0), C(n, 1), …, C(n, n). It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: Catalan
funcs.doc:
Catalan returns the nth Catalan number, C(2n, n)/(n+1). The sequence begins
1, 1, 2, 5, 14, 42, … It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: CatalanFloat
funcs.doc:
CatalanFloat returns the nth Catalan number as a float64.
It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: CatalanSequence
funcs.doc:
CatalanSequence returns the Catalan numbers C(0), C(1), ..., C(n), using
C(i+1) = C(i) (2)(2i+1) / (i+2).

funcs.name: CatalanTriangle
funcs.doc:
CatalanTriangle returns the entry C(n, k) of Catalan's triangle, the number
of lattice paths that never rise above the diagonal, equal to
binom(n+k, k)·(n-k+1)/(n+1). Row n sums to the (n+1)th Catalan number.
It returns 0 when k is outside [0, n] and panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: CentralBinomial
funcs.doc:
CentralBinomial returns the central binomial coefficient C(2n, n).
It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: CombinationList
funcs.doc:
CombinationList returns all k-element index subsets of {0, 1, ..., n-1}, in
lexicographic order, each as a strictly increasing slice.

funcs.name: CombinationListOf
funcs.doc:
CombinationListOf returns all k-element combinations of items, in the order
induced by index position, each as a fresh slice.

funcs.name: CombinationRank
funcs.doc:
CombinationRank returns the colexicographic rank of the strictly increasing
combination c in the combinatorial number system: sum of C(c[i], i+1).

funcs.name: CombinationUnrank
funcs.doc:
CombinationUnrank returns the k-combination (strictly increasing) whose
colexicographic rank is rank, inverting CombinationRank.

funcs.name: Combinations
funcs.doc:
Combinations returns the number of unordered selections of k items from n,
nCr = C(n, k). It is a synonym for Binomial. It returns 0 when k is outside
[0, n] and panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: CombinationsFloat
funcs.doc:
CombinationsFloat returns C(n, k) as a float64, evaluated via logarithms so
that it stays finite for large arguments. It returns 0 when k is outside
[0, n] and panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: CombinationsWithRepetition
funcs.doc:
CombinationsWithRepetition returns the number of multisets of size r drawn
from n distinct items, C(n+r-1, r). It panics when either argument is
negative.

funcs.name: CompositionList
funcs.doc:
CompositionList returns all compositions of n into positive parts, in
lexicographic order.

funcs.name: CompositionListInto
funcs.doc:
CompositionListInto returns all compositions of n into exactly k positive
parts.

funcs.name: CompositionNumber
funcs.doc:
CompositionNumber returns the number of compositions (ordered partitions) of
n into positive parts, which is 2^(n-1) for n >= 1 and 1 for n == 0.

funcs.name: CompositionNumberInto
funcs.doc:
CompositionNumberInto returns the number of compositions of n into exactly k
positive parts, which is C(n-1, k-1).

funcs.name: Compositions
funcs.doc:
Compositions returns the number of ordered compositions of n into positive
parts, 2^(n-1) for n >= 1 and 1 for n = 0. It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: DelannoyNumber
funcs.doc:
DelannoyNumber returns the Delannoy number D(m, n), the number of lattice
paths from (0,0) to (m,n) using steps east, north and north-east. It
satisfies D(m, n) = D(m-1, n) + D(m, n-1) + D(m-1, n-1). It panics when
either argument is negative.

funcs.name: Derangement
funcs.doc:
Derangement is a synonym for Subfactorial: it returns the number of
permutations of n elements that leave no element fixed. It panics when n < 0.

funcs.name: DerangementList
funcs.doc:
DerangementList returns every derangement of {0, 1, ..., n-1} as a slice, in
lexicographic order.

funcs.name: DerangementNumber
funcs.doc:
DerangementNumber returns !n, the number of permutations of n elements with no
fixed point, using the recurrence !n = (n-1)(!(n-1) + !(n-2)).

funcs.name: DerangementProbability
funcs.doc:
DerangementProbability returns !n / n!, the probability that a uniformly
random permutation of n elements is a derangement. It converges to 1/e.

funcs.name: DoubleFactorial
funcs.doc:
DoubleFactorial returns the double factorial n!! = n·(n-2)·(n-4)·…, stopping
at 2 or 1. By convention 0!! = (-1)!! = 1. It panics when n < -1.

funcs.name: DoubleFactorialFloat
funcs.doc:
DoubleFactorialFloat returns the double factorial n!! as a float64.
It panics when n < -1.

funcs.name: EulerNumber
funcs.doc:
EulerNumber returns the n-th Euler (secant) number E(n). Odd-indexed values
are zero; E(2m) = (-1)^m A(2m).

funcs.name: EulerNumberSequence
funcs.doc: EulerNumberSequence returns the Euler numbers E(0), E(1), ..., E(n).

funcs.name: EulerianNumber
funcs.doc:
EulerianNumber returns the Eulerian number A(n, k), the number of
permutations of n elements with exactly k ascents. It satisfies
A(n, k) = (k+1)·A(n-1, k) + (n-k)·A(n-1, k-1). It returns 0 for arguments
outside the valid triangle and panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: EulerianRow
funcs.doc:
EulerianRow returns row n of the Eulerian triangle: the counts of
permutations of n elements with 0, 1, ..., n-1 ascents. The row sums to n!.

funcs.name: EulerianSecondOrder
funcs.doc:
EulerianSecondOrder returns the second-order Eulerian number <<n, k>>,
satisfying <<n, k>> = (k+1)·<<n-1, k>> + (2n-1-k)·<<n-1, k-1>> with
<<0, 0>> = 1. These count Stirling permutations by descents. It returns 0 for
arguments outside the valid triangle and panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: EulerianTriangle
funcs.doc: EulerianTriangle returns rows 0 through n of the Eulerian triangle.

funcs.name: Factorial
funcs.doc:
Factorial returns n! = 1·2·…·n as an exact integer. Factorial(0) is 1.
It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: FactorialFloat
funcs.doc:
FactorialFloat returns n! as a float64, using the Gamma function so that the
result stays finite up to n == 170. It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: FactorialNumberSystem
funcs.doc:
FactorialNumberSystem returns the factoradic (Lehmer) digits of rank as a
length-n slice; digit i lies in [0, n-1-i] and the final digit is always 0.

funcs.name: FallingFactorial
funcs.doc:
FallingFactorial returns the falling factorial
(x)_n = x·(x-1)·…·(x-n+1), with the empty product FallingFactorial(x, 0) = 1.
It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: FallingFactorialFloat
funcs.signature: func FallingFactorialFloat(x float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
FallingFactorialFloat returns the falling factorial (x)_n for a real base x.
It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: Fibonacci
funcs.doc:
Fibonacci returns the nth Fibonacci number, F(0) = 0, F(1) = 1 and
F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2). It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: GammaBinomial
funcs.doc:
GammaBinomial returns the generalized binomial coefficient for real
arguments, C(x, y) = Γ(x+1) / (Γ(y+1)·Γ(x-y+1)). For non-negative integer
arguments it agrees with the ordinary binomial coefficient.

funcs.name: GeneralizedPentagonal
funcs.doc:
GeneralizedPentagonal returns the i-th generalized pentagonal number in the
order 0, 1, 2, 5, 7, 12, 15, ... generated by k = 0, 1, -1, 2, -2, 3, ...

funcs.name: GrayCodeAt
funcs.doc:
GrayCodeAt returns the Gray code at position rank in the standard reflected
Gray sequence; it is an alias for GrayEncode.

funcs.name: GrayCodeRank
funcs.doc:
GrayCodeRank returns the position (rank) of the Gray code g within the
standard reflected Gray sequence; it is an alias for GrayDecode.

funcs.name: GrayCodeSequence
funcs.doc:
GrayCodeSequence returns the 2^bits reflected Gray codes in order. Consecutive
entries (and the last and first) differ in exactly one bit.

funcs.name: GrayDecode
funcs.doc: GrayDecode returns the integer whose Gray code is g, inverting GrayEncode.

funcs.name: GrayEncode
funcs.doc: GrayEncode returns the reflected binary Gray code of n (n XOR (n >> 1)).

funcs.name: HarmonicNumber
funcs.doc:
HarmonicNumber returns the nth harmonic number H(n) = 1 + 1/2 + … + 1/n as a
float64, with H(0) = 0. It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: HarmonicNumberGeneralized
funcs.doc:
HarmonicNumberGeneralized returns the generalized harmonic number
H(n, m) = 1 + 1/2^m + … + 1/n^m as a float64, with H(0, m) = 0. It panics
when n is negative.

funcs.name: HyperFactorial
funcs.doc:
HyperFactorial returns the hyperfactorial H(n) = 1^1·2^2·…·n^n, with
HyperFactorial(0) = 1. It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: InvolutionList
funcs.doc:
InvolutionList returns every involution of {0, 1, ..., n-1} as a slice, in
lexicographic order.

funcs.name: InvolutionNumber
funcs.doc:
InvolutionNumber returns the number of involutions (self-inverse permutations)
of n elements, using I(n) = I(n-1) + (n-1) I(n-2).

funcs.name: LahNumber
funcs.doc:
LahNumber returns the unsigned Lah number L(n, k) = C(n-1, k-1)·n!/k!, the
number of ways to partition n elements into k non-empty ordered lists.
L(0, 0) is 1. It returns 0 for arguments outside the valid triangle.

funcs.name: LogBinomial
funcs.doc:
LogBinomial returns the natural logarithm of the binomial coefficient
C(n, k). It panics when n is negative or k is outside [0, n].

funcs.name: LogCombinations
funcs.doc:
LogCombinations returns the natural logarithm of C(n, k). It is a synonym for
LogBinomial. It panics when n is negative or k is outside [0, n].

funcs.name: LogFactorial
funcs.doc:
LogFactorial returns the natural logarithm of n!, computed from the log-Gamma
function so that it is accurate for very large n. It panics when n is
negative.

funcs.name: LogMultinomial
funcs.doc:
LogMultinomial returns the natural logarithm of the multinomial coefficient
for the given part sizes. It panics when any argument is negative.

funcs.name: LogPermutations
funcs.doc:
LogPermutations returns the natural logarithm of nPr. It panics when either
argument is negative or when r > n (for which the logarithm is undefined).

funcs.name: Lucas
funcs.doc:
Lucas returns the nth Lucas number, L(0) = 2, L(1) = 1 and
L(n) = L(n-1) + L(n-2). It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: LyndonWordNumber
funcs.doc:
LyndonWordNumber returns the number of Lyndon words of length n over a k-letter
alphabet: (1/n) sum_{d|n} mu(d) k^(n/d).

funcs.name: MotzkinNumber
funcs.doc:
MotzkinNumber returns the nth Motzkin number, counting the ways of drawing
non-intersecting chords between n points on a circle. The sequence begins
1, 1, 2, 4, 9, 21, 51, … It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: MotzkinSequence
funcs.doc:
MotzkinSequence returns the Motzkin numbers M(0), M(1), ..., M(n), using
M(i) = M(i-1) + sum_{j=0}^{i-2} M(j) M(i-2-j).

funcs.name: Multifactorial
funcs.doc:
Multifactorial returns the k-fold factorial n·(n-k)·(n-2k)·… down to the
smallest positive term. Multifactorial(n, 1) equals Factorial(n) and
Multifactorial(n, 2) equals DoubleFactorial(n). It panics when k < 1 or
n < 0.

funcs.name: Multinomial
funcs.doc:
Multinomial returns the multinomial coefficient (k1+k2+…)! / (k1!·k2!·…),
the number of ways to partition a set of that many items into labelled groups
of the given sizes. It panics when any argument is negative.

funcs.name: MultinomialFloat
funcs.doc:
MultinomialFloat returns the multinomial coefficient as a float64, evaluated
through logarithms. It panics when any argument is negative.

funcs.name: MultisetCoefficient
funcs.doc:
MultisetCoefficient returns the number of multisets of size k drawn from n
distinct kinds, i.e. C(n+k-1, k).

funcs.name: MultisetPermutationList
funcs.doc:
MultisetPermutationList expands the count vector counts into a multiset (value
i repeated counts[i] times) and returns all its distinct permutations.

funcs.name: MultisetPermutationNumber
funcs.doc:
MultisetPermutationNumber returns the number of distinct permutations of a
multiset whose element multiplicities are counts, i.e. the multinomial
coefficient (sum counts)! / prod(counts[i]!).

funcs.name: MultisetPermutations
funcs.doc:
MultisetPermutations returns all distinct permutations of items (a multiset),
in lexicographic order, each as a fresh slice.

funcs.name: NarayanaNumber
funcs.doc:
NarayanaNumber returns the Narayana number N(n, k) = C(n, k)·C(n, k-1)/n,
counting Dyck paths of length 2n with exactly k peaks. Row n sums to the nth
Catalan number. It returns 0 for arguments outside [1, n].

funcs.name: NarayanaTriangle
funcs.doc: NarayanaTriangle returns rows 0 through n of the Narayana triangle.

funcs.name: NarayanaTriangleRow
funcs.doc:
NarayanaTriangleRow returns row n of the Narayana triangle, the values
N(n,1), ..., N(n,n) where N(n,k) = C(n,k) C(n,k-1) / n. The row sums to the
n-th Catalan number.

funcs.name: NecklaceNumber
funcs.doc:
NecklaceNumber returns the number of distinct necklaces of length n using up
to k colors, counted up to rotation: (1/n) sum_{d|n} phi(d) k^(n/d).

funcs.name: NextCombination
funcs.doc:
NextCombination advances c, a strictly increasing k-combination of indices
drawn from {0, ..., n-1}, to the next combination in lexicographic order. It
returns true on success, or false (leaving c unchanged) when c is the last
combination.

funcs.name: NextPartition
funcs.doc:
NextPartition advances p (a non-increasing partition) in place to the next
partition of the same integer in reverse lexicographic order. It returns the
updated slice (which may be reallocated) and true, or the unchanged input and
false when p is already the final partition (all ones).

funcs.name: NextPermutation
funcs.doc:
NextPermutation rearranges a into the lexicographically next greater
permutation of its elements and returns true. If a is already the highest
permutation it is reversed to the lowest (sorted ascending) permutation and
false is returned. Equal elements are handled correctly.

funcs.name: PartitionConjugate
funcs.doc:
PartitionConjugate returns the conjugate (transpose) of the partition p, which
must be given in non-increasing order. The conjugate is also non-increasing.

funcs.name: PartitionCount
funcs.doc:
PartitionCount returns p(n), the number of unordered ways to write n as a sum
of positive integers, computed with Euler's pentagonal-number recurrence. The
sequence begins 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, … It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: PartitionDurfeeSquare
funcs.doc:
PartitionDurfeeSquare returns the side length of the Durfee square of the
partition p (the largest d with at least d parts each of size >= d). p must be
non-increasing.

funcs.name: PartitionNumber
funcs.doc:
PartitionNumber returns p(n), the number of integer partitions of n, computed
by an O(n^2) dynamic program. It returns 1 for n == 0 and 0 for n < 0.

funcs.name: PartitionNumberAtMost
funcs.doc:
PartitionNumberAtMost returns the number of partitions of n into at most k
parts, equivalently the number of partitions of n whose parts are all <= k.

funcs.name: PartitionNumberDistinct
funcs.doc:
PartitionNumberDistinct returns the number of partitions of n into distinct
(unequal) positive parts.

funcs.name: PartitionNumberInto
funcs.doc:
PartitionNumberInto returns the number of partitions of n into exactly k
positive parts, using the recurrence P(n,k) = P(n-1,k-1) + P(n-k,k).

funcs.name: PartitionNumberOdd
funcs.doc:
PartitionNumberOdd returns the number of partitions of n into odd parts.
By Euler's theorem this equals PartitionNumberDistinct(n).

funcs.name: PartitionNumberTable
funcs.doc: PartitionNumberTable returns the slice [p(0), p(1), ..., p(n)].

funcs.name: PartitionRank
funcs.doc:
PartitionRank returns the rank of the partition p (largest part minus the
number of parts). The empty partition has rank 0.

funcs.name: Partitions
funcs.doc:
Partitions returns all integer partitions of n as non-increasing slices, in
reverse lexicographic order (starting with {n} and ending with all ones).

funcs.name: PartitionsDistinct
funcs.doc:
PartitionsDistinct returns all partitions of n into distinct positive parts,
each as a strictly decreasing slice.

funcs.name: PartitionsInto
funcs.doc:
PartitionsInto returns all partitions of n into exactly k positive parts,
each as a non-increasing slice.

funcs.name: PartitionsIntoKParts
funcs.doc:
PartitionsIntoKParts returns the number of partitions of n into exactly k
positive parts, using the recurrence p(n, k) = p(n-1, k-1) + p(n-k, k) with
p(0, 0) = 1. It returns 0 for arguments outside the valid range and panics
when either argument is negative.

funcs.name: PascalRow
funcs.doc:
PascalRow returns row n of Pascal's triangle, the binomial coefficients
C(n, 0), C(n, 1), …, C(n, n). It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: PascalTriangle
funcs.doc:
PascalTriangle returns rows 0 through n of Pascal's triangle.
It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: PentagonalNumber
funcs.doc:
PentagonalNumber returns the k-th (signed) pentagonal number k(3k-1)/2. It
accepts negative k, yielding the generalized pentagonal numbers.

funcs.name: PermutationParity
funcs.doc:
PermutationParity returns the sign of the permutation a of {0,...,n-1}:
+1 if it has an even number of inversions, -1 if odd.

funcs.name: PermutationRank
funcs.doc:
PermutationRank returns the lexicographic rank (0-based) of the permutation a,
which must be a permutation of {0, 1, ..., len(a)-1}.

funcs.name: PermutationUnrank
funcs.doc:
PermutationUnrank returns the permutation of {0, 1, ..., n-1} whose
lexicographic rank (0-based) is rank.

funcs.name: Permutations
funcs.doc:
Permutations returns the number of ordered arrangements of r items chosen
from n, nPr = n!/(n-r)!. It returns 0 when r > n and panics when either
argument is negative.

funcs.name: PermutationsFloat
funcs.doc:
PermutationsFloat returns nPr as a float64, evaluated through logarithms so
that it remains finite for large arguments. It returns 0 when r > n and
panics when either argument is negative.

funcs.name: PermutationsWithRepetition
funcs.doc:
PermutationsWithRepetition returns n^r, the number of ordered sequences of
length r drawn from n symbols when repetition is allowed. It panics when
either argument is negative.

funcs.name: PowerSet
funcs.doc:
PowerSet returns every subset of items as a fresh slice, ordered by the binary
value of the inclusion mask (the empty set first).

funcs.name: PowerSetSize
funcs.doc: PowerSetSize returns 2^n, the number of subsets of an n-element set.

funcs.name: PrevPermutation
funcs.doc:
PrevPermutation rearranges a into the lexicographically previous permutation
of its elements and returns true. If a is already the lowest permutation it is
reversed to the highest permutation and false is returned.

funcs.name: Primorial
funcs.doc:
Primorial returns n# — the product of all prime numbers not exceeding n.
Primorial(0) and Primorial(1) are 1. It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: RencontresNumber
funcs.doc:
RencontresNumber returns the number of permutations of n elements with
exactly k fixed points, C(n, k)·!(n-k). Row n sums to n!. It returns 0 for
arguments outside [0, n] and panics when either argument is negative.

funcs.name: RisingFactorial
funcs.doc:
RisingFactorial returns the rising factorial (Pochhammer symbol)
x^(n) = x·(x+1)·…·(x+n-1), with the empty product RisingFactorial(x, 0) = 1.
It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: RisingFactorialFloat
funcs.signature: func RisingFactorialFloat(x float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
RisingFactorialFloat returns the rising factorial x^(n) for a real base x.
It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: SchroederNumber
funcs.doc:
SchroederNumber returns the nth large Schröder number, counting the lattice
paths from (0,0) to (n,n) using steps east, north and north-east that never
rise above the diagonal. The sequence begins 1, 2, 6, 22, 90, 394, …
It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: SecantNumber
funcs.doc:
SecantNumber returns the n-th secant number |E(2n)| = A(2n), the number of
alternating permutations of even length 2n.

funcs.name: SignedLahNumber
funcs.doc:
SignedLahNumber returns the signed Lah number (-1)^n·L(n, k), the
coefficients that express rising factorials in terms of falling factorials.
It returns 0 for arguments outside the valid triangle.

funcs.name: StirlingApproximation
funcs.doc:
StirlingApproximation returns Stirling's asymptotic estimate of n!,
sqrt(2·π·n)·(n/e)^n, as a float64. StirlingApproximation(0) is 1. It panics
when n is negative.

funcs.name: StirlingFirst
funcs.doc:
StirlingFirst returns the signed Stirling number of the first kind
s(n, k) = (-1)^(n-k)·c(n, k), the coefficients relating falling factorials to
ordinary powers. It returns 0 for arguments outside the valid triangle.

funcs.name: StirlingFirstRow
funcs.doc:
StirlingFirstRow returns the unsigned Stirling numbers of the first kind
c(n, 0), c(n, 1), …, c(n, n). It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: StirlingFirstUnsigned
funcs.doc:
StirlingFirstUnsigned returns the unsigned Stirling number of the first kind
c(n, k), the number of permutations of n elements with exactly k disjoint
cycles. It satisfies c(n, k) = c(n-1, k-1) + (n-1)·c(n-1, k) with c(0, 0) = 1.
It returns 0 for arguments outside the valid triangle.

funcs.name: StirlingSecond
funcs.doc:
StirlingSecond returns the Stirling number of the second kind S(n, k), the
number of ways to partition n elements into k non-empty unlabelled subsets.
It satisfies S(n, k) = k·S(n-1, k) + S(n-1, k-1) with S(0, 0) = 1. It returns
0 for arguments outside the valid triangle.

funcs.name: StirlingSecondRow
funcs.doc:
StirlingSecondRow returns the Stirling numbers of the second kind
S(n, 0), S(n, 1), …, S(n, n). It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: Subfactorial
funcs.doc:
Subfactorial returns the number of derangements !n, i.e. the number of
permutations of n elements with no fixed point. It satisfies
!n = (n-1)·(!(n-1) + !(n-2)) with !0 = 1 and !1 = 0. It panics when n < 0.

funcs.name: SubfactorialFloat
funcs.doc:
SubfactorialFloat returns the number of derangements !n as a float64, using
the closed form round(n!/e). It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: SubsetList
funcs.doc:
SubsetList returns every subset of {0, 1, ..., n-1} as an index slice, ordered
by the binary value of the inclusion mask.

funcs.name: Superfactorial
funcs.doc:
Superfactorial returns the product of the first n factorials,
1!·2!·…·n!, with Superfactorial(0) = 1. It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: TangentNumber
funcs.doc:
TangentNumber returns the n-th tangent number A(2n+1), the number of
alternating permutations of odd length 2n+1.

funcs.name: TelephoneNumber
funcs.doc:
TelephoneNumber returns the nth telephone (involution) number, the number of
self-inverse permutations of n elements. It satisfies
T(n) = T(n-1) + (n-1)·T(n-2) with T(0) = T(1) = 1 and begins
1, 1, 2, 4, 10, 26, 76, … It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: Tribonacci
funcs.doc:
Tribonacci returns the nth tribonacci number with T(0) = T(1) = 0, T(2) = 1
and T(n) = T(n-1) + T(n-2) + T(n-3). The sequence begins
0, 0, 1, 1, 2, 4, 7, 13, … It panics when n is negative.

funcs.name: WeakCompositionList
funcs.doc:
WeakCompositionList returns all weak compositions of n into exactly k
non-negative parts, in lexicographic order.

funcs.name: WeakCompositionNumber
funcs.doc:
WeakCompositionNumber returns the number of weak compositions of n into
exactly k non-negative parts, which is C(n+k-1, k-1).

funcs.name: WeakCompositions
funcs.doc:
WeakCompositions returns the number of ways to write n as an ordered sum of k
non-negative integers, C(n+k-1, k-1). It panics when either argument is
negative.

funcs.name: ZigzagNumber
funcs.doc:
ZigzagNumber returns the n-th Euler up/down (zigzag) number A(n): the number
of alternating permutations of n elements. A(0) = A(1) = 1.

packages.name: complexanalysis
packages.synopsis: Package complexanalysis provides numerical tools for complex analysis using only the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package complexanalysis provides numerical tools for complex analysis using
only the Go standard library.

It is organised into a few areas:

  - Elementary and inverse functions on complex128 with well-defined
    principal branches (see elementary.go): [Sqrt], [Cbrt], [Exp], [Log],
    [Pow], the trigonometric and hyperbolic families, and their inverses.
  - Special functions (see special.go): the complex [Gamma] and [LogGamma]
    via the Lanczos approximation, [Digamma], [Beta], the error functions
    [Erf]/[Erfc], the Riemann [Zeta] function via Borwein's algorithm, and
    the combinatorial [Factorial], [RisingFactorial] and [Binomial].
  - Numerical contour integration and its consequences (see contour.go):
    [IntegrateCircle], [IntegrateSegment], [IntegratePolygon] and the general
    [ContourIntegral]; [Residue], [ResidueSimplePole] and [ResidueOrderM];
    the [CauchyIntegralValue] and [CauchyDerivative] formulas; the
    [WindingNumber]; and the [ArgumentPrinciple] with [CountZeros].
  - Conformal maps (see conformal.go): the [Mobius] transformation type with
    composition, inversion and fixed points, together with the [CrossRatio],
    the [CayleyTransform] and the [JoukowskiMap].
  - Laurent and Taylor coefficient extraction and numeric analytic
    continuation (see laurent.go): [LaurentCoefficient], [TaylorCoefficient],
    [PowerSeriesEval] and [AnalyticContinuation].

Every function that samples an analytic function does so by evaluating a
user-supplied [Function]. Contour methods use the composite trapezoidal rule
on a circular contour, which converges geometrically for functions that are
analytic in an annulus, so modest sample counts (a few hundred points) give
near machine-precision results.

The package is deterministic and depends only on math and math/cmplx.

types.name: Function
types.signature: type Function func(complex128) complex128
types.doc:
Function is a complex-valued function of a single complex variable. It is the
common argument type for the numerical routines in this package.

types.name: Mobius
types.doc:
Mobius represents a Mobius (linear fractional) transformation
z -> (A*z + B) / (C*z + D). Such maps are the conformal automorphisms of the
extended complex plane (the Riemann sphere).

funcs.name: IdentityMobius
funcs.doc: IdentityMobius returns the identity transformation z -> z.

funcs.name: InversionMap
funcs.doc: InversionMap returns the Mobius map z -> 1/z.

funcs.name: MobiusFromPoints
funcs.signature: func MobiusFromPoints(z1, z2, z3, w1, w2, w3 complex128) Mobius
funcs.doc:
MobiusFromPoints returns the unique Mobius transformation that sends the three
distinct points z1, z2, z3 to w1, w2, w3 respectively. It is built by
composing the standard map taking z1, z2, z3 to 0, 1, infinity with the
inverse of the analogous map for the w points.

funcs.name: NewMobius
funcs.signature: func NewMobius(a, b, c, d complex128) Mobius
funcs.doc: NewMobius constructs the Mobius transformation with the given coefficients.

funcs.name: RotationMap
funcs.doc: RotationMap returns the rotation z -> e^{i*theta} * z about the origin.

funcs.name: ScalingMap
funcs.doc:
ScalingMap returns the Mobius map z -> a*z. For |a| = 1 this is a pure
rotation and RotationMap is a convenient alias.

funcs.name: TranslationMap
funcs.doc: TranslationMap returns the Mobius map z -> z + b.

methods.name: Apply
methods.doc:
Apply evaluates the transformation at z. When the denominator vanishes it
returns a complex infinity, matching the point at infinity on the sphere.

methods.name: Compose
methods.doc:
Compose returns the transformation m(other(z)), the composition of m after
other. Its coefficient matrix is the product of the two coefficient matrices.

methods.name: Determinant
methods.doc:
Determinant returns A*D - B*C. A Mobius map is invertible exactly when this is
non-zero.

methods.name: FixedPoints
methods.doc:
FixedPoints returns the fixed points of the transformation, the solutions of
C*z^2 + (D-A)*z - B = 0. For a non-identity map this is one or two points;
when C == 0 the point at infinity is a fixed point and only the finite ones
are returned. The identity map (and any map fixing every point) returns nil.

methods.name: Inverse
methods.doc: Inverse returns the inverse transformation z -> (D*z - B) / (-C*z + A).

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns an equivalent transformation whose coefficient matrix has
determinant 1, dividing all coefficients by a square root of the current
determinant. A zero-determinant map is returned unchanged.

funcs.name: Abs
funcs.doc: Abs returns the modulus (absolute value) |z|.

funcs.name: Acos
funcs.doc: Acos returns the principal inverse cosine of z.

funcs.name: Acosh
funcs.doc: Acosh returns the principal inverse hyperbolic cosine of z.

funcs.name: AnalyticContinuation
funcs.signature: func AnalyticContinuation(f Function, from, to complex128, order int, radius float64, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
AnalyticContinuation returns the value of f at the point to, reconstructed
from its Taylor expansion about the point from. It computes the Taylor
coefficients up to the given order on a circle of the given radius about
from, then sums the truncated series at to. The step |to-from| should be
smaller than the radius of convergence for the series to be accurate.

funcs.name: AnalyticContinuationPath
funcs.signature: func AnalyticContinuationPath(f Function, path []complex128, order int, radius float64, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
AnalyticContinuationPath continues f numerically along the given path of
centers and returns the reconstructed value at the final point. Starting from
path[0], it repeatedly forms the Taylor expansion of f about the current
center (using a circle of the given radius and n points, to the given order)
and evaluates it at the next path point, which becomes the new center. This
mirrors classical analytic continuation along a chain of overlapping disks.
It returns 0 for an empty path and f(path[0]) for a single point.

funcs.name: ApproxEqual
funcs.signature: func ApproxEqual(a, b complex128, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc: ApproxEqual reports whether |a-b| <= tol.

funcs.name: Arg
funcs.doc: Arg returns the principal argument of z in the interval (-pi, pi].

funcs.name: ArgumentPrinciple
funcs.signature: func ArgumentPrinciple(f Function, center complex128, radius float64, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
ArgumentPrinciple returns the value of 1/(2*pi*i) * integral of f'(z)/f(z)
around the positively oriented circle of the given center and radius. By the
argument principle this equals the number of zeros minus the number of poles
of f inside the circle, counted with multiplicity. The derivative f' is
approximated by central finite differences. The exact count is returned by
CountZeros after rounding.

funcs.name: Asin
funcs.doc: Asin returns the principal inverse sine of z.

funcs.name: Asinh
funcs.doc: Asinh returns the principal inverse hyperbolic sine of z.

funcs.name: Atan
funcs.doc: Atan returns the principal inverse tangent of z.

funcs.name: Atanh
funcs.doc: Atanh returns the principal inverse hyperbolic tangent of z.

funcs.name: Beta
funcs.doc:
Beta returns the Euler beta function B(a, b) = Gamma(a)Gamma(b)/Gamma(a+b),
evaluated through LogGamma to remain stable for moderately large arguments.

funcs.name: Binomial
funcs.signature: func Binomial(z complex128, k int) complex128
funcs.doc:
Binomial returns the generalized binomial coefficient C(z, k) =
Gamma(z+1)/(Gamma(k+1)Gamma(z-k+1)) for a non-negative integer k, evaluated
as a falling-factorial product so it is exact for polynomial z.

funcs.name: CauchyDerivative
funcs.signature: func CauchyDerivative(f Function, z0, center complex128, k int, radius float64, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
CauchyDerivative evaluates the k-th derivative of f at z0 using the Cauchy
integral formula for derivatives,
f^(k)(z0) = k!/(2*pi*i) * integral of f(z)/(z-z0)^(k+1) around the circle of
the given center and radius. The point z0 must lie strictly inside the circle
and f must be analytic there. k == 0 returns f(z0). It panics if k < 0.

funcs.name: CauchyIntegralValue
funcs.signature: func CauchyIntegralValue(f Function, z0, center complex128, radius float64, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
CauchyIntegralValue evaluates f at the point z0 inside a circle of the given
center and radius using the Cauchy integral formula,
f(z0) = 1/(2*pi*i) * integral of f(z)/(z-z0) around the circle. The point z0
must lie strictly inside the circle and f must be analytic there.

funcs.name: CayleyTransform
funcs.doc:
CayleyTransform returns the value of the Cayley transform (z-i)/(z+i), which
maps the upper half-plane conformally onto the open unit disk.

funcs.name: Cbrt
funcs.doc:
Cbrt returns the principal cube root of z, exp(Log(z)/3), which is real and
positive for real positive z.

funcs.name: Conj
funcs.doc: Conj returns the complex conjugate of z.

funcs.name: ContourIntegral
funcs.signature: func ContourIntegral(f Function, gamma func(float64) complex128, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
ContourIntegral approximates the integral of f along a general contour given
by the parametrization gamma over t in [0, 1]. The derivative gamma'(t) is
estimated by central finite differences, and the composite trapezoidal rule
with n subintervals is used. If the contour is closed (gamma(0) == gamma(1)),
the periodic trapezoidal rule is highly accurate. It panics if n <= 0.

funcs.name: Cos
funcs.doc: Cos returns the cosine of z.

funcs.name: Cosh
funcs.doc: Cosh returns the hyperbolic cosine of z.

funcs.name: Cot
funcs.doc: Cot returns the cotangent of z, cos(z)/sin(z).

funcs.name: Coth
funcs.doc: Coth returns the hyperbolic cotangent of z, cosh(z)/sinh(z).

funcs.name: CountZeros
funcs.signature: func CountZeros(f Function, center complex128, radius float64, n int) int
funcs.doc:
CountZeros returns the number of zeros of an analytic function f inside the
circle of the given center and radius, counted with multiplicity, by rounding
the argument-principle integral. It assumes f has no poles inside the circle
and no zeros on it.

funcs.name: CrossRatio
funcs.signature: func CrossRatio(z, z1, z2, z3 complex128) complex128
funcs.doc:
CrossRatio returns the cross-ratio (z-z1)(z2-z3) / ((z-z3)(z2-z1)) of the four
points. The cross-ratio is invariant under every Mobius transformation.

funcs.name: Csc
funcs.doc: Csc returns the cosecant of z, 1/sin(z).

funcs.name: Digamma
funcs.doc:
Digamma returns the logarithmic derivative of the gamma function,
psi(z) = Gamma'(z)/Gamma(z), using recurrence to raise the real part and then
the standard asymptotic expansion.

funcs.name: Erf
funcs.doc:
Erf returns the error function of z, 2/sqrt(pi) times the integral of
exp(-t^2) from 0 to z, summed from its Taylor series. The series converges
for every z but loses precision for large |z| (roughly |z| > 6); within that
range it is accurate to about 1e-12.

funcs.name: Erfc
funcs.doc: Erfc returns the complementary error function 1 - Erf(z).

funcs.name: Exp
funcs.doc: Exp returns e**z.

funcs.name: Factorial
funcs.doc:
Factorial returns z! defined as Gamma(z+1); for a non-negative integer z it
equals the ordinary factorial.

funcs.name: Gamma
funcs.doc:
Gamma returns the value of the Euler gamma function at z, computed with the
Lanczos approximation and the reflection formula for Re(z) < 0.5. It is
accurate to roughly 1e-13 relative error away from the poles at the
non-positive integers.

funcs.name: Im
funcs.doc: Im returns the imaginary part of z.

funcs.name: IntegrateCircle
funcs.signature: func IntegrateCircle(f Function, center complex128, radius float64, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
IntegrateCircle approximates the contour integral of f around the positively
oriented circle of the given center and radius, using the composite
trapezoidal rule with n sample points. Because the integrand is periodic,
this rule converges geometrically when f is analytic in an annulus around the
circle. It panics if n <= 0 or radius <= 0.

funcs.name: IntegratePolygon
funcs.signature: func IntegratePolygon(f Function, vertices []complex128, nPerEdge int) complex128
funcs.doc:
IntegratePolygon approximates the integral of f along the closed polygonal
contour through the given vertices (the last vertex is joined back to the
first), applying IntegrateSegment with nPerEdge points on each edge. It
returns 0 for fewer than two vertices.

funcs.name: IntegrateSegment
funcs.signature: func IntegrateSegment(f Function, a, b complex128, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
IntegrateSegment approximates the integral of f along the straight line
segment from a to b using the composite Simpson rule with n subintervals
(n is rounded up to the next even number). It panics if n <= 0.

funcs.name: InverseCayleyTransform
funcs.doc:
InverseCayleyTransform returns i*(1+w)/(1-w), the inverse of the Cayley
transform, mapping the unit disk back to the upper half-plane.

funcs.name: IsZero
funcs.signature: func IsZero(z complex128, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc: IsZero reports whether |z| <= tol.

funcs.name: JoukowskiMap
funcs.doc:
JoukowskiMap returns the Joukowski map (z + 1/z)/2, which maps circles about
the origin to confocal ellipses and is used in classical airfoil theory. It
returns a complex infinity at z = 0.

funcs.name: LaurentCoefficient
funcs.signature: func LaurentCoefficient(f Function, center complex128, k int, radius float64, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
LaurentCoefficient returns the k-th Laurent coefficient c_k of f expanded
about center, computed from the contour integral
c_k = 1/(2*pi*i) * integral of f(z)/(z-center)^(k+1) around the circle of the
given radius with n sample points. The index k may be negative; c_{-1} is the
residue. The circle must lie in an annulus of analyticity of f.

funcs.name: LaurentCoefficients
funcs.signature: func LaurentCoefficients(f Function, center complex128, lo, hi int, radius float64, n int) []complex128
funcs.doc:
LaurentCoefficients returns the Laurent coefficients c_k of f about center for
k running from lo to hi inclusive, as a slice indexed so that result[k-lo] is
c_k. It returns nil when hi < lo.

funcs.name: Log
funcs.doc:
Log returns the principal branch of the natural logarithm of z, with
imaginary part (the argument) in (-pi, pi].

funcs.name: LogBranch
funcs.signature: func LogBranch(z complex128, k int) complex128
funcs.doc:
LogBranch returns the value of the natural logarithm of z on branch k, that
is Log(z) + 2*pi*i*k. Branch 0 is the principal branch.

funcs.name: LogGamma
funcs.doc:
LogGamma returns the principal branch of the logarithm of the gamma function
at z. For Re(z) >= 0.5 it uses the Lanczos series directly, avoiding the
overflow that Gamma would suffer for large arguments; elsewhere it falls back
to Log(Gamma(z)).

funcs.name: NthRoots
funcs.doc:
NthRoots returns all n distinct n-th roots of z, ordered by increasing
argument starting from the principal root. It returns nil for n <= 0.

funcs.name: Polar
funcs.doc:
Polar constructs a complex number from a modulus r and an argument theta,
i.e. r*(cos(theta) + i*sin(theta)).

funcs.name: Pow
funcs.doc:
Pow returns z raised to the power w using the principal branch, exp(w*Log(z)).
Pow(0, 0) is defined to be 1.

funcs.name: PowerSeriesEval
funcs.signature: func PowerSeriesEval(coeffs []complex128, center, z complex128) complex128
funcs.doc:
PowerSeriesEval evaluates the power series with the given coefficients about
center at the point z, returning sum_j coeffs[j] * (z-center)^j via Horner's
method.

funcs.name: Re
funcs.doc: Re returns the real part of z.

funcs.name: Reciprocal
funcs.doc: Reciprocal returns 1/z.

funcs.name: Rect
funcs.doc: Rect constructs a complex number from its real and imaginary parts.

funcs.name: Residue
funcs.signature: func Residue(f Function, z0 complex128, radius float64, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
Residue approximates the residue of f at the isolated singularity z0 by
integrating f around a small circle of the given radius and dividing by
2*pi*i. The radius must enclose no singularity other than z0.

funcs.name: ResidueOrderM
funcs.signature: func ResidueOrderM(f Function, z0 complex128, m int, radius float64, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
ResidueOrderM returns the residue of f at a pole z0 of order m using the
derivative formula Res = lim_{z->z0} 1/(m-1)! d^{m-1}/dz^{m-1} [(z-z0)^m f(z)].
The (m-1)-th derivative is obtained from a Cauchy integral around a circle of
the given radius, so f must be analytic on and inside that circle except at
z0. It panics if m < 1.

funcs.name: ResidueSimplePole
funcs.signature: func ResidueSimplePole(f Function, z0 complex128, eps float64) complex128
funcs.doc:
ResidueSimplePole returns the residue of f at a simple pole z0, computed as
the limit of (z-z0)f(z) as z approaches z0 via a small symmetric average of
radius eps around z0.

funcs.name: RisingFactorial
funcs.signature: func RisingFactorial(z complex128, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
RisingFactorial returns the Pochhammer symbol (z)_n = z(z+1)...(z+n-1),
computed as Gamma(z+n)/Gamma(z). It returns 1 for n == 0.

funcs.name: Sec
funcs.doc: Sec returns the secant of z, 1/cos(z).

funcs.name: Sign
funcs.doc:
Sign returns z/|z|, the unit complex number with the same argument as z, or 0
when z is 0.

funcs.name: Sin
funcs.doc: Sin returns the sine of z.

funcs.name: Sinh
funcs.doc: Sinh returns the hyperbolic sine of z.

funcs.name: Sqrt
funcs.doc:
Sqrt returns the principal square root of z, the branch whose result lies in
the right half-plane (or on the non-negative imaginary axis).

funcs.name: Tan
funcs.doc: Tan returns the tangent of z.

funcs.name: Tanh
funcs.doc: Tanh returns the hyperbolic tangent of z.

funcs.name: TaylorCoefficient
funcs.signature: func TaylorCoefficient(f Function, center complex128, k int, radius float64, n int) complex128
funcs.doc:
TaylorCoefficient returns the k-th Taylor coefficient of an analytic function
f about center, a_k = f^(k)(center)/k!, computed from the same contour
integral as LaurentCoefficient. It panics if k < 0.

funcs.name: TaylorCoefficients
funcs.signature: func TaylorCoefficients(f Function, center complex128, count int, radius float64, n int) []complex128
funcs.doc:
TaylorCoefficients returns the first count Taylor coefficients (orders 0
through count-1) of an analytic function f about center. It returns nil for
count <= 0.

funcs.name: WindingNumber
funcs.signature: func WindingNumber(gamma func(float64) complex128, z0 complex128, n int) int
funcs.doc:
WindingNumber returns the winding number (index) of the closed curve gamma
about the point z0, computed by accumulating the change in the argument of
gamma(t)-z0 over t in [0, 1] with n samples and dividing by 2*pi. The result
is rounded to the nearest integer. z0 must not lie on the curve.

funcs.name: Zeta
funcs.doc:
Zeta returns the Riemann zeta function at s via the Dirichlet eta function
and analytic continuation, eta(s) = (1 - 2^(1-s)) zeta(s). It is valid across
the complex plane except at the pole s = 1, and is accurate to about 1e-12
for moderate |Im(s)|.

packages.name: contfrac
packages.synopsis: Package contfrac is a self-contained toolkit for continued fractions and Diophantine approximation, built entirely on the Go standard library (math, math/big, sort, errors, fmt, strings).
packages.doc:
Package contfrac is a self-contained toolkit for continued fractions and
Diophantine approximation, built entirely on the Go standard library
(math, math/big, sort, errors, fmt, strings).

It is a sibling subpackage of github.com/malcolmston/algebra but does not
depend on it. Everything is expressed in terms of a small set of value
types defined here:

  - [Frac] is an exact rational with int64 numerator and denominator, with
    the usual field of arithmetic and ordering operations plus conversions
    to and from continued fractions.
  - [CF] is a finite continued fraction [a0; a1, a2, ...] stored as a slice
    of partial quotients. The Euclidean/floor convention is used, so a0 may
    be zero or negative but every later term is a positive integer.
  - [QuadraticSurd] represents (P + sqrt(D))/Q and [PeriodicCF] represents an
    eventually periodic continued fraction; the two convert to one another,
    realising the classical theorem of Lagrange.

# Contents

Core expansion and evaluation: [FromRational], [FromFloat], [FromBigFloat],
[Convergents], [BestApproximation], [Continuant].

Quadratic irrationals: [SqrtCF], [SqrtCFPeriod], [QuadraticSurd.CF],
[PeriodicCF.Surd], and the Pell-equation solvers [PellFundamental],
[PellSolutions], [PellNegative].

Trees and sequences of fractions: [Mediant], [SternBrocotPath],
[SternBrocotFromPath], [FareySequence], [FareySuccessor], [PathToCF].

Decompositions and constants: [EgyptianFraction], [ECF], [PiCF],
[GoldenRatioCF].

All floating-point routines document their tolerance and every exact routine
is closed over the integers or math/big, so results are reproducible.

vars.doc: Common sentinel errors returned by the package.

types.name: CF
types.signature: type CF []int64
types.doc:
CF is a finite continued fraction [a0; a1, a2, ...] represented by its slice
of partial quotients. The floor convention is used: a0 may be zero or
negative, while every later term is a positive integer for a well-formed
expansion. An empty CF denotes the value 0.

funcs.name: ECF
funcs.doc: ECF returns the first n partial quotients of the continued fraction of e.

funcs.name: FromBigFloat
funcs.doc:
FromBigFloat returns up to n partial quotients of the continued fraction of a
*big.Float, using the float's own precision for each reciprocal step. It is
the high-precision counterpart of [FromFloat] and underlies [PiCF].

funcs.name: FromFloat
funcs.signature: func FromFloat(x float64, maxTerms int) CF
funcs.doc:
FromFloat returns up to maxTerms partial quotients of the continued fraction
of x, stopping early once the fractional part is negligible. It uses the
default tolerance 1e-12; see [FromFloatEps] for control over the stopping
criterion.

funcs.name: FromFloatEps
funcs.signature: func FromFloatEps(x float64, eps float64, maxTerms int) CF
funcs.doc:
FromFloatEps returns up to maxTerms partial quotients of the continued
fraction of x. Expansion stops once the remaining fractional part is at most
eps (indicating x is, to within tolerance, exactly represented) or once
maxTerms terms have been produced.

funcs.name: FromFrac
funcs.doc: FromFrac returns the continued-fraction expansion of f.

funcs.name: FromRational
funcs.doc:
FromRational returns the canonical continued-fraction expansion of the
rational p/q using the Euclidean algorithm. It panics if q == 0. Every
partial quotient after the first is positive, and the final term is greater
than one unless the expansion has a single term.

funcs.name: FromRationalBig
funcs.doc:
FromRationalBig returns the continued-fraction expansion of a *big.Rat as a
CF of int64 terms. The terms of a continued fraction are typically far
smaller than the numerator and denominator, so int64 terms suffice for
realistic inputs; callers needing unbounded terms should expand manually.

funcs.name: GoldenRatioCF
funcs.doc:
GoldenRatioCF returns the first n partial quotients of the continued fraction
of the golden ratio phi = (1 + sqrt(5))/2 = [1; 1, 1, 1, ...], which is all
ones.

funcs.name: ParseCF
funcs.doc:
ParseCF parses the bracket notation produced by [CF.String], for example
"[3; 7, 15, 1]" or "[3]". Whitespace is ignored and either ';' or ',' may
separate terms.

funcs.name: PathToCF
funcs.doc:
PathToCF converts a Stern-Brocot L/R path into the continued fraction of the
fraction it names. It is the inverse of [CFToPath]; the empty path yields the
continued fraction [1].

funcs.name: PiCF
funcs.doc:
PiCF returns the first n partial quotients of the continued fraction of pi,
which begins [3; 7, 15, 1, 292, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 3, 1, 14, ...]. The terms are
extracted from a high-precision computation of pi, so the result is exact for
the number of terms requested.

funcs.name: SqrtCFExpand
funcs.signature: func SqrtCFExpand(n int64, count int) CF
funcs.doc:
