Difference returns a new set containing the elements of a that are not in b
(the relative complement a \ b).

funcs.name: Intersection
funcs.doc:
Intersection returns a new set containing exactly the elements common to all
of the given sets. With no arguments it returns an empty set.

funcs.name: NewSet
funcs.doc: NewSet returns a new Set containing the given items, with duplicates removed.

funcs.name: SymmetricDifference
funcs.signature: func SymmetricDifference[T comparable](a, b Set[T]) Set[T]
funcs.doc:
SymmetricDifference returns a new set containing the elements that are in
exactly one of a and b.

funcs.name: Union
funcs.doc:
Union returns a new set containing every element that appears in any of the
given sets.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add inserts the given items into the set. Items already present are ignored.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent copy of the set.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc: Contains reports whether item is a member of the set.

methods.name: Elements
methods.doc: Elements returns the members of the set as a slice in unspecified order.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether the set has exactly the same members as other.

methods.name: IsDisjoint
methods.doc: IsDisjoint reports whether the receiver and other share no common element.

methods.name: IsSubset
methods.doc: IsSubset reports whether every element of the receiver is also in other.

methods.name: IsSuperset
methods.doc: IsSuperset reports whether the receiver contains every element of other.

methods.name: Len
methods.doc: Len returns the number of elements in the set (its cardinality).

methods.name: Remove
methods.doc: Remove deletes the given items from the set. Items not present are ignored.

funcs.name: BitString
funcs.signature: func BitString(x uint64, width int) string
funcs.doc:
BitString returns the base-2 representation of x, left-padded with zeros to at
least width characters. A width of zero or less yields the minimal-length
representation.

funcs.name: CartesianProductN
funcs.doc:
CartesianProductN returns the Cartesian product of any number of homogeneous
slices as a slice of tuples, each tuple having one element from each input
slice in order. The last input varies fastest. If any input slice is empty the
product is empty; with no inputs the result contains a single empty tuple.

funcs.name: ClearBit
funcs.signature: func ClearBit(x uint64, i uint) uint64
funcs.doc:
ClearBit returns x with bit i set to zero. Bits are numbered from zero at the
least-significant end.

funcs.name: ClearLowestSetBit
funcs.doc:
ClearLowestSetBit returns x with its lowest set bit cleared. It returns 0 when
x is zero.

funcs.name: DeBruijnSequence
funcs.doc:
DeBruijnSequence returns a De Bruijn sequence B(k, n): a sequence of symbols
drawn from the alphabet {0, 1, ..., k-1} of length k**n in which every possible
length-n string over the alphabet occurs exactly once as a (cyclic)
contiguous substring.

It uses the standard prefer-smallest generation via Lyndon words and returns
an error when k or n is less than one.

funcs.name: DeBruijnString
funcs.signature: func DeBruijnString(alphabet string, n int) (string, error)
funcs.doc:
DeBruijnString returns a De Bruijn sequence of order n over the given alphabet
as a string. The alphabet is treated as an ordered list of runes; every
length-n string over those runes appears exactly once as a cyclic substring.

It returns an error when the alphabet is empty or n is less than one.

funcs.name: FromBase
funcs.signature: func FromBase(s string, base int) (int64, error)
funcs.doc:
FromBase parses the string s as a signed integer in the given base, which must
be in the range 2..36. An optional leading plus or minus sign is accepted. It
returns an error for an out-of-range base, an empty string, an invalid digit,
or a value that does not fit in int64.

funcs.name: FromBaseUint
funcs.signature: func FromBaseUint(s string, base int) (uint64, error)
funcs.doc:
FromBaseUint parses the string s as an unsigned integer in the given base,
which must be in the range 2..36. An optional leading plus sign is accepted.
It returns an error for an out-of-range base, an empty string, an invalid
digit, or a value that overflows uint64.

funcs.name: GrayDecode
funcs.doc:
GrayDecode converts a reflected binary Gray code g back into the ordinary
binary value it represents. It is the inverse of GrayEncode.

funcs.name: GrayEncode
funcs.doc:
GrayEncode converts the binary value x into its reflected binary Gray code,
in which successive integers differ in exactly one bit.

funcs.name: HammingDistance
funcs.doc:
HammingDistance returns the number of bit positions at which a and b differ,
which is the population count of their exclusive-or.

funcs.name: HammingDistanceBytes
funcs.doc:
HammingDistanceBytes returns the number of differing bits between two equal
length byte slices. It returns an error if the slices differ in length.

funcs.name: HammingDistanceString
funcs.doc:
HammingDistanceString returns the number of positions at which two equal
length strings differ, comparing by rune. It returns an error when the strings
contain a different number of runes.

funcs.name: IntToRoman
funcs.doc:
IntToRoman converts an integer in the range 1..3999 into its canonical Roman
numeral using subtractive notation (for example 1994 becomes "MCMXCIV"). It
returns an error for values outside that range.

funcs.name: IntToWords
funcs.doc:
IntToWords converts a signed 64-bit integer into its English cardinal words,
covering the full int64 range up to quintillions. Zero renders as "zero" and
negative values are prefixed with "negative".

funcs.name: IntToWordsOrdinal
funcs.doc:
IntToWordsOrdinal converts a signed 64-bit integer into its English ordinal
words (for example 21 becomes "twenty-first" and 100 becomes "one hundredth").
Negative values are prefixed with "negative".

funcs.name: IsPowerOfTwo
funcs.doc: IsPowerOfTwo reports whether x is a positive power of two (1, 2, 4, 8, ...).

funcs.name: IsValidRoman
funcs.doc:
IsValidRoman reports whether s is a well-formed canonical Roman numeral in the
range 1..3999. Input is treated case-insensitively.

funcs.name: LeadingZeros
funcs.doc:
LeadingZeros returns the number of consecutive zero bits at the high end of x.
It returns 64 when x is zero.

funcs.name: LevenshteinDistance
funcs.doc:
LevenshteinDistance returns the Levenshtein edit distance between two strings:
the minimum number of single-character insertions, deletions and substitutions
needed to transform a into b. Comparison is by rune.

funcs.name: Log2Ceil
funcs.doc:
Log2Ceil returns the ceiling of the base-2 logarithm of x, equivalently the
smallest e such that 2**e >= x. It returns -1 when x is zero and 0 when x is
one.

funcs.name: Log2Floor
funcs.doc:
Log2Floor returns the floor of the base-2 logarithm of x, equivalently the
index of its highest set bit. It returns -1 when x is zero.

funcs.name: LongestCommonSubsequence
funcs.doc:
LongestCommonSubsequence returns one longest common subsequence of two strings
as a string, comparing by rune. When several subsequences share the maximum
length, the one preferring earlier positions in a is returned.

funcs.name: LongestCommonSubsequenceLength
funcs.doc:
LongestCommonSubsequenceLength returns the length of the longest common
subsequence of two strings, comparing by rune. A subsequence keeps relative
order but need not be contiguous.

funcs.name: LowestSetBit
funcs.doc:
LowestSetBit returns x with all bits cleared except its lowest set bit. It
returns 0 when x is zero.

funcs.name: MortonDecode2D
funcs.doc:
MortonDecode2D recovers the two coordinates x and y from a 2-D Morton code m.
It is the inverse of MortonEncode2D.

funcs.name: MortonDecode3D
funcs.signature: func MortonDecode3D(m uint64) (x, y, z uint32)
funcs.doc:
MortonDecode3D recovers the three coordinates x, y and z from a 3-D Morton
code m. It is the inverse of MortonEncode3D.

funcs.name: MortonEncode2D
funcs.doc:
MortonEncode2D interleaves the bits of x and y into a single Morton (Z-order)
code, with the bits of x occupying the even positions and the bits of y the
odd positions.

funcs.name: MortonEncode3D
funcs.signature: func MortonEncode3D(x, y, z uint32) uint64
funcs.doc:
MortonEncode3D interleaves the low 21 bits of x, y and z into a single 3-D
Morton (Z-order) code. Only the low 21 bits of each coordinate are used, so
the result fits in 63 bits.

funcs.name: NextPowerOfTwo
funcs.doc:
NextPowerOfTwo returns the smallest power of two that is greater than or equal
to x. It returns 1 for x of 0 or 1. The result is undefined (overflows to 0)
when x exceeds the largest representable power of two.

funcs.name: OptimalStringAlignmentDistance
funcs.doc:
OptimalStringAlignmentDistance returns the optimal string alignment distance
between two strings, the restricted form of the Damerau-Levenshtein distance
that additionally allows the transposition of two adjacent characters as a
single edit, subject to the constraint that no substring is edited more than
once. Comparison is by rune.

funcs.name: Parity
funcs.doc:
Parity returns the parity of x: 1 if the number of set bits is odd and 0 if
it is even.

funcs.name: PopCount
funcs.doc:
PopCount returns the number of one bits (the population count) in x.

It uses the classic SWAR (SIMD-within-a-register) algorithm and runs in a
fixed number of operations independent of the value of x.

funcs.name: PowerSet
funcs.doc:
PowerSet returns every subset of the given elements as a slice of slices. The
input is treated as an ordered collection; each output subset preserves the
relative order of the input, and the empty subset is included. The result has
2**len(elements) entries, so the input length must be modest.

funcs.name: PrevPowerOfTwo
funcs.doc:
PrevPowerOfTwo returns the largest power of two that is less than or equal to
x. It returns 0 when x is zero.

funcs.name: ReverseBits
funcs.doc:
ReverseBits returns x with the order of its 64 bits reversed, so that bit i of
the result equals bit 63-i of x.

funcs.name: ReverseBits32
funcs.doc: ReverseBits32 returns x with the order of its 32 bits reversed.

funcs.name: RomanToInt
funcs.doc:
RomanToInt converts a Roman numeral into its integer value. Input is treated
case-insensitively. The numeral must be in canonical (standard subtractive)
form; strings such as "IIII" or "VV" are rejected with an error, as are
strings containing non-Roman characters.

funcs.name: RotateLeft
funcs.signature: func RotateLeft(x uint64, k int) uint64
funcs.doc:
RotateLeft returns x rotated left by k bits within a 64-bit word. Negative
values of k rotate right; k is reduced modulo 64.

funcs.name: RotateRight
funcs.signature: func RotateRight(x uint64, k int) uint64
funcs.doc:
RotateRight returns x rotated right by k bits within a 64-bit word. Negative
values of k rotate left; k is reduced modulo 64.

funcs.name: RunLengthDecode
funcs.doc:
RunLengthDecode expands a sequence of runs back into the original slice. Runs
with a non-positive count contribute no elements.

funcs.name: RunLengthDecodeString
funcs.doc:
RunLengthDecodeString expands a string produced by RunLengthEncodeString back
into its original form. It returns an error if the input is not a sequence of
decimal-count / single-rune pairs.

funcs.name: RunLengthEncodeString
funcs.doc:
RunLengthEncodeString compresses a string into the form "<count><rune>" for
each maximal run (for example "aaabbc" becomes "3a2b1c"). The string is
processed by rune, so multi-byte UTF-8 characters are handled correctly.

funcs.name: SetBit
funcs.signature: func SetBit(x uint64, i uint) uint64
funcs.doc:
SetBit returns x with bit i set to one. Bits are numbered from zero at the
least-significant end.

funcs.name: TestBit
funcs.signature: func TestBit(x uint64, i uint) bool
funcs.doc:
TestBit reports whether bit i of x is set. Bits are numbered from zero at the
least-significant end.

funcs.name: ToBase
funcs.signature: func ToBase(n int64, base int) (string, error)
funcs.doc:
ToBase returns the representation of the signed integer n in the given base,
which must be in the range 2..36. Negative values are prefixed with a minus
sign. It returns an error for an out-of-range base.

funcs.name: ToBaseUint
funcs.signature: func ToBaseUint(n uint64, base int) (string, error)
funcs.doc:
ToBaseUint returns the representation of n in the given base, which must be in
the range 2..36. Digit values ten and above use lowercase letters. It returns
an error for an out-of-range base.

funcs.name: ToggleBit
funcs.signature: func ToggleBit(x uint64, i uint) uint64
funcs.doc:
ToggleBit returns x with bit i flipped. Bits are numbered from zero at the
least-significant end.

funcs.name: TrailingZeros
funcs.doc:
TrailingZeros returns the number of consecutive zero bits at the low end of x,
counted using a De Bruijn perfect-hash table. It returns 64 when x is zero.

packages.name: dynamical
packages.synopsis: Package dynamical provides primitives for the study of discrete and continuous dynamical systems, implemented with the Go standard library only.
packages.doc:
Package dynamical provides primitives for the study of discrete and
continuous dynamical systems, implemented with the Go standard library only.

The package is organized around a handful of small concepts:

  - Iterated one-dimensional maps ([Map1D]) such as the logistic, tent,
    sine, Gauss, cubic and circle maps, together with helpers to build
    parameterized families as closures.
  - Iterated two-dimensional maps ([Map2D]) over [Point2D] values, most
    notably the Henon map.
  - Orbits and trajectories: forward iteration with optional transient
    discarding, and single n-th iterate evaluation.
  - Fixed points and their linear stability, including closed-form fixed
    points and multipliers for the logistic and tent maps.
  - Lyapunov exponents estimated both from the analytic derivative and by
    the trajectory-separation (renormalization) method, in one and two
    dimensions.
  - Bifurcation-diagram sampling for parameterized one-dimensional families.
  - Period detection for periodic and eventually-periodic orbits.
  - Cobweb (staircase) diagram segment data for visualizing 1-D iteration.
  - Newton's method in the real and complex plane, and the basin-of-
    attraction grid produced by Newton iteration of a complex map.
  - Continuous flows in three dimensions ([Flow3D]) integrated with a
    fixed-step classical Runge-Kutta (RK4) scheme, with the Lorenz and
    Rossler systems provided as concrete examples.

All routines are deterministic: given the same inputs they produce the same
outputs, and no global random state is used.

types.name: BifurcationPoint
types.doc:
BifurcationPoint holds the sampled long-term states of a one-dimensional map
at a single parameter value. Param is the parameter; Values are the attractor
samples collected after discarding transients. Plotting Values against Param
for many parameters yields a bifurcation diagram.

funcs.name: Bifurcation
funcs.signature: func Bifurcation(family func(param float64) Map1D, pmin, pmax float64, steps int, x0 float64, transient, samples int) []BifurcationPoint
funcs.doc:
Bifurcation samples the asymptotic behavior of a one-parameter family of
one-dimensional maps. For each of steps parameter values evenly spaced from
pmin to pmax (inclusive), it builds the map family(param), iterates from x0
discarding transient points, then records the next samples iterates as the
attractor samples for that parameter. It returns one [BifurcationPoint] per
parameter value.

steps must be at least 1; a single step samples pmin only.

funcs.name: LogisticBifurcation
funcs.signature: func LogisticBifurcation(rmin, rmax float64, steps int, x0 float64, transient, samples int) []BifurcationPoint
funcs.doc:
LogisticBifurcation is a convenience wrapper around [Bifurcation] for the
logistic family x -> r*x*(1-x), sweeping r from rmin to rmax.

types.name: CobwebSegment
types.doc:
CobwebSegment is one line segment of a cobweb (staircase) diagram, joining
the point (X0, Y0) to (X1, Y1). Cobweb diagrams alternate vertical segments
(from the diagonal up or down to the graph of f) with horizontal segments
(from the graph across to the diagonal), visualizing the iteration of a
one-dimensional map.

funcs.name: Cobweb
funcs.signature: func Cobweb(f Map1D, x0 float64, n int) []CobwebSegment
funcs.doc:
Cobweb returns the segments of the cobweb diagram for n iterations of the
map f starting at x0. The construction begins on the diagonal at (x0, x0)
and, for each step, adds a vertical segment up to the graph (x, f(x))
followed by a horizontal segment across to the diagonal (f(x), f(x)). The
result therefore contains 2*n segments (for n >= 0).

types.name: Flow3D
types.signature: type Flow3D func(Vec3) Vec3
types.doc:
Flow3D is the vector field of a continuous three-dimensional dynamical
system: it maps a state s to its time derivative ds/dt = f(s).

types.name: LorenzParams
types.doc: LorenzParams holds the three parameters of the Lorenz system.

funcs.name: DefaultLorenz
funcs.doc:
DefaultLorenz returns the classical chaotic Lorenz parameters
sigma = 10, rho = 28, beta = 8/3.

methods.name: Field
methods.doc:
Field returns the Lorenz vector field

	dx/dt = sigma*(y - x)
	dy/dt = x*(rho - z) - y
	dz/dt = x*y - beta*z

as a [Flow3D] closure for these parameters.

methods.name: LorenzFixedPoints
methods.doc:
LorenzFixedPoints returns the equilibria of the Lorenz system with parameters
p. The origin is always an equilibrium; for rho > 1 the two symmetric points
C+ and C- at (+-sqrt(beta*(rho-1)), +-sqrt(beta*(rho-1)), rho-1) are included.

types.name: Map1D
types.signature: type Map1D func(float64) float64
types.doc:
Map1D is a real function of one real variable, used as the iteration rule of
a one-dimensional discrete dynamical system x_{n+1} = f(x_n).

funcs.name: CircleRotation
funcs.doc:
CircleRotation returns the rigid rotation theta -> theta + omega mod 1 as a
[Map1D] closure.

funcs.name: DoublingMap
funcs.doc: DoublingMap returns the doubling map x -> 2x mod 1 as a [Map1D] closure.

funcs.name: LogisticMap
funcs.doc:
LogisticMap returns the logistic map x -> r*x*(1-x) as a [Map1D] closure for
the fixed parameter r.

funcs.name: TentMap
funcs.doc: TentMap returns the tent map with slope parameter mu as a [Map1D] closure.

types.name: Map2D
types.signature: type Map2D func(Point2D) Point2D
types.doc:
Map2D is a map of the plane to itself, used as the iteration rule of a
two-dimensional discrete dynamical system p_{n+1} = f(p_n).

funcs.name: HenonMap
funcs.doc: HenonMap returns the Henon map with parameters a and b as a [Map2D] closure.

types.name: NewtonResult
types.doc:
NewtonResult holds the outcome of a complex Newton iteration: the point Root
reached, the number of Iterations performed, and whether the iteration
Converged to within the requested tolerance.

funcs.name: NewtonComplex
funcs.signature: func NewtonComplex(f, df func(complex128) complex128, z0 complex128, maxIter int, tol float64) NewtonResult
funcs.doc:
NewtonComplex applies Newton's method z -> z - f(z)/f'(z) in the complex
plane, starting at z0, using the function f and its derivative df. It stops
when |f(z)| <= tol (reporting convergence) or after maxIter steps.

types.name: Point2D
types.doc:
Point2D is a point in the plane, used as the state of a two-dimensional
discrete dynamical system.

funcs.name: HenonFixedPoints
funcs.doc:
HenonFixedPoints returns the real fixed points of the Henon map with
parameters a and b. They are the solutions of a*x^2 + (1-b)*x - 1 = 0 with
y = b*x; the slice is empty when the discriminant is negative and has one or
two entries otherwise.

funcs.name: NthIterate2D
funcs.signature: func NthIterate2D(f Map2D, p0 Point2D, n int) Point2D
funcs.doc: NthIterate2D returns the n-th iterate f^n(p0) of the two-dimensional map f.

funcs.name: Orbit2D
funcs.signature: func Orbit2D(f Map2D, p0 Point2D, n int) []Point2D
funcs.doc:
Orbit2D returns the forward orbit p_0, p_1, ..., p_n of the two-dimensional
map f starting from p0, as a slice of length n+1.

funcs.name: OrbitTransient2D
funcs.signature: func OrbitTransient2D(f Map2D, p0 Point2D, transient, n int) []Point2D
funcs.doc:
OrbitTransient2D iterates the map f from p0 discarding the first transient
points, then returns the following n+1 points of the orbit.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the vector sum p + q.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean norm (length) of p treated as a vector.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the point p with both coordinates multiplied by s.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the vector difference p - q.

types.name: RosslerParams
types.doc: RosslerParams holds the three parameters of the Rossler system.

funcs.name: DefaultRossler
funcs.doc:
DefaultRossler returns commonly used chaotic Rossler parameters
a = 0.2, b = 0.2, c = 5.7.

methods.name: Field
methods.doc:
Field returns the Rossler vector field

	dx/dt = -y - z
	dy/dt = x + a*y
	dz/dt = b + z*(x - c)

as a [Flow3D] closure for these parameters.

types.name: Stability
types.signature: type Stability int
types.doc:
Stability classifies the linear stability of a fixed or periodic point of a
one-dimensional map according to the magnitude of its multiplier (the map
derivative there).

funcs.name: ClassifyStability
funcs.doc:
ClassifyStability classifies a fixed point by its multiplier m using
tolerance tol to decide the borderline neutral case |m| == 1. A multiplier
of exactly zero is reported as [SuperStable].

funcs.name: LogisticStability
funcs.doc:
LogisticStability returns the stability class of each fixed point returned by
[LogisticFixedPoints] for parameter r, using tolerance tol for the neutral
case. The multiplier at 0 is r and at 1 - 1/r is 2 - r.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a human-readable name for the stability class.

types.name: Vec3
types.doc:
Vec3 is a point or vector in three-dimensional space, used as the state of a
continuous dynamical system.

funcs.name: Integrate3D
funcs.signature: func Integrate3D(f Flow3D, s0 Vec3, dt float64, n int) []Vec3
funcs.doc:
Integrate3D integrates the vector field f from the initial state s0 for n
steps of size dt using [RK4Step3D], returning the trajectory as a slice of
length n+1 whose first element is s0.

funcs.name: LorenzTrajectory
funcs.signature: func LorenzTrajectory(p LorenzParams, s0 Vec3, dt float64, n int) []Vec3
funcs.doc:
LorenzTrajectory integrates the Lorenz system with parameters p from state s0
for n RK4 steps of size dt, returning the trajectory (length n+1).

funcs.name: RK4Step3D
funcs.signature: func RK4Step3D(f Flow3D, s Vec3, dt float64) Vec3
funcs.doc:
RK4Step3D advances the state s by one step of size dt under the vector field
f using the classical fourth-order Runge-Kutta method, returning the new
state. The local truncation error is O(dt^5) per step.

funcs.name: RosslerTrajectory
funcs.signature: func RosslerTrajectory(p RosslerParams, s0 Vec3, dt float64, n int) []Vec3
funcs.doc:
RosslerTrajectory integrates the Rossler system with parameters p from state
s0 for n RK4 steps of size dt, returning the trajectory (length n+1).

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the vector sum v + w.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the dot product v . w.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean norm (length) of v.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns v with every component multiplied by s.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the vector difference v - w.

funcs.name: CircleMap
funcs.signature: func CircleMap(omega, k, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc:
CircleMap evaluates the standard circle map
f(theta) = theta + omega - (k/2pi)*sin(2pi*theta), reduced modulo 1.
With k = 0 it reduces to rigid rotation by omega.

funcs.name: ClassifyRoot
funcs.signature: func ClassifyRoot(roots []complex128, z complex128, tol float64) int
funcs.doc:
ClassifyRoot returns the index in roots of the entry nearest to z, provided
that nearest entry lies within tol; otherwise it returns -1. It is used to
label which basin of attraction a Newton iterate has fallen into.

funcs.name: CubicMap
funcs.doc: CubicMap evaluates the cubic map f(x) = a*x - x^3 at x with parameter a.

funcs.name: DetectPeriod
funcs.signature: func DetectPeriod(f Map1D, x0 float64, transient, maxPeriod int, tol float64) int
funcs.doc:
DetectPeriod attempts to determine the period of the orbit of f starting at
x0. It first iterates transient times to settle onto the attractor, records
the resulting reference point, then searches for the smallest period p in
1..maxPeriod such that the p-th further iterate returns to within tol of the
reference point. It returns that period, or 0 if none is found within
maxPeriod (for example on a chaotic or very long orbit).

funcs.name: DetectPeriod2D
funcs.signature: func DetectPeriod2D(f Map2D, p0 Point2D, transient, maxPeriod int, tol float64) int
funcs.doc:
DetectPeriod2D is the two-dimensional analogue of [DetectPeriod] for a map of
the plane, measuring return in Euclidean distance.

funcs.name: Doubling
funcs.doc: Doubling evaluates the dyadic doubling (Bernoulli) map f(x) = 2x mod 1.

funcs.name: FixedPoint1D
funcs.signature: func FixedPoint1D(f, df Map1D, x0 float64, maxIter int, tol float64) (root float64, iters int, converged bool)
funcs.doc:
FixedPoint1D finds a fixed point of the map f near x0 by applying Newton's
method to g(x) = f(x) - x, using the analytic derivative df of f. It returns
the located point, the number of iterations performed and whether the
iteration converged to within tol.

funcs.name: GaussMap
funcs.signature: func GaussMap(alpha, beta, x float64) float64
funcs.doc: GaussMap evaluates the Gauss (mouse) map f(x) = exp(-alpha*x^2) + beta.

funcs.name: Henon
funcs.signature: func Henon(a, b, x, y float64) (float64, float64)
funcs.doc:
Henon evaluates one step of the Henon map
(x, y) -> (1 - a*x^2 + y, b*x) with parameters a and b.

funcs.name: HenonJacobian
funcs.signature: func HenonJacobian(a, b float64, p Point2D) [2][2]float64
funcs.doc:
HenonJacobian returns the 2x2 Jacobian matrix of the Henon map at point p,
stored in row-major order: {{-2*a*x, 1}, {b, 0}}.

funcs.name: IsFixedPoint
funcs.signature: func IsFixedPoint(f Map1D, x, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc:
IsFixedPoint reports whether x is a fixed point of f, that is whether
|f(x) - x| <= tol.

funcs.name: IsPeriodicPoint
funcs.signature: func IsPeriodicPoint(f Map1D, x0 float64, period int, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc:
IsPeriodicPoint reports whether x0 is a periodic point of f with period
exactly period, that is whether f^period(x0) returns to within tol of x0
while no smaller positive multiple of the iterate does. A period <= 0
returns false.

funcs.name: Logistic
funcs.doc:
Logistic evaluates the logistic map f(x) = r*x*(1-x) at x with parameter r.
For r in [0,4] and x in [0,1] the unit interval is mapped into itself.

funcs.name: LogisticDeriv
funcs.doc: LogisticDeriv evaluates the derivative f'(x) = r*(1-2x) of the logistic map.

funcs.name: LogisticFixedPoints
funcs.doc:
LogisticFixedPoints returns the fixed points of the logistic map with
parameter r. There is always the origin 0; the second fixed point 1 - 1/r
exists (and is distinct) for r != 0 and is returned whenever r is nonzero.

funcs.name: Lyapunov
funcs.signature: func Lyapunov(f, df Map1D, x0 float64, transient, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
Lyapunov estimates the Lyapunov exponent of the one-dimensional map f from
the analytic derivative df. Starting at x0 it discards the first transient
iterates, then averages log|df(x)| over the next n iterates. The result is
the mean exponential rate of separation of nearby trajectories; a positive
value indicates sensitive dependence on initial conditions (chaos).

For the tent map with slope mu the derivative has constant magnitude mu, so
the estimate equals log(mu) exactly; for the logistic map at r = 4 it
approaches log(2).

funcs.name: Lyapunov2D
funcs.signature: func Lyapunov2D(f Map2D, jac func(Point2D) [2][2]float64, p0 Point2D, transient, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
Lyapunov2D estimates the largest Lyapunov exponent of a two-dimensional map f
whose Jacobian at any point is supplied by jac (row-major 2x2). A tangent
vector is transported along the orbit by repeated multiplication with the
Jacobian and renormalized to unit length each step; the accumulated
logarithmic growth divided by n is returned. The first transient iterates of
the base orbit are discarded first.

funcs.name: LyapunovHenon
funcs.signature: func LyapunovHenon(a, b float64, transient, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
LyapunovHenon estimates the largest Lyapunov exponent of the Henon map with
parameters a and b by [Lyapunov2D], starting from the origin. For the
classical parameters a = 1.4, b = 0.3 the exponent is approximately 0.419.

funcs.name: LyapunovSeparation
funcs.signature: func LyapunovSeparation(f Map1D, x0, d0 float64, transient, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
LyapunovSeparation estimates the Lyapunov exponent of the map f without an
analytic derivative, using the trajectory-separation (renormalization)
method. Two orbits are launched an initial distance d0 apart; after each
step the separation is measured, its logarithmic growth accumulated, and the
perturbed orbit rescaled back to distance d0. The first transient iterates
are discarded before accumulation begins.

funcs.name: Multiplier
funcs.signature: func Multiplier(df Map1D, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Multiplier evaluates the derivative df at x, i.e. the multiplier of the map
at the point x. For a fixed point this determines its linear stability.

funcs.name: NewtonBasin
funcs.signature: func NewtonBasin(f, df func(complex128) complex128, roots []complex128, xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax float64, nx, ny, maxIter int, tol float64) [][]int
funcs.doc:
NewtonBasin computes the Newton basin-of-attraction grid for the complex map
f with derivative df over the rectangle [xmin,xmax] x [ymin,ymax], sampled on
an nx-by-ny grid. Each grid point is used as a starting value for Newton's
method; the resulting root is matched against the supplied roots by
[ClassifyRoot]. The returned matrix has ny rows and nx columns in row-major
order: entry [j][i] is the index of the root that the sample at column i,
row j converged to, or -1 if it did not converge to any listed root.

funcs.name: NewtonReal
funcs.signature: func NewtonReal(f, df Map1D, x0 float64, maxIter int, tol float64) (root float64, iters int, converged bool)
funcs.doc:
NewtonReal applies Newton's method x -> x - f(x)/f'(x) on the real line,
starting at x0, using the function f and its derivative df. It returns the
located root, the number of iterations performed, and whether |f(x)| <= tol
was achieved.

funcs.name: NthIterate
funcs.signature: func NthIterate(f Map1D, x0 float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
NthIterate returns the n-th iterate f^n(x0) of the map f. For n <= 0 it
returns x0 unchanged.

funcs.name: Orbit
funcs.signature: func Orbit(f Map1D, x0 float64, n int) []float64
funcs.doc:
Orbit returns the forward orbit x_0, x_1, ..., x_n of the one-dimensional
map f starting from x0, that is a slice of length n+1 whose first element is
x0 and whose k-th element is the k-th iterate of f applied to x0.

funcs.name: OrbitTransient
funcs.signature: func OrbitTransient(f Map1D, x0 float64, transient, n int) []float64
funcs.doc:
OrbitTransient iterates the map f from x0 discarding the first transient
points, then returns the following n+1 points of the orbit. It is used to
sample the attractor of f while suppressing initial transient behavior.

funcs.name: QuadraticMap
funcs.doc:
QuadraticMap evaluates the quadratic map f(x) = x^2 + c, the canonical form
to which every real quadratic map is conjugate.

funcs.name: SineMap
funcs.doc: SineMap evaluates the sine map f(x) = r*sin(pi*x) at x with parameter r.

funcs.name: Tent
funcs.doc:
Tent evaluates the tent map at x with slope parameter mu: the value is mu*x
for x < 1/2 and mu*(1-x) for x >= 1/2.

funcs.name: TentFixedPoints
funcs.doc:
TentFixedPoints returns the fixed points of the tent map with slope mu.
The origin 0 is always fixed; for mu > 1 the point mu/(1+mu) in the right
branch is fixed as well and is included.

packages.name: ecc
packages.synopsis: Package ecc implements elliptic-curve arithmetic in short Weierstrass form y^2 = x^3 + A*x + B, both over a prime finite field GF(p) and over the field of rational numbers Q.
packages.doc:
Package ecc implements elliptic-curve arithmetic in short Weierstrass form
y^2 = x^3 + A*x + B, both over a prime finite field GF(p) and over the field
of rational numbers Q.

The package provides the geometric group law (point addition, doubling,
negation and scalar multiplication), curve invariants (discriminant and
j-invariant), point-counting utilities (naive enumeration and a
baby-step/giant-step point-order routine), SEC1 point compression and
decompression, and the finite-field number theory required to build higher
level protocols on top: an Elliptic-Curve Diffie-Hellman shared-secret
derivation and the ECDSA signing and verification equations over GF(p).

Two concrete curve types are exposed:

  - CurveFp models a curve over the prime field GF(p) using math/big
    integers. Its points are affine PointFp values with an explicit
    point-at-infinity flag.
  - CurveQ models a curve over the rationals Q using math/big rationals. Its
    points are affine PointQ values, likewise carrying an infinity flag.

All routines are deterministic and depend only on the Go standard library.
Functions never mutate their big.Int or big.Rat arguments; every result is a
freshly allocated value.

types.name: CompressedPoint
types.doc:
CompressedPoint holds the SEC1-style compressed representation of an affine
point: its x-coordinate together with a single parity bit selecting one of
the two possible y values.

types.name: CurveFp
types.doc:
CurveFp is a short Weierstrass curve y^2 = x^3 + A*x + B defined over the
prime field GF(P). The parameters must satisfy the non-singularity condition
4*A^3 + 27*B^2 != 0 (mod P), which NewCurveFp enforces.

funcs.name: NewCurveFp
funcs.doc:
NewCurveFp constructs a curve y^2 = x^3 + a*x + b over GF(p). The prime p must
be greater than 3 and the curve must be non-singular (its discriminant must
be non-zero modulo p); otherwise a non-nil error is returned. The coefficients
a and b are copied and reduced modulo p, so the caller may reuse them freely.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc:
Add returns the sum p + q under the elliptic-curve group law. It handles the
identity, the doubling case, and inverse pairs summing to infinity.

methods.name: Cofactor
methods.doc:
Cofactor returns the cofactor #E(GF(p)) / n, the index of the order-n subgroup
in the full group, together with a boolean that is false when n does not
divide the group order. The full order is obtained by naive point counting,
so this is intended for small primes.

methods.name: Compress
methods.doc:
Compress returns the compressed representation of pt, storing its
x-coordinate and the parity of its y-coordinate. The point at infinity is
encoded with the Infinity flag set.

methods.name: CompressBytes
methods.doc:
CompressBytes returns the SEC1 compressed octet string of pt: a single byte
0x00 for the point at infinity, or the prefix 0x02 (even y) or 0x03 (odd y)
followed by the x-coordinate encoded as a big-endian fixed-length field
element. The field element length is ceil(bitlen(P)/8).

methods.name: CountPointsNaive
methods.doc:
CountPointsNaive returns the order #E(GF(p)) of the curve group, that is the
number of affine points plus one for the point at infinity, by enumerating
every x in GF(p) and counting the corresponding square roots. Its running
time is proportional to p, so it is intended for small primes and for
validating faster routines.

methods.name: Decompress
methods.doc:
Decompress reconstructs the affine point from a compressed representation by
solving y^2 = x^3 + A*x + B for y and selecting the root matching the stored
parity bit. It returns an error if the x-coordinate is not the abscissa of
any curve point (the right-hand side is a non-residue).

methods.name: DecompressBytes
methods.doc:
DecompressBytes parses a SEC1 compressed octet string produced by
CompressBytes and returns the corresponding affine point. It returns an error
for a malformed prefix, an incorrect length, or an x-coordinate that is not
on the curve.

methods.name: Discriminant
methods.doc:
Discriminant returns the curve discriminant -16*(4*A^3 + 27*B^2) reduced
modulo P. A curve is non-singular exactly when this value is non-zero.

methods.name: Double
methods.doc:
Double returns 2*pt using the tangent-line group law. Doubling a point whose
y-coordinate is zero yields the point at infinity.

methods.name: ECDHSharedSecret
methods.signature: func (c *CurveFp) ECDHSharedSecret(d *big.Int, q PointFp) (secret *big.Int, shared PointFp, ok bool)
methods.doc:
ECDHSharedSecret computes the Elliptic-Curve Diffie-Hellman shared secret
d*Q, where d is the local private scalar and Q is the remote party's public
point. It returns the x-coordinate of the shared point, the conventional
shared secret value, together with the full shared point. The boolean result
is false if the shared point is the point at infinity, which indicates an
invalid or small-subgroup public key.

methods.name: ECDSASign
methods.signature: func (c *CurveFp) ECDSASign(g PointFp, n, d, e, k *big.Int) (ECDSASignature, error)
methods.doc:
ECDSASign produces an ECDSA signature (r, s) for the integer message digest e
under the private key d, using the base point G of prime subgroup order n and
the caller-supplied per-message secret k. Supplying k explicitly keeps the
routine deterministic and testable; production callers must draw a fresh,
uniformly random, secret k in [1, n) for every signature. It returns an error
when k is out of range or when the resulting r or s would be zero, in which
case the caller should retry with a different k.

methods.name: ECDSAVerify
methods.signature: func (c *CurveFp) ECDSAVerify(g, q PointFp, n, e *big.Int, sig ECDSASignature) bool
methods.doc:
ECDSAVerify checks an ECDSA signature (r, s) against the message digest e and
the public key Q, using the base point G of prime subgroup order n. It returns
true exactly when the signature is valid. The signature components must lie in
[1, n); values outside that range are rejected.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc:
Equal reports whether two points on the curve are equal, treating all
point-at-infinity values as a single element.

methods.name: GenerateKeyPair
methods.signature: func (c *CurveFp) GenerateKeyPair(g PointFp, n *big.Int, reader io.Reader) (priv *big.Int, pub PointFp, err error)
methods.doc:
GenerateKeyPair draws a private scalar uniformly from [1, n) using the
supplied reader and returns it together with the public point priv*G. It is
deterministic given a deterministic reader, which makes it testable; callers
wanting real keys should pass crypto/rand.Reader. It returns an error if the
reader fails.

methods.name: HasseInterval
methods.doc:
HasseInterval returns the closed interval [lo, hi] of admissible group orders
#E(GF(p)) guaranteed by the Hasse theorem: lo = p + 1 - 2*floor(sqrt(p)) - 1
and hi = p + 1 + 2*floor(sqrt(p)) + 1, a superset of the true bound that is
safe to use as an integer search window.

methods.name: Identity
methods.doc:
Identity returns the point at infinity, the neutral element of the curve
group.

methods.name: IsGenerator
methods.doc:
IsGenerator reports whether pt has order exactly n in the curve group, i.e.
whether n*pt is the identity and pt generates a subgroup of size n. It uses
the baby-step/giant-step order routine.

methods.name: IsOnCurve
methods.doc:
IsOnCurve reports whether pt lies on the curve. The point at infinity is
always considered to be on the curve.

methods.name: IsSmooth
methods.doc:
IsSmooth reports whether the curve is non-singular over GF(P), i.e. whether
its discriminant is non-zero. Curves built with NewCurveFp are always smooth.

methods.name: JInvariant
methods.doc:
JInvariant returns the j-invariant 1728 * 4*A^3 / (4*A^3 + 27*B^2) of the
curve, reduced modulo P. Curves with equal j-invariant are isomorphic over
the algebraic closure of GF(P). It panics only if the curve is singular,
which NewCurveFp precludes.

methods.name: Negate
methods.doc:
Negate returns the additive inverse -pt, the reflection of pt across the
x-axis. The inverse of the point at infinity is itself.

methods.name: NewPoint
methods.doc:
NewPoint constructs the affine point (x, y) on the curve, reducing the
coordinates modulo P. It returns an error if the point does not satisfy the
curve equation.

methods.name: OnCurveX
methods.doc:
OnCurveX reports whether the field element x is the abscissa of some point on
the curve, i.e. whether x^3 + A*x + B is a square modulo P.

methods.name: PointOrderBSGS
methods.doc:
PointOrderBSGS returns the order of the point pt using the baby-step
giant-step algorithm, which runs in time proportional to sqrt(#E) rather than
#E. It first locates a multiple of the order inside the Hasse window and then
reduces that multiple to the exact order by dividing out prime factors. The
point at infinity has order 1.

methods.name: PointOrderNaive
methods.doc:
PointOrderNaive returns the order of the point pt in the curve group: the
least positive n with n*pt equal to the point at infinity. It repeatedly adds
pt, bounded by the upper end of the Hasse interval, and returns nil if no such
n is found within that bound (which cannot happen for a point actually on the
curve). The point at infinity has order 1.

methods.name: PublicKeyFp
methods.doc:
PublicKeyFp derives the public key d*G from a private scalar d and a base
point G on the curve. The scalar d must be non-zero; the returned point is
the caller's public key.

methods.name: RHS
methods.doc:
RHS returns the right-hand side x^3 + A*x + B of the curve equation evaluated
at x, reduced modulo P.

methods.name: SameJInvariant
methods.doc:
SameJInvariant reports whether this curve and other share the same
j-invariant over the same base field, a necessary condition for the two
curves to be isomorphic over the algebraic closure of GF(p). It returns false
if the curves have different moduli.

methods.name: ScalarMul
methods.doc:
ScalarMul returns k*pt using the constant-structure double-and-add algorithm.
A negative scalar is handled by negating the point, so k*pt = (-k)*(-pt), and
a zero scalar yields the point at infinity.

methods.name: TraceOfFrobenius
methods.doc:
TraceOfFrobenius returns the trace of Frobenius t = p + 1 - #E(GF(p)), where
#E is the group order. By the Hasse bound |t| <= 2*sqrt(p).

types.name: CurveQ
types.doc:
CurveQ is a short Weierstrass curve y^2 = x^3 + A*x + B defined over the
rational numbers Q. The parameters must satisfy 4*A^3 + 27*B^2 != 0, which
NewCurveQ enforces.

funcs.name: NewCurveQ
funcs.doc:
NewCurveQ constructs a curve y^2 = x^3 + a*x + b over Q. The curve must be
non-singular (its discriminant must be non-zero); otherwise a non-nil error
is returned. The coefficients are copied, so the caller may reuse them.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc:
Add returns the sum p + q over Q under the elliptic-curve group law. It
handles the identity, the doubling case, and inverse pairs summing to
infinity.

methods.name: Discriminant
methods.doc:
Discriminant returns the exact rational discriminant -16*(4*A^3 + 27*B^2) of
the curve over Q. A curve is non-singular exactly when this value is non-zero.

methods.name: Double
methods.doc:
Double returns 2*pt over Q using the tangent-line group law. Doubling a point
with zero y-coordinate yields the point at infinity.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc:
Equal reports whether two rational points are equal, treating all
point-at-infinity values as a single element.

methods.name: Identity
methods.doc:
Identity returns the point at infinity, the neutral element of the curve
group over Q.

methods.name: IsOnCurve
methods.doc:
IsOnCurve reports whether the rational point pt lies on the curve. The point
at infinity is always considered to be on the curve.

methods.name: JInvariant
methods.doc:
JInvariant returns the exact rational j-invariant
1728 * 4*A^3 / (4*A^3 + 27*B^2) of the curve over Q. It panics only if the
curve is singular, which NewCurveQ precludes.

methods.name: Negate
methods.doc:
Negate returns the additive inverse -pt, the reflection of pt across the
x-axis. The inverse of the point at infinity is itself.

methods.name: NewPoint
methods.doc:
NewPoint constructs the affine rational point (x, y) on the curve. It returns
an error if the point does not satisfy the curve equation.

methods.name: RHS
methods.doc:
RHS returns the exact value x^3 + A*x + B of the curve equation's right-hand
side evaluated at the rational x.

methods.name: ScalarMul
methods.doc:
ScalarMul returns k*pt over Q using double-and-add. A negative scalar negates
the point, and a zero scalar yields the point at infinity. Because rational
coordinates grow rapidly under repeated addition, this is intended for small
scalars.

types.name: ECDSASignature
types.doc: ECDSASignature holds the pair (R, S) produced by the ECDSA signing equation.

types.name: NamedCurve
types.doc:
NamedCurve bundles a curve over GF(p) with the standard domain parameters used
by cryptographic protocols: a fixed base point G that generates a subgroup of
prime order N, and the cofactor H = #E / N.

funcs.name: P256
funcs.doc:
P256 returns the NIST P-256 curve (also called secp256r1 or prime256v1),
y^2 = x^3 - 3*x + B over a 256-bit prime field, together with its generator,
subgroup order and cofactor 1.

funcs.name: Secp256k1
funcs.doc:
Secp256k1 returns the standard SEC 2 curve secp256k1, y^2 = x^3 + 7 over a
256-bit prime field, together with its generator, subgroup order and cofactor
1. This is the curve used by, among others, Bitcoin.

methods.name: GenerateKeyPair
methods.signature: func (nc NamedCurve) GenerateKeyPair(reader io.Reader) (priv *big.Int, pub PointFp, err error)
methods.doc:
GenerateKeyPair draws a private key for the named curve from the supplied
reader and returns it together with the corresponding public point. It is a
convenience wrapper over CurveFp.GenerateKeyPair using the curve's standard
base point and subgroup order.

types.name: PointFp
types.doc:
PointFp is an affine point on a curve over GF(p). The zero of the group, the
point at infinity, is represented by Infinity set to true, in which case X
and Y are ignored.

types.name: PointQ
types.doc:
PointQ is an affine point on a curve over the rational field Q. The zero of
the group, the point at infinity, is represented by Infinity set to true, in
which case X and Y are ignored.

funcs.name: DiscriminantFp
funcs.doc:
DiscriminantFp returns the discriminant -16*(4*a^3 + 27*b^2) of the curve
y^2 = x^3 + a*x + b modulo p, without constructing a CurveFp. The modulus p
must be positive.

funcs.name: IsQuadraticResidue
funcs.doc:
IsQuadraticResidue reports whether a is a non-zero quadratic residue modulo
the odd prime p, i.e. whether there exists x with x^2 = a (mod p) and
a is not congruent to 0.

funcs.name: JInvariantFp
funcs.doc:
JInvariantFp returns the j-invariant 1728 * 4*a^3 / (4*a^3 + 27*b^2) of the
curve y^2 = x^3 + a*x + b modulo p, without constructing a CurveFp, together
with a boolean that is false when the curve is singular (its discriminant is
zero, making the j-invariant undefined).

funcs.name: Legendre
funcs.doc:
Legendre returns the Legendre symbol (a / p) for an odd prime p, one of the
values -1, 0 or +1: 0 when p divides a, +1 when a is a non-zero quadratic
residue modulo p, and -1 when a is a quadratic non-residue. It is implemented
via the Jacobi symbol, which coincides with the Legendre symbol for prime p.

funcs.name: Mod
funcs.doc:
Mod returns the representative of a modulo p in the range [0, p). The modulus
p must be positive. Unlike the raw big.Int remainder, the result is always
non-negative.

funcs.name: ModAdd
funcs.doc: ModAdd returns (a + b) mod p in the range [0, p) for a positive modulus p.

funcs.name: ModDiv
funcs.doc:
ModDiv returns (a / b) mod p, that is a times the modular inverse of b, along
with a boolean reporting whether b is invertible modulo p. When b is not
invertible the returned integer is nil.

funcs.name: ModExp
funcs.doc:
ModExp returns (base ^ exp) mod p for a positive modulus p. Negative
exponents are supported when base is invertible modulo p; in that case the
modular inverse of base is raised to the magnitude of exp.

funcs.name: ModInverse
funcs.doc:
ModInverse returns the multiplicative inverse of a modulo p together with a
boolean reporting whether the inverse exists. The inverse exists exactly when
gcd(a, p) = 1. When it does not exist the returned integer is nil.

funcs.name: ModMul
funcs.doc: ModMul returns (a * b) mod p in the range [0, p) for a positive modulus p.

funcs.name: ModNeg
funcs.doc: ModNeg returns (-a) mod p in the range [0, p) for a positive modulus p.

funcs.name: ModSqrt
funcs.doc:
ModSqrt returns a square root of a modulo an odd prime p together with a
boolean reporting whether a is a quadratic residue. When a has no square root
the returned integer is nil. The smaller of the two roots r and p-r is
returned, so the result is canonical. This routine assumes p is prime.

funcs.name: ModSub
funcs.doc: ModSub returns (a - b) mod p in the range [0, p) for a positive modulus p.

packages.name: exterioralgebra
packages.synopsis: Package exterioralgebra implements the exterior (Grassmann) algebra Λ(Rⁿ) together with the calculus of differential forms on Rⁿ.
packages.doc:
Package exterioralgebra implements the exterior (Grassmann) algebra Λ(Rⁿ)
together with the calculus of differential forms on Rⁿ.

The central value type is [Form], an element of the exterior algebra over an
n-dimensional real vector space. A Form is a (possibly mixed-grade)
multivector: a real linear combination of basis blades e_{i₁}∧…∧e_{i_k},
with 0 ≤ i₁ < … < i_k < n. Each basis blade is encoded compactly as a
bitmask whose set bits are the participating indices, so the grade of a
blade is the population count of its mask. Coefficients are stored sparsely,
so only the nonzero terms occupy memory.

On Forms the package provides:

  - construction ([New], [Scalar], [Basis1], [BasisBlade], [FromVector],
    [VolumeForm], [One]) and inspection ([Form.Coeff], [Form.Terms],
    [Form.Grade], [Form.Grades], [Form.IsHomogeneous], [Form.String]);
  - the graded-vector-space operations ([Form.Add], [Form.Sub], [Form.Neg],
    [Form.Scale], [Form.GradeProject], [Form.ScalarPart]);
  - the exterior (wedge) product ([Wedge], [Form.Wedge], [WedgeAll],
    [Form.WedgePow]);
  - the interior product / contraction ([InteriorProduct],
    [Form.LeftContract], [Form.RightContract]) and the Euclidean inner
    product and norm ([InnerProduct], [Form.Norm]);
  - the Hodge star for the Euclidean and for a general diagonal metric
    ([HodgeStar], [HodgeStarMetric], [InverseHodgeStar]), verified against
    the de Rham identity ** = (−1)^{k(n−k)} id.

For the differential calculus, [Poly] is a dense-free multivariate real
polynomial and [PForm] is a differential form whose coefficients are
polynomials. Because polynomial partial derivatives are computed exactly,
the exterior derivative [PForm.ExteriorDerivative] satisfies d² = 0 exactly,
the pullback [PForm.Pullback] along a polynomial map commutes with d, and
the Hodge–de Rham Laplacian [PForm.HodgeLaplacian] reproduces the componentwise
scalar Laplacian (up to the geometer's sign). A numerical exterior derivative
([NumExteriorDerivative]) is also provided for arbitrary smooth coefficient
fields.

All computation is deterministic and uses only the Go standard library.
Operations that cannot express failure in their signature (for example
[Form.Coeff]) return a neutral value on misuse; the others return one of the
sentinel errors [ErrDim], [ErrIndex] or [ErrGrade].

vars.signature: var ErrDim = errors.New("exterioralgebra: incompatible or invalid dimension")
vars.doc:
ErrDim is returned when two operands live in exterior algebras of different
ambient dimension, or when a supplied dimension is invalid.

vars.signature: var ErrGrade = errors.New("exterioralgebra: invalid or incompatible grade")
vars.doc:
ErrGrade is returned when a grade argument is negative or exceeds the ambient
dimension, or when a homogeneous operation receives a mixed-grade Form.

vars.signature: var ErrIndex = errors.New("exterioralgebra: basis index out of range or repeated")
vars.doc:
ErrIndex is returned when a basis index is negative, is not less than the
ambient dimension, or when a blade specification repeats an index.

vars.signature: var ErrMap = errors.New("exterioralgebra: malformed polynomial map")
vars.doc:
ErrMap is returned when a polynomial map has the wrong number of components
for the requested pullback, or when its components disagree on arity.

types.name: Form
types.doc:
Form is an element of the exterior algebra Λ(Rⁿ): a real linear combination
of basis blades. Each basis blade e_{i₁}∧…∧e_{i_k} (with i₁<…<i_k) is keyed
by the bitmask whose set bits are its indices, and only nonzero coefficients
are stored. A Form therefore represents an arbitrary, possibly mixed-grade,
multivector or constant differential form.

The zero value is not usable; construct Forms with [New] and friends.

funcs.name: AddAll
funcs.doc:
AddAll returns the sum of any number of Forms, all of which must share an
ambient dimension. With no arguments it panics, since the dimension is
unknown; use [New] for an explicit zero.

funcs.name: AllBasisBlades
funcs.doc:
AllBasisBlades returns all 2ⁿ basis blades of Λ(Rⁿ) as unit Forms, ordered by
grade and then by mask.

funcs.name: Basis
funcs.doc:
Basis returns every grade-k basis blade of Λ(Rⁿ) as a unit-coefficient Form,
listed in canonical (increasing mask) order. The result has GradeDimension(n,k)
entries.

funcs.name: Basis1
funcs.doc:
Basis1 returns the grade-1 basis Form e_i in Λ(Rⁿ). It panics if i is out of
range.

funcs.name: BasisBlade
funcs.doc:
BasisBlade returns the basis blade e_{idx[0]}∧…∧e_{idx[k-1]} in Λ(Rⁿ) with
unit coefficient, reduced to its canonical sorted representative with the
appropriate sign. If two indices coincide the blade is zero; the returned
error is non-nil only when an index is out of range.

funcs.name: BasisBladeCoeff
funcs.signature: func BasisBladeCoeff(n int, c float64, idx ...int) (*Form, error)
funcs.doc:
BasisBladeCoeff returns c·e_{idx[0]}∧…∧e_{idx[k-1]} in Λ(Rⁿ). It behaves like
[BasisBlade] but scales the blade by c.

funcs.name: FromMasks
funcs.signature: func FromMasks(n int, terms map[uint]float64) (*Form, error)
funcs.doc:
FromMasks builds a Form in Λ(Rⁿ) directly from a map of blade bitmasks to
coefficients. Masks with bits at or beyond position n produce [ErrIndex].

funcs.name: FromVector
funcs.doc:
FromVector returns the grade-1 Form whose e_i coefficient is v[i]. The ambient
dimension of the result equals len(v).

funcs.name: HodgeStar
funcs.doc:
HodgeStar returns the Euclidean Hodge dual ★f, computed with the positively
oriented orthonormal frame and volume element e_0∧…∧e_{n-1}. On a grade-k
blade e_I it returns the sign-corrected complementary blade so that
e_I ∧ ★e_I = vol. It maps grade k to grade n−k and satisfies
★★ = (−1)^{k(n−k)} on grade-k Forms.

funcs.name: HodgeStarMetric
funcs.doc:
HodgeStarMetric returns the Hodge dual of f with respect to a pseudo-Euclidean
metric whose diagonal signature entries are each +1 or −1. The volume element
is again e_0∧…∧e_{n-1}. It returns [ErrDim] if signature has the wrong length
and [ErrGrade] if any entry is not ±1. With an all-+1 signature it agrees
with [HodgeStar].

funcs.name: InteriorProduct
funcs.doc:
InteriorProduct returns the interior product (contraction) ι_v ω of the
vector v into the Form omega, lowering grade by one. Only the grade-1 part of
v is used, and ι_v = Σ_i v_i ι_{e_i}. The interior product is an
antiderivation: ι_v(α∧β) = (ι_v α)∧β + (−1)^{deg α} α∧(ι_v β). It panics if
the ambient dimensions differ.

funcs.name: InverseHodgeStar
funcs.doc:
InverseHodgeStar returns the inverse Euclidean Hodge dual, the unique
operator with InverseHodgeStar(HodgeStar(f)) == f. On a grade-k Form it
equals (−1)^{k(n−k)}★.

funcs.name: LinearCombination
funcs.doc:
LinearCombination returns Σ coeffs[i]·forms[i]. The slices must have equal
length and all Forms must share an ambient dimension; it panics otherwise.

funcs.name: New
funcs.doc: New returns the zero Form of the exterior algebra Λ(Rⁿ). It panics if n < 0.

funcs.name: NumExteriorDerivative
funcs.signature: func NumExteriorDerivative(field FormField, x []float64, h float64) *Form
funcs.doc:
NumExteriorDerivative returns the exterior derivative of the form field at the
point x, evaluated with symmetric finite differences of step h. For a field
Σ_I a_I(x) dx^I it forms Σ_I Σ_j (∂a_I/∂x_j) dx^j∧dx^I by differentiating each
blade coefficient. The ambient dimension is taken from field(x).

funcs.name: NumGradient
funcs.signature: func NumGradient(f ScalarField, x []float64, h float64) *Form
funcs.doc:
NumGradient returns the exterior derivative of the 0-form f at the point x,
namely the grade-1 Form Σ_i (∂f/∂x_i) dx^i, using a symmetric finite
difference of step h on each coordinate. A typical choice is h ≈ 1e-5.

funcs.name: One
funcs.doc: One returns the multiplicative identity of Λ(Rⁿ), the scalar 1.

funcs.name: Scalar
funcs.doc: Scalar returns the grade-0 Form with the given scalar coefficient in Λ(Rⁿ).

funcs.name: VolumeForm
funcs.doc:
VolumeForm returns the canonical top-grade unit volume element
e_0∧…∧e_{n-1} of Λ(Rⁿ). For n == 0 it is the scalar 1.

funcs.name: Wedge
funcs.doc: Wedge returns the exterior product a∧b as a package-level convenience.

funcs.name: WedgeAll
funcs.doc:
WedgeAll returns the exterior product forms[0]∧forms[1]∧… evaluated left to
right. With no arguments it panics; with one it returns a clone.

funcs.name: WedgeVectors
funcs.doc:
WedgeVectors returns the exterior product of the given vectors, each a
grade-1 Form built from a coefficient slice. All vectors must have the same
length, which becomes the ambient dimension; it returns [ErrDim] otherwise.

funcs.name: Zero
funcs.doc: Zero is an alias for [New]: it returns the additive identity of Λ(Rⁿ).

methods.name: Add
methods.doc:
Add returns the sum f+g. It panics if f and g have different ambient
dimensions.

methods.name: BivectorPart
methods.doc: BivectorPart returns the grade-2 part of f.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent deep copy of f.

methods.name: Coeff
methods.doc:
Coeff returns the coefficient of the blade e_{idx[0]}∧…∧e_{idx[k-1]},
accounting for the sign introduced by sorting the indices. It returns 0 for a
blade that is zero (a repeated index) or that names an out-of-range index.

methods.name: CoeffMask
methods.doc:
CoeffMask returns the coefficient of the sorted basis blade encoded by mask.
Bits of mask outside the ambient dimension are ignored.

methods.name: Coeffs
methods.doc: Coeffs returns a fresh copy of the blade→coefficient map underlying f.

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc:
Dim returns the ambient dimension n of the exterior algebra Λ(Rⁿ) that f
belongs to.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc:
Equal reports whether f and g are exactly equal: same ambient dimension and
identical coefficients on every blade.

methods.name: EqualTol
methods.doc:
EqualTol reports whether f and g have the same ambient dimension and every
blade coefficient agrees to within an absolute tolerance tol.

methods.name: EvenPart
methods.doc:
EvenPart returns the sum of the even-grade blades of f (the even subalgebra
component).

methods.name: Grade
methods.doc:
Grade returns the common grade of a homogeneous Form. ok is false when f is
mixed-grade; the zero Form reports grade 0 with ok true.

methods.name: GradeInvolution
methods.doc:
GradeInvolution returns the grade involution α(f), which negates every
odd-grade blade: on a grade-k blade it multiplies by (−1)^k.

methods.name: GradeProject
methods.doc:
GradeProject returns the homogeneous grade-k part of f, a new Form containing
only the blades of exactly k indices. For k outside [0,n] it returns the zero
Form.

methods.name: Grades
methods.doc: Grades returns the sorted list of distinct grades present in f.

methods.name: Interior
methods.doc:
Interior is the method form of [InteriorProduct]: omega.Interior(v) returns
ι_v omega.

methods.name: IsBlade
methods.doc:
IsBlade reports whether f is a single basis blade times a scalar (at most one
nonzero term), and hence trivially decomposable.

methods.name: IsHomogeneous
methods.doc:
IsHomogeneous reports whether every nonzero term of f has the same grade. The
zero Form is considered homogeneous.

methods.name: IsScalar
methods.doc: IsScalar reports whether f has only a grade-0 part (it may be the zero Form).

methods.name: IsVector
methods.doc: IsVector reports whether every nonzero blade of f has grade 1.

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc: IsZero reports whether f is the zero Form (has no nonzero terms).

methods.name: LeftContract
methods.doc:
LeftContract returns the left contraction f ⌋ g under the Euclidean metric,
the bilinear product characterised by ⟨f⌋g, h⟩ = ⟨g, f∧h⟩. On homogeneous
grades r and s it produces grade s−r. It panics if the dimensions differ.

methods.name: Masks
methods.doc:
Masks returns the bitmasks of the nonzero blades of f, ordered by grade and
then by mask.

methods.name: MaxGrade
methods.doc: MaxGrade returns the largest grade present in f, or 0 for the zero Form.

methods.name: MinGrade
methods.doc: MinGrade returns the smallest grade present in f, or 0 for the zero Form.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns the additive inverse -f.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean norm √⟨f, f⟩ of f.

methods.name: NormSq
methods.doc: NormSq returns the squared Euclidean norm ⟨f, f⟩ of f.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns f scaled to unit Euclidean norm, together with the original
norm. If f is zero it returns a zero Form and norm 0.

methods.name: NumTerms
methods.doc: NumTerms returns the number of nonzero basis blades in f.

methods.name: OddPart
methods.doc: OddPart returns the sum of the odd-grade blades of f.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.doc:
Reverse returns the reversion f̃ of f, which reverses the order of the vector
factors in every blade. On a grade-k blade it multiplies by
(−1)^{k(k−1)/2}. Reversion is an anti-automorphism of the exterior algebra.

methods.name: RightContract
methods.doc:
RightContract returns the right contraction f ⌊ g under the Euclidean metric,
characterised by ⟨f⌊g, h⟩ = ⟨f, h∧g⟩. On homogeneous grades r and s it
produces grade r−s. It panics if the dimensions differ.

methods.name: ScalarPart
methods.doc: ScalarPart returns the grade-0 coefficient of f as a plain float64.

methods.name: ScalarValue
methods.doc: ScalarValue is an alias for [Form.ScalarPart]: the grade-0 coefficient of f.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns c·f, the scalar multiple of f by c.

methods.name: Set
methods.doc:
Set sets the coefficient of the blade e_{idx[0]}∧…∧e_{idx[k-1]} to c, taking
the sorting sign into account. It returns [ErrIndex] if an index is out of
range or repeated.

methods.name: SetMask
methods.doc:
SetMask sets the coefficient of the sorted blade encoded by mask to c,
removing the term when c == 0. Bits of mask outside the ambient dimension are
masked off.

methods.name: Star
methods.doc: Star is the method form of [HodgeStar]: f.Star() returns ★f.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String renders f in the usual e_{i…} basis notation, for example
"2 + 3 e0∧e1". The zero Form renders as "0".

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc:
Sub returns the difference f-g. It panics if f and g have different ambient
dimensions.

methods.name: Terms
methods.doc:
Terms returns the nonzero blades of f as a slice of [Term], ordered by grade
and then by mask. The returned slice is freshly allocated.

methods.name: ToVector
methods.doc:
ToVector returns the grade-1 part of f as a coefficient slice of length n,
so that the i-th entry is the coefficient of e_i.

methods.name: TopPart
methods.doc: TopPart returns the top-grade (grade-n) part of f as a new Form.

methods.name: VectorPart
methods.doc: VectorPart returns the grade-1 part of f as a new Form.

methods.name: Wedge
methods.doc:
Wedge returns the exterior product f∧g. The product is bilinear and, on
homogeneous grades p and q, is graded-anticommutative:
f∧g = (−1)^{pq} g∧f. It panics if the ambient dimensions differ.

methods.name: WedgePow
methods.doc:
WedgePow returns the k-fold exterior power f∧…∧f (k factors). WedgePow(0) is
the scalar 1. Because a 1-form wedged with itself vanishes, high powers are
often zero.

types.name: FormField
types.signature: type FormField func(x []float64) *Form
types.doc:
FormField is a differential form on Rⁿ given pointwise: it maps a position x
to the constant [Form] of coefficients at that point. All returned Forms are
expected to share the same ambient dimension and grade structure.

types.name: PForm
types.doc:
PForm is a differential form on Rⁿ whose coefficients are polynomials: a
finite sum Σ_I P_I(x) dx^I where each P_I is a [Poly] in the n coordinates
and dx^I ranges over basis blades. Because polynomial partial derivatives are
exact, PForm supports an exterior derivative that satisfies d² = 0 exactly, a
pullback that commutes with d, and an exact Hodge–de Rham Laplacian.

The zero value is not usable; construct PForms with [NewPForm], [PConst],
[Dx] and [PBasisBlade].

funcs.name: Dx
funcs.doc:
Dx returns the constant basis 1-form dx^i on Rⁿ. It panics if i is out of
range.

funcs.name: NewPForm
funcs.doc: NewPForm returns the zero differential form on Rⁿ. It panics if n < 0.

funcs.name: PBasisBlade
funcs.signature: func PBasisBlade(n int, coeff *Poly, idx ...int) (*PForm, error)
funcs.doc:
PBasisBlade returns the differential form coeff·dx^{idx[0]}∧… on Rⁿ, reducing
the blade to canonical sorted order with the appropriate sign. The polynomial
coeff must have arity n. It returns [ErrIndex] for an out-of-range index and
[ErrDim] on an arity mismatch.

funcs.name: PConst
funcs.doc:
PConst returns the grade-0 differential form whose single coefficient is the
polynomial p. The ambient dimension equals the arity of p.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the sum w+u. It panics if the ambient dimensions differ.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent deep copy of w.

methods.name: Codifferential
methods.doc:
Codifferential returns the codifferential δw with respect to the Euclidean
metric, defined grade-by-grade on a grade-k component by
δ = (−1)^{n(k+1)+1} ★d★. It lowers grade by one and is the formal adjoint of
the exterior derivative.

methods.name: CoeffMask
methods.doc:
CoeffMask returns the polynomial coefficient of the sorted blade encoded by
mask, or the zero polynomial if that blade is absent.

methods.name: D
methods.doc: D is a short alias for [PForm.ExteriorDerivative].

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc: Dim returns the ambient dimension n of the form.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether w and u are the same polynomial form.

methods.name: EqualTol
methods.doc:
EqualTol reports whether w and u have the same ambient dimension and every
coefficient polynomial agrees to within the absolute tolerance tol.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc:
Eval evaluates every coefficient polynomial of w at the point x and returns
the resulting constant [Form]. It panics if len(x) != n.

methods.name: ExteriorDerivative
methods.doc:
ExteriorDerivative returns the exterior derivative dw. For w = Σ_I P_I dx^I it
is Σ_I Σ_j (∂P_I/∂x_j) dx^j∧dx^I, computed from exact polynomial partials.
Consequently d(dw) is exactly the zero form for every w.

methods.name: GradeProject
methods.doc: GradeProject returns the homogeneous grade-k part of w.

methods.name: Grades
methods.doc:
Grades returns the sorted list of distinct grades present in the polynomial
form w.

methods.name: HodgeLaplacian
methods.doc:
HodgeLaplacian returns the Hodge–de Rham Laplacian Δw = (dδ + δd)w with
respect to the Euclidean metric. On a grade-0 form P it equals −Σ_i ∂²P/∂x_i²,
the geometer's (positive-semidefinite) sign convention.

methods.name: HodgeStar
methods.doc:
HodgeStar returns the Euclidean Hodge dual ★w of the polynomial form, applying
the blade-level Hodge map coefficient-wise. It maps grade k to grade n−k.

methods.name: InteriorConst
methods.doc:
InteriorConst returns the interior product ι_v w of the polynomial form w with
the constant vector v of length n, lowering grade by one. It panics if len(v)
differs from the ambient dimension.

methods.name: IsClosed
methods.doc: IsClosed reports whether w is a closed form, that is dw = 0.

methods.name: IsHomogeneous
methods.doc: IsHomogeneous reports whether every nonzero blade of w has the same grade.

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc: IsZero reports whether w has no nonzero coefficient polynomials.

methods.name: Masks
methods.doc:
Masks returns the bitmasks of the nonzero blades of w, ordered by grade and
then by mask.

methods.name: MaxGrade
methods.doc: MaxGrade returns the largest grade present in w, or 0 for the zero form.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns the additive inverse −w.

methods.name: NumTerms
methods.doc: NumTerms returns the number of nonzero blades in w.

methods.name: Pullback
methods.doc:
Pullback returns the pullback φ*w of w along the polynomial map φ whose n
component polynomials are given in phi, each of the same arity m. The result
is a differential form on Rᵐ. Pullback is an algebra homomorphism that
commutes with the exterior derivative: φ*(dw) = d(φ*w). It returns [ErrMap]
unless len(phi) == n and all components share an arity.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns c·w, the real scalar multiple of w.

methods.name: ScalePoly
methods.doc:
ScalePoly returns the product of w with the scalar polynomial p, multiplying
every coefficient by p. It panics if the arity of p differs from n.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String renders w with each blade shown as its coefficient polynomial times
the differential dx^I, for example "(2 x0) dx0 + (1) dx0∧dx1".

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the difference w−u. It panics if the ambient dimensions differ.

methods.name: Wedge
methods.doc:
Wedge returns the exterior product w∧u of two polynomial forms. It panics if
the ambient dimensions differ.

types.name: Poly
types.doc:
Poly is a multivariate polynomial in n real variables x_0,…,x_{n-1}. It is
stored sparsely as a map from exponent vectors to nonzero coefficients, so it
can represent polynomials of any degree without a dense coefficient array.
Poly is the coefficient ring used by [PForm] for differential forms with
polynomial coefficients.

The zero value is not usable; build polynomials with [NewPoly], [ConstPoly],
[Var] and [Monomial].

funcs.name: ConstPoly
funcs.doc: ConstPoly returns the constant polynomial c in n variables.

funcs.name: Monomial
funcs.doc:
Monomial returns the polynomial coeff·∏ x_i^{exp[i]} in n = len(exp)
variables.

funcs.name: NewPoly
funcs.doc: NewPoly returns the zero polynomial in n variables. It panics if n < 0.

funcs.name: PolyCurl
funcs.doc:
PolyCurl returns the curl of a 3-dimensional vector field field = (P,Q,R),
namely (∂R/∂x₁−∂Q/∂x₂, ∂P/∂x₂−∂R/∂x₀, ∂Q/∂x₀−∂P/∂x₁). It returns [ErrDim]
unless there are exactly three components each of arity 3.

funcs.name: PolyDivergence
funcs.doc:
PolyDivergence returns the divergence Σ_i ∂F_i/∂x_i of the vector field whose
components are field. It returns [ErrDim] unless there are exactly n
components, each of arity n.

funcs.name: PolyGradient
funcs.doc:
PolyGradient returns the gradient of the scalar field f as its n component
polynomials, with entry i equal to ∂f/∂x_i. It realises the first map of the
de Rham complex, d on 0-forms.

funcs.name: PolyLaplacian
funcs.doc: PolyLaplacian is a package-level alias for [Poly.Laplacian].

funcs.name: Var
funcs.doc:
Var returns the polynomial equal to the single variable x_i in n variables.
It panics if i is out of range.

funcs.name: Vars
funcs.doc: Vars returns the n coordinate polynomials x_0,…,x_{n-1} of arity n.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the sum p+q. It panics if the arities differ.

methods.name: Arity
methods.doc: Arity returns the number of variables n of p.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent deep copy of p.

methods.name: Coefficient
methods.doc:
Coefficient returns the coefficient of the monomial ∏ x_i^{exp[i]} in p. It
returns 0 for a length mismatch or an absent monomial.

methods.name: Compose
methods.doc:
Compose substitutes each variable x_i with the polynomial subs[i], returning
the composed polynomial in the common arity of the substitutes. It requires
len(subs) == n and that every substitute has the same arity; it returns
[ErrMap] otherwise.

methods.name: ConstantTerm
methods.doc: ConstantTerm returns the coefficient of the constant monomial of p.

methods.name: Degree
methods.doc:
Degree returns the total degree of p, the largest sum of exponents over its
monomials. The zero polynomial has degree −1 by convention.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc:
Equal reports whether p and q are the same polynomial (same arity and
coefficients).

methods.name: EqualTol
methods.doc:
EqualTol reports whether p and q have the same arity and all coefficients
agree to within an absolute tolerance tol.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc:
Eval evaluates p at the point x, which must have length n. It panics on a
length mismatch.

methods.name: Gradient
methods.doc:
Gradient returns the n partial derivatives of p as a slice, with entry i
equal to ∂p/∂x_i.

methods.name: Hessian
methods.doc:
Hessian returns the n×n matrix of second partial derivatives of p, with entry
[i][j] equal to ∂²p/∂x_i∂x_j.

methods.name: IsConstant
methods.doc: IsConstant reports whether p has no variable dependence (degree ≤ 0).

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc: IsZero reports whether p is the zero polynomial.

methods.name: Laplacian
methods.doc: Laplacian returns the scalar Laplacian Σ_i ∂²p/∂x_i² of the polynomial p.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the product p·q. It panics if the arities differ.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns −p.

methods.name: NumTerms
methods.doc: NumTerms returns the number of nonzero monomials in p.

methods.name: Partial
methods.doc:
Partial returns the partial derivative ∂p/∂x_i. It panics if i is out of
range.

methods.name: Pow
methods.doc:
Pow returns p raised to the non-negative integer power k. Pow(0) is the
constant 1. It panics if k < 0.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns c·p.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders p in conventional notation such as "2 x0^2 + 3 x1 - 1".

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the difference p−q. It panics if the arities differ.

types.name: ScalarField
types.signature: type ScalarField func(x []float64) float64
types.doc:
ScalarField is a smooth real-valued function on Rⁿ, used as the coefficient
of a numerically differentiated differential form.

types.name: Term
types.doc:
Term is one basis blade of a [Form] together with its coefficient. Indices is
the sorted index list of the blade and Mask its bitmask encoding.

funcs.name: AlgebraDimension
funcs.doc:
AlgebraDimension returns the dimension 2ⁿ of the full exterior algebra
Λ(Rⁿ) as a real vector space.

funcs.name: Angle
funcs.doc:
Angle returns the angle in radians between two nonzero homogeneous Forms of
the same grade, defined via the Euclidean inner product. It returns NaN if
either operand is zero.

funcs.name: Binomial
funcs.doc:
Binomial returns the binomial coefficient C(n,k), the number of k-element
subsets of an n-element set. It returns 0 when k is negative or exceeds n.

funcs.name: CrossProduct
funcs.doc:
CrossProduct returns the 3-dimensional cross product a×b, computed as the
Hodge dual of the wedge a∧b in Λ(R³). It returns [ErrDim] unless both inputs
have length 3.

funcs.name: Determinant
funcs.doc:
Determinant returns the determinant of the n×n matrix whose rows are the n
supplied vectors of Rⁿ, computed as the top-grade coefficient of their wedge
product. It returns [ErrDim] unless there are exactly n vectors each of
length n.

funcs.name: Dot
funcs.doc: Dot is an alias for [InnerProduct].

funcs.name: EuclideanSignature
funcs.doc: EuclideanSignature returns the all-+1 metric signature of length n.

funcs.name: FullMask
funcs.doc:
FullMask returns the bitmask 0b1…1 with the low n bits set, encoding the
top-grade basis blade e_0∧…∧e_{n-1} of Λ(Rⁿ).

funcs.name: GradeDimension
funcs.doc:
GradeDimension returns the dimension of the grade-k subspace Λ^k(Rⁿ), which
equals C(n,k).

funcs.name: HodgeSignSquared
funcs.doc:
HodgeSignSquared returns the scalar ε with ★★ = ε·id on grade-k Forms of
Λ(Rⁿ) under the Euclidean metric, namely (−1)^{k(n−k)}.

funcs.name: IndicesToMask
funcs.signature: func IndicesToMask(n int, idx ...int) (mask uint, sign int, ok bool)
funcs.doc:
IndicesToMask converts an ordered list of basis indices into the bitmask of
the corresponding blade together with the sign of the permutation that sorts
the indices into increasing order. A repeated index yields a zero sign
(the blade is zero) and ok == false; a negative index or one that is not less
than n also yields ok == false.

When ok is true the returned mask encodes the sorted blade and sign is ±1,
so that e_{idx[0]}∧…∧e_{idx[k-1]} = sign · e_mask.

funcs.name: InnerProduct
funcs.doc:
InnerProduct returns the Euclidean inner product ⟨f, g⟩ obtained by declaring
the basis blades orthonormal: it is the sum over blades of the products of
matching coefficients. It panics if the ambient dimensions differ.

funcs.name: LorentzSignature
funcs.doc:
LorentzSignature returns the Lorentzian signature (−1,+1,…,+1) of length n,
with the time index 0 carrying the −1. For n == 0 it returns an empty slice.

funcs.name: MaskToIndices
funcs.doc:
MaskToIndices returns the sorted slice of basis indices whose bits are set in
mask. The result is always in strictly increasing order.

funcs.name: NumDirectionalDerivative
funcs.signature: func NumDirectionalDerivative(f ScalarField, x, v []float64, h float64) float64
funcs.doc:
NumDirectionalDerivative returns the directional derivative of the scalar
field f at x along the direction vector v, computed as a symmetric finite
difference of step h. It equals ⟨∇f, v⟩ in the limit h→0.

funcs.name: Popcount
funcs.doc:
Popcount returns the number of set bits in mask, i.e. the grade of the basis
blade that mask encodes.

funcs.name: WedgeSign
funcs.doc:
WedgeSign returns the graded-commutation sign (−1)^{pq} relating α∧β to β∧α
for a grade-p and a grade-q homogeneous Form.

examples.name: HodgeStar
examples.code: fmt.Println(HodgeStar(Basis1(3, 0)))

examples.name: Form (Wedge)
examples.code:
u := FromVector([]float64{1, 0, 0})
v := FromVector([]float64{0, 1, 0})
fmt.Println(u.Wedge(v))

examples.name: PForm (ExteriorDerivative)
examples.code:
n := 2
f := Var(n, 0).Mul(Var(n, 1))
fmt.Println(PConst(f).ExteriorDerivative())

packages.name: fin
packages.synopsis: Package fin is a small, dependency-free financial mathematics toolkit written entirely with the Go standard library (only the math package).
packages.doc:
Package fin is a small, dependency-free financial mathematics toolkit
written entirely with the Go standard library (only the math package). It is
completely standalone: it does not import the parent algebra package, and it
