performs no I/O, no concurrency and no random sampling, so every result is
deterministic.

The package is organised into five groups.

# Time value of money

The Excel-compatible core solvers [FV], [PV], [PMT], [NPER] and [RATE] all
operate on the standard cash-flow equation

	pv·(1+r)ⁿ + pmt·(1+r·t)·((1+r)ⁿ−1)/r + fv = 0

where t selects [EndOfPeriod] (an ordinary annuity) or [BeginningOfPeriod]
(an annuity due). Convenience helpers cover single sums ([FVLumpSum],
[PVLumpSum]), level annuities ([FVAnnuity], [PVAnnuity], [FVAnnuityDue],
[PVAnnuityDue]), perpetuities ([PVPerpetuity], [PVGrowingPerpetuity]),
growing annuities ([PVGrowingAnnuity]), and continuous compounding
([FVContinuous], [PVContinuous]). Rate conversions are provided by
[EffectiveRate], [NominalRate], [EffectiveFromContinuous] and
[ContinuousFromEffective].

# Cash-flow analysis

[NPV], [IRR], [IRRBisection], [MIRR], [XNPV] and [XIRR] evaluate and solve
discounted-cash-flow problems. [CAGR] reports a compound annual growth rate.

# Fixed income

[BondPrice], [BondYTM], [ZeroCouponPrice], [CurrentYield], [AccruedInterest],
[MacaulayDuration], [ModifiedDuration] and [Convexity] price coupon bonds and
measure their interest-rate sensitivity.

# Options

[BlackScholesPrice] (with [BlackScholesCall], [BlackScholesPut]) prices
European options under the Black–Scholes–Merton model with a continuous
dividend yield. The Greeks [Delta], [Gamma], [Vega], [Theta] and [Rho] are
available individually or bundled by [ComputeGreeks]; [ImpliedVolatility]
inverts the price.

# Loans, depreciation and performance

[AmortizationSchedule], [RemainingBalance] and [TotalInterest] analyse
fully-amortising loans. [StraightLineDepreciation], [DecliningBalance],
[DoubleDecliningBalance] and [SumOfYearsDigits] (with their *Schedule
variants) model asset depreciation. [SharpeRatio], [SortinoRatio],
[Volatility], [AnnualizedReturn] and [HoldingPeriodReturn] summarise an
investment return series.

# Conventions

Following spreadsheet convention, cash paid out is negative and cash received
is positive, so [PV] and [PMT] of a positive future value are typically
negative. Functions that have no meaningful result for their inputs (an empty
series, a non-converging root search, a non-positive argument to a logarithm)
return NaN rather than panicking, so callers can test with [math.IsNaN].

types.name: AmortizationRow
types.doc:
AmortizationRow describes one period of a fully-amortising loan repayment
schedule.

funcs.name: AmortizationSchedule
funcs.signature: func AmortizationSchedule(principal, rate float64, nper int) []AmortizationRow
funcs.doc:
AmortizationSchedule returns the period-by-period repayment schedule of a
fully-amortising loan of the given principal at per-period rate over nper
periods. Each row splits the level payment into its interest and principal
components and reports the remaining balance. Payment and its components are
reported as positive amounts. The final balance is driven to zero exactly.

types.name: Greeks
types.doc:
Greeks bundles the first-order option sensitivities (and second-order Gamma)
returned by [ComputeGreeks]. All values are per unit change in the underlying
parameter: Vega per 1.00 of volatility, Theta per year, Rho per 1.00 of the
interest rate.

funcs.name: ComputeGreeks
funcs.signature: func ComputeGreeks(otype OptionType, s, k, t, r, q, sigma float64) Greeks
funcs.doc:
ComputeGreeks returns all option sensitivities in a single [Greeks] value for
an option of the given OptionType, avoiding repeated evaluation of the shared
d1/d2 terms. Field conventions match the individual Greek functions.

types.name: OptionType
types.signature: type OptionType int
types.doc:
OptionType distinguishes a call option from a put option in the
Black–Scholes–Merton pricing functions.

types.name: PaymentTiming
types.signature: type PaymentTiming int
types.doc:
PaymentTiming selects whether the periodic payments of an annuity occur at
the end of each period (an ordinary annuity) or at the beginning (an annuity
due). It is the analogue of the "type" argument in spreadsheet TVM functions.

funcs.name: AccruedInterest
funcs.signature: func AccruedInterest(face, couponRate float64, freq int, daysSinceCoupon, daysInPeriod float64) float64
funcs.doc:
AccruedInterest returns the coupon interest accrued between coupon dates on a
bond with the given face value and annual couponRate, where daysSinceCoupon
days have elapsed of a coupon period of daysInPeriod days. It uses simple
straight-line (actual/actual within period) accrual.

funcs.name: Annualize
funcs.signature: func Annualize(periodVol float64, periodsPerYear float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Annualize scales a per-period volatility to an annual figure given the number
of periods per year, multiplying by √periodsPerYear (the square-root-of-time
rule). It returns NaN for periodsPerYear ≤ 0.

funcs.name: AnnualizedReturn
funcs.doc:
AnnualizedReturn returns the geometric mean per-period return of a series
compounded to a single period figure: (∏(1+rᵢ))^(1/n) − 1. This is the
constant per-period rate that reproduces the series' [HoldingPeriodReturn].
It returns NaN for an empty series.

funcs.name: AnnuityFactor
funcs.doc:
AnnuityFactor returns the present-value annuity factor
(1−(1+rate)^−nper)/rate, the present value of a unit ordinary annuity. It is
nper when rate == 0.

funcs.name: BlackScholesCall
funcs.signature: func BlackScholesCall(s, k, t, r, q, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BlackScholesCall returns the price of a European call option under the
Black–Scholes–Merton model with continuous dividend yield q. The parameters
are spot s, strike k, time to expiry t in years, risk-free rate r and
volatility sigma.

funcs.name: BlackScholesPrice
funcs.signature: func BlackScholesPrice(otype OptionType, s, k, t, r, q, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BlackScholesPrice returns the Black–Scholes–Merton price of a European option
of the given OptionType, for spot price s, strike k, time to expiry t (in
years), risk-free rate r, dividend yield q and volatility sigma (all rates
annualised, continuously compounded). See [BlackScholesCall] and
[BlackScholesPut] for type-specific wrappers.

funcs.name: BlackScholesPut
funcs.signature: func BlackScholesPut(s, k, t, r, q, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BlackScholesPut returns the price of a European put option under the
Black–Scholes–Merton model with continuous dividend yield q, for spot s,
strike k, time to expiry t in years, risk-free rate r and volatility sigma.

funcs.name: BondPrice
funcs.signature: func BondPrice(face, couponRate, yield, years float64, freq int) float64
funcs.doc:
BondPrice returns the fair price of a fixed-coupon bond given its face value,
annual couponRate (as a decimal, e.g. 0.05 for 5%), annual yield to maturity
yield, time to maturity in years, and the number of coupon payments per year
freq (e.g. 2 for semi-annual). The price is the present value of all future
coupons plus the redemption of face at maturity, discounted at yield/freq per
period.

funcs.name: BondYTM
funcs.signature: func BondYTM(face, couponRate, price, years float64, freq int) float64
funcs.doc:
BondYTM returns the annual yield to maturity that makes the [BondPrice] of a
bond with the given face value, annual couponRate, years to maturity and
coupon frequency freq equal to its observed market price. It solves by
bisection over (0, 1] (0% to 100%) and returns NaN if no yield in that range
reproduces the price.

funcs.name: CAGR
funcs.signature: func CAGR(begin, end, years float64) float64
funcs.doc:
CAGR returns the compound annual growth rate that takes an initial value
begin to a final value end over the given number of years:
(end/begin)^(1/years) − 1. It returns NaN when begin ≤ 0 or years ≤ 0.

funcs.name: ContinuousFromEffective
funcs.doc:
ContinuousFromEffective converts an effective annual rate into the equivalent
continuously compounded annual rate: ln(1+effective). It returns NaN when
effective ≤ −1.

funcs.name: Convexity
funcs.signature: func Convexity(face, couponRate, yield, years float64, freq int) float64
funcs.doc:
Convexity returns the convexity (in years squared) of a fixed-coupon bond,
the second-order sensitivity of price to yield. Together with
[ModifiedDuration] it gives the price approximation
ΔP/P ≈ −ModDur·Δy + ½·Convexity·Δy², improving the linear duration estimate
for larger yield moves.

funcs.name: CurrentYield
funcs.signature: func CurrentYield(face, couponRate, price float64) float64
funcs.doc:
CurrentYield returns a bond's current yield: its annual coupon income
divided by its market price, face·couponRate/price. It returns NaN for a
non-positive price.

funcs.name: DecliningBalance
funcs.signature: func DecliningBalance(cost, salvage, rate float64, period, life int) float64
funcs.doc:
DecliningBalance returns the depreciation expense in a single 1-based period
under the declining-balance method at the given per-period rate (as a
decimal). Depreciation is charged on the reducing book value but is capped so
the book value never falls below salvage.

funcs.name: Delta
funcs.signature: func Delta(otype OptionType, s, k, t, r, q, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Delta returns the option delta ∂price/∂spot for an option of the given
OptionType under Black–Scholes–Merton, with the usual parameters. A call
delta lies in [0, e^(−qt)] and a put delta in [−e^(−qt), 0].

funcs.name: DiscountFactor
funcs.doc:
DiscountFactor returns the single-period-to-nper discount factor
1/(1+rate)^nper, the present value of one unit of currency received nper
periods from now.

funcs.name: DoubleDecliningBalance
funcs.signature: func DoubleDecliningBalance(cost, salvage float64, period, life int) float64
funcs.doc:
DoubleDecliningBalance returns the depreciation expense in a single 1-based
period under the double-declining-balance method, which uses a rate of
2/life on the reducing book value, capped so book value never falls below
salvage. It returns NaN for life ≤ 0.

funcs.name: DoubleDecliningSchedule
funcs.signature: func DoubleDecliningSchedule(cost, salvage float64, life int) []float64
funcs.doc:
DoubleDecliningSchedule returns the depreciation expense for each of the life
periods under the double-declining-balance method. The reported expenses sum
to at most cost − salvage.

funcs.name: EffectiveFromContinuous
funcs.doc:
EffectiveFromContinuous converts a continuously compounded annual rate into
the equivalent effective annual rate: e^rate − 1.

funcs.name: EffectiveRate
funcs.signature: func EffectiveRate(nominal float64, m float64) float64
funcs.doc:
EffectiveRate converts a nominal annual rate compounded m times per year into
the equivalent effective annual rate (EAR): (1+nominal/m)^m − 1. It returns
NaN for m ≤ 0.

funcs.name: FV
funcs.signature: func FV(rate float64, nper, pmt, pv float64, when PaymentTiming) float64
funcs.doc:
FV returns the future value of an investment described by the standard
cash-flow equation, given a constant per-period interest rate, a number of
periods nper, a fixed per-period payment pmt, a present value pv, and the
payment timing when. Following spreadsheet convention, money paid out is
negative and money received is positive; for a loan or savings plan with
positive deposits pmt and pv, the returned future value is negative.

funcs.name: FVAnnuity
funcs.signature: func FVAnnuity(pmt, rate, nper float64) float64
funcs.doc:
FVAnnuity returns the future value of an ordinary annuity of nper level
payments pmt at per-period rate: pmt·((1+rate)^nper−1)/rate. Payments are
treated as positive inflows, so the result is positive.

funcs.name: FVAnnuityDue
funcs.signature: func FVAnnuityDue(pmt, rate, nper float64) float64
funcs.doc:
FVAnnuityDue returns the future value of an annuity due (payments at the
beginning of each period) of nper level payments pmt at per-period rate. It
equals [FVAnnuity] scaled by (1+rate).

funcs.name: FVContinuous
funcs.signature: func FVContinuous(pv, rate, t float64) float64
funcs.doc:
FVContinuous returns the future value of pv under continuous compounding at
annual rate for time t years: pv·e^(rate·t).

funcs.name: FVLumpSum
funcs.signature: func FVLumpSum(pv, rate, nper float64) float64
funcs.doc:
FVLumpSum returns the future value of a single present amount pv compounded
for nper periods at per-period rate: pv·(1+rate)^nper.

funcs.name: Gamma
funcs.signature: func Gamma(s, k, t, r, q, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Gamma returns the option gamma ∂²price/∂spot², the rate of change of [Delta].
Gamma is identical for calls and puts with the same parameters.

funcs.name: HoldingPeriodReturn
funcs.doc:
HoldingPeriodReturn returns the total return of chaining a series of periodic
returns (each expressed as a decimal, e.g. 0.05 for 5%): the product of
(1+r) minus one. For an empty series it returns 0.

funcs.name: IRR
funcs.doc:
IRR returns the internal rate of return of a series of equally-spaced cash
flows: the per-period rate at which [NPV] equals zero. The first cash flow is
at time 0. It uses Newton's method from guess and falls back to bisection
over (−1, ∞); it returns NaN if no rate is found. A conventional series has
exactly one sign change and a unique IRR. Pass guess = 0.1 for a typical
starting point.

funcs.name: IRRBisection
funcs.doc:
IRRBisection returns the internal rate of return found purely by bisection
over the bracket (−0.9999999, 1e6). It is slower but more robust than the
Newton path in [IRR] and needs no initial guess. It returns NaN when the net
present value does not change sign across the bracket.

funcs.name: ImpliedVolatility
funcs.signature: func ImpliedVolatility(otype OptionType, price, s, k, t, r, q float64) float64
funcs.doc:
ImpliedVolatility returns the volatility that makes the Black–Scholes–Merton
price of an option of the given OptionType equal the observed market price,
given spot s, strike k, time to expiry t, rate r and dividend yield q. It
uses Newton's method seeded at 0.2 with a bisection fallback over (0, 5], and
returns NaN when the price is not attainable (for example below intrinsic
value).

funcs.name: LogReturn
funcs.doc:
LogReturn returns the continuously compounded (log) return from an opening
value begin to a closing value end: ln(end/begin). It returns NaN when either
value is non-positive.

funcs.name: MIRR
funcs.signature: func MIRR(cashflows []float64, financeRate, reinvestRate float64) float64
funcs.doc:
MIRR returns the modified internal rate of return of a series of
equally-spaced cash flows, where negative flows are financed at financeRate
and positive flows are reinvested at reinvestRate. Unlike [IRR] it always has
a unique real value for a conventional project. It returns NaN when there are
fewer than two periods or when there are no negative flows to finance.

funcs.name: MacaulayDuration
funcs.signature: func MacaulayDuration(face, couponRate, yield, years float64, freq int) float64
funcs.doc:
MacaulayDuration returns the Macaulay duration (in years) of a fixed-coupon
bond: the present-value-weighted average time to each cash flow, discounted
at yield. Duration measures a bond's effective maturity and underlies its
interest-rate sensitivity.

funcs.name: ModifiedDuration
funcs.signature: func ModifiedDuration(face, couponRate, yield, years float64, freq int) float64
funcs.doc:
ModifiedDuration returns the modified duration (in years) of a fixed-coupon
bond, the Macaulay duration divided by (1+yield/freq). It approximates the
percentage change in price for a one-unit (100%) change in yield; multiply by
a small yield change to estimate the price move.

funcs.name: NPER
funcs.signature: func NPER(rate, pmt, pv, fv float64, when PaymentTiming) float64
funcs.doc:
NPER returns the number of periods required to move from present value pv to
future value fv with a constant per-period payment pmt at per-period rate,
and payment timing when. It returns NaN when no positive solution exists
(for example when the signs make the logarithm undefined).

funcs.name: NPV
funcs.doc:
NPV returns the net present value of a series of equally-spaced cash flows
discounted at a constant per-period rate. The first element cashflows[0] is
treated as occurring at time 0 (it is not discounted), cashflows[1] at time
1, and so on. This matches the textbook definition; note it differs from the
spreadsheet NPV function, which discounts the first element by one period.

funcs.name: NominalRate
funcs.signature: func NominalRate(effective float64, m float64) float64
funcs.doc:
NominalRate converts an effective annual rate into the equivalent nominal
annual rate compounded m times per year: m·((1+effective)^(1/m) − 1). It
returns NaN for m ≤ 0.

funcs.name: PMT
funcs.signature: func PMT(rate float64, nper, pv, fv float64, when PaymentTiming) float64
funcs.doc:
PMT returns the constant per-period payment that amortises a present value
pv to a future value fv over nper periods at the given per-period rate, with
payment timing when. For a standard loan (positive pv, zero fv) the payment
is negative, representing an outflow each period.

funcs.name: PV
funcs.signature: func PV(rate float64, nper, pmt, fv float64, when PaymentTiming) float64
funcs.doc:
PV returns the present value of a stream given a constant per-period rate,
nper periods, a per-period payment pmt, a future value fv, and payment
timing when. It inverts the same cash-flow equation as [FV]. With a positive
future value the present value is typically negative (an outflow today).

funcs.name: PVAnnuity
funcs.signature: func PVAnnuity(pmt, rate, nper float64) float64
funcs.doc:
PVAnnuity returns the present value of an ordinary annuity of nper level
payments pmt at per-period rate: pmt·(1−(1+rate)^−nper)/rate.

funcs.name: PVAnnuityDue
funcs.signature: func PVAnnuityDue(pmt, rate, nper float64) float64
funcs.doc:
PVAnnuityDue returns the present value of an annuity due (payments at the
beginning of each period) of nper level payments pmt at per-period rate. It
equals [PVAnnuity] scaled by (1+rate).

funcs.name: PVContinuous
funcs.signature: func PVContinuous(fv, rate, t float64) float64
funcs.doc:
PVContinuous returns the present value of fv under continuous compounding at
annual rate for time t years: fv·e^(−rate·t).

funcs.name: PVGrowingAnnuity
funcs.signature: func PVGrowingAnnuity(pmt, rate, growth, nper float64) float64
funcs.doc:
PVGrowingAnnuity returns the present value of a growing annuity: nper
payments whose first payment is pmt and which grow at rate growth each
period, discounted at rate. It handles the rate == growth case exactly.

funcs.name: PVGrowingPerpetuity
funcs.signature: func PVGrowingPerpetuity(pmt, rate, growth float64) float64
funcs.doc:
PVGrowingPerpetuity returns the present value of a perpetuity whose first
payment pmt grows at rate growth each period, discounted at rate: the
Gordon growth value pmt/(rate−growth). It returns NaN when rate ≤ growth.

funcs.name: PVLumpSum
funcs.signature: func PVLumpSum(fv, rate, nper float64) float64
funcs.doc:
PVLumpSum returns the present value of a single future amount fv discounted
over nper periods at per-period rate: fv/(1+rate)^nper.

funcs.name: PVPerpetuity
funcs.doc:
PVPerpetuity returns the present value of a perpetuity paying pmt every
period forever at per-period rate: pmt/rate. It returns NaN for rate ≤ 0.

funcs.name: RATE
funcs.signature: func RATE(nper, pmt, pv, fv float64, when PaymentTiming, guess float64) float64
funcs.doc:
RATE returns the constant per-period interest rate that satisfies the
cash-flow equation for nper periods, per-period payment pmt, present value
pv, future value fv and payment timing when, starting from guess. It uses
Newton's method with a numerical derivative and falls back to bisection over
a wide bracket; it returns NaN if no rate can be found. Pass guess = 0.1 for
a typical starting point.

funcs.name: RemainingBalance
funcs.signature: func RemainingBalance(principal, rate float64, nper, periodsPaid int) float64
funcs.doc:
RemainingBalance returns the outstanding principal on a fully-amortising loan
of the given original principal at per-period rate over nper periods, after
periodsPaid level payments have been made. It equals the present value of the
remaining payments.

funcs.name: Rho
funcs.signature: func Rho(otype OptionType, s, k, t, r, q, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Rho returns the option rho ∂price/∂rate for an option of the given
OptionType, the sensitivity to a 1.00 (100%) change in the risk-free rate.
Divide by 100 for the change per 1% (percentage point).

funcs.name: SharpeRatio
funcs.doc:
SharpeRatio returns the Sharpe ratio of a series of periodic returns given a
per-period riskFree rate: the mean excess return divided by the standard
deviation of returns. It measures risk-adjusted performance and returns NaN
for fewer than two observations or zero volatility.

funcs.name: SimpleReturn
funcs.doc:
SimpleReturn returns the simple (arithmetic) return from an opening value
begin to a closing value end: (end − begin)/begin. It returns NaN when begin
is zero.

funcs.name: SortinoRatio
funcs.doc:
SortinoRatio returns the Sortino ratio of a series of periodic returns given
a per-period riskFree rate: the mean excess return divided by the downside
deviation (the root-mean-square of shortfalls below riskFree). Unlike
[SharpeRatio] it penalises only downside volatility. It returns NaN when
there is no downside deviation or fewer than one observation.

funcs.name: StraightLineDepreciation
funcs.signature: func StraightLineDepreciation(cost, salvage, life float64) float64
funcs.doc:
StraightLineDepreciation returns the constant per-period depreciation expense
of an asset under the straight-line method: (cost − salvage)/life. It returns
NaN for life ≤ 0.

funcs.name: StraightLineSchedule
funcs.signature: func StraightLineSchedule(cost, salvage float64, life int) []float64
funcs.doc:
StraightLineSchedule returns the per-period depreciation expense for each of
the life periods under the straight-line method. Every element equals
[StraightLineDepreciation]; the slice has one entry per period.

funcs.name: SumOfYearsDigits
funcs.signature: func SumOfYearsDigits(cost, salvage float64, period, life int) float64
funcs.doc:
SumOfYearsDigits returns the depreciation expense in a single 1-based period
under the sum-of-years'-digits method. The remaining life (life−period+1) is
weighted by the total of the digits 1..life, front-loading depreciation. It
returns NaN for life ≤ 0.

funcs.name: SumOfYearsDigitsSchedule
funcs.signature: func SumOfYearsDigitsSchedule(cost, salvage float64, life int) []float64
funcs.doc:
SumOfYearsDigitsSchedule returns the depreciation expense for each of the
life periods under the sum-of-years'-digits method. The expenses sum exactly
to cost − salvage.

funcs.name: Theta
funcs.signature: func Theta(otype OptionType, s, k, t, r, q, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Theta returns the option theta ∂price/∂time for an option of the given
OptionType, expressed per year (usually negative, reflecting time decay).
Divide by 365 for the approximate one-day theta.

funcs.name: TotalInterest
funcs.signature: func TotalInterest(principal, rate float64, nper int) float64
funcs.doc:
TotalInterest returns the total interest paid over the life of a
fully-amortising loan of the given principal at per-period rate over nper
periods: the sum of all level payments minus the principal.

funcs.name: Vega
funcs.signature: func Vega(s, k, t, r, q, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Vega returns the option vega ∂price/∂volatility, the sensitivity to a 1.00
(100%) change in volatility. Divide by 100 for the change per 1% (percentage
point). Vega is identical for calls and puts with the same parameters.

funcs.name: Volatility
funcs.doc:
Volatility returns the sample standard deviation of a series of periodic
returns, the standard measure of investment risk. It returns NaN for fewer
than two observations. Use [Annualize] to scale it to another horizon.

funcs.name: XIRR
funcs.signature: func XIRR(cashflows, times []float64, guess float64) float64
funcs.doc:
XIRR returns the annual internal rate of return for cash flows occurring at
arbitrary times (in years), the rate at which [XNPV] is zero. It uses
Newton's method from guess with a bisection fallback and returns NaN if no
rate is found or the slice lengths differ.

funcs.name: XNPV
funcs.signature: func XNPV(rate float64, cashflows, times []float64) float64
funcs.doc:
XNPV returns the net present value of cash flows that occur at arbitrary
times, discounted at an annual rate. The times slice gives the time in years
of each corresponding cash flow (times[0] is usually 0). The two slices must
have equal length; otherwise it returns NaN.

funcs.name: ZeroCouponPrice
funcs.signature: func ZeroCouponPrice(face, yield, years float64, freq int) float64
funcs.doc:
ZeroCouponPrice returns the price of a zero-coupon bond: the present value of
its face value discounted at annual yield compounded freq times per year over
the given years to maturity. With freq = 1 this is face/(1+yield)^years.

packages.name: fractal
packages.synopsis: Package fractal provides pure–Go, standard-library-only tools for computing and exploring classic fractals.
packages.doc:
Package fractal provides pure–Go, standard-library-only tools for computing
and exploring classic fractals.

The package is organized around several independent topics:

  - Escape-time fractals: the Mandelbrot set and Julia sets, including
    per-point escape iteration counts, smooth (normalized) iteration counts
    for continuous coloring, closed-form interior tests (main cardioid and
    period-2 bulb), and evaluation over a rectangular grid via a [Viewport].
  - Fractal dimension: box-counting dimension of a finite point set, a
    generic log-log least-squares slope helper, and the exact self-similar
    (Hausdorff) dimension of strictly self-similar sets.
  - L-systems: deterministic context-free string rewriting ([LSystem]) plus a
    turtle-graphics interpreter that turns a command string into line
    [Segment]s or a vertex path, with presets for the Koch curve, Sierpinski
    triangle, dragon curve, and a branching plant.
  - Iterated function systems: affine contraction maps ([AffineMap]), the
    random "chaos game" ([IFS.ChaosGame]), and presets for the Barnsley fern
    and Sierpinski triangle/carpet.
  - Deterministic geometric fractals: the Koch curve and snowflake,
    the Sierpinski triangle by recursive subdivision, and the Cantor set.

All routines use only the Go standard library and are deterministic: any
randomness (the chaos game) is driven by an explicit caller-supplied seed.

Complex numbers are represented with the builtin complex128 type; planar
points use [Point2D].

types.name: AffineMap
types.doc:
AffineMap is a 2-D affine transformation of the form

	x' = A*x + B*y + E
	y' = C*x + D*y + F

Such contraction maps are the building blocks of iterated function systems.

funcs.name: NewAffineMap
funcs.signature: func NewAffineMap(a, b, c, d, e, f float64) AffineMap
funcs.doc:
NewAffineMap constructs an AffineMap from its six coefficients in the order
a, b, c, d, e, f matching the field layout x' = a*x+b*y+e, y' = c*x+d*y+f.

methods.name: Apply
methods.doc: Apply returns the image of p under the affine map.

types.name: EscapeResult
types.doc:
EscapeResult records the outcome of iterating a quadratic map z -> z^2 + c
until the orbit escapes a bailout radius or a maximum iteration count is
reached. Escaped reports whether the bailout was exceeded; Iterations is the
number of completed iterations at that moment (equal to the maximum when the
orbit did not escape); FinalZ is the last computed orbit value.

funcs.name: JuliaEscape
funcs.signature: func JuliaEscape(z, c complex128, maxIter int, bailout float64) EscapeResult
funcs.doc:
JuliaEscape computes the escape-time result for the filled Julia set of the
map z -> z^2 + c starting from the seed point z. The orbit escapes once |z|
exceeds bailout (at least 2). Iteration stops after maxIter steps.

funcs.name: MandelbrotEscape
funcs.signature: func MandelbrotEscape(c complex128, maxIter int, bailout float64) EscapeResult
funcs.doc:
MandelbrotEscape computes the escape-time result for the Mandelbrot map
z -> z^2 + c starting from z = 0. The point c is considered to have escaped
once |z| exceeds bailout (which should be at least 2, the Mandelbrot escape
radius). Iteration stops after maxIter steps.

methods.name: Smooth
methods.doc:
Smooth returns the fractional (normalized) iteration count for an escaped
result, giving continuous values suitable for smooth coloring. It uses the
standard renormalization mu = n + 1 - log2(log|z| / log(bailout)). For a
result that did not escape it returns the integer iteration count unchanged.
The estimate is most accurate when bailout is large (for example 256).

types.name: Grid
types.doc:
Grid is a dense Width×Height array of scalar values stored in row-major order
(index = y*Width + x). It is the natural output of rasterizing an
escape-time fractal over a rectangular region.

funcs.name: JuliaGrid
funcs.signature: func JuliaGrid(c complex128, v Viewport, width, height, maxIter int, bailout float64) *Grid
funcs.doc:
JuliaGrid rasterizes the filled Julia set of z -> z^2 + c over the given
viewport into a width×height [Grid]. Each cell holds the smooth escape
iteration count of the seed sampled at that pixel. Row 0 corresponds to the
top of the viewport.

funcs.name: MandelbrotGrid
funcs.signature: func MandelbrotGrid(v Viewport, width, height, maxIter int, bailout float64) *Grid
funcs.doc:
MandelbrotGrid rasterizes the Mandelbrot set over the given viewport into a
width×height [Grid]. Each cell holds the smooth escape iteration count (see
[EscapeResult.Smooth]) of the complex value sampled at that pixel, so interior
(non-escaping) points hold maxIter. Row 0 corresponds to the top of the
viewport. See [Viewport.PixelToComplex] for the pixel-to-complex mapping.

funcs.name: NewGrid
funcs.doc:
NewGrid allocates a zero-filled Grid with the given dimensions. It panics if
width or height is negative.

methods.name: At
methods.doc:
At returns the value at column x, row y. It panics if the coordinates are out
of range.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns a deep copy of the grid.

methods.name: CountFinite
methods.doc:
CountFinite returns the number of grid cells whose value is finite (neither
NaN nor infinite).

methods.name: Max
methods.doc: Max returns the largest value in the grid, or 0 for an empty grid.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns the arithmetic mean of all grid values, or 0 for an empty grid.

methods.name: Min
methods.doc: Min returns the smallest value in the grid, or 0 for an empty grid.

methods.name: MinMax
methods.doc:
MinMax returns the minimum and maximum values in the grid. For an empty grid
it returns (0, 0).

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns a new Grid whose values are linearly rescaled so that the
original minimum maps to 0 and the original maximum maps to 1. If all values
are equal the result is all zeros. The receiver is not modified.

methods.name: Row
methods.doc:
Row returns the y-th row of the grid as a slice that aliases the underlying
storage. Mutating it mutates the grid.

methods.name: Set
methods.signature: func (g *Grid) Set(x, y int, v float64)
methods.doc:
Set stores v at column x, row y. It panics if the coordinates are out of
range.

types.name: IFS
types.doc:
IFS is an iterated function system: a list of affine contraction Maps with an
equal-length list of selection Weights. When run as a chaos game the map at
index i is chosen with probability Weights[i] / sum(Weights). If Weights is
nil or empty the maps are chosen uniformly.

funcs.name: BarnsleyFern
funcs.doc:
BarnsleyFern returns the four-map iterated function system that generates
Michael Barnsley's fern. The maps and their selection probabilities are the
classical values; the attractor fits within roughly x in [-2.2, 2.7] and
y in [0, 10].

funcs.name: SierpinskiCarpetIFS
funcs.doc:
SierpinskiCarpetIFS returns the eight-map iterated function system whose
attractor is the Sierpinski carpet: the unit square is divided into a 3x3
grid and every cell except the center maps back to the whole via a 1/3-scale
contraction. The maps are chosen with equal probability.

funcs.name: SierpinskiTriangleIFS
funcs.doc:
SierpinskiTriangleIFS returns the three-map iterated function system whose
attractor is the Sierpinski triangle with vertices (0,0), (1,0) and
(0.5, 0.5). Each map scales the plane by 1/2 toward one vertex; the maps are
chosen with equal probability.

methods.name: ChaosGame
methods.signature: func (s IFS) ChaosGame(n int, seed int64) []Point2D
methods.doc:
ChaosGame runs the chaos game starting from the origin, discarding 20
transient points, and returns n attractor points. It is a convenience wrapper
around [IFS.ChaosGameFrom]. The output is deterministic for a given seed.

methods.name: ChaosGameFrom
methods.signature: func (s IFS) ChaosGameFrom(start Point2D, n, transient int, seed int64) []Point2D
methods.doc:
ChaosGameFrom runs the random-iteration ("chaos game") algorithm starting
from the point start: it repeatedly picks a map according to the system's
weights and applies it, discarding the first transient points before
recording n points. Randomness is driven solely by seed, so the output is
fully deterministic for a given seed. It returns n points and panics if the
system has no maps or n is negative.

types.name: Interval
types.doc: Interval is a closed real interval [Start, End].

funcs.name: CantorSet
funcs.signature: func CantorSet(start, end float64, iterations int) []Interval
funcs.doc:
CantorSet returns the intervals remaining after iterations steps of the
middle-thirds Cantor construction applied to [start, end]. Iteration 0
returns the single interval [start, end]; each step replaces every interval
by its first and last thirds, so the result contains 2^iterations intervals
with total length (end-start)*(2/3)^iterations. Intervals are returned in
increasing order. It panics if iterations is negative.

methods.name: Length
methods.doc: Length returns End - Start.

types.name: LSystem
types.doc:
LSystem is a deterministic context-free Lindenmayer system: an Axiom string
and a set of production Rules mapping a single symbol (rune) to a replacement
string. Symbols with no rule are left unchanged (they act as constants).

funcs.name: DragonLSystem
funcs.doc:
DragonLSystem returns the Heighway dragon curve L-system: axiom "F" with
rules X -> X+YF+ and Y -> -FX-Y, rendered with a turn angle of 90 degrees.
The symbols X and Y control the recursion and draw nothing themselves.

funcs.name: KochLSystem
funcs.doc:
KochLSystem returns the classic Koch curve L-system: axiom "F" with the rule
F -> F+F--F+F. Rendered with a turn angle of 60 degrees it produces the Koch
curve.

funcs.name: NewLSystem
funcs.signature: func NewLSystem(axiom string, rules map[rune]string) LSystem
funcs.doc:
NewLSystem constructs an LSystem from an axiom and a rule map. The rule map is
copied so later mutation of the argument does not affect the system.

funcs.name: PlantLSystem
funcs.doc:
PlantLSystem returns a branching plant L-system: axiom "X" with rules
X -> F+[[X]-X]-F[-FX]+X and F -> FF, rendered with a turn angle of about 25
degrees. It uses the bracket commands to create branches.

funcs.name: SierpinskiLSystem
funcs.doc:
SierpinskiLSystem returns an L-system whose turtle rendering (turn angle 60
degrees) draws the Sierpinski triangle. Axiom "F-G-G" with rules
F -> F-G+F+G-F and G -> GG.

methods.name: Expand
methods.doc:
Expand applies the production rules iterations times starting from the axiom
and returns the resulting string. Zero iterations returns the axiom itself.
It panics if iterations is negative.

methods.name: Step
methods.doc:
Step applies every production rule once to s in parallel, replacing each
symbol by its rule's right-hand side (or leaving it unchanged if it has no
rule) and returning the rewritten string.

types.name: Point2D
types.doc: Point2D is a point in the Euclidean plane with coordinates X and Y.

funcs.name: BoundingBox
funcs.doc:
BoundingBox returns the axis-aligned bounding box of pts as its minimum and
maximum corners. It returns two zero points when pts is empty.

funcs.name: KochCurve
funcs.signature: func KochCurve(p0, p1 Point2D, iterations int) []Point2D
funcs.doc:
KochCurve returns the polyline vertices of the Koch curve constructed between
the endpoints p0 and p1 after the given number of iterations. Iteration 0
returns the two endpoints [p0, p1]; each further iteration replaces every
segment by four segments (a bump raised on its middle third), so the result
has 4^iterations + 1 points. Bumps are raised to the left of the p0->p1
direction. It panics if iterations is negative.

funcs.name: KochSnowflake
funcs.signature: func KochSnowflake(center Point2D, radius float64, iterations int) []Point2D
funcs.doc:
KochSnowflake returns the closed polyline of the Koch snowflake inscribed in
the circle of the given radius centered at center, after the given number of
iterations. The base is an equilateral triangle whose corners lie on the
circle; each edge is replaced by a Koch curve with bumps pointing outward.
The returned slice is closed (its last point equals its first). It panics if
iterations is negative.

funcs.name: TurtlePath
funcs.doc:
TurtlePath interprets a command string and returns the ordered vertices
visited by the turtle: the start position followed by the endpoint of every
forward move (both drawing and non-drawing). It is intended for non-branching
L-systems; when brackets are present the returned polyline includes the jumps
created by popping the stack. See [Turtle] for the command set.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the vector sum p+q.

methods.name: Dist
methods.doc: Dist returns the Euclidean distance between p and q.

methods.name: Lerp
methods.signature: func (p Point2D) Lerp(q Point2D, t float64) Point2D
methods.doc: Lerp returns the linear interpolation between p (t=0) and q (t=1).

methods.name: Midpoint
methods.doc: Midpoint returns the point halfway between p and q.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean length of p treated as a vector from the origin.

methods.name: Rotate
methods.doc: Rotate returns p rotated counterclockwise about the origin by angle radians.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns p with both coordinates multiplied by s.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the vector difference p-q.

types.name: Segment
types.doc:
Segment is a directed line segment from (X0,Y0) to (X1,Y1), the drawing
primitive produced by the turtle interpreter.

funcs.name: Turtle
funcs.doc:
Turtle interprets a command string as turtle graphics and returns the drawn
line segments. The recognized commands are:

	F, G : move forward by Step, drawing a segment
	f, g : move forward by Step without drawing
	+    : turn left  (counterclockwise) by AngleDeg
	-    : turn right (clockwise)        by AngleDeg
	|    : turn around (180 degrees)
	[    : push the current state onto a stack
	]    : pop and restore the most recently pushed state

All other symbols are ignored, so L-system variables such as 'X' and 'Y' act
as no-ops. Bracketed sections produce disconnected segment groups, allowing
branching structures.

types.name: Triangle
types.doc: Triangle is a triangle with vertices A, B and C.

funcs.name: SierpinskiTriangle
funcs.signature: func SierpinskiTriangle(a, b, c Point2D, depth int) []Triangle
funcs.doc:
SierpinskiTriangle returns the list of filled sub-triangles produced by
recursively removing the central triangle from the triangle (a, b, c) to the
given depth. Depth 0 returns the single input triangle; each level replaces
every triangle by its three corner sub-triangles, so the result contains
3^depth triangles. It panics if depth is negative.

types.name: TurtleConfig
types.doc:
TurtleConfig parameterizes the turtle-graphics interpreter. Step is the
forward move length; AngleDeg is the turn angle in degrees applied by the
'+' and '-' commands; StartAngleDeg is the initial heading in degrees
measured counterclockwise from the positive x-axis; Start is the initial pen
position.

types.name: TurtleState
types.doc:
TurtleState is the pose of the turtle: its position and its heading in
radians measured counterclockwise from the positive x-axis.

types.name: Viewport
types.doc:
Viewport is an axis-aligned rectangle in the complex plane, used to map
integer pixel coordinates onto complex values. XMin/XMax bound the real axis
and YMin/YMax bound the imaginary axis.

funcs.name: NewViewport
funcs.signature: func NewViewport(centerRe, centerIm, radius float64) Viewport
funcs.doc:
NewViewport returns the square viewport centered at (centerRe, centerIm) with
the given half-width (radius) along each axis.

methods.name: Aspect
methods.doc:
Aspect returns the width-to-height ratio of the viewport. It returns 0 when
the height is zero.

methods.name: Center
methods.doc: Center returns the complex number at the center of the viewport.

methods.name: PixelToComplex
methods.signature: func (v Viewport) PixelToComplex(px, py, width, height int) complex128
methods.doc:
PixelToComplex maps the pixel at column px, row py of a width×height raster to
the complex value it samples. Column 0 maps to XMin and column width-1 maps
to XMax; row 0 maps to YMax (top) and row height-1 maps to YMin (bottom), so
increasing the row index moves downward as is conventional for images. When
width or height is 1 the corresponding axis collapses to its minimum edge.

methods.name: SpanX
methods.doc: SpanX returns the width of the viewport along the real axis.

methods.name: SpanY
methods.doc: SpanY returns the height of the viewport along the imaginary axis.

methods.name: Zoom
methods.doc:
Zoom returns a new viewport with the same center whose span along each axis is
multiplied by factor. A factor below 1 zooms in; above 1 zooms out.

funcs.name: BoxCount
funcs.doc:
BoxCount returns the number of distinct axis-aligned grid boxes of side
boxSize that contain at least one of the given points. It panics if boxSize
is not positive. This is the count N(epsilon) used in box-counting dimension
estimation.

funcs.name: BoxCountingDimension
funcs.doc:
BoxCountingDimension estimates the box-counting (Minkowski–Bouligand)
dimension of a finite point set by counting occupied boxes at each of the
given box sizes and returning the slope of log N(epsilon) versus
log(1/epsilon). At least two box sizes are required; the sizes must be
positive. Smaller boxes give a better estimate for a true fractal but require
densely sampled points.

funcs.name: BoxCountingDimensionFromCounts
funcs.doc:
BoxCountingDimensionFromCounts estimates a fractal dimension directly from a
table of (box size, occupied-box count) samples: it returns the slope of
log(count) versus log(1/size). This is useful when counts were obtained
externally. The slices must have equal length of at least two, and all
entries must be positive. For an exact power law count = k*size^(-D) the
returned value is exactly D.

funcs.name: FitLine
funcs.doc:
FitLine returns the slope and intercept of the ordinary least-squares line
y = slope*x + intercept fitted to the paired samples xs, ys. It panics if the
slices differ in length or contain fewer than two points. When every x is
identical the slope is 0 and the intercept is the mean of ys.

funcs.name: FitSlope
funcs.doc:
FitSlope returns the slope of the ordinary least-squares line y = m*x + b
fitted to the paired samples xs, ys. It panics if the slices differ in length
or contain fewer than two points, and returns 0 when every x is identical.

funcs.name: HausdorffDimensionSelfSimilar
funcs.signature: func HausdorffDimensionSelfSimilar(n int, scale float64) float64
funcs.doc:
HausdorffDimensionSelfSimilar returns the exact similarity (Hausdorff)
dimension of a strictly self-similar set composed of n non-overlapping copies
of itself, each scaled by the ratio scale (0 < scale < 1). The dimension is
log(n)/log(1/scale). For example n=3, scale=1/2 gives the Sierpinski triangle
dimension log2(3) ≈ 1.585; n=4, scale=1/3 gives the Koch curve dimension
log(4)/log(3) ≈ 1.262. It panics if n < 1 or scale is not in (0,1).

funcs.name: InJuliaSet
funcs.signature: func InJuliaSet(z, c complex128, maxIter int) bool
funcs.doc:
InJuliaSet reports whether the seed z lies in the filled Julia set of
z -> z^2 + c, i.e. whether its orbit fails to escape radius 2 within maxIter
iterations.

funcs.name: InMainCardioid
funcs.doc:
InMainCardioid reports, in closed form, whether c lies inside the main
cardioid of the Mandelbrot set — the large heart-shaped region containing all
c for which z -> z^2 + c has an attracting fixed point. Writing
p = |c - 1/4| = sqrt((Re c - 1/4)^2 + (Im c)^2), the membership criterion is
Re(c) <= p - 2*p^2 + 1/4, which holds exactly on the closed cardioid.

funcs.name: InMandelbrotSet
funcs.signature: func InMandelbrotSet(c complex128, maxIter int) bool
funcs.doc:
InMandelbrotSet reports whether c lies in the Mandelbrot set, i.e. whether
the orbit of z -> z^2 + c starting at 0 fails to escape radius 2 within
maxIter iterations. Because the test is truncated at maxIter, points very
near the boundary may be misclassified as members.

funcs.name: InPeriod2Bulb
funcs.doc:
InPeriod2Bulb reports, in closed form, whether c lies inside the period-2
bulb of the Mandelbrot set: the disk of radius 1/4 centered at -1. Membership
is exactly |c + 1| <= 1/4.

funcs.name: JuliaSmooth
funcs.signature: func JuliaSmooth(z, c complex128, maxIter int, bailout float64) float64
funcs.doc:
JuliaSmooth returns the smooth (fractional) escape iteration count for the
Julia map z -> z^2 + c seeded at z. See [EscapeResult.Smooth].

funcs.name: MandelbrotSmooth
funcs.signature: func MandelbrotSmooth(c complex128, maxIter int, bailout float64) float64
funcs.doc:
MandelbrotSmooth returns the smooth (fractional) escape iteration count for
the Mandelbrot map at c. See [EscapeResult.Smooth]. A large bailout (for
example 256) gives the smoothest result.

funcs.name: Orbit
funcs.signature: func Orbit(z0, c complex128, n int) []complex128
funcs.doc:
Orbit returns the first n+1 points of the orbit of z -> z^2 + c starting at
z0, that is [z0, f(z0), f(f(z0)), ...] of length n+1. For n < 0 it returns
nil.

packages.name: galois
packages.synopsis: Package galois implements arithmetic and structure theory for finite fields GF(p^n), built entirely on the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package galois implements arithmetic and structure theory for finite fields
GF(p^n), built entirely on the Go standard library.

A finite field (Galois field) of order q = p^n exists for every prime p and
positive integer n. This package models both the prime field GF(p) and the
extension fields GF(p^n), providing element arithmetic (add, subtract,
multiply, invert, divide, exponentiate) together with the deeper structural
tools that make finite fields useful in coding theory and cryptography.

# Representation

Prime-field elements are ordinary residues handled by the modular-arithmetic
helpers ([AddMod], [MulMod], [InvMod], [PowMod], …) operating on
math/big integers reduced into the range [0, p).

Polynomials over GF(p) are represented by [Poly], a little-endian slice of
coefficients paired with the characteristic p. Extension fields are modelled
by [Field], which pins a prime p, a degree n, and a monic irreducible
modulus polynomial of degree n; elements are [Element] values, residues in
GF(p)[x] modulo that polynomial.

# Capabilities

  - Prime-field modular arithmetic, quadratic residues and modular square
    roots ([SqrtMod], Tonelli–Shanks), multiplicative order and primitive
    roots.
  - Dense polynomial arithmetic over GF(p): division, gcd, extended gcd,
    modular exponentiation, derivatives, evaluation and composition.
  - Irreducibility testing (Rabin), counting irreducibles, and searching for
    irreducible and primitive polynomials, including a deterministic
    Conway-style canonical choice.
  - Full polynomial factorisation over GF(p): square-free decomposition and
    Berlekamp's deterministic algorithm.
  - Extension-field element arithmetic, the Frobenius automorphism, trace and
    norm to the prime field, minimal polynomials, conjugates and cyclotomic
    cosets.
  - Multiplicative order of field elements, primitive elements, and discrete
    logarithms by the baby-step giant-step method.
  - The subfield lattice of GF(p^n).

Everything is exact: computations run over math/big integers, so results are
mathematically sound rather than floating-point approximations.

types.name: Element
types.doc:
Element is a member of a Field, represented by its unique residue polynomial
of degree less than the field degree.

funcs.name: ConjugateElements
funcs.doc:
ConjugateElements returns the Galois conjugates of a. It is a package-level
wrapper over (*Element).Conjugates.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns a + b in the field.

methods.name: Coeffs
methods.doc:
Coeffs returns the little-endian coefficient vector of the element, padded to
the field degree n.

methods.name: Conjugates
methods.doc:
Conjugates returns the Galois conjugates of a, namely a, a^p, a^(p^2), … up
to but not including the first repetition. Their count is the degree of a's
minimal polynomial over GF(p).

methods.name: Copy
methods.doc: Copy returns an independent copy of the element.

methods.name: Div
methods.doc: Div returns a / b in the field, or an error when b is zero.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether a and b are the same element of the same field.

methods.name: Frobenius
methods.doc:
Frobenius returns the image of a under the Frobenius automorphism x ↦ x^p,
the canonical generator of the Galois group of GF(p^n) over GF(p).

methods.name: FrobeniusPow
methods.doc:
FrobeniusPow returns the k-fold Frobenius image a^(p^k). Negative k are
reduced modulo n, matching the cyclic Galois group of order n.

methods.name: Inv
methods.doc: Inv returns the multiplicative inverse of a, or an error when a is zero.

methods.name: IsOne
methods.doc: IsOne reports whether a is the multiplicative identity.

methods.name: IsPrimitive
methods.doc:
IsPrimitive reports whether a generates the whole multiplicative group of the
field, i.e. its order equals p^n - 1.

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc: IsZero reports whether a is the additive identity.

methods.name: MinimalPoly
methods.doc:
MinimalPoly returns the minimal polynomial of a over GF(p): the unique monic
polynomial of least degree with a as a root, equal to the product of
(x - c) over the distinct conjugates c of a.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns a * b in the field.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns the additive inverse of a.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc:
Norm returns the field norm N(a) = a^((p^n-1)/(p-1)) = a · a^p · … · a^(p^(n-1))
of a down to the prime field GF(p), returned as an integer in [0, p).

methods.name: Order
methods.doc:
Order returns the multiplicative order of the non-zero element a: the least
positive k with a^k = 1. It returns an error when a is zero.

methods.name: Pow
methods.doc:
Pow returns a raised to the integer power e. Negative exponents invert a
first and require a to be non-zero.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the element via its residue polynomial.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns a - b in the field.

methods.name: ToInt
methods.doc:
ToInt returns the integer index in [0, p^n) obtained by reading the element's
coefficient vector as base-p digits. It is the inverse of ElementFromInt.

methods.name: Trace
methods.doc:
Trace returns the field trace Tr(a) = a + a^p + … + a^(p^(n-1)) of a down to
the prime field GF(p), returned as an integer in [0, p).

types.name: Field
types.doc:
Field represents the finite field GF(p^n). It fixes the prime characteristic
P, the extension degree N, and a monic irreducible modulus polynomial Mod of
degree N over GF(P). Field elements are residues of GF(P)[x] modulo Mod.

funcs.name: NewField
funcs.doc:
NewField returns GF(p^n) defined by the given monic modulus polynomial, which
must be irreducible of degree n >= 1 over GF(p). It returns an error
otherwise.

funcs.name: NewFieldFromDegree
funcs.doc:
NewFieldFromDegree returns GF(p^n) using an automatically discovered monic
irreducible modulus of degree n.

funcs.name: NewPrimeField
funcs.doc:
NewPrimeField returns the prime field GF(p). It returns an error when p is
not prime.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns a + b, a convenience wrapper over Element.Add.

methods.name: Characteristic
methods.doc: Characteristic returns the prime characteristic p of the field.

methods.name: Degree
methods.doc: Degree returns the extension degree n, so the field is GF(p^n).

methods.name: Div
methods.doc: Div returns a / b.

methods.name: Element
methods.doc:
Element returns the field element for the given little-endian int64
coefficients over GF(p), reduced into the field.

methods.name: ElementFromCoeffs
methods.doc:
ElementFromCoeffs returns the field element for the given little-endian
big.Int coefficients, reduced into the field.

methods.name: ElementFromInt
methods.doc:
ElementFromInt maps an integer index k in [0, p^n) to a field element by
writing k in base p as the element's coefficient vector. It is the inverse of
(*Element).ToInt.

methods.name: Elements
methods.doc:
Elements enumerates every element of the field in index order. It is intended
for small fields; the slice has p^n entries.

methods.name: FrobeniusAutomorphism
methods.doc:
FrobeniusAutomorphism returns the Frobenius map x ↦ x^p of the field as a Go
function.

methods.name: FromPoly
methods.doc:
FromPoly returns the field element represented by the polynomial p reduced
modulo the field modulus.

methods.name: Generator
methods.doc:
Generator is a synonym for PrimitiveElement, returning a multiplicative
generator of the field.

methods.name: Inv
methods.doc: Inv returns the inverse of a.

methods.name: Modulus
methods.doc: Modulus returns a copy of the field's defining irreducible polynomial.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns a * b.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns -a.

methods.name: NonzeroElements
methods.doc:
NonzeroElements enumerates every non-zero element of the field in index
order. It is intended for small fields.

methods.name: One
methods.doc: One returns the multiplicative identity of the field.

methods.name: Order
methods.doc: Order returns the number of elements in the field, p^n.

methods.name: Pow
methods.doc: Pow returns a^e.

methods.name: PrimitiveElement
methods.doc:
PrimitiveElement returns a generator of the multiplicative group of the
field: an element of order p^n - 1. It scans elements by index and returns
the first primitive one.

methods.name: PrimitiveElementX
methods.doc:
PrimitiveElementX returns the residue class of x, the canonical generator of
GF(p^n) as GF(p)[x]/(mod). It is a field generator but not necessarily a
generator of the multiplicative group.

methods.name: RandomElement
methods.doc: RandomElement returns a uniformly random element drawn from r.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String describes the field as "GF(p^n)".

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns a - b.

methods.name: SubfieldOrders
methods.doc:
SubfieldOrders returns the orders p^d of all subfields of the field, in
increasing order.

methods.name: Subfields
methods.doc:
Subfields returns the degrees d dividing n for which GF(p^d) is a subfield of
the field GF(p^n), in increasing order. GF(p) (d=1) and the field itself
(d=n) are always included.

methods.name: Zero
methods.doc: Zero returns the additive identity of the field.

types.name: MatModP
types.doc:
MatModP is a dense matrix with entries in the prime field GF(p). Entries are
stored in Data[row][col], each reduced into [0, p).

funcs.name: IdentityModP
funcs.doc: IdentityModP returns the n×n identity matrix over GF(p).

funcs.name: NewMatModP
funcs.doc: NewMatModP returns a rows×cols zero matrix over GF(p).

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent copy of the matrix.

methods.name: Get
methods.doc: Get returns the entry at (i, j).

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc:
Mul returns the matrix product m*other over GF(p). The inner dimensions must
agree; a mismatch returns nil.

methods.name: NullSpace
methods.doc:
NullSpace returns a basis for the right null space {x : m·x = 0} over GF(p),
each basis vector represented as a length-Cols slice.

methods.name: Rank
methods.doc: Rank returns the rank of the matrix over GF(p).

methods.name: RowReduce
methods.doc:
RowReduce returns the reduced row-echelon form of the matrix together with
its rank. The receiver is left unchanged.

methods.name: Set
methods.doc: Set stores v (reduced mod p) at (i, j).

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc:
Sub returns the entrywise difference m-other over GF(p). A shape mismatch
returns nil.

methods.name: Transpose
methods.doc: Transpose returns the transpose of the matrix.

types.name: Poly
types.doc:
Poly is a dense univariate polynomial over the prime field GF(p).
Coefficients are stored little-endian: Coeff[i] is the coefficient of x^i,
reduced into [0, p). A normalised polynomial has no trailing zero
coefficients, so the zero polynomial has an empty Coeff slice.

funcs.name: AllIrreduciblePolys
funcs.doc:
AllIrreduciblePolys returns every monic irreducible polynomial of degree n
over GF(p). It enumerates all p^n candidates and is intended for small fields.

funcs.name: CanonicalIrreduciblePoly
funcs.doc:
CanonicalIrreduciblePoly returns the deterministic canonical (lexicographically
least by coefficient index) monic irreducible polynomial of degree n over
GF(p). It is identical to FindIrreducible.

funcs.name: ConwayStylePoly
funcs.doc:
ConwayStylePoly returns a deterministic Conway-style defining polynomial for
GF(p^n): the lexicographically least primitive polynomial of degree n over
GF(p). Unlike true Conway polynomials it does not enforce norm-compatibility
across subfields, but it is a well-defined, reproducible canonical choice.

funcs.name: FindIrreducible
funcs.doc:
FindIrreducible returns the first monic irreducible polynomial of degree n
over GF(p), scanning candidates by ascending coefficient index.

funcs.name: FindPrimitivePoly
funcs.doc:
FindPrimitivePoly returns the first primitive polynomial of degree n over
GF(p), scanning candidates by ascending coefficient index.

funcs.name: MinimalPolynomial
funcs.doc:
MinimalPolynomial returns the minimal polynomial of a over GF(p). It is a
package-level wrapper over (*Element).MinimalPoly.

funcs.name: NewPoly
funcs.doc:
NewPoly builds a polynomial over GF(p) from int64 coefficients given
little-endian (constant term first).

funcs.name: NewPolyBig
funcs.doc:
NewPolyBig builds a polynomial over GF(p) from big.Int coefficients given
little-endian. The input slice is copied.

funcs.name: PolyConst
funcs.doc: PolyConst returns the constant polynomial c over GF(p).

funcs.name: PolyGcd
funcs.doc: PolyGcd returns the monic greatest common divisor of a and b.

funcs.name: PolyLcm
funcs.doc: PolyLcm returns the monic least common multiple of a and b.

funcs.name: PolyMonomial
funcs.doc: PolyMonomial returns the monomial coeff·x^deg over GF(p).

funcs.name: PolyMulMod
funcs.doc: PolyMulMod returns (a * b) mod m over GF(p).

funcs.name: PolyOne
funcs.doc: PolyOne returns the constant polynomial 1 over GF(p).

funcs.name: PolyPowMod
funcs.doc: PolyPowMod returns a^e mod m over GF(p) for a non-negative exponent e.

funcs.name: PolyX
funcs.doc: PolyX returns the monomial x over GF(p).

funcs.name: PolyZero
funcs.doc: PolyZero returns the zero polynomial over GF(p).

funcs.name: PthRootPoly
funcs.doc:
PthRootPoly returns the p-th root of f, assuming f is a perfect p-th power in
GF(p)[x] (its only non-zero coefficients occur at exponents divisible by p).
Over a prime field the coefficient p-th roots equal the coefficients
themselves by Fermat's little theorem.

funcs.name: RandomIrreducible
funcs.signature: func RandomIrreducible(p *big.Int, n int, r *rand.Rand) (*Poly, error)
funcs.doc:
RandomIrreducible returns a random monic irreducible polynomial of degree n
over GF(p), drawing candidate coefficient vectors from r until one is
irreducible.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the sum a + b over GF(p).

methods.name: Coefficient
methods.doc: Coefficient returns the coefficient of x^i, or 0 when i is out of range.

methods.name: Compose
methods.doc: Compose returns the composition a(b(x)) over GF(p).

methods.name: Copy
methods.doc: Copy returns an independent deep copy of the polynomial.

methods.name: Degree
methods.doc:
Degree returns the degree of the polynomial. The zero polynomial has
degree -1 by convention.

methods.name: Derivative
methods.doc: Derivative returns the formal derivative of the polynomial over GF(p).

methods.name: DivMod
methods.doc:
DivMod returns the quotient q and remainder r of a divided by b, satisfying
a = q*b + r with deg(r) < deg(b). It returns an error when b is zero.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether a and b are equal as polynomials over the same field.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc:
Eval evaluates the polynomial at the field element x using Horner's rule,
returning a value in [0, p).

methods.name: ExtendedGcd
methods.doc:
ExtendedGcd returns g = gcd(a, b) together with cofactors s and t satisfying
a*s + b*t = g. The returned g is monic (unless it is zero).

methods.name: Gcd
methods.doc:
Gcd returns the monic greatest common divisor of a and b over GF(p). The gcd
of two zero polynomials is zero.

methods.name: IsConstant
methods.doc: IsConstant reports whether the polynomial has degree 0 or is zero.

methods.name: IsMonic
methods.doc: IsMonic reports whether the leading coefficient is 1.

methods.name: IsOne
methods.doc: IsOne reports whether the polynomial is the constant 1.

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc: IsZero reports whether the polynomial is the zero polynomial.

methods.name: LeadingCoeff
methods.doc:
LeadingCoeff returns the coefficient of the highest-degree term, or 0 for the
zero polynomial.

methods.name: Monic
methods.doc:
Monic returns a scaled so that its leading coefficient is 1. The zero
polynomial is returned unchanged.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the product a * b over GF(p).

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns the additive inverse -a over GF(p).

methods.name: Pow
methods.doc: Pow returns a raised to the non-negative integer power e.

methods.name: PowMod
methods.doc:
PowMod returns a^e reduced modulo the polynomial m, using square-and-multiply.
The exponent e must be non-negative.

methods.name: Quo
methods.doc: Quo returns the quotient of a divided by b.

methods.name: Rem
methods.doc: Rem returns the remainder of a divided by b (a mod b).

methods.name: ScalarMul
methods.doc: ScalarMul returns the polynomial scaled by the field element s.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String renders the polynomial in descending-degree infix form such as
"x^2 + 3x + 1", or "0" for the zero polynomial.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the difference a - b over GF(p).

methods.name: ToIndex
methods.doc:
ToIndex encodes a polynomial's coefficient vector as a big integer for stable
ordering: Σ Coeff[i]·p^i.

types.name: PolyFactor
types.doc:
PolyFactor pairs an irreducible (or square-free) monic polynomial factor with
the multiplicity at which it occurs.

funcs.name: Factor
funcs.doc:
Factor returns the complete factorisation of f over GF(p): the leading
coefficient together with the distinct monic irreducible factors and their
multiplicities. The product of the factors (each raised to its multiplicity),
scaled by the leading coefficient, reconstructs f.

funcs.name: SquareFreeFactorization
funcs.doc:
SquareFreeFactorization returns the square-free decomposition of f: a set of
pairwise-coprime square-free monic factors together with their multiplicities,
whose product (each raised to its multiplicity) equals the monic part of f.

types.name: PrimePower
types.doc:
PrimePower records a prime together with its exponent in a factorisation,
so that n = Prime^Exp times the other factors.

funcs.name: FactorInt
funcs.doc:
FactorInt returns the prime factorisation of |n| as a slice of PrimePower
entries sorted by increasing prime. FactorInt(0) and FactorInt(±1) return an
empty slice.

funcs.name: AddMod
funcs.doc: AddMod returns (a + b) mod p in the range [0, p).

funcs.name: BabyStepGiantStep
funcs.doc: BabyStepGiantStep is an alias for DiscreteLog.

funcs.name: CyclotomicCoset
funcs.doc:
CyclotomicCoset returns the q-cyclotomic coset of s modulo q^n - 1, that is
the sorted set {s, s·q, s·q², …} reduced modulo q^n - 1.

funcs.name: CyclotomicCosets
funcs.doc:
CyclotomicCosets returns the partition of {0, 1, …, q^n-2} into q-cyclotomic
cosets modulo q^n - 1, each coset sorted and the list ordered by smallest
representative.

funcs.name: DiscreteLog
funcs.doc:
DiscreteLog returns the discrete logarithm x of target to the base, the least
non-negative x with base^x = target, computed by the baby-step giant-step
algorithm. It returns an error when the fields differ or no logarithm exists
(target is not a power of base).

funcs.name: DistinctFactorCount
funcs.doc: DistinctFactorCount returns the number of distinct irreducible factors of f.

funcs.name: DivMod
funcs.doc:
DivMod returns (a / b) mod p, that is a times the inverse of b, or an error
when b is not invertible.

funcs.name: DivisorsInt
funcs.doc:
DivisorsInt returns all positive divisors of |n| in increasing order.
DivisorsInt(0) returns an empty slice.

funcs.name: EulerPhi
funcs.doc:
EulerPhi returns Euler's totient φ(n): the count of integers in [1, n]
coprime to n. EulerPhi(1) == 1.

funcs.name: ExtendedGcd
funcs.doc:
ExtendedGcd returns g = gcd(a, b) together with Bézout coefficients x and y
satisfying a*x + b*y = g.

funcs.name: FactorCount
funcs.doc:
FactorCount returns the number of irreducible factors of f counted with
multiplicity.

funcs.name: FieldNorm
funcs.doc:
FieldNorm returns the norm of a to the prime field. It is a package-level
wrapper over (*Element).Norm.

funcs.name: FieldTrace
funcs.doc:
FieldTrace returns the trace of a to the prime field. It is a package-level
wrapper over (*Element).Trace.

funcs.name: Gcd
funcs.doc: Gcd returns the non-negative greatest common divisor of a and b.

funcs.name: IntPow
funcs.doc: IntPow returns base raised to the power exp (exp >= 0) as an exact integer.

funcs.name: InvMod
funcs.doc:
InvMod returns the multiplicative inverse of a modulo p, or an error when a
is not invertible (gcd(a, p) != 1).

funcs.name: IsIrreducible
funcs.doc:
IsIrreducible reports whether f is irreducible over GF(p) using Rabin's test.
Constants and the zero polynomial are not irreducible; every degree-1
polynomial is irreducible.

funcs.name: IsPrimeInt
funcs.doc:
IsPrimeInt reports whether n is a prime integer. It uses a deterministic
small-factor screen followed by a strong probabilistic (Baillie–PSW style)
test via math/big, which has no known composite false positives.

funcs.name: IsPrimitivePoly
funcs.doc:
IsPrimitivePoly reports whether f is a primitive polynomial over GF(p): a
monic irreducible polynomial whose root x generates the whole multiplicative
group of GF(p^deg f), i.e. x has order p^(deg f) - 1.

funcs.name: IsPrimitiveRoot
funcs.doc:
IsPrimitiveRoot reports whether a is a primitive root modulo the prime p,
i.e. a generator of the cyclic group of units of order p-1.

funcs.name: IsQuadraticResidue
funcs.doc:
IsQuadraticResidue reports whether a is a square modulo the prime p. Zero is
treated as a residue.

funcs.name: IsSquareFree
funcs.doc: IsSquareFree reports whether f has no repeated irreducible factor.

funcs.name: IsSubfieldDegree
funcs.doc: IsSubfieldDegree reports whether GF(p^d) embeds in GF(p^n), i.e. d divides n.

funcs.name: Jacobi
funcs.doc: Jacobi returns the Jacobi symbol (a/n) for odd n > 0, one of -1, 0, +1.

funcs.name: Lcm
funcs.doc: Lcm returns the non-negative least common multiple of a and b. Lcm(0, x) is 0.

funcs.name: Legendre
funcs.doc:
Legendre returns the Legendre symbol (a/p) for an odd prime p: it is 0 when
p divides a, +1 when a is a non-zero quadratic residue, and -1 otherwise.

funcs.name: MobiusMu
funcs.doc:
MobiusMu returns the Möbius function μ(n): 0 if n is divisible by a square
greater than 1, otherwise (-1)^k where k is the number of distinct primes.

funcs.name: Mod
funcs.doc: Mod returns a reduced into the canonical range [0, p) for the modulus p > 0.

funcs.name: MulMod
funcs.doc: MulMod returns (a * b) mod p in the range [0, p).

funcs.name: MultiplicativeOrder
funcs.doc:
MultiplicativeOrder returns the multiplicative order of a in the group of
units modulo the prime p: the smallest positive k with a^k ≡ 1 (mod p). It
returns an error when a is 0 modulo p. The modulus is assumed prime, so the
group order is p-1.

funcs.name: NegMod
funcs.doc: NegMod returns (-a) mod p in the range [0, p).

funcs.name: NextPrime
funcs.doc: NextPrime returns the smallest prime strictly greater than n.

funcs.name: NumDivisors
funcs.doc: NumDivisors returns the number of positive divisors of |n|.

funcs.name: NumberOfIrreducibles
funcs.doc:
NumberOfIrreducibles returns the number of monic irreducible polynomials of
degree n over GF(p), via the necklace formula
(1/n)·Σ_{d|n} μ(d)·p^(n/d).

funcs.name: PolyEqual
funcs.doc: PolyEqual reports whether two polynomials are equal.

funcs.name: PowMod
funcs.doc:
PowMod returns a raised to the power e modulo p. Negative exponents invert a
first and therefore require a to be invertible.

funcs.name: PrimeFactors
funcs.doc: PrimeFactors returns the distinct prime factors of |n| in increasing order.

funcs.name: PrimitiveRoot
funcs.doc: PrimitiveRoot returns the smallest primitive root modulo the prime p.

funcs.name: SqrtMod
funcs.doc:
SqrtMod returns a square root r of a modulo the prime p, so that
r*r ≡ a (mod p). It uses the Tonelli–Shanks algorithm and returns an error
when a is a non-residue. When a root exists, p-r is the other root.

funcs.name: SubMod
funcs.doc: SubMod returns (a - b) mod p in the range [0, p).

funcs.name: SubfieldDegrees
funcs.doc:
SubfieldDegrees returns the degrees of the subfields of GF(p^n): the positive
divisors of n.

examples.name: DiscreteLog
examples.code:
f, _ := NewPrimeField(big.NewInt(7))
base := f.Element(3)
x, _ := DiscreteLog(base, f.Element(5))
fmt.Printf("3^%v = 5 (mod 7)\n", x)

examples.name: Field
examples.code:
f, _ := NewField(big.NewInt(2), NewPoly(big.NewInt(2), 1, 1, 0, 1))
fmt.Println(f)

x := f.Element(0, 1)
cube, _ := x.Pow(big.NewInt(3))
fmt.Println("x^3 =", cube)

ord, _ := x.Order()
fmt.Println("order of x =", ord)

fmt.Println("Tr(x) =", x.Trace())
fmt.Println("N(x)  =", x.Norm())
examples.output:
GF(2^3)
x^3 = x + 1
order of x = 7
Tr(x) = 0
N(x)  = 1

packages.name: gametheory
packages.synopsis: Package gametheory implements classical game theory: normal-form games, Nash equilibria (pure and mixed), zero-sum solutions via linear programming, dominated-strategy elimination, Pareto frontiers, cooperative solution concepts (the Shapley and Banzhaf values, the core), and a small toolkit of Prisoner's-dilemma and canonical-game helpers.
packages.doc:
Package gametheory implements classical game theory: normal-form games,
Nash equilibria (pure and mixed), zero-sum solutions via linear programming,
dominated-strategy elimination, Pareto frontiers, cooperative solution
concepts (the Shapley and Banzhaf values, the core), and a small toolkit of
Prisoner's-dilemma and canonical-game helpers.

The central type is Game, a two-player normal-form game holding separate
payoff matrices for the row and column players. On top of it the package
offers:

  - equilibrium analysis: PureNashEquilibria and, via support enumeration,
    the full set of mixed equilibria (MixedNashEquilibria);
  - zero-sum solving: SolveZeroSum computes the value of the game and a pair
    of optimal minimax strategies by linear programming (a self-contained
    Bland's-rule simplex), with Maximin, Minimax and SaddlePoint helpers for
    the pure case;
  - dominance: strict and weak domination tests and iterated elimination;
  - efficiency: Pareto frontiers and the social optimum;
  - cooperation: CooperativeGame with the Shapley value, the Banzhaf value,
    core membership tests and structural predicates (superadditivity,
    monotonicity, convexity), plus WeightedVotingGame;
  - the Prisoner's dilemma: PrisonersDilemma with iterated-play simulation
    and stock strategies (TitForTat, GrimTrigger, WinStayLoseShift, ...),
    and constructors for the canonical games MatchingPennies, StagHunt,
    BattleOfTheSexes and GameOfChicken.

All routines use only the Go standard library, are deterministic, and are
validated in the accompanying tests against closed-form reference values.

consts.doc:
Pure-strategy indices shared by the Prisoner's-dilemma helpers and iterated
play: Cooperate is strategy 0 and Defect is strategy 1.

vars.signature: var ErrDistribution = errors.New("gametheory: probabilities must be non-negative and sum to one")
vars.doc:
ErrDistribution is returned when a slice is not a valid probability
distribution.

vars.signature: var ErrNotZeroSum = errors.New("gametheory: game is not zero-sum")
vars.doc: ErrNotZeroSum is returned by Game.SolveZeroSum when the game is not zero-sum.

vars.signature: var ErrPayoffOrder = errors.New("gametheory: Prisoner's dilemma requires T > R > P > S")
vars.doc: ErrPayoffOrder is returned when Prisoner's-dilemma payoffs violate T>R>P>S.

vars.signature: var ErrPlayers = errors.New("gametheory: player count must be between 1 and 20")
vars.doc: ErrPlayers is returned when a player count is out of the supported range.

vars.signature: var ErrShape = errors.New("gametheory: payoff matrices must be non-empty, rectangular and of equal shape")
vars.doc: ErrShape is returned when payoff matrices are empty, ragged, or mismatched.

vars.signature: var ErrSolve = errors.New("gametheory: failed to solve linear program")
vars.doc: ErrSolve is returned when the internal linear program fails to solve.

types.name: CooperativeGame
types.doc:
CooperativeGame is a transferable-utility cooperative game on a fixed set of
players {0, 1, ..., Players-1}. A coalition is encoded as a bitmask whose bit
i is set when player i belongs to the coalition. The worth of every coalition
is precomputed and stored in a slice of length 2^Players indexed by that
bitmask, so lookups and the solution concepts run without re-invoking the
characteristic function.

funcs.name: NewCooperativeGame
funcs.signature: func NewCooperativeGame(players int, characteristic func(coalition uint) float64) (CooperativeGame, error)
funcs.doc:
NewCooperativeGame builds a CooperativeGame with the given number of players
by evaluating the characteristic function on every one of the 2^players
coalition bitmasks. The characteristic function should return the worth
v(coalition); the empty coalition's value is stored as evaluated (callers
conventionally return 0 for it). players must be between 1 and 20 inclusive.

funcs.name: NewCooperativeGameFromValues
funcs.doc:
NewCooperativeGameFromValues builds a CooperativeGame from a fully specified
worth table, where values[mask] is v(mask) for each coalition bitmask. The
slice length must equal 2^players for some players in [1, 20]. The slice is
copied.

funcs.name: WeightedVotingGame
funcs.doc:
WeightedVotingGame constructs the simple cooperative game in which a coalition
wins (worth 1) when the total weight of its members is at least quota, and
loses (worth 0) otherwise. weights must have between 1 and 20 entries.

methods.name: BanzhafValue
methods.doc:
BanzhafValue returns the (non-normalized) Banzhaf value of the game: for each
player, the average marginal contribution taken with equal weight over all
2^(n-1) coalitions not containing that player,

	β_i = 1 / 2^(n-1) · Σ_{S ⊆ N\{i}} (v(S ∪ {i}) - v(S)).

Unlike the Shapley value, the Banzhaf value need not sum to v(N).

methods.name: GrandCoalitionValue
methods.doc: GrandCoalitionValue returns v(N), the worth of the coalition of all players.

methods.name: IsConvex
methods.doc:
IsConvex reports whether the game is convex (supermodular): for all coalitions
S and T, v(S ∪ T) + v(S ∩ T) >= v(S) + v(T) within tolerance tol. Convex games
always have a non-empty core containing the Shapley value.

methods.name: IsEssential
methods.doc:
IsEssential reports whether the game is essential: the grand coalition is
worth strictly more (by more than tol) than the players acting alone, i.e.
v(N) > Σ_i v({i}). Only essential games have a non-trivial solution.

methods.name: IsInCore
methods.signature: func (cg CooperativeGame) IsInCore(x []float64, tol float64) bool
methods.doc:
IsInCore reports whether allocation x is in the core of the game: it must be
efficient (Σ x_i = v(N)) and coalitionally rational (Σ_{i∈S} x_i >= v(S) for
every coalition S), all within tolerance tol. len(x) must equal Players.

methods.name: IsMonotone
methods.doc:
IsMonotone reports whether the game is monotone: enlarging a coalition never
decreases its worth, i.e. v(S) <= v(T) whenever S ⊆ T, within tolerance tol.

methods.name: IsSuperadditive
methods.doc:
IsSuperadditive reports whether the game is superadditive: for every pair of
disjoint coalitions S and T, v(S ∪ T) >= v(S) + v(T) within tolerance tol.

methods.name: MarginalContribution
methods.signature: func (cg CooperativeGame) MarginalContribution(player int, coalition uint) float64
methods.doc:
MarginalContribution returns v(coalition ∪ {player}) - v(coalition), the value
player adds when joining the coalition. The coalition must not already
contain player.

methods.name: ShapleyValue
methods.doc:
ShapleyValue returns the Shapley value of the game: a fair allocation of the
grand coalition's worth in which each player receives the probability-weighted
average of their marginal contributions over all coalitions they may join,

	φ_i = Σ_{S ⊆ N\{i}} |S|! (n-|S|-1)! / n! · (v(S ∪ {i}) - v(S)).

The returned slice has one entry per player and, for any game, sums to v(N).

methods.name: Worth
methods.doc:
Worth returns v(coalition), the worth of the coalition encoded by the given
bitmask.

types.name: Game
types.doc:
Game is a finite two-player game in normal (strategic) form. The row player
chooses a row i in [0, m) and the column player a column j in [0, n); the
resulting payoffs are Row[i][j] to the row player and Col[i][j] to the
column player. Both matrices are m-by-n and are shared by value on copy, so
callers should treat a Game as immutable once constructed.

funcs.name: BattleOfTheSexes
funcs.doc:
BattleOfTheSexes returns the Battle of the Sexes coordination game. Each
player prefers a different one of two events (strategy 0 and strategy 1) but
both prefer being together to being apart: the row player earns 2 at
(0,0) and 1 at (1,1), the column player 1 at (0,0) and 2 at (1,1), and both
earn 0 when they miscoordinate. It has two pure Nash equilibria and one mixed.

funcs.name: GameOfChicken
funcs.doc:
GameOfChicken returns the game of Chicken (also called Hawk-Dove). Each player
chooses Dare/Swerve (strategy 0 = Swerve, 1 = Dare): mutual swerving pays
(3,3), swerving against a darer pays the swerver 1 and the darer 4, and mutual
daring is the disaster (0,0). It has two pure Nash equilibria, one for each
player daring while the other swerves, and one symmetric mixed equilibrium.

funcs.name: MatchingPennies
funcs.doc:
MatchingPennies returns the classic zero-sum game of matching pennies. Each
