player chooses Heads (strategy 0) or Tails (strategy 1); the row player wins
(+1) when the pennies match and loses (-1) otherwise, and the column player's
payoffs are the negation. Its unique Nash equilibrium is both players mixing
50/50, with game value 0.

funcs.name: NewGame
funcs.doc:
NewGame constructs a Game from the row and column payoff matrices, which must
be non-empty, rectangular, and of identical dimensions. The input slices are
deep-copied so later mutation of the arguments does not affect the Game.

funcs.name: NewZeroSumGame
funcs.doc:
NewZeroSumGame constructs a zero-sum Game from a single payoff matrix
interpreted as the row player's payoff; the column player's payoff is its
negation. The matrix must be non-empty and rectangular.

funcs.name: StagHunt
funcs.doc:
StagHunt returns the Stag Hunt coordination game with the common payoffs
(Stag,Stag)=(4,4), (Stag,Hare)=(1,3), (Hare,Stag)=(3,1), (Hare,Hare)=(3,3),
where strategy 0 is Stag and 1 is Hare. It has two pure Nash equilibria, the
payoff-dominant (Stag,Stag) and the risk-dominant (Hare,Hare).

methods.name: BestResponseColValue
methods.doc:
BestResponseColValue returns the maximum expected payoff the column player can
obtain against the row player's mixed strategy p.

methods.name: BestResponseRowValue
methods.doc:
BestResponseRowValue returns the maximum expected payoff the row player can
obtain against the column player's mixed strategy q.

methods.name: ColBestResponses
methods.signature: func (g Game) ColBestResponses(p MixedStrategy, tol float64) []int
methods.doc:
ColBestResponses returns the sorted column-player pure strategies that
maximize the column player's expected payoff against the row player's mixed
strategy p, treating strategies within tol of the maximum as tied best
responses.

methods.name: ColPayoff
methods.signature: func (g Game) ColPayoff(i, j int) float64
methods.doc:
ColPayoff returns the column player's payoff when the row player plays i and
the column player plays j.

methods.name: ColStrictlyDominates
methods.signature: func (g Game) ColStrictlyDominates(j, l int) bool
methods.doc:
ColStrictlyDominates reports whether column strategy j strictly dominates
column strategy l for the column player: Col[i][j] > Col[i][l] for every row i.

methods.name: ColWeaklyDominates
methods.signature: func (g Game) ColWeaklyDominates(j, l int) bool
methods.doc:
ColWeaklyDominates reports whether column strategy j weakly dominates column
strategy l: Col[i][j] >= Col[i][l] for every row i, with strict inequality in
at least one row.

methods.name: DominantColStrategy
methods.doc:
DominantColStrategy returns a column strategy that strictly dominates every
other column strategy, together with true, if one exists; otherwise it
returns -1 and false.

methods.name: DominantRowStrategy
methods.doc:
DominantRowStrategy returns a row strategy that strictly dominates every other
row strategy, together with true, if one exists; otherwise it returns -1 and
false.

methods.name: ExpectedColPayoff
methods.signature: func (g Game) ExpectedColPayoff(p, q MixedStrategy) float64
methods.doc:
ExpectedColPayoff returns the column player's expected payoff when the row
player plays mixed strategy p and the column player plays mixed strategy q.

methods.name: ExpectedRowPayoff
methods.signature: func (g Game) ExpectedRowPayoff(p, q MixedStrategy) float64
methods.doc:
ExpectedRowPayoff returns the row player's expected payoff when the row player
plays mixed strategy p and the column player plays mixed strategy q.

methods.name: HasPureNash
methods.doc:
HasPureNash reports whether the game has at least one pure-strategy Nash
equilibrium, testing deviations within tolerance tol.

methods.name: IsConstantSum
methods.doc:
IsConstantSum reports whether the game is constant-sum, i.e. Row[i][j] +
Col[i][j] equals the same constant in every cell within tolerance tol. The
common sum and true are returned when the game is constant-sum; otherwise the
sum of the first cell and false are returned.

methods.name: IsParetoOptimal
methods.signature: func (g Game) IsParetoOptimal(i, j int) bool
methods.doc:
IsParetoOptimal reports whether the pure-strategy profile (i, j) is
Pareto-optimal among all pure profiles: no other profile yields both players
at least as much payoff and at least one player strictly more.

methods.name: IsPureNash
methods.signature: func (g Game) IsPureNash(i, j int, tol float64) bool
methods.doc:
IsPureNash reports whether the pure-strategy profile (i, j) is a Nash
equilibrium: neither player can strictly improve their payoff by a unilateral
deviation to another pure strategy, judged within tolerance tol.

methods.name: IsSymmetric
methods.doc:
IsSymmetric reports whether the game is symmetric: it must be square and
satisfy Col[i][j] == Row[j][i] for all i, j within tolerance tol, so that the
two players face identical strategic situations.

methods.name: IsZeroSum
methods.doc:
IsZeroSum reports whether the game is zero-sum, i.e. Row[i][j]+Col[i][j] is
zero for every cell within the tolerance tol.

methods.name: IteratedStrictDominance
methods.doc:
IteratedStrictDominance performs iterated elimination of strictly dominated
pure strategies (by other pure strategies) until no further elimination is
possible, and returns the sorted indices of the surviving row and column
strategies. Because only strictly dominated strategies are removed, the
result is independent of the order of elimination.

methods.name: IteratedWeakDominance
methods.doc:
IteratedWeakDominance performs iterated elimination of weakly dominated pure
strategies (by other pure strategies) until no further elimination is
possible, and returns the sorted indices of the surviving row and column
strategies. Note that, unlike strict dominance, the surviving set for weak
dominance can depend on the order of elimination; this method uses a fixed,
deterministic lowest-index-first order.

methods.name: MixedNashEquilibria
methods.doc:
MixedNashEquilibria returns mixed-strategy Nash equilibria of the two-player
game found by support enumeration over all pairs of equal-sized supports.
Pure equilibria appear as singleton-support equilibria, so the result is a
superset of PureNashEquilibria expressed as mixed strategies. Duplicate
equilibria (which can arise in degenerate games) are removed within tolerance
tol. For non-degenerate games this returns the complete finite set of
equilibria; degenerate games may have a continuum, of which only the
enumerated vertices are reported.

methods.name: MixedNashEquilibriaWithSupport
methods.signature: func (g Game) MixedNashEquilibriaWithSupport(rowSupport, colSupport []int, tol float64) (MixedEquilibrium, bool)
methods.doc:
MixedNashEquilibriaWithSupport attempts to find a mixed Nash equilibrium whose
row-player support is rowSupport and whose column-player support is
colSupport. The two supports must have equal size. It solves the indifference
conditions for each player, verifies non-negativity and the best-response
(no-profitable-deviation) conditions within tolerance tol, and returns the
equilibrium together with true on success, or the zero value and false when
no equilibrium exists on the given supports.

methods.name: NumColStrategies
methods.doc:
NumColStrategies returns n, the number of pure strategies available to the
column player.

methods.name: NumRowStrategies
methods.doc:
NumRowStrategies returns m, the number of pure strategies available to the
row player.

methods.name: ParetoFrontier
methods.doc:
ParetoFrontier returns the pure-strategy profiles whose payoff pair
(RowPayoff, ColPayoff) is Pareto-optimal: no other pure profile gives both
players at least as much with at least one strictly better. The profiles are
returned in lexicographic (row, column) order.

methods.name: PureNashEquilibria
methods.doc:
PureNashEquilibria returns all pure-strategy Nash equilibria of the game as
PureProfile values, in lexicographic (row, column) order. Strategies within
tolerance tol are treated as tied when testing for profitable deviations.

methods.name: RowBestResponses
methods.signature: func (g Game) RowBestResponses(q MixedStrategy, tol float64) []int
methods.doc:
RowBestResponses returns the sorted row-player pure strategies that maximize
the row player's expected payoff against the column player's mixed strategy q,
treating strategies within tol of the maximum as tied best responses.

methods.name: RowPayoff
methods.signature: func (g Game) RowPayoff(i, j int) float64
methods.doc:
RowPayoff returns the row player's payoff when the row player plays i and the
column player plays j.

methods.name: RowStrictlyDominates
methods.signature: func (g Game) RowStrictlyDominates(i, k int) bool
methods.doc:
RowStrictlyDominates reports whether row strategy i strictly dominates row
strategy k for the row player: Row[i][j] > Row[k][j] for every column j.

methods.name: RowWeaklyDominates
methods.signature: func (g Game) RowWeaklyDominates(i, k int) bool
methods.doc:
RowWeaklyDominates reports whether row strategy i weakly dominates row
strategy k: Row[i][j] >= Row[k][j] for every column j, with strict inequality
in at least one column.

methods.name: SocialOptimum
methods.doc:
SocialOptimum returns the pure-strategy profile maximizing the sum of the two
players' payoffs (the utilitarian social welfare) and that maximal sum. Ties
are broken by lexicographic (row, column) order.

methods.name: SolveZeroSum
methods.doc:
SolveZeroSum solves the receiver as a zero-sum game, returning ErrNotZeroSum
if Row[i][j]+Col[i][j] is not zero (within 1e-9) in every cell. The row
player's payoff matrix is used as the matrix game.

methods.name: StrictlyDominatedColStrategies
methods.doc:
StrictlyDominatedColStrategies returns the sorted indices of column strategies
that are strictly dominated by some other single (pure) column strategy.

methods.name: StrictlyDominatedRowStrategies
methods.doc:
StrictlyDominatedRowStrategies returns the sorted indices of row strategies
that are strictly dominated by some other single (pure) row strategy.

methods.name: Transpose
methods.doc:
Transpose returns the game viewed from the column player's seat: the players
swap roles, so the returned game has n rows and m columns with row payoffs
Col[j][i] and column payoffs Row[j][i].

methods.name: Validate
methods.doc:
Validate reports whether the receiver's payoff matrices are well-formed
(non-empty, rectangular and of equal shape), returning ErrShape otherwise.

types.name: IPDStrategy
types.signature: type IPDStrategy func(myHistory, oppHistory []int) int
types.doc:
IPDStrategy is a deterministic strategy for the iterated Prisoner's dilemma.
Given the caller's own past moves (myHistory) and the opponent's past moves
(oppHistory), both equal-length slices of Cooperate/Defect ordered oldest to
newest, it returns the move to play this round.

types.name: MixedEquilibrium
types.doc:
MixedEquilibrium is a mixed-strategy Nash equilibrium: Row and Col are the two
players' equilibrium mixed strategies, and RowValue and ColValue are the
corresponding equilibrium expected payoffs.

types.name: MixedStrategy
types.signature: type MixedStrategy []float64
types.doc:
MixedStrategy is a probability distribution over a player's pure strategies:
entry i is the probability of playing pure strategy i. A valid mixed strategy
has non-negative entries summing to one.

funcs.name: NewMixedStrategy
funcs.doc:
NewMixedStrategy validates probs as a probability distribution (non-negative
entries summing to one within a small tolerance) and returns a copy of it as
a MixedStrategy.

funcs.name: PureStrategy
funcs.doc:
PureStrategy returns the degenerate mixed strategy over n pure strategies
that plays pure strategy i with probability one. It panics if i is out of
range.

funcs.name: UniformStrategy
funcs.doc:
UniformStrategy returns the uniform mixed strategy over n pure strategies,
assigning probability 1/n to each. It panics if n is not positive.

methods.name: Entropy
methods.doc:
Entropy returns the Shannon entropy of the distribution in bits (base-2 log).
Zero-probability outcomes contribute nothing.

methods.name: IsValid
methods.doc:
IsValid reports whether the receiver is a valid probability distribution:
every entry is at least -tol and the entries sum to one within tol.

methods.name: Support
methods.doc:
Support returns the sorted indices of pure strategies played with probability
greater than tol.

types.name: PrisonersDilemma
types.doc:
PrisonersDilemma holds the four payoffs of a symmetric Prisoner's dilemma from
one player's perspective: T is the temptation payoff (defect against a
cooperator), R the reward for mutual cooperation, P the punishment for mutual
defection, and S the sucker's payoff (cooperate against a defector). A proper
dilemma requires T > R > P > S.

funcs.name: NewPrisonersDilemma
funcs.signature: func NewPrisonersDilemma(t, r, p, s float64) (PrisonersDilemma, error)
funcs.doc:
NewPrisonersDilemma constructs a PrisonersDilemma, returning ErrPayoffOrder
unless the payoffs satisfy the defining ordering t > r > p > s.

funcs.name: StandardPrisonersDilemma
funcs.doc:
StandardPrisonersDilemma returns the canonical Prisoner's dilemma with the
textbook payoffs T=5, R=3, P=1, S=0.

methods.name: Game
methods.doc:
Game returns the two-player normal-form game for the Prisoner's dilemma, with
row and column strategy 0 = Cooperate and 1 = Defect. The row player's payoff
matrix is [[R, S], [T, P]] and the column player's is its transpose.

methods.name: IsIterationStable
methods.doc:
IsIterationStable reports whether the payoffs satisfy 2R > T + S, the extra
condition ensuring that in the iterated game sustained mutual cooperation
out-scores alternating exploitation.

methods.name: IsValid
methods.doc:
IsValid reports whether the payoffs satisfy the Prisoner's-dilemma ordering
T > R > P > S.

types.name: PureProfile
types.doc:
PureProfile is a pair of pure strategies, one for each player: Row is the row
player's strategy index and Col the column player's.

types.name: ZeroSumSolution
types.doc:
ZeroSumSolution is the solution of a two-player zero-sum game: Value is the
value of the game to the row (maximizing) player, RowStrategy is an optimal
maximin mixed strategy for the row player, and ColStrategy is an optimal
minimax mixed strategy for the column player.

funcs.name: SolveZeroSum
funcs.doc:
SolveZeroSum solves the matrix game whose payoff matrix (to the maximizing
row player) is payoff, returning the value of the game and a pair of optimal
minimax mixed strategies. It uses the linear-programming formulation of a
matrix game, solved by a self-contained simplex method, and is exact for
rational data up to floating-point round-off. The matrix must be non-empty
and rectangular.

funcs.name: AlwaysCooperate
funcs.doc: AlwaysCooperate is the IPD strategy that cooperates every round.

funcs.name: AlwaysDefect
funcs.doc: AlwaysDefect is the IPD strategy that defects every round.

funcs.name: ExpectedPayoff
funcs.signature: func ExpectedPayoff(a [][]float64, p, q MixedStrategy) float64
funcs.doc:
ExpectedPayoff returns the bilinear form p^T A q, the expected value of the
payoff matrix A when the row is drawn from distribution p and the column from
distribution q. It panics if the lengths of p, q do not match A's shape.

funcs.name: GameValue
funcs.doc:
GameValue returns the value (to the maximizing row player) of the matrix game
with the given row-player payoff matrix.

funcs.name: GrimTrigger
funcs.doc:
GrimTrigger is the IPD strategy that cooperates until the opponent defects
even once, after which it defects forever.

funcs.name: Maximin
funcs.doc:
Maximin returns the row player's pure maximin strategy and its security
value: the row that maximizes the row's worst-case (minimum over columns)
payoff, and that maximized minimum.

funcs.name: Minimax
funcs.doc:
Minimax returns the column player's pure minimax strategy and its value: the
column that minimizes the column's worst-case (maximum over rows) payoff to
the row player, and that minimized maximum.

funcs.name: ParetoDominates
funcs.doc:
ParetoDominates reports whether payoff vector a Pareto-dominates payoff
vector b: a is at least as large as b in every coordinate and strictly larger
in at least one. Both slices must have the same length. Larger is better in
every objective.

funcs.name: ParetoOptimalIndices
funcs.doc:
ParetoOptimalIndices returns the sorted indices of the Pareto-optimal points
among the given points (each a vector of objectives, all maximized): a point
is Pareto-optimal when no other point Pareto-dominates it. All points must
share the same dimension.

funcs.name: PlayIteratedPD
funcs.signature: func PlayIteratedPD(pd PrisonersDilemma, a, b IPDStrategy, rounds int) (scoreA, scoreB float64, historyA, historyB []int)
funcs.doc:
PlayIteratedPD plays the iterated Prisoner's dilemma between strategies a and
b for the given number of rounds and returns each player's total accumulated
payoff along with the full move histories (oldest to newest). Each round both
strategies choose simultaneously based on the history so far; payoffs use the
PrisonersDilemma's T, R, P, S. rounds must be non-negative.

funcs.name: SaddlePoint
funcs.doc:
SaddlePoint reports whether the matrix game has a pure-strategy saddle point,
i.e. a cell that is simultaneously the minimum of its row and the maximum of
its column, equivalently where the pure maximin equals the pure minimax. When
one exists it returns its row and column indices and true; otherwise it
returns -1, -1, false. The saddle-point value equals payoff[i][j].

funcs.name: TitForTat
funcs.doc:
TitForTat is the IPD strategy that cooperates on the first round and
thereafter copies the opponent's most recent move.

funcs.name: WinStayLoseShift
funcs.doc:
WinStayLoseShift is the IPD strategy (also called Pavlov) that cooperates on
the first round, then repeats its previous move after a "good" outcome (mutual
cooperation or successfully defecting against a cooperator) and switches its
move after a "bad" outcome.

packages.name: geodesy
packages.synopsis: Package geodesy implements geodetic computations and map projections on the WGS-84 reference ellipsoid (and other configurable ellipsoids) using only the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package geodesy implements geodetic computations and map projections on
the WGS-84 reference ellipsoid (and other configurable ellipsoids) using
only the Go standard library.

The package covers the classical geodesy toolbox:

  - Great-circle (spherical) calculations: Haversine, spherical law of
    cosines and equirectangular distances, initial and final bearings,
    midpoints, intermediate/interpolated points, destination points,
    cross-track and along-track distances, path intersection, maximum
    latitude of a great circle, and spherical polygon/triangle area.
  - Rhumb-line (loxodrome) distance, bearing, midpoint and destination.
  - Ellipsoidal geodesics via Vincenty's direct and inverse formulae,
    including geodesic interpolation and waypoint generation.
  - Map projections: spherical and Web (EPSG:3857) Mercator, the
    Krüger-series transverse Mercator, and the Universal Transverse
    Mercator (UTM) grid, with forward and inverse transforms.
  - Military Grid Reference System (MGRS) encoding and decoding.
  - Earth-Centred Earth-Fixed (ECEF) <-> geodetic conversions and local
    East-North-Up (ENU) tangent-plane transforms.
  - Angle helpers: degree/radian conversion, normalisation, and
    degrees-minutes-seconds (DMS) parsing and formatting.

All computation is performed in float64. Angles supplied to and returned
from the public API are in degrees unless a function name explicitly says
otherwise (Rad suffix), and distances are in metres. Functions are
deterministic: identical inputs yield identical outputs.

consts.doc: Length unit conversion factors (metres per unit).
Mean Earth radii and reference constants (metres).
UTM grid constants.

consts.signature: const WebMercatorMaxLatitude = 85.05112877980659
consts.doc:
WebMercatorMaxLatitude is the latitude limit (~85.0511°) of the square Web
Mercator (EPSG:3857) projection.

vars.doc: Common sentinel errors returned by the package.
Reference ellipsoids in common use.

types.name: AER
types.doc:
AER is a local look-angle: azimuth and elevation in degrees and slant range
in metres, relative to an observer at a geodetic reference point.

funcs.name: ENUToAER
funcs.doc:
ENUToAER converts a local East-North-Up offset to azimuth/elevation/range
look-angles.

funcs.name: LookAngles
funcs.signature: func LookAngles(obsLat, obsLon, obsHeight, tgtLat, tgtLon, tgtHeight float64, e Ellipsoid) AER
funcs.doc:
LookAngles returns the azimuth/elevation/range from an observer at the given
geodetic reference point (degrees, metres height) to a target geodetic point,
on the given ellipsoid.

types.name: BoundingBox
types.doc:
BoundingBox is an axis-aligned latitude/longitude rectangle, defined by its
south-west (minimum) and north-east (maximum) corners. It does not handle
boxes that span the ±180° antimeridian.

funcs.name: BoundingBoxAround
funcs.signature: func BoundingBoxAround(center LatLon, radius float64) BoundingBox
funcs.doc:
BoundingBoxAround returns a bounding box centred on the given point that
encloses a circle of the given radius (metres), computed on the WGS-84 mean
sphere. The box is a latitude/longitude rectangle tangent to that circle.

funcs.name: BoundingBoxOfPoints
funcs.doc:
BoundingBoxOfPoints returns the smallest bounding box containing all the given
points. It returns the zero box for an empty slice.

funcs.name: NewBoundingBox
funcs.doc:
NewBoundingBox returns the bounding box with the given south-west and
north-east corners, normalising the corner ordering.

methods.name: Center
methods.doc: Center returns the centre point of the box.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc: Contains reports whether the point lies within the box (inclusive of edges).

methods.name: Corners
methods.doc:
Corners returns the four corners of the box in the order south-west,
south-east, north-east, north-west.

methods.name: Expand
methods.doc:
Expand returns a copy of the box grown outward by the given amount of metres
on all sides (WGS-84 mean sphere).

methods.name: HeightMeters
methods.doc:
HeightMeters returns the north-south extent of the box in metres on the
WGS-84 mean sphere.

methods.name: Intersects
methods.doc: Intersects reports whether two boxes overlap (sharing an edge counts).

methods.name: NorthEast
methods.doc: NorthEast returns the north-east corner of the box.

methods.name: SouthWest
methods.doc: SouthWest returns the south-west corner of the box.

methods.name: Union
methods.doc: Union returns the smallest box containing both b and o.

methods.name: WidthMeters
methods.doc:
WidthMeters returns the east-west extent of the box in metres at its centre
latitude on the WGS-84 mean sphere.

types.name: DirectResult
types.doc:
DirectResult holds the solution of the geodesic direct problem: the point
reached from a start point along a geodesic, and the azimuth there.

funcs.name: VincentyDirect
funcs.signature: func VincentyDirect(start LatLon, bearingDeg, distance float64, e Ellipsoid) DirectResult
funcs.doc:
VincentyDirect solves the direct geodesic problem on the given ellipsoid:
from a start point, travel distance metres along the initial azimuth
(degrees) and return the destination and the azimuth there.

types.name: ECEF
types.doc:
ECEF is a point in the Earth-Centred, Earth-Fixed Cartesian frame, with the
origin at the centre of mass, the Z axis toward the reference pole, the X
axis toward the intersection of the equator and the prime meridian, and the
Y axis completing a right-handed system. Units are metres.

funcs.name: ENUToECEF
funcs.signature: func ENUToECEF(l ENU, refLat, refLon, refHeight float64, e Ellipsoid) ECEF
funcs.doc:
ENUToECEF converts a local East-North-Up offset back to an ECEF point,
relative to a geodetic reference origin on the given ellipsoid.

funcs.name: GeodeticToECEF
funcs.signature: func GeodeticToECEF(lat, lon, height float64, e Ellipsoid) ECEF
funcs.doc:
GeodeticToECEF converts geodetic latitude/longitude (degrees) and ellipsoidal
height (metres) to ECEF coordinates on the given ellipsoid.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the vector sum of two ECEF points.

methods.name: Cross
methods.doc: Cross returns the cross product of two ECEF vectors.

methods.name: Distance
methods.doc: Distance returns the straight-line (chord) distance between two ECEF points.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the dot product of two ECEF vectors.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean length of the ECEF vector.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns the unit vector in the direction of c (the zero vector is
returned unchanged).

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the ECEF vector scaled by s.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the vector difference c - o.

types.name: ENU
types.doc:
ENU is a local tangent-plane coordinate: East, North and Up offsets in metres
relative to a reference geodetic origin.

funcs.name: AERToENU
funcs.doc:
AERToENU converts azimuth/elevation/range look-angles to a local
East-North-Up offset.

funcs.name: ECEFToENU
funcs.signature: func ECEFToENU(c ECEF, refLat, refLon, refHeight float64, e Ellipsoid) ENU
funcs.doc:
ECEFToENU converts an ECEF point to local East-North-Up coordinates relative
to a geodetic reference origin (degrees, metres) on the given ellipsoid.

funcs.name: GeodeticToENU
funcs.signature: func GeodeticToENU(lat, lon, height, refLat, refLon, refHeight float64, e Ellipsoid) ENU
funcs.doc:
GeodeticToENU converts a geodetic point (degrees, metres) to local
East-North-Up coordinates relative to a geodetic reference origin.

methods.name: HorizontalRange
methods.doc:
HorizontalRange returns the horizontal (ground) distance in metres of the ENU
offset, ignoring the vertical component.

methods.name: SlantRange
methods.doc: SlantRange returns the full 3-D distance in metres of the ENU offset.

types.name: Ellipsoid
types.doc:
Ellipsoid is a biaxial reference ellipsoid defined by its semi-major axis A
(metres) and flattening F.

funcs.name: NewEllipsoid
funcs.doc:
NewEllipsoid builds an ellipsoid from a semi-major axis and inverse
flattening 1/f. Passing invFlattening <= 0 yields a sphere.

methods.name: AuthalicRadius
methods.doc: AuthalicRadius returns the radius of a sphere with the same surface area.

methods.name: FirstEccentricity
methods.doc: FirstEccentricity returns the first eccentricity e.

methods.name: FirstEccentricitySquared
methods.doc: FirstEccentricitySquared returns e² = f(2-f).

methods.name: Flattening
methods.doc: Flattening returns the flattening f = (a-b)/a.

methods.name: GaussianRadius
methods.doc:
GaussianRadius returns the Gaussian (geometric mean) radius of curvature
sqrt(M·N) at the given geodetic latitude (degrees).

methods.name: InverseFlattening
methods.doc: InverseFlattening returns 1/f, or +Inf for a sphere.

methods.name: IsSphere
methods.doc: IsSphere reports whether the ellipsoid is a perfect sphere (f == 0).

methods.name: MeanRadius
methods.doc: MeanRadius returns the arithmetic mean radius R1 = (2a+b)/3.

methods.name: MeridianRadius
methods.doc:
MeridianRadius returns the meridional radius of curvature M at the given
geodetic latitude (degrees).

methods.name: PrimeVerticalRadius
methods.doc:
PrimeVerticalRadius returns the radius of curvature N in the prime vertical
at the given geodetic latitude (degrees).

methods.name: SecondEccentricity
methods.doc: SecondEccentricity returns the second eccentricity e'.

methods.name: SecondEccentricitySquared
methods.doc: SecondEccentricitySquared returns e'² = e²/(1-e²).

methods.name: SemiMajorAxis
methods.doc: SemiMajorAxis returns the equatorial radius a in metres.

methods.name: SemiMinorAxis
methods.doc: SemiMinorAxis returns the polar radius b = a(1-f) in metres.

methods.name: ThirdFlattening
methods.doc: ThirdFlattening returns n = f/(2-f), used by the Krüger series.

methods.name: VolumetricRadius
methods.doc:
VolumetricRadius returns the radius of a sphere with the same volume,
(a²b)^(1/3).

types.name: InverseResult
types.doc:
InverseResult holds the solution of the geodesic inverse problem: the
geodesic distance between two points and the forward azimuths at each end.

funcs.name: VincentyInverse
funcs.signature: func VincentyInverse(p1, p2 LatLon, e Ellipsoid) (InverseResult, error)
funcs.doc:
VincentyInverse solves the inverse geodesic problem on the given ellipsoid
using Vincenty's formula, returning the distance and initial/final bearings.
It returns ErrNoConvergence for near-antipodal points where the algorithm
does not converge.

types.name: LatLon
types.doc: LatLon is a geodetic position given by latitude and longitude in degrees.

funcs.name: Destination
funcs.signature: func Destination(start LatLon, bearingDeg, distance float64) LatLon
funcs.doc:
Destination returns the point reached from start along a great circle on the
given initial bearing (degrees) for distance metres, on the WGS-84 mean
sphere.

funcs.name: DestinationR
funcs.signature: func DestinationR(start LatLon, bearingDeg, distance, radius float64) LatLon
funcs.doc:
DestinationR returns the point reached by travelling distance metres along a
great circle from the start point on the given initial bearing (degrees), on
a sphere of the given radius.

funcs.name: GeodesicInterpolate
funcs.signature: func GeodesicInterpolate(p1, p2 LatLon, fraction float64, e Ellipsoid) (LatLon, error)
funcs.doc:
GeodesicInterpolate returns the point at the given fraction of the geodesic
distance from p1 (fraction 0) to p2 (fraction 1) on the given ellipsoid.

funcs.name: GeodesicWaypoints
funcs.signature: func GeodesicWaypoints(p1, p2 LatLon, n int, e Ellipsoid) ([]LatLon, error)
funcs.doc:
GeodesicWaypoints returns n+1 points evenly spaced by geodesic distance from
p1 to p2 inclusive on the given ellipsoid. n must be >= 1.

funcs.name: IntermediatePoint
funcs.signature: func IntermediatePoint(p1, p2 LatLon, fraction float64) LatLon
funcs.doc:
IntermediatePoint returns the point at the given fraction of the great-circle
distance from p1 (fraction 0) to p2 (fraction 1).

funcs.name: InterpolatePoints
funcs.signature: func InterpolatePoints(p1, p2 LatLon, n int) []LatLon
funcs.doc:
InterpolatePoints returns n+1 points evenly spaced (by fraction of the total
great-circle distance) from p1 to p2 inclusive. n must be >= 1.

funcs.name: Intersection
funcs.signature: func Intersection(p1 LatLon, bearing1 float64, p2 LatLon, bearing2 float64) (LatLon, error)
funcs.doc:
Intersection returns the point of intersection of two great-circle paths,
each defined by a point and an initial bearing (degrees). It returns
ErrNoConvergence if the paths are (anti)parallel and do not intersect
uniquely.

funcs.name: MGRSToLatLon
funcs.doc:
MGRSToLatLon decodes an MGRS reference string into a WGS-84 geodetic position
(the south-west corner of the referenced square when the numeric location is
omitted, or of the referenced cell otherwise).

funcs.name: Midpoint
funcs.doc: Midpoint returns the half-way point along the great circle between p1 and p2.

funcs.name: NewLatLon
funcs.doc: NewLatLon constructs a LatLon from latitude and longitude in degrees.

funcs.name: RhumbDestination
funcs.signature: func RhumbDestination(start LatLon, bearingDeg, distance float64) LatLon
funcs.doc: RhumbDestination is RhumbDestinationR on the WGS-84 mean sphere.

funcs.name: RhumbDestinationR
funcs.signature: func RhumbDestinationR(start LatLon, bearingDeg, distance, radius float64) LatLon
funcs.doc:
RhumbDestinationR returns the point reached by travelling distance metres
along a rhumb line from start on the given constant bearing (degrees), on a
sphere of the given radius.

funcs.name: RhumbMidpoint
funcs.doc:
RhumbMidpoint returns the point half-way along the rhumb line between p1 and
p2 (loxodromic midpoint).

funcs.name: UTMToLatLon
funcs.doc: UTMToLatLon converts a UTM grid coordinate to a WGS-84 geodetic position.

funcs.name: UTMToLatLonEllipsoid
funcs.signature: func UTMToLatLonEllipsoid(u UTMCoord, e Ellipsoid) (LatLon, error)
funcs.doc:
UTMToLatLonEllipsoid converts a UTM coordinate to a geodetic position on the
given ellipsoid.

funcs.name: VincentyDestination
funcs.signature: func VincentyDestination(start LatLon, bearingDeg, distance float64) LatLon
funcs.doc:
VincentyDestination returns the point reached from start along a WGS-84
geodesic on the given initial bearing (degrees) for distance metres.

methods.name: Antipode
methods.doc: Antipode returns the point diametrically opposite on the sphere.

methods.name: CrossTrackDistanceTo
methods.signature: func (p LatLon) CrossTrackDistanceTo(start, end LatLon) float64
methods.doc:
CrossTrackDistanceTo returns the signed distance in metres of p from the
great circle defined by start and end (WGS-84 mean sphere).

methods.name: DestinationPoint
methods.signature: func (p LatLon) DestinationPoint(bearing, distance float64) LatLon
methods.doc:
DestinationPoint returns the point reached from p along a great circle on the
given bearing (degrees) for distance metres on the WGS-84 mean sphere.

methods.name: DistanceTo
methods.doc:
DistanceTo returns the great-circle distance in metres from p to q on the
WGS-84 mean sphere (Haversine).

methods.name: Equal
methods.signature: func (p LatLon) Equal(q LatLon, tol float64) bool
methods.doc: Equal reports whether two positions are within tol degrees on both axes.

methods.name: FinalBearingTo
methods.doc: FinalBearingTo returns the final great-circle bearing (degrees) from p to q.

methods.name: GeodesicDistanceTo
methods.doc:
GeodesicDistanceTo returns the geodesic distance in metres from p to q on the
WGS-84 ellipsoid (Vincenty inverse).

methods.name: InitialBearingTo
methods.doc:
InitialBearingTo returns the initial great-circle bearing (degrees) from p to
q.

methods.name: LatRad
methods.doc: LatRad returns the latitude in radians.

methods.name: LonRad
methods.doc: LonRad returns the longitude in radians.

methods.name: MidpointTo
methods.doc: MidpointTo returns the great-circle midpoint between p and q.

methods.name: Normalized
methods.doc:
Normalized returns the position with latitude clamped to [-90,90] and
longitude wrapped to (-180,180].

methods.name: RhumbBearingTo
methods.doc: RhumbBearingTo returns the constant rhumb-line bearing (degrees) from p to q.

methods.name: RhumbDistanceTo
methods.doc:
RhumbDistanceTo returns the rhumb-line distance in metres from p to q on the
WGS-84 mean sphere.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the position in signed decimal degrees.

methods.name: ToECEF
methods.doc:
ToECEF converts p at the given ellipsoidal height (metres) to ECEF
coordinates on the WGS-84 ellipsoid.

methods.name: ToMGRS
methods.doc: ToMGRS encodes p as an MGRS reference string with the given precision.

methods.name: ToUTM
methods.doc: ToUTM converts p to a UTM grid coordinate on WGS-84.

methods.name: Valid
methods.doc:
Valid reports whether the latitude is in [-90,90] and longitude in
[-180,180].

types.name: UTMCoord
types.doc: UTMCoord is a Universal Transverse Mercator grid coordinate.

funcs.name: LatLonToUTM
funcs.doc:
LatLonToUTM converts a WGS-84 geodetic position to a UTM grid coordinate. It
returns ErrPolarRegion for latitudes outside the UTM band [-80, 84).

funcs.name: LatLonToUTMEllipsoid
funcs.signature: func LatLonToUTMEllipsoid(p LatLon, e Ellipsoid) (UTMCoord, error)
funcs.doc:
LatLonToUTMEllipsoid converts a geodetic position to a UTM coordinate on the
given ellipsoid.

funcs.name: ParseMGRS
funcs.doc:
ParseMGRS decodes an MGRS reference string into a UTM coordinate. Whitespace
is ignored, and the reference may omit the numeric location (grid square
only), in which case the coordinate refers to the south-west corner of the
100 km square.

funcs.name: ParseUTM
funcs.doc:
ParseUTM parses a UTM string in the form produced by UTMCoord.String, for
example "31 N 448252 5411932" or "31N 448252 5411932".

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String renders a UTM coordinate as "zone hemisphere easting northing", for
example "31 N 448252 5411932".

funcs.name: AlongTrackDistance
funcs.signature: func AlongTrackDistance(p, start, end LatLon) float64
funcs.doc: AlongTrackDistance is AlongTrackDistanceR on the WGS-84 mean sphere.

funcs.name: AlongTrackDistanceR
funcs.signature: func AlongTrackDistanceR(p, start, end LatLon, radius float64) float64
funcs.doc:
AlongTrackDistanceR returns the distance in metres from the start point to the
closest point on the great circle start->end to the foot of the
perpendicular from p, on a sphere of the given radius.

funcs.name: AngularDistance
funcs.doc:
AngularDistance returns the great-circle angular separation between two
points, in radians, using the Haversine formula.

funcs.name: AuthalicLatitude
funcs.signature: func AuthalicLatitude(latDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
AuthalicLatitude returns the authalic (equal-area) latitude ξ (degrees) at the
given geodetic latitude on the ellipsoid.

funcs.name: BackAzimuth
funcs.doc:
BackAzimuth returns the reverse bearing (degrees, [0,360)) of a forward
bearing.

funcs.name: BearingToCompass16
funcs.doc:
BearingToCompass16 returns the 16-point compass abbreviation (N, NNE, NE,
...) nearest to the given bearing (degrees).

funcs.name: BearingToCompass32
funcs.doc:
BearingToCompass32 returns the 32-point compass abbreviation nearest to the
given bearing (degrees).

funcs.name: BearingToCompass8
funcs.doc:
BearingToCompass8 returns the 8-point compass abbreviation (N, NE, E, ...)
nearest to the given bearing (degrees).

funcs.name: ChordDistance
funcs.signature: func ChordDistance(p1, p2 LatLon, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
ChordDistance returns the straight-line distance in metres through the Earth
between two geodetic surface points on the given ellipsoid (height 0).

funcs.name: CompassToBearing
funcs.doc:
CompassToBearing returns the central bearing (degrees) of the named compass
point (case-insensitive, for example "NE" or "SSW"). It returns
ErrInvalidCoordinate if the name is not a recognised 32-point abbreviation.

funcs.name: ConformalLatitude
funcs.signature: func ConformalLatitude(latDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
ConformalLatitude returns the conformal latitude χ (degrees) at the given
geodetic latitude on the ellipsoid.

funcs.name: CrossTrackDistance
funcs.signature: func CrossTrackDistance(p, start, end LatLon) float64
funcs.doc: CrossTrackDistance is CrossTrackDistanceR on the WGS-84 mean sphere.

funcs.name: CrossTrackDistanceR
funcs.signature: func CrossTrackDistanceR(p, start, end LatLon, radius float64) float64
funcs.doc:
CrossTrackDistanceR returns the signed distance in metres of point p from the
great circle defined by the start and end points, on a sphere of the given
radius. The result is positive if p lies to the left of the start->end path
and negative if to the right.

funcs.name: DMSToDegrees
funcs.signature: func DMSToDegrees(d, m, s float64) float64
funcs.doc:
DMSToDegrees converts a degrees/minutes/seconds triple to decimal degrees.
The sign of the result follows the sign convention of deg (all components
are treated with the magnitude of deg's sign); minutes and seconds must be
non-negative magnitudes.

funcs.name: DegToGrad
funcs.doc: DegToGrad converts degrees to gradians (400 gradians = 360 degrees).

funcs.name: DegToRad
funcs.doc: DegToRad converts an angle from degrees to radians.

funcs.name: DegreesToDMS
funcs.signature: func DegreesToDMS(deg float64) (d int, m int, s float64)
funcs.doc:
DegreesToDMS decomposes decimal degrees into a signed degree component and
non-negative minutes and seconds. The sign is carried by the degrees
return value (and, when degrees is zero, cannot be represented; callers
needing the sign of a sub-degree value should inspect the input).

funcs.name: ECEFToGeodetic
funcs.signature: func ECEFToGeodetic(c ECEF, e Ellipsoid) (lat, lon, height float64)
funcs.doc:
ECEFToGeodetic converts ECEF coordinates to geodetic latitude/longitude
(degrees) and ellipsoidal height (metres) on the given ellipsoid, using
Bowring's method refined by one iteration for sub-millimetre accuracy.

funcs.name: ENUToGeodetic
funcs.signature: func ENUToGeodetic(l ENU, refLat, refLon, refHeight float64, e Ellipsoid) (lat, lon, height float64)
funcs.doc:
ENUToGeodetic converts local East-North-Up coordinates back to a geodetic
position (degrees latitude/longitude, metres height).

funcs.name: EllipsoidalMercatorForward
funcs.signature: func EllipsoidalMercatorForward(lat, lon float64, e Ellipsoid) (x, y float64)
funcs.doc:
EllipsoidalMercatorForward projects geodetic latitude/longitude (degrees) to
the conformal ellipsoidal Mercator projection (metres) on the given
ellipsoid, centred on the prime meridian.

funcs.name: EllipsoidalMercatorInverse
funcs.signature: func EllipsoidalMercatorInverse(x, y float64, e Ellipsoid) (lat, lon float64)
funcs.doc:
EllipsoidalMercatorInverse recovers geodetic latitude/longitude (degrees)
from ellipsoidal Mercator coordinates (metres) on the given ellipsoid.

funcs.name: EquirectangularDistance
funcs.signature: func EquirectangularDistance(p1, p2 LatLon, radius float64) float64
funcs.doc:
EquirectangularDistance returns an approximate distance in metres using the
equirectangular (flat-Earth) projection. It is fast and adequate for small
separations but degrades over long distances and near the poles.

funcs.name: FeetToMeters
funcs.doc: FeetToMeters converts feet to metres.

funcs.name: FinalBearing
funcs.doc:
FinalBearing returns the bearing at which the great circle from p1 arrives at
p2, in degrees clockwise from north in [0,360).

funcs.name: FinalBearingRad
funcs.doc:
FinalBearingRad returns the bearing (radians, [0,2π)) at which the great
circle from p1 arrives at p2.

funcs.name: FormatDMS
funcs.signature: func FormatDMS(deg float64, pos, neg byte, secPrec int) string
funcs.doc:
FormatDMS renders decimal degrees as a degrees/minutes/seconds string with
the given hemisphere characters for positive and negative values (for
example 'N'/'S' or 'E'/'W'). Seconds are shown with secPrec decimals.

funcs.name: GeocentricLatitude
funcs.signature: func GeocentricLatitude(latDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
GeocentricLatitude returns the geocentric latitude (degrees) of a point on the
ellipsoid surface at the given geodetic latitude.

funcs.name: GeocentricToGeodeticLatitude
funcs.doc:
GeocentricToGeodeticLatitude returns the geodetic latitude (degrees) for a
point on the ellipsoid surface at the given geocentric latitude.

funcs.name: GradToDeg
funcs.doc: GradToDeg converts gradians to degrees.

funcs.name: GradToRad
funcs.doc: GradToRad converts gradians to radians.

funcs.name: GridConvergence
funcs.signature: func GridConvergence(lat, lon, lon0, k0, falseEasting, falseNorthing float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
GridConvergence returns the meridian convergence angle (degrees) of a
transverse Mercator projection at the given geodetic point: the angle from
grid north to true north, such that true azimuth = grid azimuth +
convergence. It is computed numerically from the projection with the given
parameters, so it is valid for any transverse Mercator grid.

funcs.name: HaversineDistance
funcs.doc:
HaversineDistance returns the great-circle distance in metres on a sphere of
the WGS-84 mean radius.

funcs.name: HaversineDistanceR
funcs.signature: func HaversineDistanceR(p1, p2 LatLon, radius float64) float64
funcs.doc:
HaversineDistanceR returns the great-circle distance between two points on a
sphere of the given radius, using the Haversine formula.

funcs.name: InitialBearing
funcs.doc:
InitialBearing returns the initial bearing from p1 to p2 along the great
circle, in degrees clockwise from north in [0,360).

funcs.name: InitialBearingRad
funcs.doc:
InitialBearingRad returns the initial (forward azimuth) bearing from p1 to p2
along the great circle, in radians measured clockwise from north in [0,2π).

funcs.name: IsometricLatitude
funcs.signature: func IsometricLatitude(latDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
IsometricLatitude returns the isometric latitude ψ (dimensionless) at the
given geodetic latitude (degrees) on the ellipsoid. It is the vertical
coordinate (scaled by the equatorial radius) of the Mercator projection.

funcs.name: KilometersToMeters
funcs.doc: KilometersToMeters converts kilometres to metres.

funcs.name: LatLonToMGRS
funcs.signature: func LatLonToMGRS(p LatLon, precision int) (string, error)
funcs.doc:
LatLonToMGRS encodes a WGS-84 geodetic position as an MGRS reference string
with the given precision (1..5). It returns ErrPolarRegion outside the MGRS
latitude band.

funcs.name: LengthOfDegreeOfLatitude
funcs.signature: func LengthOfDegreeOfLatitude(latDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
LengthOfDegreeOfLatitude returns the north-south distance (metres) spanned by
one degree of latitude at the given latitude (degrees) on the ellipsoid.

funcs.name: LengthOfDegreeOfLongitude
funcs.signature: func LengthOfDegreeOfLongitude(latDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
LengthOfDegreeOfLongitude returns the east-west distance (metres) spanned by
one degree of longitude at the given latitude (degrees) on the ellipsoid.

funcs.name: LonLatToTile
funcs.signature: func LonLatToTile(lat, lon float64, zoom int) (tx, ty int)
funcs.doc:
LonLatToTile returns the slippy-map tile column and row (x, y) containing the
given WGS-84 latitude/longitude (degrees) at the given zoom level, following
the OpenStreetMap / Google/XYZ tiling scheme.

funcs.name: MGRSLatBand
funcs.doc:
MGRSLatBand returns the MGRS/UTM latitude band letter for the given latitude
(degrees). Bands run C (-80°) through X (84°), omitting I and O; latitudes
outside [-80, 84) return a NUL byte.

funcs.name: MaxLatitude
funcs.signature: func MaxLatitude(p LatLon, bearingDeg float64) float64
funcs.doc:
MaxLatitude returns the maximum latitude reached by a great circle that
crosses the given point at the given bearing (degrees), via Clairaut's
relation. The minimum latitude on the same circle is the negation.

funcs.name: MercatorForward
funcs.signature: func MercatorForward(lat, lon, radius float64) (x, y float64)
funcs.doc:
MercatorForward projects a spherical latitude/longitude (degrees) to planar
(x, y) coordinates in metres on a sphere of the given radius, with the
projection centred on the prime meridian. x is eastings, y is northings.

funcs.name: MercatorInverse
funcs.signature: func MercatorInverse(x, y, radius float64) (lat, lon float64)
funcs.doc:
MercatorInverse recovers spherical latitude/longitude (degrees) from planar
Mercator (x, y) coordinates in metres on a sphere of the given radius.

funcs.name: MeridianArcBetween
funcs.signature: func MeridianArcBetween(lat1, lat2 float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
MeridianArcBetween returns the meridian arc distance (metres) between two
latitudes (degrees) along the same meridian on the given ellipsoid.

funcs.name: MeridianArcLength
funcs.signature: func MeridianArcLength(latDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
MeridianArcLength returns the meridian arc distance (metres) from the equator
to the given geodetic latitude (degrees) on the given ellipsoid.

funcs.name: MetersToFeet
funcs.doc: MetersToFeet converts metres to feet.

funcs.name: MetersToKilometers
funcs.doc: MetersToKilometers converts metres to kilometres.

funcs.name: MetersToMiles
funcs.doc: MetersToMiles converts metres to statute miles.

funcs.name: MetersToNauticalMiles
funcs.doc: MetersToNauticalMiles converts metres to nautical miles.

funcs.name: MilesToMeters
funcs.doc: MilesToMeters converts statute miles to metres.

funcs.name: NauticalMilesToMeters
funcs.doc: NauticalMilesToMeters converts nautical miles to metres.

funcs.name: NormalizeBearing
funcs.doc:
NormalizeBearing reduces a compass bearing to the half-open interval
[0, 360). It is an alias for NormalizeDegrees expressing intent.

funcs.name: NormalizeDegrees
funcs.doc: NormalizeDegrees reduces an angle to the half-open interval [0, 360).

funcs.name: NormalizeLatitude
funcs.doc: NormalizeLatitude clamps a latitude to the closed interval [-90, 90].

funcs.name: NormalizeLongitude
funcs.doc:
NormalizeLongitude wraps a longitude into the half-open interval
[-180, 180); the antimeridian is represented as -180.

funcs.name: NormalizeRadians
funcs.doc:
NormalizeRadians reduces an angle in radians to the half-open interval
[0, 2π).

funcs.name: ParallelArcLength
funcs.signature: func ParallelArcLength(latDeg, dLonDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
ParallelArcLength returns the distance (metres) along a parallel of latitude
spanning dLon degrees of longitude at the given latitude on the ellipsoid.

funcs.name: ParallelChordDistance
funcs.signature: func ParallelChordDistance(latDeg, dLonDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
ParallelChordDistance returns the true east-west distance (metres) along the
parallel at latitude latDeg spanning dLon degrees of longitude on the
ellipsoid. It is used internally by PointScaleFactor and is a thin alias of
ParallelArcLength preserving sign-free magnitude.

funcs.name: ParseDMS
funcs.doc:
ParseDMS parses a coordinate written in degrees/minutes/seconds into decimal
degrees. It accepts flexible input such as "51°28'40.12\"N", "51 28 40.12 N",
"-51.5", or "2°20′56″E". Hemisphere letters N/E give a positive result and
S/W a negative one; a leading sign is also honoured.

funcs.name: PointInSphericalPolygon
funcs.doc:
PointInSphericalPolygon reports whether p lies inside the spherical polygon
defined by vertices (in order, closed automatically), using a winding /
ray-crossing test in longitude at the point's latitude. It assumes the
polygon does not enclose a pole and edges are treated as rhumb-like segments
in the lat/lon plane, which is adequate for modest polygons.

funcs.name: PointScaleFactor
funcs.signature: func PointScaleFactor(lat, lon, lon0, k0, falseEasting, falseNorthing float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
PointScaleFactor returns the local scale factor of a transverse Mercator
projection at the given geodetic point (the ratio of grid distance to true
ellipsoidal distance), computed numerically for the given parameters.

funcs.name: QuarterMeridian
funcs.doc:
QuarterMeridian returns the length (metres) of a meridian quadrant (equator
to pole) on the given ellipsoid.

funcs.name: RadToDeg
funcs.doc: RadToDeg converts an angle from radians to degrees.

funcs.name: RadToGrad
funcs.doc: RadToGrad converts radians to gradians.

funcs.name: RadiusOfParallel
funcs.signature: func RadiusOfParallel(latDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
RadiusOfParallel returns the radius (metres) of the circle of latitude (small
circle) at the given latitude (degrees), N·cosφ, on the ellipsoid.

funcs.name: RectifyingLatitude
funcs.signature: func RectifyingLatitude(latDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
RectifyingLatitude returns the rectifying latitude μ (degrees) at the given
geodetic latitude, the latitude scaled so that meridian arc length is
proportional to it.

funcs.name: ReducedLatitude
funcs.signature: func ReducedLatitude(latDeg float64, e Ellipsoid) float64
funcs.doc:
ReducedLatitude returns the reduced (parametric) latitude β in degrees for a
point on the ellipsoid surface at geodetic latitude latDeg.

funcs.name: RelativeBearing
funcs.doc:
RelativeBearing returns the signed angle (degrees, (-180,180]) from bearing
from to bearing to, that is the turn required to go from one heading to the
other (positive clockwise).

funcs.name: RhumbBearing
funcs.doc:
RhumbBearing returns the constant compass bearing of the rhumb line from p1
to p2, in degrees clockwise from north in [0,360).

funcs.name: RhumbDistance
funcs.doc: RhumbDistance is RhumbDistanceR on the WGS-84 mean sphere.

funcs.name: RhumbDistanceR
funcs.signature: func RhumbDistanceR(p1, p2 LatLon, radius float64) float64
funcs.doc:
RhumbDistanceR returns the rhumb-line (loxodrome) distance in metres between
two points on a sphere of the given radius. A rhumb line crosses every
meridian at the same angle and appears as a straight line on a Mercator map.

funcs.name: SphericalExcess
funcs.signature: func SphericalExcess(a, b, c float64) float64
funcs.doc:
SphericalExcess returns the spherical excess (E = A + B + C - π) in radians of
a spherical triangle with the three given interior angles in radians. The
triangle area on a sphere of radius R is E·R².

funcs.name: SphericalLawOfCosinesDistance
funcs.signature: func SphericalLawOfCosinesDistance(p1, p2 LatLon, radius float64) float64
funcs.doc:
SphericalLawOfCosinesDistance returns the great-circle distance in metres on
a sphere of the given radius, using the spherical law of cosines. It is
simpler than the Haversine formula but less accurate for very short
distances.

funcs.name: SphericalPolygonArea
funcs.doc:
SphericalPolygonArea returns the area in square metres enclosed by the
spherical polygon whose vertices are given in order (the polygon is closed
automatically), on a sphere of the given radius. The edges are treated as
great-circle arcs and the area is computed exactly from the spherical excess
via the Gauss-Bonnet theorem, so it is correct for arbitrarily large
polygons (as long as they are simple and do not enclose a pole). Winding
order does not affect the magnitude.

funcs.name: SphericalPolygonPerimeter
funcs.doc:
SphericalPolygonPerimeter returns the great-circle perimeter in metres of the
polygon (closed automatically) on a sphere of the given radius.

funcs.name: SphericalTriangleArea
funcs.signature: func SphericalTriangleArea(p1, p2, p3 LatLon, radius float64) float64
funcs.doc:
SphericalTriangleArea returns the area in square metres of the spherical
triangle with the three given vertices, on a sphere of the given radius.

funcs.name: TileToLonLat
funcs.signature: func TileToLonLat(tx, ty, zoom int) (lat, lon float64)
funcs.doc:
TileToLonLat returns the WGS-84 latitude/longitude (degrees) of the
north-west corner of the slippy-map tile (tx, ty) at the given zoom level.

funcs.name: TransverseMercatorForward
funcs.signature: func TransverseMercatorForward(lat, lon, lon0, k0, falseEasting, falseNorthing float64, e Ellipsoid) (easting, northing float64)
funcs.doc:
TransverseMercatorForward projects geodetic latitude/longitude (degrees) to a
transverse Mercator grid on the given ellipsoid, using the Krüger n-series
(accurate to sub-millimetre within a few degrees of the central meridian).
lon0 is the central meridian (degrees), k0 the central scale factor, and
falseEasting/falseNorthing the grid origin offsets (metres). It returns the
easting and northing in metres.

funcs.name: TransverseMercatorInverse
funcs.signature: func TransverseMercatorInverse(easting, northing, lon0, k0, falseEasting, falseNorthing float64, e Ellipsoid) (lat, lon float64)
funcs.doc:
TransverseMercatorInverse recovers geodetic latitude/longitude (degrees) from
a transverse Mercator grid coordinate (easting, northing in metres) on the
given ellipsoid, inverting TransverseMercatorForward with the same
parameters.

funcs.name: UTMCentralMeridian
funcs.doc:
UTMCentralMeridian returns the central meridian (degrees) of the given UTM
zone.

funcs.name: UTMGridConvergence
funcs.doc:
UTMGridConvergence returns the grid convergence angle (degrees) of the UTM
projection at the given WGS-84 point.

funcs.name: UTMPointScale
funcs.doc:
UTMPointScale returns the local scale factor of the UTM projection at the
given WGS-84 point.

funcs.name: UTMToMGRS
funcs.signature: func UTMToMGRS(u UTMCoord, lat float64, precision int) (string, error)
funcs.doc:
UTMToMGRS encodes a UTM coordinate as an MGRS reference string with the given
precision (number of digits per easting/northing, 1..5; 5 = 1 m resolution).
The latitude band is derived from the supplied latitude (degrees), which must
match the coordinate. Passing precision <= 0 yields the grid-square-only
reference (for example "31UDQ").

funcs.name: UTMZone
funcs.doc:
UTMZone returns the UTM zone number (1..60) for the given longitude and
latitude (degrees), applying the Norway and Svalbard zone exceptions.

funcs.name: VincentyDistance
funcs.doc:
VincentyDistance returns the geodesic distance in metres between two points
on the WGS-84 ellipsoid.

funcs.name: VincentyFinalBearing
funcs.doc:
VincentyFinalBearing returns the final azimuth (degrees) of the geodesic from
p1 to p2 on the WGS-84 ellipsoid.

funcs.name: VincentyInitialBearing
funcs.doc:
VincentyInitialBearing returns the initial azimuth (degrees) of the geodesic
from p1 to p2 on the WGS-84 ellipsoid.

funcs.name: WebMercatorForward
funcs.signature: func WebMercatorForward(lat, lon float64) (x, y float64)
funcs.doc:
WebMercatorForward projects WGS-84 latitude/longitude (degrees) to Web
Mercator (EPSG:3857 / "Pseudo-Mercator") coordinates in metres, as used by
most web map tile services. Latitudes are clamped to ±WebMercatorMaxLatitude.

funcs.name: WebMercatorInverse
funcs.signature: func WebMercatorInverse(x, y float64) (lat, lon float64)
funcs.doc:
WebMercatorInverse recovers WGS-84 latitude/longitude (degrees) from Web
Mercator (EPSG:3857) coordinates in metres.

funcs.name: WrapPi
funcs.doc: WrapPi wraps an angle in radians to the half-open interval (-π, π].

funcs.name: WrapPi180
funcs.doc: WrapPi180 wraps an angle in degrees to the half-open interval (-180, 180].

examples.name: HaversineDistance
VincentyInverse

packages.name: geom2d
packages.synopsis: Package geom2d provides primitives for 2D computational geometry.
packages.doc:
Package geom2d provides primitives for 2D computational geometry.

It defines the value types [Point2] (a location in the plane) and [Vec2]
(a displacement), together with the supporting [Line] and [Segment] types.
On top of these it offers the standard toolbox of planar geometry: vector
arithmetic (add, subtract, scale, dot, cross), norms and angles, rotation
and reflection, projection, linear interpolation, point/point,
point/line and point/segment distances, closest-point queries, orientation
and collinearity predicates, and line and segment intersection.

All computation is done in float64 using only the Go standard library.
Routines are deterministic: given identical inputs they return identical
results. Predicates that must tolerate floating-point noise accept or use an
explicit epsilon so callers control the trade-off between robustness and
strictness.

types.name: BoundingBox
types.doc:
BoundingBox is an axis-aligned rectangle spanning from Min (lower-left) to
Max (upper-right). A valid box satisfies Min.X <= Max.X and Min.Y <= Max.Y.

funcs.name: BoundingBoxOf
funcs.doc:
BoundingBoxOf returns the smallest axis-aligned box containing all the given
points. The second result is false when no points are supplied.

funcs.name: NewBoundingBox
funcs.doc:
NewBoundingBox returns the axis-aligned box spanning the two opposite corners
p and q. The corners may be supplied in any order; the result is normalised
so that Min holds the componentwise minimum and Max the componentwise
maximum.

funcs.name: PolygonBoundingBox
funcs.doc:
PolygonBoundingBox returns the axis-aligned bounding box of the polygon pts.
The second result is false when pts is empty.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns the area of the box.

methods.name: Center
methods.doc: Center returns the centre point of the box.

methods.name: ContainsPoint
methods.doc:
ContainsPoint reports whether p lies within the closed box (boundary
included).

methods.name: Corners
methods.doc:
Corners returns the four corners of the box in counter-clockwise order
starting from Min (lower-left).

methods.name: Height
methods.doc: Height returns the vertical extent of the box.

methods.name: Intersection
methods.doc:
Intersection returns the overlapping box of b and o. The second result is
false when the two boxes do not intersect.

methods.name: Intersects
methods.doc:
Intersects reports whether the two boxes overlap (sharing an edge counts as
intersecting).

methods.name: Perimeter
methods.doc: Perimeter returns the perimeter of the box.

methods.name: Union
methods.doc: Union returns the smallest box containing both b and o.

methods.name: Width
methods.doc: Width returns the horizontal extent of the box.

types.name: Circle
types.doc:
Circle is the set of points at exactly Radius distance from Center. A
non-negative Radius is expected; a zero radius degenerates to a single point.

funcs.name: CircleFrom3Points
funcs.signature: func CircleFrom3Points(a, b, c Point2) (Circle, bool)
funcs.doc:
CircleFrom3Points returns the unique circle passing through three points. The
second result is false when the points are collinear (no finite circle
exists).

funcs.name: NewCircle
funcs.signature: func NewCircle(c Point2, radius float64) Circle
funcs.doc: NewCircle returns the circle centred at c with the given radius.

funcs.name: TriangleCircumcircle
funcs.signature: func TriangleCircumcircle(a, b, c Point2) (Circle, bool)
funcs.doc:
TriangleCircumcircle returns the circle through points a, b and c. The second
result is false when the points are collinear.

funcs.name: TriangleIncircle
funcs.signature: func TriangleIncircle(a, b, c Point2) (Circle, bool)
funcs.doc:
TriangleIncircle returns the inscribed circle of the triangle with vertices
a, b and c. The second result is false for a degenerate triangle.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns the area enclosed by the circle.

methods.name: BoundingBox
methods.doc:
BoundingBox returns the axis-aligned bounding box that tightly encloses the
circle.

methods.name: Circumference
methods.doc: Circumference returns the perimeter (circumference) of the circle.

methods.name: ContainsPoint
methods.doc: ContainsPoint reports whether p lies inside or on the circle.

methods.name: Diameter
methods.doc: Diameter returns the diameter of the circle.

methods.name: OnBoundary
methods.signature: func (c Circle) OnBoundary(p Point2, eps float64) bool
methods.doc: OnBoundary reports whether p lies within eps of the circle boundary.

methods.name: PointAtAngle
methods.doc:
PointAtAngle returns the point on the circle at the given angle in radians,
measured counter-clockwise from the positive x-axis.

types.name: Line
types.doc:
Line is the infinite straight line passing through the two distinct points
A and B. The direction of the line is B-A.

funcs.name: PerpendicularBisector
funcs.doc:
PerpendicularBisector returns the line that passes through the midpoint of
p and q at right angles to the segment pq. The returned line's A is the
midpoint and its direction is perpendicular to q-p.

methods.name: ClosestPoint
methods.doc: ClosestPoint returns the point on the line nearest to p.

methods.name: Contains
methods.signature: func (l Line) Contains(p Point2, eps float64) bool
methods.doc:
Contains reports whether p lies on the infinite line l within tolerance eps
(measured as perpendicular distance).

methods.name: Direction
methods.doc: Direction returns the unit direction vector of the line (from A toward B).

methods.name: Distance
methods.doc: Distance returns the perpendicular distance from p to the line.

methods.name: PointAt
methods.doc:
PointAt returns the point A + t*(B-A) on the line, so t=0 gives A and t=1
gives B.

methods.name: Vec
methods.doc: Vec returns the displacement vector from A to B along the line.

types.name: Point2
types.doc: Point2 is a point (location) in the Euclidean plane.

funcs.name: Centroid
funcs.doc:
Centroid returns the arithmetic mean (centroid) of the given points. It
returns the origin when no points are supplied.

funcs.name: CircleCircleIntersection
funcs.doc:
CircleCircleIntersection returns the intersection points of two circles. It
returns zero points when the circles are separate or one strictly contains
the other, one point when they are tangent, and two points when they cross.
Coincident circles (infinitely many intersections) return zero points with a
false second result; all other cases return true.

funcs.name: CircleLineIntersection
funcs.doc:
CircleLineIntersection returns the intersection points of circle c with the
infinite line l. It returns zero, one (tangent) or two points, ordered along
the direction of the line.

funcs.name: CircleSegmentIntersection
funcs.doc:
CircleSegmentIntersection returns the intersection points of circle c with
the finite segment s, restricted to points that actually lie on the segment.

funcs.name: ClosestPointOnLine
funcs.signature: func ClosestPointOnLine(p Point2, l Line) Point2
funcs.doc:
ClosestPointOnLine returns the point on the infinite line l nearest to p
(the foot of the perpendicular from p). If l is degenerate it returns l.A.

funcs.name: ClosestPointOnSegment
funcs.signature: func ClosestPointOnSegment(p Point2, s Segment) Point2
funcs.doc:
ClosestPointOnSegment returns the point on segment s nearest to p, obtained
by projecting p onto the segment's line and clamping to the endpoints.

funcs.name: Lerp
funcs.signature: func Lerp(p, q Point2, t float64) Point2
funcs.doc:
Lerp returns the point interpolated between p and q by parameter t:
(1-t)*p + t*q. t=0 gives p and t=1 gives q.

funcs.name: LineIntersection
funcs.signature: func LineIntersection(l1, l2 Line, eps float64) (Point2, bool)
funcs.doc:
LineIntersection returns the intersection point of the infinite lines l1 and
l2 and reports whether it exists. Parallel (or coincident) lines return
(zero, false); eps bounds the cross product below which the lines are treated
as parallel.

funcs.name: LineSegmentIntersection
funcs.signature: func LineSegmentIntersection(l Line, s Segment, eps float64) (Point2, bool)
funcs.doc:
LineSegmentIntersection returns the intersection point of infinite line l
with segment s and reports whether it exists. A parallel line, or a crossing
that falls outside the segment, returns (zero, false).

funcs.name: Midpoint
funcs.doc: Midpoint returns the point halfway between p and q.

funcs.name: NewPoint2
funcs.doc: NewPoint2 returns the point (x, y).

funcs.name: PointFromPolar
funcs.doc:
PointFromPolar returns the point at distance r and angle theta (radians,
measured counter-clockwise from the positive X axis) from the origin.

funcs.name: PolygonCentroid
funcs.doc:
PolygonCentroid returns the area centroid (centre of mass of a uniform
lamina) of the closed polygon pts. For a degenerate polygon whose signed area
is (near) zero it falls back to the arithmetic mean of the vertices. The
second result reports whether the true area-weighted centroid was used.

funcs.name: ProjectPointOntoLine
funcs.signature: func ProjectPointOntoLine(p Point2, l Line) Point2
funcs.doc:
ProjectPointOntoLine returns the foot of the perpendicular from p to line l.
It is an alias for [ClosestPointOnLine].

funcs.name: ReflectPointAcrossLine
funcs.signature: func ReflectPointAcrossLine(p Point2, l Line) Point2
funcs.doc:
ReflectPointAcrossLine returns p reflected across the infinite line l. The
reflection maps p to the point on the far side of l at equal perpendicular
distance.

funcs.name: RotateAround
funcs.signature: func RotateAround(p, c Point2, theta float64) Point2
funcs.doc:
RotateAround returns p rotated by theta radians counter-clockwise about the
pivot point c.

funcs.name: SegmentIntersection
funcs.signature: func SegmentIntersection(s1, s2 Segment, eps float64) (Point2, bool)
funcs.doc:
SegmentIntersection returns the unique intersection point of segments s1 and
s2 and reports whether such a single point exists. Non-intersecting,
parallel, or collinearly overlapping segments return (zero, false); use
[SegmentsIntersect] to detect overlap without a unique point.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the point p displaced by the vector v.

methods.name: ApproxEqual
methods.signature: func (p Point2) ApproxEqual(q Point2, eps float64) bool
methods.doc:
ApproxEqual reports whether p and q are within eps (absolute) in each
coordinate.

methods.name: DistanceSqTo
methods.doc: DistanceSqTo returns the squared Euclidean distance from p to q.

methods.name: DistanceTo
methods.doc: DistanceTo returns the Euclidean distance from p to q.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether p and q are exactly equal in both coordinates.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the vector from q to p (that is, p-q).

methods.name: Vec
methods.doc: Vec returns the position vector of p (the displacement from the origin to p).

types.name: Polygon
types.signature: type Polygon []Point2
types.doc:
Polygon is a planar polygon described by an ordered list of vertices. The
polygon is treated as closed: the edge from the last vertex back to the
first is implicit and must not be repeated in the vertex slice. Vertices may
be ordered clockwise or counter-clockwise; routines that care about the
winding direction document their expectations individually.

funcs.name: ClipPolygon
funcs.doc:
ClipPolygon clips the subject polygon against a convex clip polygon using the
Sutherland-Hodgman algorithm and returns the intersection as a new polygon.
The clip polygon must be convex and wound counter-clockwise; the subject may
be any simple polygon. The result may be empty when the polygons do not
overlap.

funcs.name: ClipPolygonHalfPlane
funcs.signature: func ClipPolygonHalfPlane(subject []Point2, a, b Point2) Polygon
funcs.doc:
ClipPolygonHalfPlane clips the subject polygon against the half-plane lying
to the left of the directed line through a and b (the inside, for a
counter-clockwise convex boundary). It returns the portion of the subject on
the inside as a new polygon, which may be empty.

funcs.name: ConvexHull
funcs.doc:
ConvexHull returns the convex hull of the input points as a polygon wound
counter-clockwise, using Andrew's monotone-chain algorithm in O(n log n)
time. Interior and collinear boundary points are omitted; the returned hull
contains only true vertices. Duplicate input points are tolerated. Fewer than
three unique points return the (de-duplicated, sorted) input unchanged.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns the non-negative area enclosed by the polygon.

methods.name: BoundingBox
methods.doc:
BoundingBox returns the axis-aligned bounding box of the polygon. The second
result is false when the polygon has no vertices.

methods.name: Centroid
methods.doc:
Centroid returns the area centroid of the polygon. The boolean reports
whether an area-weighted centroid was computed (false when the polygon is
degenerate and the vertex average was returned instead).

methods.name: ContainsPoint
methods.doc:
ContainsPoint reports whether p lies inside the polygon using the ray-casting
rule.

methods.name: ContainsPointWinding
methods.doc:
ContainsPointWinding reports whether p lies inside the polygon using the
non-zero winding rule.

methods.name: Edges
methods.doc:
Edges returns the polygon's boundary edges as segments, including the closing
edge from the last vertex back to the first. A polygon with fewer than two
vertices yields no edges.

methods.name: EnsureCCW
methods.doc:
EnsureCCW returns a polygon with the same shape guaranteed to be wound
counter-clockwise, reversing the receiver only when necessary. The result may
alias the receiver when it is already counter-clockwise.

methods.name: EnsureCW
methods.doc:
EnsureCW returns a polygon with the same shape guaranteed to be wound
clockwise, reversing the receiver only when necessary.

methods.name: IsClockwise
methods.doc:
IsClockwise reports whether the polygon vertices are ordered clockwise
(negative signed area).

methods.name: IsConvex
methods.doc: IsConvex reports whether the polygon is convex.

methods.name: IsCounterClockwise
methods.doc:
IsCounterClockwise reports whether the polygon vertices are ordered
counter-clockwise (positive signed area).

methods.name: NumVertices
methods.doc: NumVertices returns the number of vertices in the polygon.

methods.name: OnBoundary
methods.signature: func (poly Polygon) OnBoundary(p Point2, eps float64) bool
methods.doc: OnBoundary reports whether p lies within eps of the polygon boundary.

methods.name: Perimeter
methods.doc: Perimeter returns the total boundary length of the polygon.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.doc:
Reverse returns a new polygon with the vertex order reversed, flipping the
winding direction. The receiver is not modified.

methods.name: RotateAbout
methods.signature: func (poly Polygon) RotateAbout(theta float64, center Point2) Polygon
methods.doc:
RotateAbout returns a copy of the polygon rotated by theta radians (positive
counter-clockwise) about the fixed point center.

methods.name: ScaleAbout
methods.signature: func (poly Polygon) ScaleAbout(factor float64, center Point2) Polygon
methods.doc:
ScaleAbout returns a copy of the polygon scaled by factor about the fixed
point center. A factor greater than one enlarges the polygon; a negative
factor also reflects it through center.

methods.name: SignedArea
methods.doc:
SignedArea returns the signed shoelace area of the polygon; positive for
counter-clockwise winding, negative for clockwise.

methods.name: Translate
methods.doc: Translate returns a copy of the polygon with every vertex displaced by v.

methods.name: WindingNumber
methods.doc: WindingNumber returns the winding number of the polygon around p.

types.name: Segment
types.doc: Segment is the finite straight segment with endpoints A and B.

methods.name: AsLine
methods.doc: AsLine returns the infinite line supporting the segment.

methods.name: ClosestPoint
methods.doc: ClosestPoint returns the point on the segment nearest to p.

methods.name: Contains
methods.signature: func (s Segment) Contains(p Point2, eps float64) bool
methods.doc:
Contains reports whether p lies on segment s within tolerance eps (measured
as distance to the closest point on the segment).

methods.name: Direction
methods.doc: Direction returns the unit direction vector of the segment (from A toward B).

methods.name: Distance
methods.doc: Distance returns the distance from p to the closest point on the segment.

methods.name: Length
methods.doc: Length returns the Euclidean length of the segment.

methods.name: LengthSq
methods.doc: LengthSq returns the squared Euclidean length of the segment.

methods.name: Midpoint
methods.doc: Midpoint returns the midpoint of the segment.

methods.name: PointAt
methods.doc:
PointAt returns the point A + t*(B-A); t is not clamped, so values outside
[0, 1] lie on the segment's supporting line beyond its endpoints.

methods.name: Vec
methods.doc: Vec returns the displacement vector from A to B along the segment.

types.name: Triangle
types.doc: Triangle is the triangle with vertices A, B and C.

funcs.name: NewTriangle
funcs.signature: func NewTriangle(a, b, c Point2) Triangle
funcs.doc: NewTriangle returns the triangle with the given vertices.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns the non-negative area of the triangle.

methods.name: Barycentric
methods.signature: func (t Triangle) Barycentric(p Point2) (u, v, w float64, ok bool)
methods.doc:
Barycentric returns the barycentric coordinates (u, v, w) of point p with
respect to the triangle, satisfying p = u*A + v*B + w*C and u+v+w = 1. The
final boolean is false for a degenerate (zero-area) triangle, for which the
coordinates are undefined and returned as zero.

methods.name: BoundingBox
methods.doc: BoundingBox returns the axis-aligned bounding box of the triangle.

methods.name: Centroid
methods.doc:
Centroid returns the triangle's centroid (the average of its vertices, which
coincides with the centre of mass).

methods.name: Circumcenter
methods.doc:
Circumcenter returns the centre of the triangle's circumscribed circle (the
point equidistant from all three vertices). The second result is false for a
degenerate triangle whose vertices are collinear.

methods.name: Circumcircle
methods.doc:
Circumcircle returns the circle passing through all three vertices of the
triangle. The second result is false for a degenerate triangle.

methods.name: Circumradius
methods.doc:
Circumradius returns the radius of the triangle's circumscribed circle. The
second result is false for a degenerate triangle.

methods.name: ContainsPoint
methods.doc:
ContainsPoint reports whether p lies inside or on the boundary of the
triangle, using barycentric coordinates. A degenerate triangle contains no
points.

methods.name: Incenter
methods.doc:
Incenter returns the centre of the triangle's inscribed circle, the
intersection of the angle bisectors. The second result is false for a
degenerate triangle whose perimeter is (near) zero.

methods.name: Incircle
methods.doc:
Incircle returns the largest circle contained in the triangle, tangent to all
three sides. The second result is false for a degenerate triangle.

methods.name: Inradius
methods.doc:
Inradius returns the radius of the triangle's inscribed circle, equal to the
area divided by the semiperimeter. The second result is false for a
degenerate triangle.

methods.name: Orthocenter
methods.doc:
Orthocenter returns the point where the triangle's three altitudes meet. The
second result is false for a degenerate triangle.

methods.name: Perimeter
methods.doc: Perimeter returns the sum of the triangle's three side lengths.

methods.name: PointFromBarycentric
methods.signature: func (t Triangle) PointFromBarycentric(u, v, w float64) Point2
methods.doc:
PointFromBarycentric returns the Cartesian point with barycentric coordinates
(u, v, w) relative to the triangle. The coordinates are used as supplied and
are not required to sum to one.

methods.name: SignedArea
methods.doc:
SignedArea returns the signed area of the triangle; positive when A, B, C are
in counter-clockwise order, negative when clockwise.

types.name: Vec2
types.doc: Vec2 is a vector (displacement or direction) in the Euclidean plane.

funcs.name: Add
funcs.doc: Add returns the vector sum u+v.

funcs.name: NewVec2
funcs.doc: NewVec2 returns the vector (x, y).

funcs.name: Scale
funcs.signature: func Scale(u Vec2, s float64) Vec2
funcs.doc: Scale returns the vector u multiplied by the scalar s.

funcs.name: Sub
funcs.doc: Sub returns the vector difference u-v.

funcs.name: VecFromPolar
funcs.doc:
VecFromPolar returns the vector of magnitude r pointing at angle theta
(radians, counter-clockwise from the positive X axis).

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the vector sum u+v.

methods.name: Angle
methods.doc:
Angle returns the direction of u in radians in (-π, π], measured
counter-clockwise from the positive X axis. The zero vector yields 0.

methods.name: AngleBetween
methods.doc:
AngleBetween returns the unsigned angle in [0, π] between u and v. If either
vector has zero length it returns 0.

methods.name: ApproxEqual
methods.signature: func (u Vec2) ApproxEqual(v Vec2, eps float64) bool
methods.doc:
ApproxEqual reports whether u and v are within eps (absolute) in each
component.

methods.name: AsPoint
methods.doc: AsPoint returns v interpreted as the point at position v from the origin.

methods.name: Cross
methods.doc:
Cross returns the 2D cross product u×v = u.X*v.Y - u.Y*v.X, the signed area
of the parallelogram spanned by u and v. It is positive when v lies
counter-clockwise from u.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the dot (scalar) product u·v = u.X*v.X + u.Y*v.Y.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether u and v are exactly equal in both components.

methods.name: Hadamard
methods.doc: Hadamard returns the component-wise product (u.X*v.X, u.Y*v.Y).

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc:
IsZero reports whether u is the zero vector within eps: its length is at
most eps.

methods.name: Lerp
methods.signature: func (u Vec2) Lerp(v Vec2, t float64) Vec2
