methods.doc: Lerp returns the linear interpolation (1-t)*u + t*v.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns the vector -u.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean length |u| of the vector.

methods.name: NormSq
methods.doc: NormSq returns the squared Euclidean length |u|², avoiding the square root.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns the unit vector in the direction of u and its original
length. If u is the zero vector it returns the zero vector and length 0.

methods.name: Perp
methods.doc: Perp returns u rotated 90° counter-clockwise: (-u.Y, u.X).

methods.name: PerpCW
methods.doc: PerpCW returns u rotated 90° clockwise: (u.Y, -u.X).

methods.name: Project
methods.doc:
Project returns the vector projection of u onto v (the component of u
parallel to v). If v is the zero vector it returns the zero vector.

methods.name: Reflect
methods.doc:
Reflect returns u reflected across the line through the origin with
direction axis. If axis is the zero vector u is returned unchanged.

methods.name: ReflectAcrossNormal
methods.doc:
ReflectAcrossNormal returns u reflected as if bouncing off a surface whose
unit normal is n: u - 2(u·n)n. The normal should be a unit vector for a
length-preserving reflection.

methods.name: Reject
methods.doc:
Reject returns the component of u orthogonal to v, that is u minus the
projection of u onto v.

methods.name: Rotate
methods.doc:
Rotate returns u rotated by theta radians counter-clockwise about the
origin.

methods.name: ScalarProject
methods.doc:
ScalarProject returns the signed length of the projection of u onto v.
If v is the zero vector it returns 0.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the vector u multiplied by the scalar s.

methods.name: SignedAngle
methods.doc:
SignedAngle returns the signed angle in (-π, π] from u to v, positive when v
is counter-clockwise from u.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the vector difference u-v.

methods.name: Unit
methods.doc:
Unit returns the unit vector in the direction of u, or the zero vector if u
has zero length.

funcs.name: AngleAt
funcs.signature: func AngleAt(a, b, c Point2) float64
funcs.doc:
AngleAt returns the interior angle in [0, π] at vertex b of the path a-b-c,
that is the angle between the vectors b→a and b→c.

funcs.name: Barycentric
funcs.signature: func Barycentric(p, a, b, c Point2) (u, v, w float64, ok bool)
funcs.doc:
Barycentric returns the barycentric coordinates of point p with respect to
the triangle a, b, c. See Triangle.Barycentric for details.

funcs.name: CCW
funcs.signature: func CCW(a, b, c Point2, eps float64) bool
funcs.doc:
CCW reports whether the points a, b, c are in strict counter-clockwise
order, using eps to reject near-collinear triples.

funcs.name: Collinear
funcs.signature: func Collinear(a, b, c Point2, eps float64) bool
funcs.doc:
Collinear reports whether the points a, b, c lie on a common line, judged by
twice the triangle area being at most eps in magnitude.

funcs.name: ConvexDiameter
funcs.doc:
ConvexDiameter returns the diameter (maximum pairwise distance) of the input
point set, computed with rotating calipers over the convex hull.

funcs.name: ConvexDiameterPoints
funcs.doc:
ConvexDiameterPoints returns the two points of the input set that are
farthest apart (the diameter of the point set) together with that distance,
computed via a convex hull followed by the rotating-calipers method in
O(n log n) time. When fewer than two points are supplied the two results are
equal and the distance is zero.

funcs.name: Cross
funcs.doc: Cross returns the 2D cross product u×v.

funcs.name: Distance
funcs.doc: Distance returns the Euclidean distance between points p and q.

funcs.name: DistanceSq
funcs.doc:
DistanceSq returns the squared Euclidean distance between points p and q,
avoiding the square root.

funcs.name: Dot
funcs.doc: Dot returns the dot product u·v.

funcs.name: IsConvexPolygon
funcs.doc:
IsConvexPolygon reports whether the closed polygon pts is convex. All turns
along the boundary must have the same orientation (collinear vertices are
permitted). Fewer than three vertices are considered non-convex.

funcs.name: OnPolygonBoundary
funcs.signature: func OnPolygonBoundary(p Point2, pts []Point2, eps float64) bool
funcs.doc:
OnPolygonBoundary reports whether p lies within eps of any edge of the closed
polygon pts.

funcs.name: OnSegment
funcs.signature: func OnSegment(p Point2, s Segment, eps float64) bool
funcs.doc:
OnSegment reports whether point p, already assumed to be collinear with the
segment's endpoints within eps, lies within the segment's extent. Use
[Segment.Contains] for a full membership test.

funcs.name: Orientation
funcs.signature: func Orientation(a, b, c Point2, eps float64) int
funcs.doc:
Orientation returns +1 if the points a, b, c make a counter-clockwise turn,
-1 if they make a clockwise turn, and 0 if they are collinear, judged within
eps applied to the signed area.

funcs.name: PointInPolygon
funcs.doc:
PointInPolygon reports whether point p lies inside the closed polygon pts
using the ray-casting (even-odd / crossing-number) rule. Points exactly on
the boundary are reported inconsistently by this rule and should be tested
separately with OnPolygonBoundary if that matters. The polygon may be
non-convex but must be simple (non-self-intersecting) for a meaningful
result.

funcs.name: PointInPolygonWinding
funcs.doc:
PointInPolygonWinding reports whether p lies inside the closed polygon pts
using the non-zero winding rule (the winding number is non-zero).

funcs.name: PointLineDistance
funcs.signature: func PointLineDistance(p Point2, l Line) float64
funcs.doc:
PointLineDistance returns the perpendicular distance from p to the infinite
line l. If l is degenerate it returns the distance from p to l.A.

funcs.name: PointSegmentDistance
funcs.signature: func PointSegmentDistance(p Point2, s Segment) float64
funcs.doc:
PointSegmentDistance returns the distance from p to the closest point on
segment s.

funcs.name: PolygonArea
funcs.doc:
PolygonArea returns the non-negative area enclosed by the polygon pts,
independent of vertex winding order.

funcs.name: PolygonPerimeter
funcs.doc:
PolygonPerimeter returns the total edge length of the closed polygon pts,
including the closing edge from the last vertex back to the first. Fewer than
two vertices yield zero.

funcs.name: PolygonSignedArea
funcs.doc:
PolygonSignedArea returns the signed area of the closed polygon defined by
pts using the shoelace (Gauss) formula. The result is positive when the
vertices are ordered counter-clockwise and negative when they are ordered
clockwise. Fewer than three vertices yield zero.

funcs.name: SegmentSegmentDistance
funcs.signature: func SegmentSegmentDistance(s1, s2 Segment, eps float64) float64
funcs.doc:
SegmentSegmentDistance returns the minimum distance between segments s1 and
s2, which is zero when they intersect.

funcs.name: SegmentsIntersect
funcs.signature: func SegmentsIntersect(s1, s2 Segment, eps float64) bool
funcs.doc:
SegmentsIntersect reports whether segments s1 and s2 share at least one
point. It handles the collinear-overlap and shared-endpoint cases, using eps
for the orientation and on-segment tests.

funcs.name: SignedArea2
funcs.signature: func SignedArea2(a, b, c Point2) float64
funcs.doc:
SignedArea2 returns twice the signed area of triangle abc, equal to the
cross product (b-a)×(c-a). It is positive when a, b, c are in
counter-clockwise order and negative when clockwise.

funcs.name: SignedPointLineDistance
funcs.signature: func SignedPointLineDistance(p Point2, l Line) float64
funcs.doc:
SignedPointLineDistance returns the signed perpendicular distance from p to
line l. The result is positive when p lies to the left of the direction A→B
and negative to the right. A degenerate line yields the unsigned distance to
l.A.

funcs.name: Slope
funcs.doc:
Slope returns the slope (dy/dx) of the line through p and q and whether it is
finite. For a vertical line it returns (0, false).

funcs.name: TriangleArea
funcs.signature: func TriangleArea(a, b, c Point2) float64
funcs.doc: TriangleArea returns the (non-negative) area of triangle abc.

funcs.name: WindingNumber
funcs.doc:
WindingNumber returns the winding number of the closed polygon pts around
point p: the signed number of times the polygon boundary travels
counter-clockwise around p. A non-zero winding number means p is enclosed.
This is robust for self-intersecting polygons where the even-odd rule is
ambiguous.

packages.name: geom3d
packages.synopsis: Package geom3d provides primitives for 3D computational geometry.
packages.doc:
Package geom3d provides primitives for 3D computational geometry.

The core value type is [Vec3], a three-component vector used both as a
location (point) and as a displacement (direction). On top of it the
package offers the standard toolbox of spatial geometry: vector arithmetic
(add, subtract, scale, dot, cross, triple product), norms, distances,
angles, normalization, projection/rejection and reflection; 3x3 matrix
algebra ([Mat3]) with determinant, inverse, transpose and multiplication;
rotation matrices about the coordinate axes and about an arbitrary axis
(Rodrigues' rotation formula); conversion between Euler angles and rotation
matrices; planes ([Plane]) and lines ([Line3]) with closest-point and
distance queries; ray casting ([Ray]) against planes, triangles
(Moeller-Trumbore), axis-aligned boxes ([AABB]) and spheres ([Sphere]);
barycentric coordinates; and intersection tests between boxes and spheres.

All computation is done in float64 using only the Go standard library.
Routines are deterministic: given identical inputs they return identical
results. Predicates that must tolerate floating-point noise accept or use an
explicit epsilon so callers control the trade-off between robustness and
strictness.

types.name: AABB
types.doc:
AABB is an axis-aligned bounding box defined by its minimum and maximum
corners. A valid box has Min[i] <= Max[i] for every axis i.

funcs.name: AABBFromPoints
funcs.doc:
AABBFromPoints returns the smallest axis-aligned box containing all the given
points, and true. It returns the zero box and false if no points are given.

funcs.name: NewAABB
funcs.doc:
NewAABB returns the axis-aligned box with the given corners, normalizing them
so that Min holds the component-wise minimum and Max the component-wise
maximum.

methods.name: Center
methods.doc: Center returns the geometric center of the box.

methods.name: ClosestPoint
methods.doc:
ClosestPoint returns the point within the box (on its surface or interior)
nearest to p, obtained by clamping p to the box on each axis.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc: Contains reports whether point p lies inside or on the boundary of the box.

methods.name: Expand
methods.doc:
Expand returns the box grown outward by the vector v on every side; each
component of v is subtracted from Min and added to Max.

methods.name: Extents
methods.doc: Extents returns the half-size of the box along each axis.

methods.name: IntersectRay
methods.signature: func (bx AABB) IntersectRay(r Ray) (tmin, tmax float64, ok bool)
methods.doc:
IntersectRay intersects a ray with the box using the slab method. On a hit it
returns the entry parameter tmin (distance along the ray to the first
intersection, clamped to 0 if the origin is inside), the exit parameter tmax,
and true. It returns false if the ray misses the box or the box lies entirely
behind the origin.

methods.name: IntersectsAABB
methods.doc:
IntersectsAABB reports whether the box overlaps the other box, touching
boundaries included.

methods.name: IntersectsSphere
methods.doc: IntersectsSphere reports whether the box overlaps the sphere s.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc: Size returns the full extent of the box along each axis (Max - Min).

methods.name: Union
methods.doc: Union returns the smallest axis-aligned box containing both bx and other.

types.name: EulerAngles
types.doc:
EulerAngles holds a set of Tait-Bryan angles in radians using the aerospace
convention: Roll about the x-axis, Pitch about the y-axis and Yaw about the
z-axis.

funcs.name: MatrixToEuler
funcs.doc:
MatrixToEuler extracts the Tait-Bryan angles (intrinsic Z-Y-X convention)
that produce the rotation matrix m, inverting [EulerToMatrix]. Near the
gimbal-lock poles (pitch = ±pi/2) roll is set to zero and yaw absorbs the
combined rotation. The matrix is assumed to be a proper rotation.

types.name: Line3
types.doc:
Line3 is the infinite straight line passing through Point in the direction
Dir. Dir need not be unit length but must be non-zero for the geometric
queries to be meaningful.

funcs.name: LineThrough
funcs.doc:
LineThrough returns the line passing through the two distinct points a and b,
with direction b-a.

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At returns the point Point + t*Dir on the line.

methods.name: ClosestPoint
methods.doc: ClosestPoint returns the point on the line nearest to p.

methods.name: Distance
methods.doc:
Distance returns the perpendicular distance from point p to the line. It
returns the distance from p to Point if the line's direction has zero length.

methods.name: Param
methods.doc:
Param returns the parameter t of the point on the line closest to p, so that
[Line3.At](t) is the closest point. It returns 0 if the line's direction has
zero length.

types.name: Mat3
types.signature: type Mat3 [3][3]float64
types.doc:
Mat3 is a 3x3 matrix of float64 stored in row-major order: element m[r][c]
lives in row r and column c. Vectors are treated as column vectors and
transformed on the left by [Mat3.MulVec].

funcs.name: AxisAngleMatrix
funcs.signature: func AxisAngleMatrix(axis Vec3, angle float64) Mat3
funcs.doc:
AxisAngleMatrix returns the rotation matrix for a rotation by angle radians
about the given axis, using Rodrigues' rotation formula. The axis is
normalized internally; if it has zero length the identity matrix is
returned.

funcs.name: EulerToMatrix
funcs.doc:
EulerToMatrix returns the rotation matrix for the given Tait-Bryan angles
using the intrinsic Z-Y-X convention, i.e. R = Rz(yaw)·Ry(pitch)·Rx(roll).
Applying the result to a vector first rolls, then pitches, then yaws it.

funcs.name: Mat3FromColumns
funcs.signature: func Mat3FromColumns(c0, c1, c2 Vec3) Mat3
funcs.doc:
Mat3FromColumns builds a matrix whose columns are the given vectors c0, c1
and c2.

funcs.name: Mat3FromRows
funcs.signature: func Mat3FromRows(r0, r1, r2 Vec3) Mat3
funcs.doc: Mat3FromRows builds a matrix whose rows are the given vectors r0, r1 and r2.

funcs.name: Mat3Identity
funcs.doc: Mat3Identity returns the 3x3 identity matrix.

funcs.name: RotationX
funcs.doc:
RotationX returns the matrix that rotates a vector by angle radians about the
x-axis, following the right-hand rule.

funcs.name: RotationY
funcs.doc:
RotationY returns the matrix that rotates a vector by angle radians about the
y-axis, following the right-hand rule.

funcs.name: RotationZ
funcs.doc:
RotationZ returns the matrix that rotates a vector by angle radians about the
z-axis, following the right-hand rule.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the component-wise sum m+n.

methods.name: Col
methods.doc:
Col returns column j (0, 1 or 2) of m as a vector. It panics if j is out of
range.

methods.name: Determinant
methods.doc: Determinant returns the determinant of m.

methods.name: Equal
methods.signature: func (m Mat3) Equal(n Mat3, eps float64) bool
methods.doc:
Equal reports whether m and n are equal within the absolute tolerance eps,
compared element-wise.

methods.name: Inverse
methods.doc:
Inverse returns the matrix inverse of m and true, or the zero matrix and
false if m is singular (its determinant is zero within the package
tolerance).

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the matrix product m*n.

methods.name: MulVec
methods.doc: MulVec returns the matrix-vector product m*v, treating v as a column vector.

methods.name: Row
methods.doc:
Row returns row i (0, 1 or 2) of m as a vector. It panics if i is out of
range.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns m with every element multiplied by the scalar s.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a human-readable, row-by-row representation of m.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the component-wise difference m-n.

methods.name: Trace
methods.doc: Trace returns the sum of the diagonal elements of m.

methods.name: Transpose
methods.doc: Transpose returns the transpose of m.

types.name: Plane
types.doc:
Plane is an oriented plane in space, represented by the equation
Normal·x = D. Points on the positive side of the plane (in the direction of
Normal) have Normal·x > D. Normal is not required to be unit length, but the
distance queries assume it is; call [Plane.Normalize] to enforce this.

funcs.name: PlaneFromPointNormal
funcs.doc:
PlaneFromPointNormal returns the plane passing through point p with the given
normal. The normal is normalized so that distance queries are metric.

funcs.name: PlaneFromPoints
funcs.signature: func PlaneFromPoints(a, b, c Vec3) (Plane, bool)
funcs.doc:
PlaneFromPoints returns the plane through the three points a, b and c and
true, or the zero plane and false if the points are collinear. The normal
follows the right-hand rule from (b-a)×(c-a) and is unit length.

methods.name: ClosestPoint
methods.doc:
ClosestPoint returns the orthogonal projection of point p onto the plane, the
point on the plane nearest to p. The plane's normal is assumed to be unit
length.

methods.name: Contains
methods.signature: func (pl Plane) Contains(p Vec3, eps float64) bool
methods.doc:
Contains reports whether point p lies on the plane within the absolute
tolerance eps. The plane's normal is assumed to be unit length.

methods.name: Distance
methods.doc:
Distance returns the unsigned (absolute) distance from point p to the plane.
The plane's normal is assumed to be unit length.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns the plane rescaled so that Normal has unit length while
describing the same set of points. If the normal has zero length the plane
is returned unchanged.

methods.name: Side
methods.signature: func (pl Plane) Side(p Vec3, eps float64) int
methods.doc:
Side reports which side of the plane point p lies on: +1 for the positive
(normal) side, -1 for the negative side, and 0 if p lies on the plane within
the absolute tolerance eps. The plane's normal is assumed to be unit length.

methods.name: SignedDistance
methods.doc:
SignedDistance returns the signed distance from point p to the plane. It is
positive on the side the normal points toward and negative on the other. The
plane's normal is assumed to be unit length.

types.name: Ray
types.doc:
Ray is a half-line starting at Origin and extending in the direction Dir.
Dir need not be unit length; parameters returned by the intersection tests
are expressed in units of Dir's length.

funcs.name: NewRay
funcs.doc:
NewRay returns the ray from origin with the given direction. The direction is
normalized so that intersection parameters are distances along the ray.

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At returns the point Origin + t*Dir on the ray.

methods.name: IntersectPlane
methods.signature: func (r Ray) IntersectPlane(pl Plane) (t float64, ok bool)
methods.doc:
IntersectPlane returns the parameter t at which the ray meets the plane and
true, or 0 and false if the ray is parallel to the plane (or points away and
never reaches it). A returned t is negative if the intersection lies behind
the ray's origin. The plane's normal is assumed to be unit length.

types.name: Sphere
types.doc: Sphere is a solid ball defined by its Center and Radius.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc: Contains reports whether point p lies inside or on the surface of the sphere.

methods.name: IntersectRay
methods.signature: func (s Sphere) IntersectRay(r Ray) (tmin, tmax float64, ok bool)
methods.doc:
IntersectRay intersects a ray with the sphere. On a hit it returns the entry
parameter tmin and exit parameter tmax along the ray (both may coincide for a
tangent ray) and true. If the origin is inside the sphere tmin is negative.
It returns false when the ray misses the sphere or only meets it behind the
origin. The ray direction is assumed to be unit length.

methods.name: IntersectsAABB
methods.doc:
IntersectsAABB reports whether the sphere overlaps the axis-aligned box bx,
by comparing the box's closest point to the sphere's radius.

methods.name: IntersectsSphere
methods.doc:
IntersectsSphere reports whether the sphere overlaps the other sphere,
touching surfaces included.

types.name: Vec3
types.doc:
Vec3 is a vector in three-dimensional Euclidean space. It is used both as a
position (a point) and as a displacement or direction, depending on context.

funcs.name: ClosestPointsBetweenLines
funcs.signature: func ClosestPointsBetweenLines(l1, l2 Line3) (p1, p2 Vec3, ok bool)
funcs.doc:
ClosestPointsBetweenLines returns the pair of points, one on line l1 and one
on line l2, that are closest to each other, together with true. If the lines
are parallel it returns an arbitrary closest pair (the foot of l1.Point on
l2, paired with l1.Point) and false.

funcs.name: NewVec3
funcs.signature: func NewVec3(x, y, z float64) Vec3
funcs.doc: NewVec3 returns the vector with the given components.

funcs.name: Rodrigues
funcs.signature: func Rodrigues(v, axis Vec3, angle float64) Vec3
funcs.doc:
Rodrigues rotates the vector v by angle radians about the given axis using
Rodrigues' rotation formula, without forming a matrix. The axis is
normalized internally; if it has zero length v is returned unchanged.

methods.name: Abs
methods.doc: Abs returns the component-wise absolute value of v.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the component-wise sum v+w.

methods.name: Angle
methods.doc:
Angle returns the unsigned angle in radians between v and w, in the range
[0, pi]. It returns 0 if either vector has zero length.

methods.name: Cross
methods.doc:
Cross returns the cross (vector) product v×w. The result is orthogonal to
both v and w and follows the right-hand rule.

methods.name: Distance
methods.doc: Distance returns the Euclidean distance between the points v and w.

methods.name: DistanceSq
methods.doc: DistanceSq returns the squared Euclidean distance between the points v and w.

methods.name: Div
methods.doc:
Div returns v with every component divided by the scalar s. It panics if s
is zero.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the dot (inner) product v·w.

methods.name: Equal
methods.signature: func (v Vec3) Equal(w Vec3, eps float64) bool
methods.doc:
Equal reports whether v and w are equal within the absolute tolerance eps,
compared component-wise.

methods.name: Hadamard
methods.doc: Hadamard returns the component-wise (Hadamard) product of v and w.

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc:
IsZero reports whether every component of v is zero within the package
default tolerance.

methods.name: Length
methods.doc: Length returns the Euclidean length (magnitude) of v.

methods.name: LengthSq
methods.doc:
LengthSq returns the squared Euclidean length v·v. It avoids the square root
of [Vec3.Length] and is preferred when only relative magnitudes matter.

methods.name: Lerp
methods.signature: func (v Vec3) Lerp(w Vec3, t float64) Vec3
methods.doc:
Lerp returns the linear interpolation (1-t)*v + t*w. With t=0 it returns v
and with t=1 it returns w; values outside [0,1] extrapolate.

methods.name: Max
methods.doc: Max returns the component-wise maximum of v and w.

methods.name: Min
methods.doc: Min returns the component-wise minimum of v and w.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns the additive inverse -v.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns the unit vector pointing in the same direction as v,
together with the original length. If v has zero length the zero vector and
a length of 0 are returned.

methods.name: Project
methods.doc:
Project returns the vector projection of v onto w, that is the component of
v parallel to w. It returns the zero vector if w has zero length.

methods.name: Reflect
methods.doc:
Reflect returns v reflected across the plane whose unit normal is n. The
caller is responsible for supplying a unit-length normal; the reflection is
v - 2*(v·n)*n.

methods.name: Reject
methods.doc:
Reject returns the vector rejection of v from w, that is the component of v
orthogonal to w: v minus its projection onto w.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns v with every component multiplied by the scalar s.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the component-wise difference v-w.

methods.name: Unit
methods.doc:
Unit returns the unit vector in the direction of v, or the zero vector if v
has zero length. It is a convenience wrapper over [Vec3.Normalize] that
discards the length.

funcs.name: Barycentric
funcs.signature: func Barycentric(p, a, b, c Vec3) (u, v, w float64, ok bool)
funcs.doc:
Barycentric returns the barycentric coordinates (u, v, w) of point p with
respect to triangle (a, b, c), so that p ≈ u*a + v*b + w*c and u+v+w = 1. The
point is projected onto the triangle's plane if it does not lie exactly on
it. It returns false if the triangle is degenerate (its vertices are
collinear).

funcs.name: PointInTriangle
funcs.signature: func PointInTriangle(p, a, b, c Vec3) bool
funcs.doc:
PointInTriangle reports whether point p lies inside (or on the edge of)
triangle (a, b, c). The point is assumed to lie in the triangle's plane;
callers testing an arbitrary point should first project it. A degenerate
triangle yields false.

funcs.name: RayTriangle
funcs.signature: func RayTriangle(r Ray, a, b, c Vec3) (t, u, v float64, ok bool)
funcs.doc:
RayTriangle intersects a ray with triangle (a, b, c) using the
Moeller-Trumbore algorithm. On a hit it returns the ray parameter t (distance
along Dir to the intersection), the barycentric coordinates u and v within
the triangle (with the a-weight equal to 1-u-v), and true. It returns false
if the ray is parallel to the triangle, misses it, or would intersect behind
the origin. Both faces of the triangle are considered.

funcs.name: ScalarTriple
funcs.signature: func ScalarTriple(a, b, c Vec3) float64
funcs.doc:
ScalarTriple returns the scalar triple product a·(b×c), which equals the
signed volume of the parallelepiped spanned by a, b and c.

packages.name: graph
packages.synopsis: Package graph implements graph data structures and classical graph algorithms using only the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package graph implements graph data structures and classical graph
algorithms using only the Go standard library.

The central type is [Graph], an adjacency-list graph whose vertices are
arbitrary non-negative or negative integer identifiers. A graph may be
directed or undirected and every edge carries a float64 weight (unweighted
edges use a weight of 1). Construct graphs with [New] or [NewDirected] and
populate them with [Graph.AddEdge] and [Graph.AddWeightedEdge].

The package provides:

  - Traversal and structure: [Graph.BFS], [Graph.DFS], preorder and
    postorder walks, [Graph.ConnectedComponents], [Graph.TopologicalSort],
    [Graph.HasCycle], [Graph.IsBipartite] and [Graph.IsTree].
  - Shortest paths: [Graph.Dijkstra], [Graph.BellmanFord],
    [Graph.FloydWarshall] and [Graph.AStar].
  - Minimum spanning trees: [Graph.Kruskal] and [Graph.Prim], backed by the
    [UnionFind] disjoint-set structure.
  - Strong connectivity: [Graph.TarjanSCC], [Graph.KosarajuSCC] and
    [Graph.TransitiveClosure].
  - Coloring and flows: [Graph.GreedyColoring], [Graph.EdmondsKarp] max-flow
    and [Graph.MaxBipartiteMatching].

All algorithms are deterministic: neighbor iteration and every returned
ordering break ties by ascending vertex identifier, so repeated runs on the
same input yield identical results. The package has no third-party
dependencies.

vars.signature: var ErrNegativeCycle = errors.New("graph: negative-weight cycle")
vars.doc:
ErrNegativeCycle reports that a shortest-path computation detected a cycle of
negative total weight reachable from the source, so finite shortest paths do
not exist.

vars.signature: var ErrNegativeWeight = errors.New("graph: negative edge weight")
vars.doc:
ErrNegativeWeight reports that an algorithm requiring non-negative edge
weights (such as [Graph.Dijkstra] or [Graph.AStar]) encountered a negative
weight.

vars.signature: var ErrNotDAG = errors.New("graph: not a directed acyclic graph")
vars.doc:
ErrNotDAG reports that an operation requiring a directed acyclic graph was
applied to a graph that is undirected or contains a cycle.

vars.signature: var ErrNotUndirected = errors.New("graph: operation requires an undirected graph")
vars.doc:
ErrNotUndirected reports that an operation requiring an undirected graph (such
as [Graph.Kruskal] or [Graph.Prim]) was applied to a directed graph.

vars.signature: var ErrVertexNotFound = errors.New("graph: vertex not found")
vars.doc:
ErrVertexNotFound reports that an operation referenced a vertex that is not
present in the graph.

types.name: Edge
types.doc:
Edge is a weighted connection from vertex From to vertex To. For undirected
graphs each edge is reported once with From <= To by [Graph.Edges].

types.name: Graph

funcs.name: New
funcs.doc: New returns an empty undirected graph.

funcs.name: NewDirected
funcs.doc: NewDirected returns an empty directed graph.

methods.name: AStar
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) AStar(start, goal int, h func(int) float64) (path []int, weight float64, err error)
methods.doc:
AStar finds a minimum-weight path from start to goal over non-negative edge
weights, guided by the heuristic h, which must estimate the remaining cost
from a vertex to goal and be admissible (never overestimate) for the result
to be optimal. It returns the path, its total weight, and whether goal was
reached. It returns [ErrVertexNotFound] if start or goal is absent, or
[ErrNegativeWeight] if a relaxed edge weight is negative.

methods.name: AddEdge
methods.doc:
AddEdge adds an edge between u and v with weight 1, creating either vertex if
necessary. For an undirected graph the reverse edge is added as well. Adding
an existing edge overwrites its weight with 1.

methods.name: AddVertex
methods.doc:
AddVertex adds v to the graph if it is not already present. Adding an
existing vertex is a no-op.

methods.name: AddWeightedEdge
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) AddWeightedEdge(u, v int, w float64)
methods.doc:
AddWeightedEdge adds an edge from u to v with the given weight, creating
either vertex if necessary. For an undirected graph the reverse edge is added
with the same weight. Adding an existing edge overwrites its weight.

methods.name: AdjacencyMatrix
methods.doc:
AdjacencyMatrix returns the weighted adjacency matrix of the graph together
with the vertex ordering (ascending) that indexes its rows and columns. Entry
[i][j] is the weight of the edge from vertex order[i] to order[j], or 0 if no
such edge exists.

methods.name: BFS
methods.doc:
BFS returns the vertices reachable from start in breadth-first order,
including start itself. Neighbors are visited in ascending order. It returns
[ErrVertexNotFound] if start is absent.

methods.name: BFSDistances
methods.doc:
BFSDistances returns, for every vertex reachable from start, the minimum
number of edges from start to that vertex (start maps to 0). Unreachable
vertices are omitted. It returns [ErrVertexNotFound] if start is absent.

methods.name: BellmanFord
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) BellmanFord(start int) (dist map[int]float64, prev map[int]int, err error)
methods.doc:
BellmanFord computes single-source shortest-path distances from start and
tolerates negative edge weights. It returns a map of reachable vertices to
their distance and a predecessor map suitable for [Graph.PathTo]. It returns
[ErrVertexNotFound] if start is absent, or [ErrNegativeCycle] if a
negative-weight cycle is reachable from start.

methods.name: BellmanFordPath
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) BellmanFordPath(start, goal int) (path []int, weight float64, err error)
methods.doc:
BellmanFordPath returns the minimum-weight path from start to goal, tolerating
negative edge weights, together with its total weight. The boolean result
reports whether goal is reachable. It returns an error under the same
conditions as [Graph.BellmanFord].

methods.name: ChromaticNumberUpperBound
methods.doc:
ChromaticNumberUpperBound returns an easily computed upper bound on the
chromatic number: one more than the maximum vertex degree (Brooks' bound in
its simplest form). The true chromatic number is at most this value.

methods.name: Condensation
methods.doc:
Condensation returns the condensation of a directed graph: a new directed
graph whose vertices are the indices of the strongly connected components (as
ordered by [Graph.TarjanSCC]) with an edge i->j whenever some edge of the
original graph runs from component i to a different component j. The
condensation is always acyclic. The second result maps each original vertex
to its component index.

methods.name: ConnectedComponents
methods.doc:
ConnectedComponents returns the connected components of the graph. For a
directed graph the components are the weakly connected components (edge
orientation is ignored). Each component is a slice of vertices in ascending
order, and the components are ordered by their smallest vertex.

methods.name: Copy
methods.doc: Copy returns a deep copy of the graph.

methods.name: DFS
methods.doc:
DFS returns the vertices reachable from start in depth-first preorder,
including start itself. Neighbors are visited in ascending order. It returns
[ErrVertexNotFound] if start is absent.

methods.name: DFSPostorder
methods.doc:
DFSPostorder returns the vertices reachable from start in depth-first
postorder: each vertex is recorded after all its descendants. Neighbors are
visited in ascending order. It returns [ErrVertexNotFound] if start is absent.

methods.name: DFSPreorder
methods.doc:
DFSPreorder returns the vertices reachable from start in depth-first
preorder: each vertex is recorded when first visited. Neighbors are visited
in ascending order. It returns [ErrVertexNotFound] if start is absent.

methods.name: Degree
methods.doc:
Degree returns the degree of v: for an undirected graph the number of
incident edges (a self-loop counts twice), and for a directed graph the sum
of in-degree and out-degree. It returns [ErrVertexNotFound] if v is absent.

methods.name: DegreeSequence
methods.doc:
DegreeSequence returns the degrees of all vertices sorted in non-increasing
order. The definition of degree matches [Graph.Degree].

methods.name: Dijkstra
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) Dijkstra(start int) (dist map[int]float64, prev map[int]int, err error)
methods.doc:
Dijkstra computes single-source shortest-path distances from start over
non-negative edge weights. It returns a map of reachable vertices to their
distance from start and a predecessor map suitable for [Graph.PathTo].
Unreachable vertices are omitted. It returns [ErrVertexNotFound] if start is
absent, or [ErrNegativeWeight] if any reachable edge weight is negative.

methods.name: DijkstraPath
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) DijkstraPath(start, goal int) (path []int, weight float64, err error)
methods.doc:
DijkstraPath returns the minimum-weight path from start to goal over
non-negative edge weights, together with its total weight. The boolean result
reports whether goal is reachable. It returns an error under the same
conditions as [Graph.Dijkstra].

methods.name: Directed
methods.doc: Directed reports whether the graph is directed.

methods.name: Edges
methods.doc:
Edges returns all edges of the graph. For a directed graph every edge is
returned. For an undirected graph each edge is returned once, oriented so
that From <= To. Edges are sorted by (From, To).

methods.name: EdmondsKarp
methods.doc:
EdmondsKarp computes the maximum flow from source to sink in a directed graph,
treating each edge weight as its capacity, using the Edmonds-Karp algorithm
(BFS-augmenting-path Ford-Fulkerson). It returns the value of the maximum
flow. It returns [ErrVertexNotFound] if source or sink is absent, or
[ErrNegativeWeight] if any capacity is negative. When source equals sink the
flow is zero.

The graph is not modified. An undirected graph is treated as having, for each
edge {u,v}, forward capacity in both directions equal to the edge weight.

methods.name: FloydWarshall
methods.doc:
FloydWarshall computes all-pairs shortest-path distances. It returns a nested
map where dist[u][v] is the shortest distance from u to v, or
math.Inf(1) if v is unreachable from u; dist[u][u] is 0 unless a negative
self-reachable cycle exists. It returns [ErrNegativeCycle] if the graph
contains a negative-weight cycle.

methods.name: GreedyColoring
methods.doc:
GreedyColoring assigns each vertex a color (a non-negative integer) so that no
edge joins two vertices of the same color, using the greedy first-fit
heuristic. Vertices are processed in ascending order and each receives the
smallest color not used by an already-colored neighbor, ignoring edge
orientation. The number of colors used is at most one more than the maximum
degree; the result is a valid coloring but not necessarily optimal. A
self-loop makes proper coloring impossible and is reported by the coloring
still being returned, so callers that may have self-loops should validate with
[Graph.IsProperColoring].

methods.name: HasCycle
methods.doc:
HasCycle reports whether the graph contains a cycle. For directed graphs it
detects directed cycles; for undirected graphs it detects any cycle,
disregarding trivial back-edges to the immediate parent. A self-loop always
counts as a cycle.

methods.name: HasEdge
methods.doc:
HasEdge reports whether an edge from u to v exists. For undirected graphs the
orientation is irrelevant.

methods.name: HasVertex
methods.doc: HasVertex reports whether v is present in the graph.

methods.name: InDegree
methods.doc:
InDegree returns the number of edges entering v. For an undirected graph it
equals the out-degree. It returns [ErrVertexNotFound] if v is absent.

methods.name: IsBipartite
methods.doc:
IsBipartite reports whether the graph is bipartite (2-colorable), ignoring
edge orientation. A graph with no edges is bipartite.

methods.name: IsConnected
methods.doc:
IsConnected reports whether the graph is connected (weakly connected for
directed graphs). The empty graph and single-vertex graph are considered
connected.

methods.name: IsDAG
methods.doc: IsDAG reports whether the graph is a directed acyclic graph.

methods.name: IsProperColoring
methods.doc:
IsProperColoring reports whether color assigns different colors to the two
endpoints of every edge, ignoring edge orientation. A graph with a self-loop
can never be properly colored.

methods.name: IsTree
methods.doc:
IsTree reports whether an undirected graph is a tree: connected and acyclic
with exactly NumVertices-1 edges. The empty graph is not a tree; a single
vertex is. Directed graphs are never reported as trees by this method.

methods.name: KosarajuSCC
methods.doc:
KosarajuSCC returns the strongly connected components of a directed graph
using Kosaraju's two-pass algorithm. The result matches [Graph.TarjanSCC]:
each component is sorted ascending and the components are ordered by their
smallest vertex.

methods.name: Kruskal
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) Kruskal() (mst []Edge, total float64, err error)
methods.doc:
Kruskal computes a minimum spanning tree (or minimum spanning forest, if the
graph is disconnected) of an undirected graph using Kruskal's algorithm. It
returns the chosen edges and their total weight. Edges are considered in
non-decreasing weight order, breaking ties by (From, To) for determinism. It
returns [ErrNotUndirected] for a directed graph.

methods.name: MaxBipartiteMatching
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) MaxBipartiteMatching(left, right []int) (matching map[int]int, size int)
methods.doc:
MaxBipartiteMatching computes a maximum-cardinality matching between the left
and right vertex sets of a bipartite graph using Kuhn's augmenting-path
algorithm. Only edges from a left vertex to a right vertex are used
(orientation is ignored, but at least one such adjacency must exist). It
returns a map from each matched left vertex to its matched right vertex and
the size of the matching. Left vertices are processed in ascending order for
deterministic results.

methods.name: MinCut
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) MinCut(source, sink int) (value float64, sourceSide []int, err error)
methods.doc:
MinCut computes a minimum s-t cut of a directed graph whose edge weights are
capacities. It returns the cut value (equal to the maximum flow), the set of
vertices on the source side of the cut, and any error from the underlying
[Graph.EdmondsKarp] computation. Vertices reachable from source in the final
residual graph form the source side.

methods.name: Neighbors
methods.doc:
Neighbors returns the out-neighbors of v in ascending order. It returns
[ErrVertexNotFound] if v is absent.

methods.name: NumConnectedComponents
methods.doc:
NumConnectedComponents returns the number of connected components (weakly
connected for directed graphs). An empty graph has zero components.

methods.name: NumEdges
methods.doc:
NumEdges returns the number of edges (the size of the graph). Each undirected
edge is counted once.

methods.name: NumVertices
methods.doc: NumVertices returns the number of vertices (the order of the graph).

methods.name: OutDegree
methods.doc:
OutDegree returns the number of edges leaving v. It returns
[ErrVertexNotFound] if v is absent.

methods.name: PathTo
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) PathTo(prev map[int]int, start, goal int) []int
methods.doc:
PathTo reconstructs the path from start to goal from a predecessor map as
returned by [Graph.Dijkstra], [Graph.BellmanFord] or [Graph.AStar]. The start
vertex must map to itself in prev. It returns nil if goal is unreachable.

methods.name: Prim
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) Prim(start int) (mst []Edge, total float64, err error)
methods.doc:
Prim computes a minimum spanning tree of the connected component containing
start in an undirected graph using Prim's algorithm. It returns the chosen
edges and their total weight. It returns [ErrNotUndirected] for a directed
graph or [ErrVertexNotFound] if start is absent. To span every component of a
possibly disconnected graph, use [Graph.Kruskal].

methods.name: ReachableFrom
methods.doc:
ReachableFrom returns the set of vertices reachable from start (including
start) as a map. It returns [ErrVertexNotFound] if start is absent.

methods.name: RemoveEdge
methods.doc:
RemoveEdge removes the edge from u to v if present. For an undirected graph
the reverse edge is removed as well. Removing an absent edge is a no-op.

methods.name: RemoveVertex
methods.doc:
RemoveVertex removes v and all edges incident to it. Removing an absent
vertex is a no-op.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.doc:
Reverse returns a new directed graph with every edge reversed. For an
undirected graph it returns a copy, since undirected edges are symmetric.

methods.name: ShortestPathBFS
methods.doc:
ShortestPathBFS returns a path with the fewest edges from start to goal in an
unweighted sense, and whether such a path exists. Edge weights are ignored.
The returned path begins with start and ends with goal.

methods.name: TarjanSCC
methods.doc:
TarjanSCC returns the strongly connected components of a directed graph using
Tarjan's algorithm. For an undirected graph the strongly connected components
coincide with the connected components. Each component is returned as a slice
of vertices in ascending order, and the components are ordered by their
smallest vertex.

methods.name: TopologicalSort
methods.doc:
TopologicalSort returns a topological ordering of a directed acyclic graph
using Kahn's algorithm. Among vertices whose prerequisites are all satisfied
it always emits the smallest identifier first, giving a deterministic order.
It returns ErrNotDAG if the graph is undirected or contains a cycle.

methods.name: TransitiveClosure
methods.doc:
TransitiveClosure returns the reachability relation of the graph as a nested
map: closure[u][v] is true when there is a directed path (of one or more
edges) from u to v. A vertex reaches itself only if it lies on a cycle or has
a self-loop.

methods.name: TwoColoring
methods.doc:
TwoColoring attempts to 2-color the graph so that adjacent vertices receive
different colors (0 or 1), ignoring edge orientation. It returns the coloring
and true if the graph is bipartite, or nil and false otherwise.

methods.name: Vertices
methods.doc: Vertices returns all vertices in ascending order.

methods.name: Weight
methods.doc: Weight returns the weight of the edge from u to v and whether it exists.

types.name: UnionFind
types.doc:
UnionFind is a disjoint-set (union-find) data structure over a fixed set of
integer elements, with union by rank and path compression. It answers
connectivity queries in near-constant amortized time and is used by
[Graph.Kruskal].

funcs.name: NewUnionFind
funcs.doc:
NewUnionFind returns a union-find structure whose given elements each start in
their own singleton set. Duplicate elements are ignored.

methods.name: Connected
methods.doc: Connected reports whether x and y are in the same set.

methods.name: Count
methods.doc: Count returns the number of disjoint sets currently maintained.

methods.name: Find
methods.doc:
Find returns the canonical representative of the set containing x, adding x as
a new singleton if it was not present. Path compression is applied.

methods.name: MakeSet
methods.doc: MakeSet adds x as a new singleton set if it is not already present.

methods.name: Union
methods.doc:
Union merges the sets containing x and y and reports whether a merge occurred
(that is, whether they were previously in different sets). Absent elements are
added automatically.

funcs.name: NumColors
funcs.doc: NumColors returns the number of distinct colors that appear in a coloring.

packages.name: graphspectral
packages.synopsis: Package graphspectral implements spectral graph theory using only the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package graphspectral implements spectral graph theory using only the Go
standard library.

The package centres on two data types. [Matrix] is a small dense real matrix
with the linear-algebra operations required for spectral analysis: addition,
multiplication, transposition, determinants, linear solves, inverses,
symmetric eigendecomposition (via the cyclic Jacobi method) and the leading
eigenpair by power iteration. [Graph] is a finite undirected, optionally
weighted, graph stored as a symmetric adjacency matrix; it produces the
adjacency, degree, (combinatorial) Laplacian, normalized Laplacian,
random-walk Laplacian, signless Laplacian, transition and incidence matrices,
and answers structural questions such as connectivity, bipartiteness,
regularity and density.

On top of these it provides the classical spectral quantities: the adjacency
and Laplacian spectra, the algebraic connectivity and the Fiedler vector,
spectral bisection and k-way spectral clustering, the adjacency and Laplacian
spectral gaps, the graph energy and Laplacian energy, the Estrada index, the
number of spanning trees via the Matrix-Tree theorem, the Kirchhoff index and
effective resistances through the Laplacian pseudoinverse, and the two Cheeger
bounds relating the algebraic connectivity to the isoperimetric (Cheeger)
constant.

A family of centrality measures is included: degree, eigenvector, Katz,
closeness and harmonic centralities, together with PageRank (both uniform and
personalized) computed by power iteration on the Google matrix. Standard
convenient graph constructors (path, cycle, complete, star, wheel, complete
bipartite and empty graphs) are provided, largely because their spectra are
known in closed form and make excellent test fixtures.

Every routine is deterministic and depends only on packages math, sort,
errors, fmt and strings. Vectors are plain []float64 slices; matrices are
dense and are never assumed sparse.

vars.doc:
Sentinel errors returned throughout the package. Callers may test for these
with errors.Is.

types.name: EigenResult
types.doc:
EigenResult holds the result of a symmetric eigendecomposition: the
eigenvalues and their corresponding eigenvectors, stored as the columns of a
matrix. The pair (Values[i], Vectors.Col(i)) satisfies A·v = λ·v.

funcs.name: EigenSymmetric
funcs.doc:
EigenSymmetric computes all eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a real symmetric
matrix using the cyclic Jacobi method. It returns ErrNotSquare if the matrix
is not square and ErrNotSymmetric if it is not symmetric to within 1e-9. The
returned eigenvectors are orthonormal; the pairs are not sorted.

methods.name: Largest
methods.doc: Largest returns the largest eigenvalue and its eigenvector.

methods.name: Len
methods.doc: Len returns the number of eigenpairs.

methods.name: Pair
methods.doc: Pair returns the i-th eigenvalue together with its eigenvector.

methods.name: Smallest
methods.doc: Smallest returns the smallest eigenvalue and its eigenvector.

methods.name: SortAscending
methods.doc: SortAscending reorders the eigenpairs so the eigenvalues increase.

methods.name: SortDescending
methods.doc: SortDescending reorders the eigenpairs so the eigenvalues decrease.

methods.name: Vector
methods.doc: Vector returns the i-th eigenvector (column i of Vectors).

types.name: Graph
types.doc:
Graph is a finite, undirected, optionally weighted graph on the vertex set
{0, 1, …, n-1}. It is stored as a symmetric adjacency matrix; an edge weight
of zero means "no edge". Self-loops (nonzero diagonal entries) are permitted
and are reflected in the degree.

funcs.name: CompleteBipartiteGraph
funcs.doc:
CompleteBipartiteGraph returns K_{m,n}: vertices 0..m-1 form one side, vertices
m..m+n-1 form the other, and every cross pair is joined.

funcs.name: CompleteGraph
funcs.doc:
CompleteGraph returns the complete graph K_n in which every pair of distinct
vertices is joined by an edge.

funcs.name: CycleGraph
funcs.doc:
CycleGraph returns the cycle C_n on n vertices (n >= 3 for a genuine cycle;
for n < 3 it degenerates to a path or single edge).

funcs.name: EmptyGraph
funcs.doc: EmptyGraph returns the edgeless graph on n vertices.

funcs.name: GridGraph
funcs.doc:
GridGraph returns the r-by-c grid (lattice) graph, the Cartesian product of
P_r and P_c, with vertex (i, j) indexed as i*c + j.

funcs.name: NewGraph
funcs.doc: NewGraph returns an edgeless graph on n vertices. It panics if n is negative.

funcs.name: NewGraphFromAdjacency
funcs.doc:
NewGraphFromAdjacency builds a graph from a square symmetric adjacency matrix.
The matrix is copied and symmetrized defensively. It returns ErrNotSquare or
ErrNotSymmetric on an invalid matrix.

funcs.name: PathGraph
funcs.doc:
PathGraph returns the path P_n: vertices 0..n-1 joined in a line
0-1-2-…-(n-1).

funcs.name: StarGraph
funcs.doc:
StarGraph returns the star K_{1,n-1}: a central vertex 0 joined to each of the
remaining n-1 vertices.

funcs.name: WheelGraph
funcs.doc:
WheelGraph returns the wheel W_n: a cycle on vertices 1..n-1 plus a hub vertex
0 joined to every cycle vertex. It requires n >= 4 to form a proper wheel.

methods.name: AddEdge
methods.doc:
AddEdge adds (or, for a weighted graph, sets to weight 1) an undirected edge
between u and v. It panics on an out-of-range vertex.

methods.name: Adjacency
methods.doc:
Adjacency returns a copy of the adjacency matrix A, where A[i][j] is the
weight of the edge between i and j.

methods.name: Complement
methods.doc:
Complement returns the complement of an unweighted simple graph: vertices are
adjacent in the result exactly when they are non-adjacent (and distinct) in g.

methods.name: ConnectedComponents
methods.doc:
ConnectedComponents returns a labelling of the vertices into connected
components, using a breadth-first flood fill over the (unweighted) edge set.
Component labels are consecutive integers starting at 0, assigned in order of
first discovery.

methods.name: Degree
methods.doc:
Degree returns the (integer) number of neighbours of v in an unweighted graph,
counting a self-loop once. For weighted degree use [Graph.WeightedDegree].

methods.name: DegreeMatrix
methods.doc:
DegreeMatrix returns the diagonal degree matrix D whose (v, v) entry is the
weighted degree of v.

methods.name: Degrees
methods.doc: Degrees returns the weighted degree of every vertex.

methods.name: Density
methods.doc:
Density returns the edge density of an unweighted view: the number of edges
(ignoring self-loops) divided by the number of possible edges n(n-1)/2. It
returns 0 for graphs with fewer than two vertices.

methods.name: HasEdge
methods.doc: HasEdge reports whether there is a (nonzero-weight) edge between u and v.

methods.name: IncidenceMatrix
methods.doc:
IncidenceMatrix returns the (oriented) vertex-edge incidence matrix B of an
unweighted view of the graph. B has one column per edge (in ascending
(u, v) order with u < v); the column for edge (u, v) has -1 in row u and +1 in
row v. Self-loops are skipped. The identity B·Bᵀ = L holds for the unweighted
Laplacian.

methods.name: IsBipartite
methods.doc:
IsBipartite reports whether the graph is bipartite, and if so returns a
two-colouring (side 0 or 1) of its vertices. Isolated vertices are placed on
side 0. When the graph is not bipartite the returned slice is nil.

methods.name: IsConnected
methods.doc: IsConnected reports whether the graph has exactly one connected component.

methods.name: IsRegular
methods.doc:
IsRegular reports whether every vertex has the same weighted degree (to within
1e-9), and returns that common degree.

methods.name: Laplacian
methods.doc: Laplacian returns the combinatorial Laplacian L = D - A.

methods.name: MaxDegree
methods.doc: MaxDegree returns the largest weighted degree, or 0 for an empty graph.

methods.name: MinDegree
methods.doc: MinDegree returns the smallest weighted degree, or 0 for an empty graph.

methods.name: Neighbors
methods.doc: Neighbors returns the vertices adjacent to v, in ascending order.

methods.name: NormalizedLaplacian
methods.doc:
NormalizedLaplacian returns the symmetric normalized Laplacian
Lsym = I - D^{-1/2} A D^{-1/2}. Isolated vertices (degree 0) contribute a unit
diagonal entry and zero off-diagonal entries.

methods.name: NumConnectedComponents
methods.doc:
NumConnectedComponents returns the number of connected components. An empty
graph has zero components.

methods.name: Order
methods.doc: Order returns the number of vertices.

methods.name: RandomWalkLaplacian
methods.doc:
RandomWalkLaplacian returns the random-walk Laplacian Lrw = I - D^{-1} A.
Isolated vertices contribute a unit diagonal entry. Note Lrw is generally not
symmetric.

methods.name: RemoveEdge
methods.doc: RemoveEdge deletes the edge between u and v.

methods.name: SetWeight
methods.signature: func (g *Graph) SetWeight(u, v int, w float64)
methods.doc:
SetWeight sets the weight of the undirected edge between u and v (and, by
symmetry, between v and u). A weight of zero removes the edge. It panics on an
out-of-range vertex.

methods.name: SignlessLaplacian
methods.doc: SignlessLaplacian returns the signless Laplacian Q = D + A.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc:
Size returns the number of edges. Each undirected edge is counted once; a
self-loop counts as one edge.

methods.name: TotalWeight
methods.doc:
TotalWeight returns the sum of all edge weights, counting each undirected edge
once.

methods.name: TransitionMatrix
methods.doc:
TransitionMatrix returns the random-walk transition matrix P = D^{-1} A, whose
row i is the probability distribution of a single step from vertex i. Rows for
isolated vertices are all zero.

methods.name: Weight
methods.doc: Weight returns the weight of the edge between u and v (0 if there is none).

methods.name: WeightedDegree
methods.doc:
WeightedDegree returns the weighted degree of vertex v: the sum of the weights
of its incident edges. A self-loop of weight w contributes w.

types.name: Matrix
types.doc: Matrix is a dense matrix of float64 values stored in row-major order.

funcs.name: AllPairsShortestPaths
funcs.doc:
AllPairsShortestPaths returns the matrix of weighted shortest-path distances
between every ordered pair of vertices, computed by running Dijkstra from each
source. Unreachable pairs hold +Inf.

funcs.name: DiagMatrix
funcs.doc: DiagMatrix returns a square diagonal matrix whose diagonal is d.

funcs.name: IdentityMatrix
funcs.doc: IdentityMatrix returns the n-by-n identity matrix.

funcs.name: Inverse
funcs.doc:
Inverse returns the inverse of a square matrix using Gauss-Jordan elimination
with partial pivoting. It returns ErrNotSquare or ErrSingular as appropriate.

funcs.name: LaplacianPseudoinverse
funcs.doc:
LaplacianPseudoinverse returns the Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse L⁺ of the
combinatorial Laplacian, computed from its eigendecomposition by inverting the
eigenvalues that exceed tol and zeroing the rest. If tol <= 0 a default of
1e-9 is used.

funcs.name: NewMatrix
funcs.doc:
NewMatrix returns a new rows-by-cols matrix with every entry zero. It panics
if rows or cols is negative.

funcs.name: NewMatrixFromRows
funcs.doc:
NewMatrixFromRows builds a matrix from a slice of equal-length rows. It returns
ErrDimensionMismatch if the rows have differing lengths and ErrEmpty if no
rows are given.

funcs.name: OuterProduct
funcs.doc: OuterProduct returns the rank-one matrix a·bᵀ.

methods.name: Add
methods.signature: func (m *Matrix) Add(i, j int, v float64)
methods.doc: Add increments the entry in row i, column j by v.

methods.name: Apply
methods.doc: Apply returns a new matrix whose entries are f applied element-wise.

methods.name: ApproxEqual
methods.doc:
ApproxEqual reports whether m and o have the same shape and agree entry-wise
to within absolute tolerance tol.

methods.name: At
methods.doc:
At returns the entry in row i, column j. It panics if the index is out of
range.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent copy of the matrix.

methods.name: Col
methods.doc: Col returns a copy of column j.

methods.name: ColSums
methods.doc: ColSums returns the sum of each column.

methods.name: Cols
methods.doc: Cols returns the number of columns.

methods.name: Diagonal
methods.doc: Diagonal returns a copy of the main diagonal.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether m and o have the same shape and identical entries.

methods.name: FrobeniusNorm
methods.doc: FrobeniusNorm returns the square root of the sum of squared entries.

methods.name: IsSquare
methods.doc: IsSquare reports whether the matrix has equal numbers of rows and columns.

methods.name: IsSymmetric
methods.doc:
IsSymmetric reports whether the matrix is square and symmetric to within
absolute tolerance tol.

methods.name: MaxAbs
methods.doc: MaxAbs returns the largest absolute entry.

methods.name: Minus
methods.doc:
Minus returns the difference m-o. It returns ErrDimensionMismatch on a shape
mismatch.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc:
Mul returns the matrix product m*o. It returns ErrDimensionMismatch when the
inner dimensions disagree.

methods.name: MulVec
methods.doc:
MulVec returns the matrix-vector product m*v. It returns ErrDimensionMismatch
if len(v) differs from the number of columns.

methods.name: Plus
methods.doc: Plus returns the sum m+o. It returns ErrDimensionMismatch on a shape mismatch.

methods.name: Pow
methods.doc:
Pow returns m raised to the non-negative integer power p by repeated squaring.
It returns ErrNotSquare for a non-square matrix and ErrInvalidArgument for a
negative exponent.

methods.name: Row
methods.doc: Row returns a copy of row i.

methods.name: RowSums
methods.doc: RowSums returns the sum of each row.

methods.name: Rows
methods.doc: Rows returns the number of rows.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns a new matrix equal to s*m.

methods.name: Set
methods.signature: func (m *Matrix) Set(i, j int, v float64)
methods.doc:
Set assigns v to the entry in row i, column j. It panics if the index is out
of range.

methods.name: SetRow
methods.doc:
SetRow overwrites row i with the values in r. It returns ErrDimensionMismatch
if len(r) differs from the number of columns.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the matrix with each entry formatted using %g.

methods.name: SubMatrix
methods.doc:
SubMatrix returns the matrix obtained by deleting the given set of row indices
and column indices. Indices out of range are ignored.

methods.name: Symmetrize
methods.doc: Symmetrize returns (m + mᵀ)/2, the symmetric part of a square matrix.

methods.name: ToRows
methods.doc: ToRows returns the matrix as a fresh slice of row slices.

methods.name: Trace
methods.doc:
Trace returns the sum of the diagonal entries. It returns 0 for a non-square
matrix's shorter diagonal is used.

methods.name: Transpose
methods.doc: Transpose returns the transpose of the matrix.

funcs.name: AdjacencySpectrum
funcs.doc:
AdjacencySpectrum returns the eigenvalues of the adjacency matrix in ascending
order.

funcs.name: AlgebraicConnectivity
funcs.doc:
AlgebraicConnectivity returns the second-smallest eigenvalue of the Laplacian
(the Fiedler value). It is positive exactly when the graph is connected. It
returns ErrEmpty for graphs with fewer than two vertices.

funcs.name: ArgMax
funcs.doc:
ArgMax returns the index of the (first) maximum entry of v, or -1 if v is
empty.

funcs.name: ArgMin
funcs.doc:
ArgMin returns the index of the (first) minimum entry of v, or -1 if v is
empty.

funcs.name: BFSDistances
funcs.doc:
BFSDistances returns the number of edges on a shortest (unweighted) path from
source to every vertex, ignoring edge weights. Unreachable vertices are marked
with +Inf and the source with 0.

funcs.name: CheegerConstant
funcs.doc:
CheegerConstant returns the exact Cheeger (conductance) constant, the minimum
conductance over all nonempty proper vertex subsets, computed by brute force.
Because it enumerates all 2^n subsets it is intended for small graphs; it
returns ErrInvalidArgument when n exceeds 22.

funcs.name: CheegerLowerBound
funcs.doc:
CheegerLowerBound returns λ/2, the lower Cheeger bound on the conductance
constant, where λ is the second-smallest eigenvalue of the normalized
Laplacian. It satisfies λ/2 <= h(G).

funcs.name: CheegerUpperBound
funcs.doc:
CheegerUpperBound returns sqrt(2λ), the upper Cheeger bound on the conductance
constant, where λ is the second-smallest eigenvalue of the normalized
Laplacian. It satisfies h(G) <= sqrt(2λ).

funcs.name: ClosenessCentrality
funcs.doc:
ClosenessCentrality returns the closeness centrality of every vertex using
weighted shortest-path distances. For a vertex that reaches r-1 others the
value is (r-1)/Σd · (r-1)/(n-1), the Wasserman-Faust normalization that keeps
disconnected graphs meaningful. Isolated vertices score 0.

funcs.name: Conductance
funcs.doc:
Conductance returns the conductance of the cut (S, V\S):
|∂S| / min(vol(S), vol(V\S)). It returns 0 when S is empty or all of V, or
when the smaller side has zero volume.

funcs.name: CosineSimilarity
funcs.doc:
CosineSimilarity returns the cosine of the angle between a and b. It returns 0
if either vector is zero or the lengths differ.

funcs.name: CutSize
funcs.doc:
CutSize returns the total weight of edges crossing between the two blocks of a
bipartition given by part (entries 0 or 1).

funcs.name: DegreeCentrality
funcs.doc:
DegreeCentrality returns the degree centrality of every vertex: its weighted
degree divided by n-1, so that in an unweighted graph a vertex adjacent to all
others scores 1. For n < 2 the raw degrees are returned.

funcs.name: Determinant
funcs.doc:
Determinant returns the determinant of a square matrix, computed by Gaussian
elimination with partial pivoting. It returns ErrNotSquare for a non-square
matrix.

funcs.name: Diameter
funcs.doc:
Diameter returns the greatest weighted shortest-path distance between any two
vertices of a connected graph. It returns +Inf if the graph is disconnected.

funcs.name: DijkstraDistances
funcs.doc:
DijkstraDistances returns the weighted shortest-path distance from source to
every vertex, treating each edge weight as a positive length. Unreachable
vertices are marked with +Inf. Negative weights are not supported and yield
undefined results.

funcs.name: Dot
funcs.doc:
Dot returns the Euclidean inner product of a and b. It returns 0 when the
slices have different lengths.

funcs.name: EdgeBoundary
funcs.doc:
EdgeBoundary returns the total weight of edges with exactly one endpoint in
the vertex set S (given as a membership map). This is the size of the cut
(S, V\S).

funcs.name: EffectiveResistance
funcs.signature: func EffectiveResistance(g *Graph, s, t int) (float64, error)
funcs.doc:
EffectiveResistance returns the effective (electrical) resistance between
vertices s and t, treating each edge as a unit conductance times its weight.
It is computed as L⁺_ss + L⁺_tt - 2·L⁺_st.

funcs.name: Eigenvalues
funcs.doc: Eigenvalues returns the eigenvalues of a symmetric matrix in ascending order.

funcs.name: EigenvectorCentrality
funcs.signature: func EigenvectorCentrality(g *Graph, maxIter int, tol float64) ([]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
EigenvectorCentrality returns the eigenvector centrality: the (non-negative)
dominant eigenvector of the adjacency matrix, scaled to unit Euclidean norm.
It uses the power method with the given iteration budget and tolerance;
defaults of 1000 and 1e-12 apply when non-positive values are passed. For a
connected graph the Perron-Frobenius theorem guarantees a positive vector.

funcs.name: EstradaIndex
funcs.doc:
EstradaIndex returns the Estrada index Σ exp(λ_i) over the adjacency
eigenvalues.

funcs.name: FiedlerValue
funcs.doc: FiedlerValue is a synonym for [AlgebraicConnectivity].

funcs.name: FiedlerVector
funcs.doc:
FiedlerVector returns the eigenvector of the Laplacian associated with the
second-smallest eigenvalue. It returns ErrEmpty for graphs with fewer than two
vertices.

funcs.name: GraphEnergy
funcs.doc:
GraphEnergy returns the graph energy: the sum of the absolute values of the
adjacency eigenvalues.

funcs.name: HarmonicCentrality
funcs.doc:
HarmonicCentrality returns the harmonic centrality of every vertex,
Σ_{u≠v} 1/d(v,u) divided by n-1, using weighted shortest-path distances.
Unreachable vertices contribute nothing, so the measure is well defined on
disconnected graphs.

funcs.name: KMeans
funcs.doc:
KMeans runs Lloyd's algorithm on the given points (each a fixed-length
coordinate slice) with k clusters and at most maxIter iterations, returning a
cluster label in [0, k) for every point. Initial centres are chosen
deterministically by farthest-first traversal starting from the first point,
so the result is reproducible. It returns nil if k is not in [1, len(points)].

funcs.name: KatzCentrality
funcs.signature: func KatzCentrality(g *Graph, alpha, beta float64) ([]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
KatzCentrality returns the Katz centrality x = (I - α·A)^{-1}·β·1, solving the
corresponding linear system. For convergence of the underlying series α must
be smaller than the reciprocal of the spectral radius; the linear solve
itself only requires I - α·A to be nonsingular. It returns ErrInvalidArgument
for a non-positive beta.

funcs.name: KirchhoffIndex
funcs.doc:
KirchhoffIndex returns the Kirchhoff index: the sum of effective resistances
over all unordered vertex pairs, equal to n·Σ 1/μ_i over the nonzero Laplacian
eigenvalues.

funcs.name: LaplacianEnergy
funcs.doc:
LaplacianEnergy returns the Laplacian energy Σ |μ_i - 2m/n|, where the μ_i are
the Laplacian eigenvalues and 2m/n is the average (weighted) degree.

funcs.name: LaplacianSpectralGap
funcs.doc:
LaplacianSpectralGap returns the difference between the second-smallest and
smallest Laplacian eigenvalues. For a connected graph this equals the
algebraic connectivity.

funcs.name: LaplacianSpectrum
funcs.doc:
LaplacianSpectrum returns the eigenvalues of the combinatorial Laplacian in
ascending order. The smallest is always (numerically) zero.

funcs.name: Norm1
funcs.doc: Norm1 returns the L1 (taxicab) norm of v, the sum of absolute values.

funcs.name: Norm2
funcs.doc: Norm2 returns the Euclidean (L2) norm of v.

funcs.name: NormInf
funcs.doc: NormInf returns the L-infinity (maximum absolute value) norm of v.

funcs.name: Normalize
funcs.doc:
Normalize returns a new unit-length copy of v under the Euclidean norm. A zero
vector is returned unchanged (as a copy).

funcs.name: NormalizeL1
funcs.doc:
NormalizeL1 returns a new copy of v scaled so that its L1 norm is one. A vector
whose entries sum in absolute value to zero is returned unchanged.

funcs.name: NormalizedAlgebraicConnectivity
funcs.doc:
NormalizedAlgebraicConnectivity returns the second-smallest eigenvalue of the
normalized Laplacian, the spectral quantity appearing in the Cheeger
inequality.

funcs.name: NormalizedCut
funcs.doc:
NormalizedCut returns the normalized-cut value of a bipartition (part entries
0 or 1): cut(A,B)/vol(A) + cut(A,B)/vol(B).

funcs.name: NormalizedLaplacianSpectrum
funcs.doc:
NormalizedLaplacianSpectrum returns the eigenvalues of the symmetric
normalized Laplacian in ascending order. All lie in [0, 2].

funcs.name: NumberOfComponentsSpectral
funcs.doc:
NumberOfComponentsSpectral returns the number of connected components inferred
from the spectrum: the multiplicity of the zero eigenvalue of the Laplacian
(eigenvalues below tol are treated as zero). If tol <= 0 a default of 1e-8 is
used.

funcs.name: NumberOfSpanningTrees
funcs.doc:
NumberOfSpanningTrees returns [SpanningTreeCount] rounded to the nearest
integer, appropriate for unweighted graphs.

funcs.name: Ones
funcs.doc: Ones returns a new vector of length n whose entries are all one.

funcs.name: PageRank
funcs.signature: func PageRank(g *Graph, damping float64, maxIter int, tol float64) ([]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
PageRank returns the PageRank vector of the graph by power iteration on the
Google matrix with the given damping factor (typically 0.85). Dangling
vertices (degree 0) redistribute their mass uniformly. Defaults of maxIter =
1000 and tol = 1e-12 apply when non-positive values are given. The result is a
probability distribution summing to 1. It returns ErrInvalidArgument if
damping is not in [0, 1).

funcs.name: PersonalizedPageRank
funcs.signature: func PersonalizedPageRank(g *Graph, damping float64, restart []float64, maxIter int, tol float64) ([]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
PersonalizedPageRank returns PageRank with a custom teleportation
distribution: at each step the random surfer restarts according to restart
(which need not be normalized; it is normalized internally) instead of
uniformly. It returns ErrDimensionMismatch if len(restart) != n and
ErrInvalidArgument if damping is not in [0, 1) or restart sums to zero.

funcs.name: PowerIteration
funcs.signature: func PowerIteration(a *Matrix, maxIter int, tol float64) (float64, []float64, error)
funcs.doc:
PowerIteration estimates the dominant eigenpair (largest magnitude eigenvalue)
of a square matrix by the power method, starting from a uniform vector. It
returns the eigenvalue, its unit eigenvector, and ErrNoConvergence if the
Rayleigh quotient has not settled within maxIter iterations to tolerance tol.

funcs.name: Rank
funcs.doc:
Rank returns the numerical rank of a matrix: the number of pivots found during
Gaussian elimination whose magnitude exceeds tol.

funcs.name: RayleighQuotient
funcs.doc:
RayleighQuotient returns xᵀAx / xᵀx, an estimate of an eigenvalue associated
with the direction x. It returns 0 for a zero vector or a dimension mismatch.

funcs.name: SignlessLaplacianSpectrum
funcs.doc:
SignlessLaplacianSpectrum returns the eigenvalues of the signless Laplacian
Q = D + A in ascending order.

funcs.name: SolveLinear
funcs.doc:
SolveLinear solves the system A·x = b for a square matrix A, using Gaussian
elimination with partial pivoting. It returns ErrNotSquare, ErrDimensionMismatch
or ErrSingular as appropriate.

funcs.name: SortedFloats
funcs.doc: SortedFloats returns a sorted (ascending) copy of v.

funcs.name: SpanningTreeCount
funcs.doc:
SpanningTreeCount returns the (weighted) number of spanning trees via the
Matrix-Tree theorem: the determinant of the Laplacian with its last row and
column deleted. For an unweighted connected graph this is an integer. It
returns 0 for a disconnected graph and ErrEmpty for a graph with no vertices.

funcs.name: SpectralBisection
funcs.doc:
SpectralBisection partitions the vertices into two blocks using the sign of
the Fiedler vector: vertices with a negative Fiedler coordinate are labelled 0
and the rest are labelled 1. This is the classical spectral graph bisection.

funcs.name: SpectralBisectionMedian
funcs.doc:
SpectralBisectionMedian partitions the vertices into two balanced blocks by
thresholding the Fiedler vector at its median: the vertices whose Fiedler
coordinate is above the median form block 1, the rest block 0.

funcs.name: SpectralClustering
funcs.doc:
SpectralClustering partitions the vertices into k clusters by embedding each
vertex into the space spanned by the k eigenvectors of the normalized
Laplacian with the smallest eigenvalues, then running deterministic k-means
(Lloyd's algorithm with farthest-first seeding) on the embedded points. It
returns a label in [0, k) for every vertex. It returns ErrInvalidArgument if k
is not in [1, n].

funcs.name: SpectralGap
funcs.doc:
SpectralGap returns the adjacency spectral gap: the difference between the
largest and second-largest adjacency eigenvalues. It returns ErrEmpty for
graphs with fewer than two vertices.

funcs.name: SpectralOrdering
funcs.doc:
SpectralOrdering returns a permutation of the vertices sorted by their Fiedler
vector value in ascending order. Reordering a graph this way tends to place
well-connected vertices near one another.

funcs.name: SpectralRadius
funcs.doc: SpectralRadius returns the largest absolute eigenvalue of a symmetric matrix.

funcs.name: SpectralRadiusAdjacency
funcs.doc:
SpectralRadiusAdjacency returns the largest absolute adjacency eigenvalue,
also known as the spectral radius of the graph.

funcs.name: VecAXPY
funcs.doc:
VecAXPY returns a*x + y (a scalar times x, plus y). It returns nil on a length
mismatch.

funcs.name: VecAdd
funcs.doc: VecAdd returns the element-wise sum a+b. It returns nil on a length mismatch.

funcs.name: VecApproxEqual
funcs.signature: func VecApproxEqual(a, b []float64, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc:
VecApproxEqual reports whether a and b agree to within absolute tolerance tol
in every coordinate.

funcs.name: VecClone
funcs.doc: VecClone returns a copy of v.

funcs.name: VecDistance
funcs.doc:
VecDistance returns the Euclidean distance between a and b, or +Inf on a
length mismatch.

funcs.name: VecEqual
funcs.doc: VecEqual reports whether a and b are element-wise identical.

funcs.name: VecMax
funcs.doc: VecMax returns the maximum entry of v, or 0 for an empty slice.

funcs.name: VecMaxInt
funcs.doc: VecMaxInt returns the maximum entry of an int slice, or -1 for an empty slice.

funcs.name: VecMean
funcs.doc:
VecMean returns the arithmetic mean of the entries of v, or 0 for an empty
slice.

funcs.name: VecMin
funcs.doc: VecMin returns the minimum entry of v, or 0 for an empty slice.

funcs.name: VecScale
funcs.doc: VecScale returns a new vector equal to s*v.

funcs.name: VecSub
funcs.doc:
VecSub returns the element-wise difference a-b. It returns nil on a length
mismatch.

funcs.name: VecSum
funcs.doc: VecSum returns the sum of the entries of v.

funcs.name: Volume
funcs.doc: Volume returns the sum of the weighted degrees of the vertices in S.

funcs.name: Zeros
funcs.doc: Zeros returns a new zero vector of length n.

examples.name: NumberOfSpanningTrees
examples.code:
g := CompleteGraph(4)
trees, _ := NumberOfSpanningTrees(g)
ac, _ := AlgebraicConnectivity(g)
fmt.Printf("spanning trees: %d\n", trees)
fmt.Printf("algebraic connectivity: %.1f\n", ac)
examples.doc:
ExampleNumberOfSpanningTrees demonstrates the Matrix-Tree theorem: the
complete graph K4 has 4^(4-2) = 16 spanning trees by Cayley's formula.

packages.name: groups
packages.synopsis: Package groups is a self-contained finite-group and modular-arithmetic toolkit built entirely on the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package groups is a self-contained finite-group and modular-arithmetic
toolkit built entirely on the Go standard library.

It is a sibling subpackage of github.com/malcolmston/algebra but does not
depend on it: every routine works directly on machine integers, small
permutation slices, or explicit finite-group tables. All results are
deterministic and validated in the accompanying tests against closed-form
reference values.

# Contents

Integer gcd domain and the Euclidean algorithm: [Gcd], [ExtendedGcd], [Lcm],
[GcdMany], [LcmMany], [Coprime].

Modular ring Z/nZ: [Mod], [ModAdd], [ModSub], [ModMul], [ModNeg], [ModPow],
[ModInverse], [CyclicOrder], [ElementOrderZn], [MultiplicativeOrder],
[EulerTotient], [UnitsModN], [IsUnitModN], [IsPrimitiveRoot],
[PrimitiveRoots].

Prime field GF(p): [IsPrime], [GFAdd], [GFSub], [GFNeg], [GFMul], [GFInv],
[GFDiv], [GFPow].

Permutations and the symmetric/alternating groups: the [Perm] type with
[Identity], [Compose], [Perm.Inverse], [Perm.Order], [Perm.Sign],
[Perm.Cycles], [Perm.CycleType], [PermFromCycles], [Transposition],
[NumInversions], [SymmetricGroup], [AlternatingGroup],
[SymmetricGroupOrder], [AlternatingGroupOrder], [Factorial].

Dihedral groups: [DihedralElement] with [DihedralRotation],
[DihedralReflection], [DihedralIdentity], [DihedralCompose],
[DihedralInverse], [DihedralElementOrder], [DihedralOrder], [DihedralGroup].

Abstract finite groups (Cayley tables, subgroups, cosets): [FiniteGroup]
with [NewFiniteGroup], [CyclicGroupZn], [SymmetricGroupTable],
[FiniteGroup.CayleyTable], [FiniteGroup.Inverse], [FiniteGroup.ElementOrder],
[FiniteGroup.IsAbelian], [FiniteGroup.IsCyclic], [FiniteGroup.Center],
[FiniteGroup.GeneratedSubgroup], [FiniteGroup.IsSubgroup],
[FiniteGroup.LeftCoset], [FiniteGroup.RightCoset], [FiniteGroup.Cosets],
[FiniteGroup.Index], [FiniteGroup.IsValid].

Polynomials over a field, gcd domain via the Euclidean algorithm: the [Poly]
type over the rationals ([PolyAdd], [PolyMul], [PolyDivMod], [PolyGCD],
[PolyEval], ...) and [][]int polynomials over GF(p) ([PolyModAdd],
[PolyModMul], [PolyModDivMod], [PolyModGCD], [PolyModEval]).

# Conventions

Permutations are zero-based: a [Perm] p of length n is a bijection of
{0,1,...,n-1} with p[i] the image of i. Modulus arguments must be positive
and GF(p) routines require p to be prime; violating a documented
precondition panics rather than returning a silently wrong value.

types.name: DihedralElement
types.doc:
DihedralElement represents an element of a dihedral group D_n, the symmetry
group of a regular n-gon with 2n elements. Every element is either a
rotation r^k or a reflection s·r^k, encoded by the boolean Reflection and the
rotation exponent Rotation taken modulo n. The identity is
{Reflection: false, Rotation: 0}.

funcs.name: DihedralCompose
funcs.signature: func DihedralCompose(n int, a, b DihedralElement) DihedralElement
funcs.doc:
DihedralCompose returns the product a·b in D_n using the defining relations
r^n = s^2 = e and s·r = r^-1·s. Composition is applied left to right in the
usual group sense (a followed by b under the convention a·b). The argument n
must be positive.

funcs.name: DihedralGroup
funcs.doc:
DihedralGroup returns all 2n elements of D_n: the n rotations r^0..r^(n-1)
followed by the n reflections s·r^0..s·r^(n-1). The argument n must be
positive.

funcs.name: DihedralIdentity
funcs.doc:
