is used for exact integer lattice computations such as the Hermite normal
form and integer determinants.

funcs.name: IdentityIntMatrix
funcs.doc: IdentityIntMatrix returns the n-by-n integer identity matrix.

funcs.name: IntMatrixFromBasis
funcs.doc:
IntMatrixFromBasis converts a basis with integer entries to an IntMatrix. It
returns ErrBadParameter if any entry is not (within rounding) an integer.

funcs.name: NewIntMatrix
funcs.doc:
NewIntMatrix builds an integer matrix from int64 rows. Each row must have
equal length. It panics on a ragged input.

funcs.name: ZeroIntMatrix
funcs.doc: ZeroIntMatrix returns an r-by-c integer matrix of zeros.

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At returns a copy of the entry in row i and column j.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent deep copy of m.

methods.name: Cols
methods.doc: Cols returns the number of columns of m.

methods.name: Det
methods.doc:
Det returns the exact integer determinant of a square matrix using the
fraction-free Bareiss algorithm. It returns ErrNotSquare for a rectangular
matrix.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether m and n have the same shape and equal entries.

methods.name: HNFRank
methods.doc:
HNFRank returns the number of nonzero pivot rows in the Hermite normal form,
which equals the rank of the integer matrix.

methods.name: HermiteNormalForm
methods.doc:
HermiteNormalForm returns the row Hermite normal form of the integer matrix:
an upper-triangular row-echelon matrix H obtained from m by unimodular row
operations, with positive pivots and every entry above a pivot reduced into
the half-open interval [0, pivot). The lattice generated by the rows of m is
preserved.

methods.name: HermiteNormalFormWithTransform
methods.signature: func (m IntMatrix) HermiteNormalFormWithTransform() (h IntMatrix, u IntMatrix)
methods.doc:
HermiteNormalFormWithTransform returns the Hermite normal form H together with
a unimodular matrix U such that U*m = H.

methods.name: IsHermiteNormalForm
methods.doc:
IsHermiteNormalForm reports whether m is already in row Hermite normal form:
pivots occur in strictly increasing columns, every pivot is positive, entries
below pivots are zero and entries above a pivot lie in [0, pivot).

methods.name: IsSquare
methods.doc: IsSquare reports whether m has equal numbers of rows and columns.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the exact product m*n. It panics if the inner dimensions differ.

methods.name: Rat
methods.doc: Rat returns the exact rational version of m.

methods.name: Rows
methods.doc: Rows returns the number of rows of m.

methods.name: Set
methods.signature: func (m IntMatrix) Set(i, j int, x *big.Int)
methods.doc: Set stores a copy of x in row i and column j.

methods.name: SetInt64
methods.signature: func (m IntMatrix) SetInt64(i, j int, x int64)
methods.doc: SetInt64 stores the int64 value x in row i and column j.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders m with one row per line.

methods.name: Transpose
methods.doc: Transpose returns the transpose of m.

types.name: Matrix
types.doc: Matrix is a dense real matrix stored row-major with float64 entries.

funcs.name: DiagMatrix
funcs.doc: DiagMatrix returns the square matrix with the given diagonal entries.

funcs.name: IdentityMatrix
funcs.doc: IdentityMatrix returns the n-by-n identity matrix.

funcs.name: NewMatrix
funcs.doc:
NewMatrix builds a rows-by-cols matrix from the given row slices. Each row
must have length cols. It panics if the shape is inconsistent.

funcs.name: ZeroMatrix
funcs.doc: ZeroMatrix returns an r-by-c matrix of zeros.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns m+n. It panics if the shapes differ.

methods.name: ApproxEqual
methods.signature: func (m Matrix) ApproxEqual(n Matrix, tol float64) bool
methods.doc:
ApproxEqual reports whether m and n have the same shape and every entry
agrees within the absolute tolerance tol.

methods.name: At
methods.signature: func (m Matrix) At(i, j int) float64
methods.doc: At returns the entry in row i and column j.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent copy of m.

methods.name: Col
methods.doc: Col returns a copy of column j as a Vec.

methods.name: Cols
methods.doc: Cols returns the number of columns of m.

methods.name: Det
methods.doc:
Det returns the determinant of a square matrix via LU decomposition with
partial pivoting. It returns ErrNotSquare for a rectangular matrix.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether m and n have the same shape and entries.

methods.name: Inverse
methods.doc:
Inverse returns the inverse of a square matrix by Gauss-Jordan elimination
with partial pivoting. It returns ErrNotSquare for a rectangular matrix and
ErrSingular when the matrix is (numerically) singular.

methods.name: IsSquare
methods.doc: IsSquare reports whether m has equal numbers of rows and columns.

methods.name: IsSymmetric
methods.doc:
IsSymmetric reports whether m is square and equal to its transpose within the
tolerance tol.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc:
Mul returns the matrix product m*n. It panics if the inner dimensions do not
agree.

methods.name: MulVec
methods.doc:
MulVec returns the matrix-vector product m*v. It panics if the dimensions do
not agree.

methods.name: Rank
methods.doc:
Rank returns the numerical rank of m computed by Gaussian elimination, using
tol as the threshold below which a pivot is treated as zero.

methods.name: Row
methods.doc: Row returns a copy of row i as a Vec.

methods.name: Rows
methods.doc: Rows returns the number of rows of m.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the scalar multiple s*m.

methods.name: Set
methods.signature: func (m Matrix) Set(i, j int, x float64)
methods.doc: Set stores x in row i and column j.

methods.name: Solve
methods.doc:
Solve returns the solution x of m*x = b for a square matrix m using Gaussian
elimination with partial pivoting. It returns ErrNotSquare, ErrDimMismatch or
ErrSingular as appropriate.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders m with one row per line.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns m-n. It panics if the shapes differ.

methods.name: Trace
methods.doc:
Trace returns the sum of the diagonal entries of a square matrix. It returns
ErrNotSquare for a rectangular matrix.

methods.name: Transpose
methods.doc: Transpose returns the transpose of m.

types.name: RatMatrix
types.doc: RatMatrix is a dense matrix of exact rationals stored row-major.

funcs.name: IdentityRatMatrix
funcs.doc: IdentityRatMatrix returns the n-by-n rational identity matrix.

funcs.name: NewRatMatrix
funcs.doc:
NewRatMatrix builds a rows-by-cols rational matrix from integer rows. Each
row must have equal length. It panics on a ragged input.

funcs.name: RatMatrixFromMatrix
funcs.doc: RatMatrixFromMatrix converts a float64 Matrix to an exact RatMatrix.

funcs.name: ZeroRatMatrix
funcs.doc: ZeroRatMatrix returns an r-by-c rational matrix of zeros.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the exact sum m+n. It panics if the shapes differ.

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At returns a copy of the entry in row i and column j.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent deep copy of m.

methods.name: Cols
methods.doc: Cols returns the number of columns of m.

methods.name: Det
methods.doc:
Det returns the exact determinant of a square rational matrix by fraction
(exact) Gaussian elimination. It returns ErrNotSquare for a rectangular
matrix.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether m and n have the same shape and exactly equal entries.

methods.name: Float
methods.doc: Float returns the float64 approximation of m as a Matrix.

methods.name: Inverse
methods.doc:
Inverse returns the exact inverse of a square rational matrix by Gauss-Jordan
elimination. It returns ErrNotSquare or ErrSingular as appropriate.

methods.name: IsSquare
methods.doc: IsSquare reports whether m has equal numbers of rows and columns.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the exact product m*n. It panics if the inner dimensions differ.

methods.name: Rows
methods.doc: Rows returns the number of rows of m.

methods.name: Set
methods.signature: func (m RatMatrix) Set(i, j int, x *big.Rat)
methods.doc: Set stores a copy of x in row i and column j.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders m with one row per line using rational entries.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the exact difference m-n. It panics if the shapes differ.

methods.name: Transpose
methods.doc: Transpose returns the transpose of m.

types.name: RatVec
types.signature: type RatVec []*big.Rat
types.doc:
RatVec is an exact rational vector represented as a slice of *big.Rat
coordinates. It is used where exactness matters, such as exact Gram
determinants and dual bases.

funcs.name: NewRatVec
funcs.doc:
NewRatVec returns an n-dimensional rational zero vector with freshly
allocated entries.

funcs.name: RatVecFromFloats
funcs.doc:
RatVecFromFloats builds a RatVec from float64 coordinates, converting each
exactly to a rational. Non-finite inputs yield a zero entry.

funcs.name: RatVecFromInts
funcs.doc: RatVecFromInts builds a RatVec from integer coordinates.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the exact sum v+w. It panics if the dimensions differ.

methods.name: AddScaled
methods.signature: func (v RatVec) AddScaled(w RatVec, s *big.Rat) RatVec
methods.doc:
AddScaled returns the exact combination v + s*w. It panics if the dimensions
differ.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent deep copy of v.

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc: Dim returns the number of coordinates in v.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc:
Dot returns the exact inner product <v, w>. It panics if the dimensions
differ.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether v and w have identical dimension and coordinates.

methods.name: Float
methods.doc: Float returns the float64 approximation of v as a Vec.

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc: IsZero reports whether every coordinate of v is exactly zero.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns -v.

methods.name: Norm2
methods.doc: Norm2 returns the exact squared Euclidean norm <v, v>.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the exact scalar multiple s*v.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders v as a bracketed, space-separated list of rationals.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the exact difference v-w. It panics if the dimensions differ.

types.name: Vec
types.signature: type Vec []float64
types.doc:
Vec is a real vector represented as a slice of float64 coordinates. It is the
numeric vector type used by the reduction and enumeration routines.

funcs.name: Combine
funcs.doc:
Combine returns the linear combination sum_i coeffs[i]*vecs[i]. It panics if
coeffs and vecs have different lengths, and returns nil for an empty input.

funcs.name: IntCombine
funcs.doc:
IntCombine returns the integer linear combination sum_i coeffs[i]*vecs[i]. It
panics if the lengths differ and returns nil for an empty input.

funcs.name: NewVec
funcs.doc: NewVec returns a Vec with the given coordinates.

funcs.name: UnitVec
funcs.doc:
UnitVec returns the n-dimensional standard basis vector e_i (a 1 in position
i and zeros elsewhere). It panics if i is out of range.

funcs.name: VecFromInts
funcs.doc: VecFromInts builds a Vec from integer coordinates.

funcs.name: ZeroVec
funcs.doc: ZeroVec returns the n-dimensional zero vector.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the sum v+w. It panics if the dimensions differ.

methods.name: AddScaled
methods.signature: func (v Vec) AddScaled(w Vec, s float64) Vec
methods.doc: AddScaled returns v + s*w. It panics if the dimensions differ.

methods.name: Angle
methods.doc: Angle returns the angle in radians between v and w in the range [0, pi].

methods.name: ApproxEqual
methods.signature: func (v Vec) ApproxEqual(w Vec, tol float64) bool
methods.doc:
ApproxEqual reports whether v and w have the same dimension and every
coordinate agrees within the absolute tolerance tol.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent copy of v.

methods.name: Cosine
methods.doc:
Cosine returns the cosine of the angle between v and w. It returns 0 when
either vector is zero.

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc: Dim returns the number of coordinates in v.

methods.name: Dist
methods.doc: Dist returns the Euclidean distance between v and w.

methods.name: Dist2
methods.doc: Dist2 returns the squared Euclidean distance between v and w.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc:
Dot returns the Euclidean inner product <v, w>. It panics if the dimensions
differ.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether v and w have identical dimensions and coordinates.

methods.name: IsOrthogonal
methods.signature: func (v Vec) IsOrthogonal(w Vec, tol float64) bool
methods.doc: IsOrthogonal reports whether <v, w> is zero within the tolerance tol.

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc: IsZero reports whether every coordinate of v is exactly zero.

methods.name: L1
methods.doc: L1 returns the L1 (taxicab) norm of v, the sum of absolute values.

methods.name: LInf
methods.doc: LInf returns the L-infinity (maximum absolute value) norm of v.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns -v.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean (L2) norm of v.

methods.name: Norm2
methods.doc: Norm2 returns the squared Euclidean norm <v, v>.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns the unit vector in the direction of v. It returns a copy of
v (the zero vector) when v has zero norm.

methods.name: Proj
methods.doc:
Proj returns the orthogonal projection of v onto the line spanned by w. It
returns the zero vector when w is the zero vector.

methods.name: ProjCoeff
methods.doc:
ProjCoeff returns the scalar <v, w>/<w, w>, the coefficient of the orthogonal
projection of v onto w. It returns 0 when w is the zero vector.

methods.name: Reject
methods.doc:
Reject returns the component of v orthogonal to w, that is v minus its
projection onto w.

methods.name: Round
methods.doc:
Round returns the vector obtained by rounding each coordinate of v to the
nearest integer (halves away from zero).

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the scalar multiple s*v.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders v as a bracketed, space-separated list of coordinates.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the difference v-w. It panics if the dimensions differ.

methods.name: Sum
methods.doc: Sum returns the sum of the coordinates of v.

funcs.name: BallVolume
funcs.signature: func BallVolume(n int, r float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BallVolume returns the volume of the n-dimensional Euclidean ball of radius r,
namely pi^(n/2)/Gamma(n/2+1) * r^n.

funcs.name: GaussianHeuristic
funcs.signature: func GaussianHeuristic(n int, det float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GaussianHeuristic returns the Gaussian heuristic estimate of the first
minimum, (det / V_n)^(1/n), the expected shortest length for a random lattice
of the given rank and determinant.

funcs.name: HermiteBound
funcs.signature: func HermiteBound(n int, det float64) float64
funcs.doc:
HermiteBound returns the Hermite bound sqrt(gamma_n) * det^(1/n) on the first
minimum lambda_1 of a rank-n lattice with the given determinant.

funcs.name: HermiteConstant
funcs.doc:
HermiteConstant returns Hermite's constant gamma_n. For 1 <= n <= 8 the exact
value is returned; for larger n it returns Minkowski's upper bound (see
HermiteConstantUpperBound), which is an over-estimate of the true constant.

funcs.name: HermiteConstantLowerBound
funcs.doc:
HermiteConstantLowerBound returns a Minkowski-Hlawka style lower bound on
Hermite's constant, gamma_n >= (n/(2*pi*e)) for large n; it is only a rough
asymptotic estimate for small n.

funcs.name: HermiteConstantUpperBound
funcs.doc:
HermiteConstantUpperBound returns Minkowski's upper bound on Hermite's
constant, gamma_n <= (2/pi) * Gamma(2 + n/2)^(2/n).

funcs.name: MinkowskiBound
funcs.signature: func MinkowskiBound(n int, det float64) float64
funcs.doc:
MinkowskiBound returns the upper bound on the first minimum lambda_1 given by
Minkowski's convex body theorem for a lattice of the given rank and
determinant: lambda_1 <= 2 * (det / V_n)^(1/n), where V_n is the unit ball
volume.

funcs.name: MinkowskiSecondLower
funcs.signature: func MinkowskiSecondLower(n int, det float64) float64
funcs.doc:
MinkowskiSecondLower returns the lower bound of Minkowski's second theorem on
the product of the successive minima: prod_i lambda_i >= 2^n * det / (n! V_n).

funcs.name: MinkowskiSecondUpper
funcs.signature: func MinkowskiSecondUpper(n int, det float64) float64
funcs.doc:
MinkowskiSecondUpper returns the upper bound of Minkowski's second theorem on
the product of the successive minima: prod_i lambda_i <= 2^n * det / V_n.

funcs.name: RankinBound
funcs.doc:
RankinBound returns Rankin's bound gamma_{n,m} <= gamma_n^m used to bound the
minimum determinant of an m-dimensional sublattice; here it returns
gamma_n^m as a convenience.

funcs.name: SameLattice
funcs.doc:
SameLattice reports whether two integer bases of the same ambient dimension
generate the same lattice, by comparing their Hermite normal forms. It
returns ErrBadParameter if either basis has non-integer entries.

funcs.name: UnitBallVolume
funcs.doc:
UnitBallVolume returns the volume of the n-dimensional unit ball,
pi^(n/2)/Gamma(n/2+1).

examples.name: Basis (LLL)
examples.code:
b := NewBasis(
	NewVec(1, 1, 1),
	NewVec(-1, 0, 2),
	NewVec(3, 5, 6),
)
red, _ := b.LLL(0.75)
fmt.Println(red.IsLLLReduced(0.75, 1e-9))
fmt.Printf("%.0f\n", red.Determinant())
examples.doc:
ExampleBasis_LLL demonstrates LLL reduction shrinking a skewed basis while
preserving the lattice determinant.

examples.name: Basis (ShortestVector)
examples.code:
b := NewBasis(NewVec(1, 1), NewVec(2, 0))
res, _ := b.ShortestVector()
fmt.Printf("%.6f\n", res.Norm())
examples.doc:
ExampleBasis_ShortestVector finds the shortest nonzero vector of the D2
checkerboard lattice, whose length is sqrt(2).

packages.name: liealgebra
packages.synopsis: Package liealgebra implements computational tools for Lie algebras and root systems using only the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package liealgebra implements computational tools for Lie algebras and root
systems using only the Go standard library.

The package is organised around a handful of small, self-contained numeric
types and a large collection of genuinely distinct operations on them:

  - Dense real and complex matrices ([Matrix], [CMatrix]) with the arithmetic
    needed for matrix Lie algebras: addition, multiplication, transpose,
    conjugate transpose, trace, Frobenius norm, LU based determinant, inverse
    and linear solve.

  - The Lie bracket (commutator) [Bracket] and anticommutator, the adjoint
    action [AdjointAction], the Jacobi identity residual and test
    ([JacobiResidual], [SatisfiesJacobi]), structure constants
    ([StructureConstants]), the Killing form ([KillingForm], [KillingFormValue])
    and the trace form, together with semisimplicity tests.

  - Concrete generators of the low dimensional Lie algebras: sl(2) with its
    standard E, F, H basis ([SL2Generators]), so(3) rotation generators
    ([SO3Generators]), su(2) via the Pauli matrices ([SU2Generators],
    [PauliMatrices]), su(3) via the Gell-Mann matrices ([GellMannMatrices]),
    and arbitrary spin-j representations ([SpinMatrices]).

  - The exponential map for matrices ([MatExp], [CMatExp]) implemented with
    scaling and squaring and a diagonal Padé approximant, plus integer powers
    and the leading Baker-Campbell-Hausdorff terms.

  - Root system combinatorics for the classical families A, B, C and D and
    the exceptional types: Cartan matrices ([CartanMatrix]), simple and
    positive roots ([SimpleRoots], [PositiveRoots]), fundamental weights,
    Weyl group orders ([WeylGroupOrder]), Coxeter numbers, Dynkin diagram
    adjacency, Weyl reflections and the Weyl dimension and Casimir formulas.

All computation is deterministic. Operations that can fail (dimension
mismatches, singular systems, unknown Dynkin types) return a sentinel error
such as [ErrDim], [ErrSingular] or [ErrType]; a few pure accessors panic on
out-of-range indices.

vars.doc: Sentinel errors returned throughout the package.

types.name: CMatrix
types.doc:
CMatrix is a dense complex matrix stored in row-major order. Complex matrices
are needed for the unitary Lie algebras su(n), whose generators are naturally
complex. The zero value is not usable; construct with [NewCMatrix].

funcs.name: CAnticommutator
funcs.doc: CAnticommutator returns {A,B} = AB + BA of two complex matrices.

funcs.name: CBracket
funcs.doc: CBracket returns the Lie bracket [A,B] = AB - BA of two complex matrices.

funcs.name: CMatExp
funcs.doc:
CMatExp returns the matrix exponential exp(A) of a square complex matrix by
scaling and squaring with a truncated Taylor series.

funcs.name: CMatPow
funcs.doc: CMatPow returns a complex matrix raised to a nonnegative integer power.

funcs.name: DiagCMatrix
funcs.doc: DiagCMatrix returns the square complex matrix with the given diagonal.

funcs.name: GellMannMatrices
funcs.doc: GellMannMatrices returns all eight Gell-Mann matrices λ_1..λ_8.

funcs.name: GellMannMatrix
funcs.doc:
GellMannMatrix returns the i-th Gell-Mann matrix, i in 1..8. These are the
eight Hermitian traceless generators of su(3). It panics if i is out of range.

funcs.name: IdentityCMatrix
funcs.doc: IdentityCMatrix returns the n-by-n complex identity matrix.

funcs.name: NewCMatrix
funcs.doc: NewCMatrix returns a rows-by-cols zero complex matrix.

funcs.name: NewCMatrixFromRows
funcs.doc:
NewCMatrixFromRows builds a complex matrix from equal-length rows, returning
[ErrDim] if the rows are ragged.

funcs.name: PauliMatrices
funcs.doc:
PauliMatrices returns the three Pauli matrices (σ_x, σ_y, σ_z). They are
Hermitian, traceless, unitary and satisfy σ_iσ_j = δ_ij I + i ε_ijk σ_k.

funcs.name: PauliX
funcs.doc: PauliX returns the Pauli matrix σ_x = [[0,1],[1,0]].

funcs.name: PauliY
funcs.doc: PauliY returns the Pauli matrix σ_y = [[0,-i],[i,0]].

funcs.name: PauliZ
funcs.doc: PauliZ returns the Pauli matrix σ_z = [[1,0],[0,-1]].

funcs.name: RealToComplex
funcs.doc: RealToComplex promotes a real matrix to a complex matrix.

funcs.name: SU2Generators
funcs.doc:
SU2Generators returns the anti-Hermitian generators of su(2) given by
T_i = -i σ_i / 2, satisfying [T_i, T_j] = ε_ijk T_k. These are the generators
for which the exponential map lands in SU(2).

funcs.name: SU2SpinMatrices
funcs.doc:
SU2SpinMatrices returns the Hermitian spin-1/2 operators (S_x, S_y, S_z) =
(σ_x, σ_y, σ_z)/2 satisfying [S_i,S_j] = i ε_ijk S_k.

funcs.name: SU3Generators
funcs.doc:
SU3Generators returns the anti-Hermitian su(3) generators T_a = -i λ_a / 2
for a in 1..8.

funcs.name: SpinJz
funcs.doc: SpinJz returns the diagonal Jz operator for spin j.

funcs.name: SpinLowering
funcs.doc: SpinLowering returns the lowering operator J- for spin j.

funcs.name: SpinMatrices
funcs.signature: func SpinMatrices(j float64) (jx, jy, jz *CMatrix, err error)
funcs.doc:
SpinMatrices returns the (2j+1)-dimensional Hermitian angular-momentum
operators (Jx, Jy, Jz) for spin/quantum number j (a nonnegative half-integer
such as 0.5, 1, 1.5, ...). They satisfy [Ji,Jj] = i ε_ijk Jk. It returns
[ErrRange] if 2j is not a nonnegative integer.

funcs.name: SpinRaising
funcs.doc: SpinRaising returns the raising operator J+ for spin j.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns m+b or [ErrDim].

methods.name: AntiHermitianPart
methods.doc: AntiHermitianPart returns (m-mᴴ)/2, the anti-Hermitian part.

methods.name: ApproxEqual
methods.doc:
ApproxEqual reports shape equality with every entry agreeing to within tol in
complex modulus.

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At returns element (i,j), panicking on an out-of-range index.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns a deep copy.

methods.name: Conjugate
methods.doc: Conjugate returns the entrywise complex conjugate.

methods.name: ConjugateTranspose
methods.doc: ConjugateTranspose returns the Hermitian conjugate (dagger) mᴴ.

methods.name: Dagger
methods.doc: Dagger is an alias for [CMatrix.ConjugateTranspose].

methods.name: Dims
methods.doc: Dims returns the number of rows and columns.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports exact equality of shape and entries.

methods.name: FrobeniusNorm
methods.doc: FrobeniusNorm returns the square root of the sum of squared moduli.

methods.name: HermitianPart
methods.doc: HermitianPart returns (m+mᴴ)/2, the Hermitian part of a square complex matrix.

methods.name: Imag
methods.doc: Imag returns the imaginary part of each entry as a real matrix.

methods.name: IsAntiHermitian
methods.doc:
IsAntiHermitian reports whether m equals the negative of its conjugate
transpose within tol (a skew-Hermitian matrix, as in su(n)).

methods.name: IsHermitian
methods.doc: IsHermitian reports whether m equals its conjugate transpose within tol.

methods.name: IsSquare
methods.doc: IsSquare reports whether the matrix is square.

methods.name: IsTraceless
methods.doc: IsTraceless reports whether the trace is zero within tol.

methods.name: IsUnitary
methods.doc: IsUnitary reports whether mᴴm is the identity within tol.

methods.name: Kronecker
methods.doc: Kronecker returns the Kronecker (tensor) product m ⊗ b of complex matrices.

methods.name: MaxAbs
methods.doc: MaxAbs returns the largest entry modulus.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the matrix product m*b or [ErrDim].

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns the additive inverse.

methods.name: Real
methods.doc: Real returns the real part of each entry as a real matrix.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the matrix times the complex scalar s.

methods.name: Set
methods.signature: func (m *CMatrix) Set(i, j int, v complex128)
methods.doc: Set assigns v to element (i,j), panicking on an out-of-range index.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns m-b or [ErrDim].

methods.name: Trace
methods.doc: Trace returns the sum of diagonal entries, or [ErrNotSquare].

methods.name: Transpose
methods.doc: Transpose returns the (unconjugated) transpose.

types.name: Matrix
types.doc:
Matrix is a dense real matrix stored in row-major order. The zero value is
not usable; construct matrices with [NewMatrix] and the related helpers.

funcs.name: AdjointAction
funcs.doc:
AdjointAction returns ad_X(Y) = [X,Y], the action of X in the adjoint
representation on the matrix Y.

funcs.name: AdjointMatrix
funcs.doc:
AdjointMatrix returns the matrix of ad_{e_index} in the given basis: the
d-by-d matrix M with M[k][j] = c[k][index][j] where c are the structure
constants. Column j is the coordinate vector of [e_index, e_j].

funcs.name: Anticommutator
funcs.doc: Anticommutator returns {A,B} = AB + BA of two square real matrices.

funcs.name: BCHApprox
funcs.doc:
BCHApprox returns the second-order Baker-Campbell-Hausdorff approximation to
log(exp(X)exp(Y)):

	X + Y + ½[X,Y] + (1/12)([X,[X,Y]] + [Y,[Y,X]]).

It is exact through third order in X and Y and is the standard truncation for
small elements of a matrix Lie algebra.

funcs.name: Bracket
funcs.doc:
Bracket returns the Lie bracket (commutator) [A,B] = AB - BA of two square
real matrices. It returns [ErrDim] if the shapes are incompatible.

funcs.name: CartanMatrix
funcs.signature: func CartanMatrix(family string, rank int) (*Matrix, error)
funcs.doc:
CartanMatrix returns the Cartan matrix of the Lie algebra of the given Dynkin
type. The family is one of "A","B","C","D" (classical) or "E","F","G"
(exceptional); rank must be admissible. Entry A[i][j] equals
2(α_i,α_j)/(α_j,α_j) in the Bourbaki convention.

funcs.name: CartanMatrixA
funcs.doc: CartanMatrixA returns the Cartan matrix of type A_n (the Lie algebra sl(n+1)).

funcs.name: CartanMatrixB
funcs.doc: CartanMatrixB returns the Cartan matrix of type B_n (the Lie algebra so(2n+1)).

funcs.name: CartanMatrixC
funcs.doc: CartanMatrixC returns the Cartan matrix of type C_n (the Lie algebra sp(2n)).

funcs.name: CartanMatrixD
funcs.doc: CartanMatrixD returns the Cartan matrix of type D_n (the Lie algebra so(2n)).

funcs.name: CartanMatrixFromRoots
funcs.doc:
CartanMatrixFromRoots builds the Cartan matrix A[i][j] = 2(α_i,α_j)/(α_j,α_j)
from a list of simple roots given as Euclidean coordinate vectors. It returns
[ErrDim] if any root is zero or the coordinate lengths disagree.

funcs.name: DiagMatrix
funcs.doc: DiagMatrix returns the square matrix with the given diagonal entries.

funcs.name: DynkinBondMatrix
funcs.signature: func DynkinBondMatrix(family string, rank int) (*Matrix, error)
funcs.doc:
DynkinBondMatrix returns the matrix B[i][j] = A[i][j]*A[j][i], whose value
(0,1,2,3) counts the number of bonds between nodes i and j in the Dynkin
diagram.

funcs.name: DynkinDiagramAdjacency
funcs.signature: func DynkinDiagramAdjacency(family string, rank int) (*Matrix, error)
funcs.doc:
DynkinDiagramAdjacency returns the 0/1 adjacency matrix of the Dynkin diagram:
nodes i and j are adjacent when the Cartan entry A[i][j] is nonzero.

funcs.name: ExpBracketSeries
funcs.signature: func ExpBracketSeries(x, y *Matrix, terms int) (*Matrix, error)
funcs.doc:
ExpBracketSeries returns the truncated series for Ad_{exp(X)}(Y) =
exp(ad_X)(Y) = Σ_{k≥0} (1/k!) ad_X^k(Y), summed to the given number of terms
(terms>=1). This is the finite version of the identity exp(X)Y exp(-X).

funcs.name: ExpMap
funcs.doc:
ExpMap is an alias for [MatExp]; for a Lie-algebra element X it returns the
corresponding group element exp(X).

funcs.name: HeisenbergGenerators
funcs.doc:
HeisenbergGenerators returns the three matrices (X, Y, Z) generating the
3-dimensional Heisenberg Lie algebra of strictly upper-triangular 3x3 real
matrices, satisfying [X,Y]=Z and [X,Z]=[Y,Z]=0.

funcs.name: IdentityMatrix
funcs.doc: IdentityMatrix returns the n-by-n identity matrix.

funcs.name: Inverse
funcs.doc:
Inverse returns the matrix inverse, or [ErrSingular] if the matrix is not
invertible.

funcs.name: InverseCartanMatrix
funcs.signature: func InverseCartanMatrix(family string, rank int) (*Matrix, error)
funcs.doc:
InverseCartanMatrix returns the inverse of the Cartan matrix. Its rows give
the fundamental weights expressed in the basis of simple roots.

funcs.name: JacobiResidual
funcs.doc:
JacobiResidual returns [[A,B],C] + [[B,C],A] + [[C,A],B], which is identically
the zero matrix for any associative product and hence for matrix Lie
algebras. It is a convenient numerical check of the Jacobi identity.

funcs.name: KillingForm
funcs.doc:
KillingForm returns the Killing form matrix K of a Lie algebra given by a
basis, with entries K[i][j] = tr(ad_{e_i} ad_{e_j}). The result is symmetric.

funcs.name: MatExp
funcs.doc:
MatExp returns the matrix exponential exp(A) of a square real matrix, computed
by scaling and squaring with a truncated Taylor series. It returns
[ErrNotSquare] for non-square input.

funcs.name: MatPow
funcs.doc:
MatPow returns A raised to a nonnegative integer power using exponentiation by
squaring; A^0 is the identity. It returns [ErrNotSquare] for non-square input
and [ErrRange] for a negative power.

funcs.name: NestedBracket
funcs.doc:
NestedBracket returns the left-nested bracket [x0,[x1,[...,xk]...]] for a list
of matrices. An empty list returns [ErrDim]; a single element returns it.

funcs.name: NewMatrix
funcs.doc: NewMatrix returns a rows-by-cols zero matrix.

funcs.name: NewMatrixFromRows
funcs.doc:
NewMatrixFromRows builds a matrix from a slice of equal-length rows.
It returns [ErrDim] if the rows are ragged.

funcs.name: RootBilinearForm
funcs.signature: func RootBilinearForm(family string, rank int) (*Matrix, error)
funcs.doc:
RootBilinearForm returns the symmetric matrix B with B[i][j] = (α_i,α_j), the
inner products of the simple roots normalised so that the longest roots have
squared length 2. It is derived from the Cartan matrix and underlies the
Weyl dimension and Casimir formulas.

funcs.name: SL2CartanH
funcs.doc: SL2CartanH returns the Cartan generator H of sl(2).

funcs.name: SL2Generators
funcs.doc:
SL2Generators returns the standard basis (E, F, H) of the Lie algebra sl(2)
of traceless 2x2 real matrices, satisfying [H,E]=2E, [H,F]=-2F and [E,F]=H.

	E = [[0,1],[0,0]], F = [[0,0],[1,0]], H = [[1,0],[0,-1]].

funcs.name: SL2LoweringF
funcs.doc: SL2LoweringF returns the lowering generator F of sl(2).

funcs.name: SL2RaisingE
funcs.doc: SL2RaisingE returns the raising generator E of sl(2).

funcs.name: SO3FromVector
funcs.doc:
SO3FromVector returns the 3x3 antisymmetric matrix [w]_× representing the
cross product w×(·). It returns [ErrDim] unless w has length 3.

funcs.name: SO3Generators
funcs.doc:
SO3Generators returns the three real antisymmetric generators (Lx, Ly, Lz) of
the rotation algebra so(3), satisfying [Lx,Ly]=Lz and cyclic permutations.
Each Li is (L_i)_{jk} = -ε_{ijk}.

funcs.name: WeylReflectionMatrix
funcs.doc:
WeylReflectionMatrix returns the orthogonal matrix of the reflection across
the hyperplane orthogonal to the given root in Euclidean coordinates.

funcs.name: ZeroMatrix
funcs.doc: ZeroMatrix returns an n-by-n zero matrix.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns m+b or [ErrDim] on a shape mismatch.

methods.name: AntiSymmetrize
methods.doc: AntiSymmetrize returns (m-mᵀ)/2, the antisymmetric part of a square matrix.

methods.name: ApproxEqual
methods.doc:
ApproxEqual reports whether a and b have the same shape and every entry
agrees to within absolute tolerance tol.

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At returns element (i,j). It panics if the index is out of range.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns a deep copy of the matrix.

methods.name: Col
methods.doc: Col returns a copy of column j.

methods.name: Dims
methods.doc: Dims returns the number of rows and columns.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether a and b have the same shape and identical entries.

methods.name: FrobeniusNorm
methods.doc: FrobeniusNorm returns the square root of the sum of squared entries.

methods.name: IsAntisymmetric
methods.doc:
IsAntisymmetric reports whether m equals the negative of its transpose within
tolerance tol (a real antisymmetric/skew matrix).

methods.name: IsDiagonal
methods.doc: IsDiagonal reports whether all off-diagonal entries vanish within tol.

methods.name: IsSquare
methods.doc: IsSquare reports whether the matrix has equal row and column counts.

methods.name: IsSymmetric
methods.doc: IsSymmetric reports whether m equals its transpose within tolerance tol.

methods.name: IsTraceless
methods.doc: IsTraceless reports whether the trace is zero within tolerance tol.

methods.name: Kronecker
methods.doc: Kronecker returns the Kronecker (tensor) product m ⊗ b.

methods.name: MatVec
methods.doc: MatVec returns the matrix-vector product m*x or [ErrDim] on a mismatch.

methods.name: MaxAbs
methods.doc: MaxAbs returns the largest absolute entry, or 0 for an empty matrix.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc:
Mul returns the matrix product m*b or [ErrDim] if the inner dimensions
disagree.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns the additive inverse of the matrix.

methods.name: Row
methods.doc: Row returns a copy of row i.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the matrix multiplied by the scalar s.

methods.name: Set
methods.signature: func (m *Matrix) Set(i, j int, v float64)
methods.doc: Set assigns v to element (i,j). It panics if the index is out of range.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns m-b or [ErrDim] on a shape mismatch.

methods.name: Symmetrize
methods.doc: Symmetrize returns (m+mᵀ)/2, the symmetric part of a square matrix.

methods.name: Trace
methods.doc:
Trace returns the sum of the diagonal entries. It returns [ErrNotSquare] for
a non-square matrix.

methods.name: Transpose
methods.doc: Transpose returns the transpose of the matrix.

methods.name: Vec
methods.doc: Vec flattens the matrix into a single row-major slice (a fresh copy).

funcs.name: AdjointDimension
funcs.signature: func AdjointDimension(family string, rank int) (int, error)
funcs.doc:
AdjointDimension returns the dimension of the adjoint representation, which
equals the dimension of the Lie algebra itself.

funcs.name: AllRoots
funcs.signature: func AllRoots(family string, rank int) ([][]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
AllRoots returns all roots (positive together with their negatives) of a
classical type as Euclidean coordinate vectors.

funcs.name: CTraceForm
funcs.doc: CTraceForm returns the complex trace form tr(AB) of two complex matrices.

funcs.name: CartanInteger
funcs.doc:
CartanInteger returns the Cartan integer ⟨α,β∨⟩ = 2(α,β)/(β,β) of two
Euclidean root vectors.

funcs.name: CartanMatrixDeterminant
funcs.signature: func CartanMatrixDeterminant(family string, rank int) (float64, error)
funcs.doc:
CartanMatrixDeterminant returns the determinant of the Cartan matrix, an
important invariant equal to the order of the center of the simply connected
group (the index of the root lattice in the weight lattice).

funcs.name: CasimirEigenvalue
funcs.signature: func CasimirEigenvalue(family string, rank int, dynkin []int) (float64, error)
funcs.doc:
CasimirEigenvalue returns the eigenvalue of the quadratic Casimir operator on
the irreducible representation with the given highest weight (Dynkin labels),
computed as (λ, λ+2ρ) in the normalisation where the long roots have squared
length 2. It returns [ErrDim] if the label count does not match the rank.

funcs.name: CasimirSU2
funcs.doc:
CasimirSU2 returns the physics-convention quadratic Casimir eigenvalue j(j+1)
of the spin-j irreducible representation of su(2).

funcs.name: CasimirTraceNormalization
funcs.doc:
CasimirTraceNormalization returns the constant c such that the Killing form
equals c times the trace form on the defining representation, given a basis;
it is computed as the ratio of the (0,0) entries of the two Gram matrices and
returns [ErrRange] when the trace form entry is zero.

funcs.name: Coroot
funcs.doc: Coroot returns the coroot α∨ = 2α/(α,α) of a Euclidean root vector.

funcs.name: CoxeterNumber
funcs.signature: func CoxeterNumber(family string, rank int) (int, error)
funcs.doc:
CoxeterNumber returns the Coxeter number h of the given Dynkin type, equal to
(number of roots)/rank and to one plus the height of the highest root.

funcs.name: Det
funcs.doc: Det returns the determinant of a square matrix.

funcs.name: DimensionSLn
funcs.doc: DimensionSLn returns the dimension n^2-1 of the Lie algebra sl(n) = A_{n-1}.

funcs.name: DimensionSOn
funcs.doc: DimensionSOn returns the dimension n(n-1)/2 of the Lie algebra so(n).

funcs.name: DimensionSPn
funcs.doc: DimensionSPn returns the dimension n(2n+1) of the Lie algebra sp(2n) = C_n.

funcs.name: DimensionSU2
funcs.doc:
DimensionSU2 returns the dimension 2j+1 of the spin-j representation of su(2).
It returns [ErrRange] if 2j is not a nonnegative integer.

funcs.name: DimensionSUn
funcs.doc: DimensionSUn returns the dimension n^2-1 of the Lie algebra su(n).

funcs.name: DualCoxeterFromRoots
funcs.signature: func DualCoxeterFromRoots(family string, rank int) (int, error)
funcs.doc:
DualCoxeterFromRoots returns the dual Coxeter number computed as
1 + (ρ, θ∨) where θ is the highest root; provided as an independent
cross-check of [DualCoxeterNumber] for classical and exceptional types.

funcs.name: DualCoxeterNumber
funcs.signature: func DualCoxeterNumber(family string, rank int) (int, error)
funcs.doc: DualCoxeterNumber returns the dual Coxeter number h∨ of the given Dynkin type.

funcs.name: FundamentalWeightsRootBasis
funcs.signature: func FundamentalWeightsRootBasis(family string, rank int) ([][]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
FundamentalWeightsRootBasis returns the fundamental weights expressed in the
basis of simple roots; row i is ω_i. They are the rows of the inverse Cartan
matrix.

funcs.name: HighestRootLabel
funcs.signature: func HighestRootLabel(family string, rank int) ([]int, error)
funcs.doc:
HighestRootLabel returns the coefficient vector (in the simple-root basis) of
the highest root, the unique positive root of maximal height.

funcs.name: IsAbelian
funcs.doc:
IsAbelian reports whether every pair of basis elements commutes to within
tolerance tol, i.e. whether the spanned Lie algebra is abelian.

funcs.name: IsClosedUnderBracket
funcs.doc:
IsClosedUnderBracket reports whether the bracket of every pair of basis
elements lies in the span of the basis to within tolerance tol, i.e. whether
the basis spans a Lie subalgebra.

funcs.name: IsNilpotentMatrix
funcs.doc:
IsNilpotentMatrix reports whether the square matrix m is nilpotent, i.e. some
power m^k (k up to its dimension) vanishes to within tolerance tol.

funcs.name: IsSemisimple
funcs.doc:
IsSemisimple reports whether the Killing form of the given basis is
nondegenerate (Cartan's criterion), tested by |det K| > tol.

funcs.name: IsSimplyLaced
funcs.signature: func IsSimplyLaced(family string, rank int) (bool, error)
funcs.doc:
IsSimplyLaced reports whether the Dynkin type is simply laced, i.e. all
bonds are single (types A, D, E).

funcs.name: JacobiResidualConstants
funcs.doc:
JacobiResidualConstants returns the maximum absolute value of the Jacobi
identity applied to structure constants:
Σ_m ( c[m][i][j] c[n][m][k] + c[m][j][k] c[n][m][i] + c[m][k][i] c[n][m][j] ).
It is zero for a genuine Lie algebra and provides a coordinate-space Jacobi
check independent of the matrix realisation.

funcs.name: KillingFormRank
funcs.doc:
KillingFormRank returns the rank of the Killing form of a basis. It equals the
dimension of the algebra for a semisimple Lie algebra and is smaller when the
radical is nonzero.

funcs.name: KillingFormValue
funcs.doc:
KillingFormValue returns the Killing form value K(X,Y) = tr(ad_X ad_Y) for two
elements X and Y given by their coordinate vectors in the supplied basis.

funcs.name: LieAlgebraDimension
funcs.signature: func LieAlgebraDimension(family string, rank int) (int, error)
funcs.doc:
LieAlgebraDimension returns the dimension of the semisimple Lie algebra of the
given type, equal to rank + number of roots.

funcs.name: LieAlgebraName
funcs.signature: func LieAlgebraName(family string, rank int) (string, error)
funcs.doc:
LieAlgebraName returns a human-readable classical name for the given Dynkin
type, such as "sl(4)" for A_3 or "so(7)" for B_3.

funcs.name: NumPositiveRoots
funcs.signature: func NumPositiveRoots(family string, rank int) (int, error)
funcs.doc: NumPositiveRoots returns the number of positive roots of the given type.

funcs.name: NumRoots
funcs.signature: func NumRoots(family string, rank int) (int, error)
funcs.doc: NumRoots returns the total number of roots (positive and negative).

funcs.name: PositiveRootLabels
funcs.signature: func PositiveRootLabels(family string, rank int) ([][]int, error)
funcs.doc:
PositiveRootLabels returns every positive root of the given type expressed as
an integer coefficient vector in the basis of simple roots. The algorithm is
the standard height-by-height root-string construction driven by the Cartan
matrix and works uniformly for classical and exceptional types.

funcs.name: PositiveRoots
funcs.signature: func PositiveRoots(family string, rank int) ([][]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
PositiveRoots returns the positive roots of a classical type as Euclidean
coordinate vectors. It returns [ErrType] for exceptional types.

funcs.name: Rank
funcs.doc:
Rank returns the numerical rank of a matrix using Gaussian elimination with
the given tolerance for treating a pivot as zero.

funcs.name: RootInnerProduct
funcs.doc:
RootInnerProduct returns the inner product of two vectors expressed in the
simple-root basis using the bilinear form B (see [RootBilinearForm]).

funcs.name: SO3ToVector
funcs.doc:
SO3ToVector maps a 3x3 antisymmetric matrix to its axial vector (w) such that
the matrix acts as w×. It returns [ErrDim] if the matrix is not 3x3.

funcs.name: SatisfiesJacobi
funcs.signature: func SatisfiesJacobi(a, b, c *Matrix, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc:
SatisfiesJacobi reports whether the Jacobi identity holds for A, B, C to
within tolerance tol (measured by the max absolute entry of the residual).

funcs.name: SimpleRoots
funcs.signature: func SimpleRoots(family string, rank int) ([][]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
SimpleRoots returns the simple roots of a classical type (A, B, C or D) as
Euclidean coordinate vectors. Type A_n lives in R^(n+1) with α_i = e_i-e_{i+1};
types B_n, C_n and D_n live in R^n. It returns [ErrType] for exceptional types
(use [PositiveRootLabels] for those).

funcs.name: Solve
funcs.doc:
Solve solves the linear system A x = b for a square A. It returns
[ErrSingular] if A is not invertible and [ErrDim] on a length mismatch.

funcs.name: SolveLeastSquares
funcs.doc:
SolveLeastSquares solves the (possibly overdetermined) system A x = b in the
least-squares sense via the normal equations AᵀA x = Aᵀb. It requires A to
have full column rank and returns [ErrRank] otherwise.

funcs.name: SpectralRadiusBound
funcs.doc:
SpectralRadiusBound returns an upper bound on the spectral radius of a real
matrix via the maximum absolute row sum (an induced ∞-norm).

funcs.name: StructureConstants
funcs.doc:
StructureConstants computes the structure constants c[k][i][j] of a matrix
Lie algebra with the given basis, defined by [e_i, e_j] = Σ_k c[k][i][j] e_k.
The basis matrices must be square, of equal size and linearly independent;
otherwise [ErrRank] or [ErrDim] is returned. The bracket of any two basis
elements must lie in the span of the basis (a genuine subalgebra).

funcs.name: TraceForm
funcs.doc:
TraceForm returns the trace form tr(AB) of two square matrices. For many
matrix Lie algebras this is proportional to the Killing form.

funcs.name: VecAdd
funcs.doc: VecAdd returns a+b, or [ErrDim] on a length mismatch.

funcs.name: VecDot
funcs.doc:
VecDot returns the Euclidean dot product of two equal-length vectors, or
[ErrDim] on a length mismatch.

funcs.name: VecEqual
funcs.signature: func VecEqual(a, b []float64, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc: VecEqual reports whether two vectors are equal to within tolerance tol.

funcs.name: VecNorm
funcs.doc: VecNorm returns the Euclidean norm of a vector.

funcs.name: VecNormSquared
funcs.doc: VecNormSquared returns the squared Euclidean norm of a vector.

funcs.name: VecScale
funcs.doc: VecScale returns the vector scaled by s.

funcs.name: VecSub
funcs.doc: VecSub returns a-b, or [ErrDim] on a length mismatch.

funcs.name: WeylDimension
funcs.signature: func WeylDimension(family string, rank int, dynkin []int) (int, error)
funcs.doc:
WeylDimension returns the dimension of the irreducible representation of the
given Dynkin type with highest weight specified by nonnegative Dynkin labels
(a_1,...,a_rank), via the Weyl dimension formula

	dim = Π_{α>0} (λ+ρ, α) / (ρ, α).

The result is rounded to the nearest integer. It returns [ErrDim] if the
number of labels does not match the rank.

funcs.name: WeylGroupOrder
funcs.signature: func WeylGroupOrder(family string, rank int) (*big.Int, error)
funcs.doc:
WeylGroupOrder returns the order of the Weyl group of the given Dynkin type as
an arbitrary-precision integer.

funcs.name: WeylOrbit
funcs.signature: func WeylOrbit(simple [][]float64, v []float64, tol float64) ([][]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
WeylOrbit returns the orbit of a Euclidean vector v under the Weyl group
generated by reflections in the given simple roots. It repeatedly applies all
simple reflections until the orbit stabilises (finite for a genuine root
system) and returns the distinct vectors within tolerance tol.

funcs.name: WeylReflection
funcs.doc:
WeylReflection returns the reflection of the vector v across the hyperplane
orthogonal to root, s_root(v) = v - 2(v,root)/(root,root) root, in Euclidean
coordinates. It returns [ErrDim] on a length mismatch or a zero root.

funcs.name: WeylVectorRootBasis
funcs.signature: func WeylVectorRootBasis(family string, rank int) ([]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
WeylVectorRootBasis returns the Weyl vector ρ (the sum of the fundamental
weights, equivalently half the sum of the positive roots) expressed in the
simple-root basis.

examples.name: WeylDimension
examples.code:
dim, _ := WeylDimension("A", 2, []int{1, 1})
fmt.Println(dim)
examples.doc:
ExampleWeylDimension prints the dimension of the adjoint representation of
su(3), whose highest weight has Dynkin labels (1,1).

examples.name: WeylGroupOrder
examples.code:
w, _ := WeylGroupOrder("E", 8)
fmt.Println(w.String())
examples.doc: ExampleWeylGroupOrder prints the order of the Weyl group of E8.

examples.name: Bracket
examples.code:
e, f, h := SL2Generators()
br, _ := Bracket(e, f)
fmt.Println(br.Equal(h))
examples.doc: ExampleBracket demonstrates the sl(2) commutation relation [E,F]=H.

packages.name: linprog
packages.synopsis: Package linprog implements linear and convex optimization in pure Go using only the standard library.
packages.doc:
Package linprog implements linear and convex optimization in pure Go using
only the standard library.

The package is organized around a small number of problem types and the
classical algorithms that solve them:

  - [LP] holds a general linear program (minimize or maximize a linear
    objective subject to linear <=, =, >= constraints with x >= 0). It can be
    reduced to [StandardLP] equality/nonnegative form and solved with the
    two-phase primal [Simplex] method, which uses Bland's rule to guarantee
    termination without cycling.

  - Integer programs are solved by branch and bound on the LP relaxation via
    [LP.SolveInteger].

  - Duality utilities build the [DualCanonical] program and check
    complementary slackness and the LP KKT conditions.

  - The transportation and assignment problems are solved by [Transportation]
    (reducing to a standard LP) and the O(n^3) [Hungarian] algorithm.

  - Convex quadratic programs are solved either exactly for the
    equality-constrained case ([SolveQPEquality], a single KKT linear system)
    or by a primal [SolveQP] active-set method for inequality constraints,
    with [ComputeKKTResidual] reporting the first-order optimality residuals.

All routines are deterministic and depend only on the Go standard library.
Vectors are []float64 and matrices are row-major [][]float64.

consts.signature: const Eps = 1e-9
consts.doc:
Eps is the default numerical tolerance used throughout the package for
pivot selection, feasibility checks and reduced-cost sign tests.

consts.signature: const IntTol = 1e-6
consts.doc:
IntTol is the tolerance within which a relaxed variable value is treated as
integral by the branch-and-bound solver.

consts.signature: const MaxBranchNodes = 200000
consts.doc:
MaxBranchNodes caps the number of branch-and-bound nodes explored before the
solver gives up and returns [StatusIterations] with the best incumbent found
so far.

vars.signature: var ErrDimension = errors.New("linprog: dimension mismatch")
vars.doc:
ErrDimension is returned when the dimensions of the supplied vectors and
matrices are inconsistent with the requested operation.

vars.signature: var ErrNotSquare = errors.New("linprog: cost matrix must be square")
vars.doc: ErrNotSquare is returned by [Hungarian] when the cost matrix is not square.

vars.signature: var ErrSingular = errors.New("linprog: singular matrix")
vars.doc:
ErrSingular is returned by the linear-system solvers when the coefficient
matrix is singular (or numerically singular) so that no unique solution
exists.

vars.signature: var ErrUnbalanced = errors.New("linprog: unbalanced transportation problem")
vars.doc:
ErrUnbalanced is returned by [Transportation] when total supply does not
equal total demand.

types.name: KKTResidual
types.doc:
KKTResidual holds the first-order (Karush-Kuhn-Tucker) optimality residuals
of a candidate solution to a [QP]. Every field is a nonnegative violation
magnitude; all are zero at a KKT point.

funcs.name: ComputeKKTResidual
funcs.signature: func ComputeKKTResidual(qp QP, x, lambda, mu []float64) KKTResidual
funcs.doc:
ComputeKKTResidual evaluates the [KKTResidual] of the candidate primal point
x with equality multipliers lambda and inequality multipliers mu for the
quadratic program qp.

methods.name: Max
methods.doc: Max returns the largest of the five residuals.

methods.name: Satisfied
methods.doc: Satisfied reports whether every residual is within tol.

types.name: LP
types.doc:
LP is a general linear program over nonnegative variables:

	optimize   C · x            (Minimize or Maximize per Sense)
	subject to A[i] · x  Rel[i]  B[i]   for each constraint i
	           x >= 0

A is a row-major matrix with one row per constraint and one column per
variable. C has one entry per variable. Rel and B have one entry per
constraint.

funcs.name: DualCanonical
funcs.doc:
DualCanonical builds the dual of the canonical primal

	minimize   c · x
	subject to A x >= b
	           x >= 0

whose dual is

	maximize   b · y
	subject to A^T y <= c
	           y >= 0.

The returned [LP] has [Maximize] sense, one dual variable per primal
constraint, and one dual constraint per primal variable. Strong duality
guarantees the two optimal objective values coincide when both are finite.

funcs.name: NewLP
funcs.signature: func NewLP(sense Sense, c []float64, a [][]float64, rel []Relation, b []float64) LP
funcs.doc:
NewLP constructs an [LP] from its parts. The slices are copied so the
returned program is independent of the caller's data. It panics if the
dimensions are inconsistent.

methods.name: AddConstraint
methods.signature: func (lp LP) AddConstraint(row []float64, rel Relation, rhs float64) LP
methods.doc:
AddConstraint returns a copy of the program with one additional constraint
row appended. The inputs are copied. It panics if row's length does not
equal NumVars.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent deep copy of the program.

methods.name: Feasible
methods.signature: func (lp LP) Feasible(x []float64, tol float64) bool
methods.doc:
Feasible reports whether x satisfies every constraint (within tol) and is
nonnegative (within tol).

methods.name: NumConstraints
methods.doc: NumConstraints returns the number of constraint rows.

methods.name: NumVars
methods.doc: NumVars returns the number of decision variables.

methods.name: Objective
methods.doc: Objective returns the raw objective value C · x, ignoring [Sense].

methods.name: Solve
methods.doc:
Solve solves the general [LP] by reducing it to standard form and running
the two-phase [Simplex]. The returned [LPSolution] reports the original
variables and the value of the original objective (with the program's
[Sense] respected).

methods.name: SolveBinary
methods.doc:
SolveBinary solves the integer program in which the variables flagged in
binary are additionally restricted to {0,1} by appending x[j] <= 1 upper
bounds before branch and bound. Variables with a false flag are left
continuous and nonnegative.

methods.name: SolveInteger
methods.doc:
SolveInteger solves the mixed-integer linear program obtained from lp by
requiring the variables flagged in integer to take integer values. It uses
branch and bound on the LP relaxation, branching on the first fractional
integer-constrained variable and pruning nodes that cannot improve on the
incumbent.

integer must have length NumVars; a true entry constrains that variable to
integers. The returned [LPSolution] reports the best integer-feasible point,
or [StatusInfeasible] if none exists.

methods.name: Standard
methods.doc:
Standard reduces the general program to [StandardLP] equality form. Each
"<=" constraint gains a nonnegative slack variable, each ">=" constraint a
nonnegative surplus variable, and each "=" constraint is copied unchanged.
The objective is negated when [Sense] is [Maximize] so that the returned
minimization has the same optimizer.

The first NumVars columns of the returned program are the original
(structural) variables in the same order, so a solution to the standard
program restricts to a solution of the original one by truncation.

types.name: LPKKT
types.doc:
LPKKT holds the four first-order optimality residuals for a canonical
primal/dual LP pair. Every field is a nonnegative violation magnitude; all
are zero at an optimal primal-dual pair.

funcs.name: CheckKKTLP
funcs.signature: func CheckKKTLP(c []float64, a [][]float64, b []float64, x, y []float64) LPKKT
funcs.doc:
CheckKKTLP computes the [LPKKT] residuals for the canonical primal

	minimize c·x s.t. A x >= b, x >= 0

with dual variables y (for the constraints) at primal point x.

methods.name: Max
methods.doc: Max returns the largest of the four residuals.

methods.name: Satisfied
methods.doc: Satisfied reports whether every residual is within tol.

types.name: LPSolution
types.doc: LPSolution is the outcome of solving a general [LP].

types.name: QP
types.doc:
QP is a convex quadratic program:

	minimize   1/2 x^T Q x + C · x
	subject to A x = B          (equality constraints)
	           G x <= H         (inequality constraints)

Q must be symmetric positive semidefinite for the problem to be convex. Any
of A/B and G/H may be empty. Variables are free (not sign restricted) unless
bounds are supplied as rows of G.

methods.name: Feasible
methods.signature: func (qp QP) Feasible(x []float64, tol float64) bool
methods.doc:
Feasible reports whether x satisfies A x = B (within tol) and G x <= H
(within tol).

methods.name: Gradient
methods.doc: Gradient returns the objective gradient Q x + C at x.

methods.name: NumVars
methods.doc: NumVars returns the number of decision variables.

methods.name: Objective
methods.doc: Objective returns 1/2 x^T Q x + C · x.

types.name: QPSolution
types.doc: QPSolution is the outcome of solving a [QP] with [SolveQP].

funcs.name: SolveQP
funcs.doc:
SolveQP solves the convex [QP] with inequality (and optional equality)
constraints by a primal active-set method starting from the feasible point
x0. At each iteration it solves an equality-constrained QP over the current
working set, either stepping toward the subproblem solution (adding a
blocking constraint) or dropping a constraint whose multiplier is negative,
until the KKT conditions hold.

x0 must be feasible (see [QP.Feasible]); this is the standard requirement of
a primal active-set method. The returned [QPSolution] reports the optimizer
and the equality/inequality multipliers.

types.name: Relation
types.signature: type Relation int
types.doc: Relation is the comparison operator of a linear constraint row.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns "<=", "=" or ">=".

types.name: Sense
types.signature: type Sense int
types.doc:
Sense selects whether a linear or quadratic objective is minimized or
maximized.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns "Minimize" or "Maximize".

types.name: SimplexResult
types.doc:
SimplexResult is the outcome of running the [Simplex] method on a
[StandardLP].

funcs.name: Simplex
funcs.doc:
Simplex solves the standard-form program by the two-phase primal simplex
method. Phase 1 minimizes the sum of artificial variables to find a basic
feasible solution (reporting [StatusInfeasible] if none exists); phase 2
minimizes the true objective (reporting [StatusUnbounded] if the objective
decreases without bound). Bland's anti-cycling rule guarantees termination.

types.name: StandardLP
types.doc:
StandardLP is a linear program in standard equality form:

	minimize   C · x
	subject to A x = B
	           x >= 0

It is the form consumed directly by [Simplex].

methods.name: Feasible
methods.signature: func (s StandardLP) Feasible(x []float64, tol float64) bool
methods.doc: Feasible reports whether x satisfies A x = B (within tol) and x >= 0.

methods.name: NumConstraints
methods.doc: NumConstraints returns the number of equality rows.

methods.name: NumVars
methods.doc: NumVars returns the number of variables in the standard program.

methods.name: Objective
methods.doc: Objective returns C · x.

types.name: Status
types.signature: type Status int
types.doc: Status classifies the outcome of solving an optimization problem.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a human-readable name for the status.

funcs.name: AssignmentCost
funcs.doc:
AssignmentCost returns the total cost of the assignment, summing
cost[i][assign[i]] over all rows.

funcs.name: CloneMatrix
funcs.doc: CloneMatrix returns an independent deep copy of the row-major matrix A.

funcs.name: ComplementarySlackness
funcs.signature: func ComplementarySlackness(c []float64, a [][]float64, b []float64, x, y []float64, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc:
ComplementarySlackness reports whether a primal point x and dual point y
satisfy the complementary slackness conditions for the canonical pair from
[DualCanonical], within tol. Specifically, for every constraint i either the
dual variable y[i] is zero or the primal constraint A[i]·x = b[i] is tight,
and for every variable j either x[j] is zero or the dual constraint
(A^T y)[j] = c[j] is tight.

funcs.name: Dot
funcs.doc:
Dot returns the inner product of two equal-length vectors.
It panics if the lengths differ.

funcs.name: DualityGap
funcs.doc:
DualityGap returns primalObj - dualObj. For a minimizing primal and its
maximizing dual, weak duality makes this quantity nonnegative, and it is
zero at optimality.

funcs.name: Hungarian
funcs.signature: func Hungarian(cost [][]float64) (assign []int, total float64, err error)
funcs.doc:
Hungarian solves the assignment problem: given an n-by-n cost matrix, it
finds a permutation assignment of rows to columns that minimizes the total
cost. It runs the O(n^3) Kuhn-Munkres algorithm with dual potentials.

It returns assign, where assign[i] is the column assigned to row i, and the
minimized total cost. A non-square matrix yields [ErrNotSquare].

funcs.name: HungarianMax
funcs.signature: func HungarianMax(value [][]float64) (assign []int, total float64, err error)
funcs.doc:
HungarianMax solves the maximization assignment problem by negating the cost
matrix, running [Hungarian], and returning the assignment together with the
maximized total value.

funcs.name: InfNorm
funcs.doc:
InfNorm returns the infinity norm (maximum absolute value) of a vector.
It returns 0 for an empty vector.

funcs.name: IsBalanced
funcs.signature: func IsBalanced(supply, demand []float64, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc: IsBalanced reports whether total supply equals total demand within tol.

funcs.name: IsIntegral
funcs.doc: IsIntegral reports whether every entry of x is within tol of an integer.

funcs.name: MatTVec
funcs.doc:
MatTVec returns the product A^T*x, i.e. the matrix-vector product of the
transpose of the m-by-n matrix A with the length-m vector x. The result has
length n. It panics on a dimension mismatch.

funcs.name: MatVec
funcs.doc:
MatVec returns the matrix-vector product A*x where A is an m-by-n row-major
matrix and x has length n. The result has length m. It panics on a
dimension mismatch.

funcs.name: NorthWestCorner
funcs.doc:
NorthWestCorner returns an initial basic feasible flow for a balanced
transportation problem using the north-west-corner rule. The result is an
m-by-n matrix whose row sums equal supply and whose column sums equal
demand.

funcs.name: SolveLinear
funcs.doc:
SolveLinear solves the square linear system A*x = b for x using Gaussian
elimination with partial pivoting. A must be an n-by-n matrix and b must
have length n. It returns [ErrSingular] if A is (numerically) singular and
[ErrDimension] if the shapes are inconsistent. A and b are not modified.

funcs.name: SolveQPEquality
funcs.signature: func SolveQPEquality(q [][]float64, c []float64, a [][]float64, b []float64) (x, lambda []float64, err error)
funcs.doc:
SolveQPEquality solves the equality-constrained convex QP

	minimize 1/2 x^T Q x + c · x  subject to  A x = b

by forming and solving the single symmetric KKT linear system

	[ Q  A^T ] [ x ]   [ -c ]
	[ A   0  ] [ λ ] = [  b ]

It returns the optimizer x and the Lagrange multipliers λ (one per equality
row). The Q block must make the KKT matrix nonsingular (Q positive definite
on the null space of A); otherwise [ErrSingular] is returned.

funcs.name: Transportation
funcs.signature: func Transportation(supply, demand []float64, cost [][]float64) (flow [][]float64, total float64, err error)
funcs.doc:
Transportation solves the balanced transportation problem: ship from m
sources with the given supplies to n sinks with the given demands at the
given per-unit costs, minimizing total cost while meeting every supply and
demand exactly. It reduces the problem to a [StandardLP] and solves it with
[Simplex].

It returns the optimal flow matrix (m-by-n) and total cost. Total supply
must equal total demand or [ErrUnbalanced] is returned.

funcs.name: Transpose
funcs.doc:
Transpose returns the transpose of the m-by-n row-major matrix A as an
n-by-m matrix. A rectangular but ragged input panics.

funcs.name: WeakDualityHolds
funcs.signature: func WeakDualityHolds(primalObj, dualObj, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc:
WeakDualityHolds reports whether the primal (minimizing) and dual
(maximizing) objective values respect weak duality, primalObj >= dualObj,
within tol.

packages.name: logic
packages.synopsis: Package logic implements propositional logic and Boolean algebra using only the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package logic implements propositional logic and Boolean algebra using only
the Go standard library.

The package is organised into a few areas:

  - Boolean primitives (ops.go): the elementary connectives [And], [Or],
    [Not], [Xor], [Nand], [Nor], [Xnor], [Implies] and [Iff], together with
    the variadic reductions [AndAll], [OrAll], [XorAll] and the derived
    gates [Majority] and [Mux].
  - Expression trees, parsing and evaluation (expr.go): the sealed [Expr]
    interface implemented by [Var], [Const], [UnaryExpr] and [BinaryExpr];
    the constructors [NewVar], [NewConst], [NewNot], [NewAnd], [NewOr],
    [NewXor], [NewNand], [NewNor], [NewXnor], [NewImplies] and [NewIff]; the
    [Parse]/[MustParse] recursive-descent parser; and the helpers [Vars] and
    [EvalString].
  - Truth tables (truthtable.go): the [TruthTable] and [TruthRow] types built
    by [NewTruthTable], plus [Assignments], [Minterms], [Maxterms],
    [IndexToAssignment] and [AssignmentToIndex].
  - Semantic analysis (analyze.go): [IsTautology], [IsContradiction],
    [IsSatisfiable], [IsContingency], [FindModel], [AllModels],
    [CountModels], [Equivalent] and [Entails].
  - Normal forms (normalform.go): [ToNNF], [ToCNF], [ToDNF], [Simplify],
    [CNFString], [DNFString], [IsCNF] and [IsDNF].
  - Two-level minimisation (minimize.go): the [Implicant] cube type, the
    [QuineMcCluskey] engine with [PrimeImplicants] and
    [EssentialPrimeImplicants], the cover routines [MinimizeSOP] and
    [SOPString], the Gray-code helpers [GrayCode], [GrayEncode] and
    [GrayDecode], the [KarnaughMap] grouping type built by [NewKarnaughMap],
    and the bit utilities [PopCount] and [BitString].

The syntax accepted by [Parse] uses C-like and word operators. Variables are
identifiers; the constants are written 1/0, T/F or true/false. Connectives,
from lowest to highest binding, are: <-> (iff), -> (implies), | (or), ^
(xor), & (and) and the unary ! (not); the words and, or, not, xor, nand, nor,
xnor, implies and iff are accepted as well, as are the aliases && * . for
and, || + for or and ~ for not.

Every routine is deterministic and depends only on the Go standard library.

types.name: BinaryExpr
types.doc: BinaryExpr applies a [BinaryOp] to a left and right operand.

funcs.name: NewAnd
funcs.doc: NewAnd returns the conjunction l & r.

funcs.name: NewIff
funcs.doc: NewIff returns the biconditional l <-> r.

funcs.name: NewImplies
funcs.doc: NewImplies returns the material conditional l -> r.

funcs.name: NewNand
funcs.doc: NewNand returns the negated conjunction of l and r.

funcs.name: NewNor
funcs.doc: NewNor returns the negated disjunction of l and r.

funcs.name: NewOr
funcs.doc: NewOr returns the disjunction l | r.

funcs.name: NewXnor
funcs.doc: NewXnor returns the negated exclusive disjunction (equivalence) of l and r.

funcs.name: NewXor
funcs.doc: NewXor returns the exclusive disjunction l ^ r.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc: Eval evaluates both operands and applies the binary connective.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the binary expression with explicit parentheses.

types.name: BinaryOp
types.signature: type BinaryOp int
types.doc: BinaryOp identifies a binary connective.

consts.signature:
const (
	IffOp     BinaryOp = iota // <-> biconditional (equivalence)
	ImpliesOp                 // -> material conditional
	OrOp                      // | inclusive disjunction
	NorOp                     // negated disjunction
	XorOp                     // ^ exclusive disjunction
	XnorOp                    // negated exclusive disjunction
	AndOp                     // & conjunction
	NandOp                    // negated conjunction
)
consts.doc:
The binary connectives, ordered from loosest to tightest binding as parsed by
[Parse].

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the canonical source symbol for the binary operator.

types.name: Const
types.signature: type Const bool
types.doc: Const is a Boolean constant, either true or false.

consts.doc: True and False are the Boolean constant expressions.

funcs.name: NewConst
funcs.doc: NewConst returns the constant expression for b.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc: Eval returns the constant's value; it never fails.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the constant as "T" or "F".

types.name: Expr
types.doc:
Expr is a node in a propositional-logic expression tree. The interface is
sealed: only the [Var], [Const], [UnaryExpr] and [BinaryExpr] types defined
in this package implement it, which lets the package reason exhaustively over
the concrete node kinds.

funcs.name: MustParse
funcs.doc:
MustParse is like [Parse] but panics if s does not parse. It is intended for
package-level expressions known to be valid and for tests.

funcs.name: Parse
funcs.doc:
Parse parses a propositional-logic expression. See the package documentation
for the accepted grammar. Binding tightens from <-> through ->, |, ^, & to
the unary !; the word forms and, or, not, xor, nand, nor, xnor, implies and
iff are accepted alongside the symbols.

funcs.name: Simplify
funcs.doc:
Simplify returns an equivalent expression reduced by constant folding and the
standard Boolean identities (identity, domination, idempotence and
complementation). It performs local, semantics-preserving rewrites rather
than full minimisation; use [MinimizeSOP] for optimal two-level results.

funcs.name: ToCNF
funcs.doc:
ToCNF returns the canonical (full) conjunctive normal form of e: an And of
maxterms, one disjunction of literals for each falsifying assignment. A
tautology yields the constant T; a contradiction over no variables yields F.

funcs.name: ToDNF
funcs.doc:
ToDNF returns the canonical (full) disjunctive normal form of e: an Or of
minterms, one conjunction of literals for each satisfying assignment. A
contradiction yields the constant F; a tautology over no variables yields T.

funcs.name: ToNNF
funcs.doc:
ToNNF converts e to negation normal form: an equivalent expression built only
from And, Or and negations applied directly to variables or constants. All
derived connectives (implication, equivalence, exclusive-or and the negated
gates) are expanded and negations are driven inward with the De Morgan and
double-negation laws.

types.name: Implicant
types.doc:
Implicant is a product-term cube over numVars variables. Each variable
position is either fixed to a value or eliminated (a "dash"). The zero value
is not meaningful; construct implicants with [PrimeImplicants] or the
minimisation routines.

funcs.name: EssentialPrimeImplicants
funcs.doc:
EssentialPrimeImplicants returns the essential prime implicants of the
function defined by minterms and dontCares over numVars variables: those
prime implicants that are the sole cover of some true minterm.

funcs.name: MinimizeSOP
funcs.doc:
MinimizeSOP minimises the expression e and returns a minimal sum-of-products
cover as a set of implicants over the variables of [Vars](e). The variable
order is lexicographic, matching the minterm indexing used elsewhere in the
package.

funcs.name: PrimeImplicants
funcs.doc:
PrimeImplicants computes the prime implicants of the Boolean function defined
by the given minterms and don't-care terms over numVars variables, using the
tabular Quine-McCluskey combination step. Don't-cares participate in the
combination but the reported covers include only true minterms. The result is
sorted deterministically.

funcs.name: QuineMcCluskey
funcs.doc:
QuineMcCluskey minimises the Boolean function given by minterms and dontCares
over numVars variables and returns a sum-of-products cover as a set of
implicants. It selects all essential prime implicants and then greedily adds
prime implicants that cover the most still-uncovered minterms, breaking ties
toward fewer literals for a deterministic result. An empty minterm list (the
constant-false function) yields a nil cover.

methods.name: Covers
methods.doc: Covers reports whether the cube contains the given minterm index.

methods.name: Expression
methods.doc:
Expression renders the cube as a product term over the given variable names,
where vars[0] is the most-significant position. A fixed 1 yields the plain
variable, a fixed 0 its negation, and a dash omits the variable. The empty
cube (all dashes) renders as the constant "T". It panics if len(vars) does
not equal NumVars.

methods.name: LiteralCount
methods.doc:
LiteralCount returns the number of fixed variable positions in the cube,
i.e. the number of literals in the product term.

methods.name: Minterms
methods.doc:
Minterms returns the original minterms that the cube covers, as recorded when
it was produced by the minimisation routines. The slice is sorted ascending.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String renders the cube as a pattern of numVars characters, most-significant
position first, using '1', '0' and '-' for a dash.

types.name: KarnaughMap
types.doc:
KarnaughMap is a Karnaugh map: a Gray-coded grid view of a Boolean function
used to visualise adjacency and read off groupings.

funcs.name: NewKarnaughMap
funcs.doc:
NewKarnaughMap builds a Karnaugh map for numVars variables in which the given
minterms are set to true. The higher-order half of the variables index the
rows and the lower-order half index the columns, each in Gray-code order.

methods.name: At
methods.doc:
At returns the cell value at the given grid row and column. The row and
column positions are interpreted through the Gray-code header ordering, so
physically adjacent cells differ in a single variable.

methods.name: Cols
methods.doc:
Cols returns the number of columns in the map (2 raised to the
column-variable count).

methods.name: Groups
methods.doc:
Groups returns the prime implicants of the mapped function, which correspond
exactly to the maximal rectangular groupings of adjacent true cells. The
result is sorted deterministically.

methods.name: Rows
methods.doc:
Rows returns the number of rows in the map (2 raised to the row-variable
count).

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String renders the map as a Gray-coded grid of 1s and 0s with binary row and
column headers.

types.name: TruthRow
types.doc:
TruthRow is a single line of a [TruthTable]: an assignment of truth values to
the variables together with the resulting value of the expression.

types.name: TruthTable
types.doc:
TruthTable is the exhaustive evaluation of an expression over all 2^n
assignments of its n variables.

funcs.name: NewTruthTable
funcs.doc:
NewTruthTable evaluates e over every assignment of its variables and returns
the resulting truth table. Variables are ordered lexicographically and the
rows run from the all-false assignment to the all-true one, treating the
first variable as the most-significant bit. Eval errors cannot occur because
every variable is bound.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String renders the truth table as an aligned ASCII grid with a header row of
variable names, a "=" result column, and one line per assignment using 1 and
0 for true and false.

types.name: UnaryExpr
types.doc: UnaryExpr applies a [UnaryOp] to a single operand.

funcs.name: NewNot
funcs.doc: NewNot returns the negation of x.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc: Eval evaluates the operand and applies the unary connective.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the negation, parenthesising a compound operand.

types.name: UnaryOp
types.signature: type UnaryOp int
types.doc: UnaryOp identifies a unary connective. The only unary connective is [NotOp].

consts.signature: const NotOp UnaryOp = iota
consts.doc: NotOp is the logical-negation connective.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the source symbol for the unary operator.

types.name: Var
types.signature: type Var string
types.doc: Var is a propositional variable identified by its name.

funcs.name: NewVar
funcs.doc: NewVar returns the variable with the given name.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc: Eval looks the variable up in env, returning an error when it is unbound.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the variable's name.

funcs.name: AllModels
funcs.doc:
AllModels returns every satisfying assignment of e, in ascending
assignment-index order.

funcs.name: And
funcs.doc:
And returns the logical conjunction of a and b: true when both operands are
true.

funcs.name: AndAll
funcs.doc:
AndAll returns the conjunction of every value in vals. The empty product is
true, the identity element for And.

funcs.name: AssignmentToIndex
funcs.doc:
AssignmentToIndex encodes an assignment over vars back into its row index,
the inverse of [IndexToAssignment]. A variable missing from env is treated as
false.

funcs.name: Assignments
funcs.doc:
Assignments enumerates all 2^len(vars) truth assignments over vars, in
ascending index order. The input slice is sorted before enumeration so the
ordering matches [NewTruthTable].

funcs.name: BitString
funcs.doc:
BitString returns the binary representation of x in exactly numVars digits,
most-significant bit first.

funcs.name: CNFString
funcs.doc:
CNFString renders the canonical conjunctive normal form of e as a product-of-
sums string. A tautology renders as "T".

funcs.name: CountModels
funcs.doc: CountModels returns the number of assignments that satisfy e.

funcs.name: DNFString
funcs.doc:
DNFString renders the canonical disjunctive normal form of e as a sum-of-
products string, using juxtaposition-free "&"/"|" notation with negation
written as a leading "!". A contradiction renders as "F".

funcs.name: Entails
funcs.doc:
Entails reports whether a logically entails b (written a ⊨ b): every
assignment that satisfies a also satisfies b. Equivalently, a -> b is a
tautology.

funcs.name: Equivalent
funcs.doc:
Equivalent reports whether a and b are logically equivalent: they yield the
same truth value under every assignment of their combined variables.

funcs.name: EvalString
funcs.signature: func EvalString(s string, env map[string]bool) (bool, error)
funcs.doc: EvalString parses s and evaluates it under env in a single call.

funcs.name: FindModel
funcs.doc:
FindModel returns a satisfying assignment for e and true, or a nil map and
false when e is unsatisfiable. The search proceeds in ascending
assignment-index order, so the returned model is deterministic.

funcs.name: GrayCode
funcs.doc:
GrayCode returns the reflected binary Gray code sequence for n bits: the
2^n values GrayEncode(0), GrayEncode(1), ..., GrayEncode(2^n-1), in which
adjacent entries (cyclically) differ in a single bit.

funcs.name: GrayDecode
