B is the Erlang-B blocking probability at offered load a.

funcs.name: ErlangB
funcs.signature: func ErlangB(c int, a float64) float64
funcs.doc:
ErlangB returns the Erlang-B loss probability (blocking probability) of an
M/M/c/c loss system with c servers and offered load a erlangs. It is computed
with the numerically stable recurrence B(0,a)=1,
B(n,a) = a*B(n-1,a) / (n + a*B(n-1,a)). It returns NaN for a negative load or
non-positive server count.

funcs.name: ErlangBSeries
funcs.doc:
ErlangBSeries returns the Erlang-B blocking probabilities for all server
counts from 0 through c at offered load a, produced by a single sweep of the
stable recurrence. Element i holds B(i,a); B(0,a)=1. It returns nil for a
negative load or negative c.

funcs.name: ErlangBServersFor
funcs.signature: func ErlangBServersFor(a, gos float64, maxServers int) int
funcs.doc:
ErlangBServersFor returns the smallest number of servers whose Erlang-B
blocking probability at offered load a is at or below the target grade of
service gos (a probability in (0,1]). It returns 0 for a=0 and -1 if no
value up to maxServers achieves the target.

funcs.name: ErlangC
funcs.signature: func ErlangC(c int, a float64) float64
funcs.doc:
ErlangC returns the Erlang-C delay probability, the probability that an
arriving customer must wait in an M/M/c queue with c servers and offered load
a erlangs. It is derived from the Erlang-B value via
C = B / (1 - (a/c)(1-B)). It returns NaN when the offered load is not below
the number of servers (the queue would be unstable).

funcs.name: ErlangCDF
funcs.signature: func ErlangCDF(k int, lambda, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
ErlangCDF returns the cumulative distribution of an Erlang-k distribution at
x>=0, 1 - e^{-lambda x} sum_{i=0}^{k-1} (lambda x)^i / i!. It returns NaN for
invalid parameters.

funcs.name: ErlangCFromB
funcs.signature: func ErlangCFromB(c int, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
ErlangCFromB returns the Erlang-C delay probability computed from a
precomputed Erlang-B value b for c servers and offered load a. It returns NaN
for an unstable system.

funcs.name: ErlangCMeanWait
funcs.signature: func ErlangCMeanWait(c int, lambda, mu float64) float64
funcs.doc:
ErlangCMeanWait returns the mean queueing delay in an M/M/c queue with c
servers, arrival rate lambda and per-server service rate mu, computed from
the Erlang-C formula as C / (c*mu - lambda). It returns NaN for an unstable
system.

funcs.name: ErlangCServersFor
funcs.signature: func ErlangCServersFor(a, p float64, maxServers int) int
funcs.doc:
ErlangCServersFor returns the smallest number of servers whose Erlang-C delay
probability at offered load a is at or below the target probability p. The
search starts at ceil(a)+1 to guarantee stability and stops at maxServers,
returning -1 on failure.

funcs.name: ErlangCWaitTailProb
funcs.signature: func ErlangCWaitTailProb(c int, lambda, mu, t float64) float64
funcs.doc:
ErlangCWaitTailProb returns the probability that the queueing delay in an
M/M/c queue exceeds t, C * exp(-(c*mu - lambda) t), where C is the Erlang-C
value at offered load lambda/mu. It returns NaN for an unstable system.

funcs.name: ErlangMean
funcs.signature: func ErlangMean(k int, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc: ErlangMean returns the mean k/lambda of an Erlang-k distribution.

funcs.name: ErlangPDF
funcs.signature: func ErlangPDF(k int, lambda, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
ErlangPDF returns the density of an Erlang-k distribution (sum of k iid
exponentials of rate lambda) at x>=0. It returns NaN for invalid parameters.

funcs.name: ExponentialCDF
funcs.doc:
ExponentialCDF returns the exponential cumulative probability
1-exp(-rate*x) for x>=0. It returns NaN for a non-positive rate and 0 for
x<0.

funcs.name: ExponentialMean
funcs.doc: ExponentialMean returns the mean 1/rate of an exponential distribution.

funcs.name: ExponentialPDF
funcs.doc:
ExponentialPDF returns the exponential probability density rate*exp(-rate*x)
for x>=0. It returns NaN for a non-positive rate and 0 for x<0.

funcs.name: ExponentialTail
funcs.doc:
ExponentialTail returns the exponential survival probability
P(X>t)=exp(-rate*t) for t>=0. It returns NaN for a non-positive rate and 1
for t<=0.

funcs.name: Factorial
funcs.doc:
Factorial returns n! as a float64. It returns NaN for negative n and +Inf
once the exact value overflows float64 (for n greater than 170).

funcs.name: KingmanWq
funcs.signature: func KingmanWq(rho, ca2, cs2, serviceMean float64) float64
funcs.doc:
KingmanWq returns the Kingman heavy-traffic approximation of the mean G/G/1
waiting time given the utilization rho, the interarrival and service SCVs and
the mean service time: (rho/(1-rho)) * ((ca2+cs2)/2) * serviceMean. It
returns +Inf for rho>=1 and NaN for invalid inputs.

funcs.name: LittleL
funcs.doc:
LittleL returns the mean number in system L = lambda*W from the arrival rate
and mean sojourn time via Little's law. It returns NaN for negative inputs.

funcs.name: LittleLambda
funcs.doc:
LittleLambda returns the arrival rate lambda = L/W implied by Little's law
from the mean number in system and mean sojourn time. It returns NaN for a
non-positive W or negative L.

funcs.name: LittleLq
funcs.doc:
LittleLq returns the mean queue length Lq = lambda*Wq from the arrival rate
and mean waiting time via Little's law applied to the queue. It returns NaN
for negative inputs.

funcs.name: LittleW
funcs.doc:
LittleW returns the mean sojourn time W = L/lambda from the mean number in
system and the arrival rate via Little's law. It returns NaN for a
non-positive arrival rate or negative L.

funcs.name: LittleWq
funcs.doc:
LittleWq returns the mean waiting time in queue Wq = Lq/lambda from the mean
queue length and arrival rate via Little's law. It returns NaN for a
non-positive arrival rate or negative Lq.

funcs.name: LogFactorial
funcs.doc:
LogFactorial returns the natural logarithm of n! using the log-gamma
function, remaining finite for large n. It returns NaN for negative n.

funcs.name: MD1Lq
funcs.doc: MD1Lq returns the M/D/1 mean queue length rho^2/(2(1-rho)).

funcs.name: MD1Wq
funcs.doc: MD1Wq returns the M/D/1 mean waiting time.

funcs.name: MG1Lq
funcs.signature: func MG1Lq(lambda, serviceMean, serviceVar float64) float64
funcs.doc:
MG1Lq returns the M/G/1 Pollaczek–Khinchine mean queue length from the
arrival rate, mean service time and service-time variance.

funcs.name: MG1Wq
funcs.signature: func MG1Wq(lambda, serviceMean, serviceVar float64) float64
funcs.doc:
MG1Wq returns the M/G/1 mean waiting time from the arrival rate, mean service
time and service-time variance.

funcs.name: MM1KBlockingProb
funcs.signature: func MM1KBlockingProb(lambda, mu float64, k int) float64
funcs.doc: MM1KBlockingProb returns the M/M/1/K loss probability P(N=K).

funcs.name: MM1KL
funcs.signature: func MM1KL(lambda, mu float64, k int) float64
funcs.doc: MM1KL returns the mean number in an M/M/1/K system.

funcs.name: MM1L
funcs.doc: MM1L returns the mean number in an M/M/1 system, rho/(1-rho).

funcs.name: MM1Lq
funcs.doc: MM1Lq returns the mean M/M/1 queue length rho^2/(1-rho).

funcs.name: MM1P0
funcs.doc: MM1P0 returns the M/M/1 empty-system probability 1-rho.

funcs.name: MM1Pn
funcs.signature: func MM1Pn(lambda, mu float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc: MM1Pn returns the M/M/1 state probability (1-rho)rho^n.

funcs.name: MM1Rho
funcs.doc: MM1Rho returns the M/M/1 utilization lambda/mu.

funcs.name: MM1W
funcs.doc: MM1W returns the mean M/M/1 sojourn time 1/(mu-lambda).

funcs.name: MM1WaitTailProb
funcs.signature: func MM1WaitTailProb(lambda, mu, t float64) float64
funcs.doc: MM1WaitTailProb returns P(W>t) for an M/M/1 queue.

funcs.name: MM1Wq
funcs.doc: MM1Wq returns the mean M/M/1 waiting time lambda/(mu(mu-lambda)).

funcs.name: MMInfL
funcs.doc: MMInfL returns the mean number in an M/M/infinity system, lambda/mu.

funcs.name: MMInfPn
funcs.signature: func MMInfPn(lambda, mu float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc: MMInfPn returns the M/M/infinity state probability (Poisson mass).

funcs.name: MMcDelayProb
funcs.signature: func MMcDelayProb(lambda, mu float64, c int) float64
funcs.doc: MMcDelayProb returns the Erlang-C delay probability for an M/M/c queue.

funcs.name: MMcKBlockingProb
funcs.signature: func MMcKBlockingProb(lambda, mu float64, c, k int) float64
funcs.doc: MMcKBlockingProb returns the M/M/c/K loss probability P(N=K).

funcs.name: MMcKL
funcs.signature: func MMcKL(lambda, mu float64, c, k int) float64
funcs.doc: MMcKL returns the mean number in an M/M/c/K system.

funcs.name: MMcL
funcs.signature: func MMcL(lambda, mu float64, c int) float64
funcs.doc: MMcL returns the mean number in an M/M/c system.

funcs.name: MMcLq
funcs.signature: func MMcLq(lambda, mu float64, c int) float64
funcs.doc: MMcLq returns the mean M/M/c queue length.

funcs.name: MMcP0
funcs.signature: func MMcP0(lambda, mu float64, c int) float64
funcs.doc: MMcP0 returns the M/M/c empty-system probability.

funcs.name: MMcPn
funcs.signature: func MMcPn(lambda, mu float64, c, n int) float64
funcs.doc: MMcPn returns the M/M/c state probability P(N=n).

funcs.name: MMcW
funcs.signature: func MMcW(lambda, mu float64, c int) float64
funcs.doc: MMcW returns the mean M/M/c sojourn time.

funcs.name: MMcWq
funcs.signature: func MMcWq(lambda, mu float64, c int) float64
funcs.doc: MMcWq returns the mean M/M/c waiting time.

funcs.name: OfferedLoad
funcs.doc:
OfferedLoad returns the offered load (traffic intensity in erlangs)
a = lambda/mu, the mean number of busy servers in an unrestricted system.
It returns NaN when mu is not positive or lambda is negative.

funcs.name: OfferedLoadFromCarried
funcs.doc:
OfferedLoadFromCarried returns the offered load implied by a measured carried
load and blocking probability, carried/(1-blocking). It returns NaN for a
blocking probability outside [0,1).

funcs.name: PoissonCDF
funcs.signature: func PoissonCDF(k int, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc:
PoissonCDF returns the Poisson cumulative probability P(N<=k) with mean
lambda. It returns NaN for lambda<0 and 0 for k<0.

funcs.name: PoissonPMF
funcs.signature: func PoissonPMF(k int, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc:
PoissonPMF returns the Poisson probability mass P(N=k) with mean lambda,
computed in log space for numerical stability. It returns NaN for lambda<0
and 0 for negative k.

funcs.name: PollaczekKhinchineLq
funcs.doc:
PollaczekKhinchineLq returns the Pollaczek–Khinchine mean queue length for an
M/G/1 queue with utilization rho and service-time squared coefficient of
variation scv: rho^2(1+scv)/(2(1-rho)). It returns +Inf for rho>=1 and NaN
for invalid inputs.

funcs.name: PollaczekKhinchineWq
funcs.signature: func PollaczekKhinchineWq(rho, scv, serviceMean float64) float64
funcs.doc:
PollaczekKhinchineWq returns the Pollaczek–Khinchine mean waiting time
rho*(1+scv)/(2(1-rho)) * E[S], where E[S] is the mean service time. It
returns +Inf for rho>=1 and NaN for invalid inputs.

funcs.name: SojournFromWait
funcs.doc:
SojournFromWait returns the mean sojourn time W = Wq + 1/mu obtained by
adding a mean service time to the mean waiting time. It returns NaN for a
non-positive service rate or negative Wq.

funcs.name: SquaredCoefficientOfVariation
funcs.doc:
SquaredCoefficientOfVariation returns the squared coefficient of variation
(SCV) variance/mean^2 of a distribution given its mean and variance. It
returns NaN when the mean is zero or an argument is negative.

funcs.name: TrafficFromMetrics
funcs.doc:
TrafficFromMetrics returns the arrival rate implied by a mean number in
system L and a mean sojourn time W via Little's law, an alias for
[LittleLambda].

funcs.name: Utilization
funcs.signature: func Utilization(lambda, mu float64, c int) float64
funcs.doc:
Utilization returns the per-server utilization rho = lambda/(c*mu) of a
c-server queue. It returns NaN for invalid arguments.

funcs.name: WaitFromSojourn
funcs.doc:
WaitFromSojourn returns the mean waiting time Wq = W - 1/mu obtained by
subtracting the mean service time from the mean sojourn time. It returns NaN
for a non-positive service rate or negative W.

examples.name: ErlangC
examples.code: fmt.Printf("%.4f\n", ErlangC(14, 10))

examples.name: MM1
examples.code:
q, _ := NewMM1(8, 10)
fmt.Printf("rho=%.2f L=%.2f W=%.2f\n", q.Rho(), q.L(), q.W())

packages.name: reliability
packages.synopsis: Package reliability provides reliability engineering and survival-analysis primitives built entirely on the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package reliability provides reliability engineering and survival-analysis
primitives built entirely on the Go standard library.

The package covers four broad areas.

Lifetime distributions: closed-form probability density, cumulative
distribution, reliability (survival), hazard-rate, cumulative-hazard,
quantile and summary-statistic functions for the exponential, Weibull,
lognormal, gamma, Rayleigh, normal, Gompertz and Gompertz–Makeham models,
together with mean-time-to-failure (MTTF), conditional reliability and
mean-residual-life helpers. A flexible additive [BathtubHazard] model and a
two-term [HjorthHazard] model reproduce the classic decreasing/constant/
increasing "bathtub" failure-rate curve.

System reliability: reliability-block-diagram combinators for series,
parallel (active redundancy), k-out-of-n and bridge structures, exact
evaluation for non-identical components, standby-redundancy formulas and
redundancy-allocation helpers such as [MinParallelForReliability].

Availability: inherent, operational and steady-state availability from
MTBF/MTTF/MTTR data or from failure/repair rates, plus availability of
series and parallel repairable systems.

Survival analysis: the [KaplanMeier] product-limit survival estimator with
Greenwood variance, the [NelsonAalen] cumulative-hazard estimator, median
and restricted-mean survival, log-rank testing and hazard-ratio estimation
from right-censored data.

Unless stated otherwise, times and rates must be non-negative and shape and
scale parameters must be strictly positive; functions return NaN for
arguments outside their natural domain rather than panicking. All routines
are deterministic and depend only on the standard library.

types.name: KMEstimate
types.signature:
type KMEstimate struct {
	Times    []float64 // distinct failure times
	AtRisk   []int     // number at risk just before each time
	Events   []int     // number of failures at each time
	Censored []int     // number censored in the interval ending at each time
	Survival []float64 // estimated survival S(t) at and after each time
	Variance []float64 // Greenwood variance of S(t)
	StdErr   []float64 // standard error of S(t)
}
types.doc:
KMEstimate holds the result of a Kaplan–Meier product-limit survival
estimate. The slices are parallel and ordered by ascending event time; only
times at which at least one failure occurred are recorded.

funcs.name: KaplanMeier
funcs.doc:
KaplanMeier computes the Kaplan–Meier product-limit estimator of the survival
function from right-censored data. events[i] is true when times[i] is an
observed failure and false when it is a right-censoring time. The Greenwood
formula supplies the variance of each survival estimate.

methods.name: CumulativeHazardAt
methods.doc:
CumulativeHazardAt returns the Kaplan–Meier-derived cumulative hazard
-ln S(t).

methods.name: MedianSurvival
methods.doc:
MedianSurvival returns the smallest failure time at which the estimated
survival drops to 0.5 or below. If survival never reaches 0.5 (heavy
censoring) it returns NaN, signalling that the median is not reached.

methods.name: Quantile
methods.doc:
Quantile returns the smallest failure time at which the estimated survival
drops to 1-p or below, i.e. the p-quantile of the lifetime distribution. It
returns NaN when the quantile is not reached.

methods.name: RestrictedMeanSurvival
methods.doc:
RestrictedMeanSurvival returns the restricted mean survival time over [0,tau],
the area under the Kaplan–Meier curve up to the horizon tau. It equals the
expected lifetime truncated at tau.

methods.name: SurvivalAt
methods.doc:
SurvivalAt returns the Kaplan–Meier survival estimate S(t). It is a
right-continuous step function equal to 1 before the first failure time and
holding its most recent value between failures.

types.name: LogRankResult
types.signature:
type LogRankResult struct {
	Observed1   float64 // observed failures in group 1
	Expected1   float64 // expected failures in group 1 under H0
	Variance    float64 // variance of (Observed1-Expected1)
	ChiSquare   float64 // log-rank chi-square statistic (1 d.f.)
	PValue      float64 // two-sided p-value
	HazardRatio float64 // one-step hazard-ratio estimate, group 1 vs group 2
}
types.doc:
LogRankResult holds the outcome of a two-sample log-rank test comparing the
survival of group 1 against group 2.

funcs.name: LogRankTest
funcs.signature: func LogRankTest(times1 []float64, events1 []bool, times2 []float64, events2 []bool) (*LogRankResult, error)
funcs.doc:
LogRankTest performs the two-sample log-rank test on right-censored survival
data. Each group is supplied as parallel time/event slices where an event
value of true marks an observed failure. The returned chi-square statistic
has one degree of freedom.

types.name: NAEstimate
types.signature:
type NAEstimate struct {
	Times     []float64 // distinct failure times
	AtRisk    []int     // number at risk just before each time
	Events    []int     // number of failures at each time
	CumHazard []float64 // estimated cumulative hazard H(t)
	Variance  []float64 // variance of H(t)
}
types.doc:
NAEstimate holds the result of a Nelson–Aalen cumulative-hazard estimate.
The slices are parallel and ordered by ascending event time.

funcs.name: NelsonAalen
funcs.doc:
NelsonAalen computes the Nelson–Aalen estimator of the cumulative hazard
function from right-censored data, together with its variance. events[i] is
true for an observed failure and false for a right-censoring time.

methods.name: CumulativeHazardAt
methods.doc:
CumulativeHazardAt returns the Nelson–Aalen cumulative hazard estimate H(t),
a right-continuous step function equal to 0 before the first failure.

methods.name: SurvivalAt
methods.doc:
SurvivalAt returns the survival estimate exp(-H(t)) implied by the
Nelson–Aalen cumulative hazard.

funcs.name: AchievedAvailability
funcs.doc:
AchievedAvailability returns MTBM/(MTBM+MMT), the availability based on the
mean time between maintenance actions (both corrective and preventive) and
the mean maintenance time.

funcs.name: AllocateFailureRateSeriesEqual
funcs.doc:
AllocateFailureRateSeriesEqual apportions an overall series-system failure
rate equally across n subsystems, returning the per-subsystem rate
lambdaTotal/n.

funcs.name: AllocateReliabilitySeriesEqual
funcs.signature: func AllocateReliabilitySeriesEqual(target float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
AllocateReliabilitySeriesEqual applies the equal-apportionment technique to a
series system: to achieve an overall target reliability across n independent
subsystems, each subsystem must reach target^{1/n}.

funcs.name: Availability
funcs.doc:
Availability returns the steady-state availability MTBF/(MTBF+MTTR) of a
repairable item from its mean time between failures and mean time to repair.

funcs.name: AvailabilityFromRates
funcs.doc:
AvailabilityFromRates returns the steady-state availability from a failure
rate lambda and repair rate mu, an alias of SteadyStateAvailability.

funcs.name: AvailabilityKofN
funcs.signature: func AvailabilityKofN(k, n int, a float64) float64
funcs.doc:
AvailabilityKofN returns the availability of a k-out-of-n system of identical
units each with availability a, using the same binomial sum as
KofNReliability.

funcs.name: AvailabilityParallelSystem
funcs.doc:
AvailabilityParallelSystem returns the availability of a parallel system,
1-∏(1-aᵢ).

funcs.name: AvailabilitySeriesSystem
funcs.doc:
AvailabilitySeriesSystem returns the availability of a series system, the
product of its component availabilities.

funcs.name: BathtubCDF
funcs.signature: func BathtubCDF(t, a, b, c, e float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BathtubCDF returns the failure probability F(t)=1-R(t) of the additive
bathtub model.

funcs.name: BathtubCumulativeHazard
funcs.signature: func BathtubCumulativeHazard(t, a, b, c, e float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BathtubCumulativeHazard returns the cumulative hazard of the additive
bathtub model,

	H(t) = (a/b)(1-e^{-b t}) + c·t + (e/2)·t².

funcs.name: BathtubHazard
funcs.signature: func BathtubHazard(t, a, b, c, e float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BathtubHazard evaluates an additive bathtub-shaped hazard rate at time t>=0:

	h(t) = a·e^{-b t} + c + e·t

The first term (a>=0, b>0) models decreasing infant-mortality failures, the
constant c>=0 the useful-life period, and the linear term e>=0 the
increasing wear-out phase. Suitable parameter choices reproduce the classic
bathtub curve.

funcs.name: BathtubPDF
funcs.signature: func BathtubPDF(t, a, b, c, e float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BathtubPDF returns the probability density h(t)R(t) of the additive bathtub
model.

funcs.name: BathtubReliability
funcs.signature: func BathtubReliability(t, a, b, c, e float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BathtubReliability returns the survival probability R(t)=exp(-H(t)) of the
additive bathtub model.

funcs.name: BridgeReliability
funcs.signature: func BridgeReliability(r1, r2, r3, r4, r5 float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BridgeReliability returns the exact reliability of the classic five-component
bridge network, evaluated by conditioning (pivotal decomposition) on the
bridging component r5. Components r1 and r2 form the two upper links, r3 and
r4 the two lower links, and r5 the bridge between the mid-nodes. Sources and
terminals are assumed perfectly reliable.

funcs.name: ChiSquarePValue1DF
funcs.doc:
ChiSquarePValue1DF returns the upper-tail probability P(X>=x) for a
chi-square random variable with one degree of freedom, useful for
interpreting a log-rank statistic.

funcs.name: CriticalityImportance
funcs.signature: func CriticalityImportance(birnbaum, rComp, rSys float64) float64
funcs.doc:
CriticalityImportance returns the criticality (Lambert) importance of a
component: its Birnbaum importance weighted by its own unreliability and
normalized by the overall system unreliability. rComp is the component
reliability and rSys the system reliability.

funcs.name: DesignLifeForReliability
funcs.doc:
DesignLifeForReliability returns the maximum operating time at which a
constant-failure-rate item still meets a target reliability rTarget:
t=-ln(rTarget)/λ.

funcs.name: EmpiricalCDF
funcs.doc:
EmpiricalCDF returns the fraction of a sample of failure times that are less
than or equal to t, an estimate of F(t) for complete data.

funcs.name: EmpiricalHazardRate
funcs.signature: func EmpiricalHazardRate(times []float64, t, dt float64) float64
funcs.doc:
EmpiricalHazardRate estimates the average hazard rate over the interval
(t, t+dt] from complete data as (survivors past t that fail within dt)
divided by (dt times survivors past t).

funcs.name: EmpiricalReliability
funcs.doc:
EmpiricalReliability returns the fraction of a sample of failure times that
exceed t, an unbiased estimate of R(t) for complete data.

funcs.name: ExpectedDowntime
funcs.doc:
ExpectedDowntime returns the expected downtime over a mission of the given
duration for an item with the supplied availability: (1-availability)·mission.

funcs.name: ExpectedUptime
funcs.doc:
ExpectedUptime returns the expected uptime over a mission of the given
duration for an item with the supplied availability: availability·mission.

funcs.name: ExponentialCDF
funcs.doc:
ExponentialCDF returns the failure probability F(t)=1-e^{-λt} for the
exponential distribution with failure rate lambda>0 at time t>=0.

funcs.name: ExponentialConditionalReliability
funcs.signature: func ExponentialConditionalReliability(t, x, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc:
ExponentialConditionalReliability returns the probability that a component
surviving to age t survives an additional time x. By the memoryless
property this equals e^{-λx} independent of t.

funcs.name: ExponentialCumulativeHazard
funcs.doc:
ExponentialCumulativeHazard returns the cumulative hazard H(t)=λt of the
exponential distribution.

funcs.name: ExponentialHazard
funcs.doc:
ExponentialHazard returns the (constant) hazard rate h(t)=λ of the
exponential distribution.

funcs.name: ExponentialMTTF
funcs.doc: ExponentialMTTF returns the mean time to failure 1/λ, identical to the mean.

funcs.name: ExponentialMean
funcs.doc: ExponentialMean returns the mean lifetime 1/λ.

funcs.name: ExponentialMeanResidualLife
funcs.doc:
ExponentialMeanResidualLife returns the mean remaining life given survival to
age t, which is the constant 1/λ for the exponential distribution.

funcs.name: ExponentialMedian
funcs.doc: ExponentialMedian returns the median lifetime ln(2)/λ.

funcs.name: ExponentialPDF
funcs.doc:
ExponentialPDF returns the probability density f(t)=λe^{-λt} of the
exponential lifetime distribution with failure rate lambda>0 at time t>=0.

funcs.name: ExponentialQuantile
funcs.doc:
ExponentialQuantile returns the time t at which F(t)=p, i.e.
t=-ln(1-p)/λ, for p in [0,1).

funcs.name: ExponentialRateFromMTTF
funcs.doc: ExponentialRateFromMTTF recovers the failure rate λ=1/MTTF.

funcs.name: ExponentialRateFromReliability
funcs.doc:
ExponentialRateFromReliability recovers the failure rate λ from a reliability
observation R at time t>0: λ=-ln(R)/t.

funcs.name: ExponentialReliability
funcs.doc:
ExponentialReliability returns the survival probability R(t)=e^{-λt} for the
exponential distribution with failure rate lambda>0 at time t>=0.

funcs.name: ExponentialStdDev
funcs.doc:
ExponentialStdDev returns the standard deviation 1/λ of the exponential
distribution.

funcs.name: ExponentialVariance
funcs.doc:
ExponentialVariance returns the variance 1/λ² of the exponential
distribution.

funcs.name: FIT
funcs.doc:
FIT returns the failure-in-time rate: the number of failures expected per one
billion (10^9) device-hours for a constant hourly failure rate.

funcs.name: FITToRate
funcs.doc:
FITToRate converts a failure-in-time value (failures per 10^9 hours) back to
an hourly failure rate.

funcs.name: FailureRate
funcs.doc:
FailureRate returns the instantaneous hazard rate f/R from a probability
density f and a reliability (survival) value r. It returns NaN if r is not
positive.

funcs.name: FailureRateFromMTBF
funcs.doc: FailureRateFromMTBF returns the constant failure rate 1/MTBF.

funcs.name: FailuresPerMillionHours
funcs.doc:
FailuresPerMillionHours converts a failure rate expressed per unit time into
the FIT-like measure of expected failures per one million hours.

funcs.name: GammaCDF
funcs.signature: func GammaCDF(t, k, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GammaCDF returns the failure probability F(t)=P(k,t/θ), the regularized
lower incomplete gamma function.

funcs.name: GammaCumulativeHazard
funcs.signature: func GammaCumulativeHazard(t, k, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc: GammaCumulativeHazard returns the cumulative hazard H(t)=-ln R(t).

funcs.name: GammaHazard
funcs.signature: func GammaHazard(t, k, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc: GammaHazard returns the hazard rate f(t)/R(t) of the gamma distribution.

funcs.name: GammaMTTF
funcs.doc: GammaMTTF returns the mean time to failure, identical to the mean.

funcs.name: GammaMean
funcs.doc: GammaMean returns the mean lifetime kθ.

funcs.name: GammaMeanResidualLife
funcs.signature: func GammaMeanResidualLife(t, k, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GammaMeanResidualLife returns the mean remaining life given survival to age
t, computed by numerically integrating the reliability from t to infinity.

funcs.name: GammaMode
funcs.doc:
GammaMode returns the mode of the gamma distribution. For k>=1 it is
(k-1)θ; for k<1 the density is unbounded at 0 and the mode is 0.

funcs.name: GammaPDF
funcs.signature: func GammaPDF(t, k, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GammaPDF returns the probability density of the gamma lifetime distribution
with shape k>0 and scale theta>0 at time t>=0:
f(t)=t^{k-1}e^{-t/θ}/(θ^k Γ(k)).

funcs.name: GammaQuantile
funcs.signature: func GammaQuantile(p, k, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GammaQuantile returns the time at which F(t)=p, computed by inverting the
regularized incomplete gamma function.

funcs.name: GammaReliability
funcs.signature: func GammaReliability(t, k, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GammaReliability returns the survival probability R(t)=Q(k,t/θ), the
regularized upper incomplete gamma function.

funcs.name: GammaStdDev
funcs.doc: GammaStdDev returns the standard deviation of the gamma distribution.

funcs.name: GammaVariance
funcs.doc: GammaVariance returns the variance kθ².

funcs.name: GompertzCDF
funcs.signature: func GompertzCDF(t, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc: GompertzCDF returns the failure probability F(t)=1-R(t).

funcs.name: GompertzCumulativeHazard
funcs.signature: func GompertzCumulativeHazard(t, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GompertzCumulativeHazard returns the cumulative hazard
H(t)=(a/b)(e^{bt}-1) of the Gompertz distribution.

funcs.name: GompertzHazard
funcs.signature: func GompertzHazard(t, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GompertzHazard returns the exponentially increasing hazard rate
h(t)=a·e^{bt} of the Gompertz lifetime distribution, with a>0 and b>0.

funcs.name: GompertzMakehamCDF
funcs.signature: func GompertzMakehamCDF(t, lambda, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc: GompertzMakehamCDF returns the failure probability F(t)=1-R(t).

funcs.name: GompertzMakehamCumulativeHazard
funcs.signature: func GompertzMakehamCumulativeHazard(t, lambda, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GompertzMakehamCumulativeHazard returns the cumulative hazard
H(t)=λt+(a/b)(e^{bt}-1).

funcs.name: GompertzMakehamHazard
funcs.signature: func GompertzMakehamHazard(t, lambda, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GompertzMakehamHazard returns the hazard rate h(t)=λ+a·e^{bt} of the
Gompertz–Makeham law, which adds an age-independent Makeham term lambda>=0
to the Gompertz hazard.

funcs.name: GompertzMakehamPDF
funcs.signature: func GompertzMakehamPDF(t, lambda, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc: GompertzMakehamPDF returns the probability density h(t)R(t).

funcs.name: GompertzMakehamReliability
funcs.signature: func GompertzMakehamReliability(t, lambda, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GompertzMakehamReliability returns the survival probability R(t)=exp(-H(t))
of the Gompertz–Makeham law.

funcs.name: GompertzMean
funcs.doc:
GompertzMean returns the mean lifetime, computed by numerically integrating
the reliability from 0 to infinity.

funcs.name: GompertzMedian
funcs.doc: GompertzMedian returns the median lifetime, the 0.5 quantile.

funcs.name: GompertzPDF
funcs.signature: func GompertzPDF(t, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GompertzPDF returns the probability density h(t)R(t) of the Gompertz
distribution.

funcs.name: GompertzQuantile
funcs.signature: func GompertzQuantile(p, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GompertzQuantile returns the time at which F(t)=p:
t=(1/b)ln(1-(b/a)ln(1-p)).

funcs.name: GompertzReliability
funcs.signature: func GompertzReliability(t, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
GompertzReliability returns the survival probability
R(t)=exp(-(a/b)(e^{bt}-1)).

funcs.name: HazardFromReliability
funcs.doc:
HazardFromReliability converts a reliability value r into its cumulative
hazard H=-ln(r).

funcs.name: HazardRatio
funcs.doc:
HazardRatio returns the ratio of two hazard rates h1/h2, the constant
proportional-hazards multiplier of group 1 relative to group 2.

funcs.name: HjorthCDF
funcs.signature: func HjorthCDF(t, delta, beta, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc: HjorthCDF returns the failure probability F(t)=1-R(t) of the Hjorth model.

funcs.name: HjorthCumulativeHazard
funcs.signature: func HjorthCumulativeHazard(t, delta, beta, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc:
HjorthCumulativeHazard returns the cumulative hazard of the Hjorth model,

	H(t) = δ·t²/2 + (β/θ)·ln(1+θ·t).

funcs.name: HjorthHazard
funcs.signature: func HjorthHazard(t, delta, beta, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc:
HjorthHazard evaluates the Hjorth-distribution hazard rate at time t>=0:

	h(t) = δ·t + β/(1+θ·t)

with δ>=0, β>=0 and θ>0. Depending on the parameters the hazard can be
increasing, decreasing, or bathtub-shaped, making the Hjorth model a compact
alternative to the additive bathtub curve.

funcs.name: HjorthPDF
funcs.signature: func HjorthPDF(t, delta, beta, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc: HjorthPDF returns the probability density h(t)R(t) of the Hjorth model.

funcs.name: HjorthReliability
funcs.signature: func HjorthReliability(t, delta, beta, theta float64) float64
funcs.doc:
HjorthReliability returns the survival probability
R(t)=exp(-δt²/2)·(1+θt)^{-β/θ}.

funcs.name: InherentAvailability
funcs.doc:
InherentAvailability returns MTTF/(MTTF+MTTR), the availability that accounts
only for corrective maintenance under idealized support conditions.

funcs.name: InstantaneousAvailability
funcs.signature: func InstantaneousAvailability(t, lambda, mu float64) float64
funcs.doc:
InstantaneousAvailability returns the availability at time t of a two-state
Markov model that starts operational:
A(t)=μ/(λ+μ)+λ/(λ+μ)·e^{-(λ+μ)t}.

funcs.name: KofNReliability
funcs.signature: func KofNReliability(k, n int, r float64) float64
funcs.doc:
KofNReliability returns the reliability of a k-out-of-n system of identical
components, each with reliability r, that works when at least k of its n
components work: Σ_{i=k}^{n} C(n,i) rⁱ(1-r)^{n-i}.

funcs.name: KofNReliabilityGeneral
funcs.doc:
KofNReliabilityGeneral returns the exact reliability of a k-out-of-n system
whose n components may have different reliabilities rs. It works when at
least k components work and is evaluated by a dynamic program over the
probability of exactly j working components.

funcs.name: LognormalCDF
funcs.signature: func LognormalCDF(t, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc: LognormalCDF returns the failure probability F(t)=Φ((ln t-μ)/σ).

funcs.name: LognormalCumulativeHazard
funcs.signature: func LognormalCumulativeHazard(t, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc: LognormalCumulativeHazard returns the cumulative hazard H(t)=-ln R(t).

funcs.name: LognormalHazard
funcs.signature: func LognormalHazard(t, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
LognormalHazard returns the hazard rate f(t)/R(t) of the lognormal
distribution, which rises from 0, peaks, and then decreases.

funcs.name: LognormalMTTF
funcs.doc: LognormalMTTF returns the mean time to failure, identical to the mean.

funcs.name: LognormalMean
funcs.doc: LognormalMean returns the mean lifetime exp(μ+σ²/2).

funcs.name: LognormalMeanResidualLife
funcs.signature: func LognormalMeanResidualLife(t, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
LognormalMeanResidualLife returns the mean remaining life given survival to
age t, computed by numerically integrating the reliability from t to
infinity.

funcs.name: LognormalMedian
funcs.doc: LognormalMedian returns the median lifetime e^{μ}.

funcs.name: LognormalMode
funcs.doc: LognormalMode returns the mode of the lognormal distribution, e^{μ-σ²}.

funcs.name: LognormalPDF
funcs.signature: func LognormalPDF(t, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
LognormalPDF returns the probability density of the lognormal lifetime
distribution whose logarithm is normal with mean mu and standard deviation
sigma>0, at time t>0.

funcs.name: LognormalQuantile
funcs.signature: func LognormalQuantile(p, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
LognormalQuantile returns the time at which F(t)=p:
t=exp(μ+σΦ^{-1}(p)).

funcs.name: LognormalReliability
funcs.signature: func LognormalReliability(t, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc: LognormalReliability returns the survival probability R(t)=1-Φ((ln t-μ)/σ).

funcs.name: LognormalStdDev
funcs.doc: LognormalStdDev returns the standard deviation of the lognormal distribution.

funcs.name: LognormalVariance
funcs.doc: LognormalVariance returns the variance (e^{σ²}-1)e^{2μ+σ²}.

funcs.name: MTBF
funcs.doc: MTBF returns the mean time between failures MTTF+MTTR of a repairable item.

funcs.name: MTBFFromData
funcs.signature: func MTBFFromData(totalOperatingTime float64, failures int) float64
funcs.doc:
MTBFFromData estimates the mean time between failures as the total operating
time divided by the number of failures observed.

funcs.name: MTBFFromFailureRate
funcs.doc:
MTBFFromFailureRate returns the mean time between failures 1/λ for a constant
failure rate lambda>0.

funcs.name: MTTFFromData
funcs.doc:
MTTFFromData estimates the mean time to failure as the arithmetic mean of a
set of (complete, uncensored) failure times. It returns an error if the
slice is empty or contains a negative time.

funcs.name: MTTFFromReliability
funcs.signature: func MTTFFromReliability(r func(float64) float64, scale float64) float64
funcs.doc:
MTTFFromReliability computes the mean time to failure by numerically
integrating a reliability function R over [0,∞). The function must be
non-increasing and eventually decay to zero; scale is a characteristic time
used to size the integration panels.

funcs.name: MTTR
funcs.doc: MTTR returns the mean time to repair 1/μ from a constant repair rate mu>0.

funcs.name: MaxSeriesForReliability
funcs.doc:
MaxSeriesForReliability returns the largest number of identical series
components, each with reliability r in (0,1], whose product reliability still
meets or exceeds target: n=⌊ln(target)/ln(r)⌋.

funcs.name: MeanRank
funcs.doc:
MeanRank returns the mean plotting position i/(n+1) of the i-th of n ordered
failures. The rank i is 1-based.

funcs.name: MedianRank
funcs.doc:
MedianRank returns Bernard's approximation to the median rank of the i-th of
n ordered failures, (i-0.3)/(n+0.4), commonly used to plot empirical
probabilities on Weibull paper. The rank i is 1-based.

funcs.name: MinParallelForReliability
funcs.doc:
MinParallelForReliability returns the smallest number of identical parallel
components, each with reliability r in (0,1), needed to reach a system
reliability of at least target: n=⌈ln(1-target)/ln(1-r)⌉.

funcs.name: NormalCDF
funcs.signature: func NormalCDF(t, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc: NormalCDF returns the failure probability F(t)=Φ((t-μ)/σ).

funcs.name: NormalCumulativeHazard
funcs.signature: func NormalCumulativeHazard(t, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc: NormalCumulativeHazard returns the cumulative hazard H(t)=-ln R(t).

funcs.name: NormalHazard
funcs.signature: func NormalHazard(t, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
NormalHazard returns the hazard rate f(t)/R(t) of the normal distribution,
which increases monotonically with t.

funcs.name: NormalMTTF
funcs.doc:
NormalMTTF returns the mean time to failure, equal to mu for the normal
model.

funcs.name: NormalPDF
funcs.signature: func NormalPDF(t, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
NormalPDF returns the probability density of the normal lifetime
distribution with mean mu and standard deviation sigma>0 at time t. Although
the normal distribution has support over the whole real line, it is a common
wear-out lifetime model when mu is large relative to sigma.

funcs.name: NormalQuantile
funcs.signature: func NormalQuantile(p, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
NormalQuantile returns the time at which F(t)=p:
t=μ+σΦ^{-1}(p).

funcs.name: NormalReliability
funcs.signature: func NormalReliability(t, mu, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc: NormalReliability returns the survival probability R(t)=1-Φ((t-μ)/σ).

funcs.name: OperationalAvailability
funcs.doc:
OperationalAvailability returns uptime/(uptime+downtime), the availability
observed in operation including all sources of downtime.

funcs.name: ParallelFailureProbability
funcs.doc:
ParallelFailureProbability returns the probability that a parallel system
fails, the product ∏(1-rᵢ).

funcs.name: ParallelReliability
funcs.doc:
ParallelReliability returns the reliability of a parallel (active-redundant)
system, 1-∏(1-rᵢ). A parallel system fails only if every component fails.
With no arguments it returns 0 (an empty system never works).

funcs.name: ParallelReliabilityIdentical
funcs.signature: func ParallelReliabilityIdentical(r float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
ParallelReliabilityIdentical returns the reliability 1-(1-r)ⁿ of a parallel
system of n identical components each with reliability r.

funcs.name: ParallelSeriesReliability
funcs.doc:
ParallelSeriesReliability returns the reliability of a system built from
series strings connected in parallel: each inner slice is a series string
and the strings are combined in parallel.

funcs.name: ParallelSystemMTTF
funcs.signature: func ParallelSystemMTTF(lambda float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
ParallelSystemMTTF returns the mean time to failure of a parallel system of n
identical exponential units with failure rate lambda, which is the harmonic
sum (1/λ)Σ_{i=1}^{n}1/i.

funcs.name: ProportionalHazardsReliability
funcs.doc:
ProportionalHazardsReliability returns the reliability of an item whose
hazard is a constant multiple hr of a baseline hazard: R(t)=R0(t)^{hr},
where r0 is the baseline reliability at t. This is the survival form of the
Cox proportional-hazards model.

funcs.name: RayleighCDF
funcs.doc: RayleighCDF returns the failure probability F(t)=1-e^{-t²/(2σ²)}.

funcs.name: RayleighCumulativeHazard
funcs.doc: RayleighCumulativeHazard returns the cumulative hazard H(t)=t²/(2σ²).

funcs.name: RayleighHazard
funcs.doc: RayleighHazard returns the linearly increasing hazard rate h(t)=t/σ².

funcs.name: RayleighMTTF
funcs.doc: RayleighMTTF returns the mean time to failure, identical to the mean.

funcs.name: RayleighMean
funcs.doc: RayleighMean returns the mean lifetime σ√(π/2).

funcs.name: RayleighMedian
funcs.doc: RayleighMedian returns the median lifetime σ√(2ln2).

funcs.name: RayleighMode
funcs.doc: RayleighMode returns the mode of the Rayleigh distribution, equal to σ.

funcs.name: RayleighPDF
funcs.doc:
RayleighPDF returns the probability density of the Rayleigh lifetime
distribution with scale sigma>0 at time t>=0:
f(t)=(t/σ²)e^{-t²/(2σ²)}. The Rayleigh model is the Weibull model with
shape k=2 and a linearly increasing hazard rate.

funcs.name: RayleighQuantile
funcs.doc:
RayleighQuantile returns the time at which F(t)=p:
t=σ√(-2ln(1-p)).

funcs.name: RayleighReliability
funcs.doc: RayleighReliability returns the survival probability R(t)=e^{-t²/(2σ²)}.

funcs.name: RayleighStdDev
funcs.doc: RayleighStdDev returns the standard deviation of the Rayleigh distribution.

funcs.name: RayleighVariance
funcs.doc: RayleighVariance returns the variance (2-π/2)σ².

funcs.name: RedundancyGain
funcs.doc:
RedundancyGain returns the improvement in reliability obtained by placing a
single spare component of reliability r in active parallel with an existing
component of reliability r: the difference (1-(1-r)²)-r = r(1-r).

funcs.name: ReliabilityAtTime
funcs.doc:
ReliabilityAtTime returns the reliability of a constant-failure-rate item at
time t: R(t)=e^{-λt}. It is a convenience alias of ExponentialReliability.

funcs.name: ReliabilityFromHazard
funcs.doc:
ReliabilityFromHazard converts a cumulative hazard H>=0 into a reliability
value R=e^{-H}.

funcs.name: ReliabilityImportanceBirnbaum
funcs.doc:
ReliabilityImportanceBirnbaum returns the Birnbaum reliability importance of
a component in a system, the partial derivative of system reliability with
respect to that component's reliability. It is estimated from the system
reliability with the component forced to work (rWorking) and forced to fail
(rFailed): I_B = R(working) - R(failed).

funcs.name: RepairRateFromMTTR
funcs.doc: RepairRateFromMTTR returns the repair rate μ=1/MTTR.

funcs.name: SeriesFailureProbability
funcs.doc:
SeriesFailureProbability returns the probability that a series system fails,
1 minus its reliability.

funcs.name: SeriesParallelReliability
funcs.doc:
SeriesParallelReliability returns the reliability of a system built from
parallel groups connected in series: each inner slice is a parallel
sub-system and the sub-systems are combined in series.

funcs.name: SeriesReliability
funcs.doc:
SeriesReliability returns the reliability of a series system, the product of
the component reliabilities. A series system works only if every component
works. With no arguments it returns 1 (the empty product).

funcs.name: SeriesReliabilityIdentical
funcs.signature: func SeriesReliabilityIdentical(r float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
SeriesReliabilityIdentical returns the reliability rⁿ of a series system of n
identical components each with reliability r.

funcs.name: SeriesSystemFailureRate
funcs.doc:
SeriesSystemFailureRate returns the total failure rate Σλᵢ of a series system
of independent constant-rate components.

funcs.name: SeriesSystemMTTF
funcs.doc:
SeriesSystemMTTF returns the mean time to failure 1/Σλᵢ of a series system of
independent constant-rate components.

funcs.name: SortedFailureTimes
funcs.doc: SortedFailureTimes returns a sorted copy of a slice of failure times.

funcs.name: StandardNormalCDF
funcs.doc:
StandardNormalCDF returns Φ(x), the standard-normal cumulative distribution
function.

funcs.name: StandardNormalQuantile
funcs.doc:
StandardNormalQuantile returns Φ^{-1}(p), the standard-normal quantile
(probit) function.

funcs.name: StandbyRedundancyMTTF
funcs.signature: func StandbyRedundancyMTTF(lambda float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
StandbyRedundancyMTTF returns the mean time to failure n/λ of a cold-standby
system of n identical exponential units with per-unit failure rate lambda
and perfect switching.

funcs.name: StandbyRedundancyReliability
funcs.signature: func StandbyRedundancyReliability(t, lambda float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
StandbyRedundancyReliability returns the reliability at time t of a system of
n identical exponential units (one active, n-1 in cold standby with perfect
switching) with per-unit failure rate lambda. The number of failures before
system failure follows a Poisson process, giving
R(t)=e^{-λt}Σ_{i=0}^{n-1}(λt)^i/i!.

funcs.name: SteadyStateAvailability
funcs.doc:
SteadyStateAvailability returns the limiting availability μ/(λ+μ) of a
two-state (up/down) Markov model with constant failure rate lambda and
constant repair rate mu.

funcs.name: Unavailability
funcs.doc: Unavailability returns 1 minus the availability computed from MTBF and MTTR.

funcs.name: WeibullB10Life
funcs.doc:
WeibullB10Life returns the B10 life, the time by which 10% of the population
has failed (the 0.10 quantile).

funcs.name: WeibullCDF
funcs.signature: func WeibullCDF(t, k, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc: WeibullCDF returns the failure probability F(t)=1-e^{-(t/λ)^k}.

funcs.name: WeibullCharacteristicLife
funcs.doc:
WeibullCharacteristicLife returns the scale parameter λ, the time by which
63.2% of a Weibull-distributed population has failed.

funcs.name: WeibullConditionalReliability
funcs.signature: func WeibullConditionalReliability(t, x, k, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc:
WeibullConditionalReliability returns the probability that a component of age
t survives an additional time x: R(t+x)/R(t).

funcs.name: WeibullCumulativeHazard
funcs.signature: func WeibullCumulativeHazard(t, k, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc: WeibullCumulativeHazard returns the cumulative hazard H(t)=(t/λ)^k.

funcs.name: WeibullHazard
funcs.signature: func WeibullHazard(t, k, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc:
WeibullHazard returns the hazard rate h(t)=(k/λ)(t/λ)^{k-1}. For k<1 the
hazard decreases (infant mortality), k=1 gives a constant rate and k>1 gives
an increasing (wear-out) rate.

funcs.name: WeibullMTTF
funcs.doc: WeibullMTTF returns the mean time to failure, identical to the mean.

funcs.name: WeibullMean
funcs.doc: WeibullMean returns the mean lifetime λΓ(1+1/k).

funcs.name: WeibullMeanResidualLife
funcs.signature: func WeibullMeanResidualLife(t, k, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc:
WeibullMeanResidualLife returns the mean remaining life given survival to age
t, computed by numerically integrating the reliability from t to infinity.

funcs.name: WeibullMedian
funcs.doc: WeibullMedian returns the median lifetime λ(ln2)^{1/k}.

funcs.name: WeibullMode
funcs.doc:
WeibullMode returns the mode of the Weibull distribution. For k<=1 the mode
is 0; for k>1 it is λ((k-1)/k)^{1/k}.

funcs.name: WeibullPDF
funcs.signature: func WeibullPDF(t, k, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc:
WeibullPDF returns the probability density of the two-parameter Weibull
distribution with shape k>0 and scale lambda>0 at time t>=0:
f(t)=(k/λ)(t/λ)^{k-1} e^{-(t/λ)^k}.

funcs.name: WeibullQuantile
funcs.signature: func WeibullQuantile(p, k, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc:
WeibullQuantile returns the time at which F(t)=p:
t=λ(-ln(1-p))^{1/k}.

funcs.name: WeibullReliability
funcs.signature: func WeibullReliability(t, k, lambda float64) float64
funcs.doc: WeibullReliability returns the survival probability R(t)=e^{-(t/λ)^k}.

funcs.name: WeibullStdDev
funcs.doc: WeibullStdDev returns the standard deviation of the Weibull distribution.

funcs.name: WeibullVariance
funcs.doc: WeibullVariance returns the variance λ²[Γ(1+2/k)-Γ(1+1/k)²].

examples.name: Availability
KofNReliability
SeriesReliability
WeibullReliability
KaplanMeier

packages.name: rootfind
packages.synopsis: Package rootfind implements polynomial and nonlinear root finding in pure Go.
packages.doc:
Package rootfind implements polynomial and nonlinear root finding in pure Go.

The package is organized around two polynomial representations and a family
of solvers that operate on them or on arbitrary scalar functions.

# Polynomial representation

Polynomials are stored as coefficient slices in ascending order of power: the
slice p represents the polynomial

	p(x) = p[0] + p[1]*x + p[2]*x^2 + ... + p[n]*x^n

so that p[i] is the coefficient of x^i and the last element is the leading
coefficient. [Poly] holds real coefficients ([]float64) and [CPoly] holds
complex coefficients ([]complex128). This ordering makes indexing by power
trivial and matches Horner evaluation from the high-order end.

# Global polynomial solvers

Several methods find all roots of a polynomial at once. [DurandKerner]
(the Weierstrass method) and [AberthEhrlich] are simultaneous iterations that
converge to every complex root in parallel; [Bairstow] extracts real
quadratic factors to recover complex-conjugate pairs using only real
arithmetic; and [CompanionEigenvalues] finds roots as the eigenvalues of the
companion matrix via the Francis double-shift QR algorithm. [PolyRoots] is a
high-level convenience that returns all roots, and [RealRoots] filters those
with negligible imaginary part.

# Real-root theory

[SturmSequence] and the counting routines built on it give the exact number
of distinct real roots in any interval, from which [IsolateRoots] produces
disjoint bracketing intervals that are then refined to full precision. The
classical sign-based theorems are provided too: [DescartesRuleOfSigns],
[BudanFourierCount], and a collection of a-priori root-magnitude bounds such
as [CauchyBound], [LagrangeBound], and [FujiwaraBound].

# Scalar solvers

For a general continuous function of one variable the package offers bracketing
methods ([Bisection], [FalsePosition], [Brent], [Ridders]), open methods
([Secant], [Steffensen], [FixedPoint]), and derivative-based methods
([Newton], [Halley]). [Laguerre] is a globally very reliable polynomial
solver. Each returns a [Result] recording the located root, the residual, the
iteration count, and whether convergence was achieved.

# Multiplicity and deflation

gcd(p, p') isolates repeated factors: [SquareFree] returns the squarefree
part, [SquareFreeFactorization] performs Yun's algorithm to group roots by
multiplicity, and [Multiplicity] measures the order of a specific root.
Roots may be removed one at a time with [Poly.DeflateReal] or [CPoly.Deflate].

All algorithms use only the Go standard library and perform no allocation
beyond what their results require.

consts.signature: const DefaultMaxIter = 200
consts.doc:
DefaultMaxIter is the default iteration budget used by scalar solvers when the
caller passes a non-positive maximum.

consts.signature: const DefaultRootTol = 1e-14
consts.doc:
DefaultRootTol is the default convergence tolerance for the simultaneous
polynomial root solvers.

consts.signature: const DefaultTol = 1e-12
consts.doc:
DefaultTol is the default absolute tolerance used by scalar solvers when the
caller passes a non-positive tolerance.

vars.signature: var ErrBadInput = errors.New("rootfind: invalid input")
vars.doc:
ErrBadInput is returned for malformed arguments that are not covered by a more
specific sentinel error, such as a negative tolerance or iteration count.

vars.signature: var ErrDegreeTooLow = errors.New("rootfind: polynomial degree too low for this operation")
vars.doc:
ErrDegreeTooLow is returned when a routine requires a polynomial of at least
a certain degree (for example a nonconstant polynomial) and it is not met.

vars.signature: var ErrEmptyInterval = errors.New("rootfind: invalid or empty interval")
vars.doc:
ErrEmptyInterval is returned by interval routines when the supplied interval
is degenerate or has its endpoints reversed.

vars.signature: var ErrNoBracket = errors.New("rootfind: endpoints do not bracket a root")
vars.doc:
ErrNoBracket is returned by a bracketing solver when the supplied endpoints
do not straddle a root, i.e. the function has the same sign at both ends.

vars.signature: var ErrNoConvergence = errors.New("rootfind: iteration did not converge")
vars.doc:
ErrNoConvergence is returned by an iterative solver that failed to reach the
requested tolerance within its iteration budget.

vars.signature: var ErrZeroDerivative = errors.New("rootfind: derivative vanished during iteration")
vars.doc:
ErrZeroDerivative is returned by a derivative-based solver when the derivative
vanishes at the current iterate, preventing a well-defined update step.

vars.signature: var ErrZeroPolynomial = errors.New("rootfind: operation undefined for the zero polynomial")
vars.doc:
ErrZeroPolynomial is returned when an operation that is undefined on the zero
polynomial (such as division or making a polynomial monic) receives one.

types.name: CPoly
types.signature: type CPoly []complex128
types.doc:
CPoly is a complex polynomial stored as coefficients in ascending order of
power: c[i] is the coefficient of x^i and c[len(c)-1] is the leading
coefficient.

funcs.name: CFromRoots
funcs.doc:
CFromRoots builds the monic complex polynomial with the given roots, the
product (x - r0)(x - r1)... .

funcs.name: NewCPoly
funcs.doc:
NewCPoly returns a CPoly built from the given ascending-order coefficients.
The arguments are copied.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the sum c+q.

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At is an alias for Eval, evaluating c(x).

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent copy of c.

methods.name: Coeff
methods.doc: Coeff returns the coefficient of x^i, or 0 when i is out of range.

methods.name: Deflate
methods.signature: func (c CPoly) Deflate(r complex128) (quo CPoly, remainder complex128)
methods.doc:
Deflate divides c by the linear factor (x - r), returning the quotient and the
remainder c(r) via synthetic division. When r is a root the remainder is
(near) zero.

methods.name: Degree
methods.doc:
Degree returns the degree of c, the largest index with a nonzero coefficient,
or -1 for the zero polynomial.

methods.name: Derivative
methods.doc: Derivative returns the formal derivative c'(x).

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc: Eval evaluates c(x) using Horner's method.

methods.name: EvalDeriv
methods.signature: func (c CPoly) EvalDeriv(x complex128) (val, deriv complex128)
methods.doc: EvalDeriv evaluates c(x) and c'(x) together in a single Horner sweep.

methods.name: EvalDeriv2
methods.signature: func (c CPoly) EvalDeriv2(x complex128) (val, d1, d2 complex128)
methods.doc: EvalDeriv2 evaluates c, c', and c” at x in one Horner sweep.

methods.name: IsMonic
methods.doc: IsMonic reports whether the leading coefficient of c equals 1 within tol.

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc: IsZero reports whether c is the zero polynomial.

methods.name: LeadingCoeff
methods.doc: LeadingCoeff returns the leading coefficient, or 0 for the zero polynomial.

methods.name: Monic
methods.doc:
Monic returns c divided by its leading coefficient. It returns
ErrZeroPolynomial when c is zero.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the product c*q by convolution.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns -c.

methods.name: NumTerms
methods.doc: NumTerms returns the number of nonzero coefficients of c.

methods.name: Pow
methods.doc: Pow returns c raised to the nonnegative integer power k by repeated squaring.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the polynomial s*c.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String renders c in descending-power notation with parenthesized complex
coefficients, for example "(1+2i)x^2 + (3+0i)".

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the difference c-q.

methods.name: ToReal
methods.doc:
ToReal returns the real polynomial formed by taking the real part of each
coefficient of c, discarding imaginary parts.

methods.name: Trim
methods.doc:
Trim returns c with trailing zero coefficients removed; the underlying array
is shared with c.

types.name: ComplexRootMultiplicity
types.doc: ComplexRootMultiplicity pairs a complex root with its multiplicity.

funcs.name: GroupComplexRoots
funcs.doc:
GroupComplexRoots clusters a list of complex roots so that roots within
distance tol of one another are merged into a single representative (their
centroid) carrying a multiplicity equal to the cluster size. This recovers
multiplicity from the output of a numerical global solver, where a root of
multiplicity m appears as a tight cluster of m nearby approximate roots.

types.name: Func
types.signature: type Func func(x float64) float64
types.doc: Func is a real scalar function of one real variable.

types.name: Poly
types.signature: type Poly []float64
types.doc:
Poly is a real polynomial stored as coefficients in ascending order of power:
p[i] is the coefficient of x^i, so p[len(p)-1] is the leading coefficient.
The zero polynomial is represented by an empty or all-zero slice.

funcs.name: DeflateRoots
funcs.doc:
DeflateRoots divides p successively by (x - r) for each r in roots, returning
the final quotient. It is the composition of repeated [Poly.DeflateReal] and
removes the listed roots from p.

funcs.name: FourierSequence
funcs.doc:
FourierSequence returns the Fourier (derivative) sequence of p:
p, p', p”, ... , p^(n), a chain of length deg(p)+1 whose sign variations feed
the Budan-Fourier theorem.

funcs.name: FromRoots
funcs.doc:
FromRoots builds the monic real polynomial whose roots are exactly the given
values, i.e. the product (x - r0)(x - r1)... .

funcs.name: FromRootsWithLead
funcs.doc:
FromRootsWithLead builds the real polynomial lead*(x-r0)(x-r1)... with the
given leading coefficient.

funcs.name: NewPoly
funcs.doc:
NewPoly returns a Poly built from the given ascending-order coefficients. The
arguments are copied, so the caller may reuse the slice afterwards.

funcs.name: PolyFromDesc
funcs.doc:
PolyFromDesc returns a Poly from coefficients given in descending order of
power, i.e. highest-degree coefficient first, as polynomials are usually
written. It is the inverse of [Poly.CoeffsDesc].

funcs.name: SquareFree
funcs.doc:
SquareFree returns the squarefree part of p, that is p divided by
gcd(p, p'). The result has the same roots as p but each with multiplicity one.
It returns ErrZeroPolynomial for the zero polynomial and a constant for a
nonzero constant input.

funcs.name: SturmSequence
funcs.doc:
SturmSequence returns the canonical Sturm chain of the polynomial p:

	p0 = p, p1 = p', p_{k+1} = -(p_{k-1} mod p_k)

continuing until a constant is reached. The sign-variation counts of this
sequence, via [SturmVariations], give the exact number of distinct real roots
of p in any interval by Sturm's theorem. The zero polynomial yields an empty
sequence.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the sum p+q.

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At is an alias for Eval, evaluating p(x).

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent copy of p.

methods.name: Coeff
methods.doc: Coeff returns the coefficient of x^i, or 0 when i is out of range.

methods.name: CoeffsDesc
methods.doc:
CoeffsDesc returns the coefficients of p in descending order of power,
highest-degree first, trimmed of leading zeros. The zero polynomial yields a
single 0.

methods.name: Compose
methods.doc:
Compose returns the polynomial p(q(x)) formed by substituting q into p, using
Horner's method over polynomial arithmetic.

methods.name: DeflateReal
methods.signature: func (p Poly) DeflateReal(r float64) (quo Poly, remainder float64)
methods.doc:
DeflateReal divides p by the linear factor (x - r), returning the quotient and
the remainder p(r). When r is an exact root the remainder is (near) zero. The
division is performed by synthetic (Horner) division.

methods.name: Degree
methods.doc:
Degree returns the degree of p, that is the largest index whose coefficient
is nonzero. The zero polynomial has degree -1 by convention.

methods.name: Derivative
methods.doc: Derivative returns the formal derivative p'(x).

methods.name: DivMod
methods.signature: func (p Poly) DivMod(d Poly) (q, r Poly, err error)
methods.doc:
DivMod divides p by d and returns the quotient q and remainder r satisfying
p = q*d + r with deg(r) < deg(d). It returns ErrZeroPolynomial when d is zero.

methods.name: Equal
methods.signature: func (p Poly) Equal(q Poly, tol float64) bool
methods.doc:
Equal reports whether p and q are equal as polynomials, that is every
coefficient agrees within tol after ignoring trailing zeros.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc:
Eval evaluates p(x) using Horner's method, which is both fast and numerically
stable.

methods.name: EvalComplex
methods.doc: EvalComplex evaluates p at a complex argument using Horner's method.

methods.name: EvalDeriv
methods.signature: func (p Poly) EvalDeriv(x float64) (val, deriv float64)
methods.doc:
EvalDeriv evaluates p(x) and p'(x) simultaneously with a single Horner sweep,
returning the value and the first derivative.

methods.name: EvalDeriv2
methods.signature: func (p Poly) EvalDeriv2(x float64) (val, d1, d2 float64)
methods.doc: EvalDeriv2 evaluates p, p', and p” at x in a single Horner sweep.

methods.name: GCD
methods.doc:
GCD returns a greatest common divisor of p and q using the Euclidean
algorithm, normalized to be monic. The GCD of two zero polynomials is zero.

methods.name: InfNorm
methods.doc:
InfNorm returns the largest absolute coefficient of p (the sup norm of its
coefficient vector).

methods.name: Integral
methods.doc: Integral returns an antiderivative of p whose constant term equals c.

methods.name: IsConstant
methods.doc: IsConstant reports whether p has degree 0 or is the zero polynomial.

methods.name: IsMonic
methods.doc: IsMonic reports whether the leading coefficient of p equals 1 within tol.

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc: IsZero reports whether p is the zero polynomial, i.e. every coefficient is 0.

methods.name: L1Norm
methods.doc: L1Norm returns the sum of absolute values of the coefficients of p.

methods.name: L2Norm
methods.doc: L2Norm returns the Euclidean norm of the coefficient vector of p.

methods.name: LeadingCoeff
methods.doc:
LeadingCoeff returns the coefficient of the highest-degree term, or 0 for the
zero polynomial.

methods.name: Monic
methods.doc:
Monic returns p divided by its leading coefficient, so the result has leading
coefficient 1. It returns ErrZeroPolynomial when p is zero.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc:
Mul returns the product p*q computed by convolution of the coefficient
sequences.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns -p.

methods.name: NumTerms
methods.doc: NumTerms returns the number of nonzero coefficients of p.

methods.name: Pow
methods.doc:
Pow returns p raised to the nonnegative integer power k by repeated squaring.
Pow(0) is the constant polynomial 1.

methods.name: ProductOfRoots
methods.doc:
ProductOfRoots returns the product of all roots of p counted with
multiplicity, which by Vieta's formulas equals (-1)^n * a_0/a_n.

methods.name: Quo
methods.doc: Quo returns the quotient of p divided by d, discarding the remainder.

methods.name: ReflectX
methods.doc:
ReflectX returns the polynomial p(-x), whose positive roots are the negatives
of the negative roots of p.

methods.name: Rem
methods.doc: Rem returns the remainder of p divided by d.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.doc:
Reverse returns the reversal (reciprocal) polynomial x^n * p(1/x), whose
coefficient sequence is that of p reversed. Its nonzero roots are the
reciprocals of the nonzero roots of p.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the polynomial s*p.

methods.name: ShiftScale
methods.signature: func (p Poly) ShiftScale(a, b float64) Poly
methods.doc:
ShiftScale returns the polynomial p(a*x + b), the composition of p with the
affine map x |-> a*x + b.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String renders p in conventional descending-power notation, for example
"2x^3 - x + 5". The zero polynomial renders as "0".

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the difference p-q.

methods.name: SumOfRoots
methods.doc:
SumOfRoots returns the sum of all roots of p counted with multiplicity, which
by Vieta's formulas equals -a_{n-1}/a_n. It returns 0 for constant
polynomials.

methods.name: ToComplex
methods.doc: ToComplex converts a real polynomial to its complex counterpart.

methods.name: Trim
methods.doc:
Trim returns p with trailing (high-order) zero coefficients removed. The
result always has length equal to Degree()+1, or length zero for the zero
polynomial. The underlying array is shared with p.

types.name: Result
types.doc: Result records the outcome of a scalar root-finding iteration.

funcs.name: Bisection
funcs.signature: func Bisection(f Func, a, b, tol float64, maxIter int) (Result, error)
funcs.doc:
Bisection finds a root of f in the bracketing interval [a, b] by repeated
halving. It requires f(a) and f(b) to have opposite signs and returns
ErrNoBracket otherwise. Bisection is unconditionally convergent and halves
the bracket each step.

funcs.name: Brent
funcs.signature: func Brent(f Func, a, b, tol float64, maxIter int) (Result, error)
funcs.doc:
Brent finds a root of f in [a, b] using Brent's method, which combines
bisection, the secant method, and inverse quadratic interpolation. It is the
recommended general-purpose bracketing solver: robust like bisection yet
usually superlinear. It requires f(a) and f(b) to have opposite signs.

funcs.name: FalsePosition
funcs.signature: func FalsePosition(f Func, a, b, tol float64, maxIter int) (Result, error)
funcs.doc:
FalsePosition (regula falsi) finds a root in [a, b] by interpolating a secant
line through the bracket endpoints and keeping the subinterval that still
brackets the root. It requires an initial sign change.

funcs.name: FixedPoint
funcs.signature: func FixedPoint(g Func, x0, tol float64, maxIter int) (Result, error)
funcs.doc:
FixedPoint iterates x <- g(x) from x0 to find a fixed point of g, which is a
root of f(x) = g(x) - x. Convergence requires |g'| < 1 near the fixed point.

funcs.name: Halley
funcs.signature: func Halley(f, df, d2f Func, x0, tol float64, maxIter int) (Result, error)
funcs.doc:
Halley finds a root of f using Halley's third-order method, given the first
derivative df and second derivative d2f. It converges cubically near a simple
root, faster than Newton's method at the cost of a second derivative.

funcs.name: Illinois
funcs.signature: func Illinois(f Func, a, b, tol float64, maxIter int) (Result, error)
funcs.doc:
Illinois is the Illinois variant of the false-position method: when an
endpoint is retained across consecutive iterations its function value is
halved, which cures the slow one-sided convergence of plain regula falsi and
restores superlinear behaviour while keeping the guaranteed bracket.

funcs.name: Newton
funcs.signature: func Newton(f, df Func, x0, tol float64, maxIter int) (Result, error)
funcs.doc:
Newton finds a root of f using Newton's method, given the derivative df. It
converges quadratically near a simple root but may diverge from a poor start
or where df vanishes, in which case ErrZeroDerivative is returned.

funcs.name: Ridders
funcs.signature: func Ridders(f Func, a, b, tol float64, maxIter int) (Result, error)
funcs.doc:
Ridders finds a root of f in the bracket [a, b] using Ridders' method, which
applies an exponential correction to the false-position estimate and converges
quadratically while always maintaining a bracket. It requires a sign change.

funcs.name: Secant
funcs.signature: func Secant(f Func, x0, x1, tol float64, maxIter int) (Result, error)
funcs.doc:
Secant finds a root of f from two initial guesses x0 and x1 using the secant
iteration, which approximates the derivative by a finite difference. It
converges superlinearly (order ~1.618) but is not guaranteed to bracket.

funcs.name: Steffensen
funcs.signature: func Steffensen(f Func, x0, tol float64, maxIter int) (Result, error)
funcs.doc:
Steffensen finds a root of f using Steffensen's method, a derivative-free
iteration that achieves quadratic convergence by estimating the derivative
from f(x) and f(x+f(x)). It needs only a single starting point.

types.name: RootMultiplicity
types.doc: RootMultiplicity pairs a real root value with its multiplicity in p.

funcs.name: RealRootsWithMultiplicity
funcs.doc:
RealRootsWithMultiplicity returns the distinct real roots of p together with
their multiplicities. Distinct roots are located by [SturmRealRoots] and each
multiplicity is measured by the derivative test in [Multiplicity]. The result
is sorted by root location.

types.name: SquareFreeFactor
types.doc:
SquareFreeFactor is one factor of a squarefree factorization: the polynomial
Factor collects exactly the roots that occur with the given Multiplicity.

funcs.name: SquareFreeFactorization
funcs.doc:
SquareFreeFactorization returns the squarefree factorization of p using Yun's
algorithm: it decomposes p (up to its leading constant) as a product
prod_i a_i(x)^i where each a_i is monic and squarefree and the a_i are
pairwise coprime. Only factors of positive degree are returned, sorted by
increasing multiplicity. This groups the roots of p by their multiplicity
exactly, using only gcd computations.

funcs.name: AberthEhrlich
funcs.signature: func AberthEhrlich(c CPoly, tol float64, maxIter int) ([]complex128, int, error)
funcs.doc:
AberthEhrlich finds all complex roots of a polynomial simultaneously using the
Aberth-Ehrlich iteration, a third-order-per-step refinement of Durand-Kerner
that uses the logarithmic derivative p'/p. It is typically faster and more
robust than Durand-Kerner, converging in far fewer iterations for high-degree
polynomials.

funcs.name: AllRootsReal
funcs.signature: func AllRootsReal(p Poly, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc:
AllRootsReal reports whether every root of p is real, i.e. the count of
distinct real roots accounts for the full degree once multiplicity is
included.

funcs.name: AnnulusBounds
funcs.doc:
AnnulusBounds returns a lower and an upper bound r0 <= |z| <= r1 that enclose
every nonzero root z of p in a complex annulus. When 0 is a root the lower
bound is 0.

funcs.name: Bairstow
funcs.signature: func Bairstow(p Poly, u0, v0, tol float64, maxIter int) (u, v float64, iters int, err error)
funcs.doc:
Bairstow extracts a real quadratic factor x^2 + u*x + v from the real
polynomial p, starting from initial guesses u0, v0, using Bairstow's method.
It returns the coefficients u and v of the converged factor, from which the
corresponding pair of (possibly complex-conjugate) roots follows via
[QuadraticRoots]. Bairstow's method finds complex roots of a real polynomial
using only real arithmetic.

funcs.name: BairstowRoots
funcs.signature: func BairstowRoots(p Poly, tol float64, maxIter int) ([]complex128, error)
funcs.doc:
BairstowRoots finds all roots of the real polynomial p by repeatedly applying
[Bairstow] to peel off quadratic factors and deflating, dropping to a linear
solve when a single degree remains. It returns every root (real roots have
zero imaginary part) using only real arithmetic internally. Roots are polished
against the original polynomial.

funcs.name: BracketOutward
funcs.signature: func BracketOutward(f Func, a, b, factor float64, maxIter int) (lo, hi float64, err error)
funcs.doc:
BracketOutward searches outward from the interval [a, b] by geometrically
expanding it until f changes sign across the endpoints, returning the widened
bracket. It is a convenience for seeding bracketing solvers. It returns
ErrNoBracket if no sign change is found within maxIter expansions.

funcs.name: BudanFourierCount
funcs.signature: func BudanFourierCount(p Poly, a, b float64) int
funcs.doc:
BudanFourierCount returns V(a) - V(b), where V is the sign-variation count of
the Fourier sequence of p. By the Budan-Fourier theorem this value is an upper
bound, differing from the true count of real roots of p in (a, b] (with
multiplicity) by a nonnegative even integer, and has the same parity as that
count. The endpoints must satisfy a < b.

funcs.name: BudanFourierUpperBound
funcs.doc:
BudanFourierUpperBound returns an upper bound on the number of real roots of p
(with multiplicity) in the open positive axis (0, +inf), via the Fourier
sequence evaluated at 0 and at a value beyond all roots.

funcs.name: CPolyRoots
funcs.doc:
CPolyRoots returns all complex roots of the complex polynomial c using the
Aberth-Ehrlich method, sorted by real then imaginary part.

funcs.name: CauchyBound
funcs.doc:
CauchyBound returns Cauchy's bound: every (real or complex) root z of p
satisfies |z| <= 1 + max_{i<n} |a_i / a_n|, where a_n is the leading
coefficient. It returns 0 for constant polynomials.

funcs.name: CoeffSignVariations
funcs.doc:
CoeffSignVariations returns the number of sign changes among the coefficients
of p taken in order of ascending power (zeros skipped). By Descartes' rule of
signs this is an upper bound, of the correct parity, on the number of positive
real roots of p counted with multiplicity.

funcs.name: CompanionEigenvalues
funcs.doc:
CompanionEigenvalues returns all roots of the polynomial p as the eigenvalues
of its companion matrix, computed with the Francis double-shift QR algorithm
on the real Hessenberg form (the classic EISPACK hqr routine). This is a
robust, allocation-light way to obtain every complex root without complex
arithmetic in the iteration. Roots are returned sorted by real then imaginary
part.

funcs.name: CompanionMatrix
funcs.doc:
CompanionMatrix returns the companion matrix of the polynomial p as a dense
row-major n-by-n slice, where n is the degree of p. The characteristic
polynomial of the returned matrix equals p made monic, so its eigenvalues are
exactly the roots of p. The matrix is in upper-Hessenberg form with a unit
subdiagonal. It returns ErrDegreeTooLow for constant polynomials.

funcs.name: CountComplexRoots
funcs.doc:
CountComplexRoots returns the number of roots of c counted with multiplicity,
which by the fundamental theorem of algebra equals its degree.

funcs.name: CountRealRoots
funcs.doc:
CountRealRoots returns the total number of distinct real roots of p (each
counted once regardless of multiplicity) using Sturm's theorem over the whole
real line.

funcs.name: CountRealRootsInInterval
funcs.signature: func CountRealRootsInInterval(p Poly, a, b float64) int
funcs.doc:
CountRealRootsInInterval returns the number of distinct real roots of p in the
half-open interval (a, b].

funcs.name: DescartesNegativeBound
funcs.doc:
DescartesNegativeBound returns the Descartes upper bound on the number of
negative real roots of p, obtained by applying the rule of signs to p(-x).

funcs.name: DescartesPositiveBound
funcs.doc:
DescartesPositiveBound returns the Descartes upper bound on the number of
positive real roots of p (counted with multiplicity): the sign-variation count
of its coefficient sequence. The true number of positive roots equals this
value minus a nonnegative even integer.

funcs.name: DescartesRuleOfSigns
funcs.doc:
DescartesRuleOfSigns returns both Descartes bounds at once: an upper bound on
the number of positive real roots and an upper bound on the number of negative
real roots, each of the correct parity and counted with multiplicity.

funcs.name: DiscriminantCubic
funcs.signature: func DiscriminantCubic(a, b, c, d float64) float64
funcs.doc:
DiscriminantCubic returns the discriminant of the cubic a*x^3+b*x^2+c*x+d,
18abcd - 4b^3d + b^2c^2 - 4ac^3 - 27a^2d^2. A positive value means three
distinct real roots, zero a repeated root, and negative one real and two
complex-conjugate roots.

funcs.name: DiscriminantQuadratic
funcs.signature: func DiscriminantQuadratic(a, b, c float64) float64
funcs.doc:
DiscriminantQuadratic returns the discriminant b^2 - 4ac of a*x^2 + b*x + c.
Its sign classifies the roots: positive means two real roots, zero a repeated
real root, negative a complex-conjugate pair.

funcs.name: DistinctRealRoots
funcs.doc:
DistinctRealRoots returns the distinct real roots of p, each listed once,
refined to tolerance tol. It is [SturmRealRoots] under a descriptive name and
discards multiplicity information.

funcs.name: DurandKerner
funcs.signature: func DurandKerner(c CPoly, tol float64, maxIter int) ([]complex128, int, error)
funcs.doc:
DurandKerner finds all n complex roots of a degree-n polynomial simultaneously
using the Durand-Kerner (Weierstrass) iteration. Every estimate is refined by

	z_k <- z_k - p(z_k) / prod_{j!=k} (z_k - z_j)

The method converges from the standard spiral initialization for essentially
all polynomials. It returns the roots, the number of iterations used, and an
error when the iteration budget is exhausted before convergence.

funcs.name: DurandKernerWithInit
funcs.signature: func DurandKernerWithInit(c CPoly, init []complex128, tol float64, maxIter int) ([]complex128, int, error)
funcs.doc:
DurandKernerWithInit runs the Durand-Kerner iteration from a caller-supplied
set of initial guesses, one per root. This is useful when good approximate
roots are already known, for example when polishing the output of another
method. The number of guesses must equal the degree of c.

funcs.name: FindBrackets
funcs.signature: func FindBrackets(f Func, a, b float64, n int) [][2]float64
funcs.doc:
FindBrackets scans the interval [a, b] on a uniform grid of n subintervals and
returns every subinterval across which f changes sign. It is a simple way to
locate multiple roots of a function before refining each with a bracketing
solver.

funcs.name: FujiwaraBound
funcs.doc:
FujiwaraBound returns Fujiwara's bound on the root moduli:
|z| <= 2 * max_i ( |a_{n-i}/a_n| ^ (1/i) ), which is one of the sharpest
simple upper bounds available.

funcs.name: HalleyComplex
funcs.signature: func HalleyComplex(c CPoly, x0 complex128, tol float64, maxIter int) (complex128, int, error)
funcs.doc:
HalleyComplex finds a root of the complex polynomial c from x0 using Halley's
cubically convergent method in complex arithmetic.

funcs.name: Horner
funcs.doc:
Horner evaluates the polynomial given by ascending-order real coefficients at
x using Horner's method, without constructing a Poly.

funcs.name: HornerComplex
funcs.doc:
HornerComplex evaluates the polynomial given by ascending-order complex
coefficients at x using Horner's method.

funcs.name: IsolateRoots
funcs.doc:
IsolateRoots returns a set of disjoint intervals, each containing exactly one
distinct real root of p, covering all real roots. Bisection driven by Sturm
