BlackWinner returns the Black-method winner: the Condorcet winner when one
exists, otherwise the Borda winner.

methods.name: BordaRanking
methods.doc: BordaRanking returns candidates ordered by descending Borda score.

methods.name: BordaScore
methods.doc: BordaScore returns the Borda score of a single candidate.

methods.name: BordaScores
methods.doc:
BordaScores returns each candidate's Borda score, the total weight of voters
preferring the candidate to each other candidate summed over all opponents.
For complete strict ballots this equals the usual (n-1, n-2, …, 0) tally, and
the pairwise formulation extends it consistently to truncated ballots.

methods.name: BordaWinner
methods.doc: BordaWinner returns the highest Borda-scoring candidate.

methods.name: BucklinScores
methods.doc:
BucklinScores returns each candidate's Bucklin tally at the given depth: the
weight of ballots ranking the candidate within their top (depth+1) positions.

methods.name: BucklinWinner
methods.doc:
BucklinWinner returns the winner of the Bucklin rule: deepen the count one
preference level at a time until at least one candidate's tally reaches a
majority, then return the highest-tallying such candidate.

methods.name: CondorcetLoser
methods.doc: CondorcetLoser returns the profile's Condorcet loser, if any.

methods.name: CondorcetParadox
methods.doc:
CondorcetParadox reports whether the profile exhibits the Condorcet paradox:
no Condorcet winner exists.

methods.name: CondorcetWinner
methods.doc: CondorcetWinner returns the profile's Condorcet winner, if any.

methods.name: ContingentVoteWinner
methods.doc:
ContingentVoteWinner returns the winner of the contingent vote: if a candidate
holds a first-preference majority they win, otherwise all but the top two
first-preference candidates are eliminated and the ballots are recounted among
those two.

methods.name: CoombsWinner
methods.doc:
CoombsWinner returns the winner of the Coombs rule: repeatedly eliminate the
continuing candidate with the most active last-place votes until a candidate
holds a majority of the active first-preference vote or one remains.

methods.name: CopelandNet
methods.doc: CopelandNet returns the profile's net Copeland scores (wins minus losses).

methods.name: CopelandRanking
methods.doc: CopelandRanking returns candidates ordered by descending Copeland score.

methods.name: CopelandScores
methods.doc: CopelandScores returns the profile's Copeland scores (win=1, tie=0.5).

methods.name: CopelandWinner
methods.doc: CopelandWinner returns the highest Copeland-scoring candidate.

methods.name: Copy
methods.doc: Copy returns a deep copy of the profile.

methods.name: DowdallRanking
methods.doc: DowdallRanking returns candidates ordered by descending Dowdall score.

methods.name: DowdallScores
methods.doc:
DowdallScores returns each candidate's Dowdall (Nauru) score, awarding
1/(k+1) points for a rank at zero-based position k.

methods.name: DowdallWinner
methods.doc: DowdallWinner returns the highest Dowdall-scoring candidate.

methods.name: FirstPreferenceCounts
methods.doc:
FirstPreferenceCounts is an alias for PluralityScores, returning the top-choice
tally of every candidate.

methods.name: HasCondorcetCycle
methods.doc: HasCondorcetCycle reports whether the profile's majority relation is cyclic.

methods.name: HasCondorcetWinner
methods.doc: HasCondorcetWinner reports whether the profile has a Condorcet winner.

methods.name: HasMajorityWinner
methods.doc:
HasMajorityWinner reports whether some candidate holds a strict majority of
first preferences, returning that candidate and true when so.

methods.name: InstantRunoffRounds
methods.doc:
InstantRunoffRounds runs instant-runoff voting (IRV) and returns the ordered
rounds together with the winner. Each round eliminates the continuing
candidate with the fewest active first preferences (ties toward the lower
index) until a candidate holds a majority of the active vote or one remains.

methods.name: InstantRunoffWinner
methods.doc: InstantRunoffWinner returns the IRV winner of the profile.

methods.name: IsCondorcetWinner
methods.doc:
IsCondorcetWinner reports whether candidate c beats every other candidate
pairwise in the profile.

methods.name: KemenyRanking
methods.doc: KemenyRanking returns the profile's Kemeny–Young optimal ranking.

methods.name: KemenyWinner
methods.doc: KemenyWinner returns the top candidate of the Kemeny–Young optimal ranking.

methods.name: LastPreferenceCounts
methods.doc:
LastPreferenceCounts returns each candidate's tally of last-place (bottom
listed) rankings, the basis of the Coombs rule.

methods.name: MajorityWinner
methods.doc:
MajorityWinner returns the candidate holding a first-preference majority, or
-1 when none does.

methods.name: MiniMaxScores
methods.doc: MiniMaxScores returns the profile's minimax worst-defeat margins.

methods.name: MiniMaxWinner
methods.doc:
MiniMaxWinner returns the candidate whose worst pairwise defeat margin is
smallest, breaking ties toward the lower index.

methods.name: NansonWinner
methods.doc:
NansonWinner returns the winner of the Nanson rule: repeatedly eliminate every
continuing candidate whose Borda score is strictly below the average Borda
score until one candidate remains (ties resolved toward the lower index).

methods.name: NumBallots
methods.doc: NumBallots returns the number of distinct ballots recorded in the profile.

methods.name: Pairwise
methods.doc: Pairwise builds the PairwiseMatrix of the profile, summing ballot weights.

methods.name: PairwiseMargin
methods.doc: PairwiseMargin returns the signed majority margin of i over j in the profile.

methods.name: PluralityRanking
methods.doc:
PluralityRanking returns candidates ordered by descending first-preference
tally.

methods.name: PluralityScore
methods.doc: PluralityScore returns the first-preference tally of a single candidate.

methods.name: PluralityScores
methods.doc:
PluralityScores returns each candidate's first-preference tally: the total
weight of ballots ranking that candidate top.

methods.name: PluralityWinner
methods.doc: PluralityWinner returns the candidate with the most first preferences.

methods.name: PositionalRanking
methods.doc:
PositionalRanking returns candidates ordered by descending positional score
under the given weights.

methods.name: PositionalScores
methods.doc:
PositionalScores returns each candidate's score under an arbitrary positional
rule: a candidate ranked at position k on a ballot earns weights[k] points,
scaled by the ballot weight. Candidates not listed on a ballot earn the score
of the final position, weights[len(weights)-1]. weights must have length equal
to the candidate count.

methods.name: PositionalWinner
methods.doc:
PositionalWinner returns the winner under the positional rule with the given
position weights.

methods.name: RankedPairsRanking
methods.doc: RankedPairsRanking returns the profile's ranked-pairs ordering.

methods.name: RankedPairsWinner
methods.doc: RankedPairsWinner returns the profile's ranked-pairs (Tideman) winner.

methods.name: RemoveCandidate
methods.doc:
RemoveCandidate returns a new profile identical to p but with candidate c
removed from every ballot; the candidate count is unchanged so remaining
indices stay stable (c simply never appears).

methods.name: STV
methods.doc:
STV runs the single transferable vote with the Droop quota and fractional
(Gregory) surplus transfers, filling the requested number of seats. It returns
the elected candidates in the order they reached quota or survived. Ballot
weights start at 1 and are scaled by surplus/votes when a candidate is
elected; the lowest candidate is eliminated when no one reaches quota.

methods.name: SchulzeRanking
methods.doc: SchulzeRanking returns the profile's Schulze ranking.

methods.name: SchulzeWinner
methods.doc: SchulzeWinner returns the profile's Schulze-method winner.

methods.name: SchulzeWins
methods.doc: SchulzeWins returns the profile's per-candidate Schulze victory counts.

methods.name: SchwartzSet
methods.doc: SchwartzSet returns the profile's Schwartz set.

methods.name: SmithSet
methods.doc: SmithSet returns the profile's Smith set.

methods.name: SupplementaryVoteWinner
methods.doc:
SupplementaryVoteWinner returns the winner of the supplementary vote, the
contingent vote restricted to each ballot's first two preferences.

methods.name: TotalVoters
methods.doc: TotalVoters returns the sum of the ballot weights, i.e. the electorate size.

methods.name: TwoRoundWinner
methods.doc:
TwoRoundWinner returns the winner of the two-round (runoff) system: a
first-preference majority wins outright, otherwise the top two first-round
candidates meet in a pairwise runoff decided by the full ballots.

methods.name: UncoveredSet
methods.doc: UncoveredSet returns the profile's uncovered set.

methods.name: Validate
methods.doc:
Validate reports whether the profile is well formed: a positive candidate
count, in-range and duplicate-free ballot entries, and non-negative counts of
a length not exceeding the number of ballots.

methods.name: Weight
methods.doc:
Weight returns the number of voters casting ballot i, defaulting to 1 when
Counts does not cover that ballot.

types.name: RunoffRound
types.doc:
RunoffRound records the continuing candidates and their tallies at one round
of an elimination rule, together with the candidate eliminated that round
(-1 when the round is decisive).

types.name: ScoreProfile
types.doc:
ScoreProfile is a set of range/score ballots; Ballots[v][c] is the score voter
v assigns candidate c, bounded above by Max.

funcs.name: RangeFromRanked
funcs.doc:
RangeFromRanked derives a score profile from a ranked profile using Borda-style
position scores: a candidate at zero-based rank r on an n-candidate ballot
receives n-1-r points, and unranked candidates receive zero.

methods.name: Averages
methods.doc: Averages returns each candidate's mean score over all ballots.

methods.name: NumBallots
methods.doc: NumBallots returns the number of score ballots.

methods.name: Ranking
methods.doc: Ranking returns candidates ordered by descending total score.

methods.name: STARWinner
methods.doc:
STARWinner returns the STAR (Score Then Automatic Runoff) winner: the two
highest-scoring candidates advance to an automatic runoff won by whichever is
preferred (scored higher) on more ballots, ties broken by total score and then
the lower index.

methods.name: Totals
methods.doc: Totals returns the sum of scores each candidate receives.

methods.name: Winner
methods.doc: Winner returns the highest total-score candidate (range/score voting).

funcs.name: Adams
funcs.doc:
Adams allocates seats by the Adams method, divisor s, so every party with
positive votes receives at least one seat when seats permit.

funcs.name: ArgMaxFloat
funcs.doc:
ArgMaxFloat returns the index of the maximum value, breaking ties toward the
lowest index, and -1 for an empty slice.

funcs.name: ArgMaxInt
funcs.doc:
ArgMaxInt returns the index of the maximum value, breaking ties toward the
lowest index, and -1 for an empty slice.

funcs.name: ArgMinFloat
funcs.doc:
ArgMinFloat returns the index of the minimum value, breaking ties toward the
lowest index, and -1 for an empty slice.

funcs.name: DHondt
funcs.doc: DHondt allocates seats by the D'Hondt (Jefferson) method, divisor s+1.

funcs.name: Dean
funcs.doc:
Dean allocates seats by the Dean method, whose divisor is the harmonic mean of
s and s+1.

funcs.name: DroopLargestRemainder
funcs.doc:
DroopLargestRemainder allocates seats by the largest-remainder method with the
Hagenbach–Bischoff (exact Droop) quota total/(seats+1).

funcs.name: DroopQuota
funcs.doc:
DroopQuota returns the integer Droop quota floor(total/(seats+1))+1 used by the
single transferable vote.

funcs.name: EffectiveNumberOfParties
funcs.doc:
EffectiveNumberOfParties returns the Laakso–Taagepera effective number of
parties for the given vote (or seat) shares: the inverse of the sum of squared
shares.

funcs.name: ExactQuotas
funcs.doc: ExactQuotas returns each party's exact seat entitlement votes*seats/total.

funcs.name: GallagherIndex
funcs.doc:
GallagherIndex returns the Gallagher (least-squares) disproportionality index
between vote shares and seat shares, expressed as a percentage in [0,100].

funcs.name: HagenbachBischoffQuota
funcs.doc:
HagenbachBischoffQuota returns the exact Hagenbach–Bischoff quota
total/(seats+1).

funcs.name: Hamilton
funcs.doc:
Hamilton allocates seats by Hamilton's method, the largest-remainder method
with the Hare quota total/seats.

funcs.name: HareQuota
funcs.doc: HareQuota returns the Hare quota total/seats as a float.

funcs.name: HasAlabamaParadox
funcs.doc:
HasAlabamaParadox reports whether increasing the house size from seats to
seats+1 causes any party to lose a seat under Hamilton's method — the Alabama
paradox.

funcs.name: HighestAverages
funcs.signature: func HighestAverages(votes []int, seats int, divisor DivisorFunc) ([]int, error)
funcs.doc:
HighestAverages allocates seats among parties by the generic highest-averages
(divisor) method: it repeatedly awards the next seat to the party with the
greatest votes/divisor(seatsWon) priority, breaking ties by votes and then by
the lower party index. votes must be non-negative and seats non-negative.

funcs.name: HuntingtonHill
funcs.doc:
HuntingtonHill allocates seats by the Huntington–Hill (equal proportions)
method, whose divisor is the geometric mean sqrt(s(s+1)); every party with
positive votes receives at least one seat when seats permit.

funcs.name: InSet
funcs.doc: InSet reports whether candidate c appears in the (sorted or unsorted) set.

funcs.name: IsHouseMonotone
funcs.signature: func IsHouseMonotone(votes []int, maxSeats int, divisor DivisorFunc) bool
funcs.doc:
IsHouseMonotone reports whether the given divisor method is free of the
Alabama paradox across house sizes 1..maxSeats for these votes; every divisor
method is house-monotone, so this serves as a verification helper.

funcs.name: Jefferson
funcs.doc: Jefferson is an alias for DHondt.

funcs.name: LargestRemainder
funcs.signature: func LargestRemainder(votes []int, seats int, quota float64) ([]int, error)
funcs.doc:
LargestRemainder allocates seats by the largest-remainder (quota) method for a
given quota: each party first receives the floor of its votes/quota, then any
remaining seats are handed out by descending fractional remainder (surplus
seats are removed by ascending remainder when the floors overshoot). Ties are
broken toward the lower party index.

funcs.name: LoosemoreHanbyIndex
funcs.doc:
LoosemoreHanbyIndex returns the Loosemore–Hanby disproportionality index, half
the summed absolute differences between vote and seat shares, as a percentage.

funcs.name: LowerQuotas
funcs.doc: LowerQuotas returns the floor of each party's exact seat entitlement.

funcs.name: MajorityThreshold
funcs.doc:
MajorityThreshold returns the smallest tally that constitutes a strict
majority of total voters, floor(total/2)+1.

funcs.name: ModifiedSainteLague
funcs.doc:
ModifiedSainteLague allocates seats by the modified Sainte-Laguë method, whose
first divisor is 1.4 and whose later divisors are 3, 5, 7, ….

funcs.name: RankingFromScores
funcs.doc:
RankingFromScores returns candidate indices ordered by descending score,
breaking ties toward the lower index.

funcs.name: SainteLague
funcs.doc:
SainteLague allocates seats by the Sainte-Laguë (Webster) method, divisor
2s+1 (equivalently the odd-number divisors 1, 3, 5, …).

funcs.name: SatisfiesQuota
funcs.signature: func SatisfiesQuota(alloc, votes []int, seats int) bool
funcs.doc:
SatisfiesQuota reports whether every party's allocation lies within its lower
and upper quota, i.e. the allocation obeys the quota rule.

funcs.name: SortedInts
funcs.doc:
SortedInts returns a sorted copy of the slice, a small convenience used when
comparing candidate sets.

funcs.name: TotalSeats
funcs.doc: TotalSeats returns the sum of an allocation.

funcs.name: UpperQuotas
funcs.doc: UpperQuotas returns the ceiling of each party's exact seat entitlement.

funcs.name: ViolatesLowerQuota
funcs.signature: func ViolatesLowerQuota(alloc, votes []int, seats int) bool
funcs.doc:
ViolatesLowerQuota reports whether any party receives fewer seats than the
floor of its exact entitlement.

funcs.name: ViolatesUpperQuota
funcs.signature: func ViolatesUpperQuota(alloc, votes []int, seats int) bool
funcs.doc:
ViolatesUpperQuota reports whether any party receives more seats than the
ceiling of its exact entitlement.

funcs.name: Webster
funcs.doc: Webster is an alias for SainteLague.

funcs.name: WinnerFromScores
funcs.doc:
WinnerFromScores returns the index of the highest-scoring candidate, breaking
ties toward the lower index.

examples.name: Profile
examples.code:
p, _ := NewProfile(4,
	[]Ballot{{0, 1, 2, 3}, {1, 2, 3, 0}, {2, 3, 1, 0}, {3, 2, 1, 0}},
	[]int{42, 26, 15, 17},
)
cw, _ := p.CondorcetWinner()
fmt.Println("plurality:", p.PluralityWinner())
fmt.Println("condorcet:", cw)
fmt.Println("irv:", p.InstantRunoffWinner())

packages.name: special
packages.synopsis: Package special implements special mathematical functions in pure Go.
packages.doc:
Package special implements special mathematical functions in pure Go.

The package collects the classical higher transcendental functions that do
not appear in the standard math package, grouped by family:

  - Bessel functions of the first and second kind (J0, J1, Jn, Y0, Y1, Yn),
    the modified Bessel functions (I0, I1, In, K0, K1, Kn), the spherical
    Bessel functions (SphericalJn, SphericalYn) and the Struve functions
    (StruveH0, StruveH1).
  - Airy functions Ai, Bi and their derivatives.
  - Elliptic integrals and related quantities.
  - The error function family, including the Fresnel integrals.
  - The exponential, sine and cosine integrals.
  - The Riemann zeta family (zeta, eta, beta).
  - The Lambert W function.
  - The gamma family (log-gamma, digamma, incomplete forms).

Every routine is implemented with the Go standard library only, using series
expansions for small arguments and asymptotic expansions for large ones so
that results remain accurate across the full real line. The functions are
deterministic and aim for correctness first: they are validated in the test
suite against known closed-form and reference values.

funcs.name: Airy
funcs.signature: func Airy(x float64) (ai, aip, bi, bip float64)
funcs.doc:
Airy returns the Airy functions Ai(x), Ai'(x), Bi(x) and Bi'(x)
simultaneously. The implementation expresses the Airy functions in terms of
modified Bessel functions (for x > 0) and ordinary Bessel functions (for
x < 0), with a Maclaurin evaluation near the origin.

funcs.name: AiryAi
funcs.doc: AiryAi returns the Airy function of the first kind, Ai(x).

funcs.name: AiryAiPrime
funcs.doc:
AiryAiPrime returns the derivative of the Airy function of the first kind,
Ai'(x).

funcs.name: AiryBi
funcs.doc: AiryBi returns the Airy function of the second kind, Bi(x).

funcs.name: AiryBiPrime
funcs.doc:
AiryBiPrime returns the derivative of the Airy function of the second kind,
Bi'(x).

funcs.name: AiryZeroAi
funcs.doc:
AiryZeroAi returns an approximation to the s-th real zero (s >= 1) of the Airy
function Ai, using the standard asymptotic expansion. The returned value is
negative.

funcs.name: AiryZeroBi
funcs.doc:
AiryZeroBi returns an approximation to the s-th real zero (s >= 1) of the Airy
function Bi, using the standard asymptotic expansion. The returned value is
negative.

funcs.name: BesselI
funcs.signature: func BesselI(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BesselI returns the modified Bessel function of the first kind of integer
order n, Iₙ(x).

funcs.name: BesselI0
funcs.doc:
BesselI0 returns the modified Bessel function of the first kind of order
zero, I0(x). The result is even in x and always ≥ 1.

funcs.name: BesselI0Prime
funcs.doc: BesselI0Prime returns the derivative of I0, which equals I1(x).

funcs.name: BesselI0e
funcs.doc: BesselI0e returns the exponentially scaled I0, namely e^{-|x|} I0(x).

funcs.name: BesselI1
funcs.doc:
BesselI1 returns the modified Bessel function of the first kind of order one,
I1(x). The result is odd in x.

funcs.name: BesselI1Prime
funcs.doc: BesselI1Prime returns the derivative of I1(x).

funcs.name: BesselI1e
funcs.doc: BesselI1e returns the exponentially scaled I1, namely e^{-|x|} I1(x).

funcs.name: BesselIn
funcs.signature: func BesselIn(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BesselIn returns the modified Bessel function of the first kind of integer
order n, In(x). Negative orders satisfy In(-n, x) = In(n, x).

funcs.name: BesselInPrime
funcs.signature: func BesselInPrime(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc: BesselInPrime returns the derivative of the modified Bessel function In(x).

funcs.name: BesselIne
funcs.signature: func BesselIne(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc: BesselIne returns the exponentially scaled In, namely e^{-|x|} In(x).

funcs.name: BesselInu
funcs.doc:
BesselInu returns the modified Bessel function of the first kind of arbitrary
real order nu, I_nu(x), for x >= 0. It sums the convergent ascending power
series and is accurate for small to moderate arguments.

funcs.name: BesselJ
funcs.signature: func BesselJ(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BesselJ returns the Bessel function of the first kind of integer order n,
Jₙ(x).

funcs.name: BesselJ0
funcs.doc: BesselJ0 returns the Bessel function of the first kind of order zero, J0(x).

funcs.name: BesselJ0Prime
funcs.doc: BesselJ0Prime returns the derivative of J0, which equals -J1(x).

funcs.name: BesselJ1
funcs.doc: BesselJ1 returns the Bessel function of the first kind of order one, J1(x).

funcs.name: BesselJ1Prime
funcs.doc: BesselJ1Prime returns the derivative of J1(x).

funcs.name: BesselJZero
funcs.doc:
BesselJZero returns an approximation to the s-th positive zero (s >= 1) of the
Bessel function J_n, using McMahon's asymptotic expansion. Accuracy improves
rapidly with s.

funcs.name: BesselJn
funcs.signature: func BesselJn(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BesselJn returns the Bessel function of the first kind of integer order n,
Jn(x). It is valid for any integer n (negative orders use the reflection
Jn(-n, x) = (-1)^n Jn(n, x)).

funcs.name: BesselJnPrime
funcs.signature: func BesselJnPrime(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc: BesselJnPrime returns the derivative of the Bessel function Jn(x).

funcs.name: BesselJnu
funcs.doc:
BesselJnu returns the Bessel function of the first kind of arbitrary real
order nu, J_nu(x), for x >= 0. It sums the convergent ascending power series
and is accurate for small to moderate arguments.

funcs.name: BesselK
funcs.signature: func BesselK(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BesselK returns the modified Bessel function of the second kind of integer
order n, Kₙ(x), for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselK0
funcs.doc:
BesselK0 returns the modified Bessel function of the second kind of order
zero, K0(x). It is defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselK0Prime
funcs.doc: BesselK0Prime returns the derivative of K0, which equals -K1(x). Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselK0e
funcs.doc: BesselK0e returns the exponentially scaled K0, namely e^{x} K0(x). Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselK1
funcs.doc:
BesselK1 returns the modified Bessel function of the second kind of order
one, K1(x). It is defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselK1Prime
funcs.doc: BesselK1Prime returns the derivative of K1(x). Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselK1e
funcs.doc: BesselK1e returns the exponentially scaled K1, namely e^{x} K1(x). Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselKn
funcs.signature: func BesselKn(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BesselKn returns the modified Bessel function of the second kind of integer
order n, Kn(x). It is defined for x > 0. Negative orders satisfy
Kn(-n, x) = Kn(n, x).

funcs.name: BesselKnPrime
funcs.signature: func BesselKnPrime(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc: BesselKnPrime returns the derivative of the modified Bessel function Kn(x). Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselKne
funcs.signature: func BesselKne(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc: BesselKne returns the exponentially scaled Kn, namely e^{x} Kn(x). Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselWronskianIK
funcs.doc:
BesselWronskianIK returns the Wronskian I_n(x) K_n'(x) - I_n'(x) K_n(x),
which equals -1/x for every order n. Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselWronskianJY
funcs.doc:
BesselWronskianJY returns the Wronskian J_n(x) Y_n'(x) - J_n'(x) Y_n(x),
which equals 2/(pi x) for every order n. Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselY
funcs.signature: func BesselY(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BesselY returns the Bessel function of the second kind of integer order n,
Yₙ(x), for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselY0
funcs.doc:
BesselY0 returns the Bessel function of the second kind of order zero, Y0(x).
It is defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselY0Prime
funcs.doc: BesselY0Prime returns the derivative of Y0, which equals -Y1(x). Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselY1
funcs.doc:
BesselY1 returns the Bessel function of the second kind of order one, Y1(x).
It is defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselY1Prime
funcs.doc: BesselY1Prime returns the derivative of Y1(x). Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselYZero
funcs.doc:
BesselYZero returns an approximation to the s-th positive zero (s >= 1) of the
Bessel function Y_n, using McMahon's asymptotic expansion. Accuracy improves
rapidly with s.

funcs.name: BesselYn
funcs.signature: func BesselYn(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BesselYn returns the Bessel function of the second kind of integer order n,
Yn(x). It is defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: BesselYnPrime
funcs.signature: func BesselYnPrime(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc: BesselYnPrime returns the derivative of the Bessel function Yn(x). Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: Beta
funcs.doc: Beta returns the beta function B(a, b) = Γ(a)Γ(b)/Γ(a+b) for a, b > 0.

funcs.name: BetaInc
funcs.signature: func BetaInc(a, b, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
BetaInc returns the regularized incomplete beta function,

	I_x(a, b) = (1/B(a,b)) ∫₀ˣ t^{a-1}(1-t)^{b-1} dt,

for 0 ≤ x ≤ 1 and a, b > 0.

funcs.name: Chi
funcs.doc:
Chi returns the hyperbolic cosine integral,

	Chi(x) = γ + ln x + ∫₀ˣ (cosh(t)-1)/t dt,   x > 0.

funcs.name: Ci
funcs.doc:
Ci returns the cosine integral,

	Ci(x) = γ + ln x + ∫₀ˣ (cos(t)-1)/t dt,   x > 0.

funcs.name: Dawson
funcs.doc:
Dawson returns Dawson's integral,

	D(x) = e^{-x²} ∫₀ˣ e^{t²} dt.

The implementation uses a rational/series scheme accurate to near machine
precision across the whole real line.

funcs.name: Digamma
funcs.doc:
Digamma returns the digamma function ψ(x) = Γ'(x)/Γ(x), the logarithmic
derivative of the gamma function, for real x that is not a non-positive
integer.

funcs.name: DirichletBeta
funcs.doc:
DirichletBeta returns the Dirichlet beta function,

	β(s) = Σ_{n≥0} (-1)^n (2n+1)^{-s}.

It satisfies β(1) = π/4 and β(2) = Catalan's constant.

funcs.name: DirichletEta
funcs.doc:
DirichletEta returns the Dirichlet eta function (alternating zeta),

	η(s) = Σ_{n≥1} (-1)^{n-1} n^{-s} = (1 - 2^{1-s}) ζ(s).

funcs.name: E1
funcs.doc:
E1 returns the exponential integral E₁,

	E₁(x) = ∫₁^∞ e^{-x t}/t dt = ∫_x^∞ e^{-t}/t dt,   x > 0.

For x ≤ 0 the value is defined via analytic continuation as -Ei(-x).

funcs.name: Ei
funcs.doc:
Ei returns the exponential integral,

	Ei(x) = -∫_{-x}^∞ e^{-t}/t dt   (principal value for x > 0).

It is defined for all real x ≠ 0; Ei(0) is -Inf.

funcs.name: EllipticE
funcs.doc:
EllipticE returns the complete elliptic integral of the second kind,

	E(m) = ∫₀^{π/2} √(1 - m sin²θ) dθ,

with parameter m = k². It is defined for m ≤ 1.

funcs.name: EllipticEInc
funcs.doc:
EllipticEInc returns the incomplete elliptic integral of the second kind,

	E(φ, m) = ∫₀^φ √(1 - m sin²θ) dθ,

with amplitude φ (radians) and parameter m = k².

funcs.name: EllipticF
funcs.doc:
EllipticF returns the incomplete elliptic integral of the first kind,

	F(φ, m) = ∫₀^φ dθ / √(1 - m sin²θ),

with amplitude φ (radians) and parameter m = k².

funcs.name: EllipticK
funcs.doc:
EllipticK returns the complete elliptic integral of the first kind,

	K(m) = ∫₀^{π/2} dθ / √(1 - m sin²θ),

with parameter m = k². It is defined for m < 1.

funcs.name: EllipticPi
funcs.doc:
EllipticPi returns the complete elliptic integral of the third kind,

	Π(n, m) = ∫₀^{π/2} dθ / ((1 - n sin²θ)√(1 - m sin²θ)),

with characteristic n and parameter m = k².

funcs.name: EllipticPiInc
funcs.signature: func EllipticPiInc(n, phi, m float64) float64
funcs.doc:
EllipticPiInc returns the incomplete elliptic integral of the third kind,

	Π(n; φ, m) = ∫₀^φ dθ / ((1 - n sin²θ)√(1 - m sin²θ)).

funcs.name: En
funcs.signature: func En(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
En returns the generalized exponential integral of order n,

	Eₙ(x) = ∫₁^∞ e^{-x t}/tⁿ dt,   x ≥ 0, n ≥ 0.

E₀(x) = e^{-x}/x and E₁ coincides with the two-argument E1.

funcs.name: Erf
funcs.doc:
Erf returns the error function of x,

	erf(x) = (2/√π) ∫₀ˣ e^{-t²} dt.

It delegates to the standard library implementation.

funcs.name: Erfc
funcs.doc:
Erfc returns the complementary error function of x, erfc(x) = 1 - erf(x).
It is accurate even when erf(x) is very close to 1.

funcs.name: Erfcx
funcs.doc:
Erfcx returns the scaled complementary error function,

	erfcx(x) = e^{x²} · erfc(x).

The scaling removes the exponential decay of erfc for large positive x,
avoiding underflow.

funcs.name: Erfi
funcs.doc:
Erfi returns the imaginary error function,

	erfi(x) = -i·erf(i·x) = (2/√π) ∫₀ˣ e^{t²} dt.

It grows super-exponentially and is related to Dawson's function by
erfi(x) = (2/√π)·e^{x²}·D(x).

funcs.name: Fresnel
funcs.doc:
Fresnel returns both Fresnel integrals S(x) and C(x) simultaneously,

	S(x) = ∫₀ˣ sin(π t²/2) dt,   C(x) = ∫₀ˣ cos(π t²/2) dt.

A power series is used for small |x| and an asymptotic auxiliary-function
expansion for large |x|.

funcs.name: FresnelC
funcs.doc:
FresnelC returns the Fresnel cosine integral,

	C(x) = ∫₀ˣ cos(π t²/2) dt.

funcs.name: FresnelS
funcs.doc:
FresnelS returns the Fresnel sine integral,

	S(x) = ∫₀ˣ sin(π t²/2) dt.

funcs.name: Gamma
funcs.doc:
Gamma returns the gamma function Γ(x); it delegates to the standard library
and is provided for API completeness alongside the incomplete forms.

funcs.name: GammaP
funcs.doc:
GammaP returns the regularized lower incomplete gamma function,

	P(a, x) = (1/Γ(a)) ∫₀ˣ t^{a-1} e^{-t} dt,   a > 0, x ≥ 0.

funcs.name: GammaQ
funcs.doc:
GammaQ returns the regularized upper incomplete gamma function,

	Q(a, x) = 1 - P(a, x) = (1/Γ(a)) ∫ₓ^∞ t^{a-1} e^{-t} dt.

funcs.name: HurwitzZeta
funcs.doc:
HurwitzZeta returns the Hurwitz zeta function,

	ζ(s, a) = Σ_{n≥0} (n + a)^{-s},   s > 1, a > 0,

extended to s < 1 (s ≠ 1) by Euler–Maclaurin summation.

funcs.name: HypSinCosIntegral
funcs.doc:
HypSinCosIntegral returns the hyperbolic sine integral Shi(x) and hyperbolic
cosine integral Chi(x) simultaneously. Shi is odd; Chi is evaluated at |x|.

funcs.name: IncompleteBeta
funcs.signature: func IncompleteBeta(a, b, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
IncompleteBeta returns the (non-regularized) incomplete beta function
B(x; a, b) = ∫₀ˣ t^{a-1}(1-t)^{b-1} dt = I_x(a, b)·B(a, b).

funcs.name: InverseErf
funcs.doc:
InverseErf returns the inverse error function, the value y such that
erf(y) = x for x in the open interval (-1, 1). It returns ±Inf at ±1 and
NaN outside [-1, 1].

funcs.name: InverseErfc
funcs.doc:
InverseErfc returns the inverse complementary error function, the value y
such that erfc(y) = x for x in the open interval (0, 2). It returns
±Inf at the endpoints and NaN outside [0, 2].

funcs.name: KelvinBei
funcs.doc:
KelvinBei returns the Kelvin function bei(x) = Im[J0(x e^{3iπ/4})], summed
from its convergent power series.

funcs.name: KelvinBer
funcs.doc:
KelvinBer returns the Kelvin function ber(x) = Re[J0(x e^{3iπ/4})], summed
from its convergent power series.

funcs.name: KelvinKei
funcs.doc:
KelvinKei returns the Kelvin function kei(x) for x > 0, summed from its
series with the leading logarithmic terms. It uses

	kei(x) = -(ln(x/2)+γ) bei(x) - (π/4) ber(x)
	         + Σ_{k≥0} (-1)^k φ(2k+1) / ((2k+1)!)^2 (x/2)^{4k+2}

where φ(n) is the n-th harmonic number.

funcs.name: KelvinKer
funcs.doc:
KelvinKer returns the Kelvin function ker(x) for x > 0, summed from its
series with the leading logarithmic terms. It uses

	ker(x) = -(ln(x/2)+γ) ber(x) + (π/4) bei(x)
	         + Σ_{k≥0} (-1)^k φ(2k) / ((2k)!)^2 (x/2)^{4k}

where φ(n) is the n-th harmonic number and φ(0) = 0.

funcs.name: LambertW
funcs.doc:
LambertW returns the principal branch W₀(x) of the Lambert W function, the
solution w of w·e^w = x for x ≥ -1/e. It returns NaN for x < -1/e.

funcs.name: LambertWm1
funcs.doc:
LambertWm1 returns the secondary real branch W₋₁(x) of the Lambert W
function for -1/e ≤ x < 0, the solution w ≤ -1 of w·e^w = x.

funcs.name: Li
funcs.doc:
Li returns the logarithmic integral,

	li(x) = ∫₀ˣ dt/ln t   (principal value for x > 1),

which equals Ei(ln x). It is defined for x > 0, x ≠ 1.

funcs.name: Li2
funcs.doc:
Li2 returns the dilogarithm,

	Li₂(x) = Σ_{k≥1} x^k / k² = -∫₀ˣ ln(1-t)/t dt,

for real x ≤ 1. Analytic continuation handles x < -1 and 0 < x ≤ 1.

funcs.name: Li3
funcs.doc:
Li3 returns the trilogarithm,

	Li₃(x) = Σ_{k≥1} x^k / k³,

for real x with |x| ≤ 1 (and x < 1). Values outside are returned via the
series where it converges.

funcs.name: LogGamma
funcs.doc:
LogGamma returns the natural logarithm of the absolute value of the gamma
function, ln|Γ(x)|.

funcs.name: LowerIncompleteGamma
funcs.doc:
LowerIncompleteGamma returns the (non-regularized) lower incomplete gamma
function γ(a, x) = ∫₀ˣ t^{a-1} e^{-t} dt = P(a, x)·Γ(a).

funcs.name: ModifiedSphericalI0
funcs.doc:
ModifiedSphericalI0 returns the modified spherical Bessel function of the
first kind of order zero, i0(x) = sinh(x)/x.

funcs.name: ModifiedSphericalI1
funcs.doc:
ModifiedSphericalI1 returns the modified spherical Bessel function of the
first kind of order one, i1(x) = (x cosh(x) - sinh(x)) / x^2.

funcs.name: ModifiedSphericalK0
funcs.doc:
ModifiedSphericalK0 returns the modified spherical Bessel function of the
second kind of order zero, k0(x) = (pi/2) e^{-x}/x. Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: ModifiedSphericalK1
funcs.doc:
ModifiedSphericalK1 returns the modified spherical Bessel function of the
second kind of order one, k1(x) = (pi/2) e^{-x} (1 + 1/x) / x. Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: Polygamma
funcs.signature: func Polygamma(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Polygamma returns the polygamma function ψ⁽ⁿ⁾(x), the n-th derivative of the
digamma function, for integer order n ≥ 0 and real x > 0.

	ψ⁽ⁿ⁾(x) = (-1)^{n+1} n! Σ_{k≥0} (x+k)^{-(n+1)}   for n ≥ 1.

funcs.name: Polylog
funcs.signature: func Polylog(s int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Polylog returns the polylogarithm Liₛ(x) = Σ_{k≥1} x^k / k^s for integer
order s and real |x| < 1 (and x = 1 when s > 1, giving ζ(s)). It sums the
defining series directly.

funcs.name: ReciprocalGamma
funcs.doc:
ReciprocalGamma returns 1/Γ(x), which is entire and vanishes at the
non-positive integers.

funcs.name: RiccatiBesselChi
funcs.signature: func RiccatiBesselChi(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc: RiccatiBesselChi returns the Riccati-Bessel function chi_n(x) = -x yn(x).

funcs.name: RiccatiBesselPsi
funcs.signature: func RiccatiBesselPsi(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc: RiccatiBesselPsi returns the Riccati-Bessel function psi_n(x) = x jn(x).

funcs.name: Shi
funcs.doc:
Shi returns the hyperbolic sine integral,

	Shi(x) = ∫₀ˣ sinh(t)/t dt.

funcs.name: Si
funcs.doc:
Si returns the sine integral,

	Si(x) = ∫₀ˣ sin(t)/t dt.

funcs.name: SinCosIntegral
funcs.doc:
SinCosIntegral returns the sine integral Si(x) and cosine integral Ci(x)
simultaneously. For x < 0, Si is odd (Si(-x) = -Si(x)) and Ci(x) is
evaluated as Ci(|x|).

funcs.name: Sinc
funcs.doc:
Sinc returns the unnormalized cardinal sine, sinc(x) = sin(x)/x, with the
removable singularity Sinc(0) = 1 handled exactly.

funcs.name: SincNorm
funcs.doc:
SincNorm returns the normalized cardinal sine, sinc(x) = sin(πx)/(πx), with
SincNorm(0) = 1.

funcs.name: SphericalJ0
funcs.doc:
SphericalJ0 returns the spherical Bessel function of the first kind of order
zero, j0(x) = sin(x)/x, with the removable singularity handled at x = 0.

funcs.name: SphericalJ1
funcs.doc:
SphericalJ1 returns the spherical Bessel function of the first kind of order
one, j1(x) = sin(x)/x^2 - cos(x)/x.

funcs.name: SphericalJn
funcs.signature: func SphericalJn(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
SphericalJn returns the spherical Bessel function of the first kind of order
n, jn(x) = sqrt(pi/(2x)) J_{n+1/2}(x). It uses stable upward recurrence for
x ≥ n and downward (Miller) recurrence otherwise.

funcs.name: SphericalJnPrime
funcs.signature: func SphericalJnPrime(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
SphericalJnPrime returns the derivative of the spherical Bessel function
jn(x), using jn'(x) = j_{n-1}(x) - (n+1)/x * jn(x).

funcs.name: SphericalY0
funcs.doc:
SphericalY0 returns the spherical Bessel function of the second kind of order
zero, y0(x) = -cos(x)/x. It is defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: SphericalY1
funcs.doc:
SphericalY1 returns the spherical Bessel function of the second kind of order
one, y1(x) = -cos(x)/x^2 - sin(x)/x. It is defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: SphericalYn
funcs.signature: func SphericalYn(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
SphericalYn returns the spherical Bessel function of the second kind of order
n, yn(x). It is defined for x > 0 and uses stable upward recurrence.

funcs.name: SphericalYnPrime
funcs.signature: func SphericalYnPrime(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
SphericalYnPrime returns the derivative of the spherical Bessel function
yn(x), using yn'(x) = y_{n-1}(x) - (n+1)/x * yn(x). Defined for x > 0.

funcs.name: Struve
funcs.signature: func Struve(n int, x float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Struve returns the Struve function Hₙ(x) of integer order n by its power
series,

	Hₙ(x) = Σ_{m≥0} (-1)^m /(Γ(m+3/2) Γ(m+n+3/2)) (x/2)^{2m+n+1}.

funcs.name: StruveH0
funcs.doc:
StruveH0 returns the Struve function of order zero, H0(x). It is odd in x and
uses a power series for small |x| and the relation to Y0 for large |x|.

funcs.name: StruveH1
funcs.doc:
StruveH1 returns the Struve function of order one, H1(x). It is even in x and
uses a power series for small |x| and the relation to Y1 for large |x|.

funcs.name: StruveL0
funcs.doc:
StruveL0 returns the modified Struve function of order zero, L0(x). It is odd
in x and uses the all-positive-term power series for moderate |x|.

funcs.name: StruveL1
funcs.doc:
StruveL1 returns the modified Struve function of order one, L1(x). It is even
in x and uses the all-positive-term power series for moderate |x|.

funcs.name: Trigamma
funcs.doc:
Trigamma returns the trigamma function ψ⁽¹⁾(x), the first derivative of the
digamma function, for real x that is not a non-positive integer.

funcs.name: UpperIncompleteGamma
funcs.doc:
UpperIncompleteGamma returns the (non-regularized) upper incomplete gamma
function Γ(a, x) = ∫ₓ^∞ t^{a-1} e^{-t} dt = Q(a, x)·Γ(a).

funcs.name: Zeta
funcs.doc:
Zeta returns the Riemann zeta function ζ(s) for real s ≠ 1.

	ζ(s) = Σ_{n≥1} n^{-s}   (for s > 1, extended by analytic continuation).

The reflection formula is used for s < 0.5.

packages.name: splines
packages.synopsis: Package splines implements splines and parametric curves using nothing but the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package splines implements splines and parametric curves using nothing but
the Go standard library.

The package provides a broad, self-contained toolkit for computer-aided
geometric design and numerical interpolation:

  - Cubic splines with natural, clamped, not-a-knot and periodic end
    conditions, together with value, derivative and integral evaluation.
  - Piecewise cubic Hermite interpolation, Catmull-Rom / cardinal splines,
    shape-preserving monotone interpolation (Fritsch-Carlson) and Akima
    splines.
  - Bezier curves evaluated with de Casteljau's algorithm, plus degree
    elevation, subdivision, derivatives and the Bernstein basis.
  - B-spline curves evaluated with de Boor's algorithm, knot insertion
    (Boehm), the Cox-de Boor basis and derivatives.
  - NURBS curves (rational B-splines) with control point weights.
  - Arc-length computation and arc-length (unit-speed) reparameterisation.
  - Tensor-product Bezier and B-spline surface evaluation.

Points and vectors are represented by the [Vec] type, an alias-free slice of
float64 coordinates, so every routine works uniformly in any dimension.
Scalar (one dimensional) interpolation is available through dedicated
float64 helpers that avoid the small allocation overhead of [Vec].

All algorithms are implemented from first principles with no third-party
dependencies and no cgo.

vars.doc: Sentinel errors shared across the package.

types.name: ArcLengthParam
types.doc:
ArcLengthParam provides an arc-length (unit-speed) reparameterisation of a
curve. It stores a monotone table mapping parameter t to cumulative arc
length s and inverts it to answer both directions.

funcs.name: NewArcLengthParam
funcs.signature: func NewArcLengthParam(c Curve, n int) (*ArcLengthParam, error)
funcs.doc:
NewArcLengthParam builds an arc-length parameterisation of c using n sample
segments (n >= 1) across the curve's domain. More samples give a more
accurate length table and faster inversion.

methods.name: PointAtArcLength
methods.doc:
PointAtArcLength returns the point on the curve at cumulative arc length s,
i.e. the unit-speed sample. It is shorthand for Eval(TForS(s)).

methods.name: PointAtFraction
methods.doc:
PointAtFraction returns the point on the curve at fraction f in [0,1] of the
total arc length; f=0 is the start and f=1 is the end.

methods.name: SForT
methods.doc:
SForT returns the arc length from the start of the curve to parameter t using
linear interpolation within the length table.

methods.name: TForS
methods.doc:
TForS returns the curve parameter t at which the cumulative arc length equals
s. The table is inverted by binary search and refined by a Newton step using
the local speed, giving near machine-accurate unit-speed sampling.

methods.name: TotalLength
methods.doc: TotalLength returns the total arc length recorded by the parameterisation.

types.name: BSplineCurve
types.doc:
BSplineCurve is a (non-rational) B-spline curve defined by control points, a
knot vector and a degree.

funcs.name: NewBSplineCurve
funcs.signature: func NewBSplineCurve(ctrl []Vec, knots []float64, degree int) (*BSplineCurve, error)
funcs.doc:
NewBSplineCurve builds a B-spline curve from control points, a non-decreasing
knot vector and degree p. It validates that len(knots) == len(ctrl)+p+1, that
there are at least p+1 control points and that the knot vector is
non-decreasing.

methods.name: ControlPoints
methods.doc: ControlPoints returns an independent copy of the control points.

methods.name: Degree
methods.doc: Degree returns the degree of the curve.

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc: Dim returns the spatial dimension of the control points.

methods.name: Domain
methods.doc: Domain returns the valid parameter interval [U[p], U[n+1]] of the curve.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc: Eval returns the point on the curve at parameter u using the basis functions.

methods.name: EvalDerivative
methods.doc:
EvalDerivative returns the first derivative vector of the curve at parameter
u.

methods.name: EvalDerivatives
methods.doc:
EvalDerivatives returns the point on the curve together with its derivatives
up to order d as a slice of length d+1 (index 0 is the point). It follows
Algorithm A3.2.

methods.name: InsertKnot
methods.signature: func (b *BSplineCurve) InsertKnot(u float64, r int) (*BSplineCurve, error)
methods.doc:
InsertKnot returns a new curve with the knot u inserted r times using Boehm's
algorithm (Algorithm A5.1). The geometry is unchanged; only the control-point
representation is refined. It requires r >= 1 and r + (existing multiplicity)
<= degree.

methods.name: Knots
methods.doc: Knots returns an independent copy of the knot vector.

types.name: BSplineSurface
types.doc:
BSplineSurface is a tensor-product B-spline surface with independent degrees
and knot vectors in the u and v directions.

funcs.name: NewBSplineSurface
funcs.signature: func NewBSplineSurface(ctrl [][]Vec, knotsU, knotsV []float64, degU, degV int) (*BSplineSurface, error)
funcs.doc:
NewBSplineSurface builds a tensor-product B-spline surface. The control net
must be rectangular with len(ctrl) control rows in u and len(ctrl[0]) columns
in v; the knot vectors must satisfy len(knotsU) == rows+degU+1 and
len(knotsV) == cols+degV+1.

methods.name: DegreeU
methods.doc: DegreeU returns the surface degree in the u direction.

methods.name: DegreeV
methods.doc: DegreeV returns the surface degree in the v direction.

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc: Dim returns the spatial dimension of the control net.

methods.name: Domain
methods.signature: func (s *BSplineSurface) Domain() (uLo, uHi, vLo, vHi float64)
methods.doc: Domain returns the valid parameter rectangle [uLo,uHi] x [vLo,vHi].

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc:
Eval returns the surface point at (u,v) using the tensor product of the
univariate B-spline basis functions in each direction.

types.name: BezierCurve
types.doc:
BezierCurve is a polynomial Bezier curve defined by an ordered list of control
points of a common dimension. Its degree is one less than the number of
control points.

funcs.name: NewBezierCurve
funcs.doc:
NewBezierCurve returns a Bezier curve with the given control points. It
requires at least one control point and a uniform dimension.

methods.name: ControlPoints
methods.doc: ControlPoints returns an independent copy of the control points.

methods.name: Degree
methods.doc:
Degree returns the polynomial degree of the curve (number of control points
minus one).

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc: Dim returns the spatial dimension of the curve's control points.

methods.name: Domain
methods.doc: Domain returns the parameter domain of a Bezier curve, always [0,1].

methods.name: Elevate
methods.doc:
Elevate returns an equivalent Bezier curve of degree one higher. Degree
elevation adds a control point without changing the curve's shape and is used
to make two curves compatible for blending.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc: Eval returns the point on the curve at parameter t in [0,1].

methods.name: EvalDerivative
methods.doc:
EvalDerivative returns the first derivative vector of the curve at parameter
t.

methods.name: EvalDerivativeK
methods.signature: func (b *BezierCurve) EvalDerivativeK(k int, t float64) Vec
methods.doc:
EvalDerivativeK returns the k-th derivative vector of the curve at parameter
t (k >= 0). A zero-vector is returned once k exceeds the degree.

methods.name: Hodograph
methods.doc:
Hodograph returns the derivative curve of b as a Bezier curve of degree one
lower, whose control points are Q(i) = n*(P(i+1)-P(i)). Evaluating it gives
the derivative of b with respect to its parameter.

methods.name: Length
methods.doc:
Length returns an arc-length estimate of the curve obtained by adaptive
Gauss-Legendre quadrature of the speed |C'(t)| over [0,1].

methods.name: Polyline
methods.doc:
Polyline samples the curve at n+1 evenly spaced parameter values in [0,1] and
returns the resulting points, a convenient coarse polygonal approximation.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.doc:
Reverse returns the curve traversed in the opposite direction (control points
reversed), so Reverse().Eval(t) == Eval(1-t).

methods.name: Split
methods.doc:
Split subdivides the curve at parameter t and returns the two Bezier curves
that together reproduce it: left covers the parameter range [0,t] and right
covers [t,1], each reparameterised to [0,1].

methods.name: Tangent
methods.doc:
Tangent returns the unit tangent vector of the curve at parameter t. If the
derivative vanishes it returns the raw (zero) derivative.

types.name: BezierSurface
types.doc:
BezierSurface is a tensor-product Bezier surface defined by a rectangular
control net. Its degrees are one less than the numbers of rows and columns.

funcs.name: NewBezierSurface
funcs.doc:
NewBezierSurface builds a tensor-product Bezier surface from a rectangular
control net ctrl[i][j], where i runs over the u direction and j over the v
direction.

methods.name: DegreeU
methods.doc: DegreeU returns the surface degree in the u direction.

methods.name: DegreeV
methods.doc: DegreeV returns the surface degree in the v direction.

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc: Dim returns the spatial dimension of the control net.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc:
Eval returns the surface point at parameters (u,v) in [0,1]x[0,1] using the
Bernstein basis in both directions.

methods.name: EvalPartialU
methods.doc:
EvalPartialU returns the partial derivative of the surface with respect to u
at (u,v).

methods.name: EvalPartialV
methods.doc:
EvalPartialV returns the partial derivative of the surface with respect to v
at (u,v).

methods.name: Normal
methods.doc:
Normal returns the (unnormalised) surface normal at (u,v) as the cross product
of the u- and v-partial derivatives. It is defined only for three-dimensional
surfaces.

types.name: BoundaryType
types.signature: type BoundaryType int
types.doc:
BoundaryType selects the end conditions used when constructing a cubic
spline.

types.name: CatmullRomCurve
types.doc:
CatmullRomCurve is a parametric Catmull-Rom spline through a sequence of
points. Its parameter domain is [0, n-1] for n points, with integer values
interpolating the corresponding control points. The alpha exponent selects
uniform, centripetal or chordal knot spacing.

funcs.name: NewCatmullRomCurve
funcs.doc:
NewCatmullRomCurve builds a Catmull-Rom curve through pts using the given
alpha (0 uniform, 0.5 centripetal, 1 chordal). It needs at least two points;
the ends are handled by reflecting the boundary points to form phantom
neighbours.

methods.name: Domain
methods.doc: Domain returns the parameter interval [0, n-1] of the curve.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc: Eval returns the point on the curve at parameter u in [0, n-1].

methods.name: EvalDerivative
methods.doc:
EvalDerivative returns the first derivative of the curve at parameter u,
approximated by a symmetric finite difference (exact to rounding for the
uniform case). It lets CatmullRomCurve satisfy the [Curve] interface.

methods.name: Point
methods.signature: func (c *CatmullRomCurve) Point(k int, t float64) Vec
methods.doc:
Point returns the point on segment k (between control points k and k+1) at
local parameter t in [0,1].

types.name: CubicSpline
types.doc:
CubicSpline is a piecewise cubic interpolant of one-dimensional data. It is
produced by one of the constructors ([NaturalCubicSpline], [ClampedCubicSpline],
[NotAKnotCubicSpline], [PeriodicCubicSpline] or [NewCubicSpline]) and is safe
for concurrent evaluation once built.

funcs.name: ClampedCubicSpline
funcs.signature: func ClampedCubicSpline(x, y []float64, dyLeft, dyRight float64) (*CubicSpline, error)
funcs.doc:
ClampedCubicSpline builds a cubic spline whose first derivative equals dyLeft
at the first knot and dyRight at the last knot.

funcs.name: NaturalCubicSpline
funcs.doc:
NaturalCubicSpline builds a natural cubic spline (zero second derivative at
both ends) through (x[i], y[i]).

funcs.name: NewCubicSpline
funcs.signature: func NewCubicSpline(x, y []float64, bt BoundaryType, dyLeft, dyRight float64) (*CubicSpline, error)
funcs.doc:
NewCubicSpline builds a cubic spline through the samples (x[i], y[i]) with the
requested boundary type. For [Clamped] the arguments dyLeft and dyRight give
the imposed first derivatives at the two ends; they are ignored for the other
boundary types. The abscissae must be strictly increasing.

funcs.name: NotAKnotCubicSpline
funcs.doc:
NotAKnotCubicSpline builds a cubic spline with not-a-knot end conditions,
the default used by many numerical packages. It requires at least four
points.

funcs.name: PeriodicCubicSpline
funcs.doc:
PeriodicCubicSpline builds a periodic cubic spline; the first and last
samples must have equal ordinates (y[0] == y[len-1]).

methods.name: Boundary
methods.doc: Boundary returns the boundary type used to build the spline.

methods.name: Domain
methods.doc: Domain returns the first and last knot abscissae.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc:
Eval returns the spline value at q. Queries outside the knot range are
evaluated by extrapolating the nearest end segment.

methods.name: EvalDerivative
methods.doc: EvalDerivative returns the first derivative of the spline at q.

methods.name: EvalSecondDerivative
methods.doc: EvalSecondDerivative returns the second derivative of the spline at q.

methods.name: EvalThirdDerivative
methods.doc:
EvalThirdDerivative returns the (piecewise constant) third derivative of the
spline at q.

methods.name: Integrate
methods.doc:
Integrate returns the definite integral of the spline from a to b. The order
a > b is allowed and yields the negated result.

methods.name: Knots
methods.doc: Knots returns a copy of the spline's knot abscissae.

methods.name: Len
methods.doc: Len returns the number of polynomial segments in the spline.

methods.name: SegmentCoeffs
methods.doc:
SegmentCoeffs returns the local monomial coefficients [c0,c1,c2,c3] of
segment i, so that on that segment the spline equals
c0 + c1*s + c2*s^2 + c3*s^3 with s = q - Knots()[i].

types.name: Curve
types.doc:
Curve is the common interface implemented by the parametric curve types in
this package. Domain reports the valid parameter interval, Eval returns the
point at a parameter, and EvalDerivative returns the first derivative vector.

types.name: NURBSCurve
types.doc:
NURBSCurve is a Non-Uniform Rational B-Spline curve: a B-spline whose control
points carry positive weights, letting it represent conic sections such as
circles exactly.

funcs.name: NURBSCircle
funcs.signature: func NURBSCircle(center Vec, radius float64) (*NURBSCurve, error)
funcs.doc:
NURBSCircle returns a full circle of the given radius centred at center,
represented exactly as a quadratic NURBS curve with nine control points in the
plane spanned by the first two coordinates. center may have any dimension
>= 2; coordinates beyond the first two are copied from center unchanged.

funcs.name: NewNURBSCurve
funcs.signature: func NewNURBSCurve(ctrl []Vec, weights, knots []float64, degree int) (*NURBSCurve, error)
funcs.doc:
NewNURBSCurve builds a NURBS curve from control points, matching positive
weights, a non-decreasing knot vector and a degree. The validation mirrors
[NewBSplineCurve] and additionally requires each weight to be strictly
positive.

methods.name: ControlPoints
methods.doc: ControlPoints returns an independent copy of the control points.

methods.name: Degree
methods.doc: Degree returns the degree of the curve.

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc: Dim returns the spatial dimension of the control points.

methods.name: Domain
methods.doc: Domain returns the valid parameter interval of the curve.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc:
Eval returns the point on the NURBS curve at parameter u by evaluating the
weighted basis and dividing by the weight sum.

methods.name: EvalDerivative
methods.doc:
EvalDerivative returns the first derivative vector of the NURBS curve at
parameter u.

methods.name: EvalDerivatives
methods.doc:
EvalDerivatives returns the point on the curve and its derivatives up to order
d as a slice of length d+1, applying the rational-curve quotient rule of
Algorithm A4.2 to the homogeneous B-spline derivatives.

methods.name: Knots
methods.doc: Knots returns an independent copy of the knot vector.

methods.name: Weights
methods.doc: Weights returns an independent copy of the control-point weights.

types.name: NURBSSurface
types.doc:
NURBSSurface is a tensor-product rational B-spline surface, adding a positive
weight to every control point of a [BSplineSurface].

funcs.name: NewNURBSSurface
funcs.signature: func NewNURBSSurface(ctrl [][]Vec, weights [][]float64, knotsU, knotsV []float64, degU, degV int) (*NURBSSurface, error)
funcs.doc:
NewNURBSSurface builds a tensor-product NURBS surface. The weight net must
match the control net in shape and every weight must be strictly positive.

methods.name: DegreeU
methods.doc: DegreeU returns the surface degree in the u direction.

methods.name: DegreeV
methods.doc: DegreeV returns the surface degree in the v direction.

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc: Dim returns the spatial dimension of the control net.

methods.name: Domain
methods.signature: func (s *NURBSSurface) Domain() (uLo, uHi, vLo, vHi float64)
methods.doc: Domain returns the valid parameter rectangle of the surface.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc:
Eval returns the surface point at (u,v) using the rational tensor-product
basis (weighted sum divided by the weight sum).

types.name: PiecewiseCubic
types.doc:
PiecewiseCubic is a one-dimensional interpolant made of independent cubic
pieces, each expressed in a local monomial basis. It backs the Hermite,
Catmull-Rom, cardinal, monotone (PCHIP) and Akima constructors.

funcs.name: AkimaSpline
funcs.doc:
AkimaSpline builds an Akima cubic interpolant through (x[i], y[i]). Akima's
method uses locally weighted slopes, making it robust against wiggles caused
by outliers while remaining smooth (C1).

funcs.name: CardinalSpline
funcs.signature: func CardinalSpline(x, y []float64, tension float64) (*PiecewiseCubic, error)
funcs.doc:
CardinalSpline builds a cardinal spline through (x[i], y[i]) with the given
tension in [0,1]; tension 0 reproduces the Catmull-Rom spline while tension 1
produces zero tangents (a piecewise-linear-like flat interpolant). The
interior tangents use the non-uniform central difference and the ends use
one-sided differences.

funcs.name: CatmullRomSpline
funcs.doc:
CatmullRomSpline builds the Catmull-Rom interpolant through (x[i], y[i]); it
is the cardinal spline with zero tension.

funcs.name: HermiteSpline
funcs.doc:
HermiteSpline builds a piecewise cubic Hermite interpolant from nodal values
y and nodal first derivatives m at the abscissae x. All three slices must
share a length of at least two and x must be strictly increasing.

funcs.name: MonotoneCubicSpline
funcs.doc:
MonotoneCubicSpline builds a shape-preserving monotone cubic interpolant
using the Fritsch-Carlson method (the PCHIP algorithm). Where the sample data
is monotone the interpolant is monotone too, avoiding the overshoot of an
unconstrained cubic spline.

methods.name: Domain
methods.doc: Domain returns the first and last abscissae of the interpolant.

methods.name: Eval
methods.doc:
Eval returns the interpolant value at q, extrapolating with the nearest end
piece for queries outside the knot range.

methods.name: EvalDerivative
methods.doc: EvalDerivative returns the first derivative of the interpolant at q.

methods.name: EvalSecondDerivative
methods.doc: EvalSecondDerivative returns the second derivative of the interpolant at q.

methods.name: Integrate
methods.doc: Integrate returns the definite integral of the interpolant from a to b.

methods.name: Knots
methods.doc: Knots returns a copy of the interpolant's abscissae.

methods.name: Len
methods.doc: Len returns the number of cubic pieces.

methods.name: SegmentCoeffs
methods.doc: SegmentCoeffs returns the local monomial coefficients of piece i.

types.name: Vec
types.signature: type Vec []float64
types.doc:
Vec is a point or vector in R^n represented by its coordinates. A Vec never
aliases another Vec once produced by a constructor or arithmetic method: the
package always returns freshly allocated results, leaving inputs untouched.

funcs.name: CatmullRomPoint
funcs.signature: func CatmullRomPoint(p0, p1, p2, p3 Vec, t float64) Vec
funcs.doc:
CatmullRomPoint evaluates the uniform Catmull-Rom segment defined by four
successive control points at local parameter t in [0,1]; the returned point
lies on the curve between p1 and p2 and interpolates them at t=0 and t=1.

funcs.name: CatmullRomSegment
funcs.signature: func CatmullRomSegment(p0, p1, p2, p3 Vec, alpha, t float64) Vec
funcs.doc:
CatmullRomSegment evaluates a Catmull-Rom segment between p1 and p2 using the
Barry-Goldman non-uniform formulation with parameterisation exponent alpha
(0 = uniform, 0.5 = centripetal, 1 = chordal) at local parameter t in [0,1].
The centripetal variant (alpha = 0.5) avoids the cusps and self-intersections
that the uniform form can produce on unevenly spaced data.

funcs.name: CloneVecs
funcs.doc: CloneVecs returns an independent deep copy of a slice of vectors.

funcs.name: Cross
funcs.doc:
Cross returns the 3-D cross product a x b. It requires both vectors to be
three-dimensional.

funcs.name: DeBoor
funcs.signature: func DeBoor(p int, U []float64, ctrl []Vec, u float64) Vec
funcs.doc:
DeBoor evaluates a B-spline curve of degree p at parameter u directly with de
Boor's algorithm, given the control points and knot vector. It is an
allocation-light alternative to building a [BSplineCurve].

funcs.name: DeCasteljau
funcs.doc:
DeCasteljau evaluates the Bezier curve with the given control points at
parameter t in [0,1] using de Casteljau's algorithm, the numerically stable
standard method. It returns the point on the curve.

funcs.name: HermiteCurvePoint
funcs.signature: func HermiteCurvePoint(p0, p1, t0, t1 Vec, t float64) Vec
funcs.doc:
HermiteCurvePoint evaluates a vector-valued cubic Hermite segment with
endpoints p0, p1 and endpoint tangents (derivatives) t0, t1 at local
parameter t in [0,1]. The tangents are given directly (already scaled to the
unit interval).

funcs.name: NewVec
funcs.doc: NewVec returns a Vec containing a copy of the supplied coordinates.

funcs.name: SampleCurve
funcs.doc:
SampleCurve returns n+1 points sampled at uniformly spaced parameter values
across the curve's domain (n >= 1).

funcs.name: VecCombine
funcs.doc:
VecCombine returns the linear combination sum_i coeff[i]*pts[i]. All points
must share a dimension and coeff must match pts in length.

funcs.name: VecLerp
funcs.signature: func VecLerp(a, b Vec, t float64) Vec
funcs.doc: VecLerp returns the linear interpolation (1-t)*a + t*b of two vectors.

funcs.name: ZeroVec
funcs.doc: ZeroVec returns the zero vector of dimension n.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the component-wise sum v+w. It panics if the dimensions differ.

methods.name: AddScaled
methods.signature: func (v Vec) AddScaled(s float64, w Vec) Vec
methods.doc:
AddScaled returns v + s*w, a fused multiply-add. It panics on a dimension
mismatch.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent copy of v.

methods.name: Dim
methods.doc: Dim reports the number of coordinates in v.

methods.name: Dist
methods.doc: Dist returns the Euclidean distance between v and w.

methods.name: Dist2
methods.doc: Dist2 returns the squared Euclidean distance between v and w.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc:
Dot returns the Euclidean inner product of v and w. It panics on a dimension
mismatch.

methods.name: Equal
methods.signature: func (v Vec) Equal(w Vec, tol float64) bool
methods.doc: Equal reports whether v and w are equal within absolute tolerance tol.

methods.name: Lerp
methods.signature: func (v Vec) Lerp(w Vec, t float64) Vec
methods.doc: Lerp returns the linear interpolation (1-t)*v + t*w.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns -v.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean norm (length) of v.

methods.name: Norm2
methods.doc: Norm2 returns the squared Euclidean norm of v.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns v scaled component-wise by s.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc:
Sub returns the component-wise difference v-w. It panics if the dimensions
differ.

methods.name: Unit
methods.doc:
Unit returns v scaled to unit length. If v is the zero vector it is returned
unchanged (as a copy).

funcs.name: BasisFuns
funcs.signature: func BasisFuns(i int, u float64, p int, U []float64) []float64
funcs.doc:
BasisFuns returns the p+1 non-zero B-spline basis function values at u for the
span i (as returned by [FindSpan]). It uses the stable triangular recurrence
of Algorithm A2.2, avoiding division by zero.

funcs.name: Bernstein
funcs.signature: func Bernstein(n, i int, t float64) float64
funcs.doc:
Bernstein returns the value of the Bernstein basis polynomial
B(i,n)(t) = C(n,i) * t^i * (1-t)^(n-i).

funcs.name: BernsteinAll
funcs.doc:
BernsteinAll returns the full vector of degree-n Bernstein basis values at t,
computed with a stable triangular recurrence (no explicit powers). The result
has length n+1 and sums to one.

funcs.name: Binomial
funcs.doc:
Binomial returns the binomial coefficient C(n, k) computed with an integer
multiplicative recurrence that avoids intermediate overflow for moderate n.
It returns 0 when k is out of the range [0, n].

funcs.name: CentripetalParams
funcs.doc:
CentripetalParams returns parameter values for pts using the exponent alpha
on successive chord lengths: t[0]=0 and t[i]=t[i-1]+|P[i]-P[i-1]|^alpha. With
alpha=0.5 this yields the centripetal parameterisation commonly used to
choose interpolation knots.

funcs.name: ChordLengths
funcs.doc:
ChordLengths returns the cumulative chord-length parameter values of pts,
starting at zero; the final entry equals the total polyline length.

funcs.name: ClampedKnots
funcs.doc:
ClampedKnots returns an open uniform (clamped) knot vector so that the curve
interpolates its first and last control points. The interior knots are evenly
spaced on [0,1] and the ends have multiplicity degree+1.

funcs.name: CubicHermite
funcs.signature: func CubicHermite(y0, y1, m0, m1, h, t float64) float64
funcs.doc:
CubicHermite evaluates a single cubic Hermite segment with endpoints y0, y1,
endpoint tangents m0, m1, interval width h and local parameter t in [0,1].

funcs.name: CurveLength
funcs.doc:
CurveLength returns the total arc length of a curve over its full parameter
domain, computed by adaptive Gauss-Legendre quadrature of the speed |C'(t)|.

funcs.name: CurveLengthBetween
funcs.signature: func CurveLengthBetween(c Curve, a, b float64) float64
funcs.doc:
CurveLengthBetween returns the arc length of a curve between parameters a and
b (a <= b assumed within the domain).

funcs.name: DersBasisFuns
funcs.signature: func DersBasisFuns(i int, u float64, p, d int, U []float64) [][]float64
funcs.doc:
DersBasisFuns returns the non-zero basis functions and their derivatives up to
order d, evaluated at u for span i. The result ders[k][j] is the k-th
derivative (k = 0..d) of the j-th non-zero basis function (j = 0..p). It
implements Algorithm A2.3.

funcs.name: FindSpan
funcs.signature: func FindSpan(n, p int, u float64, U []float64) int
funcs.doc:
FindSpan returns the knot span index i such that U[i] <= u < U[i+1], for a
B-spline of degree p with n+1 control points (so n = len(ctrl)-1) and knot
vector U. It follows Algorithm A2.1 of The NURBS Book and clamps u to the
last span at the right end of the domain.

funcs.name: GaussLegendreIntegrate
funcs.signature: func GaussLegendreIntegrate(f func(float64) float64, a, b float64, n int) float64
funcs.doc:
GaussLegendreIntegrate approximates the integral of f over [a,b] with the
n-point Gauss-Legendre rule.

funcs.name: GaussLegendreRule
funcs.doc:
GaussLegendreRule returns the nodes and weights of the n-point Gauss-Legendre
quadrature rule on the interval [-1,1]. The nodes are the roots of the
degree-n Legendre polynomial found by Newton's iteration. It requires n >= 1.

funcs.name: HermiteBasis00
funcs.doc:
HermiteBasis00 is the Hermite basis function h00(t)=2t^3-3t^2+1, the weight
of the start value in a unit cubic Hermite segment.

funcs.name: HermiteBasis01
funcs.doc:
HermiteBasis01 is the Hermite basis function h01(t)=-2t^3+3t^2, the weight of
the end value.

funcs.name: HermiteBasis10
funcs.doc:
HermiteBasis10 is the Hermite basis function h10(t)=t^3-2t^2+t, the weight of
the (scaled) start tangent.

funcs.name: HermiteBasis11
funcs.doc:
HermiteBasis11 is the Hermite basis function h11(t)=t^3-t^2, the weight of the
(scaled) end tangent.

funcs.name: LinearInterpolate
funcs.signature: func LinearInterpolate(x, y []float64, q float64) (float64, error)
funcs.doc:
LinearInterpolate returns the piecewise-linear interpolant of the samples
(x[i], y[i]) evaluated at q, clamping to the end values outside the data
range. The abscissae x must be strictly increasing with len(x) >= 2 and
len(x) == len(y).

funcs.name: NearestInterpolate
funcs.signature: func NearestInterpolate(x, y []float64, q float64) (float64, error)
funcs.doc:
NearestInterpolate returns the sample ordinate whose abscissa is closest to q
(nearest-neighbour interpolation). Ties choose the left sample.

funcs.name: NormalizedChordParams
funcs.doc:
NormalizedChordParams returns chord-length parameters rescaled to the unit
interval [0,1]. If all points coincide it returns evenly spaced values.

funcs.name: OneBasisFun
funcs.signature: func OneBasisFun(p int, U []float64, i int, u float64) float64
funcs.doc:
OneBasisFun returns the value of the single B-spline basis function N(i,p)(u)
for knot vector U, implementing Algorithm A2.4. It is useful when only one
basis function is required rather than the whole non-zero set.

funcs.name: PolylineLength
funcs.doc:
PolylineLength returns the total length of the polyline through pts, the sum
of the Euclidean distances between consecutive points.

funcs.name: SolveCyclicTridiagonal
funcs.signature: func SolveCyclicTridiagonal(a, b, c, d []float64, alpha, beta float64) ([]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
SolveCyclicTridiagonal solves a tridiagonal system that additionally has the
corner entries alpha (top-right, coupling the last unknown into the first
row) and beta (bottom-left, coupling the first unknown into the last row).
Such "periodic" systems arise from periodic splines. It uses the
Sherman-Morrison formula on top of [SolveTridiagonal]. All of a, b, c and d
must have the same length n >= 2.

funcs.name: SolveTridiagonal
funcs.signature: func SolveTridiagonal(a, b, c, d []float64) ([]float64, error)
funcs.doc:
SolveTridiagonal solves the tridiagonal linear system whose sub-, main- and
super-diagonals are a, b and c and whose right-hand side is d, using the
Thomas algorithm. a[0] and c[n-1] are ignored. The four slices must share a
common length n >= 1. The returned slice is the solution vector; the inputs
are not modified.

funcs.name: StepInterpolate
funcs.signature: func StepInterpolate(x, y []float64, q float64) (float64, error)
funcs.doc:
StepInterpolate returns a left-continuous piecewise-constant interpolant: for
q in [x[i], x[i+1]) it returns y[i], clamping outside the range.

funcs.name: UniformKnots
funcs.doc:
UniformKnots returns a uniform (non-clamped) knot vector of the correct length
len(U) = numCtrl + degree + 1 with unit spacing starting at zero.

funcs.name: VecsEqual
funcs.signature: func VecsEqual(a, b []Vec, tol float64) bool
funcs.doc:
VecsEqual reports whether two vector slices are element-wise equal within
absolute tolerance tol.

examples.name: BezierCurve
examples.code:
b, _ := NewBezierCurve(
	NewVec(0, 0), NewVec(1, 2), NewVec(3, 2), NewVec(4, 0),
)
p := b.Eval(0.5)
fmt.Printf("point at t=0.5: (%.1f, %.1f)\n", p[0], p[1])
fmt.Printf("degree after elevation: %d\n", b.Elevate().Degree())
examples.output:
point at t=0.5: (2.0, 1.5)
degree after elevation: 4

examples.name: NURBSCircle
examples.code:
c, _ := NURBSCircle(NewVec(0, 0), 1)
p := c.Eval(0.25)
fmt.Printf("(%.3f, %.3f)\n", p[0], p[1])

packages.name: stats
packages.synopsis: Package stats is a small, dependency-free statistics and probability toolkit written entirely with the Go standard library (only math and sort).
packages.doc:
Package stats is a small, dependency-free statistics and probability
toolkit written entirely with the Go standard library (only math and sort).
It operates on plain []float64 data and is completely standalone: it does
not import the parent algebra package.

The package is organised into four groups.

# Descriptive statistics

Summaries of a data set: [Mean], [Median], [Mode], [Variance] and
[PopVariance], [StdDev] and [PopStdDev], [Min], [Max], [Range], [Sum],
[Product], [Quantile], [Percentile], [IQR], [Skewness], [Kurtosis],
[GeometricMean], [HarmonicMean], [Covariance], [Correlation], [ZScore] and
[WeightedMean].

Functions that need a defined result on empty or too-small inputs return
NaN rather than panicking, so callers can test with [math.IsNaN].

# Combinatorics

[Factorial], [Choose] (nCr) and [Perm] (nPr) are computed in the log-gamma
domain so they stay finite and accurate for large arguments.

# Probability distributions

Each distribution is a small value struct carrying its parameters, with
methods for the density/mass function, the cumulative distribution
function, the mean and the variance, and (where feasible) a quantile:

  - [Normal]      — PDF, CDF, Quantile, Mean, Variance.
  - [Binomial]    — PMF, CDF, Mean, Variance.
  - [Poisson]     — PMF, CDF, Mean, Variance.
  - [Uniform]     — PDF, CDF, Quantile, Mean, Variance.
  - [Exponential] — PDF, CDF, Quantile, Mean, Variance.
  - [StudentT]    — PDF, CDF, Mean, Variance.
  - [ChiSquared]  — PDF, CDF, Mean, Variance.
  - [Gamma]       — PDF, CDF, Mean, Variance.

[NormalPDF] and [NormalCDF] are convenience wrappers around the [Normal]
methods. No random sampling is provided.

# Regression

[LinearRegression] fits an ordinary-least-squares line and reports its
slope, intercept and coefficient of determination (R²).

# Conventions

The sample variants ([Variance], [StdDev], [Covariance]) use the unbiased
n-1 (Bessel) denominator; the population variants use n. [Skewness] and
[Kurtosis] use the population moment estimators, and [Kurtosis] returns
excess kurtosis (0 for a normal distribution).

types.name: Accumulator
types.doc:
Accumulator is a constant-memory, single-pass streaming replacement for the
multi-pass descriptive functions ([Mean], [Variance], [Skewness],
[Kurtosis] and friends). It maintains a running count, mean and the central
moments M2, M3 and M4 using the numerically stable Welford/Terriberry
recurrences, together with a running minimum and maximum, all updated in the
same pass.

The zero value is an empty accumulator ready for use. After construction no
method allocates, none sorts, and each observation is visited exactly once,
making Accumulator suitable for arbitrarily large or unbounded streams and,
via [Accumulator.Merge], for map-reduce over shards processed in parallel.

The summary statistics are numerically consistent with the corresponding
batch functions in this package: [Accumulator.Variance] matches [Variance],
[Accumulator.Skewness] matches [Skewness], and so on. Undefined results are
reported as NaN under the same conditions the batch functions use.

methods.name: Count
methods.doc: Count returns the number of observations pushed so far.

methods.name: Kurtosis
methods.doc:
Kurtosis returns the population excess kurtosis (the fourth standardized
moment minus 3, so a normal distribution has kurtosis 0), computed from the
running moments as n*M4/M2^2-3. It returns NaN if fewer than two
observations have been pushed or the variance is zero. It matches
[Kurtosis].

methods.name: Max
methods.doc:
Max returns the largest observation pushed so far, or NaN if none have been
pushed. It matches [Max].

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc:
Mean returns the arithmetic mean of the observations, or NaN if none have
been pushed. It matches [Mean].

methods.name: Merge
methods.doc:
Merge folds the observations summarized by o into the receiver as if they
had been pushed directly, using the pairwise-moment merge formulas of Chan,
Golub and LeVeque (extended to the third and fourth moments by Terriberry).
This enables a map-reduce style computation in which disjoint shards of a
stream are summarized by separate accumulators in parallel and then combined
exactly, with the same result as a single sequential pass.

methods.name: Min
methods.doc:
Min returns the smallest observation pushed so far, or NaN if none have been
pushed. It matches [Min].

methods.name: PopStdDev
methods.doc:
PopStdDev returns the population standard deviation (the square root of
[Accumulator.PopVariance]), or NaN if no observations have been pushed. It
matches [PopStdDev].

methods.name: PopVariance
methods.doc:
PopVariance returns the population variance using the n denominator, or NaN
if no observations have been pushed. It matches [PopVariance].

methods.name: Push
methods.doc:
Push incorporates a single observation x into the accumulator, updating the
count, mean, central moments M2, M3 and M4, and the running minimum and
maximum in one pass. It uses the numerically stable Welford/Terriberry
recurrences, which avoid the cancellation error of the naive
sum-of-squares approach.

methods.name: PushAll
methods.doc:
PushAll incorporates every observation in xs by calling [Accumulator.Push]
on each in order. It allocates nothing.

methods.name: Reset
methods.doc:
Reset returns the accumulator to its empty zero-value state so it can be
reused without allocating.

methods.name: Skewness
methods.doc:
Skewness returns the population coefficient of skewness (the third
standardized moment), computed from the running moments as
sqrt(n)*M3/M2^1.5. It returns NaN if fewer than two observations have been
pushed or the variance is zero. It matches [Skewness].

methods.name: StdDev
methods.doc:
StdDev returns the sample standard deviation (the square root of
[Accumulator.Variance]), or NaN if fewer than two observations have been
pushed. It matches [StdDev].

methods.name: Sum
methods.doc:
Sum returns the sum of all observations, computed as the mean times the
count. The sum of an empty accumulator is 0, matching [Sum].

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc:
Variance returns the unbiased sample variance using the n-1 (Bessel)
denominator, or NaN if fewer than two observations have been pushed. It
matches [Variance].

types.name: Beta
types.doc:
Beta is a beta distribution on the interval [0, 1] with shape parameters
Alpha (> 0) and Beta (> 0). It is the conjugate prior of the Bernoulli and
binomial success probability and, more generally, models a bounded quantity
whose two shape parameters trade probability mass between the endpoints.

methods.name: CDF
methods.doc:
CDF returns the cumulative probability P(X <= x), the regularized incomplete
beta function I_x(Alpha, Beta). It is 0 for x <= 0, 1 for x >= 1, and NaN when
either shape parameter is not positive.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns the mean of the distribution, Alpha/(Alpha+Beta).

methods.name: PDF
methods.doc:
PDF returns the probability density at x. It is 0 outside [0, 1] and NaN when
either shape parameter is not positive. The interior is evaluated from the
logarithm lnBeta = gammaLn(Alpha)+gammaLn(Beta)-gammaLn(Alpha+Beta) plus the
variable terms (Alpha-1)·ln x and (Beta-1)·ln(1-x), exponentiated once.

methods.name: Quantile
methods.doc:
Quantile returns the inverse CDF: the value x such that CDF(x) = p, for p in
[0, 1]. It is found by monotone bisection on the CDF. Quantile returns NaN if
p is outside [0, 1] or if either shape parameter is not positive.

methods.name: Sample
methods.doc:
Sample draws a single variate from the beta distribution using r, as
G1/(G1+G2) where G1 ~ Gamma(Alpha, 1) and G2 ~ Gamma(Beta, 1) are independent.

methods.name: Variance
methods.doc:
Variance returns the variance of the distribution,
Alpha·Beta / ((Alpha+Beta)²·(Alpha+Beta+1)).

types.name: Binomial
types.doc:
Binomial is a binomial distribution: the number of successes in N
independent trials, each succeeding with probability P. N must be >= 0 and
P must lie in [0, 1].

methods.name: CDF
