EuclideanMST returns the edges of a Euclidean minimum spanning tree of the
points. The tree's edges are a subset of the Delaunay edges; the algorithm
builds it from the Delaunay triangulation with Prim's method, falling back to
the complete graph for degenerate inputs. Edges are returned in the order
they are added to the tree.

funcs.name: GabrielEdges
funcs.doc:
GabrielEdges returns the edges (i, j) whose diametral circle (the circle with
segment ij as diameter) contains no other point. This is the Gabriel graph, a
subgraph of the Delaunay triangulation.

funcs.name: NearestNeighborEdges
funcs.doc:
NearestNeighborEdges returns the directed nearest-neighbour edges of the set:
entry i is the edge from point i to its nearest other point. It returns nil
when there are fewer than two points.

funcs.name: NewEdge
funcs.doc: NewEdge returns the edge joining vertex indices a and b.

funcs.name: RelativeNeighborhoodEdges
funcs.doc:
RelativeNeighborhoodEdges returns the edges (i, j) such that no third point k
is simultaneously closer to both i and j than they are to each other. This is
the relative neighbourhood graph, a subgraph of the Delaunay triangulation.

methods.name: Canonical
methods.doc:
Canonical returns the edge with its endpoints ordered so that A <= B. This
gives a stable key for undirected edges.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc: Contains reports whether vertex index v is an endpoint of e.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc:
Equal reports whether e and f join the same pair of vertices, ignoring
direction.

methods.name: Other
methods.doc:
Other returns the endpoint of e that is not v. It returns -1 if v is not an
endpoint of e.

methods.name: Reversed
methods.doc: Reversed returns the edge with its endpoints swapped.

types.name: Point
types.doc: Point is a location in the Euclidean plane.

funcs.name: Centroid
funcs.doc:
Centroid returns the arithmetic mean of the given points. It returns the
origin for an empty slice.

funcs.name: Circumcenter
funcs.signature: func Circumcenter(a, b, c Point) (Point, error)
funcs.doc:
Circumcenter returns the centre of the unique circle passing through a, b
and c. It returns ErrCollinear if the points are collinear.

funcs.name: ClonePoints
funcs.doc: ClonePoints returns a fresh copy of the given slice.

funcs.name: ConvexHull
funcs.doc:
ConvexHull returns the vertices of the convex hull of the given points in
counterclockwise order, using Andrew's monotone-chain algorithm. The hull
starts at the lexicographically smallest point and does not repeat it.
Collinear points on hull edges are omitted. Fewer than three distinct points
yield the deduplicated, sorted input.

funcs.name: DedupePoints
funcs.doc:
DedupePoints returns the points with exact duplicates removed, preserving the
order of first appearance.

funcs.name: GrahamHullByAngle
funcs.doc:
GrahamHullByAngle returns the convex hull computed by sorting points by polar
angle about the lowest point. The result matches ConvexHull; it is provided
as an alternative construction. Vertices are returned counterclockwise.

funcs.name: Incenter
funcs.signature: func Incenter(a, b, c Point) Point
funcs.doc:
Incenter returns the incentre of the triangle (a, b, c): the centre of its
inscribed circle, weighted by the opposite side lengths.

funcs.name: NewPoint
funcs.doc: NewPoint returns the Point with the given coordinates.

funcs.name: Origin
funcs.doc: Origin is the point (0, 0).

funcs.name: PolygonCentroid
funcs.doc:
PolygonCentroid returns the area centroid of the simple polygon. For a
degenerate (zero-area) polygon it falls back to the vertex average.

funcs.name: SortPointsXY
funcs.doc: SortPointsXY returns a copy of pts sorted lexicographically by X then Y.

funcs.name: TriangleCentroid
funcs.signature: func TriangleCentroid(a, b, c Point) Point
funcs.doc: TriangleCentroid returns the centroid (barycentre) of the triangle (a, b, c).

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the componentwise sum p + q.

methods.name: Angle
methods.doc:
Angle returns the angle of the vector p measured from the positive X axis,
in the range (-pi, pi].

methods.name: AngleTo
methods.doc: AngleTo returns the angle of the vector q - p from the positive X axis.

methods.name: ApproxEqual
methods.signature: func (p Point) ApproxEqual(q Point, eps float64) bool
methods.doc:
ApproxEqual reports whether p and q are within eps (per coordinate) of each
other.

methods.name: Cross
methods.doc: Cross returns the scalar (2D) cross product p x q = p.X*q.Y - p.Y*q.X.

methods.name: Distance
methods.doc: Distance returns the Euclidean distance between p and q.

methods.name: DistanceSq
methods.doc: DistanceSq returns the squared Euclidean distance between p and q.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the dot product p . q, treating both points as vectors.

methods.name: Equal
methods.doc: Equal reports whether p and q are exactly equal.

methods.name: IsFinite
methods.doc: IsFinite reports whether both coordinates of p are finite.

methods.name: Lerp
methods.signature: func (p Point) Lerp(q Point, t float64) Point
methods.doc: Lerp returns the linear interpolation (1-t)*p + t*q.

methods.name: Midpoint
methods.doc: Midpoint returns the point halfway between p and q.

methods.name: Neg
methods.doc: Neg returns the point reflected through the origin, -p.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean norm |p|.

methods.name: NormSq
methods.doc: NormSq returns the squared Euclidean norm |p|^2.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns the unit vector in the direction of p. If p is the zero
vector it is returned unchanged.

methods.name: Perp
methods.doc: Perp returns p rotated by +90 degrees, i.e. the vector (-p.Y, p.X).

methods.name: Rotate
methods.doc: Rotate returns p rotated about the origin by theta radians (counterclockwise).

methods.name: RotateAbout
methods.signature: func (p Point) RotateAbout(c Point, theta float64) Point
methods.doc: RotateAbout returns p rotated by theta radians about the centre c.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns p scaled by the factor s.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a human-readable representation of p.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the componentwise difference p - q.

types.name: Rect
types.doc:
Rect is an axis-aligned rectangle described by its minimum and maximum
corners. It is well formed when Min.X <= Max.X and Min.Y <= Max.Y.

funcs.name: BoundingBox
funcs.doc:
BoundingBox returns the axis-aligned bounding box of the given points. It
returns ErrEmpty when pts is empty.

funcs.name: NewRect
funcs.doc:
NewRect returns the axis-aligned rectangle spanning the two corners,
normalizing so that Min holds the smaller coordinates.

funcs.name: PolygonBoundingBox
funcs.doc:
PolygonBoundingBox returns the axis-aligned bounding box of the polygon
vertices. It returns ErrEmpty for an empty polygon.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns the area of the rectangle.

methods.name: Center
methods.doc: Center returns the centre point of the rectangle.

methods.name: Contains
methods.signature: func (r Rect) Contains(p Point, eps float64) bool
methods.doc:
Contains reports whether p lies inside or on the rectangle, within tolerance
eps.

methods.name: Corners
methods.doc:
Corners returns the four corners of the rectangle in counterclockwise order
starting from Min.

methods.name: Diagonal
methods.doc: Diagonal returns the length of the rectangle's diagonal.

methods.name: Expand
methods.doc:
Expand returns the rectangle grown outward by delta on every side. Negative
delta shrinks it.

methods.name: Height
methods.doc: Height returns the vertical extent of the rectangle.

methods.name: Perimeter
methods.doc: Perimeter returns the perimeter of the rectangle.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a human-readable representation of the rectangle.

methods.name: Union
methods.doc: Union returns the smallest rectangle containing both r and s.

methods.name: Width
methods.doc: Width returns the horizontal extent of the rectangle.

types.name: Triangle
types.doc: Triangle references three vertices by their indices into a point slice.

funcs.name: NewTriangle
funcs.signature: func NewTriangle(a, b, c int) Triangle
funcs.doc: NewTriangle returns the triangle on vertex indices a, b and c.

methods.name: Edges
methods.doc: Edges returns the triangle's three directed edges in order A->B, B->C, C->A.

methods.name: HasEdge
methods.doc:
HasEdge reports whether e is one of the triangle's edges (ignoring
direction).

methods.name: HasVertex
methods.doc: HasVertex reports whether v is one of the triangle's vertices.

methods.name: OppositeVertex
methods.doc:
OppositeVertex returns the triangle vertex not incident to edge e. It returns
-1 if e is not an edge of the triangle.

methods.name: SharesEdge
methods.doc:
SharesEdge reports whether triangles t and u share an edge, and returns that
shared edge. The boolean is false when they do not.

methods.name: Vertices
methods.doc: Vertices returns the triangle's three vertex indices as a slice.

types.name: Triangulation

funcs.name: DelaunayTriangulation
funcs.doc:
DelaunayTriangulation is an alias for Triangulate provided for readability at
call sites that want the fully qualified name.

funcs.name: Triangulate
funcs.doc:
Triangulate computes the Delaunay triangulation of the given points using the
incremental Bowyer-Watson algorithm. Duplicate points are removed before
triangulating. It returns ErrTooFewPoints if fewer than three distinct
points remain and ErrDegenerate if all points are collinear.

methods.name: Adjacency
methods.doc:
Adjacency returns the undirected adjacency lists of the point graph induced
by the triangulation edges. Entry i lists the neighbours of point i, sorted.

methods.name: BoundaryEdges
methods.doc:
BoundaryEdges returns the edges that belong to exactly one triangle; these
form the boundary of the triangulated region (the convex hull).

methods.name: Circumcenter
methods.doc:
Circumcenter returns the circumcentre of triangle i. This is the
corresponding Voronoi vertex.

methods.name: Circumcenters
methods.doc:
Circumcenters returns the circumcentres of all triangles, in triangle order.
Degenerate triangles (which should not occur in a valid Delaunay
triangulation) contribute the zero point.

methods.name: Circumcircle
methods.doc: Circumcircle returns the circumcircle of triangle i.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc:
Contains reports whether p lies within the triangulated region (its convex
hull).

methods.name: ConvexHull
methods.doc:
ConvexHull returns the convex hull of the triangulation's points in
counterclockwise order.

methods.name: EdgeLengths
methods.doc:
EdgeLengths returns the length of every unique edge, in the order returned by
Edges.

methods.name: Edges
methods.doc:
Edges returns the unique undirected edges of the triangulation, each in
canonical (A <= B) form, sorted.

methods.name: EulerCharacteristic
methods.doc:
EulerCharacteristic returns V - E + F for the triangulation, counting the
unbounded face. For a triangulation of a simply connected region this equals
2.

methods.name: IsDelaunay
methods.doc:
IsDelaunay reports whether the triangulation satisfies the empty-circumcircle
property: no point lies strictly inside any triangle's circumcircle. This is
a validation helper.

methods.name: Locate
methods.doc:
Locate returns the index of a triangle that contains the query point p, or
-1 if p lies outside the triangulated region. Boundary points are reported as
contained.

methods.name: LongestEdge
methods.doc:
LongestEdge returns the length of the longest edge and its endpoints as an
edge. The boolean is false when the triangulation has no edges.

methods.name: Neighbors
methods.doc:
Neighbors returns, for each triangle index, the indices of triangles sharing
an edge with it. The result is indexed by triangle.

methods.name: NumEdges
methods.doc: NumEdges returns the number of unique edges in the triangulation.

methods.name: NumPoints
methods.doc: NumPoints returns the number of points in the triangulation.

methods.name: NumTriangles
methods.doc: NumTriangles returns the number of triangles in the triangulation.

methods.name: Point
methods.doc: Point returns the i-th point of the triangulation.

methods.name: Points
methods.doc:
Points returns the (deduplicated) points the triangulation was built on. The
returned slice must not be modified.

methods.name: TotalArea
methods.doc:
TotalArea returns the sum of the areas of all triangles, i.e. the area of the
convex hull of the point set.

methods.name: Triangle
methods.doc: Triangle returns the i-th triangle.

methods.name: TriangleArea
methods.doc: TriangleArea returns the area of triangle i.

methods.name: TrianglePoints
methods.signature: func (tr *Triangulation) TrianglePoints(i int) (a, b, c Point)
methods.doc: TrianglePoints returns the three corner points of triangle i.

methods.name: Triangles
methods.doc:
Triangles returns the triangulation's triangles as index triples. The
returned slice must not be modified.

methods.name: VertexDegree
methods.doc:
VertexDegree returns the number of distinct neighbours of point index v in
the triangulation graph.

methods.name: VertexTriangles
methods.doc:
VertexTriangles returns, for each point index, the indices of the triangles
incident to it.

types.name: VoronoiCell
types.doc:
VoronoiCell is the region of the plane assigned to a single site. Vertices
lists the finite cell vertices in counterclockwise order. Unbounded cells
(those whose site is on the convex hull) have Bounded == false; their listed
vertices form the finite portion of the cell boundary.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns the area of the cell. Unbounded cells return positive infinity.

methods.name: Centroid
methods.doc:
Centroid returns the centroid of a bounded cell. For an unbounded or
degenerate cell it returns the vertex average (or the site if there are no
vertices).

methods.name: NumVertices
methods.doc: NumVertices returns the number of finite vertices of the cell.

methods.name: Perimeter
methods.doc: Perimeter returns the perimeter of the finite portion of the cell boundary.

types.name: VoronoiDiagram
types.doc:
VoronoiDiagram is the dual of a Delaunay triangulation: it partitions the
plane into cells, one per input site, where each cell is the set of points
closer to that site than to any other. Its finite vertices are the
circumcentres of the Delaunay triangles.

funcs.name: Voronoi
funcs.doc:
Voronoi builds the Voronoi diagram of the given sites by first constructing
their Delaunay triangulation. It returns the same errors as Triangulate.

funcs.name: VoronoiFromTriangulation
funcs.doc:
VoronoiFromTriangulation constructs the Voronoi diagram dual to an existing
Delaunay triangulation, avoiding a second triangulation pass.

methods.name: BoundedCells
methods.doc: BoundedCells returns only the cells that are finite (bounded) polygons.

methods.name: Cell
methods.doc: Cell returns the Voronoi cell of site index s.

methods.name: Cells
methods.doc: Cells returns all Voronoi cells, indexed by site.

methods.name: DelaunayEdges
methods.doc: DelaunayEdges returns the edges of the dual Delaunay triangulation.

methods.name: Edges
methods.doc: Edges returns the finite Voronoi edges.

methods.name: NearestSite
methods.doc:
NearestSite returns the index of the site closest to p and the distance to
it. It returns -1 for an empty diagram.

methods.name: NumEdges
methods.doc: NumEdges returns the number of finite Voronoi edges.

methods.name: NumSites
methods.doc: NumSites returns the number of sites.

methods.name: NumVertices
methods.doc: NumVertices returns the number of finite Voronoi vertices.

methods.name: Sites
methods.doc: Sites returns the diagram's sites (the deduplicated input points).

methods.name: Triangulation
methods.doc: Triangulation returns the underlying Delaunay triangulation.

methods.name: Vertices
methods.doc:
Vertices returns the finite Voronoi vertices (the Delaunay circumcentres),
indexed by triangle.

types.name: VoronoiEdge
types.doc:
VoronoiEdge is a finite edge of the Voronoi diagram, joining the
circumcentres of two adjacent Delaunay triangles. Tri1 and Tri2 are the two
triangle indices, and Dual is the Delaunay edge (between the two shared
sites) that the Voronoi edge crosses.

methods.name: Length
methods.doc: Length returns the Euclidean length of the Voronoi edge.

methods.name: Midpoint
methods.doc: Midpoint returns the midpoint of the Voronoi edge.

funcs.name: AllNearestNeighbors
funcs.doc:
AllNearestNeighbors returns, for each point in pts, the index of its nearest
other point. Entry i is -1 only when pts has a single element.

funcs.name: Barycentric
funcs.signature: func Barycentric(p, a, b, c Point) (u, v, w float64, ok bool)
funcs.doc:
Barycentric returns the barycentric coordinates (u, v, w) of p with respect
to the triangle (a, b, c), so that p = u*a + v*b + w*c and u+v+w = 1. The
boolean is false when the triangle is degenerate.

funcs.name: Circumradius
funcs.signature: func Circumradius(a, b, c Point) (float64, error)
funcs.doc:
Circumradius returns the radius of the circumcircle of the triangle
(a, b, c). It returns ErrCollinear if the points are collinear.

funcs.name: CircumradiusSpectrum
funcs.doc:
CircumradiusSpectrum returns the sorted list of circumradii of all Delaunay
triangles. The values are useful thresholds for choosing an alpha parameter:
picking alpha between two consecutive radii changes which triangles are
included.

funcs.name: ClosestPair
funcs.doc:
ClosestPair returns the two indices of the closest pair of points and their
distance. It uses an O(n log n) divide-and-conquer sweep. It returns
(-1, -1, +Inf) for fewer than two points.

funcs.name: Collinear
funcs.signature: func Collinear(a, b, c Point, eps float64) bool
funcs.doc:
Collinear reports whether a, b and c are collinear within tolerance eps
(applied to twice the signed area).

funcs.name: ConvexHullArea
funcs.doc: ConvexHullArea returns the area enclosed by the convex hull of the points.

funcs.name: ConvexHullIndices
funcs.doc:
ConvexHullIndices returns the indices (into pts) of the convex-hull vertices
in counterclockwise order. Duplicate coordinates resolve to the first index
with those coordinates.

funcs.name: ConvexHullPerimeter
funcs.doc: ConvexHullPerimeter returns the perimeter of the convex hull of the points.

funcs.name: Diameter
funcs.signature: func Diameter(pts []Point) (dist float64, i, j int)
funcs.doc:
Diameter returns the maximum distance between any two of the given points
(the diameter of the set) together with the indices of the realizing pair.
It evaluates candidate pairs on the convex hull using rotating calipers. It
returns 0 and (-1, -1) for fewer than two points.

funcs.name: EnclosingCircleRadius
funcs.doc:
EnclosingCircleRadius returns the radius of the minimum enclosing circle of
the given points.

funcs.name: InCircle
funcs.signature: func InCircle(a, b, c, d Point) float64
funcs.doc:
InCircle returns a positive value when d lies inside the circle through
a, b, c (assumed counterclockwise), negative when outside, and zero when the
four points are concyclic. It evaluates the classic 4x4 in-circle
determinant reduced to a 3x3 form.

funcs.name: InCircleTest
funcs.signature: func InCircleTest(a, b, c, d Point) bool
funcs.doc:
InCircleTest reports whether d lies strictly inside the circumcircle of the
triangle (a, b, c). The orientation of a, b, c is handled automatically.

funcs.name: Inradius
funcs.signature: func Inradius(a, b, c Point) float64
funcs.doc:
Inradius returns the radius of the inscribed circle of the triangle
(a, b, c).

funcs.name: IsCCW
funcs.signature: func IsCCW(a, b, c Point) bool
funcs.doc: IsCCW reports whether the triangle (a, b, c) is oriented counterclockwise.

funcs.name: IsCW
funcs.signature: func IsCW(a, b, c Point) bool
funcs.doc: IsCW reports whether the triangle (a, b, c) is oriented clockwise.

funcs.name: IsConvexPolygon
funcs.doc:
IsConvexPolygon reports whether the polygon given by its vertices in order is
convex. It tolerates collinear vertices and either winding direction.

funcs.name: KNearest
funcs.signature: func KNearest(pts []Point, q Point, k, skip int) []int
funcs.doc:
KNearest returns the indices of the k points in pts closest to q, ordered
from nearest to farthest. The index at skip is ignored (pass a negative value
to include all points). If k exceeds the number of eligible points, all are
returned.

funcs.name: NearestNeighbor
funcs.signature: func NearestNeighbor(pts []Point, q Point, skip int) (int, float64)
funcs.doc:
NearestNeighbor returns the index of the point in pts closest to the query
point q, together with the distance. The index at skip is ignored (pass a
negative value to consider all points); this makes it convenient to find a
point's nearest neighbour within its own set. It returns -1 for an empty set.

funcs.name: Orient2D
funcs.signature: func Orient2D(a, b, c Point) float64
funcs.doc:
Orient2D returns twice the signed area of the triangle (a, b, c). The result
is positive when a, b, c are in counterclockwise order, negative when
clockwise, and zero when collinear. This is the standard planar orientation
determinant.

funcs.name: Orientation
funcs.signature: func Orientation(a, b, c Point, eps float64) int
funcs.doc:
Orientation classifies the turn a -> b -> c as +1 (counterclockwise),
-1 (clockwise) or 0 (collinear) using the tolerance eps on the signed area.

funcs.name: PointInConvexPolygon
funcs.signature: func PointInConvexPolygon(p Point, poly []Point, eps float64) bool
funcs.doc:
PointInConvexPolygon reports whether p lies inside or on the convex polygon
whose vertices are given in counterclockwise order, within tolerance eps.

funcs.name: PointInPolygon
funcs.doc:
PointInPolygon reports whether p lies inside the simple polygon, using the
even-odd (ray casting) rule. Points on the boundary may be classified either
way depending on rounding.

funcs.name: PointInTriangle
funcs.signature: func PointInTriangle(p, a, b, c Point, eps float64) bool
funcs.doc:
PointInTriangle reports whether p lies inside or on the boundary of the
triangle (a, b, c), using tolerance eps on the orientation sign.

funcs.name: PolygonArea
funcs.doc: PolygonArea returns the (non-negative) area of the simple polygon.

funcs.name: PolygonIsCCW
funcs.doc: PolygonIsCCW reports whether the polygon's vertices wind counterclockwise.

funcs.name: PolygonPerimeter
funcs.doc:
PolygonPerimeter returns the perimeter of the polygon given by its vertices
in order (treated as closed).

funcs.name: PolygonSignedArea
funcs.doc:
PolygonSignedArea returns the signed area of the simple polygon given by its
vertices in order (via the shoelace formula). It is positive for
counterclockwise winding and negative for clockwise.

funcs.name: PolygonWindingNumber
funcs.doc:
PolygonWindingNumber returns the winding number of the polygon around p. A
nonzero value means p is enclosed.

funcs.name: SegmentsIntersect
funcs.signature: func SegmentsIntersect(p1, p2, q1, q2 Point) bool
funcs.doc:
SegmentsIntersect reports whether the closed segments p1p2 and q1q2 share at
least one point. Collinear overlapping segments count as intersecting.

funcs.name: TotalEdgeLength
funcs.doc:
TotalEdgeLength returns the sum of the lengths of the given edges over the
point set.

funcs.name: TriangleArea
funcs.signature: func TriangleArea(a, b, c Point) float64
funcs.doc: TriangleArea returns the (non-negative) area of the triangle (a, b, c).

funcs.name: TrianglePerimeter
funcs.signature: func TrianglePerimeter(a, b, c Point) float64
funcs.doc: TrianglePerimeter returns the perimeter of the triangle (a, b, c).

funcs.name: TriangleSignedArea
funcs.signature: func TriangleSignedArea(a, b, c Point) float64
funcs.doc:
TriangleSignedArea returns the signed area of the triangle (a, b, c),
positive for counterclockwise orientation.

examples.name: ConvexHull
examples.code:
pts := []Point{{0, 0}, {2, 0}, {2, 2}, {0, 2}, {1, 1}}
hull := ConvexHull(pts)
fmt.Println("hull size:", len(hull))
fmt.Printf("hull area: %.1f\n", PolygonArea(hull))

examples.name: Triangulate
Voronoi

packages.name: wavelet
packages.synopsis: Package wavelet provides discrete wavelet transforms and related multiresolution tools implemented with the Go standard library only.
packages.doc:
Package wavelet provides discrete wavelet transforms and related
multiresolution tools implemented with the Go standard library only.

The package is organised into a few areas:

  - Wavelet families (see wavelet.go): the orthonormal [Haar] wavelet and
    the Daubechies family via [Daubechies], with the common shortcuts [DB2]
    and [DB4]. A [Wavelet] exposes its analysis and synthesis filter banks
    through [Wavelet.DecLo], [Wavelet.DecHi], [Wavelet.RecLo] and
    [Wavelet.RecHi].
  - Single-level transforms (see dwt.go): the forward [DWT] and its inverse
    [IDWT], together with the [Coefficients] container.
  - Multiresolution analysis (see mra.go): multi-level decomposition
    [WaveDec] and reconstruction [WaveRec], the [Decomposition] type, and
    projection of each scale back to the original resolution via
    [Decomposition.MRAComponents].
  - Wavelet packets (see packet.go): the full binary [PacketTree] produced by
    [WaveletPacket], with perfect reconstruction and the Coifman-Wickerhauser
    cost measures [ShannonEntropy] and [LogEnergy].
  - Thresholding and denoising (see threshold.go): [SoftThreshold],
    [HardThreshold], the VisuShrink [UniversalThreshold], robust noise
    estimation via [EstimateNoiseSigma], and the end-to-end [Denoise].
  - Two-dimensional transforms (see dwt2d.go): a single level [DWT2D] and its
    inverse [IDWT2D] returning the [DWT2DResult] subbands.
  - Signal utilities (see signal.go): boundary extensions, linear
    [Convolve], [Downsample]/[Upsample], and small numeric helpers.

All transforms use periodic (circular) boundary handling, so every subband
has exactly half the length of its parent and the analysis operator is
orthogonal. Reconstruction is therefore the exact transpose of analysis and
achieves perfect reconstruction to within floating-point rounding. Because
the filters are orthonormal the transform preserves energy (Parseval's
relation): the sum of squares of the approximation and detail coefficients
equals the sum of squares of the input.

The package is deterministic and depends only on the math, sort and errors
standard-library packages.

types.name: Coefficients
types.doc:
Coefficients holds the approximation and detail coefficients produced by a
single level of the discrete wavelet transform.

funcs.name: Transform
funcs.doc:
Transform computes one level of the forward transform of signal with wavelet
w and returns the result as a [Coefficients] value. It panics under the same
conditions as [DWT].

methods.name: Energy
methods.doc:
Energy returns the total energy of the coefficients, the sum of squares of
the approximation and detail coefficients. For an orthogonal wavelet this
equals the energy of the original signal.

methods.name: Inverse
methods.doc:
Inverse reconstructs the signal from the coefficients using wavelet w,
inverting [Transform].

methods.name: Length
methods.doc: Length returns the reconstructed signal length, 2*len(c.Approx).

types.name: DWT2DResult
types.doc:
DWT2DResult holds the four subbands produced by one level of a separable
two-dimensional discrete wavelet transform. The transform is applied first
along rows (horizontally) and then along columns (vertically). Each subband
is an (R/2)-by-(C/2) matrix for an R-by-C input.

funcs.name: DWT2D
funcs.doc:
DWT2D computes one level of the separable two-dimensional discrete wavelet
transform of image with wavelet w, using periodic boundary handling on both
axes. It returns the four subbands. It panics if image is not a rectangular
matrix with even dimensions of at least two.

methods.name: Dims
methods.doc: Dims returns the height and width of each subband in the result.

methods.name: Energy
methods.doc:
Energy returns the total energy (sum of squares) across all four subbands.
For an orthogonal wavelet this equals the energy of the original image.

methods.name: Subbands
methods.doc: Subbands returns the four subbands in the fixed order LL, LH, HL, HH.

types.name: Decomposition
types.doc:
Decomposition is the result of a multi-level (multiresolution) discrete
wavelet transform. It stores the coarsest approximation together with the
detail coefficients captured at each level.

funcs.name: WaveDec
funcs.signature: func WaveDec(signal []float64, w Wavelet, levels int) (*Decomposition, error)
funcs.doc:
WaveDec computes a multi-level forward wavelet transform of signal with
wavelet w, cascading the low-pass branch for the requested number of levels.
It returns an error if levels is not positive, if it exceeds [MaxLevel] for
the signal length, or if any intermediate length is not even.

methods.name: ApproxComponent
methods.doc:
ApproxComponent projects the coarsest approximation back to the original
signal resolution, yielding the smooth multiresolution component of the
signal. The sum of ApproxComponent and every slice of [DetailComponents]
reproduces the original signal.

methods.name: DetailAt
methods.doc:
DetailAt returns the detail coefficients captured at the given level, where
level 1 is the finest scale and d.Levels() is the coarsest. It panics if
level is out of range.

methods.name: DetailComponents
methods.doc:
DetailComponents projects the detail coefficients of each level back to the
original signal resolution. The returned slice has one full-length component
per level, with index 0 the finest scale. Together with [ApproxComponent]
these components form an additive multiresolution decomposition of the signal.

methods.name: Energy
methods.doc:
Energy returns the total energy (sum of squares) of every stored coefficient.
For an orthogonal wavelet this equals the energy of the original signal.

methods.name: Flatten
methods.doc:
Flatten returns all coefficients concatenated into a single slice in the
order [approx, detail_coarsest, ..., detail_finest], matching the common
wavelet coefficient layout. The total length equals the original signal
length.

methods.name: Levels
methods.doc: Levels returns the number of decomposition levels stored in d.

methods.name: MRAComponents
methods.doc:
MRAComponents returns the additive multiresolution components of the signal:
the full-length smooth approximation and one full-length detail component per
level (index 0 finest). Their pointwise sum reproduces the original signal to
within floating-point rounding.

methods.name: Reconstruct
methods.doc:
Reconstruct rebuilds the original signal from the stored coefficients by
cascading the inverse transform from the coarsest to the finest level.

types.name: PacketTree
types.doc:
PacketTree is a full (balanced) wavelet packet decomposition. Unlike the
standard wavelet transform, which recurses only on the low-pass branch, the
packet transform recurses on both branches, producing a complete binary tree
of coefficient nodes. Nodes are held in natural (frequency-unordered) layout:
at level L there are 2^L nodes, and the children of node i at level L are
nodes 2i (low-pass) and 2i+1 (high-pass) at level L+1.

funcs.name: WaveletPacket
funcs.signature: func WaveletPacket(signal []float64, w Wavelet, levels int) (*PacketTree, error)
funcs.doc:
WaveletPacket computes a full wavelet packet decomposition of signal with
wavelet w down to the requested number of levels. It returns an error if
levels is not positive, if it exceeds [MaxLevel] for the signal length, or if
any intermediate node length is not even.

methods.name: Leaves
methods.doc:
Leaves returns copies of every node at the deepest level, left to right.
Concatenated, they hold the full set of leaf coefficients of the decomposition.

methods.name: Levels
methods.doc: Levels returns the depth of the packet tree.

methods.name: Node
methods.doc:
Node returns a copy of the coefficients stored at the given level and index.
It panics if either coordinate is out of range.

methods.name: NodeCount
methods.doc:
NodeCount returns the number of nodes at the given level, which is 2^level.
It panics if level is out of range.

methods.name: Reconstruct
methods.doc:
Reconstruct rebuilds the original signal from the leaf nodes of the tree by
inverting the packet transform level by level, achieving perfect
reconstruction to within floating-point rounding.

types.name: ThresholdMode
types.signature: type ThresholdMode int
types.doc: ThresholdMode selects the shrinkage rule applied to wavelet coefficients.

types.name: Wavelet
types.doc:
Wavelet is an orthonormal discrete wavelet, described by its analysis
(decomposition) and synthesis (reconstruction) filter banks. The zero value
is not usable; construct one with [Haar], [Daubechies], [DB2] or [DB4].

For the orthogonal families provided here the synthesis filters are equal to
the analysis filters, because the periodic analysis operator is orthogonal
and its inverse is its transpose.

funcs.name: DB2
funcs.doc: DB2 returns the Daubechies wavelet with two vanishing moments (four taps).

funcs.name: DB4
funcs.doc: DB4 returns the Daubechies wavelet with four vanishing moments (eight taps).

funcs.name: Daubechies
funcs.doc:
Daubechies returns the Daubechies wavelet with p vanishing moments (a filter
of 2p taps). Supported orders are p = 1 (which is [Haar]), 2, 3 and 4. It
returns an error for any other order.

funcs.name: Haar
funcs.doc:
Haar returns the Haar wavelet, the orthonormal Daubechies wavelet with one
vanishing moment (equivalently db1). Its scaling filter is
[1/sqrt(2), 1/sqrt(2)].

methods.name: DecHi
methods.doc: DecHi returns a copy of the analysis (decomposition) high-pass filter.

methods.name: DecLo
methods.doc: DecLo returns a copy of the analysis (decomposition) low-pass filter.

methods.name: IsOrthogonal
methods.doc:
IsOrthogonal reports whether the scaling filter satisfies the orthonormality
conditions of an orthogonal wavelet to within tol: unit norm and vanishing
autocorrelation at every non-zero even lag.

methods.name: Name
methods.doc: Name returns the short name of the wavelet family, such as "haar" or "db4".

methods.name: RecHi
methods.doc:
RecHi returns a copy of the synthesis (reconstruction) high-pass filter. For
the orthogonal wavelets in this package it equals the analysis high-pass
filter.

methods.name: RecLo
methods.doc:
RecLo returns a copy of the synthesis (reconstruction) low-pass filter. For
the orthogonal wavelets in this package it equals the analysis low-pass
filter.

methods.name: Taps
methods.doc: Taps returns the number of filter coefficients (the filter length).

methods.name: VanishingMoments
methods.doc:
VanishingMoments returns the number of vanishing moments of the wavelet.
Polynomial signals of degree below this number produce zero detail
coefficients away from the boundary.

funcs.name: Convolve
funcs.doc:
Convolve returns the full linear convolution of x and h, a slice of length
len(x)+len(h)-1. It returns an empty slice if either input is empty.

funcs.name: DWT
funcs.doc:
DWT computes one level of the forward discrete wavelet transform of signal
using the wavelet w with periodic boundary handling. It returns the
approximation (low-pass) and detail (high-pass) coefficients, each of length
len(signal)/2. It panics if len(signal) is odd or smaller than two.

funcs.name: Denoise
funcs.signature: func Denoise(signal []float64, w Wavelet, levels int, mode ThresholdMode) []float64
funcs.doc:
Denoise removes noise from signal by wavelet shrinkage (the VisuShrink
procedure). It performs a levels-deep decomposition with wavelet w, estimates
the noise level from the finest detail band via [EstimateNoiseSigma],
computes the [UniversalThreshold], applies the selected thresholding mode to
every detail band while leaving the approximation untouched, and reconstructs
the signal. It panics if levels is not valid for the signal length; on a
noise-free constant signal it returns the input unchanged.

funcs.name: Downsample
funcs.doc:
Downsample returns every factor-th sample of x starting at index 0. A factor
of 2 keeps the even-indexed samples. It panics if factor is not positive.

funcs.name: Energy
funcs.doc: Energy returns the sum of squares of the elements of x.

funcs.name: EstimateNoiseSigma
funcs.doc:
EstimateNoiseSigma estimates the standard deviation of white Gaussian noise
from a set of finest-scale detail coefficients using the robust median
estimator sigma = median(|detail|) / 0.6745, where 0.6745 is the standard
normal 75th percentile. It returns 0 for an empty slice.

funcs.name: HardThreshold
funcs.doc:
HardThreshold applies the hard-thresholding rule to x with threshold lambda:
x is kept when |x| > lambda and set to zero otherwise. A negative lambda is
treated as zero.

funcs.name: IDWT
funcs.doc:
IDWT reconstructs a signal from its approximation and detail coefficients
using the wavelet w, inverting a single level of [DWT]. The returned signal
has length 2*len(approx). It panics if the two coefficient slices differ in
length.

funcs.name: IDWT2D
funcs.doc:
IDWT2D reconstructs an image from its four subbands using wavelet w,
inverting a single level of [DWT2D] to within floating-point rounding. It
panics if the four subbands are not equally sized rectangular matrices.

funcs.name: IsPowerOfTwo
funcs.doc: IsPowerOfTwo reports whether n is a positive power of two.

funcs.name: L2Norm
funcs.doc: L2Norm returns the Euclidean (l2) norm of x, sqrt(sum x_i^2).

funcs.name: LogEnergy
funcs.doc:
LogEnergy returns the log-energy cost measure sum_i ln(x_i^2) taken over the
non-zero coefficients of x, another additive Coifman-Wickerhauser cost used
for best-basis selection.

funcs.name: MaxAbs
funcs.doc:
MaxAbs returns the largest absolute value among the elements of x, or 0 for
an empty slice.

funcs.name: MaxLevel
funcs.doc:
MaxLevel returns the maximum number of dyadic decomposition levels that a
signal of length n admits under periodic boundary handling, which is the
2-adic valuation of n (the number of times n can be halved while remaining
even). It returns 0 for non-positive or odd n.

funcs.name: MeanAbsoluteDeviation
funcs.doc:
MeanAbsoluteDeviation returns the median absolute deviation of x about its
own median, median(|x_i - median(x)|). It is a robust measure of spread and
returns NaN for an empty slice.

funcs.name: Median
funcs.doc:
Median returns the median of x. For an even number of elements it returns the
average of the two central values. It does not modify x and returns NaN for
an empty slice.

funcs.name: NextPowerOfTwo
funcs.doc:
NextPowerOfTwo returns the smallest power of two that is greater than or equal
to n. It returns 1 for n less than or equal to 1.

funcs.name: PeriodicExtend
funcs.doc:
PeriodicExtend returns a copy of x extended by pad samples on each side using
periodic (wrap-around) boundary handling, so the result has length
len(x)+2*pad. It panics if x is empty and pad is positive.

funcs.name: ShannonEntropy
funcs.doc:
ShannonEntropy returns the normalized Shannon entropy of x, a
Coifman-Wickerhauser cost measure of coefficient concentration. Writing
p_i = x_i^2 / sum(x^2), the entropy is -sum p_i * ln(p_i), with the
convention 0*ln(0) = 0. It is 0 when the energy is concentrated in a single
coefficient and ln(n) when it is spread uniformly. It returns 0 for a
zero-energy input.

funcs.name: SoftThreshold
funcs.doc:
SoftThreshold applies the soft-thresholding (shrinkage) rule to x with
threshold lambda: sign(x) * max(|x| - lambda, 0). A negative lambda is
treated as zero.

funcs.name: SymmetricExtend
funcs.doc:
SymmetricExtend returns a copy of x extended by pad samples on each side using
whole-sample symmetric (mirror) boundary handling, so the result has length
len(x)+2*pad. The boundary samples are not repeated. It panics if x is empty
and pad is positive.

funcs.name: Threshold
funcs.signature: func Threshold(x, lambda float64, mode ThresholdMode) float64
funcs.doc:
Threshold applies the thresholding rule selected by mode to x with threshold
lambda. It panics for an unknown mode.

funcs.name: ThresholdSlice
funcs.signature: func ThresholdSlice(xs []float64, lambda float64, mode ThresholdMode) []float64
funcs.doc:
ThresholdSlice returns a new slice with the thresholding rule selected by
mode applied elementwise to xs with threshold lambda. The input is not
modified.

funcs.name: UniversalThreshold
funcs.signature: func UniversalThreshold(n int, sigma float64) float64
funcs.doc:
UniversalThreshold returns the Donoho-Johnstone universal (VisuShrink)
threshold sigma * sqrt(2 * ln(n)) for a signal of length n and noise standard
deviation sigma. It returns 0 for n <= 1.

funcs.name: Upsample
funcs.doc:
Upsample returns x with factor-1 zeros inserted after each sample, producing
a slice of length len(x)*factor. It panics if factor is not positive.

funcs.name: WaveRec
funcs.doc:
WaveRec reconstructs the original signal from a [Decomposition], inverting
[WaveDec] to within floating-point rounding.

funcs.name: ZeroExtend
funcs.doc:
ZeroExtend returns a copy of x extended by pad zero samples on each side, so
the result has length len(x)+2*pad.

funcs.name: ZeroPadToPowerOfTwo
funcs.doc:
ZeroPadToPowerOfTwo returns a copy of x zero-padded on the right up to the
next power-of-two length. If len(x) is already a power of two the input is
returned unchanged (as a copy).
